Positioned at the foothills of the Rwenzori Mountains National Park, Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, and in the Kampala area, AWOHA UGANDA leverages the rich cultural and natural heritage of these regions to promote sustainable development and conservation.

Name: Marit'IMT
Description: Comité de Voile de l'IMT Nord Europe

Name: GEF SGP Indonesia
Description: GEF SGP Indonesia merupakan bagian dari jaringan komunitas pengelolaan sumber daya alam

Name: Yayasan Gili Matra Bersama
Description:
An international marine research center for ocean conservation, located on the beautiful Gili Islands of Indonesia.  Our research team consists of a group of marine biologists, instructors, social media experts, and entrepreneurs, united by a deep love for the sea and committed to effecting positive change. 

We hold the believe that a strong community of different individuals can make a bigger impact on marine protection than a single organization. Therefore, we collaborate with local government, stakeholders, national and international partners, as well as various universities around the world.

Name: Action des Femmes pour une Planète Bio (AFEPB)
Description: AFEPB tire son origine de l'envi de donner aux Femmes un cadre de confiance et d'échange en toute liberté sur leurs situations. Consciente de la vulnérabilité de la femme face au défis environnementaux, il est question d'amener les femmes à adhérer et s'impliquer massivement dans le processus du Développement Durable pour être/apporter un changement positif et efficace dans leur foyer, famille, communauté, etc.

Name: ICOMOS Aotearoa New Zealand
Description: ICOMOS, the International Council on Monuments and Sites, is an international non-governmental organisation of heritage professionals engaged in the conservation of places of cultural heritage value and dedicated to the conservation of the world’s historic monuments and sites.

Name: Angel Shark Project
Description:
The Angel Shark Project is a multidisciplinary initiative that gathers ecological and population data while working closely with local communities, researchers, and governments to raise awareness and promote conservation actions. Currently, it involves 12 organizations across 7 countries: Spain, Wales, Libya, Greece, Corsica, Croatia, and Ireland. 

Founded in 2014, the Angel Shark Project: Canary Islands aims to ensure the protection and conservation of angel shark populations in the Canary Islands. This initiative is a collaboration between three European partners: the Leibniz Institute for the Analysis of Biodiversity Change (LIB), the University of Las Palmas de Gran Canaria (ULPGC), and the Zoological Society of London (ZSL). 

For more information about the project and its activities, visit the official website: https://angelsharkproject.com.

Name: Ontario Biodiversity Council
Description:
The Ontario Biodiversity Council recognizes the importance of biodiversity and is taking steps to conserve it as an essential part of building an ecologically sustainable and strong future for the province. 

Council promotes the view that our wealth and prosperity, our quality of life and our well-being are directly tied to the province’s biological diversity. Council also recognizes that all of us - government, conservation organizations, and different sectors must work together to deliver the scale of change required to achieve the vision in Ontario’s Biodiversity Strategy. 

Council works to promote the understanding that: “Protecting the diversity of life on Earth – of which we humans are an integral part – requires broad societal consensus and participation. It is a challenge not for some of us, but for all of us.”

The Ontario Biodiversity Council was created in 2005 in fulfillment of an action in the province’s first Ontario’s Biodiversity Strategy. Council members represent conservation and environmental groups, industry associations, Indigenous organizations, natural history institutions, academia and government agencies.

Name: NatureMind-ED
Description:
NatureMind-Ed, as a social enterprise, leads transdisciplinary community-based biodiversity conservation and ecosystem restoration projects with the aim of building social-ecological resilience in Southern Thailand whereby nature and society interweave in a regenerative manner.


The programmes, research projects, workshops, events and activities we lead are community-based and transdisciplinary. As such community engagement, transformative learning and participatory processes are central tenets of our projects’ implementation contributing to project outcome sustainability via increased community ownership and stewardship, contextualized social innovation towards natural resource adaptive management and diversified income sources.

Transdisciplinary ecosystem restoration research implies embracing epistemological pluralism and acknowledging the multiple ways of knowing used by the myriad of actors involved in a project in order to co-design effective and sustainable solutions. Accordingly, we rely on a large portfolio of methods and tools ranging from quantitative ecology, i.e. biodiversity assessments, environmental sciences and geospatial analyses to qualitative approaches involving social sciences and humanities. The strong expertise and experience in facilitation our team has developed over the years while creating experiential education programs, provide us with strong assets to successfully implement participatory methodologies such as participatory rural appraisal, participatory mapping, natural resources calendars and many other participatory protocols. Finally one of our team’s main strength is its creative potential and genuine heart centered community partnership development approach. We use photography, film and other visual arts as well as serious games to engage with our community partners or other stakeholder in an authentic way, ensuring lasting relationship can be built and embracing our role as community resilience brokers.

Name: Fondo Golfo de México
Description: Somos una institución dedicada a crear alianzas e impulsar oportunidades enfocadas en la conservación y el aprovechamiento de los recursos naturales en la región del Golfo de México.

Name: LIFE Wetlands4Cities
Description: Wetlands4Cities is a European project aimed at increasing climate resilience by implementing actions in and around the cities of Mechelen (Belgium) and Tilburg (the Netherlands). The project focuses on restoring 360 hectares of wetlands, improving water management by separating waste from rainwater, and expanding green spaces to mitigate the impacts of flooding, drought, and heatwaves. Through these efforts, Wetlands4Cities helps make cities more sustainable and better adapted to climate change. The project involves collaboration with local municipalities, water management organizations, and nature conservation groups.

Name: O'Seas
Description:
Nous, c’est Gaspard, Diego, Paul et Raphaël, quatre jeunes originaires d’Ardèche.

Notre voyage commence dans ce département, par l’expression d’une envie commune : se rendre en Australie. Rien de très original au premier abord, dans un monde où se déplacer à 15 000 kilomètres en quelques heures est devenu normal grâce à l’avion.

Conscient des enjeux environnementaux et sociaux de notre époque, notre petit groupe s’est directement vu confronté à un problème majeur : comment se rendre à l’autre bout du monde sans prendre l’avion ? Ce mode de transport est en effet très polluant et nous voulions trouver une alternative plus en accord avec nos valeurs et notre mode de vie.

Nous avons donc réfléchi à plusieurs solutions, puis nous avons fait le choix qui nous correspondait le mieux : nous irons en Australie en voilier. Ainsi, nous avons décidé de voyager différemment et de s’engager dans un projet d’ampleur, guidé par des valeurs humaines et écologiques.

Name: Fundación Galictis
Description: La fundación GALICTIS, gestión y monitoreo de la biodiversidad es una organización sin ánimo de lucro, cuyo objetivo es promover, fomentar y desarrollar proyectos que contribuyan al conocimiento, evaluación y conservación de la biodiversidad en Colombia, a través de la investigación científica, la educación ambiental y la participación comunitaria, buscando proteger los ecosistemas y mejorar el bienestar de las comunidades locales.

Name: SpeSeas
Description: We are Trinbagonian scientists, conservationists, and communicators who wish to make positive changes to the way the ocean is used and managed in our country, our region and our world.

Name: Quartier Vert Challenge
Description: Un Sénégal Vert Pour un meilleur environnement.

Name: Angkor Wildlife & Aquarium
Description:
Angkor Wildlife & Aquarium is set to feature a 500m3 aquarium featuring freshwater species as well as a 600m3 saltwater aquarium. The main aquarium building will house freshwater species that are endemic to Cambodia, Thailand, and Vietnam. There are also 10 hectares of outdoor animal exhibits and attractions set to include tigers, river otters, and crocodiles.

The project emphasizes education as well as providing an entertainment venue catering to international tourists visiting Angkor Wat and the Siem Reap area.

The Angkor Wildlife & Aquarium opening of the first phase is planned for October 2022.

Name: Freshwater Life
Description:
We are a non-profit organization dedicated to preventing species extinctions by addressing the threat of invasive species to freshwater ecosystems. Through strategic eradication efforts, we work to restore biodiversity and protect vital habitats.
How We Work:
Project Implementation – We provide training, support, and expertise to implement effective eradication projects in collaboration with local stakeholders.
Funding & Resources – We bring project funding and help organizations identify new financial opportunities to sustain long-term conservation efforts.
Capacity Building – Through hands-on collaboration, we equip communities with the skills, tools, and standard operating procedures (SOPs) needed for future conservation success.
Strategy & Guidance – We develop tailored strategies that respect local needs, cultural values, and ecological realities, ensuring effective and lasting solutions.
Our Core Values:
Commitment to Freshwater Biodiversity – We take decisive action to mitigate invasive species threats and restore ecosystems.
Collaborative Conservation – We partner with scientists, policymakers, Indigenous communities, and local organizations to achieve shared conservation goals.
Empowering Communities – We prioritize knowledge exchange, skill-building, and culturally respectful conservation efforts.
Transparency & Learning – We uphold clear accountability in funding and ecological outcomes, recognizing that every effort—successful or not—provides valuable lessons for the future!

Name: New York Art Conservation
Description:
NYAC is a women-owned business founded in 2022. Our collective knowledge, trust, and experience is our strength, and as an equal partnership, we value a collaborative spirit and democratic exchange of ideas and professionalism. We hold our work to the highest of standards and take pride in the relationships we have developed over the years with artists and artist foundations.

Our international and multilingual team of highly skilled and specialized conservators has consistently excelled in problem-solving, finding solutions for unprecedented conservation challenges, and adjusting established treatment approaches to contemporary and non-traditional materials.

We have an innate understanding of the art market’s complexity and the specific role that conservation plays. We work closely with allied professionals and conservators in other specialities to create a full spectrum of conservation services.

In order to keep up with the latest methodologies in conservation and always striving for new heights of acquired education, we are members of and active participants in the following professional associations: AIC, AIC – CAN (founding members), ICOM-CC, INCCA, Alliance for Response NY.

Name: Transloc @ Biodiversa+ | Sharing for Better Translocations
Description:
The Transloc database, developed by the Muséum National d'Histoire Naturelle & Université Paris Saclay, is now fully operational! 

As a key tool within the BiodivERsA Transloc research project (running until December 2025), it has been continuously enriched by researchers to advance knowledge on species (re)introductions and biodiversity restoration.

 What is Transloc?
Transloc is a collaborative platform designed to support knowledge exchange among conservation practitioners. It compiles real-world translocation cases of plants, animals, fungi, and amphibians across the Western Palearctic, providing valuable insights to improve conservation strategies.

 Connect |  Exchange |  Rewild

 Join the platform, contribute & explore the database:
 https://translocations.in2p3.fr/

 Get in touch: Biodiversa.transloc@uliege.be

Let’s build a stronger network for better conservation translocations!

Name: MarineLife Conservation Foundation
Description: The MarineLife Protection Foundation is passionately dedicated to the preservation and safeguarding of marine wildlife and the delicate ecosystems of our world’s oceans. With a mission deeply rooted in environmental conservation, we are committed to ensuring the longevity of marine species and their habitats for generations to come.

Name: Mesa Conservation District
Description:
Mesa Conservation District is your local partner in conservation of natural resources; from soil and water, to wildlife habitat and agriculture.
Our mission is to support leadership, expertise, funding, and education that bring together the diverse interests in our watershed. Enhancing, protecting, and conserving our priceless resources to ensure a vibrant and abundant future for our entire community.

Name: La Terre Ferme
Description:
La Terre Ferme accueille différentes activités qui ont pour objectif de sensibiliser au vivant, de créer des liens entre la santé et l'environnement.
Les activités présentes à La Terre Ferme : 
- Les fleurs de Katell (plantes aromatiques, plantes médicinales, tisanes, ...), 
- Apivitis (du vin naturel), 
- Shiatsu.
Nous accueillons également des abeilles qui sont nos sentinelles et nos partenaires par rapport à la restauration de la bioviversité.

Name: Corredor Caipira
Description: Projeto realizado pela FEALQ, NACE-PTECA e ESALQ/USP, patrocinado pela Petrobras, para a restauração florestal e valorização da cultura do interior paulista.

Name: Sharks Pacific
Description:
Sharks Pacific’s mission is to understand the importance of sharks in Pacific communities, and to advocate for the conservation and responsible management of sharks and the related marine environment through research, outreach, and advocacy.

Sharks Pacific is working with Island communities, fishermen, policy makers, and scientists to understand sharks and rays in Pacific waters—and to create effective policies to protect vulnerable species. Through research, outreach, education and advocacy, we are working to understand the complex issues contributing to the decline of shark and ray populations and helping to create effective regulations that will protect them for future generations.

Name: SER Africa
Description:
SER members are bringing restoration to every corner of the earth
For more than three decades, the Society for Ecological Restoration (SER) has given voice to the field of ecological restoration and provided leadership in all aspects of its development. We are a dynamic global network of over 5,000 members from 100+ countries who foster the exchange of knowledge and expertise among ecological restoration practitioners and scientists from diverse disciplines and backgrounds. In addition to communicating leading-edge tools, technologies and scientific findings, SER actively promotes best practices and effective restoration policy around the world.

We are working to ensure that ecological restoration is recognized and utilized as a fundamental component of global conservation, biodiversity and sustainable development programs, and that ecological restoration projects are designed and implemented in a way that provides people with the opportunity to not only repair ecological damage, but also improve the human condition.

Name: Alianza Internacional para la Conservación Ambiental
Natoure
Description: Natoure is a fair trade marketplace for epic travels dedicated to protect forests and be inclusive with their guardians: indigenous local communities.

Name: Cylchgrawn Natur Cymru Magazine (INCC)
Description:
Natur Cymru is published twice a year by INCC. Each edition of 68 pages showcases Welsh wildlife, landscapes, projects and people, with stunning artwork and photography from local contributors.

Mae Natur Cymru'n cael ei gyhoeddi ddwywaith y flwyddyn   gan INCC. Mae pob rhifn sy'n 68 tudalen yn tynnu sylw at fywyd gwyllt Cymru, ei thirweddau, ei phrosiectau a'i phobl, gyda gwaith celf ffotograffiaeth drawiadol gan gyfranwyr lleol.

Name: IOI Ocean Academy Tunisia
Description: الأكاديمية المحيط التابعة للمعهد الدولي للمحيطات (IOI) موجودة في 21 دولة حول العالم. تونس إنضمت ليها في 2024. الأكاديمية تروج لثقافة المحيطات مبنية على الاحتياجات المحلية.

Name: Tax Credit Connection
Description: Founded in 2003, Tax Credit Connection (TCC), established itself as a leading expert in all things related to tax credits. Serving clients primarily in Colorado, we partner with both land and property owners to assist with their tax credit needs — whether its making sure their applications to the state are complete and accurate, or ensuring their tax forms are in to their accountant before the deadline.

Name: NewLands
Description:
FORESTS AS A TOOL OF CHANGE

We unite the potential of the forest as a “combat tool” against climate change, the potential of people as a community that protects forests and the potential of technology as a bridge for participation.

Name: SREPEN-RNE
Description: L'association SREPEN RNE s'engage depuis une cinquantaine d'années pour que la nature réunionnaise et les Réunionnais vivent en harmonie.

Name: Firebreak
Description:
Parce que le réchauffement climatique, l’aggravation des phénomènes et la sur sollicitation des services publics sont malheureusement une réalité… Parce que la France est une exposée aux incendies de forêts, au même titre que l’Australie, la Californie, la Grèce ou le Portugal… Parce que votre maison est votre bien le plus précieux et que le calme d’une zone forestière ou péri-urbaine ne doit pas devenir une source d’inquiétude…

1. Détection
Au travers de partenariats spécifiques, Firebreak dispose de moyens de techniques spécifiques à ce risque, permettant de détecter un départ de feu à plusieurs kilomètres.

2. Prévention
Nos techniciens vous accompagnent, vous conseillent dans le choix des dispositifs à mettre en œuvre et vous donne les moyens de répondre de manière optimale à vos obligations réglementaires.

3. Protection
Firebreak est le n°1 français des services de protection des biens contre les incendies de forêts. Complémentaire des Services d’Incendie et de Secours, notre personnel dispose de toutes les compétences et de tous les moyens nécessaires pour protéger vos biens. Notre démarche, innovante et inédite, se traduit notamment par le développement de procédures et de matériel dédiés et particulièrement efficaces.
Quelques soient vos besoins : Résidences principales ou secondaires, lotissements, domaines, centres équestres, golfs, camping, évènementiel… Firebreak propose une large gamme complète de services et solutions assurés par des spécialistes qualifiés et expérimentés afin que votre inquiétude laisse place à la sérénité.

Name: Holon Labs Foundation
Description: Holon Computing Labs is a non-profit organization dedicated to progress the cognition of collective systems. Our main use case is transforming urban conservation in the city of Houston, Texas.

Name: Turtle Island Corporation
Description:
Turtle Island, led by internationally renowned scientist Dr. Peter Praschag, aims to conserve, rescue, and breed endangered turtle species from around the world. 

Together with an international network of partner organizations and supporters, we care about saving the most endangered land vertebrates on our planet - turtles.

Our team consists largely of experts, specialized in conservation and protection of endangered turtles, as well as motivated and dedicated animal keepers and volunteers. Conservation, species protection, animal welfare and research are essential aspects of our daily work.

Name: France Nature Environnement Savoie
Description: Association de protection de l'environnement

Name: Global Sustain Certification, GSC
Description: Labélisation, Certification & Audits

Name: ONG KONGO RIVER
Description: Nous sommes une ONG, animée par la mission fondamentale de valoriser et protéger les eaux du bassin du Congo ainsi que ses précieux écosystèmes.

Name: Social Ecologies
Description: We specialize in multi-year, large-scale land restoration sites, as well as land services for homeowners and land stewards, communities and organizations, businesses and institutions. Engaging local residents — instigating ecological projects — building regenerative systems. Our services translate scientific analysis into actionable steps for our clients.

Name: Adaptatie Atelier
Description: Het Adaptatie Atelier is een nieuwe coalitie opgericht door en voor gemeenten, waterschappen en provincies die samen beleid en prikkels ontwikkelen voor klimaatadaptatie (en biodiversiteit) met en voor bedrijven en inwoners. Het Adaptatie Atelier is gestart op 22 juni 2022 met gemeente Antwerpen, Enschede, Harderwijk en Uitgeest. Contact: info@adaptatieatelier.net. [website is under construction]

Name: Te Korowai o Waiheke
Description:
Te Korowai o Waiheke is a charitable trust established by our community to eradicate predators and restore our island song together.

'Te Korowai o Waiheke’ is the culmination of many people’s vision to protect and restore the vulnerable native species (e.g. North Island kaka, grey-faced petrel, little blue penguin) that already call Waiheke home, in the first instance, by eradicating the key two predators remaining on the island, stoats and rats.
Waiheke Island represents an unparalleled opportunity for predator eradication over a diverse landscape of communities and natural environments. It will have a far-reaching profile both within NZ and internationally, as Waiheke will become the world’s first predator free urban island.

Our goal is ambitious but achievable, and will be made possible by the all-inclusive collaboration of groups and individuals on Waiheke and key project partners. The significant existing efforts of the Waiheke community to protect its native flora and fauna through many community stoat and rat control projects provides confidence that we can achieve this goal.

Predator Free Waiheke is one of five projects nationally (and the only project from Auckland) selected for funding from Predator Free 2050 Limited

Name: Matabeleland Youth Conservation Society
Description:
Matabeleland Youth Conservation Society (MYCS),  formerly Mat’land Chirpers, is a youth conservation leadership group that began through birding in September 2019 at the Centenary Park in Bulawayo. Since then our members have become an important part of the conservation community in Matabeleland. 
Our mission is to be a leading youth conservation network in Zimbabwe and on the continent, introducing young people to nature conservation, mentoring them into diverse career fields in the biodiversity economy and training them to be agents of change in their communities for the well-being of people and nature. We do this by integrating the focal themes of research, social enterprise, education and awareness and arts and culture with our conservation efforts and programs.

Name: CANOPÉE
Description: Nous conservons, créons et valorisons la forêt urbaine pour préserver la biodiversité et améliorer la qualité de vie des citoyens. Notre approche consiste en la protection, l’éducation, l’accessibilité, la restauration et le verdissement.

Name: Green Thinking Project
Description:
Agente Polinizador da Sustentabilidade | Empresa Social de Educação Ambiental | Cursos e Programas de Educação 

Ajudamos empresas e organizações a implementar estratégias rumo ao lixo zero, promovendo práticas sustentáveis, redução de resíduos e alinhamento às diretrizes ESG. Juntos, criamos impacto positivo no meio ambiente e na sociedade. Descubra como sua empresa pode liderar a trasnformação sustentável.
Location: Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul

Name: Ingwe Research Program NPC
Description:
Our Mission
To effectively conserve leopards in South Africa by empowering stakeholders through practical scientific recommendations, enabled by formal research, citizen science, novel technologies, and proactive collaboration.

Name: CHD Conservation Kenya
Description: CHD Conservation Kenya is a community-based organization that focuses on conservation through a people-centered approach. We engage indigenous communities through our awareness and education programs in Amboseli to learn from them and combine science with their rich knowledge them to conserve wildlife.

Name: Associação Mico-Leão-Dourado
Description: Associação Mico-Leão-Dourado é uma organização não-governamental criada com a missão de proteger e conservar a Mata Atlântica, com ênfase no Mico-Leão-Dourado.

Name: Galápagos Life Fund
Description: The Galapagos Life Fund (GLF) was established with the purpose of empowering and providing assistance to local environmental initiatives.

Name: Freepackers
Description:
The B Corp certified company Freepackers Travels was established in 2010 by Justine Trembicki to meet the growing demand among young people for international mobility on meaningful projects: humanitarian, environmental, and wildlife. We organize humanitarian trips for schools, sustainable summer camps for teenagers, language courses in immersion, cultural exchanges, and educational projects. These international programs aim to promote education, pedagogy, and culture. Freepackers collaborates with numerous local NGOs in 37 destinations, all personally met and carefully chosen for their ethical approach.

Since 2019, our non-profit organization, Freepackers Care, has been democratizing humanitarian actions through information sessions, workshops, conferences, financial assistance, and training. The goal of Freepackers Care is inclusive humanitarian travel accessible to all socio-professional categories, regardless of skills, resources, gender, or origin of volunteers.

Four years ago, RealStep was born in response to the increasing demand for work experiences abroad. It offers internships, certified training, and language courses abroad, focusing on projects with a sustainable and positive impact on local communities.

Today, our three structures enable us to place positive and sustainable impact at the heart of our activities, while meeting the strictest standards of social and environmental performance, public transparency, and legal responsibility.

Name: Sailowtech
Description:
Sailowtech is a scientific sailing expedition program. Its mission is to combine low-tech and frugal innovations, Open Sciences, awareness and spirit of adventure to encourage current and future generations to understand and invest in the preservation of the oceans.

DISCOVERY - PRESERVATION - TRANSMISSION 

Composed of committed young people, Sailowtech is rooted in scientific and social solidarity. It encourages participatory science through free access to its resources. It cultivates a pragmatic and rigorous approach to ecological issues by benefiting from expert advice. Finally, it includes sustainability in all its actions to propose a model of scientific expedition with minimum environmental impact.

Name: Florida Wildlife Federation
Description:
FWF is a private, statewide, non-profit 501(c)(3) conservation education organization.

Our mission is to safeguard Florida’s water, wildlife, and wild spaces by taking action today to prevent irreversible loss tomorrow.

Name: Conservation Centers for Species Survival (C2S2)
Description:
Mission:  Leadership in studying and creating self-sustaining populations ex situ and in situ of the world's most endangered animal species.

The Conservation Centers for Species Survival (C2S2) is a group of conservation centers that collectively manage more than 25,000 acres of land devoted to the survival of threatened species with special needs (including those requiring large land areas, natural group sizes and minimal public disturbance). By combining their scientific and management expertise, these centers excel in studying and creating self-sustaining populations of some of the world’s most endangered animals. Currently the six full participating facilities are the Fossil Rim Wildlife Center, Omaha's Henry Doorly Zoo and Aquarium, Smithsonian Conservation Biology Institute, the Wilds and White Oak Conservation Center.

Collectively, C2S2 has large, naturalistic areas that support significant-sized groups of animals that, in turn, allow for enhanced research in husbandry, reproductive biology, endocrinology, nutrition, genetics, behavior, health and ecology.  By focusing on species that can most benefit from its space and facilities, populations can be managed on a large scale.  Our goal is creating information, animals and populations that directly benefit that species in nature.

Name: Projet ACCLIMAT - Horizont3000
Description:
Les changements climatiques constituent aujourd’hui un phénomène global avec de graves répercussions pour de nombreux pays. Le réchauffement mondial provoque des modifications durables de notre système climatique, qui font peser une menace aux conséquences irréversibles.

Le Sénégal est très vulnérable aux impacts de la crise climatique en raison de sa situation au Sahel et de son statut d'État côtier. Des secteurs clés comme l'agriculture, la pêche et le tourisme côtier dépendent de conditions sensibles au climat. La hausse des températures, la diminution des précipitations globales et la perturbation des schémas pluviométriques traditionnels, ainsi que l'élévation du niveau de la mer, exacerbent l'érosion anthropique, la salinisation des sources d'eau, la dégradation des sols, la pénurie d'eau et le déclin de la biodiversité. Les conséquences sont déjà évidentes, avec la perte de terres arables, la réduction de la production agricole et animale, qui menacent la sécurité alimentaire et les moyens de subsistance. 

Dans le but d'aider les communautés vulnérables à améliorer leur résilience aux changements climatiques, le projet ACCLIMAT « Adaptation aux effets du Changement Climatique des communautés vulnérable au Sine Saloum, Senegal», financé par le Ministère fédéral autrichienne de la protection du climat, de l'environnement, de l'énergie, de la mobilité, de l'innovation et de la technologie et cofinancé par l’organisation catholique autrichienne le WELTHAUS Graz)  est mis en œuvre par Horizont3000 dans la région de Fatick ciblant les communes de Djilor, Mbam, Soum, Fimela, Palmarin, Djirnda, et Bassoul en étroite collaboration avec Caritas Kaolack et Carem. 

L'objectif du projet ACCLIMAT est de renforcer les capacités d'adaptation et la résilience des communautés de la zone côtière du Sine Saloum (Sénégal) par une gestion concertée et communautaire des écosystèmes, la restauration et l'utilisation durable des bases de production agricole et foresterie.

Name: African People & Wildlife
Description: African People & Wildlife (APW) works hand in hand with communities and other partners to create a healthier and more sustainable world—protecting wildlife, investing in people, and restoring balance to Africa’s vital ecosystems through effective conservation action, applied science, and collective impact. APW’s inclusive and holistic approach to conservation drives effective, measurable, and lasting outcomes for people and nature. Founded in 2005, APW is a recognized leader in the field of community-driven conservation. For more information, visit africanpeoplewildlife.org.

Name: Réserve ornithologique du Teich
Description: Située sur le Bassin d’Arcachon, la Réserve Ornithologique du Teich est un espace naturel préservé et aménagé pour accueillir les oiseaux sauvages et en faciliter l’observation.

Name: WeWild Africa
Description:
WeWild Africa provides turn-key solutions for wildlife rescue, rewilding, and landscape restoration. 

WeWild Africa was founded in 2019 by veteran conservation experts with over four decades of experience in wildlife conservation in Africa. We have worked in some of the most challenging places and situations in the world, with a never-give-up mindset.  Through partnerships with key stakeholders and committed organizations and reserves, WeWild Africa is able to make decisions quickly and provide seemingly impossible solutions. Since 2019 the organization has directly impacted over 1000 animals through rescue or rewilding.

Conservation efforts are often poorly funded and under-resourced. From Gorillas and pangolins to elephants and cheetahs, WeWild Africa engages with a diverse range of stakeholders, bridging the gap and providing essential skills, capacity, and resources to partners in conservation in need  – putting the wellbeing of animals first, always.

From urgent emergency interventions, state-owned reserves that require management support, and captive facilities that wish to reintegrate animals back into the wild safely. We actively build trusting relationships with other organizations to achieve the best results possible, because there can simply be no competition for successful conservation – only collaboration.

Rewild captive animals to support the populations of endangered species.

Emergency wildlife rescues and interventions to secure the lives of animals at risk.

Habitat management that protects critical landscapes for future rewilding.

Name: Parc national de la Vallée de la Semois
Description: Reconnu en 2022 à la suite d’un appel à projets du Gouvernement wallon, le Parc national de la Vallée de la Semois poursuit deux objectifs fondamentaux: la protection et le développement des espaces naturels et paysagers d’une part, et leur mise en valeur à travers un tourisme durable d’autre part. Pour atteindre ces objectifs, 75 actions seront menées d’ici 2026 sur le territoire du Parc national, qui s’étale sur 8 communes des provinces de Luxembourg et Namur, sur un total de près de 29.000 hectares d’un seul tenant. Ces missions, prises en charge par 9 opérateurs, sous l’égide de la fondation du Parc national de la Vallée de la Semois, se poursuivront au-delà de 2026. Un plan directeur rédigé par les porteurs du projet dessine en effet une vision ambitieuse, à un horizon de 20 ans, pour ce territoire-laboratoire.

Name: City to Ocean
Description:
City to Ocean works towards structural solutions to solve the problems of floating trash, bad water quality and lack of nature in our waterways. Therefore we organise fun trips all year round to go and fish the trash out of the water by kayak accesible for everyone like schools, families, teambuildings...

At the same time we work out solutions to really solve the problems like our Mobile Green Trashbarrier, the installation of Green Floating islands with aquatic plants and solving the problem of Sewage Overflows during rain events.

Everyone that joins us in our kayaks brings us one step closer to these structural solutions. It's a fun and educational activity while doing a good deed. One trip takes 1,5 hours, all materiel is provided and the meeting place is Akenkaai, 1000 Brussels. Simply send us a message here or through our facebook page or email to info@citytoocean.org and we organise your trip.

See you on the water!

Name: Mohonk Preserve
Description:
Mohonk Preserve protects the northern Shawangunk Ridge and inspires people to care for, enjoy and explore the natural world.

In partnership with our members, supporters, and volunteers, we protect and manage over 8,000 acres of mountain ridges, forests, fields, streams, ponds, and other unique and beautiful places. As New York's largest nonprofit nature preserve, each year we welcome over 200,000 visitors. 

The Preserve's devoted volunteers, dedicated donors, and expert staff all work in harmony for a common cause - to create a place where people and nature thrive. To accomplish our goal of protecting one of the region's most treasured and beautiful places, we conduct programs in four key areas - environmental education, conservation programs, stewardship, and visitor experience.

At the Preserve, you can enjoy scenic beauty, outdoor activities, and peace and quiet, all just 90 miles from New York City. Our carriage roads and trails are perfect for hiking, running, mountain biking, horseback riding, and cross-country skiing. We're also the home of "Gunks" climbing and bouldering, and one of the most visited climbing destinations in North America.

By visiting Mohonk Preserve, becoming a member, or making a donation, you help ensure that generations to come will be able to enjoy this special place.

Name: INNOVAA Mining Solutions
Description:
INNOVAA is a pioneering environmental technology company, dedicated to developing innovative solutions to address today's environmental challenges. Our company is committed to reconciling technological advances with environmental preservation.
Our areas of expertise cover a wide range of environmental technologies, including waste management, renewable energy, air and water quality monitoring, biodiversity conservation, and many others. We believe technology can play a crucial role in building a sustainable future.
Products and services :
Intelligent waste management: Development of automated collection, sorting and recycling systems to reduce the carbon footprint and promote a circular economy.
Renewable energy: Design and implementation of sustainable energy solutions, such as solar panels, wind turbines and energy storage technologies.
Environmental monitoring: Development of sensors and monitoring platforms to assess air, water and soil quality in real time, enabling proactive management of natural resources.
Biodiversity Conservation Technologies: Using artificial intelligence and data collection to monitor and protect fragile ecosystems, thereby promoting biodiversity conservation.
Social commitment and responsibility:
INNOVAA is committed to having a positive impact on society and the environment. We actively collaborate with non-governmental organizations, academic institutions and governments to promote sustainable practices and raise awareness of the importance of environmental preservation.

Name: Oceans Initiative
Description: Oceans Initiative is a nonprofit team of scientists on a mission to protect marine wildlife in the Pacific Northwest and beyond. Like the whales we study, we are highly migratory. We divide our time between getting our feet wet in the field, wherever our work is needed, and using our science to inform smart decisions to conserve wildlife.

Name: All Hands Ecology
Description:
All Hands Ecology, formally Audubon Canyon Ranch, is a 501(c)(3) not-for-profit environmental conservation and education organization. It was founded in 1962 to safeguard Bolinas Lagoon from irresponsible development, leading the way for the protection of Tomales Bay.

Today, All Hands Ecology acts as guardian of a system of nature preserves spanning 26 properties in Marin and Sonoma counties. These 5,000 acres provide significant habitat to wildlife and have been placed in our care by generous donors to steward and protect in perpetuity. All Hands Ecology works to improve global environmental health by conserving and stewarding valuable natural resources, managing for ecological resiliency, providing scientific solutions to ecosystem management, and teaching generations of conservationists in ways that strengthen natural and human communities.

All Hands Ecology's programs are made possible thanks to the contribution of thousands of hours of volunteer service, and donations from caring individuals, foundations and businesses. All Hands Ecology is an independent non-profit organization with 501(c)3 status. Our tax ID number is 94-6069140.

Name: Nature-Based Solutions Foundation
Description:
Launched by some of BC’s most experienced conservationists, the Nature-Based Solutions Foundation is dedicated to protecting the most endangered native ecosystems in Canada. Our primary goal is to achieve legislated protection of these ecosystems by addressing key conservation funding gaps with a core focus on helping finance sustainable economic alternatives for communities. To this end, we leverage and strategically direct private funds to unlock paths to establishing new protected areas.

Our pillars:

- Protecting native ecosystems: We use science and the best available research to identify the most endangered ecosystems for protection.

- Fostering collaboration: We look for strategic opportunities to support regional conservation partners, communities, land trusts, and First Nations governments and other governments to establish new protected areas.

- Supporting communities: We provide land-embedded communities with funding to help them develop sustainable economic alternatives and build capacity for new protected areas.

Canadian Registered Charity Number: 743624538 RR 0001

Name: ReGeCom
Description: ReGeCom aims to facilitate and promote the implementation of community based natural resources management initiatives, as a national agenda, based on standardized principles of inclusion and community participation, maximizing the benefits for the community, privet sector, Government and civil society institutions.

Name: Blue Economy Organisation (BEO)
Description: Blue Economy Organisation (BEO) is a Non-Governmental Organisation based in the United Republic of Tanzania with dedicated professionals who came together to promote the sustainable use of blue resources for improving coastal community livelihoods through innovative conservation and restoration practices of marine ecosystem health.

Name: NaturAfrica West Africa
Description:
NaturAfrica regional programme in West Africa supports the effective conservation and management of biodiversity in key forest, savannah and desert ecosystems in the region. 

It works with partner country governments and authorities and implementing agencies to improve governance of protected and other conserved areas through capacity development and the use of management tools, and involving local communities and other stakeholders in decision-making; and to develop green economy initiatives that provide inclusive and sustainable jobs for local people. 

Funded by the European Union and managed by the EU and the West Africa Economic and Monetary Union (WAEMU/UEMOA) acting on behalf of national governments, it operates in 11 transboundary landscapes in West Africa, and adopts a people-centred approach based on a respect for human rights. 

NaturAfrica is a major EU initiative comprising numerous national and regional programmes in the whole of sub-Saharan Africa, and is one flagship of the European Union's Global Gateway initiative. 

Coordination of the West Africa regional programme is implemented by the AGRECO - Dorsch Impact consortium.

Name: Koala Conservation Australia
Description:
Koala Conservation Australia (Port Macquarie Koala Hospital and Wild Koala Breeding Program and Visitor Centre) has been helping rescue and rehabilitate koalas since 1973. We are a not-for-profit organisation that includes approximately 200 volunteers dedicated to rescuing and rehabilitating koalas and conserving and restoring their natural habitats.

In 2022, the Koala was officially listed as an endangered species. 

Unfortunately, this listing was completely preventable. Recent studies indicate the koala will become extinct in New South Wales before 2050 – unless rapid and effective action is taken. Our Koala population face several threats including loss of habitat, disease, and injury and attack.

The world-first Wild Koala Breeding Program was launched in 2024, at Guulabaa - Place of Koala with the aim to arrest declining koala populations and ensure the survival of the species for generations to come.

Name: ChemiNova
Description:
ChemiNova aims to develop an intelligent computational system that goes beyond current technologies to improve the conservation, analysis and monitoring of European Cultural Heritage. Using a myriad of data, we tackle structural and chemical damages, focusing on climate change and conflicts. Our value relies on the conviction that we facilitate conservators’ work to the extent that by using a single framework they can document, digitize, classify, and share information for heritage conservation. Our impact lies in the fact that we are not building an ad hoc device, but a technology that is adaptable so that anyone can access it from anywhere. We involve local communities (citizens) in conservation practices, from providing data (citizen science) to raising awareness on the effects of climate change, natural and human hazards affecting cultural heritage.

ChemiNova has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon Europe Framework Programme under grant agreement 101132442.

Subscribe to our newsletter: https://cheminova.eu/newsletters/ and Youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/@ChemiNova_eu

Name: Latin American Association of Zoological Parks and Aquariums
Description: The Latin American Association of Zoological Parks and Aquariums is a community that passionately promotes the love, care and well-being of animals. We seek excellence in our members through comprehensive development and talent, to achieve a positive and significant impact on the conservation of biodiversity.

Name: IUCN Indigenous Peoples
Description: The PODONG Indigenous Peoples Initiative is a global initiative led by the IUCN, the International Indigenous Forum on Biodiversity (IIFB), and IUCN Indigenous Peoples' Organisations (IPO) Members  that recognizes and supports Indigenous Peoples’ contributions to the conservation of biodiversity and climate solutions through their indigenous knowledge systems, while ensuring their full and effective leadership and access to adequate resources for the implementation and achievement of the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework and the Paris Agreement.

Name: Cornish Seal Sanctuary
Description:
Based in Gweek, commanding panoramic views of the Helford River, the sanctuary is dedicated to seal rescue, rehabilitation and release, as well as raising awareness of how plastic pollution has affected our marine life in Cornwall and also provides a forever home to animals needing permanent specialist care from our team of  experts.
 
Meet the rescued seal pups going through rehabilitation during pup season in the winter, before being returned to the wild. See and hear about the resident animals and learn about their stories. Explore woodland walks with your four- legged friends, and get a bite at the café.

Name: Wetlands Conservation Organisation
Description: A Kenyan Youth-led Initiative tackling climate change crisis and biodiversity loss through conservation and restoration of wetlands.

Name: The Lake Victoria Cleanup
Description: Lake Victoria, the second largest fresh water lake in the world (by area), supports over 40 million inhabitants in Uganda, Kenya and Tanzania. However, the lake is in danger. Threatened by plastic and water pollution, climate change, overfishing, habitat destruction and biodiversity loss, scientists are warning that the lake is dying. The Lake Victoria Clean Up Project was brought to life by Mutumba Faisal from the Uganda Junior Rangers. The Lake Victoria Clean Up Project will support the local communities in Uganda by working with fishers to remove ghost gear and other plastic pollution and ensure further pollution is prevented. To close the loop, the nets and plastic will be recycled and upcycled into products that benefit the community.

Name: Sky Island Alliance
Description:
Our mission is to protect and restore the diversity of life and lands in the Sky Island region of the U.S. and Mexico. We’re working to ensure the Sky Islands are a place where nature thrives, open space and clean water are available to all, and people are connected to the region and its innate ability to enrich our lives.

We work with volunteers, scientists, land owners, public officials, and government agencies to establish protected areas, restore healthy landscapes, and promote public appreciation of the region's unique biological diversity.

Name: Redário
Description: O Redário é uma articulação entre redes e grupos de coletores de sementes nativas que visa potencializar os impactos socioambientais positivos e estruturar a base da cadeia de restauração em larga escala, com comércio justo, ampla base genética e rastreabilidade.

Name: Routes Vivantes
Description:
Protéger la faune sauvage, repenser notre rapport à la route 

Routes Vivantes est dédié à la préservation de la faune sauvage en limitant les collisions routières.

Notre mission : agir pour une meilleure cohabitation entre les infrastructures humaines et le vivant, à travers des solutions concrètes, durables et collectives.

Que vous soyez automobiliste, élu, aménageur, citoyen... vous pouvez agir.
Ensemble, faisons évoluer les mentalités et les infrastructures pour que les routes cessent d’être des pièges mortels pour la faune sauvage.

Rejoignez-nous. Chaque action compte pour préserver le vivant.

#Biodiversité #SécuritéRoutière #FauneSauvage #CorridorsÉcologiques #TrameVerte #ProtectionDuVivant

Name: Women for the Environment - Africa
Description: Our vision is that WE Africa sparks a movement of women leaders in Africa and beyond, who are role modelling a more effective and impactful way to do conservation. By delivering transformational and deeply African leadership experiences, amplified by a strong pay it forward approach and supported by a connected community, WE Africa is making tangible contributions to the sustainability and wellbeing of leaders and ecosystems. WE Africa is a beacon of hope for the future, locally and globally.

Name: Angaza Vijiji
Description:
Angaza Vijiji is a Kenyan social enterprise focused on sustainable development in undeserved and marginalized rural communities particularly through ecotourism, conservation, sustainable energy, and agricultural production.

Key Focus Areas:
a) Community Empowerment:
We empower local communities through various initiatives, including trainings, outreach programs etc 
b) Wildlife Conservation:
We are involved in wildlife conservation efforts and research, including projects focused on endangered and Near Threatened species - Endangered Hildegarde's Tomb Bat and Straw Colored Fruit Bats respectively.
c) Ecotourism: 
Angaza Vijiji promotes sustainable travel experiences that benefit local communities and the environment.
d) Sustainable Energy:
Angaza Vijiji invests in renewable energy practices to enhance livelihoods.
e) Agriculture:
Angaza Vijiji supports smart agricultural production for food security.

Name: AFC
Description:
Construire ensemble de bonnes pratiques de cueillette de plantes sauvages pour préserver nos ressources.
Fédérer les cueilleuses et cueilleurs profesionnel.les et les représenter auprès des différents acteurs.

Name: Vermont Land Trust
Description:
Rooted in Vermont since1977, we have protected more than 620,000 acres, or 11% of the private land in Vermont. These farms, forests, and community spacesshapeour identity and supportour wellbeing.Theyprovide us with healthy food and clean water, habitat for plantsand wildlife,economic opportunity, protection from a changing climate, and natural areas that inspire and delight. 

With expertise in conservation, ecology, community engagement, and finance, our team helps to: 
- Conserve land with important economic and environmental benefits by limiting development and ensuring sound land stewardship practices
- Support farmers in buying land and growing sustainable businesses 
- Restore wetlands, improveforestand soil health, and share that knowledge with all who care for land 
- Ensure that permanent legal protectionson 2,200 parcels are upheld, in partnership with landowners
- Connect all people to landthrougheducation and events

Name: NAECO
Description: Naeco est une association dédiée à l'éveil des consciences et à la préservation de la biodiversité marine à travers une approche qui unit science, art et engagement. Fondée sur la conviction que l'émerveillement est le moteur du changement, Naeco crée une synergie entre scientifiques et artistes pour explorer, documenter et partager la splendeur des océans. Cette initiative s'efforce de sensibiliser le public aux enjeux environnementaux marins, tout en cultivant une connexion émotionnelle avec la nature, et marque le début d'une histoire engagée en faveur de la conservation de notre "grande bleue".

Name: Emirates Centre for the Conservation of Houbara (ECCH)
Description:
Located on the silk road, between Samarkand and Bukhara in Uzbekistan, ECCH is a conservation Centre engaged in preservation of an endangered iconic specie: the Asian Houbara bustard (Chlamydotis macqueenii). To respond to this mission, ECCH combines a captive breeding management, with an annual release campaign achievement at the heart of the natural bustards reproduction area. Meanwhile, follow-up actions and habitat researches are conducted to improve this reinforcement program.

In 2007, ECCH’s story begins with a first collection of wild eggs realised around Navoiy region. Rapidly, the first chick produced on Centre is born in 2008. Four years later, in 2012, a release campaign is achieved in Uzbekistan, since that year more than 8,500 bustards have been produced at ECCH. All of those birds reinforce Houbara bustards wild populations, and simultaneously their sustainability.

Every year, more than 100 employees and until 200 during breeding season are involved in Houbara bustards preservation at ECCH. By joining us, you will take part in a flourishing project which will give you the opportunity to assume your responsibilities into a dynamic team. In meeting this meaningful challenge, your action will strongly impact the social and ecological developments of the region.

Name: ReHabitat
Description:
ReHabitat creates design solutions to protect and restore biodiversity.

Wildlife + habitat = healthy ecosystem.

Name: OneCanopy
Description: OneCanopy is a community-oriented reforestation company based in Loveland, Colorado. We are focused on growing native trees and shrubs for conservation efforts in the Greater Mountain West Region with plans to fully integrate across the reforestation pipeline and scale globally. As a social enterprise with triple-bottom line objectives, we strive for innovative and nimble solutions which drive lasting impact for our partners, community, and the planet. We envision a world in which a human’s relationship with their environment is symbiotic and supportive, from seed to canopy.

Name: The Riverwood Conservancy
Description: The Riverwood Conservancy is a volunteer-driven and member-based charity that protects Riverwood, a 150-acre oasis of woodlands, ravines, gardens, creeks, ponds, meadows, and trails in the heart of Mississauga, Ontario. With abundant wildlife that includes deer, beavers, mink, and 200 bird species, Riverwood is recognized as one of the most diverse ecosystems in the Credit River watershed. The Riverwood Conservancy's conservation, education, gardening, and volunteer programs help keep nature alive in the city.

Name: Wildlands, Woodlands, Farmlands & Communities
Description:
Wildlands, Woodlands, Farmlands & Communities (WWF&C) is a collaborative conservation initiative of partner organizations that share a collective vision for the future of New England and the six states it comprises. This vision unites people, groups, and communities around an integrated approach to conservation — one that recognizes the inextricable connections between land, water, and air, and all living beings, human and wild (non-human). 

The varied landscapes of this region provide countless ecological, economic, and health benefits while helping to stem the crises of climate change, biodiversity loss, and related threats to public health and safety. WWF&C partners strive to thoughtfully model collaborative conservation practices that include a diverse range of voices and knowledge, all working in concert toward a healthier, more just world.

WWF&C offers research and resources for those who are working to advance land conservation within each state, and across state lines, and outlines specific goals to protect 80% of the region as forests, both wildlands and woodlands, and farmland (by 2060), helping New England do its part to ensure the future of all life on Earth. The collective work of WWF&C partners centers on land and water conservation in recognition of nature as the source of all resources for life and environmental resilience, helping to ensure that nature’s benefits become and remain available to all people and communities.

Name: Marine Conservation Society Seychelles
Description: The Marine Conservation Society Seychelles (MCSS) is a Non-Governmental Organisation registered in Seychelles which promotes the conservation of the marine environment through education, research and the implementation of a number of programmes. Current activities include long running monitoring programmes on turtles and terrapins, coral restoration as well as several grant funded programmes and projects.

Name: NFTree
Description:
NFTree se posiciona como el facilitador tecnológico líder en financiar la conservación de ecosistemas de alto valor ecológico y mitigar el cambio climático con impacto social tangible. Nuestra misión es actuar como puente para la financiación, ofreciendo escalabilidad, trazabilidad y transparencia, maximizando los recursos para los beneficiarios para garantizar un efecto positivo y sostenible en el medio ambiente y la sociedad.

Conocemos los desafíos empresariales y el valor de nuestra naturaleza, por lo que desarrollamos soluciones innovadoras e integrales, capaces de transformar todo lo que hacemos como empresas e individuos, en una huella positiva para el Planeta. NFTree combina tecnologías avanzadas para recolectar información forestal, medir carbono capturado y evaluar la biodiversidad, haciendo esta información accesible y verificable en nuestra plataforma blockchain.

Nuestras iniciativas respetan la gobernanza local y promueven la distribución equitativa de beneficios a través de DAOs, que toman decisiones clave sobre la conservación. Cada NFTree representa un compromiso tangible con la conservación, conteniendo datos únicos de las hectáreas protegidas, biodiversidad, carbono capturado y beneficios comunitarios.

Únete a NFTree, una solución integral que canaliza recursos hacia el bien común, garantizando que los procesos cumplan con altos estándares y beneficien tanto a los bosques como a sus habitantes. Con cada compra de NFTree, contribuyes directamente a la conservación del medio ambiente y al bienestar de comunidades, respaldado por la seguridad y transparencia de la tecnología blockchain. Sé parte de una sostenibilidad innovadora y construye un presente con futuro.

Name: Meewasin Valley Authority
Description: Meewasin is a non-profit organization dedicated to conserving the cultural and natural resources of the South Saskatchewan River Valley. It serves as the means by which the three participating parties—the City of Saskatoon, the Government of Saskatchewan, and the University of Saskatchewan—manage the Meewasin Valley in the South Saskatchewan River Basin. Meewasin operates on the principle that by working together through a single agency, the partners can achieve more than they could individually.

Name: Conservest
Description: Conservest partners with industry leaders, environmentalists, businesses and financiers to elevate Wildlife & Hospitality Ventures, assisting them to achieve their full potential and lasting impact.

Name: FutureWild
Description: FutureWild partners with industry leaders, environmentalists, businesses and financiers to elevate Wildlife Hospitality Ventures and Future Earth Projects, assisting them to achieve their full potential and lasting impact.

Name: Friends of the Columbia Gorge
Description: Keeping the Columbia Gorge wild and beautiful is the core of what we do. Friends of the Columbia Gorge is the only conservation organization dedicated solely to protecting and preserving the Columbia Gorge National Scenic Area. For almost 40 years we have used a combination of old-fashioned grassroots advocacy, strategic land purchases, and cutting-edge public education tools to protect the scenic, natural, cultural, and recreational resources of the Gorge. Learn more: gorgefriends.org

Name: AQUATIC ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT ASSOCIATION(AQUAMEN)
Conservation Ecology Centre
Description:
The Conservation Ecology Centre is a nationally registered charity and non-profit ecological research and conservation organisation dedicated to a healthy and resilient Otways landscape. 

Generating knowledge to nurture Country, Conservation Ecology Centre partners with traditional owners, research institutions, NGOs, government agencies and community to better understand and protect threatened species and ecosystems by conducting vital research and restoring habitat.

Our unifying, collaborative approach has 3 essential functions:
•	Facilitating vital research to increase understanding of the Otways landscape.
•	Developing and delivering solutions to the most urgent conservation challenges facing the region.
•	Providing meaningful experiences for people to engage with nature, thereby building awareness and appreciation of wildlife and wild places.

Being based in the beautiful Otway Ranges (on the Great Ocean Road) provides a unique opportunity to facilitate research, and deliver programs, in one of Australia’s truly wild places.

Current projects include: studies of the effects of fire on Long-nosed Potoroos and other small native mammals; koala woodland habitat restoration; and the tracking and removal of introduced pest species, such as foxes, cats, pigs and deer, from the landscape. 

All projects are chosen for their potential to have a broad application and to benefit wildlife and environments locally, across Australian landscapes, and around the world.

Our mission is to develop, deliver and share solutions for effective conservation across the Otways and beyond. Our vision is a healthy and resilient Otways cared for forever.

Name: Fondation Espaces naturels de France
Description: La Fondation Espaces naturels de France oeuvre pour la préservation pérenne de la biodiversité. Pour ce faire elle entends constituer un réseau d'espaces naturels protégés bénéficiant d’une gestion écologique tout respectant et intégrant les activités humaines; sensibiliser les citoyens et acteurs économiques aux enjeux de la protection de la nature, et enfin, mobiliser la générosité de tous pour agir et soutenir la nature dans les territoires.

Name: Keystones Expedition
Description: Keystones Expedition follows conservationist Sam Chevallier's adventure across southern and eastern Africa. He discovers the urgency for protecting keystone species, exploring the intersection of science, ecology and spirituality, while fostering deeper understanding and inspiring reconnection with Mother Nature.

Name: Australian Seed Bank Partnership
Description:
The Australian Seed Bank Partnership is a not-for-profit alliance of 14 organisations, bringing together expertise from Australia’s leading botanic gardens, state environment agencies and NGOs.

 Governed by The Council of Heads of Australian Botanic Gardens Inc., we are working on strategic solutions to deal with the multitude of threats facing our biodiversity.

We aim to deliver a national effort that contributes to the conservation of Australia’s native plant diversity through collaborative and sustainable seed collecting, banking, research and knowledge sharing

Name: The Conservation Social Science Partnership
Description: The Conservation Social Science Partnership (ConSoSci) is a growing community of the world's leading conservation NGOs, social science practitioners, and researchers who recognize that the social sciences are as essential as the ecological sciences to making informed decisions on the best conservation actions that benefit both nature and people. We seek to address critical gaps in social science capacity, implementation, and accessibility in conservation.

Name: Kenya Forest Service
Description: Kenya Forest Service is a state corporation that was created through the Forest Act, 2005 (now repealed). The Service derives her mandate from the Forest Conservation and Management Act (FCMA), 2016.

Name: AmphiRept - Société Herpétologique de Touraine
Description: Étude, protection et conservation des Amphibiens et Reptiles ainsi que des milieux dans lesquels ils vivent

Name: Association T EAU T O PHIL
Description:
Agir ici avec sens et responsabilité pour là-bas, car l'Océan n'en peut déjà plus.
Ici : mon ilot de quartier et mon quotidien.
Là-bas : l'Océan, la Terre et le reste du monde.
Dans cet objectif de préserver l'océan au quotidien dès aujourd'hui, l'association recherche à construire une démarche à partager autour de la gestion de l'eau de pluie et des déchets urbains abandonnés dans nos villes pour protéger l'Océan. C'est un projet à porter dans le monde actuel avec les enfants, mais pour demain et leur avenir. Depuis la création de l'association T EAU T O PHIL,  les actions se partagent donc entre des recherches de distinctions d'adultes (VUPO, DUMP, ICI DUMP, STOP DUMP) et l'adhésion d'enfants à notre projet d'éphémère durable - 149 kg" au sein d'établissements scolaires : construire ensemble une oeuvre artistique éducative à vocation scientifique pour protéger l'océan sur le chemin de l'école.
Le projet est ambitieux, puisqu'il vise à changer notre regard et nos actions sur le Déchet Urbain abandonné (DUMP : Déchet Urbain Migrant et Polluant).

Name: Société Herpétologique de Touraine
Description: Étude, protection et conservation des Amphibiens et Reptiles ainsi que des milieux dans lesquels ils vivent

Name: Bancos de Hábitat - Cupos de Biodiversidad
Description:
Banco de Hábitat en Colombia: Un Refugio para la Biodiversidad. 
El Banco de Hábitat no solo actúa como un santuario para especies en peligro, sino que también ofrece una solución innovadora para compensar los impactos ambientales generados por actividades humanas. A través de la venta de créditos de biodiversidad, las empresas pueden cumplir con sus obligaciones ambientales y contribuir de manera tangible a la protección del medio ambiente.
Cada crédito de biodiversidad representa una hectárea de ecosistema restaurado y manejado, garantizando su protección y mantenimiento por al menos 15 años. Este enfoque no solo asegura la preservación de la biodiversidad, sino que también promueve el desarrollo sostenible y el crecimiento económico en la región

Name: Moonjelly Foundation
Description:
Moonjelly is a dynamic community of Indigenous ocean scientists and organizations dedicated to healing our oceans. We serve as a hub for philanthropic support, channeling funds directly to our frontline leaders in Indigenous communities.

Our US-based non-profit arm provides our members with essential fiscal and technical resources from the Global North, helping them to advance their vital work in their own communities.

Currently, we collaborate with scientists, leaders, and partners in The Cook Islands, French Polynesia, Hawaii, Taiwan, and Colombia. Our funding partners include Schmidt Ocean Coalition, Patchwork Collective, EarthSense Foundation, and Future Earth.

Name: GreenLab Initiative i3S
Description: With the aim of promoting a more environmentally sustainable institute, the GreenLab Initiative has been promoting and boosting a variety of initiatives aimed at reducing laboratory waste (chemical and plastic), individual waste, and energy consumption.

Name: Grupo Ituxi
Description: Ituxi está há mais de uma década no mercado oferecendo créditos de carbono de alta qualidade e confiança. Temos mais de um milhão de hectares em projetos, todos localizados no coração da Floresta Amazônica. Temos o compromisso de ajudar o planeta a reduzir as emissões de gases de efeito estuda e destruir o desmatamento dos biomas brasileiros.

Name: 90 North Foundation
Description:
The 90 North Foundation is a conservation charity focused on leading and accelerating protection of Arctic Ocean biodiversity.

To find out more about our mission, visit our website via the link in bio.

Name: Nahe der Natur - Mitmach-Museum für Naturschutz
Description:
'Nahe der Natur', das ist die einzigartige Kombination von Naturschutz und Museum, höchst lebendig: Forschen, Sammeln, Ausstellen, Bilden und Vermitteln -  Dazu haben wir als Naturschutzbetrieb eigene Flächen für Wildnis, zudem Naturgarten (besonders: Moosgarten, Schmetterlingsgarten). Sitz des Museums mit Café ist in Staudernheim, mit Flächen und Angeboten sind wir aber auch überregional aktiv: Friedlicher, kreativer und moderner Naturschutz auf Faktenbasis für Alle: Weltoffen in Vielfalt. 

Einstieg und Übersicht: www.nahe-natur.com - Themenseiten: ww.freinatur.net

Von hier sendet weltweit frei und rund um die Uhr das Web-Radio-Vollprogramm NATURADIO - 'Radio Natur verbunden'. - www.naturadio.net

Name: Savannah Tracking Ltd.
Description:
Founded in Kenya, Savannah Tracking Ltd. is at the forefront of revolutionizing wildlife research and conservation initiatives globally with our innovative tracking and identification systems.  
                                          
Our aim is to provide affordable and customized solutions that empower researchers and conservationists to make informed and impactful decisions to protect endangered species, particularly for mammals and birds.

Our advanced technology includes GPS Iridium-linked collars and LoRa devices, ensuring real-time and reliable tracking data.

We work closely with leading animal researchers, institutions, and NGOs to enhance our impact and reach. These collaborations have enabled us to expand from Kenya into Saudi Arabia, Canada and South Africa regions.

Connect with us to stay updated on our latest projects, technological advancements, and inspiring stories of how our technology is reducing human-wildlife conflicts and transforming wildlife conservation.

Name: Madagascar Whale Shark Project
Description:
The Madagascar Whale Shark Project (MWSP) is a collaborative research and conservation project that was created in 2016, following unexpected high numbers of whale sharks recorded in 2015, thanks to opportunistic data collection by a partner tourism operator, les Baleines Rand'eau. In 2019 we became a registered foundation focusing on three key aspects.

RESEARCH: Our main scientific objective is to establish the occurrence, residency and population structure of whale sharks off Nosy Be through photo-identification. We also are collecting skin samples, investigating prey distribution and have previously satellite-tagged whale sharks.

CONSERVATION: We have implemented a code of conduct since 2017 and are collecting data on the socio-economic value of whale sharks. We also actively support the development of effective legal protection for whale sharks, as well as local management initiatives.

EDUCATION: Community engagement is a key tenet of our
work, and we currently work in various schools in Nosy Be through our education programme. We also lead workshops on sustainable whale shark interactions while in the field.

Name: Apidae Association
Description:
Soutenez notre mission associative en parrainant une ruche sur nos ruchers de campagne ou en installant un rucher sur votre site d’entreprise.

Dégustez et offrez votre miel BIO labellisé au nom de votre entreprise. 

Réservez des ateliers de sensibilisation à la biodiversité, de récolte de miel, visites de ruches, création de bougies, baumes à lèvres et d’autres produits dont la matière première émane de nos ruches BIO. 

Vous êtes sensible à la biodiversité et souhaitez soutenir nos actions par une donation (déductible impôts), du bénévolat.

Rejoindre notre petite équipe pour une mission PPE+, un stage de fin d’études ou d’apprentissage.

Visitez notre site internet ou contactez-nous directement.

Name: The Association for Coastal Ecosystem Services
Description:
The Association for Coastal Ecosystem Services (ACES) is a Scottish registered charity set up with the aim of conserving coastal habitats in tropical regions whilst working towards alleviating poverty around such habitats. The mechanism for achieving this is through the use of payments for ecosystem services, primarily carbon based credit schemes.

It’s first pioneering project, a community based payment-for-ecosystem services conservation project called Mikoko Pamoja was set up by the communities of Gazi and Makongeni to conserve their nearby mangrove forests.

In doing so, the carbon that has be stored as a result of these forests not being cut down, has meant that the communities are able to benefit from the sale of carbon credits, issued by the Plan Vivo Foundation, a third party, independent non-profit organization also based in Scotland.

The money earned from these credits to date, has funded communal community development projects such as supplying new roofing materials for Gazi primary school and by paying for new books for the school of Macongeni Village.

Through research and education, The Association for Coastal Ecosystem Services has assisted in building capacity for the project, in order to ensure its long-term persistence. Together with numerous research institutions and other non-profit organizations, ACES hopes Mikoko Pamoja can act as a model for a successful payments-for-ecosystems services project in East Africa, the first of its kind with a focus of mangrove conservation and poverty alleviation.

We hope to expand our efforts in the future, with partners and private individuals who wish to contribute, making a difference for the people living in these volatile regions of the world, and the habitats that surround them.

Name: CORENA - Réserve Naturelle Communautaire du Boundou
Description:
La Réserve Naturelle Communautaire du Boundou (RNCB) est une aire protégée du sud-est du Sénégal créée en 2009 par les communautés rurales du territoire avec l’appui du Conseil Départemental de l’Isère et l’ancien Conseil Régional de Tambacounda. A cheval sur les quatre communes de Dougué, Sinthiou Fissa, Koussan et Toumboura, la RNCB est gérée par les communautés du territoire par le biais du Conservatoire de la Réserve Naturelle Communautaire du Boundou (CORENA) dirigé par les maires de ces quatre communes.

La RNCB est riche d’une grande biodiversité et de ressources naturelles que les habitants du Boundou souhaitent protéger. Le projet de la RNCB est d’allier développement économique, pour bénéficier aux populations encore très pauvres, et préservation des ressources naturelles.
Pour les populations, il s’agit donc de désenclaver leurs villages, améliorer l’accès à l’eau, aux intrants agricoles et aux financements, organiser les activités économiques communautaires et établir des règles de gestion.

Name: Sahel Eco
Description:
Sahel Eco est une association apolitique, non confessionnelle et à but non lucratif, fondé en 2004. Sahel Eco a signé avec le gouvernement du Mali un Accord cadre sous le numéro 1977 du 8 Août 2004/renouvelé sous le numéro 0440/001977 du 15 Avril 2008.
Elle s’est fixé deux objectifs stratégiques principaux à savoir : 
- Promouvoir la gestion durable des ressources naturelles renouvelables,
- Appuyer l’émergence d’une économie rurale plus diversifiée et sécurisée
DOMAINE D’INTERVENTION
L’agroforesterie, agriculture durable et sécurité alimentaire,
La résilience et l’adaptation au changement climatique,
La bonne gouvernance et le plaidoyer autour de la gestion durable des ressources naturelles,
Le renforcement des capacités des organisations communautaires de base,
Diversification de l’économie rurale et développement 
d’entreprises forestières et agricoles,
Hydraulique villageoise et Gestion Intégrée des Ressources en Eau (GIRE),
Eau, Hygiène, Assainissement, Nutrition,
Développement durable,
Développement et facilitation de systèmes innovant de communication et d’information et d’accès au marché,
Microcrédit et épargne,
Energies Renouvelables
ZONE D’INTERVENTION
Tout le territoire national avec une concentration des interventions actuellement dans les régions de Ségou, Mopti et la région de Kayes (Sahel Occidental).

Name: Campus Naturaleza UdeC
Description:
Es un proyecto de la Universidad de Concepción, enfocado en la conservación y restauración de la diversidad biológica que subsiste en los últimos ecosistemas nativos terrestres y acuáticos, adyacentes a su Campus Patrimonial.


La iniciativa será una ventana para oportunidades de investigación e instancias que promuevan la vinculación social, y los beneficios resultantes del contacto permanente de las personas con el entorno natural. Junto con ello, se incluirá un espacio de restauración ecológica de áreas degradadas, conservación in situ de los remanentes de bosque nativo, así como de conservación ex – situ de especies chilenas amenazadas.

Name: World Wetlands and Oceans Protection Rescue Association (W2OPRA)
Description: La W2OPRA est une association Camerounaise à but non lucratif fondée en 2022 par un groupe d’ingénieur Halieute dont le leader est Jacques EDMOND K. KOUNCHE jeune Ingénieur de Conception en Gestion Environnemental spécialité Gestion des Écosystèmes Aquatiques, Terrestres et des Pêches. Entièrement dévouée à la protection et la préservation des zones humides et des océans et à ceux qui en vivent

Name: YouSea Institute - Zavod YouSea
Description: The non-profit YouSea Institute is devoted to safeguarding marine biodiversity and advancing sustainable aquaculture. It operates in research, promotion, and education on marine conservation and sustainable food production in the sea, focusing on pollution reduction, invasive species monitoring, and fostering a thriving marine environment. Holding a water permit for marine organism cultivation, YouSea is innovating in multitrophic mariculture. The intention is also to educate and inform both professional and general public about these topics.

Name: World Sustainability Foundation
Description:
The World Sustainability Foundation (WSF) is a non-profit arm of the World Sustainability Organization (WSO) that aims to protect and improve the environment by promoting a culture of sustainable development.
Thanks to the assistance of experts, it provides outreach materials and support to companies with regard to sustainable production and new technologies.
The Foundation is already involved in tens of conservation projects worldwide, including those which it has established in Italy, Madagascar, Galapagos, Peru, Mongolia, and Vietnam. It does so through a collaborative network of states, institutions, research centers, universities, and businesses certified by Friend of the Sea or Friend of the Earth.

Name: Bos Dat Van Zichzelf Is
