Description: Wij, jongereninitiatief stichting 'Bos dat van Zichzelf is', zouden graag een mooie en hoopvolle toekomst zien voor de Nederlandse bossen. Daarom zetten we in op het teruggeven van de natuur aan zichzelf via de moderne Akte van Redemptie constructie, de eerste juridische constructie in Nederland die werkt om rechten te geven aan de natuur. Hierbij zijn wij ervan overtuigd dat een mentaliteitsverandering dé manier is om onze kostbare biodiversiteit te beschermen. Daarom doen we ook veel aan educatie. Onze kernwaarde is dat wij de natuur beschouwen als een intrinsieke entiteit, met eigen rechten, en haar middels deze visie haar autonomie willen teruggeven.  Help ons daarom bos zowel  juridisch als ecologisch weer op eigen benen helpen staan.

Name: Conservatoire d'espaces naturels de l'Allier - CEN Allier
Description:
Association agréée, membre du réseau national des CEN
Connaissance, préservation, gestion et valorisation du patrimoine naturel bourbonnais, sur le département de l'Allier et territoires limitrophes
Actions concrètes de préservation de la biodiversité, en concertation avec les acteurs du territoire

Name: Projet LIFE Adapto+
Description:
Face aux impacts croissants du changement climatique, le projet LIFE Adapto+ met en œuvre des Solutions fondées sur la Nature afin de promouvoir une gestion souple et durable du littoral. D’ici à 2029, cette approche bénéficiera aux populations vivant à proximité de 25 nouvelles zones côtières françaises, particulièrement vulnérables ou exposées aux effets du changement climatique.


Les acteurs identifiés comme parties prenantes dans sa mise en œuvre sont :

Coordinateurs du projet :
•	Le Conservatoire du littoral

Partenaires nationaux :
•	Le Bureau de Recherches Géologiques et Minières (BRGM),
•	Le Centre d'études et d'expertise sur les risques, l'environnement, la mobilité et l'aménagement (Cerema),
•	L’Institut national de recherche pour l’agriculture, l’alimentation et l’environnement (INRAe),
•	Réserves Naturelles de France (RNF),
•	La Fondation Tour du Valat,
•	Le Syndicat Mixte Espaces Littoraux de la Manche (SyMEL),
•	L’Office National des Forêts (ONF),
•	L’Office Français de la Biodiversité (OFB),
•	La Communauté de communes de l’île de Noirmoutier.

Financeurs :
•	Life
•	Ministère de la Transition Ecologique
•	Banque des Territoires

Name: AWE for Nature Foundation
Description:
The AWE for Nature Foundation is building a progressive conservation model for vast expanses of African wilderness. With our boots on the ground and a network of changemakers, we are aiming to transform how African conservation is funded.

AWE for Nature is a Geneva-based foundation. Our mission is to conduct on the ground operations while developing a sustainable, scalable model for long-term, financial secure conservation, beginning with our pilot project - 200,000 hectares of wilderness in Zimbabwe’s Zambezi Valley, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

There is a pressing need to address the funding gap for biodiversity. This issue is particularly urgent on the African continent, which hosts 25% of our planet’s biodiversity, but receives just 3% of global conservation funding.

Name: MPA DAY
Description:
On the 1st of August 2021 we celebrated Africa’s first ever Marine Protected Area Day. 

WHY A DAY TO CELEBRATE MARINE PROTECTED AREAS?
Well, this helps us collectively appreciate the value of MPAs and how they benefit both us and the incredible marine biodiversity they designed to protect.

It’s a day to share stories and information about why and how we need to conserve our oceans and keep building support for MPAs. We hope that MPA Day will help more South Africans recognise the important role MPAs in preserving our oceans for both people and nature.

Name: Programa Río Lempa El Salvador
Description: The Río Lempa Conservation and Restoration Program jointly managed by Catholic Relief Services (CRS) and the Environmental Investment Fund of El Salvador (FIAES), will revitalize the watershed, enhancing water quality, stream flows, aquifer recharge, and biodiversity. The initiative will improve drinking water access for millions, support regenerative agriculture to boost crop yields and climate resilience, and promote sustainable rural livelihoods and economic growth.

Name: The Bateleurs
Description: Founded in 1998, The Bateleurs is a Non-Profit Company (NPC), with over 200 volunteer pilots and aircraft. It provides its beneficiaries and the public with an aerial perspective of the environment and has coordinated several thousand flights, and diverse missions, throughout South Africa and Africa, in support of environmental issues.

Name: Maiko National Park
Description: Protecting Eastern Lowland Gorilla, Okapi, Chimpazee, Forest Elephant, Congo Peacok, Red colobus and rain forest

Name: Alianza Biofilia
Description: Somos ocho entidades dedicadas a investigar y promover la conservación de la vida, que nos unimos con el interés de despertar en el país un genuino afecto por lo vivo. Somos Alianza Biofilia.

Name: Vhembe Biosphere Reserve
Description:
The Vhembe Biosphere Reserve (“VBR”) is established within the framework of
UNESCO’s (United Nations Education, Scientific and Cultural Organisation)
interdisciplinary programme on the Man and the Biosphere (MaB). We are internationally acknowledged, and all biosphere reserves have the following functions:
1. A Conservation role by conserving the natural biodiversity, including the diversity of ecosystems, ecosystem processes, genetic diversity, species diversity and ecosystem services, as well as the conservation of cultural and historical resources and scenic beauty within its boundaries.
2. A Development role, by combining conservation objectives with the sustainable use of ecosystem resources and indigenous knowledge systems to benefit local communities by fostering socio-culturally and ecologically sustainable economic development.
3. A Logistic role, by providing research, monitoring, education and training opportunities and facilities for local, regional and worldwide purposes.

Vhembe Biosphere Reserve support, encourage and complement Local Authorities, Local Communities, District Councils, Provincial and Government Departments and all other decision-makers to apply the principles of responsible and sustainable development.

“The focus of Vhembe Biosphere Reserve is on developing projects to serve as models and learning-sites for stakeholder communities to collaborate in translating global principles of sustainable development”. These are known as Biosphere Demonstration Projects (BDP).
The demonstration projects serve as examples of how community, private and public (“PPP Projects”) can collaborate to generate profitable, sustainable livelihood enterprises in harmony with nature.

Name: University of Cambridge Conservation Research Institute
Description:
The University of Cambridge Conservation Research Institute (UCCRI) is the bridge between the UCAM and the Cambridge Conservation Initiative (CCI), a collaboration between UCAM and ten leading conservation organisations. 

The institute fosters productive interdisciplinary dialogue, bringing together colleagues from various intellectual backgrounds and traditions. It conducts ambitious research that bridges natural sciences, technology, arts, humanities, and social sciences. Additionally, it plays a crucial role in the MPhil in Conservation Leadership program, a masters course at the University of Cambridge, and contributes to the broader University-wide sustainability education agenda.

The Cambridge Conservation Research Institute (CRI) operates within the diverse academic landscape of the University of Cambridge, encompassing all six academic schools, namely the Physical Sciences, Biological Sciences, Clinical Medicine, Technology, Humanities & Social Sciences, and Arts & Humanities.

Name: Western Wildlife Conservancies Association
Description: To Build and promote wildlife conservation in an efficient,reliable,effective,safe and environmentally friendly through enhanced conservation mouth piece for socio-economic development besides,application of innovative best practices in the Western Landscape.

Name: Alabama Wildlife Federation
Description: Alabama Wildlife Federation is a private, non-governmental statewide wildlife conservation organization located in Millbrook, Alabama, United States.

Name: Ebony Forest
Description:
Ebony Forest is a registered non-profit organisation dedicated to restoring the threatened flora and fauna of Mauritius. Since 2006, we have planted more than 154,000 native and endemic plants and weeded more than 33hectares of invaded forest. In 2017, we opened our Visitor and Ecology Centre to help generate sustainable financing for our ongoing conservation activities and to inspire and teach future conservationists about the importance of nature conservation. 

Registered Non-Profit Organisation:  NSIF/2024/0016

Name: Wise Ancestors
Description:
Wise Ancestors is a nonprofit on a mission to protect biodiversity by enabling unprecedented collaboration between genomic scientists and Indigenous Peoples & Local Communities. 

We aim to address the extinction crisis with biotechnology, Indigenous Science, and Traditional Ecological Knowledge, and unprecedented collaboration. 

Wise Ancestors is committed to co-creating and developing species-specific research projects (ie. “Conservation Challenges”) with Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs) for the purpose of producing genomic information, strengthening IPLC conservation efforts, and supporting Indigenous data sovereignty.

Our goal is to contribute to worldwide efforts to significantly slow the rate of biodiversity loss while uplifting IPLC leadership.

We are dedicated to building genuine relationships with IPLCs and researchers around the world to co-develop Conservation Challenges, raise funds, recruit collaborators and coordinate their work of sequencing and biobanking in a decentralized manner, while building a resource and education hub.

Together we can accelerate IPLC-driven conservation initiatives and genomic research that protects biodiversity for a beautiful, abundant, and resilient future.

Wise Ancestors is a fiscally sponsored affiliate of United Services Agency, a 501(c)3 corporation, EIN: 77-0311835.
Donations are tax deductible to the extent allowed by law.

Name: Dahari
Description: Dahari was created in 2013 to support rural communities to restore the ecosystems of the Comoro Islands. Our team of 60 implements three models across the islands of Anjouan and Grande Comore: a forest programme which signs conservation agreements with farmers to protect key areas for biodiversity and water provisioning; an agroforests programme that provides agricultural support to farmers planting over 50,000 trees per year in their fields; a marine programme focused on supporting fishers to implement a network of community-led no-take zones.

Name: Loki Schmidt Stiftung
Description: Wir engagieren uns in Hamburg sowie deutschlandweit für die Erhaltung seltener Pflanzen und Tiere; für den Kauf und die Pflege von Flächen für den Naturschutz; für das Kennenlernen und Erleben der Natur, um unsere natürlichen Lebensgrundlagen besser zu verstehen.

Name: Marine Wave Alliance
Description: Empresa que está virada para um conceito de protecção e conservação das áreas marinhas e costeiras. Vamos tentar ao máximo obter investidores e stakeholders relevantes que partilhem desta mesma ideia e começar a trabalhar na comunicação através de programas de conservação ambiental para que o público saiba da importância que a nossa fauna e flora marinha constitui nas suas vidas.

Name: Ocean Conservation Namibia
Description: Bringing awareness to the global plastic pollution crisis through seal rescues in Namibia.

Name: White Oak Initiative
Description: Founded in 2017 by the University of Kentucky, the DendriFund and the American Forest Foundation, the White Oak Initiative brings together industries, universities, state and federal agencies, private landowners, conservation organizations and trade associations that are committed to ensuring the long-term sustainability of America’s white oak forests as well as the economic, social and environmental benefits they provide.

Name: South Carolina Wildlife Federation
Description:
Conserve. Advocate. Restore. Educate.

Keeping SC Wild Since 1931!

We need YOU to support our mission to conserve and restore wildlife and habitat through education and advocacy! Please consider donating today at SCWF.org.

Name: Jardin botanique de Ouanaminthe
Description: Situé au de la localité de Bedou, le Jardin botanique de Ouanaminthe a pour mission de valoriser la flore haïtienne. En effet, il a pour objectifs la conservation, le développement et la mise en valeur des collections de plantes médicinales, ornementales et endémiques.

Name: SeaTheFuture
Description:
SeaTheFuture is a collaborative platform where individuals and organizations can donate to ocean conservation, helping impactful projects worldwide to scale their work, whilst fostering a collective movement to act towards environmental challenges. 

The extensive creative support given to projects help give visibility and awareness to their work, and the environmental metrics and indicators provided by them keep us all involved in the progress, both allowing businesses to easily comply with ESG/CSR regulations and helping individuals see the impact of their support.

Name: HydroExpo - Hydro Mag
Description: HydroExpo est un salon professionnel ayant vocation à promouvoir les différents moyens, services et matériels mis à disposition pour l’entretien, l’aménagement, la réhabilitation et la préservation de toutes zones humides (littoraux, lacs, canaux, zones portuaires, étangs, rivières, etc.). Il s’adresse aux collectivités territoriales, aux organismes publics et privés impliqués dans la gestion et l’entretien des zones humides, aux entreprises de TP, aux entreprises d’aménagement d’espaces verts et aux opérateurs de voies navigables.

Name: Wildlife Heritage Areas
Description:
Celebrating wildlife-friendly communities and destinations; Wildlife Heritage Areas is an initiative developed by World Animal Protection and World Cetacean Alliance.

A Wildlife Heritage Area is an outstanding wildlife-watching destination where local people recognise with a deep sense of pride their natural heritage and play a central role in protecting wild animals and their habitats.

As animal welfare and sustainable tourism, in general, become more important for the travelling public, Wildlife Heritage Areas will identify places that meet the highest standards of animal welfare, wildlife conservation, community well-being, and sustainability, whilst also maintaining high tourism value.

By coming together to reconnect with, protect and respect nature, Wildlife Heritage Areas will help turn the tide on biodiversity loss and wildlife suffering by demonstrating the power of local communities to help solve the planet’s most urgent issues.

Apply to become an area using our simple online application form: https://www.wildlifeheritageareas.org/apply-now/start-your-application/

Name: SOPFIM
Sanbona Wildlife Reserve Western Cape
Description:
Uncover The Story Behind Sanbona

Immerse yourself in a landscape where wildlife and flora leave you truly captivated and transformed.

Sanbona is one of South Africa’s largest privately-owned nature reserves, stretching across 58 000 hectares of rich protected ecosystems within two biodiversity hotspots.

This vast track of land in the Little Karoo outside Montagu, a mere 3.5 hours drive from Cape Town along Route 62, is owned by a non-profit company focused on steering conservation to the forefront of the safari experience.

Sanbona offers an authentic and contrasting natural space, striving to create a balance where critically important ecosystems, endangered wildlife and flora can prosper for the benefit of future generations.

Sanbona offers four different accommodation options, each with their own distinctive style and appeal. Spend the day exploring every corner of this immense reserve, endless open plains and sculpted Cape fold mountains adorned with San rock art, the endemic flora and some of South Africa’s most sought after wildlife.

Name: Oregon Department of Land Conservation and Development
Description:
The Oregon Department of Land Conservation and Development (DLCD) is a small state agency. We work in partnership with local governments, and state and federal agencies, to address the land use needs of the public, communities, regions, and the state. The Land Conservation and Development Commission (LCDC) provides policy direction for the land use planning program and oversees DLCD operations.

We help carry out the vision and legacy of Senate Bill 100, which for 50 years has contributed to the quality and character of the natural and built environment of the state. 2023 marks the 50th anniversary of the Department of Land Conservation and Development, the Land Conservation and Development Commission, and the signing of Senate Bill 100. The program has been charged by the Legislature with managing urban growth; protecting farm and forest lands, coastal areas, and natural resource lands; and providing for safe, livable communities in concert with the vision of the local communities.

Under the statewide land use planning program, each city and county is called upon to adopt and maintain a comprehensive plan and an implementing zoning code consistent with 19 statewide planning goals. Recognizing that each city and county has unique values and aspirations, our job is to provide planning guidance and technical assistance to help communities plan for their future while considering the needs of the region and the state.

Helping cities and counties address these functions in the context of a wide range of state and local interests requires that we be problem solvers. The department’s mission reflects this active role.

Name: Big Chalk
Description:
Southern England has some of the most iconic scenery on Earth, with its distinctive chalk and limestone vistas inspiring wonder for centuries. But, look past the spectacular views, and you’ll discover some of the world’s rarest natural paradises hiding in plain sight.

The globally rare interplay of geology, soils and water creates special landscapes formed of streams, meadows, grasslands and woodlands, which support a rich diversity of wildlife.

But the climate and nature crises mean that our precious chalk and limestone ecology is under threat. Most of our nature-rich grasslands have already been lost, and our chalk streams are in poor health. 

That’s why Big Chalk exists. This urgent and ambitious partnership is forging vital connections between nature recovery projects across southern England’s diverse chalk and limestone landscapes. Uniting resources and expertise, and covering a fifth of England, Big Chalk aims to build a vast and dynamic network central to the UK’s pledge to protect 30% of land and halt the decline of nature by 2030. 

Big Chalk is undoubtedly a big idea. But, given the global importance of these ecosystems and the need to help nature adapt to climate change, we must collaborate and work at scale. 

We can’t afford to lose our magical chalk streams, which spring up through southern England’s porous foundations that filter the water to crystal clear purity. We must take care of our nature-rich grasslands, woodlands and farmland habitats so they thrive with technicolour life once more. And we must join-up our efforts to collectively save some of the rarest wildlife on Earth.

These landscapes are as crucial to people as they are to our planet. They contribute to water supplies, healthy soils, carbon storage, sustainable food production and flood management, all while improving our health and wellbeing through access to historic heritage and the beautiful outdoors.

With so much at stake for all of us, let’s look beyond the scenery-together.

Name: GoGreen Conservation
Description:
GoGreen is part of the Horizon Project funded by the European Union, and is set to take place over 48 months, beginning October 2022. The ultimate aim of this project is to create a paradigm shift in current conservation practices to one that is more sustainable and conscious of the environmental impact of the conservation of cultural heritage field. 
GoGreen aims to do this through research and development of new methods greener materials, analysis and adjustments of current environmental standards, creating policy, developing a decision-making model, and a prototype of a digital app to aid conservators in the design of preventive and remedial conservation treatments.

Name: Towards IPOS (International Platform for Ocean Sustainability)
Description:
The ocean is essential for life on Earth, and it is urgent to take action to ensure its sustainability as the ocean’s health is being harmed by human activities such as overfishing, climate change, pollution, or its artificialization. 

So far Nations have largely fallen short from meeting collectively agreed ocean sustainability targets and objectives under various international agreements, treaties and frameworks.

To effectively respond to this crisis, all decision-makers need both political will and the best available knowledge. But today, this knowledge is fragmented across numerous initiatives and multiple reports that either do not respond to the priority needs of decision-makers, or fail to adequately bring together transdisciplinary expertise (natural, economic, humanities, and social sciences, Indigenous and Local Knowledge, etc.) or insufficiently involve the diverse communities affected by the ocean crisis. 

The Towards IPOS initiative intends to address these challenges by becoming the new gateway that (re)connects knowledge and decisions for a sustainable ocean. Aimed to enlighten the action of decision makers and answer their burning questions, the Towards IPOS initiative will co-produce request-driven documents and digital tools by aggregating and synthesizing knowledge in an inclusive and holistic way, and propose recommendations for necessary decisions.

The goal is for the Towards IPOS initiative is to be established as a neutral independent entity under the United Nations umbrella. Benefiting from growing support within the international community, at both political and scientific levels, the IPOS is gaining momentum towards its launch at the United Nations Conference in June 2025. The team is currently engaged in developing the Towards IPOS mission, positioning, governance, participatory approach, partnerships, and priority topics for pilot projects.

Name: Northeast Wilderness Trust
Description:
Northeast Wilderness Trust is a non-profit land trust based in Montpelier, VT working to protect forever-wild landscapes for nature and people across the Northeast. 

As the only land trust working across New England and New York exclusively to protect land as wilderness, the organization uses various land conservation tools and often works with conservation partners including other land trusts. 

The Wilderness Trust was founded in 2002, and protects more than 95,000 acres in Maine, New Hampshire, Vermont, eastern New York, Massachusetts, and Connecticut.

Name: Invertebrates Australia
Description:
Invertebrates include the vast majority of animal diversity  - from bees, beetles and spiders to jellyfish, coral and giant squid - and are of almost unquantifiable ecological and economic importance. Many of them do our hard work, e.g. pollinating, decomposing, nutrient cycling, reef-building, and others undo our hard work, e.g. locust plagues and invasive species, but irrespective, they receive a small fraction of government and industry resourcing and media attention relative to vertebrates. 

Invertebrates Australia was founded in September 2021 by three scientists who identified a critical mismatch between the way invertebrates are seen, as one large group of cryptic creepy crawlies, and the way they truly are, hundreds of mega-diverse groups. This mismatch has far-reaching consequences and contributes to the lack of data and knowledge so by creating a group that can represent all invertebrates we hope to make a big positive impact on ecological and economic outcomes in biodiversity management.

Name: The Wilderness Project
Description: By 2030, in partnership with local communities, governments, researchers and NGOs, The Wilderness Project aims to explore, study and better protect 1.2 million square kilometres of irreplaceable African wilderness. Central to this effort is to establish detailed baselines of the largely undocumented sources and watersheds of Africa’s greatest river basins – Okavango, Zambezi, Congo, Nile, Chad and Niger. We will do this by following the Great Spine of Africa (GSoA), the geographical linkage between the water towers that feed sub-Saharan Africa.

Name: Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS)
Description:
KWS is a state corporation established by an Act of Parliament (Cap 376), now repealed by WCMA (2013), with the mandate to conserve and manage wildlife in Kenya and to enforce related laws and regulations.
KWS undertakes conservation and management of wildlife resources across all protected area systems in collaboration with stakeholders.

Name: Réseau plein air Québec
Description: Fondé en 2019, le Réseau plein air Québec (RPAQ) regroupe les 11 fédérations québécoises de plein air qui desservent, avec les centaines d’organismes qui en sont membres, les Québécoises et Québécois pratiquant une ou plusieurs activités. Les fédérations qui composent le RPAQ sont reconnues par le ministère de l’Éducation du Québec pour leur capacité à encadrer la population et à lui fournir les outils nécessaires à la pratique sécuritaire de ses activités.

Name: Zambian Carnivore Programme
Description: ZCP is a 100% field-based non-profit Zambian trust dedicated to conserving large carnivore species through Conservation Science, Action, Leadership and Coexistence.

Name: Brasil Carbono
Description: Empresa especializada na administração e comercialização de CPR verde.
Location: Passo Fundo, Rio Grande do Sul

Name: OIPR - Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves
Description: OIPR est la structure en charge des la gestion et la conservation des parcs et réserves en Côte d'Ivoire.

Name: Underwater Gardens International
Description:
Underwater Gardens International is a company formed by a multidisciplinary network of professionals and experts with wide international recognition that integrates a scientific and business perspective.

We make Ocean regeneration, adaptation, and mitigation to Climate Change financially viable while promoting local socioeconomic growth.

In addition, the creation of a regenerative park: Underwater Gardens Park - Connecting people with the sea -provides a new way of raising awareness and connecting society with the Ocean through educational entertainment (Edutainment).

We create innovative solutions "Ad hoc" Sea Gardens from the integration of scientific and business knowledge, as an effective actor for the regeneration of the Ocean, considering the realities of each place and its needs.

Our services:
Our solutions to regenerate marine life allow us to increase biodiversity, fight against Climate Change’s effects, mitigate the environmental impact of human activity and create carbon immobilization, promoting "blue growth" and the circular economy.

We have our own technology that allows us to solve highly complex problems by creating intelligent reefs that improve and accelerate the creation of marine life (SER).

We facilitate innovative socioeconomic systems that allow us to finance the maintenance of the ecosystems so they can prosper healthy, resiliently and accelerate their growth. Thanks to our monitoring, underwater gardening programs and the involvement of the public (citizen science), we ensure the success by these actions.

Name: The Caribbean Biological Corridor Initiative (CBC)
Description: The Caribbean Biological Corridor Initiative (CBC) is a regional cooperation platform comprised of the Ministries of Environment of Cuba, Haiti, Dominican Republic, Puerto Rico, and Jamaica. We seek to build a strong and collectively sustained regional institutional approach to the conservation and management of shared terrestrial and marine biodiversity of regional importance in the Caribbean. Since our creation on July 10, 2007, we have had the support of the United Nations Environment Program, funding from the European Union, among others, and the participation of national and international NGOs and institutions. We implement projects directly and through partners that are part of our governance mechanism. Our mission is to achieve the effective conservation of marine and terrestrial biodiversity of regional importance and the maintenance of ecological connectivity in key sites of the insular Caribbean, considering the challenges of a changing climate, the development needs of communities, and ensuring the coordination, integration and cooperation of all relevant stakeholders. To this end, we have the 2030 Strategic Plan as a roadmap with actions that will enable the achievement of objectives in the member countries.

Name: Decarbomed
Description:
Accueillie par la Tunisie, La première édition du #DecarboMed est organisée par l’ANME & l’UTICA en partenariat avec le PNUD, l’Union européenne et la GIZ et portera sur les mécanismes en faveur de la décarbonation des entreprises à savoir, les technologies, les services et l’accès aux financement...

DecarboMed constitue l’occasion pour présenter l’écosystème de la décarbonation en Tunisie et en méditerranée. Les participants pourront découvrir des solutions innovantes et efficaces pour mener à bien un projet de transition vers la neutralité carbone.

Le forum Decarbomed sera les 24 et 25 septembre 2024 à L'hôtel Laico Tunis

Name: AGROMAP CONGO
Description:
AGROMAP est une société sous régionale spécialisée dans le développement de projets de restauration des forêts, de conservation de la biodiversité et d’amélioration des revenus des producteurs. Nous sommes convaincus que les solutions fondées sur la nature, notamment l’agroforesterie, la reforestation, de restauration des mangroves ont une capacité de séquestration du carbone pour lutter contre les effets du changement climatique avec la forte implication des communautés locales et les petits producteurs.

Notre organisation est structurée en 4 départements : 

•AGROMAP NBS (Nature-Based Solutions) : Reforestation, Agroforesterie, Restauration de forêts & mangroves, Projets à génération de crédit carbone & Biodiversité,

•AGROMAP CONSULTING : Conseil en Stratégie ESG, Chaine d'approvisionnement responsable, plan d'aménagement de forêts, Analyse des risques de déforestation, rédaction des documents de projets de crédit carbone, Accompagnent à la due diligence de l'RDUE et ARS-1000.

•AGROMAP TECHNOLOGIE : Mapping & Géo monitoring de forêts & zones préservées, Analyses GIS, plan d'occupation des sols, Digitalisation de services agricoles, Solutions de traçabilité et Gestion des données des projets.

•AGROMAP ENERGIES RENOUVELABLES: Conception et fourniture des outils de production d’énergie (foyers améliorés, biogaz), Promotion de l’usage des énergies renouvelables, Valorisation des déchets d’origine agricole et forestières (compost, biochar),Gestion et Valorisation des Bioressources.

Name: Sureste Sostenible, A.C.
Description: Canalizamos, catalizamos y coordinamos proyectos  de conservación, uso sustentable de la biodiversidad y cambio climático a través del establecimiento de alianzas estratégicas y del trabajo en conjunto con gobiernos, organizaciones de la sociedad civil, instituciones académicas, sector privado y comunidades en los estados de Tabasco, Campeche, Yucatán y Quintana Roo.
Location: Campeche, Tabasco, Yucatán y Quintana Roo

Name: Genau Green
Description: Ofrecemos una propuesta de financiamiento innovadora y única, que democratiza el acceso a inversiones en conservación. Nuestro modelo está diseñado para facilitar la participación de un mayor número de personas en la preservación del medio ambiente, ampliando la posibilidad de contribuir a la protección de nuestro planeta.

Name: Global Reef
Description:
At Global Reef, we aim to connect a global audience with the marine world through scientific research, education programs & innovative technology. 

Headquartered in Koh Tao, in the Gulf of Thailand, we play a vital role in preserving, maintaining and protecting our surrounding coral reefs.

Name: OFFICE DE L'EAU GUADELOUPE
Description:
L’Office de l’Eau Guadeloupe est un établissement public local à caractère administratif rattaché au Conseil Départemental de la Guadeloupe.
Il est doté de la personnalité civile et de l’autonomie financière. Il est instauré par :
La Loi n°2000-1207 d’Orientation pour l’Outre-Mer du 13 décembre 2000 (art.51) et son décret d’application n°2001-1324 28 décembre 2000. 
 
Les articles L213-13 à L213-20 de la section 7 du chapitre III du titre 1er du livre II du Code de l'Environnement.

Les Offices de l’Eau dans les départements d’outre-mer disposent des mêmes prérogatives que les agences de l’eau en métropole.

Name: Sayari Earth
Description:
Sayari Earth is a mission-driven, global nature-based carbon removals social enterprise and public benefit corporation. Our mission is to partner with people to sustain life on planet Earth. Our vision is for people and nature to universally thrive and co-exist.  We develop land and aquatic carbon removal programs which target strong atmospheric, social impact and conservation benefits. We use AI and technology to support our partners to benefit from nature markets.  We provide services to Governments, communities, NGO’s and landowners to develop jurisdictional carbon removal programs that integrate different nature-based carbon pools. 

Our goal is to secure co-management agreements across 45m ha, removing 45 mt CO2e/year and impacting 9 million beneficiaries by 2032.

Name: REWILDING SOUTHERN AFRICA
Description:
Rewilding may be considered as an expansive approach to conservation, allowing nature to do what she does best to repair and revitalise habitats. Rewilding is the science of conservation which promotes ecosystem health and the protection of all natural resources, including endangered species. 

𝙍𝙀𝙒𝙄𝙇𝘿𝙄𝙉𝙂 𝙎𝙊𝙐𝙏𝙃𝙀𝙍𝙉 𝘼𝙁𝙍𝙄𝘾𝘼 provides an integrated communications platform for a greater understanding and appreciation of rewilding, and how it can revitalise entire ecosystems. It is an upmarket publication aimed at conservationists, policymakers and concerned readers for whom conservation and the improvement of natural habitats shape how they live, what they consume, and where they choose to take their rest. 

Conservation is everyone’s legacy and concern.

Name: FMF
Description:
De Friese Milieu Federatie maakt zich sterk voor de ontwikkeling van een duurzaam Fryslân, waarin een vitale natuur, een gezond milieu en een robuust landschap centraal staan. Wij lopen voorop om kansrijke en duurzame vernieuwingen te stimuleren en dagen anderen uit tot een bijdrage en tot samenwerking.

Samen voor een mooi een duurzaam Fryslân.

Name: Bhutan Trust Fund for Environmental Conservation
Description: Bhutan Trust Fund for Environmental Conservation is the world's first environmental Trust Fund established in 1991.

Name: Conservatoire botanique de Normandie
Description:
Le Conservatoire botanique de Normandie est un Établissement public de coopération environnementale (EPCE) dont le Décret ministériel n° 2021-762 du 14 juin 2021 précise les 5 missions d'intérêt général qui lui sont confiées par l'État :
-Développement de la connaissance sur la flore, la fonge, les végétations et les habitats, aux échelles territoriale, nationale et biogéographique.
-Gestion, diffusion et valorisation de données sur la flore, la fonge, les végétations et les habitats
-Contribution à la gestion conservatoire de la flore, de la fonge, des ressources phytogénétiques sauvages, des végétations, des habitats et des espaces, et à la restauration écologique
-Appui à l'élaboration et à la mise en œuvre des politiques publiques et de la réglementation aux échelles territoriales, nationale et européenne
-Communication, sensibilisation et mobilisation des acteurs.

Name: The Freecycle Network
Description:
The Freecycle Network is a non-profit, charitable organization whose mission it is to make it easier to give items away via our globally local web communities at Freecycle.org than it is to throw items away.  The Network is made up of thousands of groups with millions of members around the world.

It's a grass-roots movement of people who are giving (and getting) stuff for free in their own towns.  It's all about reuse and keeping good stuff out of landfills.  Each local group is moderated by a local volunteer and membership is free.

Name: NaturAngola
Description: A NaturAngola é uma Organização Ambiental, sem fins lucrativos, que visa desenvolver acções e projectos que promovam o desenvolvimento sustentável, a conservação da natureza e a salvaguarda do direito ambiental em Angola.

Name: ADCEJ
Description:
L’Association pour le Développement Culturel et l’Epanouissement de la Jeunesse (ADCEJ) est une organisation socio culturelle et éducative représentée dans plusieurs pays du monde et œuvrant au Bénin (Autorisation du récépissé des modifications N° 20/MISP/DC/SGM/DAI/SAAP-ASSOC/SA de l’année 2021). Elle a pour but de créer un cadre d’union et de participer au développement éducatif, culturel et au rayonnement de la jeunesse en République du BENIN. Elle a pour vision d’amener les jeunes à participer au développement de leur couche afin de surpasser une juvénilité contemporaine en crise. C’est dans ce sens que nous avions opté pour la devise « Tous unis pour une jeunesse radieuse ».
Elle a pour objectifs de :
-	Promouvoir l’unité, la fraternité, la solidarité et l’harmonie entre tous les jeunes du Bénin et assurer leur épanouissement socio culturel ;
-	Œuvrer au développement et au renforcement des liens culturels et d’amitiés entre les jeunes du Bénin et d’ailleurs ;
-	Promouvoir la culture Africaine en générale et celle du Bénin en particulier.
Elle intervient dans les domaines : socio culturel, santé, éducation et formation, eau et environnement, défense des droits des jeunes, entrepreneuriat. 
Email : adcejg@gmail.com  Twitter :@ADCEJINTER 
facebook: www.facebook.com//ADCEJINTER ; whatsapp: + 229 94-06-67-17.

Name: Safeguard The Planet Moçambique
Description: Safeguard the Planet Moçambique é um movimento nacional que promove a sustentabilidade ambiental e a saúde do planeta. Actua nos eixos de mudanças Climáticas, Degradação do solo e Poluição Ambiental, por meio de campanhas de advocacia, educação ambiental, mobilização comunitária e empoderamento juvenil.

Name: Freyja Foundation
Description: The Freyja Foundation is a private, charitable foundation dedicated to supporting innovative, scalable, and lasting conservation solutions that protect wild places and combat the climate crisis. Freyja develops innovative financing solutions and projects through philanthropy and impact investing that permanently protect and restore threatened and degraded eco-corridors as well as empower local communities.

Name: Jaguar Rescue Center Foundation
Description:
The Jaguar Rescue Center  is a temporary or permanent home for ill, injured and orphaned animals. Working with all kinds of wild animals like felines, primates, reptiles, birds etc, the JRC provides veterinary services, round-the-clock care and comfort to animals that would otherwise be unable to survive in the rainforest or in the sea of the Caribbean.

The primary goal of the JRC is to rehabilitate animals and reintroduce them back into their native habitats. Monkeys, birds, sloths, snakes and more are brought to the JRC and treated by professional veterinarians, caregivers and a hard-working team of staff and volunteers. 

In addition to the animal-care services, the JRC provides educational services to the residents and visitors of Costa Rica, as well as internship and research opportunities for biologists, veterinarians and researchers from around the world.

Name: Centro de Innovación Científica Amazónica - CINCIA
Description: The Center for Amazonian Scientific Innovation (CINCIA) is a non profit research center committed to conducting scientific research for effective conservation and environmental management, with a focus on ecological restoration in the biodiverse Peruvian Amazon. Our team collaborates with government, conservationists, and local communities to promote sustainable land use. A significant part of our research addresses the impacts of mercury pollution and Artisanal and Small-scale Gold Mining (ASGM) on tropical ecosystems. We are dedicated to building Amazonian-based scientific capacity in young scientists, with a strong commitment to promoting women and Indigenous people in STEM careers. CINCIA strives to set a standard for large-scale reforestation and biodiversity conservation, contributing to comprehensive environmental management across the Amazon region.

Name: Bats without Borders
Friend of the Earth
Description:
Friend of the Earth was started in 2018 by the international NGO World Sustainability Organization and it represents conservation programs in over 40 countries and certification standards for sustainable products and services.  

When you choose a Friend of the Earth certified product or service, you know that it is produced by means of sustainable practices and that you are contributing to conservation and awareness initiatives worldwide.

Friend of the Earth standards can potentially certify, by means of annual thrid-party audits carried out by qualified indipendent bodies, products from the following sectors: food, textiles, fashion, furniture, jewels, mining, tourism, sport, events, packaging and many others.

The Friend of the Earth conservation and awareness projects aim at protecting endangeres species and habitats, such as wetlands, forests, parrots, frogs, elephants, butterflies, rhinos, giraffes, lemurs, snails and many others. 

The World Sustainability Organization has also developed Friend of the Sea, to carry out activities similar to Friend of the Earth in the marine world.

The World Sustainability Foundation was recently created to manage Friend of the Earth and Friend of the Sea conservation and awareness activities.

Name: Moonlit Sanctuary Wildlife Conservation Park
Description:
Moonlit Sanctuary: A conservation park working to save endangered species while offering visitors unique experiences with Australian native animals.

This site is used to update our professional colleagues and associates with relevant news about Moonlit Sanctuary. To this end there will be a monthly update covering topics such as new animals and exhibits, new and improved visitor facilities and and new marketing initiatives. Because your time is precious the updates will be brief and kept relevant.. Cute animal photos and interesting animal news can be found on our Facebook page, which we update two or three times a week.



Winner of: 
* Premiers Sustainability Award for Environmental Preservation 2017
* Victorian Tourism Awards in Ecotourism 2010, 2014. 2018, 2019 and 2021.
* Victorian Tourism Award for Tourist Attraction 2018.
* South-east Melbourne Small Business of the Year 2012
* City of Casey Tourism Business of the Year 2011
* Sustainable Cities award for Preservation of the Environment 2009

* 10 ha of bushland
* feeding kangaroos and wallabies 
* cuddle koalas
* hands on animal encounters
* 0ver 70 species of mammal, birds, reptiles, frogs and insects.
* Free ranging animals and large natural enclosures

* Inspired by the work of famous British conservationist Gerald Durrell, 

* Magical evening tours
* Daily animal presentations and keeper talks
* Cafe and Gift Shop
* Catered functions
* Student groups 
* Educational programs for all levels.

* 50 minutes south-east of central Melbourne

Name: Yayasan Inisiasi Alam Rehabilitasi Indonesia (YIARI)
Description: Yayasan Inisiasi Alam Rehabilitasi Indonesia (Yayasan IAR Indonesia) is a non-profit organization with a focus on the rescue, rehabilitation and conservation of wildlife. Yayasan IAR Indonesia rescues and rehabilitates orangutans, macaques and slow lorises with the aim to prepare these animals for release back into their natural habitat. Yayasan IAR Indonesia also engages in education, awareness and outreach programs that convey messages to the public to treat all animals with compassion and care.

Name: UBi - Unternehmen Biologische Vielfalt
Description:
Das Handeln für mehr Biodiversität hat einen immer größer werdenden Einfluss auf den wirtschaftlichen Erfolg und das Wohlergehen von Unternehmen.

Wir zeigen Ihnen welchen Nutzen es bringt und mit welchen Tools Sie Biodiversität in Ihrem Verband und Unternehmen umsetzen und voran bringen können. 

Wir informieren, bieten Möglichkeiten sich zu vernetzen und machen Sie stark für eine biodiversere Zukunft.

Das Projekt UBi - Unternehmen Biologische Vielfalt wird gefördert im Bundesprogramm Biologische Vielfalt durch das Bundesamt für Naturschutz mit Mitteln des Bundesministeriums für Umwelt, Klimaschutz, Naturschutz und nukleare Sicherheit.

Name: Vivara Pro
Description:
Vivara Pro is dé expert in Europa om professionele projecten uit te voeren en producten en diensten aan te bieden voor mitigatie, verbetering van de biodiversiteit, flora & fauna compensatie en duurzaamheid. 

Vivara Pro ontwikkelt specialistische natuurbeschermingsproducten voor de professionele markt. De jarenlange samenwerking tussen Vivara Pro en haar natuurbeschermingsorganisaties heeft ertoe geleid, dat de producten voldoen aan de allerhoogste kwaliteitseisen en gecertificeerd zijn door de natuurbeschermingsorganisaties.

Name: ENGGANG
Description:
The Enggang Holdings group was formed in 2022 as an investment holding company with a long-term initiative of creating conservation and preservation landscapes, generating tangible benefits for our climate, community, and biodiversity, in line with the UN Sustainable Development Goals. 

Through these conservation projects, Enggang Holdings is dedicated to the continued preservation of our environment whilst delivering long-term benefits to society and stakeholders. 

Through our commitment to sustainability, we intend to attract inflows of both national and international funding for biodiversity conservation and nature-based solutions for climate change mitigation, through carbon financing and investments by businesses that are committed to the principles of Environmental, Social, and Governance.

Name: neede.
Description: neede. a pour objet de contribuer à la protection de l’environnement et de la biodiversité du bassin méditerranéen en mettant en œuvre des actions de sensibilisation, de recherche, de formation et d’accompagnement de projets à destination de l’ensemble des acteurs et publics des territoires du pourtour méditerranéen. Elle pose ainsi des fondations pour l’éducation et de la formation à la protection de l’environnement. Elle œuvre en ce sens à la création d'un lieu emblématique ouvert à tous, d’un centre de recherche international et d’un organisme de formation initiale et continue, permettant l'émergence d'une prise de conscience individuelle, collective et institutionnelle. Il s’agit de développer la mobilisation et les actions de tous les acteurs, publics, privés, sociaux, économiques et culturels, dans le sens de la transition environnementale.

Name: TOTM
Description:
Créée en 2019, l’association Trans Océans Tortues Marines a pour missions de développer des actions en faveur de l’étude et de la conservation des tortues marines, ainsi que de rechercher et gérer les financements nécessaires à leur mise en oeuvre.

TOTM est fort d’un conseil d’administration composé de 18 administrateurs répartis sur l’ensemble des océans et tous impliqués dans la conservation des tortues marines. 

Les missions de TOTM
• La promotion, l'étude et la conservation des tortues marines et de leurs habitats.
• La mise en œuvre d'actions en faveur des tortues marines selon les priorités identifiées par les groupes scientifiques et techniques spécialisés dans la protection des tortues marines. Les méthodes d'actions seront choisies par les membres de l'Association et validées par le Conseil d'Administration.
• Le soutien à la mise en œuvre d'actions transversales à l'échelle internationale (Union Internationale pour la Conservation de la Nature, International Sea Turtle Society, State of the World Sea Turtles...), inter-régionale (OSPAR, Barcelone…), nationale (Groupe Tortues Marines Frances, Société Herpétologique de France…) ou supranationale (Mediterranean Conference on Marine Turtles, Wider Caribbean Sea Turtle Conservation Network (WIDECAST), Memorandum of Understanding on the Conservation and Management of Marine Turtles and their Habitats of the Indian Ocean and South-East Asia(IOSEA)…), menées sur les tortues marines, leurs habitats et/ou les menaces dans les territoires français. Ce soutien pourra prendre différentes formes, notamment un appui scientifique, technique, logistique et financier.
• La réalisation et la diffusion de supports et outils pédagogiques à l'attention du grand public, des structures scolaires et de la jeunesse en général.

Crédit photo : Théo Guillaume/Te mana o te moana/Polynésie française

Name: Grevy's Zebra Trust
Description: We are the only organisation in the world with a mission focused solely on conserving the endangered Grevy’s zebra. Recognising that the survival of the Grevy’s zebra depends on its ability to coexist with people living in northern Kenya, GZT believes these communities must be at the centre of designing and driving conservation efforts. GZT therefore works in partnership with communities from monitoring Grevy’s zebra through citizen science, to co-designing site-specific and tailored solutions to threats facing the species. In all of its work, GZT seeks ways to use data and information to inform decisions and solution for positive conservation outcomes.

Name: Club Environnement Yoff
Description: Le Club Environnement Yoff est une association environnementale basée dans la Commune de Yoff mais qui intervient dans tout le Sénégal. Nous avons comme objectif de lutter contre les problématiques environnementales ( insalubrité, érosion côtière...)

Name: PLANT-MY-TREE
Description:
UNSER ZIEL

Jeder Mensch hat einen Baum gepflanzt. Das ist unsere Vision und dafür ermöglichen wir Personen und Unternehmen, sich für die Erhaltung unserer Umwelt und für unser Klima einzusetzen. 

WIE WIR ARBEITEN

1.	LOKAL – Wir pflanzen hauptsächlich in unserer Heimat, in Deutschland – damit sind wir regional, nachvollziehbar und die Wälder sind begehbar.
2.	TÜV-ZERTIFIZIERT – Die Abläufe und die entsprechenden Dokumentationen sind TÜV-zertifiziert nach DIN ISO 9001:2015.
3.	NACHHALTIGER UND LANGFRISTIGER KLIMASCHUTZ – Dieser umfasst Erstaufforstung, Waldumbau, Waldschutz und Waldverjüngung.
4.	PLANT-MY-TREE. DIE STIFTUNG – Unsere eigenen bepflanzten Flächen übergeben wir der Stiftung, welche das jeweilige Projekt mindestens 99 Jahre lang begleitet.

Name: Conservation Evidence
Description: We summarise the documented evidence for the effectiveness of conservation actions. Our free resource is designed to support anyone making decisions about how to maintain and restore biodiversity.

Name: Ranita de Darwin ONG
Description: We are dedicated to the conservation, research and outreach of amphibians in Chile, mainly Darwin's Frog and the protection of their habitats. On our website you can find information about frogs and toads that inhabit our country.

Name: Conservation Planning Specialist Group
Description:
CPSG is a Specialist Group of the International Union for Conservation of Nature Species Survival Commission (IUCN SSC) supported by a non-profit organization, the Global Conservation Network. 

CPSG’s mission is to save threatened species by increasing the effectiveness of conservation efforts worldwide. For over 40 years, we’ve accomplished this by using scientifically sound, collaborative processes that bring together people with diverse perspectives and knowledge to catalyse positive conservation change. We provide species conservation planning expertise to governments, Specialist Groups, zoos and aquariums, and other wildlife organizations.

We support an integrated approach to species conservation planning through the joint development of management strategies and conservation actions by all responsible parties, known as the One Plan Approach. We strive to ensure that a broad range of stakeholders is represented at each workshop. As a result, one comprehensive conservation plan for the species helps bridge the gap between wild and captive population management.

CPSG offers tools and processes that address commonly encountered conservation planning needs, and help to get species in need on the road to recovery. Visit out website to see what workshops, training and webinars we offer!

Name: OJECC-BÉNIN
Description:
The Organization of Youth for Water and Climate Change (OJECC-BENIN) has the overarching goal of safeguarding and protecting the environment in all its forms. Its primary objectives include promoting the Sustainable Development Goals related to water and sanitation issues on one hand, and climate change on the other; contributing to the assistance and training of communities most vulnerable to extreme climatic events; advancing environmental education, sustainable and resilient agriculture, and renewable energy in place of polluting fossil fuels; and supporting land-use planning to ensure the good health of rural populations.

OJECC-BENIN has two flagship programs complemented by various activities and projects: the Community Risk Management Program for Flooding (ProGRI) and the Adaptation and Resilience Program for Drought and Climate Migration (PARSeM).

Name: Rewilding Ukraine
Description:
As part of Rewilding Europe, we want to make Europe a wilder place, with more space for wild nature, wildlife and natural processes. In bringing back the variety of life, we will continue to explore new ways for people to enjoy and earn a fair living from the wild.

Danube Delta – an ambitious initiative in Europe
One of the least populated areas in Europe, the Danube Delta offers a unique opportunity to restore a whole spectrum of biodiverse habitats – from open estuarine systems, naturally grazed grassland and vast reed beds through to freshwater marshes, coastal lagoons, shallow lakes and riverine forests.

Working with partners, the Rewilding Danube Delta team is working to significantly improve the ecological integrity and natural functioning of 40,000 hectares of wetland and terrestrial delta habitat, using rewilding principles at on a landscape scale. Revitalised and self-governing natural processes, particularly flooding and natural grazing, will govern landscape formation, driving other natural processes, wildlife comeback, increased biodiversity and the development of a nature-based economy.

Name: Bergwaldprojekt e.V.
Description:
Wir bringen Menschen von 8 - 87 in Wälder, Moore und Offenlandbiotope. Zum gemeinsamen Arbeiten und Aufbäumen. Für Mensch und Natur.
In den Projektwochen, Pflanztagen und Corporate Volunteer-Einsätzen an bis zu 80 verschiedenen Einsatzorten in ganz Deutschland beteiligen sich jährlich Tausende Freiwillige, angeleitet von Fachkräften aus Naturschutz und Forst.

Name: Oceans 5
Description: Oceans 5 is an international funders’ collaborative dedicated to stopping overfishing, establishing marine protected areas, and constraining offshore oil and gas development - three of the highest priorities identified by marine scientists around the world. Oceans 5 is a sponsored project of Rockefeller Philanthropy Advisors.

Name: Bow Seat: Creative Action for Conservation
Description:
Bow Seat Ocean Awareness Programs is a Massachusetts-based nonprofit that educates and engages young people in ocean conservation and advocacy through the arts.

Our signature program is an annual Ocean Awareness Contest engaging tens of thousands of youth globally.

We believe that changing our world starts by inspiring and energizing the next generation of ocean caretakers to vividly imagine a better future. Our programs center the creative arts as vital tools to learn about issues impacting our environment, connect more deeply with nature, and bring communities together to care for our blue planet.

Bow Seat was founded by Linda Cabot in 2011. Since then, Bow Seat has engaged nearly 18,000 youth from all 50 U.S. states and more than 100 countries through educational initiatives such as the Ocean Awareness Contest, the Future Blue Youth Council, and monthly webinars.

Name: Aqua Viva
Description: Seit 1960 engagiert sich Aqua Viva für den Schutz und die Renaturierung der heimischen Gewässerlebensräume. Aqua Viva begeistert Kinder, Jugendliche und Erwachsene für das Thema Wasser und vermittelt an Bächen, Flüssen und Seen die Faszination dieser einzigartigen Lebensräume.

Name: ECO-IMPACT Senegal
Description: ECO-IMPACT Sénégal est une organisation dédiée à la préservation de l’environnement et à la résilience des communautés face aux défis climatiques et environnementaux. Elle s’appuie sur des valeurs de durabilité, d’inclusion et de collaboration pour sensibiliser, former et mobiliser les acteurs locaux. Elle a pour mission de promouvoir l’agroécologie, la conservation de la biodiversité et le développement de pratiques écoresponsables à travers des projets innovants et adaptés aux réalités environnementales et socio-économiques.

Name: AESVT Maroc
Description:
L’Association des Enseignants des Sciences de la Vie et de la Terre du Maroc (AESVT Maroc) est une association à but non lucratif. Créée en 1994, elle est constituée d’un réseau de 40 sections et gère un réseau de 18 centres d’éducation à l’environnement (CEE). L’AESVT Maroc vise à contribuer à l’édification d’une société moderne et solidaire conformément aux principes et valeurs du développement durable.
C’est une association de développement et d’éducation, compétente et engagée (2000 bénévoles impliqués et 13 salariés permanents) qui mène des actions concrètes en faveur de l’Environnement à travers ses 3 axes d’intervention : éducation à la transition, écosystèmes et ressources naturelles, territoire durable.

Name: Conservation Allies
Description: The single greatest challenge for local conservation organizations based in developing nations is raising funds to sustain their activities. While these are frequently the groups making a real difference and having a major impact, they simply cannot compete with the marketing dominance of the largest international organizations. Conservation Allies is unique in offering a true partnership for local conservation organizations in the countries of greatest need. It is time to get funds directly to the organizations that make the biggest difference.

Name: Conservatoire d'espaces naturels des Pays de la Loire
Description:
Le Conservatoire d’espaces naturels (CEN) des Pays de la Loire, est une association de type loi 1901 à but non lucratif. Agréé par l'État et la Région des Pays de la Loire au titre des articles L.414-11 et L.141-1 du Code de l'environnement, il a pour vocation de rayonner sur l’ensemble des Pays de la Loire en s’appuyant sur ses trois implantations de Nantes, Angers et le Mans. 
Fondée sur la maîtrise foncière et d’usage, l’action du Conservatoire contribue à la préservation du patrimoine naturel et paysager en passant par la CONNAISSANCE, la PROTECTION, la GESTION et la VALORISATION des espaces naturels et de la biodiversité associée.

Name: Association Vallée de la Millière
Description:
La Vallée de la Millière est une association fondée et présidée par Yann Arthus-Bertrand en octobre 2020.
Une réserve naturelle intégrale de biodiversité de 26,6 hectares avec un jardin pédagogique et nourricier de 1,4 hectare.

Le cœur du projet : le réensauvagement et la protection du vivant.
L’association a un objectif : sensibiliser le plus grand nombre au respect du vivant, transmettre des connaissances scientifiques accessibles.

Les actions menées (ateliers pédagogiques, accueil de publics, temps de transmission) servent à préserver, comprendre et défendre la biodiversité, aujourd’hui et pour les générations futures.

Name: Vulpro
Description:
Vultures form an important ecological component of our natural environment, cleaning up dead carcasses and decreasing the spread of some diseases. The relationship between vultures and people is also a venerable one – vultures played roles in some early societies, including the Egyptian and the Hindu societies; vultures continue to be used as symbols or metaphors in modern societies; and vulture body parts are used in muthi/traditional medicines.
Today, vultures face an unprecedented onslaught from human activities. They have to cope with electrocutions and collisions with electrical structures, poisonings, land-use changes, a decrease in food availability and exposure to toxicity through veterinary drugs, to list just a few of some of the challenges facing vultures today.
Vultures, positioned at the top of the food chain, are an indicator of the health of the environment below them – and dependent for their survival on a healthy environment. As such the work of the Vulture Conservation Programme ("VulPro") work is intended and expected to impact on many other aspects of the environment – beyond vultures.
VulPro approaches vulture conservation in an integrated, multidisciplinary fashion, with the benefits from the programme accruing to both vultures and society at large. VulPro combines education and good science, with networking, capacity building and knowledge generation.
The veterinary disciplines of toxicology, pharmacology, clinical pathology and medicine are combined with the science of cell-phone telemetry and the banking of genetic resources, with the goal being to positively influence the well-being of our natural resources to the ultimate benefit of society. In this regard, VulPro engages in a number of interrelated activities, and uses a variety of resources, in endeavouring to meet its objectives.

Name: Arid Recovery
Description: Arid Recovery is an independent not-for-profit driving conservation research in the arid zone. Our work is centred on a large (12,300 ha) predator-proof fenced reserve into which threatened species have been reintroduced. We collaborate widely with researchers and other organisations to progress arid zone ecosystem recovery, threatened species reintroduction and climate change adaptation.

Name: DRY FOREST (NGO)
Description:
DRY FOREST is a Malagasy NGO created in 2020 focused on tropical dry forests regeneration, preservation and valorization, and on fighting against poverty in local communities living surrounded by such vulnerable ecosystems. 

 DRY FOREST has launched the "Regenerate the Forest" program to reforest destroyed areas of Kirindy Mitea, one of the largest baobab forests in the world, thanks to tree-nurseries located in or nearby public primary schools. 

DRY FOREST currently manages 4 tree-nurseries in the villages of Ambararata , Ankevo and Marofihitra and in the town of Belo sur Mer, where is also located The Baobase, basecamp of the NGO in the Menabe, region known around the world for its baobabs' forests.

Name: Mycelium Ecology
Description:
Mycelium Ecology is on a mission to develop community projects with the sole purpose of conserving bio-diversity within the Western Ghats. We are a group of individuals who believe in investing in our children's future by owning and conserving rapidly depleting bio-diverse habitats. 

There is a need to do this now more than ever! An increase in demand for plantation produces, development required to complement growth, unplanned urbanization, and development projects without clearly observing its long-term impact are a reality in the Western Ghats today. We are losing precious native components within this complex ecosystem in front of our eyes.

Our approach is to create knowledge, conversations, spaces & installations for future generations to reflect.

Name: REAL Paradise Conservation
Description:
Mary-Ann du Toit, a 13 year old girl, wrote on her Vision board:  

Saving all the Rhinos , Elephants and Leopards. 

 Then she founded REAL Paradise.

Raising Funds to Protect and Reestablish Rhinos, Elephants and Leopards through competitions, vacations, sponsorships, events, school programs and more.
Sponsorship give your company exposure to an ever growing list of subscribers as well as the media.
Sponsorship also gives your tax benefits, since REAL Paradise is in the process of registering at SARS as a PBO, with the ability to issue S18A certificates.
Please contact us to discuss sponsorship options.

REAL Paradise makes raising funds FUN - with a real thrill factor for the public. 

 We have a list of sponsors who already pledged holiday accommodation as prizes for competitions.  REAL Paradise sell raffle tickets for these competitions, while building a list of subscribers at the same time.  

We impress upon our subscribers to support our sponsors - the more successful your company is, the more you'll donate to REAL Paradise.

Further, REAL Paradise use PR, free, sponsored and paid advertising to gain attention.  

In fact, since Mary-Ann is only 13 years old, that is a source of excellent newsworthiness.  Sponsoring her organization, will also bring your company into the spotlight, ready for mainstream media to pick up on your selflessness.
