Name: Troupe Brasil
Description:
A Troupe Brasil é reconhecida pela sua capacidade de entregar soluções para problemas dos mais diversos portes.

Já são 30 anos oferecendo ao mercado o que há de melhor, sempre trabalhando com foco na viabilidade e retorno ao seu cliente.

Pelo Brasil e pelo mundo, a Troupe tem deixado o legado de grandes parcerias e bons relacionamentos, acumulando testemunhos positivos que lhe garantem seguir investindo para se manter como uma das principais empresas de seu setor.

Name: Amanatari
Description: Creemos que la conservación de la Amazonía es urgente e indispensable. En Amanatari, trabajamos conectando esfuerzos y creando alianzas estratégicas para poner en valor el bosque amazónico a través de un enfoque social. Contribuimos con ciencia e innovación, y nos unimos a las comunidades indígenas y poblaciones locales, los verdaderos guardianes de este ecosistema, para protegerlo y promover su desarrollo sostenible.

Name: Houston Audubon
Description:
Houston Audubon's mission is to advance the conservation of birds and positively impact their supporting environments. We do this through conservation, education, public engagement and advocacy. A significant part of our work is owning and managing 17 nature sanctuaries totaling 3,474 acres to protect habitat for birds and provide people recreational opportunities in nature. We were accredited as a land trust in July 2017.

Houston Audubon is a financially independent 501(c)(3) charitable conservation organization and is a chapter of National Audubon Society.

Name: Human Dimensions of Natural Resources at Colorado State University
Description:
The Department of Human Dimensions of Natural Resources contributes to informed decision making and a broad based understanding of the ways in which humans value, use, and depend on the natural environment.
With a cutting edge approach to teaching in either Tourism or Human Dimensions of Natural Resources, our award winning programs equip students with dynamic coursework, experiential learning opportunities, and real world experiences that make a decisive impact on their future.

Name: West Africa Blue
Description:
West Africa Blue is a community-centric developer of blue carbon projects in West Africa. Blue’s mission is to work hand-in-hand with local communities to preserve, restore, and sustainably manage coastal ecosystems across West Africa, financed through the issuance of high-quality carbon credits. 

Our project activities will help build resilience to climate change, limit greenhouse gas emissions from deforestation, protect endangered species, and support new economic opportunities – especially for women.

Our projects are some of the first large-scale blue carbon projects in Africa and have the potential to exemplify what it means to be high-quality, community-centric and financially sustainable. We believe that a focus on sharing and collaboration with a diverse group of stakeholders and market participants will help catalyze the market throughout the region and globally.

Name: Green Technologies conseils
Description: Green Technologies est une entreprise de conseils et d'assistance en lien avec la sécurité des procédés industriels et l'environnement, l'innovation s'accorde avec les nouvelles technologies contre la pollution ou la décarbonation .

Name: Solana Center for Environmental Innovation
Description: Solana Center, is an award-winning 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, serving San Diego County. We empower innovative solutions to climate change through education, community engagement, and research.

Name: Réserve Naturelle Nationale Baie Saint-Brieuc
Description: La baie de Saint-Brieuc est reconnue comme une zone humide littorale d’importance internationale, et est classée en Réserve Naturelle Nationale.

Name: Association AL LARK
Description: AL LARK is a Cancale-based non-profit organisation dedicated to the protection of the marine environment and the study of marine mammals.

Name: Alinea Network Organisation
Description:
Across Africa, grassroots or home grown organisations are the trusted backbone of conservation, embedded in communities, mandated by local authorities, and protecting the landscapes and wildlife they call home. Yet they are often under-resourced and expected to deliver without the structural support larger institutions enjoy.

Alinea exists to change that. We are a strategic, systems-level platform that strengthens, connects, and supports the broader conservation ecosystem. We reinforce local leadership, ensuring well-placed grassroots partners have the governance, tools, and financial alignment to thrive.

We also support donors and funders by de-risking investment, rigorously vetting partners, strengthening governance, and aligning compliance so funding can flow securely and confidently to those best placed to deliver lasting impact.

We believe in systems where communities are not only stakeholders, but implementors of their own destinies, leading the conservation efforts that affect them most.

We build shared systems, inspire innovation, and foster high-trust coalitions. Our networks build networks, reducing duplication, increasing resilience, and opening pathways for collaboration. We are the partner organisations and donors can lean on for security, guidance, and support, building resilient, ethical, and impactful organisations so that every investment is secure and transformative.

Name: Agence Nationale de la Grande Muraille Verte - ANGMV Mauritanie
Description: L'Agence Nationale de la Grande Muraille Verte (ANGMV) de Mauritanie est une institution publique dédiée à la restauration des terres dégradées et à la promotion du développement durable. Sous la tutelle du Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable, l'ANGMV œuvre à renforcer la résilience des écosystèmes et des populations face aux effets du changement climatique à travers la Mauritanie.

Name: Waterland vzw
Description:
Waterland brengt het water tot leven in jouw buurt.

Waterland trekt en ondersteunt acties die op een creatieve en inspirerende manier water opnieuw aantrekkelijk maken: water als een bron van plezier, water als een bron van biodiversiteit.

Waterland realiseert met burgers, (burger-)organisaties, waterprofessionals en overheden creatieve waterprojecten. We wisselen kennis, ervaring en informatie uit over het belang en de werking van gezonde watersystemen om zo iedereen mee te krijgen in een nieuwe watercultuur in het verstedelijkte Vlaanderen.

Name: ONG SAVE OUR PLANET
Description: Une organisation de jeunesse engagée pour le bien-être de l'Homme et de la Nature. Nous œuvrons au Bénin contribuant à la protection de l'environnement et la promotion de pratiques durables.

Name: Kawartha Land Trust (KLT)
Description:
Kawartha Land Trust (KLT) is a dynamic land conservation charity with an impressive track record of growth. Our mission is to care for the lands entrusted to us and help others protect the land they love in the Kawarthas.

We create nature reserves for future generations by acquiring ecologically important properties and/or interests in properties. We also enhance our stewardship of the land by engaging landowners in land care initiatives through our Partners in Conservation program.

Founded in 2001, KLT protects 33 properties that include more than 5,350 acres of ecologically diverse lands, some of which include hiking trails that introduce thousands of people to nature in the Kawarthas every year. We have achieved our successes through the work of our dedicated and growing team of donors, volunteers and supporters. Together, we can ensure more natural spaces in the Kawarthas are preserved forever.

KLT works within the Treaty 20 Michi Saagiig Territory and we are dedicated to continuing to create meaningful connections and relationships with First Nations communities in our region as we build upon these connections.

Name: GEPOG - Group for the Study and the Protection of Birds in French Guiana
Save Our Mangroves Now!
Description:
Survival artists, nature’s kindergarten, climate savers – mangroves are versatile superheroes. 

Mangroves:

 are habitat and nursery for over 3000 fish species, 
 can store 3 to 5 times more carbon per area than tropical upland forests
 and provide livelihoods for over 120 million people. 

But this unique ecosystem is under threat: more than a third of all mangrove forests have disappeared since the mid-20th century due to agriculture, logging and urbanization. If present deforestation rates continue, all mangroves could be lost by the end of this century. 

Our vision is a world in which thriving mangrove habitats exist in harmony with the communities they support. With a focus on the Western Indian Ocean, the Save Our Mangroves Now! initiative unites governments, conservation specialists and coastal communities through a shared goal to conserve and restore mangrove habitats. We combine our vision with a mission to facilitate policies, programmes and investments that regenerate mangrove ecosystems, tackle climate change and sustain livelihoods.

Name: Week van de Circulaire Economie
Description: Het grootste landelijke inspiratie- en netwerkevenement voor koplopers en beginnende circulaire ondernemers, volgend jaar van 19 t/m 29 maart 2026.

Name: LUMO Community Wildlife Conservancy
Description:
LUMO is an acronym for Lualenyi, Mramba and Oza. These are ranches found in the Tsavo Ecosystem, who have contributed part of their land, 28,000, 10,000and 10,000 acres respectively, towards conservation. 
Its a community wildlife conservancy  providing community benefits to alleviate poverty through sustainable use of wildlife and other natural capital and enhancing community wildlife co-existence.

Name: Bureau du Marché Carbone de Côte d'Ivoire
Description: Guichet unique chargé de la mise en œuvre du cadre global de la participation de la Côte d'Ivoire aux mécanismes carbone.

Name: NatureXpairs
Description: The French Hub for Protected Areas and People is an alliance of French protected area managers with complementary skills, dedicated to the preservation, conservation and restauration of biodiversity. It provides French expertise to international partners and enriches its own practice through the exchanges of experience with other countries and a concrete peer-to-peer sharing approach.

Name: Naturschutzcenter
Description:
Das Naturschutzcenter ist der Partner für Unternehmen, Gemeinden, Vereine oder öffentliche Einrichtungen in den Bereichen Natur- und Artenschutz.

 Hochwertige und artgerechte Natur- & Tierschutzprodukte
 Nachhaltige Firmengeschenke
 Beratung und Ausstattung naturnaher Firmengelände und öffentlicher Grünflächen
 Über 30-jährige Erfahrung im Natur- und Artenschutz
 Inhabergeführtes Familienunternehmen

Unsere Eigenprodukte zeichnen sich aus durch:
 Regionale Fertigung in Süddeutschland - kurze Transportwege schonen das Klima
 Herstellung in Werkstätten für Menschen mit Behinderung
 Heimisches Massivholz aus nachhaltiger Forstwirtschaft
 Artgerechte Konstruktion - die Tiere und ihre Bedürfnisse stehen im Mittelpunkt

Name: Youth Ambassadors for Climate Justice - YACJ
Center for Collaborative Conservation
Description:
The Center for Collaborative Conservation is active in collaborative conservation efforts across Colorado, the US West and around the world. The most important boundary spanning role of the CCC is bridging the activities and interests of Colorado State University with people who do conservation in communities and local landscapes around the world.

This is LinkedIn account is officially recognized by Colorado State University; however, the views and opinions expressed on this page are not necessarily those of the University. CSU retains discretion to allow or disallow comments and/or posts on this page. For more information about CSU’s Social Media Policy, visit http://www.socialmedia.colostate.edu.

Name: Women in Nature Network (WiNN)
Description:
The Women in Nature Network (WiNN) was founded in 2013 by 14 international conservation leaders with decades of experience in nature conservation and natural resource management.  WiNN was established to build connections between women around the world to enhance their abilities to manage their natural resources and to protect the natural environment. 
WiNN is working to develop a global learning community of women in order to create and support a future generation of environmental leaders.

Name: Action for Climate and Environmental Sustainability
Description: Highly engaged in fighting Climate Change, Biodiversity loss, Waste pollution. Furthermore, We're strongly committed to Community Empowerment and development, Integrated water resources management, Food system transformation and clean energy transition.

Name: NAECO - OCEAN
Description: Nos expéditions à la voile en Méditerranée sont à la fois un laboratoire et un refuge : elles documentent la beauté et la fragilité du monde sous-marin, donnant naissance à des documentaires, séries pédagogiques et créations artistiques. Mais elles sont surtout des espaces de rencontre, où artistes, scientifiques et explorateurs conjuguent leurs talents pour raconter autrement l’océan et éveiller une éco-sensibilité collective.

Name: CSU Salazar Center for North American Conservation
Description:
The Salazar Center sparks conservation innovation and action to secure a thriving future for North America’s lands, waters and the life they support.

We are committed to connecting and uplifting people across the continent in their efforts to conserve the lands and waters that they depend upon and love. Guided by the vision of Ken Salazar—former US Secretary of Interior, US Senator, and Colorado Attorney General—we marshal human resources and ingenuity to create common ground and connectivity across wildlands, rural communities, and urban centers.

Our efforts catalyze 21st-century conservation thinking and doing by:
-Convening great thinkers from across disciplines, sectors, and the continent.
-Investing in compelling ideas, projects, partnerships, and people.
-Amplifying tools, tactics, and stories to enrich and expand the field.

Name: PolinizAÇÃO
Description: The Action Plan for the Conservation and Sustainability of Pollinators – PolinizAÇÃO – is a project whose main objective is to identify specific actions to improve the knowledge and conservation of pollinating insects at the national level, address the causes of their decline, and mobilize and raise awareness in society, based on strategic planning and cooperation at all levels.

Name: GRACE Gorillas
Description:
Located in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DR Congo) near the Tayna Nature Reserve, GRACE is the world’s only sanctuary for rescued Grauer’s gorillas, a critically endangered subspecies found only in eastern DR Congo. Grauer's gorillas have suffered a catastrophic population decline of nearly 80% in the past 20 years.

Despite operating in one of the most remote, challenging areas of the world, GRACE has become an award-winning NGO that the local Congolese community describes as “an oasis of hope.”  

GRACE has 3 program pillars that receive equal focus:
1. Rewilding Grauer’s gorillas: GRACE runs the only sanctuary in the world for critically endangered Grauer’s gorillas rescued from wildlife trafficking. We are trailblazing better ways to release gorillas back into the wild.

2. Protecting forest habitat: GRACE is working to protect 500,000 acres of remote primary tropical forest. This area forms a key habitat for critically endangered Grauer’s gorillas. The Tayna Nature Reserve is a priority habitat for Grauer’s gorilla conservation, with important remaining Grauer’s populations living in the reserve’s boundaries. Already active in Tayna, GRACE is now working to connect this vital habitat to Maiko National Park with a new conservation corridor that establishes land rights for the local communities.

3. Engaging local communities: GRACE practices community-led conservation, including implementing locally-designed solutions, conservation education, sustainable livelihood and development projects, and biodiversity monitoring to foster peaceful coexistence between people and wildlife. Prioritizing the engagement of youth and women in ecological stewardship, GRACE continues to build conservation leadership programs in schools and local community groups.

Name: Darwin Fund
Description:
Our goal is to create a lasting, high-impact, high integrity market for nature that leverages long-term institutional investment in the world’s forests and oceans. 

The Darwin Fund is a blended finance platform and natural capital on-ramp for institutional investors. Ultimately a $100M seed fund dedicated to high-integrity, science-based nature restoration and protection. Engineered to support the conservation or restoration of 60 million acres of rainforest and 100,000 miles of international coastal ocean habitat, and to help protect 5,000 at-risk species of plants and animals in the land and ocean forests of the world.

Name: SymSyn
Description:
SymSyn is building ecological intelligence infrastructure — the missing system required to understand, support, and restore living ecosystems at scale.

Every critical domain of modern society operates on infrastructure: finance, energy, transportation, communications, data. Ecology does not. As a result, conservation remains fragmented, reactive, and difficult to scale. SymSyn exists to close that gap.

We design and deploy persistent, instrumented ecological systems that integrate hardware, sensing, data collection, and artificial intelligence to generate continuous insight into ecosystem health. These systems enable ecological state sensing, cross-site correlation, predictive analysis, and adaptive intervention — transforming habitats from static structures into active participants in ecological recovery.

SymSyn’s platform spans urban environments through remote wilderness, supporting individual participation while enabling NGOs, scientists, and governments to operate with shared intelligence and long-term visibility. Our systems are designed for both temporary deployment and long-lived operation, allowing ecosystems to be monitored, compared, and strengthened over time.

By combining physical habitat architecture, field instrumentation, ecological modeling, and AI-driven analysis, SymSyn makes biodiversity restoration measurable, scalable, and investable. This is not conservation as a project — it is conservation as infrastructure.

Ecological intelligence is no longer optional.
It is inevitable.

Name: HalloGrrroen
Description: HalloGrrroen is een initiatief van Vebego Groen bedrijven en Indicia. Wij geloven in een groene- en gezonde leefomgeving waarin iedereen zijn steentje bijdraagt. Daarom bundelen wij onze krachten om samen met jullie de vergroening van tuinen en de gebouwde omgeving in Nederland te versnellen.

Name: Ngiyambandigay Wajaarr Aboriginal Corporation
Description: Caring for Country

Name: La voix des glaciers
Description: La voix des glaciers est un projet lancé à l’initiative de Camille Etienne et porté par un large collectif de citoyens, d’athlètes, de scientifiques… qui ont pour point commun de vouloir s’engager en faveur de la protection des glaciers et plus largement de nos montagnes. L’objectif de ce projet est de lancer une grande tournée de projections de films permettant d’ouvrir un grand débat sur les territoires de montagnes et les changements vers lesquels transiter pour répondre aux enjeux écologiques. Vous retrouverez sur cette page tout ce qu’il y a à savoir afin de nous rejoindre !

Name: ECO-IMPACT BURKINA
Description: Eco-Impact Burkina est un projet innovant dédié à la production et à la promotion d'emballages biodégradables au Burkina Faso. Il vise à offrir une alternative durable aux plastiques à usage unique, tout en contribuant à la protection de l'environnement et à la création d'emplois locaux. En s'inscrivant dans la transition écologique, Eco-Impact BF sensibilise les communautés aux enjeux de salubrité et soutient la mise en œuvre des lois environnementales, affirmant ainsi son rôle d'acteur engagé pour un Burkina Faso plus propre et plus responsable.

Name: Atlantic Canada Conservation Data Centre
Description: We serve the Atlantic Canadian provinces of New Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island and Newfoundland and Labrador. Our staff gather, maintain, and update spatially-accurate occurrence data for species and ecological communities of conservation concern. The AC CDC's expertise and data represent the single most comprehensive and current source of information about Atlantic Canada's biodiversity.

Name: APRS
Description:
Action to Protect Rural Scotland (APRS) was founded in 1926 and is the only Scottish organisation dedicated exclusively to protecting and enhancing all Scotland’s rural landscapes and countryside, whether at the edge of towns and cities, or in more remote settings.

Despite having such a long history (in fact the National Trust for Scotland was founded out of APRS), we are a tiny organisation with five staff. We campaign with communities, on behalf of all landscapes, from the ones surrounding our towns and cities to the wilder landscapes across rural Scotland.  Our key campaigns are for the deposit return scheme, for a new National Park for Scotland and on Greenbelt protection and policy. We believe that the quality of people’s surroundings is central to their well-being, and that the quality of Scotland’s countryside is of fundamental importance even to those who spend most of their time in towns and cities.  We support communities and individuals to look after and make the most of their local landscapes and we believe that Scotland’s countryside has a key role to play in tackling the climate emergency and addressing the nature crisis.

We work from a small, friendly office which we share with Scottish Environment LINK. All our staff members work flexibly and part time, and we will work to accommodate those with family, caring or other responsibilities.

Name: Kyaninga Forest Foundation
Description: KFF has successfully planted over 120,000 indigenous trees, leading to a dramatic increase in the abundance and biodiversity of many birds, primates, insects, and lizards in the Kyaninga Forest. Similarly, the distribution of trees to local communities has helped reduce the pressure on the remaining forest. However, the Ugandan population continues to rise at an alarming rate and therefore the need to provide sustainable sources of energy and transfer knowledge on climate-smart agricultural practices has become ever more urgent.  To begin to address this KFF is now working with communities across the wider Kabarole, Bundibugyo, Kamwenge, and Ntoroko Districts in Western Uganda to help identify sustainable methods to provide for future generations, whilst maintaining intact and stable ecosystems.

Name: Loziba Wildlife Reserve
Description: Loziba has been established to develop a sustainable conservation economy conservancy, working with our partners, investors, and communities. Our primary mission is to create a 30,000- 40 000 Ha game reserve and sanctuary for iconic species, including Black Rhino, White Rhino, and Elephant by 2027.

Name: Alphonse Foundation
Description: Bringing together key stakeholders for the conservation and restoration of Alphonse and St Francois atolls in harmony with low-impact development and eco-tourism.

Name: Kilchoan Melfort Trust
Description:
Conservation, Spirituality, Education.

We are a conservation initiative that aims to restore biodiversity and set ecosystems onto a recovery path through nature led land management practices, promotion of natural processes, carbon sequestration and maximised ecosystem health.

Our vision is to conserve the dynamic and diverse habitats of the estate. With a community-based bottom-up approach, we focus on the educational aspect and collaboration with a variety of partners and stakeholders to achieve our goals.

Kilchoan Melfort Trust is a registered Charity SC053005

Name: IUCN National Committee UK
Description: IUCN NCUK works as a convening body. We bring together expert IUCN members and facilitate collaborative discussions, sharing information and expertise, to influence nature conservation policy in the UK and beyond

Name: Mercado Net Zero - MNZ
Description: Especialistas em neutralização de gases de efeito estufa. Por meio de créditos de carbono de alta qualidade e certificados internacionalmente, auxiliamos empresas a compensarem suas emissões com educação, transparência e gestão de risco.
Location: Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul

Name: Powered By GeXI (GeoCOMPASS eXplorers Institute)
Description:
GeoCOMPASS Center | GECCO-RANGER COMM. is a globally focused scientific pathfinding & humanitarian peacekeeping initiative that supports various organizations and projects promoting environmental conservation, exploration, research, security, and emergency management.  

GeoCOMPASS Center is founded on the premise that just like a compass helps lost individuals find their way in unfamiliar territory, compassion is the guiding principle to help individuals navigate the complexities of social and environmental issues.  The GeoCOMPASS token symbolizes a commitment to safeguarding global ecosystems, emphasizing kindness and understanding, and making choices that create a better future.
GeoCOMPASS Center is founded on the premise that just like a compass helps lost individuals find their way in unfamiliar territory, compassion is the guiding principle to help individuals navigate the complexities of social and environmental issues.  The GeoCOMPASS token symbolizes a commitment to safeguarding global ecosystems, emphasizing kindness and understanding, and making choices that create a better future.

Name: EcoPlast BENIN
Description: Transformer les déchets plastiques en solutions durables.  Recyclage, innovation, durabilité.

Name: LIFE AMooRe - Austrian Moor Restoration
Description: LIFE AMooRe ist ein strategisches Natur-Projekt (SNaP) unter dem EU-Förderprogramm LIFE, das die Umsetzung der Moorstrategie Österreich 2030+ unterstützt. Es ist erst das zweite integrierte LIFE-Projekt und zugleich auch das bislang größte Moorprojekt in Österreich.

Name: The Eye Above NPC
Description:
The Eye Above (Tihlo Henhla) is a non-profit organization revolutionizing wildlife protection in Southern Africa. Our name, from the Tsonga phrase for "Eye Above," defines our mission: to provide an intelligent, aerial defense layer for endangered species.

Our Mission: Anti-Poaching & Tech Innovation
Our core focus is on combating wildlife crime, specifically rhino poaching, in the region's most challenging environments.

We achieve this using custom, cutting-edge Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS), or drones. These aren't standard aircraft, they are specialized, fixed-wing Vertical Take-off and Landing (VTOL) drones, custom-built for the African bush. This allows for rapid, autonomous deployment anywhere, maximizing airtime where it matters most.

Our systems are powered by AI programming to enhance real-time surveillance, ensuring high-efficiency threat detection and tracking of illegal activities.

Dual Impact: Conservation & Community
Our work generates a powerful dual impact:

Protecting Wildlife: We provide critical aerial monitoring for species like rhinos and support habitat preservation for others, collaborating with partners such as the Endangered Wildlife Trust (EWT) and the International Crane Foundation (ICF).

Creating Opportunity: We actively foster sustainable growth by training and employing rural community members, providing them with skilled jobs in drone operation and maintenance.

We believe the future of conservation lies in a powerful coalition of innovative technology and empowered people, making us an unstoppable force in the fight to protect our natural heritage.

Name: Fish On
Description:
We are working to protect the ocean so fishing communities have access to a healthy and thriving coast.

Our current priorities are: 
 Common-sense fishery management
 Clean water
 Equitable access
 Restoring ecosystems through protection

Name: Tubarões da Baía da Ilha Grande
Description: Conhecer para proteger é nosso lema de trabalho! O Projeto se dedica à pesquisa e conservação dos tubarões da Baía da Ilha Grande, um dos maiores hotspots de biodiversidade marinha da costa brasileira, buscando o diálogo permanente com as comunidades locais. É executado pelo Instituto Brasileiro de Conservação da Natureza - IBRACON em parceria com a Petrobras através do Programa Petrobras Socioambiental.
Location: Angra dos Reis, Rio de Janeiro

Name: Bur Oak Land Trust
Description: Bur Oak Land Trust is a nonprofit focused on biodiversity, protecting eastern Iowa's land and wildlife, and connecting people to nature.

Name: Toteme.tn
Description:
Want to be a planet protector? Discover how to shop without plastic bags and more with Tote me. 

The new Tunisian brand offering eco-friendly bags . Made from environmentally friendly materials, these bags are a perfect alternative to traditional plastic bags . They're durable and reusable, meaning you can use them over and over again, helping to reduce plastic waste .

Name: Quoll Society of Australia Inc
Description:
Established in 2017, our mission at the Quoll Society is centered on safeguarding the four Australian species of quoll, with a primary focus on the Spotted-tailed Quoll (Dasyurus maculatus). Through collaborative efforts with various partners, including National Parks staff, local councils, private landholders, our generous members, and our dedicated team of volunteers (or Quoll-unteers, as we fondly call them), we engage in comprehensive research and conservation initiatives. Our aim is to deepen our understanding of quolls, address threats to their survival, and restore crucial habitat that benefits not only quolls but also numerous other threatened species.

Given that many Australians are unfamiliar with quolls, a significant aspect of our mission is to raise public awareness about these extraordinary marsupials. Australia's four quoll species are unique, serving as the largest native marsupial predators on the mainland, second only to their Tasmanian cousin, the Tasmanian Devil. Regrettably, all four of these carnivorous species face the threat of extinction without intervention, a fate akin to their larger cousin, the Tasmanian Tiger. 

At the Quoll Society of Australia, our team of dedicated scientists and volunteers are committed to ensuring the preservation of these species. A world without quolls is one we strive to prevent, and with your support, we can make this vision a reality.

Name: Aves Aware
Description:
Aves is using the power of art, education and community action to help save the birds we love.

We've been inspired by the beauty of birds and the way they bring joy to our lives. But we know that birds are facing increased threats from habitat loss and food scarcity. Their decline is a warning sign for the health of our planet.

Our vision is to create a world where birds and their habitats are valued, protected and thriving. 

The Aves mission is to celebrate the unique beauty of the birds we love, raise awareness about their crucial role in our ecosystems, and champion conservation efforts to protect birds.

Name: International Society of Ethnobiology Congress
Description: The International Society of Ethnobiology (ISE), founded in 1988, is a global, collaborative network of individuals and organisations working to preserve vital links between human societies and the natural world.

Name: 𝖲𝗒𝗆𝗉𝗈𝗌𝗂𝗎𝗆 𝗉𝗈𝗎𝗋 𝗅𝖺 𝖦𝖾𝗌𝗍𝗂𝗈𝗇 𝖽𝖾𝗌 𝖣é𝖼𝗁𝖾𝗍𝗌 𝖾𝗇 𝖠𝖿𝗋𝗂𝗊𝗎𝖾
Description:
𝐔𝐧 𝐦𝐨𝐮𝐯𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐩𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐮𝐧𝐞 𝐀𝐟𝐫𝐢𝐪𝐮𝐞 𝐩𝐥𝐮𝐬 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐫𝐞 𝐞𝐭 𝐩𝐥𝐮𝐬 𝐛𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐞
La gestion des déchets est l’un des plus grands défis de notre époque. Face à une urbanisation galopante et à la montée des problèmes de santé et d’environnement, nous croyons qu’il est temps d’agir. Notre mission est simple mais puissante : éveiller les consciences, changer les comportements et bâtir ensemble des villes plus propres, plus sûres et plus agréables à vivre.

 𝗘𝗻𝘀𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗹𝗲, 𝗯â𝘁𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗹’𝗮𝘃𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗿
Notre initiative rassemble chercheurs, entrepreneurs, décideurs, entreprises, étudiants et citoyens autour d’un même idéal : transformer les déchets en opportunités et créer une Afrique plus verte, plus innovante et plus solidaire. Ici, chaque voix compte et chaque action contribue à dessiner un futur durable, où la propreté devient un héritage pour les générations à venir.

Name: The Yambone Project
Description: Yambone is community based conservation initiative based in Chipanje Chetu, Niassa Province, Mozambique.

Name: Findhorn, Nairn & Lossie Rivers Trust
Description:
The Findhorn, Nairn and Lossie Rivers Trust is a place-based nature restoration organisation, uniquely positioned to play a key role in improving local resilience in the face of the nature and climate emergencies.

We work at a catchment-scale, and in partnership with landowners and land managers, our local communities and businesses, blending sources of funding and delivering action to protect and restore our rivers, their watersheds, and the wildlife which inhabits them.

Name: COSOREMA; Conservación Sostenible de los Recursos Marinos y Acuáticos A.C.
Description: Mantener los ecosistemas marinos y las comunidades que dependen de ellos, a través de soluciones basadas en aspectos sociales, económicos y ambientales.

Name: Blue Carbon International
Description:
We protect ocean ecosystems at scale. Our team of world-class scientists, economists and policy makers operate at the forefront of science, nature, and sustainable development. With innovative, pre-validated, and proprietary science-based finance offerings, BCI empowers its partners to achieve holistic value for the ocean’s critical ecosystem services.

Proud to be part of the Beneath The Waves Group.

Name: Partenariat France-UICN
Description: Instrument de la politique de coopération internationale de la France, le Partenariat France-UICN joue, depuis 2005, un rôle clé dans le renforcement de l’action de l'UICN, en permettant de lancer des initiatives et des programmes novateurs qui n'auraient pu être mis en œuvre par des voies habituelles de financement. Ils jouent un rôle unique en donnant une impulsion aux travaux des réseaux scientifiques, techniques et des spécialistes des politiques de l'Union, et contribuent à promouvoir l’expertise française et francophone dans toutes les instances et les commissions thématiques de l'UICN.

Name: National Conservation Foundation
Description:
The mission of the National Conservation Foundation (NCF) is to educate and prepare conservation leaders for the sustainability and regeneration of our natural resources. The National Conservation Foundation cultivates, educates, and inspires conservation leaders of all ages.

The National Conservation Foundation does this by providing conservation districts and other conservation organizations funding for research, educational programs, and public outreach. In fulfilling its mission, NCF has two programs that work to develop the next generation of conservation leaders: the NCF-Envirothon competition, a high school environmental and natural resources program and competition, and the Next Generation Leadership Institute, a new program to cultivate the next generation of locally-led conservation leaders.

Name: Ausiris
Description:
AUSIRIS est une union associative regroupant Samuel Vincent, Angéline Cavalié, association Coste et le CPEAGL. 
Ces associations, à but non lucratif, qui partagent les mêmes valeurs, se regroupent dans cette structure commune d’organisation, de gestion, de promotion et de développement.
Elles attendent mener à bien des travaux de recherches ciblés sur les questions sociales et sociétales de notre époque, tout en disposant d’une capacité de veille au profit des associations membres, des politiques sociales, des populations et demeurer un interlocuteur pertinent et écouté.

SAMUEL VINCENT développe ses actions en direction du secteur Enfance – Famille, en partenariat avec les différentes instances nationales, départementales et locales qui concourent aux missions de prévention, de protection, d’éducation, d’animation et d’actions sociales.

ANGELINE CAVALIE souhaite apporter des réponses adaptées aux différents besoins du public et du territoire, afin que l’accompagnement éducatif et social s’adapte à la situation singulière de chaque famille, de chaque enfant, adolescent, jeune adulte.

L'ASSOCIATION COSTE accompagne 186 enfants et familles au sein de ses deux services de Protection de l’Enfance installés sur le Sud et l’Est du Gard.
Elle compte une Maison d’Enfants à Caractère Social, un service E.R.A., un Pôle Thérapeutique et un centre de formation. 

Le Comité de Protection de l'Enfance et de l'Adolescence Gard Lozère est une association laïque qui  œuvre dans l’intérêt des droits de l’enfant et de sa famille. Elle contribue à l’intérêt général et à la construction d’une société solidaire, durable et participative. Elle vise à lutter contre les inégalités, et à l’émergence d’une autre économie, nécessaire à une société plus solidaire.

Name: WildFlight ME
Description:
WildFlight Middle East are leaders in managing invasive species, we utilize eco-friendly and humane solutions to control pest birds in and around urban areas. Our services includes:

- Humane and live animal trapping 
- Wildlife control
- Pest Control Services

Name: Projecto Kitabanga
Description: O Projecto Kitabanga é um projecto de Estudo e Conservação de Tartarugas Marinhas, desenhado e implementado desde 2003 pelo Departamento de Biologia da Faculdade de Ciências Naturais da Universidade Agostinho Neto, em parceria com a Fundação Kissama, e mais recentemente com a Universidade do Namibe, e conta com a anuência do Ministério do Ambiente.

Name: Global Entomology Coalition
Description:
Our mission is to inspire worldwide environmental stewardship by fostering a cultural shift in the perception of insects through research, education, and conservation. 
By developing collaboration across borders, opening research to the public, and expanding our understanding of insects and their roles through exciting educational experiences, we can accelerate entomological knowledge and open the door to safeguarding our planet’s most vital stewards.

GEC is a nonprofit, tax-exempt charitable organization under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.

Name: Reef Renewal Curacao
Description:
The mission of Reef Renewal Foundation Curaçao is to protect and restore coral reefs in Curaçao and around the world. We seek to train, engage, and inspire the local and international community through volunteering, educational events, and outreach. We are working to develop new and innovative ways to restore reefs with the support of research collaborators and then sharing these methods with restoration projects worldwide. We aim to demonstrate that through combined efforts, there is still hope for coral reefs.

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/reefrenewalcuracao/
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/reefrenewalcuracao/

Name: vdSande bosbouw en natuurbeheer
Description: vdSande levert een breed scala aan diensten in terreinbeheer. Van onderzoek en ontwerp, tot aan de voorbereiding en uitvoering van werkzaamheden. Breed georiënteerd en creatief in oplossingen, passend maatwerk voor een duurzame toekomst van de landelijke omgeving. Vakkundig en met oog voor de praktijk.

Name: Consortium for the Conservation of the Atlantic Humpback Dolphin
Description:
The Consortium for the Conservation of the Atlantic Humpback Dolphin (CCAHD) Foundation acts as an umbrella organization that brings together international conservation management bodies, cetacean scientists from around the world, and organizations and individuals in the countries where the Critically Endangered Atlantic humpback dolphin is found. The CCAHD Foundation’s aim is to promote and support research and conservation actions throughout the species’ range, and provide robust conservation advice on the Atlantic humpback dolphin.

Our Mission Statement is: Working towards the long-term sustainability of Atlantic humpback dolphin (Sousa teuszii) populations and their habitats through research, awareness, capacity-building and action.

Name: ONG INADER
Description: Institut d'Appui pour le Développement et le Renforcement de Capacités INADER en abrégé, est une organisation non gouvernementale qui mène des projets de développement à fort impact social. Parmi les actions, un accent particulier est mis sur la préservation et la protection de l'environnement.

Name: Mangal Festival
Description: Led by the Collectif 5 Deltas with the support of the PAPBio programme, the Mangal Festival is a multi-country event whose main objective is to participate in the creation of a community aware of conservation issues and committed to the development of mangrove ecosystems in West Africa. To achieve this, a series of scientific, technical, artistic, educational and cultural events will be programmed during the project to explore the different facets of mangrove ecosystems. All the productions will be shared around a regional event that knows how to mix different audiences.

Name: HELP Congo
Description:
HELP Congo est une ONG située au Congo-Brazzaville qui a pour but de protéger et de conserver l’incroyable biodiversité du Parc National de Conkouati-Douli, inscrit sur la liste indicative de l'UNESCO.

Notre vision : être un acteur significatif et exemplaire de la conservation de la biodiversité du Parc National de Conkouati-Douli.

Nos missions : 

• Établir des partenariats solides (gestionnaires du Parc, conservateur, autres associations de protection de la biodiversité, population locale ...) pour obtenir des résultats de conservation efficaces.

• Impulser et réaliser des projets de recherche scientifique et des suivis écologiques.
• Veiller au bien-être des chimpanzés relâchés sur les îles. 
• Promouvoir la connaissance et la pratique professionnelle en matière de conservation et de recherche.
• Diffuser les connaissances pour faire évoluer les comportements vers une prise de conscience environnementale.
• Mettre en place des programmes de développement socio-économique en faveur des communautés locales.

Nous proposons des séjours d'écotourisme pour faire découvrir la biodiversité du parc et l'importance du travail de conservation. L'intégralité des bénéfices servent les projets de l'association.

Help Congo est un programme soutenu par Beauval Nature.

Name: Verein «Natur & Freizeit»
Description:
Der Verein «Natur & Freizeit» hat zum Ziel, öffentliche, auf ein breites Zielpublikum ausgerichtete Kampagnen durchzuführen, die Erholungssuchende und Sportaktive zu einem natur- und umweltschonenden Verhalten motivieren. Er fördert die Vernetzung von Akteuren aus den Bereichen Natur und Freizeit, trägt zur Entschärfung von Konflikten bei und erhöht die Verfügbarkeit von Kompetenzen, Standards sowie hochwertigen Leistungen und Produkten. 
L’association «Nature & Loisirs» a pour but de motiver les adeptes d’activités récréatives et de sport à adopter un comportement respectueux de la nature et de l’environnement, en menant des campagnes ciblées, adressées à un large public. Elle encourage la mise en réseau d’acteurs dans les domaines de la nature et des loisirs, contribue à la résolution de conflits et améliore la disponibilité de compétences, de standards ainsi que de prestations et de produits à haute valeur ajoutée.

Name: Patagonia Land Conservation
Description: WE STRIVE TO PROMOTE SUSTAINABLE LAND USE PRACTICES IN THE TOURISM, FOOD, AND REAL ESTATE SECTORS, TO ENSURE THE LONG-TERM VIABILITY OF THESE INDUSTRIES, AND TO CREATE JOB OPPORTUNITIES PROTECTING AND PRESERVING NATURAL HABITATS AND ECOSYSTEMS FOR FUTURE GENERATIONS.

Description: We want to return our planet to its green diversity in environmental protection, to maintain the diversity, beauty and health of our planet, and to pass on a livable environment to future generations. For this reason, we protect important biotopes and reforest forests.

Name: Akatia
Description:
Akatia is an Ivorian NGO founded in 2017 by Sarah Crawford and Estelle Raballand with the aim of preserving chimpanzees, which then evolved into the preservation of all primate species.
The government of Côte d'Ivoire has been carrying out law enforcement actions for several years to prevent illegal wildlife trade. Unfortunately, before the creation of Akatia, there were no suitable facilities for the care of orphaned primates, and the government was therefore unable to pursue traffickers.
As part of a historic agreement, Akatia was allocated two forests protected by the Ministry of Water and Forests: Yapo-Abbe (4,500 ha) and Comoe 1 (1,200 ha). Akatia established two sanctuaries there, marking Akatia the only organization with two primate sanctuaries in two separate forests. This unique arrangement improves the protection of wild populations of chimpanzees and primates, while conserving the rich biodiversity of the Comoé 1 and Yapo-Abbe forests, and includes the wild chimpanzee group.

Name: Ethiopian Wildlife Conservation Think Tank
Description: The Ethiopian wildlife conservation think thank (EWC-TT), was founded by a group of Ethiopian senior and renowned wildlife experts with the objective of providing technical expertise and advice to the government, non-government and private sectors.

Name: Squam Lakes Association
Description: The Squam Lakes Association is a non-profit dedicated to conserving for public benefit the natural beauty, peaceful character, and resources of the Squam Watershed. In collaboration with local and state partners the SLA promotes the protection, careful use, and shared enjoyment of the lakes, mountains, forests, open spaces, and wildlife of the Squam Lakes region.

Name: À haute voix
Description:
À haute voix est une association oeuvrant à la préservation des espaces naturels de montagne. Nous organisons et facilitons des rencontres en refuges entre des amoureux de montagne et des experts de ce milieu.

L'objectif étant de donner aux participants une vision globale des enjeux de gestion et de protection des massifs. 

Ces rendez-vous ont lieu au sein de refuges gardés accessibles. 
Ce moment d’apprentissage est à la fois convivial et participatif grâce à un format engageant comprenant :

1. Une marche d’approche avec un groupe à taille humaine
2. L’intervention d’un(e) expert(e) qui partage ses connaissances via des formats ludiques.
3. Un dîner convivial en présence de l’intervenant et des autres participants.
4. En fonction de la date selectionnée, une nuitée en refuge ou une descente à la frontale après le dîner.

Name: Lafayette Wetlands Partnership
Description:
Lafayette Wetlands Partnership hosts meetings and volunteer events to restore and maintain the watershed with stakeholders, neighbors and friends

Attendance is free at monthly meetings in the Ernie Morgan Center between the Virginia Zoological Park & Lafayette Park. The meeting can also be attended virtually or by phone

We nurture partnerships among citizens, municipal, scientific and non-profit groups to get restoration work done

When the work is done, we insist upon a commitment to the long-term stewardship of those restored wetlands

Our credo is "no one stands alone" – we will do whatever is needed to help people realize their dreams, including getting in the mud to clean up debris, install plants and grasses, or control invasive species

To learn more please sign up on our website!

Name: Urku Estudios Amazónicos
Description:
Somos una Organización sin fines de lucro que desde el año 2000 promueve proyectos e innovaciones justas, con identidad y armónicas con la naturaleza en la Amazonía Peruana.

En alianza con las organizaciones de pueblos indígenas y otras asociaciones civiles que le acompañan, han implementado más de 25 proyectos I + D + i en las áreas temáticas de la identidad, derechos y fortalecimiento de los pueblos originarios, conservación de la biodiversidad, agricultura ecológica y cambio climático; alcanzando involucrar y contribuir al desarrollo de las competencias de más de 5 mil personas y 230 mil beneficiarios indirectos, desde que inició sus acciones en el año 2000.

MUI consists of staff from the resort, as well as its three partner NGOs: The Manta Trust, Blue Marine Foundation and The Olive Ridley Project, all of whom work together to reach research, guest education and community outreach goals.

Name: NEMO IMS
Description:
NEMO IMS (NEguentropic MOney Internatioanl Monetary System)  is a global economic proposal aiming to reform monetary, banking, and financial institutions to achieve ecological transition, post-growth, and to ensure the conditions for life on Earth. The idea is to transform the extractive global economy into a sustainable and life-respecting model, moving away from extractive economic competition towards a cooperative model without sacrificing social progress and the Human Development Index.

NEMO SMI suggests that central banks coordinate around a new global exchange standard, the "NEMO Exchange Standard," which would free us from the constraints inherent in the current extractive model based on the dollar or key currencies (competition, productivism, external constraint).

It proposes the creation of new global economic institutions to accelerate the ecological transition process. The GAIA Economic Symposium would aim to determine global common interest missions and democratically define revenues labeled as "NEMO Green DTS." These would be convertible into national currencies by central banks for service provider companies.

In this way, the central currency is backed by globally consensus-labeled common good projects. This central currency then serves as a monetary regulation for the issuance of credit money by the banks.

Thus, nations obtain their liquidity by engaging in regenerative activities and protecting commons and are encouraged to use these funds in exchanges for goods and services with minimal harm.

Name: Chyulu Hills REDD+ Carbon Project
Description:

They include Forest Services (KFS), Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS), Maasai Wilderness Conservation Trust (MWCT), Big Life Foundation, David Sheldrick Wildlife trust, and four Maasai group ranches including Mbirikani, Kuku, Kuku A and Rombo.

Currently, WildCats supports projects in 5 tiger range countries within Asia. It works with implementing partners whose projects are regularly and independently reviewed providing supporters with the confidence that their money is being used effectively.

Name: myreforest
Description:
Wir möchten unsere heimischen Wälder als Lebensgrundlage erhalten und aufforsten, die Natur im Kampf gegen den Klimawandel unterstützen und die wichtigen Aufgaben des Waldes stärker im Bewusstsein der Menschen verankern.

_______

Was wir anbieten: AUFFORSTUNG VON WALDFLÄCHEN (z. B. für Unternehmen)  ||  TEAM-EVENTS & WISSENSVERMITTLUNG (z. B. für Betriebe, Vereine oder Schulklassen)  ||  EINZEL-BAUMSPENDEN & BAUMGESCHENKE

_______

Wir organisieren die Wiederaufforstung von Brachflächen in unseren heimischen Wäldern. Aus vorhandenen Schadflächen soll ein gesunder, zukunftsfähiger Mischwald werden. Wir unterstützen dabei kleine Waldbauern mit der Hilfe von Spendern und Sponsoren und ermöglichen gleichzeitig die persönliche Teilnahme an den Aufforstungsprojekten.  

Wir arbeiten regional, nachhaltig und transparent.

Name: National Biodiversity Centre, Bhutan
Description:
National Biodiversity Centre under the Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock coordinates biodiversity conservation and sustainable use in Bhutan.
Bhutan became party to the international Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) in 1995 after ratification of the Convention by the 73rd session of the National Assembly. Subsequently, Bhutan developed the first Biodiversity Action Plan (BAP) in 1998 – a guiding policy document for conservation and sustainable use of biological resources of the country. Concurrently, the National Biodiversity Centre was instituted in 1998 under the Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock as an coordinating and implementing agency of the CBD and to coordinate biodiversity conservation and sustainable use programs in the country.

Vision: Effective conservation, sustainable utilization, and equitable sharing of benefits arising from access and use of biological resources.

Mandates:

1. To coordinate and implement biodiversity conservation and sustainable use programs in the country.
2. To coordinate and implement obligations under regional and international conventions, treaties, and protocols related to biodiversity.
3. To develop policies and legal frameworks for conservation and sustainable use of biological resources.
4. To serve as the national repository for germplasm and botanical collections.
5. To serve as the national focal agency to regulate access to biological resources of the country, ensuring equitable sharing of the benefits arising from access.
6. To serve as the national focal agency for bioprospecting and documentation of traditional knowledge associated with biological resources.
7. To serve as the national clearinghouse for biodiversity information.
8. To promote regional and international institutional linkages and collaboration for technology transfer, technical capacity enhancement, and collaborative research in the field of biodiversity.

Name: WCS Gabon Program
Description: WCS Gabon est le programme gabonais de WCS (Wildlife Conservation Society), une organisation non gouvernementale (ONG) américaine dont l'objectif est la préservation de la nature (flore et faune) dans le monde.

EuropeForNature invites experts across Europe to publish 
(1) a story/post on their own social media account and/or 
(2) on the website of their own organisation. 
We also ask experts to 
(3) submit these stories to the central website www.europefornature.eu. 
Lastly, 
(4) please use hashtag #EuropeForNature when reposting green, just, and/or climate-smart stories, and like as many of them as you want

Name: Corals for Conservation (C4C)
Description:
Corals for Conservation (C4C develops strategies and techniques for promoting coral-focused climate change adaptation.  These strategies, which focus on propagating bleaching-resistant corals within locally managed marine areas, are used to train reef dependent communities, conservation officers, and tourism professionals to take an active role in fighting coral reef decline in the face of climate change.  

More on www.corals4conservation.org.

Name: Eco-Jipe Program
Description:
About Us 

Welcome to Eco Jipe Program, an NGO dedicated to environmental conservation, sustainable development, and community empowerment in Kenya’s Taita-Taveta region. We work with local communities, government bodies, and international partners to create lasting positive change, aligning our efforts with the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

 Our Story

Established through CoalitionWILD in collaboration with the WILD Foundation and Zacharia Mutinda, Eco Jipe Program aims to tackle critical environmental challenges and promote sustainable livelihoods. Our team of environmentalists, researchers, and community leaders works to enhance education, implement sustainable practices, and support alternative livelihoods, fostering a harmonious relationship between humans and nature.

 Mission

Our mission is to operate environmental, health, waste management, safety, and sanitation awareness, protection, and conservation programs through educational sessions, webinars, workshops, and research.

 Vision

To be a leading force in environmental conservation and sustainable development in the Taita-Taveta region, fostering a harmonious relationship between humans and nature.

 Our Impact

Eco Jipe Program has made significant strides in environmental conservation and community development. Our achievements include enhanced education and awareness, sustainable fishing practices, promotion of alternative livelihoods, strengthened community involvement, and regulatory compliance for long-term sustainability.

Contact us at info@ecojipeprogram.org

Name: Restitute Art Conservation Studio
Description:
Restitute Art Conservation Studio è un laboratorio specializzato nella conservazione e nel restauro di opere d’arte, nato dall’esperienza e dalla visione condivisa di Silvia Tagliante e Jole Botti, restauratrici professioniste abilitate (ai sensi del D.Lgs. 42/2004 settori 3,4 e 5)

Il nostro lavoro è rivolto a musei, fondazioni, gallerie, collezionisti privati e istituzioni culturali che cercano un partner affidabile e competente per valorizzare e proteggere il proprio patrimonio.

Ci distinguiamo per un approccio scientifico e altamente personalizzato, fondato su osservazione, studio, prevenzione e trasparenza.


 Ogni opera ha una storia. La nostra missione è proteggerla, rispettarla e renderla fruibile nel tempo.
Grazie a un metodo che unisce ricerca accademica, operatività sul campo e attenzione alle specificità dell’opera, interveniamo nel pieno rispetto dell’integrità materiale ed estetica dei beni, con soluzioni su misura e materiali compatibili e reversibili.
Offriamo servizi su tutto il ciclo di vita conservativo:

• Valutazioni preventive e consulenze

• Condition Report dettagliati

• Supervisione di allestimenti e movimentazioni

• Restauri conservativi su opere antiche e contemporanee

• Manutenzioni periodiche e inventariazione

• Imballaggi con MarvelSeal per prestiti museali

• Piani di emergenza e trattamenti biocidi

Crediamo nella conservazione consapevole, nella collaborazione con il cliente e nella formazione continua. Ogni intervento è un’occasione per costruire cultura e proteggere bellezza.

Se cerchi restauratrici competenti e appassionate per la cura delle tue opere, scrivici. Saremo felici di ascoltarti e trovare insieme la soluzione più adatta.

Name: Arbre Team
Description: Arbre Team somos un movimiento de ecología práctica que queremos concienciar sobre la necesidad de cuidar nuestros bosques, generando un impacto positivo en empresas y en la gente para que tomen conciencia de que se pueden hacer cosas útiles para cuidar del medioambiente.

Name: A Rocha Kenya
Description:
A Rocha Kenya is a Christian conservation organization that is part of larger International Network of A Rocha organizations in 20 countries around the world. 

Our mission is to make God’s love for His world known by demonstrating how to practically care for it through scientific research, advocacy, environmental education and sustainable community-based conservation programs. 

By working with the local communities, A Rocha Kenya aims to achieve the long-term conservation of threatened habitats and species. We are restoring habitats, educating the community, and empowering people to be agents and stewards of conservation.

Name: Chitral Gol Community Development and Conservation Association
Description:
Chitral Gol Community Development and Conservation Association was founded in 2005 by members of 12 Village Conservation Committees (VCC) & 11 WVCCs under the auspices of WWF, Chitral. Members of the association have long been striving to conserve and ensure sustainable use of available natural resources in Chitral Gol National Park since 1992. With the advent of Protected Areas Management Project, a GEF funded Project; all the villages around CGNP were formally organized in to VCC to ensure active participation of local communities in the park management efforts.

Later on with the aim of synchronize the efforts of all VCCs regarding sustainable management of the park resources a cluster body of these VCCs was established, which formally named as Chitral Gol Community Development and Conservation Association(CGCDCA) in 2005. Since then, the association is actively participating in conservation efforts and skill development activities undertaken by PAMP-CGNP and other organization. It has been registered under companies’ act 1984 as well as with Social Welfare Department Chitral under registration No.758 during November 2009.  CGCDCA show interest in YDC due to its expertise’s in youth development activities and have train human resource in this filed and running its youth centers from 2014.

Name: CG Randi Randi
Description:
Somos la Corporación Grupo Randi Randi, una organización no gubernamental ecuatoriana, privada y sin fines de lucro, con sede en la ciudad de Quito, provincia de Pichincha. Adoptamos la expresión kichwa "randi randi" – en español "dando y dando" – como lema, reflejando el sentido de reciprocidad que impulsa nuestro trabajo diario.

Nos especializamos en investigación y asesoramiento técnico, centrándonos en la planificación participativa para el manejo y conservación de los recursos naturales. Nuestro enfoque abarca dimensiones clave como el género y el cambio climático, reconociendo la importancia de abordar estos aspectos en la construcción de un futuro sostenible.

Ofrecemos servicios que van desde la investigación-acción participativa hasta el asesoramiento en planificación a diversas escalas. Nos destacamos por nuestro apoyo en innovación y gestión del conocimiento, impulsando soluciones que equilibren la conservación ambiental con el progreso social.

Únete a nosotros en nuestro compromiso con la sostenibilidad, la equidad y el cambio. Randi Randi: dando y dando por un mundo mejor.

Name: URAPEG FNE GUADELOUPE
Description:
Créée en 1983 à l’initiative de M. Jacques PORTECOP, M. Gérard WACTER, M. Edouard BENITO-ESPINAL et M. Yves JASOR, l’Union Régionale des Associations du Patrimoine de l’Environnement de la Nature et du Cadre de vie de la Guadeloupe est une association type loi 1901. Elle est le porte-parole de nombreuses associations regroupées au sein d'organisations dont le domaine est lié à la protection de l’environnement et de la nature.

Les missions : 

- Sensibiliser et informer le grand public et les décideurs sur les enjeux environnementaux

- Organiser des manifestations, des formations afin de toucher l’ensemble de la population tout en faisant remonter leurs avis auprès des administrations concernées

Name: AJED-PLANÈTE
Description:
Youth action for the environment and the planet development 
Le mouvement (Actions des Jeunes pour l'Environnement et le Développement de la planète (AJED-PLANETE), est une plateforme réunissant la jeunesse du continent africain en générale et celle de la République Démocratique du Congo en particulier, autour des grandes questions relatives à la dégradation des conditions de vie sur la planète. le but poursuivit est celui d'apporter des solutions durables aux problèmes environnementaux qui affectent négativement la planète et de répondre de manière rationnelle aux impératifs du développement durable, au moyen des actions et initiatives des jeunes. 
|•| Mission|•| 
Faire de la jeunesse la première ligne de la résilience protectrice de l’environnement pour un développement durable.
 |•| secteurs d'intervention |•| 
Education environnemental :
