Name: La Ruta de las Alas
Description:
Es una iniciativa de conservación que protege la biodiversidad mediante soluciones innovadoras y sostenibles. Su enfoque principal es el desarrollo de bancos de hábitat, una herramienta estratégica que permite la preservación de ecosistemas clave, cumpliendo al mismo tiempo con los requerimientos de la compensación ambiental, tanto obligatoria como voluntaria.

El proyecto está enfocado en la protección del hábitat del murciélago insectívoro Saccopteryx antioquensis, conocido como Murciélago de Sacos Alares Antioqueño, una especie endémica de Colombia que se encuentra en peligro crítico de extinción. A través del trabajo conjunto entre ciencia, territorio y comunidad, La Ruta de las Alas impulsa soluciones concretas para conservar esta especie y otros componentes de la biodiversidad asociados a su ecosistema.

Con la creación del primer banco de hábitat para murciélagos en América Latina, el proyecto propone un modelo de cooperación multisectorial que articula esfuerzos públicos, privados y comunitarios para generar impactos reales en la conservación de la fauna y flora, así como en el bienestar social y económico de las comunidades locales.

Más que una iniciativa ambiental, La Ruta de las Alas es una estrategia replicable que transforma la manera en que nos relacionamos con la naturaleza, la biodiversidad y el territorio.

Name: Rotary Fellowship of Wildlifers for Conservation Trinidad and Tobago Chapter
Description:
TOGETHER WE MAKE PROTECTION OF WILDLIFE, A WAY OF LIFE!

Introducing the Rotary Fellowship of Wildlifers for Conservation Trinidad and Tobago Chapter (Rotary District 7030)! As part of the esteemed Global Fellowship - Rotary Fellowship of Wildlifers for Conservation (RFWC) - which originated in Rotary District 3190 (Bangalore, India), the Trinidad and Tobago Chapter has recently emerged to join the global community of dedicated Rotarians, Rotaractors, and other passionate individuals. This Global Fellowship gained official recognition from Rotary International on 5th January, 2021.

The Trinidad and Tobago Chapter brings together individuals from diverse backgrounds who share a common interest in wildlife conservation. Members of the board are as follows:

    President: Maria Mohammed-Maharaj (Rotary Club of St Augustine);
    Secretary: Professor Emeritus Gary Garcia, The University of the West Indies, St Augustine Campus;
    Executive Member: Ricardo Hospedales, Agriculture Education Specialist;
    Executive Member: Veronica De Souza-Phillip (Rotary Club of Port of Spain West);
    Executive Member: Tanya Western (Rotary Club of Central Port of Spain).

The Rotary Fellowship of Wildlifers for Conservation serves as a platform for individuals who are passionate about fostering a harmonious future for both humanity and nature. Together, we strive to create a world in which both people and the environment can thrive.

Interested in joining? Go to: https://www.rotaryfellowshipforwildlife.org/join

Rotary Fellowship of Wildlifers for Conservation Global

    Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rotarywildlifers
    Instagram: @rotarywildlife

Rotary Fellowship of Wildlifers for Conservation - Trinidad and Tobago Chapter

    Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rotarywildliferstrinidadandtobago
    Instagram: @rotarywildliferstt

Listing of Charter Members: https://tinyurl.com/4mxmmduw

Name: OPAM
Description:
OPAM innovative voluntary carbon credit market platform, offering environmental solutions while meeting the growing demand for sustainability. This unique initiative will meet the needs of the African and global market in terms of carbon offsetting.

Joining the OPAM program means committing to creating a positive impact, combining economic profitability and environmental responsibility to build a more sustainable future for all.

Name: Kumbe Biodiversity
Description: Kumbe provides transparent, innovative solutions for biodiversity monitoring, delivered on-site and by local communities.

Name: Kenya Marine Mammal Research And Conservation
Description:
Our Mission and Vision
To advance understanding of marine mammal species in our Kenyan waters, through public participation and consistent research, developing ecotourism and economic potentials, and fostering conservation values. For the mutual benefit of coastal communities and for the health of marine mammal populations.
Vision – To reduce the threats to dolphin, whale and dugong along the Kenya coast and create a harmonious marine and coastal environment for people and marine life placing a value on the lives of marine mammals, and emphasis on alternative incomes for communities.

Objectives

1.	To expand our research conservation and education programs along the coast to include remote areas
2.	To gather information on vulnerable inshore species and especially those that are IUCN Red List Endangered. 
3.	Exploring remote areas, where there is scant information about populations and distribution, using visual surveys and acoustic monitoring.
4.	To incorporate preventative and management solutions to anthropogenic threats for marine mammal welfare, including bycatch, net entanglement and noise pollution
5.	To encourage alternative income funding sources to artisnal fishing including tourism
6.	To promote and market responsible dolphin and whale watching along the coast 
7.	To recruit and train local community members as Marine Champions and ocean guardians.
8.	To increase public awareness of marine mammals in Kenya, from a national heritage perspective
9.	To contribute data to national and regional marine mammal conservation strategies with our partners.

Name: The Corvus Centre for Conservation Policy
Description: The Corvus Centre for Conservation Policy is a not-for-profit, charitable organization whose vision is that public policy actively supports nature and biodiversity.

Name: Stiftung Pro Lutra
Description: Für die Rückkehr des Fischotters.

Name: The Ontario Headwaters Institute
Description: Formed in 2002 to focus on headwater health, our mandate expanded during the last 10 years and we now work to protect Ontario's Watersheds, their Natural Heritage, and our Receiving Waters.  Visit our extensive website at WWW.ONTARIOHEADWATERS.CA

Name: Envol Vert Colombia
Description: Cuidamos bosques a través de alternativas económicas a la deforestación, procesos de conservación, incidencia política y sensibilización ambiental.

Name: River Pearl PLUS
Description: The River Pearl Association awards the independent quality label "River Pearl PLUS" to natural rivers and streams in order to preserve and better protect them.

Name: Brunswick-Topsham Land Trust
Description:
Brunswick-Topsham Land Trust is a nonprofit that conserves and stewards vital natural areas, supports a vibrant local food system, and connects people with the natural world through inclusive education and recreation programs.Our goal is to protect our natural resources, cultivate care for the natural world, and in so doing, foster resilient, connected communities now and for generations to come.

We believe that when a community is deeply connected to its land, it will prosper.

Name: Fundacion Bioparque La Reserva
Description: Trabajamos por la conservación de la biodiversidad colombiana, el uso sostenible de los recursos naturales y la ciencia del bienestar animal a través de recorridos guiados de inmersión sensibilizando personas que sean más amables con la naturaleza, a la vez que damos hogar a fauna silvestre rescatada del tráfico ilegal que no puede ser libreados a causa de las secuelas del tráfico ilegal.

Name: Delta Conservation
Description:
For enquiries please email us: enquiries@deltaconservation.org

Delta Conservation is a private conservation business based in Melbourne. We specialize in the conservation of mural paintings, stone, stucco and built heritage.

Our experience
Our project experience includes work in galleries, World Heritage and National Heritage listed sites, private community engagement projects and within heritage advocacy. Trained in Australia and Europe, we formulate treatment methodologies unique to each project by utilising a mixture of traditional and contemporary materials, techniques and analyses to ensure a high-quality outcome.

Our aim
While we maintain a strong treatment skillset, we aim to minimise interventive practices on heritage materials and to preserve as much of their age and character as possible. 

Ethics and memberships
We abide strictly with the Australian Burra Charter and the Australian Institute for the Conservation of Cultural Materials (AICCM) Code of Ethics & Practice. We are members of Australia ICOMOS, AICCM and the International Institute of Conservation (IIC).

Community practices
Working with local communities across Australia, India, Italy and Switzerland has instilled in us a strong will to collaborate with and to understand the holistic tangible and intangible aspects of heritage. We believe that conservation is about collaboration, and that developing strategies for sharing and maintaining our heritage is integral to its preservation.

Name: Conservation through Poverty Alleviation International
Description:
Conservation through Poverty Alleviation International (CPALI) is a US-based 501(c)3 non-profit dedicated to a community-centered approach to conservation. We support locally led nature-based innovations that strengthen community and nature resilience. Learn about our programs at CPALI.org.

Our nature-positive textiles are produced by farmers and artisans in rural Madagascar and marketed under the brand, Tanana Madagascar. Find out more at TananaSilk.com.

Name: Gruppo Ricerche Tartarughe Marine
Description: Il Gruppo Ricerche Tartarughe Marine (GRTM) della Stazione Zoologica Anton Dohrn di Napoli conduce studi sulla biologia, l'ecologia, il comportamento e la conservazione delle tartarughe marine in tutte le loro fasi di vita. Il GRTM offre agli studenti opportunità di formazione e di ricerca sul campo, svolge attività di sensibilizzazione e di informazione delle diverse categorie portatrici di interesse e fornisce supporto scientifico alle amministrazioni locali e agli enti gestori coinvolti nella tutela delle tartarughe marine

Name: Angler Action Foundation
Description: Here at the Angler Action Foundation, we strive to help keep our aquatic ecosystems safe in Florida and other areas. Whether through volunteer work, biologic data collection, or other means of assistance, we are constantly trying to expand the ways in which we can contribute to the overall health of marine habitats. With an emphasis on information distillation and output, education to the general public is among the areas of focus that we are most excited to evolve in throughout the coming years. If we can contribute to the continued preservation of our aquatic environments for years to come, it will benefit not just those who live near the coast, but everyone across the globe in meaningful ways.

Name: Pixelated Realities
Fundación Pacífico
Description: Fundación Pacífico es el fondo ambiental regional del Pacıífico Tropical Este. Promueve la protección y el desarrollo sostenible de los recursos marinos y costeros, mediante el financiamiento sostenible y de largo plazo para inversiones regionales de impacto para la conservación.

Name: Fédération Régionale des Chasseurs de Nouvelle-Aquitaine
Description:
La Fédération régionale des chasseurs de Nouvelle-Aquitaine : quid ? 

Créée en 2016, la Fédération régionale des chasseurs de la Nouvelle-Aquitaine (FRC NA) regroupe par adhésion les douze Fédérations départementales des chasseurs de la région administrative du même nom, regroupant ainsi près de 160 000 adhérents. Association agréée au titre de la protection de l’environnement, elle assure la représentation des fédérations départementales et interdépartementales des chasseurs, de même que la défense des intérêts de la chasse et des chasseurs de sa région.
Elle œuvre à la mutualisation des actions des fédérations départementales et interdépartementales des chasseurs. Elle mène, en concertation avec les fédérations départementales, des actions d'information et d'éducation au développement durable 
Elle conduit et coordonne des actions en faveur de la faune sauvage et de ses habitats et des actions concourant directement à la connaissance, à la protection et à la reconquête de la biodiversité. 
Elle assure toutes les fonctions que lui donne la législation et la réglementation en vigueur dans les domaines sanitaire, forestier, agricole et de protection de la biodiversité. Elle développe tout projet en relation avec ces domaines, notamment en matière d’éducation à l’environnement. Elle participe à toutes les institutions de la région administrative qui interviennent dans ces domaines. 
Elle peut se voir confier la gestion de réserves naturelles et de tout autre territoire à caractère protégé.

Name: Earthfire Institute
Description:
Earthfire Institute is a nonprofit wildlife sanctuary, rehabilitation, and retreat center founded in 2000 based on the principles of Reconnection Ecology, an approach toward meaningful coexistence with all that is wild. Located on the western slope of the Teton Mountain range and on the southernmost border of the Yellowstone to Yukon wildlife corridor, Earthfire's vision is to include the voices of our planet's animals in our environmental decisions. 
Earthfire creates the opportunity for life-changing interaction between humans and wildlife. We provide both a home for animals that are not capable of living in the wild and a safe place for humans to meet them. The rescued wildlife and their astonishing stories help expand our sense of community to truly include all living beings, for all our sakes.

Our unique model of ecological advocacy begins with personal inspiration and leads to a renewed commitment to protect the global ecosystem on which all Life depends.

Name: Salmon Parks
Description: Salmon Parks are Indigenous Protected and Conserved Areas (IPCAs) on land and in the ocean that are designated under Mowachaht/Muchalaht First Nation (MMFN) law. Salmon Parks are critical habitats that salmon rely on throughout their life cycle. Salmon Parks are currently made-up of about 66,000 hectares of coastal rainforest, of which 39,000 hectares are old growth forest.

Name: Protection des mangroves de Guinée
Description:
Il est crucial de préserver les mangroves pour plusieurs raisons : elles offrent une protection contre les aléas climatiques, soutiennent la biodiversité, contribuent à la sécurité alimentaire et offrent des services écosystémiques essentiels.
Les mangroves constituent une barrière naturelle contre l'érosion côtière, les tempêtes et les inondations, protégeant les communautés côtières et les infrastructures. 
Elles abritent une grande variété de plantes, d'animaux et d'invertébrés, et servent de nurseries pour de nombreuses espèces, notamment les poissons. 
Les mangroves fournissent des aliments, des ressources naturelles et des matériaux de construction pour les communautés locales. 
Elles filtrent l'eau, stockent le carbone, stabilisent les côtes et contribuent à la qualité de l'eau. 
En résumé, la préservation des mangroves est essentielle pour la santé de l'environnement et le bien-être des populations côtières.

Name: Fédération de Vendée Pour la Pêche et la Protection du Milieu Aquatique
Description:
Nous œuvrons au quotidien :

	Sur la connaissance et la gestion des milieux aquatiques au travers de la réalisation de travaux  (Frayères, mise en conformité des ouvrage avec les exigences de  continuité écologique, passes à poissons, morphologie des cours d’eau...) et de suivis (inventaires piscicoles, écoulements des cours d'eau, thermie, poissons migrateurs...)

	Pour la promotion du loisir pêche  en eau douce en organisant de nombreuses manifestations, concours, rassemblements...

	Dans le domaine de l'éducation à l'environnement sur le site de la Maison de la Pêche  et de la Nature sur la commune de La Ferrière et sur l'ensemble du département de la Vendée auprès des scolaires, centres de loisirs et personnes en situation de handicap.

	Pour organiser la pratique de la pêche au travers de notre réseau de 38 Association Agréées pour La Pêche et la Protection du Milieu Aquatique (AAPPMA)

	Pour lutter contre le braconnage et les atteintes au milieu aquatique   au travers de notre réseau de police de la pêche mais également devant les tribunaux dans les affaires de pollutions de nos cours d’eau.
Location: La Ferrière, Pays de la Loire

Name: Nature Data Alliance
Description:
More information: https://www.noah-conference.com/nature-data-alliance/
Protecting nature requires collaboration, not competition. With the Nature Data Alliance, 100 nature data and technology companies come together under one ambitious goal – to protect and restore four billion hectares of landscapes and seascapes.

A data-driven, open-source digital ecosystem is urgently needed to measure, protect, and integrate nature in our economies. Alliance members will be building the hardware and software needed for it to materialize, so that nature-based solutions (NbS) can scale efficiently, effectively, and transparently.

Our 100 members have decades of experience in remote sensing, NbS project development and financing, conservation, restoration, agriculture, and carbon-offsetting infrastructures. Our AI systems will be able to define and prepare thousands of high-quality NbS projects ready for financing and implementation.

Our goal is to develop, finance, and monitor NbS projects that are to be implemented in close and respectful partnership with indigenous and local communities, small and medium-sized businesses, and local to regional governments. The lack of truly impactful and transparent projects (and corresponding standards) is one big challenge that we aim to address.

Name: Tanzania Alliance For Biodiversity (TABIO)
Description:
The Tanzania Alliance for Biodiversity (TABIO) is a dynamic and forward-thinking organization committed to the preservation and sustainable management of biodiversity in Tanzania. TABIO's initiatives are rooted in three key thematic areas, each playing a crucial role in advancing the cause of biodiversity conservation.

Research on Biodiversity Conservation:
TABIO recognizes the importance of informed decision-making in conservation efforts. Through dedicated research initiatives, the alliance strives to deepen our understanding of Tanzania's rich biodiversity. TABIO conducts comprehensive studies on ecosystems, flora, and fauna, aiming to identify key conservation priorities and understand the intricate relationships within ecosystems. The research arm of TABIO is dedicated to advancing scientific knowledge, providing a solid foundation for evidence-based conservation strategies.

Advocacy for Biodiversity Conservation:
Advocacy lies at the heart of TABIO's mission. The alliance actively engages with stakeholders at various levels, including local communities, government bodies, and international organizations, to champion policies and practices that promote biodiversity conservation. TABIO advocates for the integration of sustainable and nature-friendly practices into national and regional development plans. Through targeted campaigns and outreach programs, the alliance aims to raise awareness about the value of biodiversity and the critical role it plays in supporting sustainable livelihoods and ecosystems.

Networking with National and International Allies on Biodiversity Conservation:
Recognizing the interconnected nature of biodiversity conservation, TABIO places a strong emphasis on building networks and partnerships. The alliance actively collaborates with national and international organizations, governmental agencies, NGOs, research institutions.
Location: DAR ES SALAAM, Corner Ohio/Sokoine Drive

Name: Intermountain West Joint Venture
Description:
Intermountain West Joint Venture believes in forging productive, lasting relationships built on trust and respect with a diversity of partners, fostering innovations in our work, and producing tangible, on-the-ground results for habitat conservation across private and public lands in the West.

Our Mission: Conserve and enhance bird habitats at meaningful scales through capacity building, science, communications, and strategic partnerships.

Our Vision: An Intermountain West where people, birds, and other wildlife thrive.

Our headquarters is at 1001 S Higgins, Suite A1, Missoula, Montana, United States.

Name: Planet Elements
Description:
Notre solution Planet Pack transforme chaque prestation, séjour ou expérience en une action concrète pour la planète.

Notre solution de régénération de la nature permet aux entreprises de tous secteurs (hôtellerie, événementiel, voyage, sport, yachting…) d’intégrer un impact positif autour de trois piliers : Climat, Biodiversité et Océans.

Une approche simple, mesurable et transparente pensée pour bâtir une économie régénérative, où chaque activité contribue à restaurer le vivant.

Name: JV8 Central Grasslands Conservation Initiative
Description:
In 2020, the Management Board Chairs of eight Migratory Bird Joint Ventures (JVs) signed a statement of commitment to collaborate and create the JV8 Central Grasslands Conservation Initiative. This commitment provided a foundation for coordinated grasslands conservation among nearly 100 Management Board members representing conservationists across Canada, Indigenous Nations, the U.S., and Mexico. 

Our vision is that the Central Grasslands from Canada to Mexico support stable, thriving, and diverse communities of birds, other wildlife, and people into the future. Our mission is to engage and expand Migratory Bird Joint Venture (JV) partnerships across North America for the stewardship of native grasslands ecosystems. 

Building from the JV legacy of success in conserving wetland habitat to support migratory bird populations, JV8 focuses on the conservation of the Central Grasslands, including grassland uplands and embedded wetlands, by bringing together diverse allies and motivating partners to contribute energy, skills, and resources toward this collective effort. 

Together, the eight JVs stretch from Canada to Mexico with an almost complete footprint in the North American Central Grasslands landscape, reflecting shared challenges and shared habitat that includes the breeding, migration, and wintering ranges of imperiled grassland birds. JV8’s role is to support stewards and other stakeholders working across the Central Grasslands biome to deliver proactive, voluntary grassland habitat conservation using Western science and Indigenous Knowledge.

Name: Aviornis International Netherlands
Description:
Aviornis International was formed in 1973 by a group of aviculturists to be an organisation that gathers bird fanciers who are occupied in aviculture and preserving a wide variety of wild birds. 

The Dutch chapter has been established in 1978. Today, it is one of the most important organisations in Europe in its field with around 8.000 members in 6 European countries. 

The Aviornis International magazine helps to provide two of the main aims of Aviornis - to promote sound husbandry through captive breeding and to have closer co-operation between international breeders to promote their interests.

The avicultural side cannot be emphasised enough, with one of our main aims to promote sound husbandry in captive breeding and establish captive breeding programmes.

Aviornis offers a splendid full-colour magazine, with a wealth of information for starters and experienced keepers. Reports of first breeding of specific species. The possibility to join local activities like informative lectures and visits to private parks.
Support, where necessary, with requests and offers for your birds.
Promotion of contacts with other chapters and members in Europe.
Our objectives are 
- To support and co-operate with other organisations on scientific programs to preserve bird species that have become nearly extinct.
- To stimulate (inter)national exchange of young birds in order to keep       genetically healthy stocks of each species. 
- To help our members with advice and information on the husbandry and breeding management of the wide diversity of  bird species. 
- To contribute, together with other international organisations, to scientific programs, aimed at the re-introduction of bird species that have become (locally) extinct due to natural catastrophes or other reasons.

Name: SeaScapes Tyne to Tees
Description:
The SeaScapes vision is ‘To reveal and better manage the hidden heritage of our unique seascape and create opportunities for learning, access and enjoyment in order to ignite stewardship of this special place for generations to come’. 

All of our 23 projects will contribute towards achieving this vision, with vital support and contributions by local communities, volunteers, and our partners.

SeaScapes is governed by a Partnership Board with an independent Chair. We have representation from project partners and an independent community representative to ensure all decisions are made in a transparent way, for the benefit of the marine environment and local communities.

Name: Syndicat du Bassin Versant de l'Aisne Navigable
Polli
Description: POLLi is a conservation platform that brings conservation data to life.  Data collected by multiple sensor types typically mounted on drones and processed using artificial intelligence, Polli enables users to survey and identify plant or animal species across large land areas accurately and quickly.

Name: Great Southern Land Conservancy
Description:
Great Southern Land Conservancy buys land and manages it for conservation. 
Our acquisition priorities include:
 - Habitat for threatened or declining species
 - Habitats that are not represented or are poorly represented in existing protected areas
 - Habitat that forms a corridor linking or consolidating protected areas or improving landscape resilience
 - Important wetlands or other special features
 - Habitat for endemic plants or animals
 - Refugia such as high elevation forests

Name: ICOMOS-SDGWG
Description: The ICOMOS Sustainable Development Goals Working Group coordinates ICOMOS’ response to the UN 2030 Agenda, its Sustainable Development Goals, and their implementation. As such, the working group conserves, protects and engages with all types of heritage.

Name: Pennsylvania Master Naturalist
Description: Pennsylvania Master Naturalist is a statewide partnership initiative that aims to connect people with their local ecosystems through intensive natural science training and local conservation service work.

Name: Into the Bush: Voices of Wild Women
Description: A podcast hosted by women in conservation for women in conservation digging into their personal experiences and shining a spotlight on their work.

Name: Q'ochas Resilientes
Description: Q'ochas are an ancestral technology which holds campesino memories and futures in the face of climate change

Name: Laikipia Conservancies Association
Description:
The Laikipia Conservancies Association is a member-led organization established in 2019 with the goal of bringing together conservancies in Laikipia to address common challenges and amplify our individual impacts. As one of the most important wildlife areas in Kenya, the future of Laikipia as a conservation landscape depends on conservancies.

 

These institutions form a diverse mosaic, with different land tenure, governance, and management models, yet are aligned through recognition that a collaborative vision and management approach is critical to the future of conservancies as the core of a broader conservation landscape that supports people and wildlife.

Our member conservancies have made significant strides in conserving the Flora and Fauna within the Laikipia  landscape.  These  institutions have also contributed to the community well-being and local economies. Laikipia Conservancies Association amplifies these impacts by aligning efforts at the landscape scale and providing a platform to address the immense challenges that span conservancy boundaries.

Name: Projeto Onças do Iguaçu - Instituto Pró Carnívoros
Description:
O Projeto Onças do Iguaçu dedica-se à conservação da onça-pintada, um símbolo da biodiversidade brasileira, com foco no Parque Nacional do Iguaçu e seu entorno. Nosso trabalho visa a coexistência pacífica entre humanos e animais, e a preservação de espécies ameaçadas.
Nossas principais iniciativas incluem:
•
Campanha "Deixe o Bicho no Mato": Uma estratégia nacional liderada com o CENAP/ICMBio e outras instituições para reduzir a retirada de filhotes de mamíferos da natureza, conscientizando que isso priva mães de cuidarem de seus filhotes e impede uma vida livre. A arte da campanha foi doada pela bióloga Tainah de Souza Ferreira, e a impressão inicial patrocinada pela Pandhora Technologies.
•
Programa de Voluntariado: Oferece vivência prática em atividades de conservação da onça-pintada, proporcionando conhecimento sobre o Parque Nacional do Iguaçu e programas de conservação. Atualmente, não há vagas abertas.
•
Materiais Educacionais: Disponibilizamos para download guias valiosos como o "Guia de Coexistência", "Manual Carcaças", "Manual Peçonhentos", "Bichos do Parque Nacional do Iguaçu", "Guia de identificação de felinos" e um "Folder sobre segurança", promovendo conhecimento e harmonia com a natureza.
•
Pesquisa e Monitoramento: Acompanhamos onças como India (com Mitacoré), Sami, Marfim, Peter (homenagem a Peter Crawshaw, pioneiro pesquisador), Krika (homenagem à atriz Cristina Oliveira), Indira (uma fêmea guerreira e mãe de Aritana e Pururuca) e Kaluanã ("guerreiro poderoso"), muitas vezes registradas por armadilhas fotográficas.
Tivemos um papel fundamental na criação do Dia Nacional da Onça-Pintada em 29 de novembro, uma data escolhida pelo Brasil para divulgar a importância da espécie, que também se tornou o Dia Internacional da Onça-Pintada durante a COP 14 em 2018.
Contamos com parceiros estratégicos como CENAP, Instituto Pró-Carnívoros, Rede Pró UC, Programa de Conservação Mamíferos do Cerrado e Programa Amigos da Onça

Name: Rob & Bessie Welder Wildlife Foundation
Description:
The Rob & Bessie Welder Wildlife Foundation is a non-profit tax-exempt organization established in 1954 upon the passing of Robert H. Welder.   The Foundation honors the legacy of Mr. Welder through excellence in land stewardship, education and discovery of knowledge needed to manage, restore, and protect wildlife populations and their habitats while sustaining the ranching heritage.  All toward improving quality of life for wildlife and people alike. 
The Foundation fulfills our mission of cultivating the future of wildlife conservation through research, education, and preservation of outdoor and working land lifestyles by:
•	Delivering a research program that enhances our understanding of wildlife populations and their habitats and provides science-based information for decision-making on all issues impacting natural resource conservation.
•	Delivering an educational and outreach program that enhances the appreciation of and access to nature for all citizens.
•       Developing future leaders in conservation science and land stewardship.
•       Creating environmental stewards and supporters of natural resources that advocate for public policies that are critical to the long-term success of wildlife conservation. 
•	Seeking the development and use of management practices that conserve and sustain wildlife populations and their habitats.
•	Aiming to preserve our hunting, fishing, outdoor recreation and ranching heritage through promotion of ethical and sustainable conservation.

Name: Horstmann Trust
Description:
The focus is on scientific research into incubation together with conservation breeding of what has become the most endangered group of birds in the world. As well as looking into all aspects of the welfare, housing and husbandry of vultures much of the work directly helps and supports other vulture conservation organisations from around the world.
The aims and objectives of the charity is to be an effective part of the vulture conservation community and to play an active role in sharing information and resources, together with on going teaching of rehabilitation and captive breeding skills to colleagues in other organisations. Only through working with other organisations both in and ex-situ can the knowledge and expertise be exploited to its full potential, for the overall benefit of vultures.

Name: CONGO BIODIVERSITY
Description:
Congo Biodiversity is an umbrella initiative that unites multiple projects dedicated to documenting, protecting, and celebrating the natural wealth of the Republic of Congo. From the vast rainforests of the Congo Basin to its savannas, rivers, and wetlands, this country is home to some of the world’s most remarkable ecosystems and species.

Our mission is to provide a collaborative platform where researchers, naturalists, students, and local communities can share knowledge and contribute to conservation efforts. By gathering and promoting biodiversity data, Congo Biodiversity aims to raise awareness, inspire action, and support the protection of endangered species and fragile habitats.

Beyond research and conservation, the project also highlights the role of biodiversity in education, sustainable development, and ecotourism. By connecting science with local communities and global networks, Congo Biodiversity seeks to create meaningful partnerships that strengthen environmental stewardship in the Congo Basin.

Follow this page to explore field updates, new discoveries, and opportunities to engage with one of the world’s most important biodiversity hotspots.

Name: Madagascar Lemur Portal
Description: Madagascar Lemur Portal is a biodiversity-friendly technology dedicated to safeguarding Madagascar's unique lemur heritage through mobile application and website. This platform serves as a comprehensive source of information about these species, highlighting their fascinating biodiversity. The primary goal of this project is to continuously contribute to the improvement of lemur conservation in Madagascar by bridging the gap between technology and biodiversity.

Name: CCNet Australia & New Zealand
Description:
We are a network of individuals dedicated to promoting adaptive management and the Open Standards for the Practice of Conservation (Conservation Standards). We are passionate about improving the practice of conservation in Australia and globally. 

We also post to our CCNetAustralia FaceBook page: https://www.facebook.com/CCNet-Australia-102197612540686

Name: Empreintes Sauvages : Le Refuge
Description:
Né dans le contexte actuel de l’interdiction imminente des animaux dans les cirques, Empreintes Sauvages, Le Refuge est un projet de sanctuaire pour grands félins, proposant une alternative pour ces fauves mais aussi pour tous ceux, illégalement introduits, saisis ou maltraités, qui ont besoin d’un toit.

Empreintes Sauvages est destiné à accueillir et à soigner des individus, avec chacun leur histoire et leur passé, avec chacun leur caractère et leur rapport à l’Homme.

Ce sanctuaire pour grands félins se veut havre de paix pour ces animaux souvent chahutés par la vie, des animaux parfois abandonnés, maltraités, souffrants, pour qui aucune autre solution n’est possible.

Niché dans le Cher au centre de la France, le refuge d’Empreintes Sauvages offrira aux tigres, lions, guépards et panthères qui ne peuvent être réintroduits dans leur milieu naturel, une retraite bien méritée.

Name: Society for Conservation Biology at Colorado State University
Description:
The CSU Chapter of the Society for Conservation Biology builds the capacity of undergraduate and graduate students to enter impactful careers in environmental conservation, science, and stewardship. We’re an inclusive, interdisciplinary group spanning natural and social sciences, and sharing a core mission of protecting biodiversity on Earth.

The Chapter provides pre-professional experience to students through volunteer service in the community, professional education and networking, career development resources, and community-building. 

We’re proud to be part of a global network of like-minded conservation scientists, practitioners, policy-makers, and other allied professionals.

Name: Mossy Rock Ranch
Description: Another way to add value to a property is exotic hoof stock. Mossy Rock Ranch has been very active in putting together a large herd of African and other exotic hoof stock. We have now built a large herd of several species and will be offering a select surplus of animal for sale in the spring and fall of each year. Please feel free to give us a call to check on availability.

Name: Conservation Sudbury
Description: Watershed Management, Flood forecasting, natural hazards, dams, weirs, flood ways, conservation, trails, lakes, erosion, wetlands, education, drinking water source protection.

Name: Luminocean Marine Conservation
Description:
At Luminocean, we’re dedicated to conserving the vibrant ecosystems of Indonesia’s Banda Islands while empowering the local communities that depend on them. From restoring coral reefs and promoting sustainable fishing practices to conducting environmental education and supporting eco-tourism, we’re working to build a resilient future where nature and people thrive together.

Our Focus:
 Marine Conservation: Actively restoring damaged coral ecosystems and protecting marine biodiversity.
 Community Empowerment: Educating and engaging local communities in sustainable practices, especially the next generation of conservationists.
 Sustainable Development: Partnering with local businesses, institutions, and scientists to foster eco-friendly tourism, responsible resource management, and sustainable growth.

With hands-on programs and collaborative partnerships, Luminocean is transforming challenges into opportunities for lasting change. Join us in protecting the oceans, preserving cultural heritage, and building a sustainable future in the heart of the Spice Islands.

Learn more about our work, volunteer opportunities, and ways to get involved: www.luminocean.com

#MarineConservation #SustainableDevelopment #Luminocean #BandaIslands #EcoTourism

Name: River and Nature Trust
Description:
Unsere Mission: Schutz der Flüsse und ihrer Tiere 
Unser Ziel: Eine intakte Flussnatur und eine Balance der Lebewesen 
Unser Weg: Erhebung des Ist – Zustandes & Feststellung besonders betroffener Arten 
Unser Werkzeug: Kooperation mit der Wissenschaft und besonders qualifizierten Forschern.
Wir geben Fischen und Flüssen eine Stimme! 

Der „River and Nature Trust“ – Aufmerksamkeit und Hilfe für die wichtigsten Lebensadern der Erde, ihrer Tiere und Umwelt: 
Jetzt Handeln, mit Blick in die nächsten Jahrzehnte! 
Die ursprünglichen Fischpopulationen nehmen weltweit dramatisch ab, in die Lebensadern des Planeten wird nach wie vor planlos eingegriffen. Es ist zum Schämen, wie sich die Salmonidenpopulation in Österreich, aber nicht nur hier,drastisch dezimiert hat. Das hat mehrere Gründe: 
Die augenblickliche Klimaerwärmung ist nur einer davon, der Hauptgrund jedoch ist eine seit Jahrzehnten gepflegte „Loch auf – Loch zu“ oder „Trial & Error“ Mentalität, was die Behandlung, bzw. die Misshandlung der Flüsse, ihrer Läufe und ihrer Umwelt betrifft. Sprich, die Planung des “Lebensraumes Fluss” wird immer nur kurzfristig gemacht und nicht mit einem Blick über Jahrzehnte. Das wäre jedoch notwendig!  
Eine  Gruppe von Freunden der Fische und Flüsse, hat sich entschieden, den „River and Nature Trust“ ins Leben zu rufen. 
Unser Sitz ist in Österreich, unser Spektrum ein internationales: Von Beginn weg werden wir in mehreren Ländern tätig und halten Kontakte zu Journalisten und Organisationen in vielen Ländern der Welt. 
Wir werden mit einem innovativen Modell den Fischen und den Flüssen eine Zukunft geben und sind keine Mitbewerber der Organisationen und Institutionen, die das bereits tun. Vielmehr sind wir eine sinnvolle Ergänzung: 
Der River and Nature Trust wird ein Sprachrohr der Flüsse und Fische sein und sorgt dafür, dass die Botschaften aller gleichgesinnten Organisationen und Aktivits*innen stärker an die Öffentlichkeit und die Entscheider*nnen gelangen.

Name: Southwest Wildlife Conservation Center
Description:
Southwest Wildlife Conservation Center is a non profit wildlife rescue in Scottsdale, Arizona. Southwest Wildlife rescues, rehabilitates, and releases native Arizona wildlife. A tourable sanctuary is provided for animals that are not able to be released due to medical conditions or for those who were kept illegally as pets and now cannot survive on their own. 

Southwest Wildlife offers a medical clinic for rehabbing wild animals, a rescue response team for wildlife in need, and sanctuary for conservation programs such as The Mexican Gray Wolf Species Survival Plan. 

Southwest Wildlife offers tours, seasonal events, corporate volunteer opportunities, and corporate sponsorship opportunities. 

Southwest Wildlife is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that relies completely on the generous donations and

Name: Nationalparks Austria
Description: Vom Steppensee bis zum Hochgebirge, von der Waldwildnis bis zur Au, vom tosenden Fluss bis zum sanften Tal: Als international anerkannte Schutzgebiete (Kategorie II) wahren die sechs österreichischen Nationalparks Landschaften von wilder Schönheit und enormen Artenreichtum. Unter dem Dach von Nationalparks Austria arbeiten diese Naturschutzgebiete eng zusammen.
Location: Orth an der Donau, Lower Austria

Name: Akagera National Park - Rwanda
Description: Akagera lies on the eastern border of Rwanda, three hours from Kigali city making it easy to visit and admire the wildlife – from lion to rhino – that thrives in this beautiful savannah and wetland. The park has been managed by African Parks in Partnership with the Rwanda Development Board since 2010.

Name: African Bioacoustics Community
Description:
Bioacoustic research from Africa and by African scientists is not well represented in the global field and thus the African Bioacoustics Community strives to shine a light on the achievements of bioacoustics research in Africa. We work towards equal representation, access to crucial learning and networking opportunities, and providing an environment where information flows freely between scientists, research labs, countries, and continents. 
 
Sustainability and minimizing our carbon footprint is something we value highly and we work hard to ensure that our events are sustainable, plastic-free and carbon responsible. As part of our effort in this matter, we ask that members of our community consider carbon-offsetting measures or donating to a cause that aligns with our values.

The African Bioacoustics Community wants to make the science of sound accessible to the continent of Africa. We cannot achieve this without a diverse and inclusive mindset, valuing new ideas, and celebrating different approaches. We hope to foster connection and relationships between people who are passionate about the unique and fascinating field of bioacoustics.

Our ultimate goal is to provide a socially and environmentally conscious place for researchers to network, to learn and to share their research in the field of bioacoustics.
For further information email us at:
africanbioacoustics@gmail.com

Name: North Coast Land Conservancy
Description:
North Coast Land Conservancy is a nonprofit land trust dedicated to safeguarding Oregon's coastal land and seascapes. Working with a variety of community partners, we acquire and steward rich coastal habitats—from the Columbia River to the Siletz Bay. 

We manage an ever-growing network of more than 60 habitat reserves and easements across the Oregon Coast, in addition to hosting a marine program centered on Cape Falcon Marine Reserve. Recognizing the interconnectedness of all life on Earth, we work strategically to conserve core ecological systems.

Our vision is a fully functioning coastal landscape where healthy communities of people, plants and wildlife thrive.

Name: Working Lands Trust
Description:
Working Lands Trust (WLT) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation established in 2014 to ensure long-term protection and sustainability of our nation’s food and fiber supply.  We accomplish this mission through education, protection of working farms and forestlands, and advancement of public policy which promotes working lands conservation. 

WLT is a critical partner of the Eastern North Carolina Sentinel Landscape which works with willing private landowners in 33 eastern North Carolina counties to provide voluntary tools to help manage those lands in ways which are compatible to military training exercises carried out adjacent to and in the skies above landholdings near Seymour Johnson Air Force Base, Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune, Marine  Corps Air Station New River, Marine Corps Air Station Cherry Point, U.S. Army Fort Bragg, Pope Army Air Field, and several ancillary bombing ranges and outlying landing fields near these posts critical to their training exercises.  

The North Carolina Forestry Association was integrally responsible for the establishment of WLT and remains a pivotal partner as we both strive to sustain working forests in North Carolina and throughout the Southern and Eastern United States where 69% of the nation's fiber supply is grown and harvested, almost all on privately owned lands.  Both organizations strive to conserve the supply chain from the rapid urbanization in these states.

WLT would also like to acknowledge the incredible support of our partners at The Conservation Fund, the North Carolina Agricultural Development and Farmland Protection Trust Fund, the USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service, the U.S. Department of Defense Readiness and Environmental Protection Integration Challenge, and so many more friends we partner with through the Eastern North Carolina Sentinel Landscape.

Name: Save Elephant Foundation
Description:
Save Elephant Foundation is a Thai non-profit organization working to protect Asian elephants and promote compassionate, sustainable living. Founded by renowned conservationist Saengduean “Lek” Chailert, the foundation has grown into a regional movement dedicated to rescue, rehabilitation, education, and ethical tourism.

Our flagship project, Elephant Nature Park in Chiang Mai, Thailand, provides a safe haven for elephants rescued from the logging, entertainment, and street begging industries. Here, elephants are given the chance to heal—physically and emotionally—in a natural and peaceful environment. But our work doesn’t stop there.

Save Elephant Foundation supports and operates projects across Southeast Asia. In Laos, we partner with local sanctuaries to provide safe land and freedom for rescued elephants. In Cambodia, we co-manage the Cambodia Wildlife Sanctuary—protecting over 32,000 acres of forest and offering refuge to elephants and other endangered species. In India, we collaborate with local organizations to improve elephant welfare and share knowledge that leads to systemic change.

Our mission is rooted in compassion—for animals, communities, and the environment. We believe that through education, hands-on rescue, and community empowerment, we can create a future where elephants and humans live in harmony.

Every life matters. And with the support of volunteers, partners, and ethical travellers from around the world, we continue working toward a kinder world for all.

Join us—because the future of elephants depends on the actions we take today.

Name: Citizen Conservation
Description: Als Netzwerk aus institutionellen und privaten Tierhaltenden bündelt Citizen Conservation Fachkompetenz, Know-how und Kapazitäten, um koordinierte Erhaltungszuchtprogramme für bedrohte Arten aufzubauen. Eine Bewegung gegen den Verlust von Biodiversität und für die Vielfalt. Die Botschaft: Alle können mitmachen!

Name: Landschaftserhaltungsverband Landkreis Ravensburg e.V.
Description:
Gemeinsam geht´s - Der Landschaftserhaltungsverband als Brückenbauer

Landschaftserhaltungsverbände (LEV) sind Kooperationen zwischen Landnutzern, Naturschützern und Kommunen. Sie sind als gemeinnützige Vereine auf Landkreisebene organisiert und verstehen sich als Brückenbauer zwischen Mensch und Natur. Der kooperative Ansatz spiegelt sich im Vorstand wider, wo Vertreter aus Naturschutz, Landwirtschaft und die kommunale Seite gleichberechtigt vertreten sind.

Der Landschaftserhaltungsverband Landkreis Ravensburg e.V. wurde 2014 gegründet und übernahm von der PRO REGIO GmbH das Aufgabengebiet der Landschaftspflege.

Ziel des Landschaftserhaltungsverbands Ravensburg ist die Erhaltung und Entwicklung der Kulturlandschaft mit ihren schützenswerten Lebensräumen, sowie der Erhalt und die Förderung der Artenvielfalt.

Dies kann nur in Zusammenarbeit mit den Akteuren vor Ort funktionieren. Im Landschaftserhaltungsverband arbeiten Landnutzer wie Land- und Forstwirte, Gemeinden, Jäger und Naturschützer zusammen, um Naturschutzprojekte
möglichst einvernehmlich umzusetzen.

Der Landschaftserhaltungsverband versteht sich als Partner der landwirtschaftlichen Betriebe. Konsensfähige Lösungen für die Natur zu finden, ohne die Wirtschaftlichkeit der landwirtschaftlichen Betriebe aus den Augen zu verlieren, steht bei der täglichen Arbeit der Geschäftsstelle im Vordergrund. Dabei spielen Freiwilligkeit und eine fachgerechte, praxisorientierte Beratung
eine wichtige Rolle.

Um diese Ziele umsetzen zu können, werden überwiegend Fördermittel des Landes eingesetzt (Landschaftspflegerichtlinie, FAKT, weitere Agrar-Umweltmaßnahmen). Weitere Maßnahmen der Biodiversitätsstrategie werden
über Projektmittel, die von der Kreissparkassen-Stiftung Ravensburg zur Verfügung gestellt werden, umgesetzt.

Name: Big Blue Foundation
Description:
Oceans produce half of the oxygen we breathe and remove almost a third of all carbon emissions from our atmosphere. They provide food security for almost half the planet’s population, enable global trade worth more than £400 billion, and provide employment for millions of people.

Yet marine life is becoming endangered at an accelerating rate due to pollution, habitat loss, ocean acidification and industrial overfishing. Without significant change, UNESCO predicts more than 50% of the world’s marine species may face extinction by 2100.

Our Mission

Big Blue Foundation is dedicated to reversing biodiversity loss in our ocean. Placing coastal communities at the forefront of our work we empower future conservation leaders to drive positive change and create healthier oceans for all.

Name: Florida Conservation Corps
Description: The mission of the Florida Conservation Corps is to develop natural and cultural resource leaders by connecting them to areas of critical need in conservation, preservation, interpretation and resource-based recreation

Name: Fundación Flora Nativa Chilena
Description:
La Fundación Flora Nativa Chilena tiene como propósito generar espacios vinculación y diálogo en torno a la conservación de nuestro patrimonio vegetal. 

Buscamos crear instancias de encuentro entre los actores vinculados a la conservación de la flora nativa y sus ecosistemas, acercando distintas disciplinas y actuando como puente entre saberes. Asimismo, generamos, promovemos y apoyamos acciones y proyectos destinados a la producción de conocimiento sobre la conservación de la flora nativa.

Nuestro equipo está formado por agrónomos, ingenieros forestales y profesionales del paisaje, quienes compartimos la visión de articular el conocimiento interdisciplinar para fomentar la conservación, valoración y uso sostenible de la flora nativa de Chile.

Desde el año 2008, el comité fundador de la Fundación Flora Nativa organiza los Congresos de Flora Nativa de Chile.

Name: Lookout Mountain Conservancy
Description: Lookout Mountain Conservancy leads the way in community-centered conservation and environmental education by providing equitable and inclusive access to the Mountain's natural beauty, wildlife habitats, recreational opportunities, and places of historical significance.

Name: SICONA Naturschutzsyndikat
Description:
Das Naturschutzsyndikat SICONA ist in derzeit 42
Gemeinden im Süd-Westen und Zentrum Luxemburgs
aktiv und setzt sich zusammen aus den Syndikaten
SICONA Centre und SICONA Sud-Ouest. Aus einem
Pionierprojekt vor mehr als 30 Jahren hat sich eine
professionelle Struktur mit über 70 Mitarbeitern
entwickelt. Das SICONA-Team bringt den Naturschutz
in den Mitgliedsgemeinden mit vollem Einsatz voran.
Vier Abteilungen sind für die Planung, wissenschaftliche
Begleitung und praktische Umsetzung
der Projekte sowie die pädagogischen Aktivitäten
zuständig.

1. Die wissenschaftliche Abteilung ist für die
Erhebung der Daten zur Flora und Fauna
zuständig. Außerdem kümmert sie sich um das
Monitoring der Schutzmaßnahmen, indem sie
die Bestände von Zielarten überwacht.
2. Die Planungsabteilung übernimmt die
Detailplanung, führt Gespräche mit
Eigentümern und Landnutzern und erstellt
Genehmigungsanträge bei den zuständigen
Verwaltungen.
3. Die praktische Umsetzung erfolgt durch die
technische Abteilung. Mit eigenem Fuhrpark
werden Neuanlagen, Renaturierungen sowie
die Pflege von Lebensräumen durchgeführt.
4. Die pädagogische Abteilung ist eine
Investition in die Zukunft: Sie bietet den
Kindern und Jugendlichen in den
Mitgliedsgemeinden im Rahmen von
SICONA kids ein umfangreiches Naturerlebnisangebot.
Der Schutz der vielfältigen Lebensräume und unserer
heimischen Kulturlandschaft ist ein Kernbereich
der Arbeit von SICONA. Es geht um den Erhalt
unserer Biodiversität, aber auch um den Erhalt der
Lebensqualität! Der Schutz und die Wiederherstellung
von Lebensräumen bildet dabei die Grundlage für
unsere gefährdeten einheimischen Arten.
SICONA betreibt somit in enger Zusammenarbeit mit
den Mitgliedsgemeinden, Bewirtschaftern und Grundbesitzern
konkreten Naturschutz auf lokaler und
regionaler Ebene.

Name: Asociación Armonía
Description:
Asociación Armonía develops and implements conservation strategies for Bolivia’s most threatened bird species. We maintain a long-term, results-oriented focus, ensuring that all conservation actions are carried out in close collaboration with local communities and authorities.

Armonía’s objectives are achieved by protecting key ecosystems, applying sound scientific data, providing environmental education, and working hand-in-hand with the communities that share their landscapes with these birds.

Name: Sophia Point Rainforest Research Centre
Description:
Sophia Point is building the first truly accessible research and education centre in Guyana, South America. 

We are enabling Guyanese students to access, study, understand and champion their rainforest. 

We are introducing leading science and conservation to a vitally important and overlooked ecosystem. 

We are providing a platform to integrate local and indigenous environmental expertise with cutting-edge science to deliver a more sustainable, equitable future for both people and planet.

Name: Cariama Consultoria Ambiental
Description: Um projeto de pesquisa que visa entender a alta taxa de mortalidade de animais silvestres nas rodovias e estradas de Bonito/MS e diminuir as colisões veiculares com fauna e, assim, conquistar estradas mais seguras para os animais e para os usuários.

Name: Junction Creek Stewardship Committee
Description:
The Junction Creek Stewardship Committee is an integral part of the Greater Sudbury (N'Swakamok) community, working to improve the health of the unique urban waterway (Junction Creek) that connects us all, and to promote the liveability and value of our natural environment. 

Our research and monitoring programs help us gain a better understanding about the health and ecological functions of the Junction Creek watershed to inform conservation and management decisions. 

Our ecological restoration activities improve water quality, bank stability, biodiversity, ecosystem function and beautify surrounding green spaces.  

Our educational and outreach programs help to raise awareness, engage the community in watershed stewardship, and provide opportunities for people to build deeper connections with the creek.  

Our programs are driven by community participation and collaboration with partners. Together we are healing the watershed and making a positive change.

Name: Taita Taveta Wildlife Response Trust
Description:
Taita Taveta Wildlife Response Trust (TTWRT) is mission driven to bridge the gap towards human-wildlife co-existence by building resilient livelihoods for communities and secure conservation efforts in Taita Taveta"

TTWRT was founded in 2022 in response to the growing human-wildlife conflict cases in Taita Taveta County in a period made significantly harder by the livelihood challenges brought on by the drought. 

Our vision is a future in which wildlife and human communities in the Tsavo region live in harmony, where human livelihoods are not threatened by wildlife, and where conservation efforts are inclusive of and driven by the needs of local communities. We aim to create a model for co-existence, that can be replicated and scaled in other parts of Kenya and beyond, where human livelihoods are secure and sustainable, and where conservation efforts are supported and valued by local communities.

The Taita Taveta Wildlife Response Trust (TTWRT) is dedicated to promoting harmonious co-existence between wildlife and human communities in the Tsavo region within the boundaries of Taita Taveta County. We strive to bridge the gap between wildlife conservation and human livelihoods by implementing innovative, community-driven solutions that not only protect wildlife but also enhance the resilience of affected communities.

Name: SaveNature.Org
Description:
Since 1988, SaveNature.Org has focused efforts on  protecting entire ecosystems and natural communities through innovative  conservation strategies and public education. SaveNature.Org supports biologically diverse habitats worldwide by fostering awareness and raising funds through various programs, including: Adopt an Acre, Adopt a Reef, Conservation Meters, Nature Academy, and the Insect Discovery Lab.

We collaborate with schools, libraries, universities, zoos, aquariums, botanical gardens, natural history museums, science centers, businesses, and corporations to safeguard wildlife and preserve nature for future generations. Together, we work to inspire global stewardship and ensure the survival of critical ecosystems.

To date, SaveNature.Org has reached over 80 million children and adults through partnerships with 150 institutions and 2,700 schools across the United States. These efforts have raised more than $4.7 million to protect wildlife and habitats in 11 countries, preserving the plants, animals, and communities that embody Earth's rich biodiversity.

SaveNature.Org has received much recognition, including the World Wildlife Fund’s Conservation Award, the National Environmental Awards Council’s Certificate of Environmental Achievement, Congressional Recognition from the United States Congress, and the Outstanding Contribution to Conservation honor from The Nature Conservancy. Our work has been featured in prominent publications such as Newsweek, National Geographic, Time, Scholastic News, and National Geographic Kids, as well as  television programs including Bay Area Backroads, Evening Magazine, and View From the Bay.

As a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, SaveNature.Org is committed to inspiring action to save nature.

Name: Greater Hells Canyon Council
Description:
At GHCC, we work to connect, protect, and restore the wild lands, waters, native species and habitats of the Greater Hells Canyon Region, ensuring a legacy of healthy ecosystems for future generations. After years of campaigning in Washington, DC and in Hells Canyon country, HCPC and its allies prevailed. On December 31, 1975, President Gerald Ford signed into law the Hells Canyon National Recreation Area Act.

Today we continue to honor this legacy. Our work ensures that federal land managers are following bedrock environmental laws and working on behalf of endangered and endemic species across the Greater Hells Canyon Region. We work with our supporters, private landowners and regional partners to promote environmental conservation, including work on beaver coexistence, species monitoring, federal forest planning, federal logging project reviews, and alongside the Nez Perce Tribe on the Camas to Condors project. In 2021 we launched the Blue Mountains Trail, now recognized as one of two Signature Trails in Northeast Oregon.

Name: IMperium Green & IMpact
Description:
IMperium Green & IMpact - Sustainable Finance Consultancy

At IMperium Green & IMpact, we are committed to addressing the complex environmental challenges facing the African and Middle Eastern regions. 

We support our customers in the development, implementation and monitoring of tailor-made projects integrating environmental, social and governance (ESG) principles.

 Our expertise:
 Governance Effectiveness
 Impact Consulting
 Green Financing Advisory

We believe in sustainable, ethical and resilient economic growth. Let's work together for a greener, more inclusive future!

Name: NABU Hamburg
Description: NABU Hamburg is a local nature conservation organisation.

Name: Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries, & Parks
Description: The Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries, and Parks (MDWFP) is organized into six bureaus: Law Enforcement, Freshwater Fisheries, Mississippi Museum of Natural Science (MMNS), State Parks, Support Services, and Wildlife. The MDWFP manages state fishing lakes, fish hatcheries, state parks, wildlife management areas (some owned by other entities, but managed by MDWFP), three regional offices, and one museum. The MDWFP's annual budget is unlike that of many state agencies; approximately 90% of this budget is derived from user-generated funds rather than state tax funds. Primary funding sources include hunting and fishing license sales, permit and registration fees, and federal excise taxes on hunting and fishing equipment.

Name: ALUS Montérégie
Description: ALUS Montérégie est une organisation caritative qui aide les productrices et producteurs agricoles de la Montérégie à mettre en place des solutions fondées sur la nature sur leurs terres afin de soutenir l'agriculture et la biodiversité au profit des collectivités et des générations futures. ALUS Montérégie fournit une expertise, des ressources et un soutien financier direct pour l'établissement, la gestion et l'intendance de ces services écosystémiques.

Name: PT Sino Indo Mutiara
Description: PT Sino Indo Mutiara adalah perusahaan pembesaran mollusca laut terkhusus pada budidaya Kerang Mutiara dengan fokus produksi di wilayah Lombok Barat serta mewadahi pengembangan aktivitas kawasan alam di daerah konservasi.

Name: Gunung Palung Orangutan Conservation Program
Description: The Gunung Palung Orangutan Program's mission is to protect orangutan populations and forest biodiversity in and around Gunung Palung National Park through community-led conservation initiatives. Recognizing that the wellbeing of humans, wildlife, and the environment are inextricably linked, we take a Planetary Health approach to address the needs of all three. Together with our Indonesian partner organization, Yayasan Palung, we strive for a world where orangutans are safe because their forest homes are stewarded by human communities that are strong and empowered. Our programs include scientific research on the behavior and biology of wild orangutans, Sustainable Livelihoods, Village Forests, Animal Protection, and Environmental Education.

Name: Maui Forest Bird Recovery Project
Description: Maui Forest Bird Recovery Project is dedicated to conserving and protecting Maui’s endangered native forest birds and their native forest habitats.

Name: Geneva Ocean Academy
Description: Geneva Ocean Academy (GOA) est une association de conservation marine et un centre de plongée SSI Blue Oceans, engagée à sensibiliser le public suisse à la préservation des océans et à la biodiversité marine. Nous proposons des formations en écologie marine, des cours de plongée, des expéditions en biologie marine et un espace où les passionnés des océans peuvent se rencontrer.

Name: Elephant Conservation Organization
Description: Established in 2020, ECO aims to work with government entities and communities to improve elephant security in Tanzania. Our focus begins by addressing an immediate need with a model that has been widely successful in protecting elephants in neighbouring Kenya: a modern mobile wildlife vet unit.

Name: Now for Wildlife
Description: Now for Wildlife stands as a passionate organization dedicated to the conservation of endangered species in the untouched corners of our planet. These remote landscapes serve as the final sanctuaries of untouched nature, playing a vital role in wildlife conservation.

Name: Extincts C.I.C.
Description: Extincts is a Social Enterprise raising awareness of endangered animals and generating funds for conservation charities. Our plastic-free jigsaws are made from recycled cardboard and our circular economy clothing is 100% organic and can be returned to be remade.

Name: TreesUnion
Description: Preserving, growing, and diversifying the public and private tree canopy throughout Union County, NC.

Name: Les amis de Jean-Baptiste Charcot
Description:
L’association encourage, soutient et promeut les initiatives à caractère scientifique, éducatif,  patrimonial  et culturel en lien avec les destinations des missions dirigées par Jean-Baptiste Charcot et celles qui sont et seront menées dans la continuité de son œuvre dans les régions polaires, les océans et les mers.

L’association organise des interventions, des expositions et des conférences à caractère éducatif historique et/ou scientifique contribuant à faire connaître les régions polaires, les mers et les océans et sensibiliser à leur protection.

Elle peut s’associer aux cérémonies, manifestations, évènements qui s’inscrivent dans la continuité de l’« œuvre utile » de Jean-Baptiste Charcot.

L’association agit en tant que garante et référente de la véracité de l’histoire de Jean-Baptiste Charcot.

Name: Gestion des Milieux Naturels - AgroParisTech Nancy
WE WANT MOOR Verein zum Schutz der deutschen Moore e. V.
Description:
Bei WE WANT MOOR Verein zum Schutz der deutschen Moore e. V. handelt es sich um einen gemeinnützigen Verein, der sich dem Schutz der Moore verschrieben hat. 
Wir haben es uns zur Aufgabe gemacht, ein Bewusstsein für den Moorschutz in der Bevölkerung zu schaffen. Aufklärungsarbeit bildet hierfür die Grundlage, die wir durch zielgerichtete Kommunikation und Ihre Unterstützung leisten wollen. Unser Ziel ist es, Projekte aktiv umzusetzen, Moore in Deutschland zu renaturieren um somit einen Teil zum Umwelt- und Klimaschutz beizutragen.

Name: Biodiversity Nexus
Description:
Mission
Our mission is to empower and engage the youth of Rwanda in meaningful conservation activities, fostering a deep connection to nature and a commitment to preserving the country's rich biodiversity.

Vision
Our vision is a Rwanda where biodiversity thrives, wildlife is protected, and future generations are dedicated stewards of the environment, ensuring a sustainable and harmonious coexistence between people and nature.

Get Involved in Our Project

Name: La Petite Madeleine
Description:
Il est absolument nécessaire de préserver les ressources et de réinventer ensemble nos modes de consommation afin de diminuer notre impact sur l’environnement.

La Petite Madeleine est une association à but non lucratif qui agit depuis mai 2018 pour sensibiliser à cet enjeu.

Nous mettons à la portée du plus grand nombre des moyens pour consommer autrement et réduire nos déchets via l’achat d’occasion, la réparation, la revalorisation d’objets du quotidien, les ateliers participatifs et l’utilisation de matières de récupération.

Name: Landschaftspflegeverein Thermenlinie-Wienerwald-Wiener Becken
Description:
Wir setzen uns für die Biologische Vielfalt, flächenbezogenen Klimaschutz und Klimawandelanpassung in der Region Thermenlinie-Wiener Becken ein! 

Im Rahmen unserer Netzwerk-Natur-Region bringen wir aktiv Maßnahmen auf die Fläche – gemeinsam mit hunderten an Freiwilligen, vielen Schulen, 25 Niederösterreichischen Gemeinden, drei Wiener Gemeindebezirken, mehreren Unternehmen, Landwirt:innen und Vereinen sind wir im Einsatz! So schützen wir seltene und bedrohte Arten, schaffen ein breites Bewusstsein für den Schutz der biologischen Vielfalt und werden gemäß der großen ökologischen Herausforderungen unserer Zeit aktiv!

Hilf auch du mit!

Der Landschaftspflegeverein ist eine gemeinnützige Organisation. Jeder und jede kann Teil unseres Netzwerks werden und somit Lebensräume erhalten und erweitern, sowie neue Trittsteine schaffen!

Die Beteiligungsmöglichkeiten sind vielfältig: 
•	Landschaftspflegeeinsätze mit Freiwilligen und Schulklassen
•	CSR-Aktivitäten mit Unternehmen
•	Ökologisierung von Unternehmensgrünflächen und Gemeindegrünflächen
•	Neuanlage von Blühwiesen mit regionalem Saatgut
•	Pflanzen von Klimahecken
•	…und vieles mehr!

Wir freuen uns über alle, denen die Natur am Herzen liegt! Für alle Altersklassen finden sich Möglichkeiten, mitzuhelfen!

Klingt gut? Komm zu unseren Terminen und schau auf unsere Website oder kontaktiere uns!

Name: Columbia River Gorge Commission
Description:
Columbia River Gorge Commission is a bi-state public agency headquartered in White Salmon, Washington, United States, which oversees the Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area that includes parts of Clark, Skamania, Klickitat, Wasco, Hood River and Multnomah Counties.

The Columbia River Gorge Commission was established in 1987 by the states of Oregon and Washington through an interstate compact, the Columbia River Gorge Compact.

The Columbia River Gorge Commission's mission is to establish, implement and enforce policies and programs that protect and enhance the scenic, natural, recreational and cultural resources of the Columbia River Gorge, and to support the economy of the area by encouraging growth to occur in existing urban areas and allowing economic development consistent with resource protection.

The Gorge Commission works with the U.S. Forest Service to set policy for protecting the non-federal lands in the Gorge. We also serve as an appeals board for land-use decisions, support counties in administering ordinances and encourage economic development.

About the NSA: The spectacularly beautiful Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area stretches 85 miles and includes portions of three Oregon and three Washington counties. Formed by ancient volcanoes and sculpted by incredible floods, the Columbia River Gorge carves an impressive corridor through the Cascade Mountains in Oregon and Washington as the great Columbia River flows to the Pacific Ocean. As the only sea-level route from the Great Basin to the Pacific Ocean, the Columbia River Gorge is a land of contrasts. The western Gorge, with an average annual rainfall of 75 inches, is a place of misty mountains, rich forestlands and more waterfalls than any area in the country. The eastern Gorge, with an annual rainfall of less than 15 inches, is a place of rim-rock bluffs, rolling hills, farms and ranchlands.

Name: Studio alpino
Description: Studio alpino provides advice on the management of wild mammals and birds and their habitats. Dr Federico Tettamanti, current CEO, is a wildlife biologist with extensive experience with wild animals. His experiences on several year as large predator manager and his experience in behavioural study of wildlife allow him to understand and seek new solutions for wildlife conservation

Name: Acquasie World NGO
Description: We are a NGO who acts for protection and preservation of environment

Name: EIECP
Description:
The Egyptian-Italian Environmental Cooperation Program (EIECP) is a strategic partnership between the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) and the Egyptian Ministry of Environment, funded by the Italian government and supported by Egypt's debt-for-development initiative.

 EIECP aims at developing Egypt's protected areas (PAs) system in Siwa, Wadi El Gemal, and Faiyum protected areas, mainly with a view of establishing income-generating mechanisms.

 Through Community-Based Natural Resource Management (CBNRM), EIECP empowers local communities to share responsibility for conserving nature. This approach facilitates the mobilization of resources toward the financial sustainability of Egypt's PAs system, thereby supporting its conservation and sustainable development endeavors.

Key Achievements:

 - Eco-Tourism: Established an eco-tourism association in Fayoum, developed a business plan for the Fayoum Protected Area, and created new tourism routes and products in Qatrani.

- NGO Support: Assisted Fayoum tourism NGOs with grant proposals, and provided training for local NGOs and women’s cooperatives.
Museum Upgrades: Established the Wadi El Hitan Museum and Qatrani Open Museum, and handed over facilities at the Qatrani Museum.

- Infrastructure and Community: Supplied 24 boats, upgraded the Wadi Rayan waterfall site, enhanced Fayoum infrastructure, and led cleaning and volunteer programs.
- Economic Empowerment: Trained 290 women and 220 crafters, launched a beekeeping project for Ababda women, and upgraded the pottery site in Nazla village. Established four permanent craft outlets and participated in major marketing events.

- Nature Conservation: Conducted studies on species like the sooty falcon, dugong, and sea turtles. Implemented rehabilitation activities for mangroves and acacia.

- Capacity Building and Knowledge Sharing: Implemented diverse capacity-building training for locals. Published several manuals to advance CBNRM in Egypt.

Name: Biodiversité Amazonienne
Description: Biodiversité Amazonienne est une association française de loi 1901 créée en 2004 par Lotty Morey, présidente de l’association. En 2006, elle se voit octroyer par le gouvernement péruvien la concession de conservation Yanayacu-Maquia pour une période de 40 ans renouvelables. L’objectif de l’association est d’agir contre la déforestation et pour la préservation de la concession de conservation Yanayacu-Maquia, un territoire de 38 699 hectares de forêt tropicale humide, située entre les rivières Yanayacu et Maquia dans le département du Loreto au centre de l’Amazonie péruvienne.

Name: Save the Rhino Trust Namibia
Description: Living outside of a fenced protected area, the Kunene’s rhinos are considered to be the largest truly free-ranging black rhino population left in the world. These rhino are protected and monitored by staff from Save the Rhino Trust, in conjunction with Namibia’s Ministry of Environment and Tourism (MET) and local communities. Four dedicated SRT patrolling and monitoring teams cover the core rhino range area of 25,000 square kilometres to deter poaching and to record detailed data on individual rhino, such as age, sex, rhino horn size and shape, ear notches and condition.

Name: Living Woods CIC
Description:
WHAT WE DO

Primarily we are focused on supporting and empowering local people to create; care for and connect with nature. 

So here is a brief explanation of each area

CREATING NEW WOODLANDS & ORCHARDS
We work with land managers and communities to help them put ‘the right tree, in the right place, for the right reason‘ to help create beautiful, diverse and productive habitats for people and wildlife.

- collecting local native tree seeds and growing on to plant out in future woodlands through Seeds2Trees
- increasing the area of native woodlands by running community tree planting and maintenance events, e.g. Whitehills for Gateshead Council as part of the North East Community Forest
- We are always looking for new areas of land with public access to create new woodlands and community orchards. If you think you might be able to help, find out more here

CARING FOR EXISTING WOODLANDS
We work with managers and custodians to care sustainably for their woodlands and orchards
- Rekindle: working with small woodland managers to produce sustainable local firewood, charcoal and coppice products like hurdles, besoms, and bows
- We run coppicing and woodland management at our own Horsley Coppice and we ran the woodland volunteering programme for Woodland Trust in the North East until 2022
- Silvo-pasture, agroforestry, rewilding, and Miyawaki Tiny Forests are all areas of advice we offer for including more trees within farmed landscapes

CONNECTING WITH NATURE
Through contact, emotion, beauty, meaning and compassion; the five ways that humans develop pro-nature behaviours and improve their mental well being. We deliver events based in this research by Derby University
- Branching Out: events and experiences
- Volunteering: tree planting days, orchard harvesting, seed growing, apple grafting
- Bringing woods into your life – donate, sign up to our newsletter, join our freelance team, buy local small woods products (coming soon)

Name: Association DECAN (Découvrir et aider la nature)
Blue Ridge Land Conservancy
Description:
Working with rural landowners in Bedford, Botetourt, Craig, Floyd, Franklin, Montgomery and Roanoke counties and the cities of Roanoke and Salem, Virginia, to forever protect farms, forests and wildlife habitat. A 501(c)(3) nonprofit accredited by the Land Trust Accreditation Commission. 

Land Savers United dba Blue Ridge Land Conservancy

Name: Mincin Insulation Service, Inc
Description: Mincin Insulation Service is the trusted name in Home Performance for the Western Pennsylvania region.  For over 45 years now, Mincin Insulation has offered residential homeowners’ affordable comfort, delivered with superior service and expertise.  Our mission is to help our community conserve energy by offering our clients tailored energy efficiency upgrade plans that are designed with building science principles and technologies.  We promote healthy, comfortable, and sustainable lifestyles to our customers by offering retrofit residential energy efficiency upgrades designed to increase comfort throughout the entire home.

Name: REMARCO - Interreg North Sea Project
Description:
REMARCO stands for Remediation, Management, Monitoring and Cooperation addressing North Sea UXO and is an european project funded by Interreg North Sea. 
REMARCO is on a mission to combat ocean pollution and protect marine life. Through cutting-edge methods and international collaboration, we are addressing the dangers posed by toxic remnants of past conflicts. 
Our goal: assess, monitor and remediate risks for a cleaner, healthier ocean environment.

Name: StrategyMedFor
Description: EU co-funded project, StrategyMedFor is aiming at creating a plan to protect and restore Mediterranean forests considering future climate changes.

Name: Rare Species Conservatory Foundation
Description: RSCF is a hands-on organization willing to take risks that many larger organizations do not. We recognize that space for wildlife is diminishing and nature is in crisis. Our job is to develop and implement effective, real-time solutions to complex problems affecting global biodiversity, resource management, and human/wildlife conflicts. Our mission is steadfast--we protect wildlife and wild places.

Name: Department of Conservation SPA Bhopal
