Description: The batch of conservation contains diverse mix of graduates with varied skill sets and experience. Master's program in Architectural Conservation from SPA Bhopal has given the opportunity to grow holistically in order to consciously conserve Our heritage.

Name: Great Works Regional Land Trust
Description: Great Works Regional Land Trust is a member-supported organization providing conservation options to landowners and programs for community members, including 18 preserves with hiking trails.  Since 1986, Great Works Regional Land Trust has conserved over 7,000 acres of natural, historic, agricultural, forestry, scenic and recreational resources in the southern Maine communities of Eliot, South Berwick, Berwick, North Berwick, Wells and Ogunquit.

Name: Choctawhatchee Basin Alliance
Description: Choctawhatchee Basin Alliance is a non-profit organization responsible for sustaining swimmable, fishable waterways through monitoring, education, restoration, and research. CBA promotes water stewardship within the Choctawhatchee watershed, growing the network of supporters who join in CBA’s mission to provide a future for these precious, natural resources.

Name: Parc National Shisiwani
Description: Parc National Shisiwani

Name: APPREN
Description: Engagée dans l'éducation environnementale, APPREN ambitionne d'être un acteur majeur de sensibilisation et d'actions de préservation de l'Environnement.

Name: Alldays Wildlife and Communities Research Centre
Description: The AWCRC is an experiential learning and conservation research centre in the Limpopo province of South-Africa. We help students and volunteers to gain in-depth knowledge of the community and natural splendour of the South African bushveld while being immersed in a community of like-minded individuals from across the globe. We invite all prospective students and volunteers to join our dynamic group and come and gain hands-on experience in the field research and take your first steps in a conservation career!

Name: Conservation Burial Alliance
Description: The Conservation Burial Alliance is a collaborative of conservation burial grounds and invested allies that fosters the conservation and sustainable management of land with natural burial for the benefit of people and the planet. We envision conservation burial becoming a preferred choice for burial as we educate people about its environmental, psychological, and economic benefits. Working within social and ecological spaces, we engage people in community through a shared commitment to leaving an environmental legacy. We believe that natural burial is a viable strategy for conserving intrinsically valuable land and restoring depleted landscapes while improving ecological health and promoting sustainable climate change mitigation.

Name: LEAF.GREEN
Description:
The world is transitioning towards a net-zero economy in order to control the levels of GHG in the atmosphere and tackle climate change.

Nature-based projects are the most cost-effective solution to remove CO2 from the air, which is crucial to the achievement of the net-zero goal. Additionally, these projects improve the livelihood of the local communities which are typically in developing countries.

LEAF is an independent manager of nature-based projects, with the goal of offsetting over 10 million tons of annual carbon emissions whilst providing an income for thousands of people. That is enough to offset more than a fifth of the carbon generated by New York City. 

We control our assets in-house in order to ensure that the highest ESG standards are upheld. Our team has many years of experience in carbon offsets, forestry, sustainable agriculture, as well as project and community relations management. 

We strive to be the worlds leading nature-based project owner and manager.

Name: Blue Vision Tanzania
Description: The ocean is the pool of life under water, The health of the world depends on the ocean system. Disturbing the ocean is the disturbance of the Earth system. Blue Vision Tanzania is working to protect and conserve the ocean and coastal regions of Tanzania for sustainable use of it's resources and restore the Earth's healthy condition.

Name: Projet / Progetto GECO
Description:
Le projet européen GE.CO (Gestion Écologique des Conflits), cofinancé par l’Union Européenne, réunit six partenaires français et italiens autour d’un objectif commun : mieux connaître, préserver et redynamiser la biodiversité dans des espaces transfrontaliers franco-italiens soumis à des contraintes ou des conflits d’usage. Ces espaces, utilisés aujourd’hui à des fins touristiques, récréatives ou économiques, pourront à terme contribuer à améliorer la connectivité des trames vertes et bleues de l’espace transfrontalier. 
Avec la collaboration des parties prenantes du territoire, l’appui des collectivités engagées sur le terrain et la participation des scolaires, GE.CO souhaite sensibiliser et faire agir les publics concernés pour protéger leur territoire.

Il progetto europeo GE.CO (Gestione ecologica dei conflitti), cofinanziato dall'Unione Europea, riunisce sei partner italiani e francesi con un obiettivo comune: migliorare la conoscenza, la conservazione e la rivitalizzazione della biodiversità nelle aree transfrontaliere  italo-francesi soggette a vincoli o conflitti d'uso. Queste aree, attualmente utilizzate per finalità turistiche, ricreative o economiche, potrebbero col tempo contribuire a migliorare la connettività delle reti verdi e blu nell'area transfrontaliera. 
Con la collaborazione degli stakeholder locali, il sostegno delle autorità locali impegnate sul campo e la partecipazione dei ragazzi delle scuole, GE.CO mira a sensibilizzare e incoraggiare il pubblico interessato ad agire per proteggere il proprio territorio.

Les partenaires . I partner :  Oxalis, la Fédération de Pêche des Hautes-Alpes, il Parco del Monviso, le Parc Naturel Régional du Queyras, la Città metropolitana di Torino, Mines Paris - PSL.

Ce projet est cofinancé par l’Union Européenne, fonds Interreg France-Italia ALCOTRA. 

Questo progetto è cofinanziato dal fondo Interreg Francia-Italia ALCOTRA dell'Unione Europea.

Name: Synergie des Ecologistes pour l'Environnement et le Développement
thintanks North
Description: Designed for easy installation & space-saving, ThinTanks Slimline Rainwater Tanks are the thinnest available water tank for any capacity. On the forefront of ground-breaking technology, the Australia-designed ThinTanks range of rainwater storage tanks has been developed for space-saving water conservation across both residential and commercial applications.

Name: Réseau de la Jeunesse Nigeriénne sur les Changement Climatique au Niger (RJNCC-NIGER)
Description:
Le Réseau de la Jeunesse Nigérienne sur les Changements Climatiques (RJNCC) est une initiative citoyenne qui fédère les jeunes du Niger autour des enjeux environnementaux, climatiques et de développement durable. Né de la volonté de renforcer l’engagement des jeunes face à l’urgence climatique, le RJNCC agit comme un catalyseur d’actions concrètes, de sensibilisation et de plaidoyer.

Composé de jeunes leaders, d’étudiants, d’associations locales et de volontaires, le réseau œuvre à :
	•	 Promouvoir l’éducation climatique et la justice environnementale,
	•	 Renforcer la participation des jeunes aux politiques nationales et internationales sur le climat,
	•	 Soutenir les initiatives locales de résilience climatique, notamment dans les zones vulnérables,
	•	 Porter la voix des jeunes nigériens lors de forums régionaux et mondiaux (COP, forums de l’Union africaine…).

Le RJNCC se positionne comme un acteur clé dans la lutte pour un Niger plus vert, équitable et durable, en lien avec les Objectifs de Développement Durable (ODD) et l’Accord de Paris.

Name: Seconde Nature Ateliers
Description: Seconde Nature Ateliers est un (OBNL) dédié à la protection de l’environnement et à la promotion des arts du recyclage. À travers nos ateliers créatifs et éducatifs, nous sensibilisons les communautés à l’importance du recyclage et des pratiques éco-responsables. Notre mission est d’éveiller les consciences, d’encourager des habitudes durables et d’inspirer la créativité en transformant les déchets en œuvres d’art uniques. Rejoignez-nous pour cultiver une seconde nature respectueuse de notre planète.

Name: Chilengedwe Zambia
Description: Dedicated to stakeholder engagement and propagation of information on green living, green business and green economy.

Name: Young Biologists' Association of Sri Lanka
Description: Young Biologists' Association (YBA) promotes Biology, Environmental Management and Healthy Lifestyles among young generation. This was formed in 1998 by the Institute of Biology, Sri Lanka, the umbrella organization for professional Biologists' (Academics, Researchers) in the country.

Name: Proyecto Yungas
Description:
Yungas is an innovative, indigenous-led initiative designed to sustainably generate wealth in the world’s most biodiverse rainforest—the southern Peruvian Amazon. By empowering local communities, we aim to protect critical ecosystems and secure indigenous land rights, ensuring that both the environment and its people thrive.

This project is a testament to the power of indigenous leadership in conservation, blending traditional knowledge with sustainable development to safeguard one of the planet's most vital regions.

Name: CPIE Loire Océane
Description:
Loire Océane Environnement, labellisée CPIE (Centre Permanent d'Initiative pour l'Environnement) en 2014, a pour objet de contribuer au développement durable et à la préservation de l'environnement sur son territoire.
Notre association agit sur le territoire de 4 communautés de communes, situé entre Loire et Vilaine et de l'Atlantique au sillon de Bretagne.
Son rôle est de participer à l’évolution des comportements et des pratiques dans une société en mutation, pour apporter une contribution effective au développement durable du territoire en proposant une approche globale et transversale, auprès de publics variés (particuliers, scolaires, collectivités, entreprises, agriculteurs...). Cette démarche est complétée par des initiatives contribuant à la gestion de l’espace et à la valorisation des patrimoines.

Name: Wild We Stand
Description:
Wild We Stand is a global environmental collective built to challenge the status quo in conservation and to forge bonds. 

We provide a platform for frontline practitioners, students, academics, and community members to share knowledge and stories, to host transformative discussions, and imagine the future of the conservation industry built to bring people together. Fostering a new era of proactive and accessible conservation led by a justice-centred perspective that doesn’t equal complicity. We believe that empowerment and solidarity are essential across this fragmented field to drive effective change for communities and wildlife.

Join the movement at www.wildwestand.com.

Name: Central Pennsylvania Conservancy
Description: Central Pennsylvania Conservancy is an accredited regional land trust in south central Pennsylvania. We protect and conserve land and natural resources through conservation easements, land transfers, and maintaining public nature preserves.

Name: EQI KSA
Description:
Our team of environmental engineers, management specialists, ecologists and architects deliver a wide range of environmental management and design services. Our approach is based on introducing advanced natural resource management practices to improve basic infrastructure and utilities, promote safe and affordable environmental services and ensure environmentally sustainable development.

Our Areas of Expertise:
Solid Waste Management
Water Supply and Sanitation
Environmental Impact Assessment, Research and Planning
Urban Planning and Architectural Design
Natural and Cultural Resource Management
Ecotourism and Adaptive Reuse Agricultural Resource Management

Name: Fundación Artemisan
Description: La Fundación Artemisan es una organización privada sin ánimo de lucro, cuyo objetivo es promover la gestión y conservación de especies de fauna y flora a través de la investigación, la comunicación y la defensa jurídica, prestando especial atención al aprovechamiento sostenible de especies cinegéticas para el beneficio de los ecosistemas y del mundo rural.

Name: Dirección del Parque Nacional Galápagos
Description: La Dirección del Parque Nacional Galápagos (DPNG) es responsable de la conservación de la integridad ecológica y la biodiversidad de los ecosistemas insulares y marinos de las áreas protegidas del archipiélago, así como del uso racional de los bienes y servicios que estos generan para la comunidad.

Name: South African Shark Conservancy
Description: SASC is a non-profit organisation working to promote the conservation and sustainable use of vulnerable marine species and spaces. Our research is ethics-driven, meaning we have the best interests of people, species and habitats in mind. We focus on up-skilling early career scientists and conservationists through training and skills development, and work with a broad range of international partners to change the face of ocean conservation.

Name: Fondation des pêcheurs
Description:
Fondée par la Fédération Nationale de la Pêche, la Fondation des Pêcheurs agit pour préserver et protéger les milieux aquatiques et les zones humides en France. 

Nous menons des actions concrètes telles que l’acquisition de terres, la restauration des habitats naturels, et la sensibilisation du public aux enjeux écologiques. En partenariat avec des acteurs locaux et scientifiques, nous visons un avenir où la biodiversité aquatique prospère.

Name: APFBC-Communicating Conservation
Description: The Assam Project on Forest and Biodiversity Conservation Society (APFBC Society) is a Collaborative endeavor of Government of Assam and Agénce Française de Développement

Name: Africa Conservation Program
Description: Africa Conservation Program (ACP) is a pan-African, community-based conservation initiative. Founded in Uganda and rooted in Bwindi, ACP brings together conservationists, artists, cultural leaders, and youth under one shared vision: to restore harmony between people, nature, and heritage.

Name: CEPAZE
Description: Le CEPAZE (Centre d'Echanges et de Promotion des Artisans en Zones à Equiper) est à l'origine une association à vocation humanitaire et sans but lucratif qui a pour objectif d’apporter une contribution au développement économique, social et humain de pays en développement, en facilitant le transfert de compétences techniques et technologiques des nations industrialisées vers ces pays, dans le respect de leur autonomie et de leur civilisation.

Name: PEDRO MATESANZ VERA
Description:
Empresa de arqueología. Especialista en Patrimonio Histórico.
Me caracterizo por la rigurosidad, eficacia y rapidez en los proyectos en los que presto mis servicios.

Name: SNPN Réserve naturelle nationale de Camargue
AQUATIC ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT ASSOCIATION(AQUAMEN)
Walker Basin Conservancy
Description: We work to restore and maintain Walker Lake while protecting agricultural, environmental, and recreational interests throughout the Walker Basin.

Name: Eco Zamba
Description: Nous croyons en un avenir où chaque individu, chaque communauté et chaque entreprise prend part à la protection de notre environnement. Notre vision est celle d'un monde où les forêts prospèrent, où la biodiversité est préservée et où les générations futures peuvent bénéficier d'un environnement sain et équilibré.

Name: Société d'Aménagement du Littoral de Nouakchott
Description:
The project aims to protect the city of Nouakchott from the risks of submersion and flooding, heightened by the effects of climate change, while enhancing the attractiveness of the coastline to provide more opportunities for the entire population.
 
It is implemented by a joint venture in which the Government of the Islamic Republic of Mauritania invests equally alongside The Urban Resilience Fund, an urban resilience fund managed by Meridiam.

Name: Organisation for Sustainable Development and Biodiversity (ODDB ONG)
Description: ODDB NGO is a non-profit, non-political, non-denominational, non-governmental organisation created on the initiative of young Beninese people who are convinced that there can be no sustainable development without the involvement and empowerment of local communities in the management of natural resources. Our activities are focused on the forests with endangered primate species in Benin, which are used as "umbrella" species in our biodiversity conservation programmes with local populations.

Name: A Rocha India
Description:
A Rocha is a global network of organizations committed to combatting biodiversity loss via scientific research, environmental education, and community-based conservation initiatives. It has been involved in environmental conservation since 1983, with programmes that are cross-cultural in nature and encourage a community emphasis through residential field study centres, site-based projects, and advocacy. 
A Rocha India, established in 2003, is an independent body of the A Rocha network, which spans six continents and more than 20 countries.
The A Rocha India Field Study Centre, located in the heart of the community, has been effective in fostering a sense of urgency for environmental conservation in a vast number of people via various events organized at the centre. As a result of these activities, the organization has begun to address habitat and wildlife management challenges through environmental education, conservation science, and community engagement. 
As part of its mission, A Rocha India strives to investigate, improve, and address habitat issues, with a focus on identifying grassroots issues, capacity building, participatory techniques, and tangible assessments. This is being achieved through research concepts while also empowering communities to enhance their well-being and livelihoods through outreach, awareness, and policy change.

Name: Mukutan Conservancy
Description:
Mukutan is an expansive conservancy in Laikipia on the rugged edge of Kenya’s Great Rift Valley, with sweeping views overlooking Lake Baringo, and across the iconic ecosystem of the Laikipia Plateau, to the slopes of Mount Kenya and beyond. 

Life abounds through our ancient cedar forests, scrubland plains and down the mysterious Mukutan Gorge, where visitors can find peace and inspiration in this remote, storied corner of the world. 

Our name derives from the Swahili word for “a meeting place” – and sitting at the convergence of diverse biomes, cultures, languages, and ideas, we strive to be a centre for collaboration, courage, and coexistence through conservation.

Name: AFRICA TOURISM AND ENVIRONMENT INITIATIVES
Description: We are an Eco-Tourism NGO, Empowering Communities through Sustainable Tourism, Climate Action, Biodiversity conservation and Environmental Stewardship. #PetATree #EkkulaTourism #WildlifeConcerts #MissClimate #VoicesofResilience

Name: INDIGENOUS LED
Description: INDIGENOUS LED was founded by Indigenous People for Indigenous People to elevate the power of Indigenous-led conservation to address our planetary and relationship crises.

Name: Ulovane Environmental Training
Description:
Ulovane Environmental Training has been a beacon of excellence in providing comprehensive, field-guide, and nature training programs for many years. Nestled amidst the pristine landscapes of Africa, we have been passionately committed to nurturing the next generation of conservationists and ecotourism professionals.

Since our inception, our dedicated team of instructors, naturalists, and mentors have instilled a deep love and respect for the environment, fostering a profound connection between humans and nature. Our students have gained valuable knowledge and skills and have been inspired to become custodians of the earth, driven by the desire to conserve and protect its magnificent biodiversity.

Ulovane is embracing a transformative journey as we move forward, evolving to meet the ever-changing demands of the conservation and ecotourism industry. 

At Ulovane, we take pride in our commitment to delivering cutting-edge conservation education and experiences. As a leader in his field, Schalk brings a wealth of expertise to the table. With a diverse background spanning conservation biology, ecology, sustainable development, and more, Schalk ensures that our students and guests receive top-notch training and holistic perspectives on conservation and environmental stewardship.

Drawing on years of experience and a profound passion for the natural world, Schalk leads the way in inspiring the next generation of conservationists. Through innovative teaching methods and a deep understanding of the complexities of conservation, he empowers our students and guests to become true advocates for our planet’s well-being.

Schalk & Candice’s vision for Ulovane is to cultivate a community of driven individuals who share a common goal: to protect and preserve the world’s biodiversity.

Name: Iowa Wildlife Federation
Description: We envision an Iowa with abundant wildlife, well-managed public and private lands, clean water and healthy soils that can be enjoyed by current and future generations through a multitude of outdoor recreational pursuits, including hunting, fishing and wildlife watching.

Name: ARGUS Project
Description: ARGUS (Non-destructive, scalable, smart monitoring of remote cultural treasures) is a European research project aiming at advancing in the development of digital tools for the preventive preservation of effective remote monitoring of built heritage assets.

Name: Ecosolutions Worldwide
Description: Promouvoir la restauration et la conservation de terre dégradée,Offrir des solutions durables et innovantes pour les défis environnementaux,  et fournir des conseils et des stratégies pour le développement durable.

Name: Ségou vert
Description: Plantation et entretien des arbres à fin de sauver la nature et l'environnement.

Name: Asociación Red Colombiana de Reservas Naturales - RESNATUR
Description: Somos una red de personas y organizaciones de la sociedad civil, que hemos decidido manejar nuestros predios como reservas naturales. De esta manera, contribuimos a la conservación de la biodiversidad y sus servicios ecosistémicos, la producción sostenible y la construcción de tejido social.

Name: GreenCalculator
Description: Green Calculator est un jeune star-up qui se positionne au Sénégal pour des initiatives de reboisement accès sur la vente du crédit carbone.

Name: Conscious Explorer
Description:
Discover Conscious Explorer, a platform for passionate nature lovers, dedicated conservationists, and avid explorers – united by a shared vision of conscious travel.

We aim to:
- Promote conscious trips by encouraging travelers to make informed and ethical choices.
- Educate and inspire by providing a magazine full of inspiration and resources for travelers before, during, and after their journey.
- Support conservation efforts through partnering with conservation Organizations, conscious tour operators, and accommodations.

Follow along and get your regular dose of conscious travel inspiration!

Name: ECONUDGE DRC
Description: ECONUDGE DRC est une entreprise qui vise à influencer le comportement de la population, l'inciter à la politique d'assainissement commune sans instruction directe et transformer en matériaux écologiques les déchets plastiques.

Name: Living Gaia
Description:
Living Gaia is a non-profit association in Berlin, Germany.
The concern and goal of the association is to connect the knowledge and wisdom of the Indigenous cultures of the Amazon with the knowledge and wisdom of Western cultures. Since 2018, we're running the Huni Kuin Land Purchase project: A 16.800 ha reserve and model area for Indigenous-led conservation in Acre, Brazilian Amazon. https://hunikuin.org/en/

Name: Le Cercle des Pionniers de la RSE
Green Bonds Brasil
Description:
A Green Bonds Brasil (GBB) foi fundada em 2019 e desde então vem desenvolvendo serviços como fintech de carbono, especializando-se no desenvolvimento de projetos e originação de créditos de carbono e de resíduos plásticos para empresas e indústrias agropecuárias e gestoras de resíduos sólidos, que buscam novas tecnologias e sustentabilidade de processos e produtos, em direção a uma economia de baixo carbono.
A GBB utiliza instrumentos econômicos e financeiros de precificação e monetização de ativos carbono, gerando receitas para investimento em novas tecnologias e processos sustentáveis, seja pela comercialização do crédito (carbono ou plástico) ou lastreando operações de FIAGRO e Cédula de Produto Rural Verde (CPR-Verde). 


#greenbondsbrasil #creditodecarbono #economiacircular #custodiaemonitoramento

Name: Nettoyage Everest 2024
Description: Expédition de nettoyage du camp de base et du trek de l'Everest

Name: Wisconsin Land+Water Conservation Association
Description:
For over fifty years, Wisconsin Land and Water Conservation Association (WI Land+Water) has been working to improve the understanding of conservation issues and achieve tangible improvements in land use management. We are nonprofit organization that supports the local and statewide efforts of all county Land Conservation Committee supervisors and Land and Water Conservation Department staff across Wisconsin. Our mission is to protect, conserve, and enhance Wisconsin’s natural recourses by advocating for and supporting county conservation efforts, for current and future generations. 

We know that every citizen benefits from the protection of our natural resources. Land Conservation Committees and Land and Water Conservation Departments are the public’s vital link with local landowners to promote the implementation of conservation practices and achieve greater environmental stewardship of the land.

Name: OpenHive Honey
Description:
OpenHive is a beekeeping conservation organisation and community run by Kyle Petrie, Mark Earley and Jack McGrath. The simple focus of OpenHive is to harness a combined passion for beekeeping with a need for environmental sustainability. Launched in November 2019 OpenHive rears and breeds Irish black honeybees. To support this process OpenHive sells locally-produced Irish honey from apiaries in Dublin and Wicklow. Get in touch to learn more about how we can partner with you on your CSR or ESG goals.

OpenHive keeps bees naturally, with as few interventions as possible. OpenHive is committed to maintaining local biodiversity and decreasing impact on the environment, specifically aiming to help the Irish honeybee re-establish herself as a central part of the Irish ecosystem. As part of our eco-friendly approach the jars that hold the honey are screen-printed to eliminate the need for single-use plastic labelling and can be returned for future reuse.

"We want to do as much as we can to help the Irish black honeybee through our beekeeping and breeding of the bees we keep. The role of the bee is essential in the conservation of the biodiversity of the Irish habitat. We want the Apis Mellifera Mellifera to be the norm both for local beekeepers and in the wild here in Dublin. 

As the OpenHive grows we intend on being an active part of the local beekeeping community, providing services such as honey extraction and jarring. We are very excited to champion ethical and eco-friendly beekeeping methods along our journey. "
- Kyle Petrie & Mark Earley

Name: Fundación Local Partners
Description: Local Partners es una organización sin ánimo de lucro que impulsa la sostenibilidad y fortalece las cadenas de valor en la producción en el sector cafetero colombiano. En cooperación con aliados estratégicos, diseñamos y ejecutamos proyectos que mejoran la calidad de vida de los caficultores, aumentan la productividad de sus cultivos y protegen el medioambiente. Nuestra misión es construir un futuro más equitativo e innovador para el campo, promoviendo prácticas responsables y sostenibles. Creemos en el potencial de los actores privados como motor crucial de la prosperidad rural sostenible, promoviendo una relación armónica entre la producción y la naturaleza.

Name: Wetland4Change
Description:
Wetland4Change project (Interreg Euro-MED Programme within the Natural Heritage Mission) aims at validating transferable solutions based on wetland conservation and restoration for climate adaptation and mitigation in order to accelerate the capacities of wetland managers and of policy makers to cope with the climate crisis 

The main project outputs are: two wetland-based solutions tested and
validated in 5 pilot sites at 5 countries (BG, GR, IT, FR, ES): for carbon-sequestration and for flood regulation. On top of this a dynamic user-oriented transfer plan, powered by science-based knowledge, will be developed for the first time

Name: TerraWater Institute
Description: Institut TerraWater supports and develops actions to promote a reliable and sustainable energy transition, in the pursuit of climate stabilization, environmental protection and access to well-being and dignity for all populations, on a French, European and global scale.

Name: The Conservation Equity Project
Description:
The Conservation Equity Project is a community interest company with a simple goal; to increase Black British access to the conservation and wider green jobs sector by offering career-focused mentoring and paid internships in association with leading partner institutions.
#ConservationEquity #DEI

Name: African Pangolin Working Group
Description: An organisation dedicated to the conservation of the four African pangolin species, with a focus on retrieval of pangolins out of the illegal trade, rehabilitation, and release back into the wild. Education and research are also key focus areas.

Name: Henry's Fork Foundation
Description:
The Henry's Fork Foundation is the only organization whose sole purpose is to conserve, protect, and restore the unique fisheries, wildlife, and aesthetic qualities of the Henry's Fork and its watershed.

Established in 1984, the Henry's Fork Foundation (HFF) uses a science-based, collaborative approach to promote favorable streamflow, good water quality, healthy fish populations, and a positive fishing experience in the Henry’s Fork and South Fork Snake River watersheds.
 
HFF's work with partners has measurably reduced the negative effects of drought and changing climate through improved fishery health and fishing conditions.

Name: Gardiens Des Iles
Description:
L’association a pour objet de jouer le rôle de Centre de Recherche et d’Action sur la Biodiversité, l’Écologie et la Société, en contribuant à l’amélioration des connaissances, à la préservation de l’environnement et à la formation des jeunes dans les domaines liés à l’environnement, notamment les étudiants et diplômés calédoniens.

Elle vise à préserver la biodiversité de manière générale, avec une attention particulière portée aux habitats et aux espèces littorales et insulaires (oiseaux marins, récifs coralliens, espèces emblématiques), ainsi qu’aux espèces terrestres comme les chauves-souris. Elle agit également contre les espèces exotiques envahissantes.

L’association œuvre à la mise en place et à la diffusion de Solutions fondées sur la Nature, afin de restaurer les habitats et renforcer la résilience des écosystèmes et des communautés face au changement climatique.

Sa vision s’inscrit dans une démarche de développement durable, inclusive et participative, reposant sur une gestion de proximité de l’environnement, le renforcement des capacités et l’empowerment des jeunes leaders locaux de la conservation.

Enfin, l’association déploie ses missions sur l’ensemble de la Nouvelle-Calédonie, et plus largement dans le Pacifique, avec une attention particulière portée aux zones littorales et marines, y compris les îlots, notamment sur le territoire de la commune de Païta.

Name: Trap and Trigger
Description:
Trap and Trigger | Specialist Pest Control for Conservation Outcomes

Bases in Wellington, Central Otago and Northland. With team members operating in most regions of New Zealand.

At Trap and Trigger, we combine practical field expertise with ecological understanding to deliver high-impact pest control solutions that protect Aotearoa’s native biodiversity.  

Based in New Zealand, our team works across diverse and often challenging environments — from remote bush to urban reserves — using best-practice methods and innovative approaches to suppress and eradicate pest species. We’re experienced in the safe and effective use of traps, toxins, aerial control, and monitoring systems, and are committed to continuously improving outcomes for both people and nature.

Our kaupapa is grounded in collaboration. We work closely with iwi, hapū, local and central government, NGOs, and private landowners to design and deliver tailored solutions that align with community values and conservation goals.  

Whether it’s predator control, pest plant removal, biosecurity support, or large-scale eradication operations, Trap and Trigger brings a skilled, motivated team with a passion for restoring the mauri of our natural places.

 Practical. Professional. Purpose-driven.  
 Working Internationally, primarily in Aotearoa New Zealand

Name: Guanacaste Dry Forest Conservation Fund
Description:
Founded in spirit in 1985, and formalized in 1997 as the Guanacaste Dry Forest Conservation Fund (GDFCF) with a gift from the Kyoto Prize awarded to Dan Janzen, we have evolved to become the formal non-profit and close working partner to the Costa Rican government's 169,000-hectare Área de Conservación Guanacaste (ACG) within their national system of protected areas.  

GDFCF's formal mission statement is to: “Promote the long-term survival of the ecosystems and biodiversity of the Área Silvestre Protegido (wild protected area) of Área de Conservación Guanacaste in Costa Rica through conservation, education, science-based management and biodiversity development.”

Although GDFCF is a U.S. based 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization, essentially all funds go directly to Costa Rica to support 38+ resident Costa Rican biodiversity field managers/researchers, equipment, land acquisition, and educational programs in ACG.

Name: Mellivora Systems
Description: Mellivora Systems offers customised conservation law enforcement training programs and equipment for Protected Areas, equipping teams with the skills, knowledge & gear they need to effect the best results in their operational environment.

Name: Areal Bach
Description: Auf 10`000m2 im Osten der Stadt St. Gallen ist die Zwischennnutzung Areal Bach entstanden. Eine kreative Oase und inspirierender Begegnungsort für alle. Mit Kinderbaustelle, Gastronomie, Kulturgärten, Veranstaltungsplatz und Lerngarten. Eine Spielwiese auf Zeit. Eine grüne Oase mitten in einem belebten Stadtteil. Ein bunter Tummelplatz für Gross und Klein.

Name: Children's Eternal Rainforest
Description:
The Children's Eternal Rainforest, nestled in the mountains of northwestern Costa Rica, was bought and protected thanks to fundraising by children from around the world. The initial effort by a group of Swedish primary school students in the late 1980’s quickly grew to include schools, individuals, and organizations from 44 different countries. Today the Children’s Eternal Rainforest is Costa Rica’s largest private reserve, spanning 22,600 hectares (55,800 acres). It is owned and managed by a Costa Rican non-profit organization called the Monteverde Conservation League.

The Children’s Eternal Rainforest (or “BEN”, after the Spanish “Bosque Eterno de los Niños”) provides water for hundreds of surrounding communities and farms, as well as for hydroelectric projects that produce more than a third of Costa Rica's electricity. The reserve is the centerpiece of a larger block of protected areas that draws tens of thousands of visitors from around the world each year, providing the economic mainstay of thousands of local families. It also harbors an off-the-charts proportion of the world's biodiversity.

Though the BEN covers just 0.00015% of the Earth’s land surface, it is home to a wildly disproportionate amount of the world's flora and fauna – including 2% of the world's orchid species, more than 3% of the butterflies, and almost 5% of the world's birds. Birds that depend on the BEN include almost 100 North American species that either spend the whole winter there, or pass through during their spring and fall migrations. Hundreds of thousands of North American birds (yes, really!) pass through the BEN during migration. The reserve is also home to numerous endangered and endemic species, and all six of Costa Rica’s wild felines, including the elusive jaguar.

There are many valuable patches of nature in the world, but very few as biologically diverse as the BEN.

Name: Sussex Underwater
Description:
We are a group of passionate local people who want to make everyone fall in love with the local sea in Sussex.
Our journey began with the inspiring tale of our co founder Eric Smith, a 76 year old local diver who was an eye witness to destructive fishing practices which destroyed the seabed. His campaign to get trawlers banned in Sussex has been showcased in the BBC documentary (Our Lives; Our Sea Forest on BBC I player) highlighting the profound impact one individuals passion and drive can have to galvanise a change for the better. Sussex Underwater was set up so the sea was never “out of sight and out of mind” again.

Name: Friends of the Dales
Description: We are a registered membership charity campaigning for the conservation of the landscape, biodiversity and cultural heritage of the Yorkshire Dales National Park. We also aim to promote the social and economic wellbeing of local communities and encourage others from outside the Park to access, enjoy and project this special area.

Name: Stiftung Artenschutz
Description: Die Stiftung Artenschutz ist eine Gemeinschaftsinitiative von Zoologischen Gärten, Tierparks und Naturschutzorganisationen. Erklärtes Ziel ist der Erhalt existenziell gefährdeter Tierarten, für die es bislang keine ausreichende Unterstützung gibt, sowie der Schutz ihrer ursprünglichen Lebensräume in internationalen und nationalen Projekten. Der Schwerpunkt der Arbeit liegt damit im In-situ-Schutz von Arten und deren Lebensräumen.

Name: South Rift Association of Land Owners
Description: SORALO is a community-based and community-driven land trust established in 2004 to unite Maasai communities in the management of their landscape. Our mission is to maintain a healthy and connected landscape for people and wildlife in Kenya’s South Rift. Our landscape straddles between the Maasai Mara and Amboseli. In this area, local Maasai communities have lived with their livestock alongside wildlife, forests, and grasslands, maintaining a landscape of exceptional biological and cultural diversity. This rangeland hosts large mammal populations, including both wildlife and livestock. Today, this is an increasingly threatened landscape, confronting a growing population, a culture in transition, and land use changes that threaten both wildlife and their livestock. By working with the communities to reverse these threats SORALO envisions a healthy and intact landscape that sustains the pastoralist community and wildlife.

Name: Seafood Positive
Description:
OneFishTwoFish was created by Marine Biologist, Dr Chris Gillies, and is supported by a talented team of ocean lovers. 
We believe it is entirely possible to reverse biodiverse loss in the oceans within our lifetime. Our strategy is to move towards a ‘circular’ seafood economy, where fish stocks are regularly replenished, and harvest is balanced with production.

We are confident this will work because we have seen it happen every day, on small scales. scientists and communities all over the world have demonstrated ocean recovery is possible.

But they can’t do it alone, and they can’t do it without your support and help.

Name: BEV BENIN ONG
Description:
BEV Bénin ONG (ex Vivia Miel ONG) est une organisation non-gouvernementale, à but non lucratif, qui a pour mission d'apporter des réponses efficientes et cohérentes aux défis environnementaux et socio-culturels des communautés locales au Bénin. 

Les domaines d’intervention portent sur trois axes principaux, à savoir l’environnement, l’éducation et la santé. 

 ENVIRONNEMENT

Promouvoir la conservation et la gestion durable des ressources naturelles d’importance communautaire
 Développer des alternatives socio-économiques écologiquement durables au sein des couches vulnérables

 EDUCATION

Promouvoir une éducation citoyenne de qualité
Impacter le système éducatif à travers des connaissances et comportements environnementaux prospectifs et responsables

SANTÉ

Améliorer équitablement l’accès et l’utilisation des services de qualité pour la santé maternelle néonatale et infantile
Briser le cycle de transmission des maladies hydriques à travers les règles d'hygiène et d’assainissement chez les enfants de 0 à 5 ans.

Name: Fundación Parque Tantauco
Description:
El Parque Tantauco es uno de los proyectos de conservación más importantes del país, situado en el extremo sur de la región de Los Lagos, en la mística Isla de Chiloé. 

El parque abierto al público es de más 100 mil hectáreas, inserto en un sitio prioritario para la protección de su ecosistema, naturaleza, paisajes y con una red de senderos de clase mundial.

Sus objetos de conservación principal son el zorro de Darwin, la ranita de Darwin, el ciprés de las Guaitecas, el Bosque Nordpatagónico, las turberas y la cultura chona. Adicionalmente, las aguas que lo rodean son zonas de apareamiento y reproducción de las ballenas azules.

Ya son dos décadas del compromiso con la naturaleza y las futuras generaciones que tuvo el expresidente de Chile, Sebastián Piñera Echenique, al fundar este mágico parque.

Name: Site Natura2000 des îles de Houat et Hoedic
IHBC North Branch
Description: Web page for IHBC North branch.

Name: Mycosphaera
Description:
Mycosphaera actively engages in the field of mycology to promote and develop this science through various channels:

Education: Offering training, excursions, and workshops.
Popularization: Publishing blogs, articles, and organizing conferences.
Conservation: Conducting scientific inventories.
Inspiration: Showcasing the beauty of fungi through photography.
Event Organization: Hosting mycological fairs.
Resources: Facilitating networking and providing a virtual library.
Consultancy: Supporting mycological projects.
Our mission is to value, encourage, and recruit a new generation of mycologists, and to disseminate mycological knowledge. We aim to equip communities with a better understanding of ecological issues, foster the emergence of projects related to mycology, and promote mutual assistance and networking among established actors in the field.

Name: NVT
Description:
NVT is a small community driven NPO working at the cutting edge of integrated conservation in South Africa. We operate in four main arenas, namely:
Conservation, 
Education, 
Community, and 
Research. 

We take a holistic view of people and the environment, and use our four programs to help shape how people live, how they view the world around them, and how they as individuals can contribute to conserving the natural world. Nestled in the magnificent Garden Route, and situated in world renowned Nature’s Valley, we are a passionate group of conservationists that aim to make a practical difference with the work we do.

Name: OPCC - Oficina de Protección de la Calidad del Cielo del Norte de Chile
Description: Más de 20 años trabajando para preservar los cielos del norte de chile.

Name: Ilhas de Biodiversidade
Description: Plantamos Micro Florestas Densas com espécies nativas e de rápido crescimento . Utilizamos espaços urbanos e industriais .

Name: Jock Clough Marine Foundation
Description: The Jock Clough Marine Foundation supports marine conservation with a focus on Western Australia. We also provide encouragement and financial support to assist scientists in their post-graduate studies.

Name: Ocean Advocate
Description:
Ocean Advocate is a non-profit organisation inspiring people from all walks of life to actively participate in ocean conservation. We believe every one of us can make a difference in our own way. 

Check out our Ocean Advocate Summit and the Ocean Advocate Academy!

Name: STAR
Description:
STAR is a national, non-profit organization established to develop and expand the STAR framework for conservation practice evaluation, implementation, and valuation, to create a standardized approach for STAR initiatives across the US, and to provide guidance and support for new and existing STAR Affiliates. The goal of STAR is to facilitate successful and sustained conservation practice adoption and implementation by farmers and ranchers by providing STAR Affiliates a comprehensive toolkit and tailored guidance to empower producer success and to connect them to market programs.

The STAR framework includes a simple, straightforward, and free tool developed by farmers and ranchers to increase voluntary conservation practice implementation on agricultural lands. The STAR 1 to 5 rating system is used to evaluate conservation practices on individual fields using a locally-driven, science-based approach to effectively and efficiently scale grassroots conservation efforts by incentivizing and rewarding the adoption of practices that have relevance to producers and their supply chain partners. STAR bridges the gap between producers’ perceptions of sustainability and outcomes-based supply chain sustainability programs.

Name: Shield Ranch
Description:
At Shield Ranch, stewarding and protecting Texas ecosystems is our solemn purpose. Our three ranches encompass 37,000 acres of sustainably managed wildlands in Central and West Texas. The Shield Ranch Foundation, our nonprofit entity, supports Camp El Ranchito and community nature-immersion programs at its new sustainably-built Campsite at Shield Ranch. To support our ambitious land management program, we partner with renowned conservation organizations and provide transformative nature-immersion experiences for Texans. Our state is growing. Our communities are expanding. And truly wild places are becoming increasingly rare. But together, we can ensure Shield Ranch will stay wild for good.

Our Vision: Shield Ranch will always be a protected wildland; a place to share, a place to connect and a place to protect.

Our Mission: To steward and share Shield Ranch in ways that educate, inspire and transform.

To achieve this mission, we:
• Restore the land through the practice of land stewardship
• Protect the land and its resources through advocacy and engagement in regional issues
• Facilitate research and education
• Offer a diverse array of educational programs and nature immersion experiences
• Practice sustainable agriculture and provide healthy food to neighbors and local markets

Name: Collectif NOUS
Description: Association loi 1901, le Collectif NOUS, "Nouvelle Organisation pour une Utopie Solidaire", porte une ambition commune à tous ses membres : s'engager pour créer ensemble les voies alternatives locales d'un futur durable et heureux en Provence Verte.

Name: Fundación Orinoquia Biodiversa (FOB)
Description: Trabajamos por la conservación de la biodiversidad en Colombia. Por medio de nuestras investigaciones aportamos a la conservación de la biodiversidad colombiana, en pro de una adecuada planificación, uso y aprovechamiento sostenible de los recursos naturales.

Name: Notre Terre sans Pétrole
Description: Notre terre sans pétrole est une campagne lancée par la société civile congolaise dans le but de faire stopper le projet d'exploitation du pétrole en RDC.

Name: Sussex County Land Trust
Description: Sussex County Land Trust is a nonprofit conservation organization, dedicated to protecting natural, cultural, agricultural and recreational resources through land preservation, stewardship and education for today and tomorrow.

Name: APASAT ONG
Description:
Alphabétisation, Pain et Salut pour Tous (APASAT ONG), engagée pour un développement durable et équitable a travers l’éducation, la sécurité alimentaire et l’autonomisation des communautés. 

Notre mission est de Contribuer au  bien-être de la génération actuelle et future dans un monde résilient aux changements climatiques.

Name: Conservation Outcomes
Description: Conservation Outcomes is a non-profit organisation created to provide support to land that is being developed and managed for biodiversity conservation outside of traditional state protected areas.  The focus of the organisation is on securing remnant biodiversity, ecological integrity and resilience whilst contributing to poverty alleviation and meaningful socio-economic development in rural southern Africa through the development of the conservation and wildlife sector.

Name: For a Lasting Planet
Description: Conservation is as much about people as it is about wildlife. It's about understanding the behaviours and dynamics that shape our ecosystems to co-create impactful solutions that shift mindsets, foster collaboration and protect our last wild places.

Name: Hudson Highlands Land Trust
Description:
The Hudson Highlands Land Trust is a community-based non-profit land conservation organization. We conserve and care for the lands and waters of the Hudson Highlands so that people and nature can flourish together for generations to come. Our mission supports a vision of the Hudson Highlands where wildlife thrives in a healthy, connected, climate-resilient landscape and people enjoy equitable access to nature’s benefits. We cannot achieve this vision alone. HHLT engages in inclusive and creative partnerships across the Highlands region to ensure we address the interconnected needs of the human and natural communities we serve. 

HHLT holds and stewards nature preserves and works with landowners and groups of neighbors who are interested in preserving their property for future generations.  We offer free workshops and forums to bring environmental and conservation issues of concern to the forefront of public dialog. Through its educational outreach it hopes to instill a sense of the importance of stewardship in the area’s youngest residents. 

Those wishing more information may contact the Hudson Highlands Land Trust at 845-424-3358 or visit www.hhlt.org.

Name: Solo Mundo
Description:
We develop creative and sustainable business practices to help wildlife conservation efforts around the world. 

Our aim is to bring global attention to the human-wildlife conflict and promote the rescue, rehabilitation, release, and restoration of indigenous wildlife.

Name: NABU Landesverband Niedersachsen e.V.
Description:
Herzlich willkommen!

Wenn es um den Erhalt des Naturerbes in Niedersachsen geht – um seine Landschaften vom Wattenmeer bis zum Harz, von der Heide bis zur Elbtalaue – wird der NABU Niedersachsen aktiv. Denn wir möchten, dass auch kommende Generationen die Schönheit und Einzigartigkeit unserer Landschaft erleben können.

Der NABU Niedersachsen engagiert sich seit 75 Jahren für den Schutz bedrohter Lebensräume, für gefährdete Tier- und Pflanzenarten, für reine Luft und gesunde Böden. Eine naturverträgliche und nachhaltige Landnutzung liegt uns deshalb am Herzen. Für diese Ziele treten wir durch aktiven Naturschutz, politisches Engagement und Bildungsarbeit ein.

Name: ReFi Medellín
Description: Join the movement towards a more sustainable and equitable future in Medellín, Colombia with ReFI Local Node

Name: Leadership for Conservation in Africa
Description: The LCA positively influence, accelerate and bring about the protection of biodiversity areas in Africa. We focus on education and skills transfer in the conservation space, leadership development, and the protection of nature through private initiatives.

Name: Invertebrates New Zealand
Description: Invertebrates New Zealand is a conservation initiative dedicated to educating and protecting the country’s diverse and essential invertebrates. From pollinators and nutrient cyclers to key players in the food web, invertebrates are vital to the health of ecosystems. Despite their importance, these species face growing threats from habitat destruction, invasive species, and climate change.

Name: CLAMOR TERRAE
Description:
Created in 2024, the CLAMOR TERRAE endowment fund works for the protection of the environment and biodiversity, primarily focused on the Pyrenees mountain range.

The Clamor Terrae Foundation is an independent, private nonprofit organization fully funded by its members and patrons.

Founded by Denis Clement, Clamor Terrae is managed like a company with the ambition of becoming a leading player in environmental protection. Its mission is to inform, influence, and take local action with institutions and public or private decision-makers, ensuring that every decision considers its impact on the planet and the measures implemented to mitigate these impacts.

To ground its discourse and actions in factual evidence, Clamor Terrae conducts independent research in collaboration with renowned laboratories and researchers.

The three main missions of Clamor Terrae:

Inform, educate, and raise awareness
Create natural areas with strong protection status
Undertake legal actions and uphold environmental law

Name: Tasman Environmental Trust
Description:
Tasman Environmental Trust’s (TET) vision is to help create and maintain healthy, diverse ecosystems woven through the Nelson Tasman landscape. So we work with local community conservation groups to raise their profile, expand their reach and access specialist skills and resources. We help link individuals to projects, and projects to like-minded organisations.  

With a proven track record of supporting large-scale projects over the long term, Tasman Environmental Trust has built up a web of connections spanning local and central government agencies, iwi and funding organisations. But we’re also here for the micro-groups; those often-invisible volunteers who work so hard to remediate their own, special section of creek, valley or coastline. 

Together we can transform and safeguard the future environmental health of the Nelson Tasman region. Come work with us. Haere mai! Welcome!

Name: SFW Conservation
Description: SFW is a wildlife and biodiversity conservation organization working to save some of Southern Africa's most endangered wildlife by providing training and support for wildlife rangers, empowering local communities, and supporting our local partners and their conservation projects and initiatives.

Name: Groupe Tortues Marines France - GTMF
Description:
Constitué en 2007, le GTMF prend la suite du « Groupe Tortues Marines » créé par le ministère du développement durable. Il est animé par l’unité PatriNat (OFB CNRS MNHN IRD) au Muséum National d’Histoire Naturelle de Paris, qui en assure le secrétariat pour le ministère chargé de l’écologie (MTECT Ministère de la Transition Écologique et de la Cohésion des Territoires).

Le GTMF est ouvert à tous les acteurs de la gestion, de la recherche et de la conservation des Tortues marines en France. Il regroupe plus de 375 membres, impliqués dans la conservation des Tortues marines, par leurs missions et activités : administrations, institutions scientifiques et experts, associations de protection de la nature, réseaux d’observateurs, centres d’accueil du public (aquariums…) et centres de soins.

Le GTMF a pour objectifs de réfléchir aux différents aspects de la gestion, de la conservation des tortues marines en France et d’échanger des informations au sein du territoire national (France hexagonale, Corse et Outre-mer), en liaison avec les actions de conservation au plan international.

Name: BRANCH Coral Foundation
Description:
BRANCH stands for Building Reefs And Nurseries for Coral Habitats

The BRANCH Coral Foundation was founded in 2022. Our work restores and enhances Curaçao’s beautiful coral reefs using a range of restoration techniques, such as building coral nurseries filled with endangered staghorn coral and educating the local and international community. We also give our volunteers the chance to become a part of our team and to contribute to our coral restoration efforts through a fun and informative diving experience.

Name: People For Wildlife
Description:
People For Wildlife is an environmental conservation charity protecting and restoring biodiverse habitats around the globe, by creating nature-based economic opportunities for the communities that live in them.

We use the best available science, work with Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities, and connect them to people and organisations wanting to invest in nature.

Name: Cumbria Peat Partnership
Description: A partnership of organisations working to protect and restore peatlands throughout Cumbria. Coordinated by Cumbria Wildlife Trust.

Name: Eco Active
Description: Eco Active is a climate action program.

Name: DID Sécurité
Description:
Avec nos 20 années d'existence, DID Sécurité s'est imposé comme un acteur majeur et reconnu dans le domaine de la sécurité incendie. L'entreprise propose une expertise complète à 360° pour protéger les vies, les biens et assurer la continuité de votre activité.
Nos équipes de spécialistes offrent une approche globale qui couvre l'ensemble de vos besoins : de l'audit et le conseil à l'installation, le contrôle et la maintenance de vos équipements. Ils assurent également la formation de votre personnel (SST, lutte contre le feu).
Que ce soit pour une industrie, une habitation ou un établissement recevant du public, DID Sécurité fournit des solutions sur-mesure et efficaces, car protéger vos collaborateurs et votre patrimoine, c'est préparer l'avenir en toute sérénité.
DID SÉCURITÉ protège votre vie et votre patrimoine contre l'incendie.
Pour que votre travail et patrimoine ne partent pas en fumée !

Name: Painted Wolf Foundation
Description:
Our goal is to double the number of painted wolves in three decades.

The Painted Wolf Foundation is a non-profit conservation organisation aiming to reverse the downward trajectory of the endangered painted wolf (Lycaon pictus) across the whole of Africa.

Just over a century ago, there were several hundred thousand painted wolves across most of sub-Saharan Africa. Today only 6,500 remain in a few isolated pockets making it Africa’s most persecuted predator.

Our aim is to double their numbers by 2055 which will remove the painted wolf from the edge of extinction.

To achieve this, we have developed a three pillared strategy that:

     Protects existing populations
     Reclaims old territories for them to occupy
     Develops collaborative initiatives that benefit all painted wolves

The Painted Wolf Foundation has created the Painted Dog Fund in partnership with the Wildlife Conservation Network (WCN). This is a funding mechanism managed by PWF, that raises and deploys funds for projects and initiatives that protect the painted wolves across Africa.

The Painted Wolf Foundation is a charity registered with the Charities Commission of England and Wales. It was founded in 2018 by Peter Blinston, Executive Director of Painted Dog Conservation, Nicholas Dyer, award-winning wildlife photographer and entrepreneur and the late Diane Skinner, megafauna specialist and pioneering community conservationist.

Name: Alberta Conservation Association
Description:
Conserve, protect and enhance have been part of our DNA since we formed in 1997. Traveling thousands of kilometres in -40C weather tracking wolverines or working to secure spawning habitat for world class brown trout can be a typical day. Pick a project from ACA's fish, wildlife or land resource programs and conservation can be defined knee deep in muskeg, sweat and mosquitoes.

While the core of our work has remained consistent, how we do our work has changed over 25 years—from adopting drones and solar-powered off-site watering technologies to engaging new audiences and creating new opportunities between landowners, municipalities and industry. Its these relationships between stakeholders—the hunters, the anglers and trappers, education and research, the public, corporate partners and landowners—that keeps conservation relevant today.

Name: Guinée Écologie
Description:
Organisation non-gouvernementale à but non lucratif, Guinée Écologie, créée en 1989, est de par ses statuts, une association de volontaires qui ont décidé́ de conjuguer leurs efforts avec tous les acteurs du développement (services publics, secteur privé, communautés, société́ civile) pour promouvoir des actions en faveur d'une gestion rationnelle des ressources naturelles et d'une protection pérenne de l'environnement de la Guinée.
Vision 
« Un pays où l’homme vit en harmonie avec une nature dont toutes les espèces composant la diversité́ biologique sont soigneusement conservées et l’environnement protègé dans ses écosystèmes et ses habitats contre les multiples formes de dégradations, avec une participation effective et efficace des populations à la base et le soutien d’une bonne gouvernance dans un environnement politique favorable aux hommes et à la nature. 
Mission

Name: Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne
Description: Zealandia is the world’s first fully-fenced urban ecosanctuary, with an extraordinary 500-year vision to restore a Wellington valley’s forest and freshwater ecosystems as closely as possible to their pre-human state. The 225 hectare ecosanctuary is a groundbreaking conservation project that has reintroduced many species of native wildlife back into the area, 6 of which were previously absent from mainland New Zealand for over 100 years.

Name: WWF Uganda Official
Description: WWF’s mission is to stop the degradation of the planet’s natural environment and to build a future in which people live in harmony with nature, by conserving the world’s biological diversity, ensuring that the use of renewable natural resources is sustainable, and promoting the reduction of pollution and wasteful consumption.

Name: The Mohamed bin Zayed Species Conservation Fund
Description: The Mohamed bin Zayed Species Conservation Fund highlights the vital connection between environmental health and human well-being. By embracing a holistic approach to conservation and sustainability, we recognise that protecting all living things directly benefits humanity.

Name: Zimbabwe Parks & Wildlife Management Authority (ZimParks)
Description:
The Parks & Wildlife Management Authority (also known as Zimparks) is tasked with the protection, management and administration of wildlife in Zimbabwe.

Given the large and expansive resource under our stewardship, this responsibility cannot be achieved in isolation. Our network of protected areas, conservancies and trans-frontier conservation areas parks ensure that we share the load to curb wildlife crime, promote tourism and preserve our shared heritage.

We are driven by the belief that each generation’s obligation is not only to preserve the natural treasures inherited, but to protect them for the benefit and enjoyment of those that follow.

Name: Wedgetail
Description:
Wedgetail's mission is to conserve and restore biodiversity through sustainable investment. 

We do this by providing financial capital to businesses that put biodiversity at the heart of their business models, resulting in better financial returns and better outcomes for nature at the same time. 

We are based in Australia but work on global projects, unified by the biggest of ambitions to bend the curve on biodiversity loss by 2030.

Name: Rewilding France
Description:
Ces dernières décennies, la nature sauvage a connu un retour spectaculaire dans les Alpes Dauphiné. Une végétation variée couvre désormais les pentes des montagnes et collines. Forêts, landes et prairies forment une mosaïque d’habitats accueillant une faune et une flore abondantes et diversifiées. 

La mobilisation des acteurs locaux a joué un rôle clé dans le retour de la grande faune. Dès les années 60, l’ONF et les chasseurs ont réintroduit le cerf et mené diverses actions en faveur des chamois, marmottes et autres espèces. Plus récemment, réintroduction du bouquetin en 1989 et du vautour en 1996, deux succès bien accueillis localement. Profitant de cet environnement plus riche, d’autres espèces sont revenues d’elles-mêmes.

Cette dynamique positive a contribué à renforcer la notoriété de la région. Le tourisme de nature soutient maintenant de nombreuses familles locales. L’accès à la nature attirent de nouveaux habitants, transformant progressivement les perspectives socio-économiques du territoire.

Cette évolution représente aujourd’hui une opportunité née d’un processus de consultation lancé en 2022. Un comité de pilotage composé de collectivités locales a accompagné Rewilding France et Rewilding Europe dans l’identification des attentes du territoire en matière de nature.

Il en ressort que les Alpes Dauphiné suivent une trajectoire exemplaire de renaturation depuis plus de 70 ans. Poursuivre cette dynamique positive répond à un besoin clair : expérimenter et diversifier les modèles de valorisation des espaces ruraux, dans un contexte de changement climatique et de mutations sociétales.

Restaurer les processus naturels renforcera la valeur écologique du territoire et sa résilience face aux évenements climatiques extrêmes. Ce renouveau écologique fait également émerger de nouvelles opportunités économiques pour les habitants. La collaboration avec les communautés locales constitue un pilier essentiel de l’approche du rewilding.

Name: Origens Brasil
Description:
Valorizar nossas florestas, é valorizar quem vive delas.
A rede Origens Brasil é administrado pelo Imaflora. 

O Origens Brasil foi criado pelo Imaflora (Instituto de Manejo e Certificação Florestal e Agrícola), em parceria com o ISA (Instituto Socioambiental)  para agir como um elo entre empresas, consumidores, instituições de apoio, populações tradicionais e povos indígenas, promovendo relações éticas e equilibradas.

Queremos estimular uma nova lógica de desenvolvimento, com a valorização de nossas raízes, ao mesmo tempo que promovemos o equilíbrio econômico, social e ambiental.
As empresas que fazem parte da rede Origens Brasil contribuem para a conservação de nossas florestas, evidenciando a transparência na  cadeia produtiva de produtos vindos dos Territórios de Diversidade Socioambiental e contribuindo para que o Brasil gere riqueza a partir do nosso patrimônio socioambiental sem precisar depredá-lo.

VALORES

Transparência e conexão

O Origens Brasil quer gerar transparência e consciência sobre a origem da produção que consumimos. Isso é mais do que saber de onde vem o que comemos, vestimos ou usamos no dia a dia. 
É colocar luz no valor das historias das pessoas, dos territórios e das conexões responsáveis entre quem produz e quem compra.

Relações comerciais éticas

O Origens Brasil existe para valorizar as populações e seus territórios. Funcionamos como um elo entre quem produz e quem compra, promovendo conexões comerciais com ética e transparência capazes de gerar impactos positivos para as populações e seus territórios.

Rede colaborativa

As empresas, instituições de apoio local, populações tradicionais e povos indígenas que fazem parte do Origens Brasil, participam da governança da iniciativa, sobem informações de forma colaborativa na Plataforma e têm acesso exclusivo a um painel com informações sobre a origem, produção, comercialização e indicadores, que ajudam na gestão dos riscos da cadeia produtiva.

Name: Conservation International Asia-Pacific
Description: People need nature – and for over 30 years, Conservation International has worked to protect it. Through cutting-edge science, innovative policy, and global reach, we empower people to protect the nature that we all rely on for food, fresh water, and livelihoods. In the Asia-Pacific, Conservation International has been implementing conservation programs for over 20 years. We are driving innovation and investments in nature-based solutions to the climate crisis, supporting protections for critical habitats, and fostering economic development that is grounded in the conservation of nature. We work with governments, corporations, and local communities, empowering societies at all levels to create a cleaner, healthier, and more sustainable planet. In this region Conservation International Asia-Pacific has worked to protect more than 80,000 km2 on land and 3.5 million km2 at sea whilst supporting over one million local community members and investing over US$60 million into protected area trust funds. The work has led to the discovery of over 1000 new vertebrate species.

Name: Interdisciplinary Centre for Conservation Science
Description: We are a group of conservation scientists working on applied research at the interface of social and ecological systems.  We work in collaboration with non-governmental organisations, practitioners, government agencies and businesses to provide approaches which address key conservation issues in every biome.

Name: Friend of the Sea
Description:
Friend of the Sea is a program launched in 2008 by the World Sustainability Organization and it represents marine conservation and awareness programs in over 40 countries as well as certification standards for products and services which respect the marine habitat.

When you choose a Friend of the Sea certified product or service, you know that it is produced respecting sustainable practices and that you are contributing by your choice to several conservation projects worldwide.

The Friend of the Sea third-party audits are carried out every year onsite by qualified independent certification bodies. Products and services in different sectors can aim to obtain the Friend of the Sea certification: food (seafood, salt) nutraceutical (omega3, collagene); seaweeds; cosmetics (UV Creams which do not impact coral reefs); tourism (beaches, public aquariums, whale and dolphin-watching; hotels and resorts; restaurants); transportation (marine shipping, cruise lines); many others. 

Friend of the Sea conservation projects aim at protecting enangered aquatic species and habitats: dolphins, whales, turtles, sharks, sturgeons, coral reefs and many others.

Over 1500 companies in more than 80 countries have relied on Friend of the Sea to assess the sustainability of their seafood origins. conservation projects and campaigns aim at protecting endangered species and habitats. 

The World Sustainability Organization has launched also Friend of the Earth, with a mission similar to Friend of the Sea, but applied to land based species and human activities, such as agriculture, farming, mining and tourism.

Name: Projet Life ARTISAN
Description: Compte officiel du projet Life ARTISAN

Name: The Mangrove Breakthrough
Description: The Mangrove Breakthrough is a global movement to reshape how mangroves are valued, financed, and protected. It brings together governments, investors, civil society, and local communities to drive system-wide change—mobilizing $4 billion to secure the future of over 15 million hectares of mangroves by 2030. This isn’t just a conservation effort—it’s a blueprint for how nature can be integrated into global economic and climate systems.

Name: Blue Nature Alliance
Description:
The Blue Nature Alliance is a global partnership founded and led by Conservation International, The Pew Charitable Trusts, The Global Environment Facility, Minderoo Foundation, and the Rob Walton Foundation. We believe that the only way to achieve the pace and scale of conservation needed is by joining and aligning with those that share our ambition.

The Alliance is staffed by former MPA managers, technical experts, scientists, storytellers, social scientists, economists, explorers, collaborators, and coalition-builders.

Name: Natural Lands
Description:
Natural Lands is dedicated to preserving and nurturing nature’s wonders while creating opportunities for joy and discovery in the outdoors for everyone. As the Greater Philadelphia region’s oldest and largest land conservation organization, Natural Lands—which is member supported—has preserved more than 135,000 acres, including 42 nature preserves and one public garden totaling more than 23,000 acres. Some 2.5 million people live within five miles of land under the organization’s protection. 

At Natural Lands, we are passionate about our work, united by shared values, and determined to make a difference. We strive to build an inclusive organization that reflects the communities we serve and celebrates the diverse voices of our employees, partners, volunteers, and visitors.  

We welcome you to explore a career atNatural Lands.

Land for life, nature for all. 

natlands.org

Name: Agence Nationale de la Grande Muraille Verte - Tchad
Description:
L'𝗔𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗡𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗱𝗲 𝗹𝗮 𝗚𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗲 𝗠𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗹𝗹𝗲 𝗩𝗲𝗿𝘁𝗲 (ANGMV) du Tchad est une institution dédiée à la restauration écologique et au développement durable dans la région sahélienne. Fondée en 2012, notre agence s'étend sur une superficie de 2 300 km et traverse 12 provinces du Tchad, reflétant l'engagement profond du gouvernement tchadien envers la protection de l'environnement et le bien-être de ses citoyens. 

Notre mission principale est de 𝗹𝘂𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗲 𝗹𝗮 𝗱é𝘀𝗲𝗿𝘁𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, d'atténuer les effets du changement climatique et de promouvoir des pratiques agricoles durables. En collaborant avec des communautés locales, des organisations internationales et des partenaires au développement, nous mettons en œuvre des projets innovants et durables visant à restaurer les écosystèmes fragiles du Sahel.

Nos initiatives comprennent :
 𝗥𝗲𝗯𝗼𝗶𝘀𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗲𝘁 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗮𝘂𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗱𝗲𝘀 𝗧𝗲𝗿𝗿𝗲𝘀 𝗗é𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗱é𝗲𝘀 : Nous plantons des millions d'arbres chaque année pour lutter contre la désertification et restaurer la biodiversité.

 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗱𝗲 𝗹'𝗔𝗴𝗿𝗶𝗰𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗗𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲 : En introduisant des techniques agricoles résilientes, nous aidons les agriculteurs locaux à améliorer leur productivité tout en préservant les ressources naturelles.

É𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗲𝘁 𝗦𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗯𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘀𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 : Nous organisons des ateliers et des campagnes de sensibilisation pour informer les communautés locales sur l'importance de la protection de l'environnement.

 𝗗é𝘃𝗲𝗹𝗼𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗱𝗲𝘀 𝗜𝗻𝗳𝗿𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲𝘀 𝗩𝗲𝗿𝘁𝗲𝘀 : Construction de barrages de rétention d'eau, installation de systèmes d'irrigation et autres infrastructures pour soutenir l'agriculture et améliorer la gestion de l'eau.

Name: Académie de la Mer de Monaco
Description:
L’Académie de la Mer de Monaco (A2M) est un institut francophone à vocation éducative, basé à Monaco (Principauté), dédié à la connaissance des problématiques environnementales et sécuritaires marines, des relations internationales et géopolitiques tissées autour des mers et océans ainsi que du droit de la mer. L’A2M a la forme d’une association investie d’une mission d’intérêt général dont S.A.S. le Prince Albert II est le Président d’Honneur.

Pour en savoir plus, rendez-vous sur www.a2m.mc, le site de l’Académie.

Name: Conservatoire d'espaces naturels d'Auvergne
International Crane Foundation
Description: The International Crane Foundation works worldwide to conserve cranes and the ecosystems, watersheds and flyways on which they depend. We provide knowledge, leadership and inspiration to engage people in resolving threats to cranes and their diverse landscapes.

Name: IJCD | Initiatives Des Jeunes Pour Le Climat Et Le Développement
Description:
Initiatives des Jeunes pour le Climat et le Développement (IJCD) est une association dynamique et engagée fondée en 2023 par les jeunes burundais, qui vise à mobiliser les jeunes pour répondre aux défis du changement climatique et du développement durable. On s'implique dans d'autres sujets par rapport au développement, comme la paix, l'équité, l'entrepreneuriat etc...

-Vision: IJCD aspire à un monde où les jeunes sont des acteurs clés dans la lutte contre le changement climatique et le développement durable, contribuant activement à un avenir plus équitable et respectueux de l'environnement.

-Objectifs:
.Sensibiliser les jeunes aux enjeux environnementaux et aux impacts du changement climatique.
 .Promouvoir des actions concrètes de protection de l'environnement à travers des initiatives locales et régionales.
.Renforcer les capacités des jeunes en matière de développement durable et d'écologie.
.Favoriser l'innovation et l'entrepreneuriat vert parmi les jeunes pour créer des solutions durables aux défis environnementaux.

   Activités: IJCD organise diverses activités, notamment :
- Conférences et panels sur le climat et le développement durable.
- Programmes de formation sur le recyclage et la gestion des déchets.
- Campagnes de sensibilisation dans les écoles et les communautés pour encourager les pratiques écologiques.
- Projets de recyclage, tels que la transformation des déchets plastiques en pavés écologiques.

En tant que membre de l' @UNGAE (Union pour la nouvelle génération Africaine et Européenne), IJCD bénéficie d'une plateforme international pour partager ses initiatives, collaborer avec d'autres organisations, et accéder à des opportunités de partenariats internationaux.
 @burundiheroes
https://x.com/burundiheroes?t=-HWPaKzQxvrCObw7T2XBZw&s=09

Name: The Ocean Advocate
Description: The Ocean Advocate is here to inform, inspire and empower all people with facts about South African seas, ocean news, marine organisations, jobs and courses. It's built on the idea that when we learn about something, we're more likely to care for it. And there's so much to learn about the ocean. The endeavour is about collaboration and open-source information, founded on the belief that every one of us can make a difference, especially if we work together. Please sign up for the newsletter or send an email if you'd like timely ocean updates.

Name: WildLandscapes International
Description:
WildLandscapes International works with diverse stakeholders and communities to conserve big wild places for wildlife and people.

WildLandscapes International is dedicated to conserving large and connected landscapes globally significant to wildlife and crucial to local communities. Our environmental conservation strategy is to empower communities, conservationists, Indigenous Peoples, ranchers, foresters, fisherfolk, and government agencies to protect habitats for wide-ranging wildlife. To meet the challenges of scale and complexity, WildLandscapes is science-based, collaborative, efficient, and entrepreneurial.

WildLandscapes works under the radar, forming public and private partnerships to preserve wild places while strengthening local economies. With our unique skills and experience building relationships among diverse groups, we have developed partnerships that will help conserve wild lands long after the current projects have ended. 

Our staff brings over 50 years of conservation experience securing $160 million in private capital and $9.5 billion in public funds. In the three years since the founding of our organization, WildLandscapes International has already secured over $40 million in public funding and is currently working to protect over 1 million acres permanently.

Name: Southern African Wildlife College
Description:
The Southern African Wildlife College (SAWC), or better known as ‘The College’ to our students and staff, has conservation at the heart of all we do.
