Rejoignez-nous dans notre mission pour un avenir plus vert et plus durable ! Suivez notre page pour rester informé sur nos projets, nos événements et nos actions. Ensemble, nous pouvons faire une différence.

#EnsemblePourUnAvenirVert #ProtectionDeLenvironnement #Durabilité #ChangementClimatique #ÉducationEnvironnementale

Name: Water Holistic
Description:
We are an environmental consulting company providing nature-based solutions for managing rainwater resources. Our holistic ecological principle solutions imitate nature and provide integrated land and water management, renewing small water cycles and watersheds. 

We have 30 years of international experience and time-tested projects. Our founder is environmentalist and hydrologist Michal Kravčík, the Goldman Prize Award recipient and Ashoka fellow.  Our team and associates include GIS experts, environmental consultants, ecological restorers, architects and project managers. 

We provide nature-based solutions:
1.	Creation and restoration of water resources
2.	Flood and drought prevention 
3.	Urban cooling
4.	Restoration of the damaged landscapes
5.	Biodiversity support
6.	Soil improvement

We provide the following services for your needs: 
• Design climate-resilient landscapes and hazard mitigation, reducing flood impact 
•  Low-cost restoration of damaged landscapes
• Cool the environment and reduce heat islands
• Carbon recapture and sequestration
• Restore the abundance of the springs
• Biodiversity proliferation due to wetland and stream habitat regeneration
• Reconnecting waterways to their floodplains, reducing downstream nutrient pollution

Other services :
• Consulting  on rainwater harvesting, water resource engineering, and stormwater management, integrating agroecology, permaculture features, and natural farm sequencing
• Bio-climate park design and rain gardens 
• Lectures on living with rainwater and solutions for food, environmental and climate security of communities 
• A new water paradigm education for schools, teachers, ecologists, NGOs, and civil associations

When implementing our products, we proceed in the following 4 steps:
1. Analysis
2. Proposal of solutions
3. Quantification of benefits
4. Supervision of implementation
 
Please do not hesitate to contact us for consultation or further inquiries.

Name: Kwetu Training Centre
Description: Kwetu Training Centre for Sustainable Development its nonprofit organization that empower communities to embrace innovative strategies for livelihoods enhancement activities such as sustainable farming, nature-based products such as neem oil, beekeeping, and mariculture, natural resources management and climate change mitigation as well as safeguard to human rights particularly reproductive issues and HIV/AIDS as well as entrepreneurship skills and dignity.

Name: UPROAR
Description:
UPROAR is a conservation and media organization that works with partners to  develop and promote market-based solutions to support conservation and biodiversity globally.

our mission

Our mission is to create awareness and engagement in order to generate direct funding of trusted, impactful, high-profile nature-based projects and programs. We support each phase of the process with engaging marketing communications assets.

Our expertise is our uniqueness.

We take a bottoms-up approach, working backwards from the needs of our on-the-ground project partners. We are able to leverage our extensive business and conservation network s to advance our partner’s objectives.

Whether you are a corporate customer looking to find high-quality nature-based solutions or a conservation organization looking to explore market-based solutions, UPROAR can help you meet your organization’s goals.

Name: ONG PAULY AFRIQUE BIO
Description:
L'ONG PAULY AFRIQUE BIO est une organisation béninoise à but non lucratif. Elle a pour objectif de contribuer à l’avènement d’une conscience citoyenne verte en Afrique, 
en priorisant la lutte contre le réchauffement climatique, la promotion des emplois verts 
durables, la protection et la restauration de la biodiversité, l’eau et l’assainissement, la 
création de solutions novatrices, la sensibilisation et le renforcement de capacités des enfants, 
jeunes et femmes du continent Africain et du Bénin en particulier.

Name: Coral Reef Care
Description:
Coral Reef Care's mission is to conserve and protect marine ecosystems, in particular coral reefs. Our work consists of initiating and organizing reef conservation projects and creating public awareness on the importance of coral reefs and the human impact on them. We work together with local non profit organizations or research organizations that have proven durability and effectiveness over the years. This collaboration can result in a wide variety of activities such as the restoration of damaged reefs, campaigns to reduce plastic waste, creation of artificial reefs, creation of No Take Zones and the protection of specific marine species.
With our conservation projects we simultaneously strive to improve the livelihood of the local communities that depend on coral reefs. By working together with the local community we generate jobs in the conservation business. A healthier reef can also increase tourism, thus creating alternative income for fishermen. We believe that without involving and benefitting the local community, effective conservation projects are not feasible.
One of CRC's main goals is to set up more MPAs (Marine Protected Areas). Nature can be resilient under the right circumstances. A coral reef offers refuge, food and breeding grounds for marine life. By actively protecting a large, biologically significant area such as a coral reef, the reef has the best chances for recovery. Once mature, a lot of marine life will swim out of the protected area. This 'spill-over effect', the recovery of the reef itself, and the attraction an MPA has to tourism makes the creation of protected areas the most effective tool in marine conservation.

Name: SHARE THE OCEAN
Description:
Our goal is to reduce the number of collisions between marine megafauna and boats using statistic modeling, by cross-referencing species distribution data and ship routes. 
This program addresses all ocean-centered stakeholders; shipping, fishing, offshore racing and recreational boating.
We must rethink maritime habits by routing ships in a more environmentally responsible way in order to preserve the diversity of marine fauna whilst sustaining our maritime activity.
In partnership with :
- Observatoire PELAGIS, UMS3462 Univ La Rochelle /CNRS.
- PLATON - an Inria project-team joint with École Polytechnique, within CMAP (Centre de Mathématiques Appliquées, École Polytechnique – UMR7641 CNRS – Institut Polytechnique de Paris), and affiliated with the Inria Saclay Île-de-France Research Center on the École Polytechnique campus.

Name: Udzungwa Landscape Strategy (ULS)
Description: The Udzungwa Landscape Strategy (ULS) is the first large-scale conservation effort to protect the unique ecosystem of the Udzungwa Mountains in southern Tanzania. The ULS provides a focused approach to land natural resource stewardship that has been collaboratively developed and designed with and by key local stakeholders living and working in the landscape. Our mission is to conserve over 1,673 km² of forest, part of one of the world’s most exceptional biodiversity hotspots. We are currently working with local communities and Protected Area managers to ensure the long-term protection of this amazing landscape.

Name: Instituto Tamandua
Description:
Discover and support Instituto Tamanduá!
For 18 years preserving and studying the native group of South America that emerged 65 million years ago!
The Xenarthros: Sloths, Anteaters, and Armadillos.

We founded the NGO in 2005 with a dream: to generate knowledge and preserve animals originating in South America for future generations.

Made by people passionate about nature conservation for people passionate about nature conservation.

Join us on this journey!
Each action generates a significant impact. Whether promoting our work, donating, participating in our initiatives, or making other people aware of the importance of conserving native life, all these attitudes contribute to a more sustainable and harmonious reality.

Help publicize these unique animals that emerged on our continent! Visit www.tamandua.org.
Location: Jardim dos Estados, Mato Grosso do Sul

Name: EPAGA - Bassin versant de l'Aulne et de l'Hyères
Description:
L'EPAGA est un syndicat mixte ouvert reconnu comme Etablissement Public Territorial de Bassin (EPTB) depuis 2008, et qui porte le SAGE de l'Aulne. Le territoire couvre 1 892 km², de la presqu'île de Crozon jusqu'au centre Bretagne. Il s'étend sur 3 départements bretons et 90 communes. 

Les principales missions de l’EPAGA sont :

-	La mise en œuvre et l’animation du SAGE de l’Aulne
-	Le portage du programme Breizh Bocage
-	Le portage du PAEC (Projet Agro-Environnemental et Climatique) sur le bassin versant de l’Aulne
-	L’animation du site Natura 2000 Vallée de l’Aulne
-	La lutte contre les pollutions bactériologiques en rade de Brest et le rétablissement de la qualité des eaux de la rade
-	Le suivi de la qualité de l’eau et des pollutions ponctuelles sur l’ensemble des cours d’eau du bassin versant
-	La gestion du soutien d’étiage à partir de la retenue de Brennilis
-	Le portage du PAPI (Programme d’Actions et de Prévention des Inondations)
-       L'élaboration du contrat de Rade de Brest TerraRade

L'EPAGA bénéficie de financements européens FEDER pour l'animation du site Natura 2000 de la vallée de l'Aulne.

Name: Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS)
Description:
Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS) is a State corporation under the Ministry of Tourism and Wildlife established by an Act of Parliament – the Wildlife Conservation and Management Act CAP 376, of 1989 now repealed and replaced by the Wildlife Conservation and Management Act, 2013.   The overall mandate of KWS is to conserve and manage wildlife in Kenya and to enforce related laws and regulations.

The service has sole jurisdiction over national parks and supervises other wildlife management areas including national reserves, local and private sanctuaries and conservancies, all wildlife conservation and management activities outside protected areas.  

KWS also conducts conservation education, training and wildlife research.

Name: Spora Foundation
Description: We work to value and care for biodiversity, promoting sustainable practices through initiatives that foster scientific knowledge, community development, and the preservation of territories, building the path towards a sustainable future in harmony with the environment.

Name: Raukūmara Pae Maunga Restoration Project
Description:
On the beautiful east coast of Aotearoa New Zealand, Raukūmara is the last remaining, large native forest (250,000 ha) from ‘mountain to sea’ in the North Island. 

Raukūmara Pae Maunga is an iwi-led, nationally-significant conservation programme operating on a landscape scale, protecting 130,000 hectares of native forest through innovative practices, research and deep community engagement.

For tangata whenua (people of the land) Ngāti Porou and Te Whānau-ā-Apanui, this is no ordinary forest - here stands a living tupuna (ancestor), a source of mauri (life force) and the lifeblood of past, present and future generations.

Sadly, decades of degradation from invasive species have devastated the forest and birdlife, destabilised the land and deposited silt in the waterways that flow to the ocean below.

Together, the iwi are carrying out extensive and effective pest control (including the largest deer culling programme in the North Island), forest regeneration, native species protection, and deep community engagement.

Five years in operation, Raukūmara Pae Maunga is delivering results: protecting unique ecosystems and forest habitat, building workforce pathways, supporting jobs, leading research, youth development, and investing in infrastructure across isolated communities.

The programme represents not only the revival of one of Aotearoa New Zealand’s most neglected and degraded forest ranges, but also a pathway for intergenerational social, environmental, and economic transformation.

To learn more, visit: www.raukumara.org

Name: Association Recyclamer
Description:
Association Recyclamer – Agir pour Nos Océans 

Fondée en 2016, l’Association Recyclamer est une organisation dédiée à la protection des océans, à la collecte des déchets marins et à l’innovation environnementale. 

Nous combinons actions de terrain, sensibilisation et technologie pour préserver nos écosystèmes aquatiques et bâtir un futur durable. Nos actions se concentrent sur trois piliers : 

 Nettoyer : Collecte des déchets en mer, sur les côtes et dans les rivières avec des bénévoles, plongeurs et technologies avancées. 

 Sensibiliser : Programmes éducatifs en écoles et entreprises, conférences et événements pour éveiller les consciences. 

 Innover : Développement de Geneseas, robot autonome de nettoyage, et Mr. Turbino, système d’aspiration sous-marin, rendant la dépollution accessible à tous. 

Notre engagement est international : 

 Recyclamer Latinoamérica : Expansion de nos actions en Amérique latine pour éduquer et agir localement. 

 Partenariats avec France Environnement & Expédition MED : Études scientifiques sur la pollution plastique et actions de nettoyage.

  Programme AWARE : Déploiement de Mr. Turbino dans les centres de plongée pour sensibiliser et former les plongeurs. 

 Notre vision ?

 Restaurer les océans grâce à la science, l’éducation et l’innovation. 

Rejoignez-nous pour nettoyer, sensibiliser et innover pour la planète.   

Contactez-nous pour collaborer ou en savoir plus !

Name: HuarangoNature - HUB Perú: The Global Biodiversity Standard (TGBS)
Description: Asociación sin fines de lucro cuya misión busca promover la conservación, protección y restauración de bosques, hábitats y agrobiodiversidad del Perú.  Somos el HUB Perú del Global Biodiversity Standard.

Name: Kachrewaale
Description:
The Kachrewaale Foundation is an organisation dedicated to understanding and working on the problems of waste, habitat destruction, and climate change, especially in ecologically fragile areas that otherwise do not get such attention.

We’ve initiated India’s first Targeted Program for Ocean Plastics right here in the Andamans. This program will cover collection and analysis of
ocean plastic waste to design frameworks for collection and recovery of this waste across the country. We will of course use this data to advocate reducing plastic waste in our oceans!
Location: Andaman & Nicobar Island, Andaman & Nicobar Island

Name: Fincas
Description:
Bond with nature and build a better future for humanity.

The growing demand for crops and livestock products 
is accelerating the destruction of ecosystems around 
the world. Finca's "Nature Bonds" allow you to invest 
in small farmers and indigenous communities who 
pledged to combat climate change and restore nature.

Name: Conservation indica
Description: We are research nerds. We want to make research work for everyone but especially for the most marginalised - humans and non-humans. Conservation indica was born from some of the nagging questions that kept us awake at nights. What can make fast driving vehicles slow down on wildlife corridors? What is at stake when dams are built in eco-sensitive zones?  How high will pine trees travel to escape warming of their altitudes? Learn more about what makes us tick.

Name: Association Des Entomologistes de Picardie
Description:
Créée en 1988, l'Association des Entomologistes de Picardie se consacre principalement à l'étude de l'entomofaune sur le territoire Picard, englobant diverses espèces telles que les fourmis, les coléoptères, les punaises, les abeilles, les papillons, les libellules, les sauterelles, les criquets, les mouches, ainsi que d'autres invertébrés comme les araignées et les cloportes. Nos mission consistent à effectuer des travaux d'expertise entomologique, notamment à travers la réalisation d'inventaires et la rédaction de rapports d'études et de publications scientifiques. En parallèle, nous nous engageons dans une démarche de médiation scientifique en sensibilisant le grand public par le biais de conférences, d'animations et de sorties nature. 

Fort de plus de 50 adhérents bénévoles, notre association est ouverte à toute personne désireuse de contribuer à la préservation de la biodiversité et à l'avancement des connaissances sur l'entomofaune, quel que soit son niveau de compétence initial.

Name: Tropenbos Colombia
Description: Tropenbos Colombia tiene como objeto consolidar una plataforma para el fortalecimiento, promoción y divulgación de los saberes tradicionales y locales a través de acciones de formación, estímulo a la investigación local y sistematización documental, los diálogos de saberes con la academia y las instituciones, así como su inserción en planes de estudio, de manejo y de desarrollo.

Name: Tropenbos Indonesia
Description: Bridging the gaps between knowledge and practices on forested landscape governance.

Name: Arannayk Foundation
Description:
Arannayk Foundation, also known as the Bangladesh Tropical Forest Conservation Foundation, is a not-for-profit organization established in 2003 by the joint initiative of the Governments of the People's Republic of Bangladesh and the United States of America. Arannayk Foundation is registered as a not-for-profit company limited by guarantee (Reg. No.C-550 (02)/2003) and not having a share capital under section 28 of the Bangladesh Companies Act 1994.

Arannayk Foundation promotes conservation, protection, restoration and sustainable use of tropical forests in Bangladesh. It provides financial and technical assistance to organizations involved in forests and biodiversity conservation. It also implements projects financed by the other development partners.

Name: CYNESA DRCongo
Description: CYNESA DRCongo opère à travers ses activités dans les domaines de l’environnement, spécialement sur les changements climatiques. On travaille dans les paroisses, les écoles secondaires ; université, de mouvements de jeunes à différents niveaux.

Name: In the footsteps of Wallace
Description:
In the footseps of Wallace is a continuation of the 2023 naturalist expedition "In the footsteps of Darwin". This time, we'll go in the footsteps of Wallace...

Alfred Russel Wallace is a naturalist and geographer of the 19th century. His name is rather unknown and yet, he has done a great work on the theory of evolution presented by Charles Darwin. 
Wallace was also one of the greatest explorers of his time. Between 1854 and 1862, he went on an expedition to the islands of Southeast Asia, where he discovered and described many species, including several hundred that he personally named. The Wallace bird of paradise, for example, is an endemic species of the Northern Moluccas, the only place in the world where one can observe this magnificent bird. 

His greatest discovery remains the biogeographical line that divides the Asian and the Australian Fauna, later named in his honour the Wallace Line.  

From january to june 2024, we will set out to meet scientists, associations and witnesses of deforestation, as well as gathering information on the species and their protection in the Indonesian archipelago. These testimonies will allow us to make a documentary film that will be an excellent way of raising awareness.
We will broadcast it on our return and also plan to expose the best pictures of the trip through several photographic exhibitions.

In this preparatory phase, we are looking for funding and sponsors for the acquisition of shipping and optical equipment


Besides, any contact (scientific or otherwise) specialist of Wallace or the species he discovered, or any others would be invaluable to us, both in South-East Asia and in Europe..


We are also looking for a service provider for the editing of the documentary film.

If you are working on these fields, don't hesitate to contact us !

Name: ONG ACTION TERRE ENVIRONNEMENT (ATE)
Description: L'ONG Action Terre Environnement (ATE) est une organisation non gouvernementale à caractère social, apolitique, et à but non lucratif, créée en 2023. Inspirée par un engagement profond pour la préservation de l'environnement et le développement durable, notre mission est de promouvoir un environnement sain et durable, les énergies renouvelables, et d'assister les couches vulnérables à travers des œuvres humanitaires. Nous nous engageons également à favoriser l'entrepreneuriat des jeunes, l'éducation environnementale et sociale, ainsi que le tourisme en tant que moyen de conservation de la biodiversité et l'épanouissement des populations. Basée à Abomey-Calavi, au Bénin, ATE agit en faveur d'un futur dans lequel l'harmonie entre l'homme et la nature est restaurée et valorisée.

Name: A. P. Leventis Ornithological Research Institute (APLORI)
Description:
The A.P. Leventis Ornithological Research Institute (APLORI) is the Centre of Excellence in Ornithological Training & Research at the University of Jos and the leading institution of its kind in West Africa. Situated in Amurum Forest Reserve on the Jos Plateau, Nigeria, APLORI provides a unique platform for world-class research on birds and their habitats.

Beyond research, the institute plays a vital role in biodiversity conservation and offers high-level academic and field-based training to early-career scientists across Africa. Through its internationally recognized MSc in Conservation Biology and ongoing collaborative projects, APLORI is shaping the future of conservation science and policy in the region.

Name: Association EDEN-I
Description: Etude, préservation et découverte de la biodiversité marine

Name: Marine Beacon Project
Description: Funded by the EU Horizon Europe Programme, MarineBEACON is a €8.8 million, 4.5-year project that aims to address gaps in our current understanding of how fisheries bycatch impacts protected, endangered and threatened species. The project will work with fisheries, policy and conservation stakeholders to develop and test innovative, tools and techniques for better monitoring of these important species and mitigating risks of bycatch, to ensure healthier seas and more sustainable fisheries.

Name: ESA Hub
Description:
The Regional Resource Hub (RRH) is a knowledge hub for the Eastern and Southern African region that supports better decision making for fair and effective management and governance of protected and conserved areas. The Hub compiles and analyzes relevant data and provides information to support field interventions, policies dialogues and decision-making processes at local, national and regional levels.

The Regional Resource Hub offers information management systems and tools, capacity building, knowledge sharing products and communication activities aiming to reinforce the management effectiveness and governance of protected areas and surrounding communities in Eastern and Southern Africa region.

The knowledge generated through the use of the RRH tools and services is presented in different formats that support planning and decision making and the capacity development endeavours of the RRH. They include story maps, success stories, situation analysis, country and protected area reports, technical briefs and other guiding publications.

Name: Itec Materials: Protective Solutions
Description:
Itec Materials specialize in the preservation of industrial assets through 3 divisions:

1. Itec VCI materials = our long-existing temporary corrosion protection range. Itec Materials is the exclusive licensee of Cortec, world leader in VCI, for France and Northern Africa. This range also include NuCorr, our VCI range for nuclear applications.

2. Itec ESD materials: this range includes ESD pink poly packaging (Purplestat), shielding (Shieldstat) and barrier bags (Moistat) as well as full range of equipements for ESD protected areas and PPE.

3. Itec Polymers: our range of technical films like Solukem, our water soluble bags for specialty chemicals.

Name: URCPIE de Normandie
Description: L’URCPIE, en tant que tête de réseau des CPIE de Normandie, porte l’ambition d'agir en faveur de la protection de l’environnement dans une démarche écologique et solidaire. Elle agit avec les personnes et les organisations pour la prise en compte des questions environnementales dans les décisions et les comportements dans le respect des positionnements de chacun.

Name: OneSTOP Project
Description:
OneSTOP aims to harness current technologies and citizen science, while overcoming challenges posed by dispersed and fragmentary processes, policies, and knowledge, to deliver methods for identification, early detection and surveillance of invasive alien species.

The project aims to achieve transformative results to minimise the introduction,  establishment and spread of invasive alien species by integrating cutting-edged detection methods, underpinned by prioritisation and robust models, alongside stakeholder engagement to inform harmonised policies and facilitate knowledge exchange. The outcomes will be relevant for invasive alien species policy, noting the importance of enhancing collaboration and coordination across local, national, and regional scales, recognising that geographic boundaries do not confine the impact of these species. By adopting a holistic and interconnected approach, OneSTOP seeks to establish a strategy to achieve rapid and transformative progress in detecting, eradicating and controlling invasive alien animals and plants, ultimately contributing to a more secure and resilient environment. Throughout, OneSTOP is based upon the strategic actions recommended for integrated governance of biological invasions in the recently published IPBES Thematic assessment report on invasive alien species and their control (IPBES2023).

OneBiosecurity Systems and Technology for People, Places and Pathways receives funding from the European Union’s Horizon Europe Research and Innovation Programme (ID No 101180559). Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or the European Commission. Neither the EU nor the EC can be held responsible for them.

Name: Arctictis Binturong Conservation
Description:
ABConservation est une association entièrement dédiée au binturong, une espèce arboricole d'Asie du Sud-Est. L'association a mis en place depuis 2015 un programme de sensibilisation du grand public à l'environnement en France via le réseau des parcs zoologiques. Depuis 2017, nous sommes également présents à Palawan, Philippines, dans le cadre du "Bearcat Study Program", un programme d'études scientifiques sur le binturong et en 2019, nous avons lancé notre nouveau programme de sensibilisation du grand public "From Awareness to Actions for Nature Conservation", cette fois-ci à Palawan. 
Dans le cadre de nos programmes, nous accueillons au sein de notre structure des volontaires (VSI ou volontaire associatif) ainsi que des stagiaires (License & master). 
Pour tout renseignement supplémentaire, n'hésitez pas à nous contacter par mail à arctictisbinturongconservation@gmail.com

EN CE MOMENT : cagnotte pour le programme éducatif ! Rendez-vous au lien suivant pour plus d'information : https://www.helloasso.com/associations/abconservation/collectes/aidez-la-branche-educative-d-abconservation/widget-vignette?fbclid=PAY2xjawJEuDRleHRuA2FlbQIxMQABpkHnvLD46LGN00agRdmAQILvwUMewkTcnuOmd_hEt6jsY1v75O5g6mgy8A_aem_DcQbPKqmz326FKTRfAO_Fg

Name: Western Australian Coastal & Marine Network
Description:
The Western Australian Coastal and Marine Network (WACMN) supports connection, collaboration and engagement for the health and sustainability of Western Australia’s coastal and marine environments.  

Our network consists of a range of small to medium sized coastal carers, government departments, citizen scientists and hands-on conservation groups.

Name: Tucson Audubon Society
Description: Founded in 1949, Tucson Audubon is southern Arizona's leading non-profit engaging people in the conservation of birds and their habitats. We involve people through recreational birding, extend their horizons through education programs, and engage them in conservation actions from planting trees and counting birds, to working with local, state, and national policy makers.

Name: K. Lisa Yang Center for Conservation Bioacoustics
Description:
We collect and interpret sounds in nature by developing, applying, and sharing innovative conservation technologies across relevant scales to inform and advance the conservation of wildlife and habitats.

Part of the Cornell Lab of Ornithology

Name: Rainbeau
Description:
Particulièrement sensibles aux sujets liés à l’environnement, nous avons cherché comment nous pouvions, à notre niveau, améliorer l’espace public. La réponse : végétaliser les espaces contraints grâce à l'eau de pluie que nous collectons. 

En conjuguant design et fonctionnalités innovantes en lien avec l’air et l’eau du temps, Rainbeau offre de nouvelles opportunités pour les collectivités et les organisations qui souhaitent répondre aux enjeux climatiques. Nos solutions permettent d’agir en faveur de l’environnement sans sacrifier ni au design, ni au confort, tout en ajoutant des fonctionnalités pour les usagers.

Ainsi la conception, les usages possibles mais aussi la technologie embarquée apportent une dimension supplémentaire et unique aux mobiliers urbains de la gamme Rainbeau.

Name: Orkney Skate Trust
Description:
A community group collecting data on skate species, specialising in high quality field work and innovative techniques to document key marine species and habitats. 

Specialties include underwater baited camera work (BRUV), ROV surveys, drop-down camera surveys, diving surveys and tag & release fisheries science. 

We manage an Orkney based citizen science project of egg case data both cases ashore and in situ – working alongside national database recording programmes. 

Combining citizen science data with free-issue geophysical data of the marine environment we plan and undertake targeted marine conservation surveys.

Name: Robin Gut Stiftung
Description:
„Robin Gut“ wurde vor drei Jahren im Zusammenhang mit dem Wiederaufbau im Ahrtal als Initiative gegründet und setzt sich seitdem mit eigenen Aktionen dafür ein, dem Thema Nachhaltigkeit „enkelfähige“ Impulse zu geben. Aus dieser Initiative heraus gründeten Dieter Könnes und Nadine Büscher 2025 die gemeinnützige Gesellschaft Robin Gut Stiftung, die sich sowohl für die Aufforstung von Wäldern in Deutschland, als auch für soziale, lokale Projekte engagiert.

Unterstützt von den Mitstreitern der ersten Stunde, darunter Clemens Meiss und weitere engagierte Partner, verfolgt die Stiftung seither ein klares Ziel: eine enkelfähige Zukunft gestalten.

Unser Leitgedanke lautet: Gemeinsam schaffen wir es – das WIR zählt. Wir machen Bäume groß – und unsere Kinder stark!

Name: Ebla Società Cooperativa
Description:
EBLA soc. coop. è nata a Cesena nel 2006 dall’incontro di sei giovani archivisti liberi professionisti intenzionati a operare secondo una precisa etica del lavoro, nel rispetto della dignità professionale dei soci e dei collaboratori.
Oggi, una solida esperienza e una stabile rete di rapporti di collaborazione con liberi professionisti e società partner, consente ad EBLA di operare con successo sull’intero territorio nazionale e su ogni aspetto della gestione, conservazione e valorizzazione del patrimonio culturale pubblico e privato.
EBLA ricerca e forma le competenze umane e tecniche in grado di garantire soluzioni, in continua evoluzione, alle esigenze dettate dai cambiamenti tecnologici e normativi per la formazione, la gestione e la conservazione dei documenti, nonché di soddisfare le richieste di nuovi e più efficaci strumenti per la fruizione e la valorizzazione del nostro patrimonio culturale.
Ebla opera a livello nazionale collaborando con Enti pubblici e soggetti privati, avvalendosi prevalentemente del lavoro dei soci collaboratori - professionisti qualificati e motivati – ed esperti archivisti, bibliotecari e, più in generale, operatori del settore culturale.

Ebla è orientata a sostenere lo sviluppo di progetti culturali rivolti alla collettività, in collaborazione con i diversi attori presenti sul territorio.

Ebla è attenta alla crescita professionale e formativa dei soci anche nell’ottica di fornire ai propri clienti competenze e strumenti aggiornati.

Name: ICON Water Trails
Description:
Iowa Confluence Water Trails — ICON, for short — will connect 80+ improvements across 150 miles of rivers and creeks in Central Iowa.

But just as important, it will reconnect us to the magic of water. ICON is a magnet for fun and family — a high-energy playground for kayaking, rafting and paddling throughout the region. It’s a sanctuary for spending time with nature, but also, with ourselves.

The trails represent a new commitment to water safety and conservation. A movement of dreamers and doers, collaborating to improve the quality of water — and quality of life — for Central Iowa.

We all need to recharge and refocus sometimes. Nothing does the trick quite like water, and you’ll find it in abundance here in Central Iowa. Create your own iconic adventure today.

Name: AGROMAP CAMEROUN
Description:
AGROMAP est une société sous régionale spécialisée dans le développement de projets de restauration des forêts, de conservation de la biodiversité et d’amélioration des revenus des producteurs. Nous sommes convaincus que les solutions fondées sur la nature, notamment l’agroforesterie, la reforestation, de restauration des mangroves ont une capacité de séquestration du carbone pour lutter contre les effets du changement climatique avec la forte implication des communautés locales et les petits producteurs.

Notre organisation est structurée en 4 départements : 

•AGROMAP NBS (Nature-Based Solutions) : Reforestation, Agroforesterie, Restauration de forêts & mangroves, Projets à génération de crédit carbone & Biodiversité,

•AGROMAP CONSULTING : Conseil en Stratégie ESG, Chaine d'approvisionnement responsable, plan d'aménagement de forêts, Analyse des risques de déforestation, rédaction des documents de projets de crédit carbone, Accompagnent à la due diligence de l'RDUE et ARS-1000.

•AGROMAP TECHNOLOGIE : Mapping & Géo monitoring de forêts & zones préservées, Analyses GIS, plan d'occupation des sols, Digitalisation de services agricoles, Solutions de traçabilité et Gestion des données des projets.

•AGROMAP ENERGIES RENOUVELABLES: Conception et fourniture des outils de production d’énergie (foyers améliorés, biogaz), Promotion de l’usage des énergies renouvelables, Valorisation des déchets d’origine agricole et forestières (compost, biochar),Gestion et Valorisation des Bioressources.

Name: Canadian Wetlands Roundtable
Description: Our vision is to achieve sustainability of Canada's wetlands through responsible management. Our mission is to establish Canada as a global leader in the responsible management, use and conservation of wetlands based on sound science.

Name: Durrell Conservation Academy
Description:
Our founder, Gerald Durrell, believed that saving species from extinction was as much an art as a science. One of his greatest ambitions was to create a training centre which would unite these two disciplines and provide conservationists worldwide with the skills they need to prevent species extinctions. 
To date we have trained over 6,000 students from 151 countries who have learnt from some of the world’s leading conservation practitioners in the art and science of species conservation. We deliver a variety of accessible, high quality training and professional development opportunities both in-person and online. Our headquarters in Jersey, and training centre in Mauritius, provide opportunities to attend in-person courses and internships for a life-changing experience. Our online courses are an excellent way to access valuable training, connecting with, and learning alongside conservation practitioners from all around the world.

Name: Crees Foundation
Description: Nonprofit organization promoting a Sustainable Amazon in The Manu Biosphere Reserve, Perú through Research and Community projects.

Name: Comité Consultatif Coutumier Environnemental
Description: Le Comité Consultatif Coutumier Environnemental (ci-après CCCE) est une association de loi 1901 créée le 29 Octobre 2009 dans le cadre du Pacte pour un développement durable du Grand Sud signé entre la société Goro Nickel (désormais Prony Ressources), les chefferies coutumières du Grand Sud, et le Comité Rhéébù Nùù. Cette structure permet de garantir la participation des instances coutumières au suivi de l’environnement de l’usine du Sud ainsi que la prise en compte du savoir, des savoir-faire et des connaissances traditionnels kanak. Le CCCE a été mis en place pour une durée de 30 ans.

Name: BEST RUN
Description:
Qui sommes-nous ?

BESTRUN, c’est l’initiative d’un groupe d’étudiants de l’Université de La Réunion, passionnés par la Nature et sensibles aux impacts de l’homme sur la biodiversité. Issus d’horizons divers et variés, chacun apportant une opinion unique, nous nous sommes retrouvés autour d’objectifs communs : préserver la biodiversité !

Que voulons-nous ?

BESTRUN a pour objet la valorisation, la sensibilisation et l’éducation au patrimoine naturel de l’île de La Réunion et de l’océan Indien, ainsi que l’amélioration de la cohésion entre les étudiants des filières scientifiques et autres, de l’Université de La Réunion et d’ailleurs.

Que faisons-nous ?

Fondée en août 2018, les BEST ne sont pas en reste ! Après une opération de nettoyage des déchets réussie au Cap La Houssaye nous avons continuer à mettre en place des activités telles que des sorties naturalistes ou encore développer des partenariats avec des organismes locales, régionales et internationales.

Name: Wild Shots Outreach
Description: Supporting young Africans from disadvantaged communities in accessing their wildlife heritage and developing employment skills in wildlife and tourism through photography.

Name: GOVRAX
Description:
Purpose-built technology for state wildlife agencies. Built by outdoorsmen, not tech monopolies.

At GOVRAX, we design modular, scalable software solutions tailored for state fish and wildlife agencies. Instead of forcing agencies into rigid, outdated platforms, we provide a Lego-style toolkit—snap in what you need, leave out what you don’t, and integrate seamlessly with your existing systems.

We’re not just another government tech vendor. We’re a hands-on, consultant-style firm that works alongside agencies to solve real-world problems.

Proud Service Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business.

Name: Conservation Frontlines
Description: Conservation Frontlines provides accurate and balanced information that encourages ethical, sustainable, and science-based wildlife conservation. We offer monthly Dispatches, Conservation 101s, and articles on important issues in the conservation of wild lands, wildlife, and rural communities.

Name: 40 Acre Conservation League
Description: 40 Acre Conservation League is California's first Black-led land conservancy, focusing on closing the Nature Gap across California's diverse communities.

Name: Association GOVIIN KHULAN
Description:
We are the first and only non-profit conservation organization in the world with a mission focused solely on conserving the threatened Mongolian Wild Ass/Khulan (subspecies of the Asiatic Wild Ass) in Mongolia. 

Our work focuses on protecting this threatened species as an umbrella species of the Gobi Desert ecosystem, therefore also enhancing protection of other Gobi species and their habitat. 

Our activities are centered on partnering with local protected areas' administration and the local communities of our project's area(s) with a goal to reach and maintain a harmonious and sustainable Human-wildlife coexistence and lead to people and wildlife to thrive and coexist in shared and preserved landscapes. 

We work in Mongolia's Gobi Desert since 2008, including the southeast region of the Gobi Desert where the population of the Mongolian Wild Ass/Khulan is the largest in Mongolia. 

Our conservation organization is registered in France.

Name: PHOENIX & ORCHIDACEAE Association
Description:
The Association's mission is to eradicate, if not reduce, the 3 scourges that plague the island of Madagascar: - Famine: Madagascar is suffering the full force of the consequences of climate change, resulting in an extremely arid drought in a south that is already very dry to begin with, - unsanitary conditions: waste that has been accumulating for many decades and generates many diseases - loss of biodiversity:  linked to the trafficking of precious timber and the practice of ancestral crops.
The actions planned to counter these scourges are: - partnership with an emergency humanitarian aid organization to feed the populations of arid regions - recycling and recovery of waste to transform it into compost, briquettes, furniture, skips for waste collection, to produce energy - the creation of solar panel schools to teach agroecology and agroforestry. The Association wishes to transform the weaknesses of Madagascar into a great strength that would allow this country rich in biodiversity, in a young population, with living spaces to occupy, to become economically autonomous and to know how to repel the threatening scourges by living habits adapted to the 21st century, while remaining faithful to its cultural identity.

Name: Instituto para el Medio Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable del Estado de Colima
Description:
El Instituto para el Medio Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable del Estado de Colima (IMADES) es un organismo público comprometido con la protección, conservación y aprovechamiento sustentable de los recursos naturales.

Coordinamos políticas ambientales y climáticas para promover un desarrollo sostenible, resiliente e incluyente. Impulsamos planes, programas y acciones con enfoque integral para la mitigación y adaptación al cambio climático, así como la participación ciudadana y la cultura de la sustentabilidad.

Trabajamos por un ambiente sano en beneficio de la sociedad colimense, fomentando soluciones basadas en la naturaleza, el conocimiento técnico-científico y el compromiso interinstitucional.

Name: Just One Ocean
Description:
Just One Ocean is a UK based marine conservation charity established to support the advancement of scientific knowledge and to raise awareness of some of the major issues and challenges affecting our ocean. Our work has included support to film projects, production of articles, support to the arts and scientific research. Our Big Microplastic Survey was the first global citizen science microplastic project to be developed. It is now being undertaken in over 40 countries.
We believe in the necessity of partnerships and collaboration and have forged links with businesses, academic institutions and government departments all over the world.

Name: Amazoonico Wildlife Rescue Center
Description:
AmaZOOnico is a wildlife rescue centre located in the Ecuadorian Amazon rainforest that rehabilitates, reintroduces, and cares for animals who are victims of illegal trade. Visitors and volunteers are welcome to help and learn! 

All of the animals that arrive at the centre are brought by the Ministry of the Environment, the result of confiscations from people who have kept them as pets or intended to sell or trade them on the black market. Some animals arrive suffering from multiple physical and behavioural problems which make it impossible for them to live free in the forest. In those cases, we provide them with spacious and comfortable enclosures where they can live in the most natural conditions possible. In other cases, the animals arrive in good physical and mental condition and after passing through quarantine, various examinations, and a rehabilitation process, they can be successfully reintroduced back into their natural habitat.

Name: CEA-Kingdom Saudi Arabia (CEA-KSA)
Description: environment project

Name: #whynamibia Travel Media
Description:
SHOWCASING NAMIBIA's FINEST

FILMSAFARIS ROAD TRIP  is an ambitious 18-month filming project by the Conservation Film Foundation, beginning in 2024. This project will produce the first two of many series, each consisting of 13 episodes, running 24 to 30 minutes each. Building on the success of Wild Namibia, which aired on ONE AFRICA TV in 2023, and inspired by the acclaimed COVID Chronicles series, this new production aims to captivate both local and international audiences.

We invite collaboration partners within Namibia’s tourism and hospitality industry to participate in this production by supporting these episodes. For more information on our affordable value packages, please contact us at media@ FILMSAFARIS .com. 

Set to air on ONE AFRICA TV via DSTV (Channel 284) in 2025, FilmSafaris Road Trip will also reach global viewers through our rapidly growing YouTube channel. This series aims to position Namibia as a premier destination for adventure seekers, luxury travelers, and conservation enthusiasts.

Through breathtaking cinematic storytelling, the series showcases Namibia’s stunning landscapes, rich cultural heritage, and critical conservation work. With a strong focus on environmental stewardship, FILMSAFARIS ROAD TRIP  highlights the essential efforts of individuals and organizations dedicated to preserving Namibia's wildlife and ecosystems, offering a once-in-a-lifetime travel experience while promoting the country's rich conservation narrative.

See our films: https://www.youtube.com/@FILMSAFARIS

Name: Community of Practice Conservation Standard Projects on Indigenous Lands and Waters
Description:
The Community of Practice Conservation Standard Projects on Indigenous Lands and Waters (CoP Indigenous CS) is a global network of people, projects, and practitioners who use the Conservation Standards, or an adaptation of, to effectively engage and support Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs) in leading conservation initiatives.
The CoP Indigenous CS Projects is part of the global Conservation Coaches network (CCNet) a global community of trained professional coaches that use the Conservation Standards to strengthen conservation action and results around the world.

Name: Texas Longleaf Team
Description: The Texas Longleaf Team is a group of individuals, organizations and agencies that share a passion for the longleaf pine.  Together, we work to restore longleaf pine ecosystems on private and public forestlands in the state of Texas.

Name: The SeaDoc Society
Description:
The mission of the SeaDoc Society is to ensure the health of marine wildlife and their ecosystems through science and education. Our office is based on Orcas Island in the Salish Sea. 

Founded in 2000, the SeaDoc Society conducts and sponsors vital scientific research in the inland waters of the Pacific Northwest, also known as the Salish Sea. This rich and vibrant ecosystem is home to 37 species of mammals, 172 species of birds, 253 species of fish, and more than 3,000 species of invertebrates. Nearly eight million people make this region their home today while billions of dollars are generated annually by Salish Sea commercial and recreational activities.

The SeaDoc Society conducts and sponsors ground-breaking marine research to uncover the environmental factors threatening to unravel the web of life in the Salish Sea and surrounding watersheds.

And while performing world-class research is of paramount importance, ensuring that our findings effect positive change is just as vital. We think of ourselves as arbiters of truth in matters of ocean health, and it's a role we take seriously. 

One of SeaDoc’s unique strengths is translating science into action. We act as a catalyst—bringing together interested parties to share information, forge common understandings and design region-wide solutions.

Name: Varu
Description:
Somos la primera franquicia turística en el mundo creada para combatir el cambio climático mediante experiencias que integran turismo regenerativo, programas de conservación ambiental, inteligencia artificial y el uso estratégico de data geoespacial.

Nuestras actividades no solo te conectan con la belleza natural de la región, sino que también impulsan una solución local con enfoque global, desarrollada desde la ciudad de Boquete, en Panamá, para proteger y regenerar los destinos turísticos del futuro.

Únete a nosotros para explorar, aprender y generar impacto real mientras disfrutas de una experiencia significativa, sostenible y transformadora.

Bienvenido a Varu!

Name: Kelp Ark
Description: Kelp Ark is a tax-exempt 501(c)(3) non-profit organization with the mission to ensure the health and biodiversity of wild macroalgae ecosystems in the nearshore coastal zones of the American Pacific.

Name: OJECC - Niger
Description: L'Organisation des Jeunes pour l'Eau et les Changements Climatiques (OJECC NIGER) contribue pour l'atteinte des ODD 6&13

Name: UF Center for Landscape Conservation Planning
Description: An official forum within the University of Florida’s College of Design, Construction and Planning for conducting applied research on the relationship between conservation and land use and bridging the disciplines of design, planning, and wildlife conservation.

Name: Conservation Outcomes Ltd.
Description:
Conservation Outcomes (CO) is a respected provider of conservation law enforcement advisory services and professional field ranger training across Africa. We specialise in developing skilled, disciplined ranger teams equipped to address complex operational, conservation, and community challenges.

Our training programs are holistic and progressive, combining essential law enforcement competencies with an understanding of the ecological and social contexts in which rangers operate. From terrestrial and marine environments to specialised units, our approach ensures rangers are prepared for real-world demands.

All CO trainers are seasoned professionals with extensive operational experience in African conservation. Their careers span some of the continent’s most challenging and iconic protected areas.

Beyond training, Conservation Outcomes offers strategic advisory and consultancy services to NGOs, private reserves, and government agencies working to strengthen protected area integrity and effectiveness.

Led by Brian Harris and a dedicated team with over 150 years of combined experience, our work has contributed to the success of conservation efforts across the continent, including:

* Grumeti Reserves, Tanzania
* Gorongosa National Park & Karingani Game Reserve, Mozambique
* Gashaka Gumti National Park, Nigeria
* Addo Elephant National Park, South Africa
* Sanbona Wildlife Reserve, South Africa
* Table Mountain National Park, South Africa

Services:

* Field Ranger Training - Terrestrial and Marine 
* Protected Area Security Planning & Risk Assessments
* Strategic Advisory & Consultancy

Our clients include:

* NGOs
* Private Conservation Organisations
* Government Agencies

info@conservationoutcomes.org

Name: CREDI-ONG BENIN
Description:
CREDI-ONG œuvre pour un monde plus vert et bleu où l'homme vit décemment et en harmonie avec la nature. Elle intervient dans la protection de l'environnement, l'agriculture durable et l'éducation à la citoyenneté mondiale. CREDI-ONG est initiatrice et gestionnaire du Parc Naturel Communautaire de la Vallée du Sitatunga depuis 2007. Un Parc d'environ 80.000 hectares couvrant 11 arrondissement dans trois communes du Bénin (Abomey-Calavi, Sô-Ava et Zè) et faisant partie intégrante de la Réserve de Biosphère de la Basse Vallée de l'Ouémé (RB-BVO).
Quelques domaines d'expertise:
- La gestion communautaire des ressources naturelles
- Le suivi écologique
- Le reboisement et l'enrichissement et la restauration des forêts
- Conservation ex-situ des crocodiles et des Sitatunga
- Le Volontariat et les échanges
- Education populaire à l'environnement, à l'agriculture durable et à la citoyenneté mondiale
- L'agroécologie et l'entrepreneuriat vert
- L'écotourisme 
etc.

Name: Reef Grazer Research
Description: The Reef Grazer Research group of Van Hall Larenstein, University of Applied sciences is actively working to restore the balance between algae and coral reefs by enhancing the populations of Diadema antillarum, long-spined sea urchin, a crucial herbivore that was nearly wiped out in 1983 due to an unknown disease. These urchins are vital for algae control and help support coral settlement and growth. Additionally, the we are exploring the feasibility of enhancing the populations of other native herbivorous grazing species, such as the Caribbean king crab (M. spinosissimus), whelk (C. Pica), and white sea urchin (T. ventricosus), to further boost grazing pressure and aid in reef restoration.

Name: Reteti Elephant Sanctuary
Description:
Reteti Elephant Sanctuary is located in Namunyak Community Conservancy in Northern Kenya – home to the nomadic Samburu people. 

Established by the indigenous community in 2016 to care for orphaned elephant calves, the our mission is to release them back into the wild. Reteti is the result of a widely recognised and expanding grassroots conservation movement driven by communities across this key region – a movement that is growing new economies, transforming lives, and conserving natural resources.

Many elephant calves are orphaned every year in Northern Kenya due to drought, man-made wells, human-wildlife conflict, and natural mortality. The sanctuary was established in response to urgent requests from the local community to rescue these orphans and bring them to safety. 

In the past, it was difficult and expensive to rescue orphaned elephant calves in such a remote region, and local communities saw little value in raising the alarm. Today, with Reteti, there’s a strong desire to assist orphaned calves and help them remain in the landscape of their birth. 

The Kenya Wildlife Service and Samburu County Government have promoted the establishment of the sanctuary, with local communities leading the rescue, rehabilitation, and release of the calves.

Reteti’s Keepers are all recruited from within Namunyak Community Conservancy. They are all part of the Samburu community, which has lived alongside wildlife for thousands of years and deeply respect the elephants with whom they share the land and its resources.

Name: Gwent Wildlife Trust
Description: Gwent Wildlife Trust is Gwent's leading conservation charity, working to restore wildlife and bring people closer to nature.

Name: HaejooX
Description: #ClimateAction #BlueCarbon #Biodiversity #NatureBasedSolution

Name: Massachusetts Land Trust Coalition
Description:
The Massachusetts Land Trust Coalition advances land conservation across Massachusetts by providing education, tools, networking, and advocacy for land trusts and their partners.

Vision Statement
The Massachusetts Land Trust Coalition envisions a robust and resilient mosaic of conserved open spaces that serves the diverse needs of people and wildlife across Massachusetts and is sustained by an effective and united land conservation community.

What We Do
We strengthen, connect, and unify over 140 land trusts that work across Massachusetts to protect farms and forests, conserve wildlife habitat, preserve water quality, buffer communities from impacts of climate change, and create parks and trails that provide healthy outdoor recreation opportunities.

We build the capacity of the conservation community by organizing regular training and networking opportunities, including one of the nation's largest annual land conservation conferences.

We are the voice of Massachusetts’ land trusts on policy issues, advocating for strong legislation and innovative funding at the state level.

We facilitate innovative collaborations among land trusts and public and private partners to advance shared priorities.

Name: STOP PLÁSTICO
Description:
Desde STOP PLÁSTICO trabajamos para y por la sensibilización medioambiental, sobre todo en lo referente al plástico y a sus residuos.

En nuestros paseos por el campo, con demasiada frecuencia, nos encontrábamos con escombros tirados por la naturaleza, enseres y basuras en cunetas, caminos, bajo puentes,… en los lugares más escondidos e insospechados.

Al principio salíamos con nuestra bolsa de basura e íbamos recogiendo todo lo que “pillabamos” hasta llenarla y siempre volvíamos con esa extraña sensación de que con eso no era suficiente, que había que hacer algo más..

Así que, decidimos dar un paso al frente, plantarnos ante las administraciones y hacerles llegar nuestras quejas.

Ahora, teléfono en mano, fotografiamos y localizamos todos los vertidos que nos encontramos en nuestro camino.

Y así nació este proyecto, una herramienta para la sensibilización ambiental y canal de denuncia ante las administraciones.
En 2023 Stop Plástico ha dado un paso más y nos hemos conformado como asociación sin animo de lucro.

Name: Nyungwe National Park-Rwanda
Description:
Nyungwe is the largest remaining tract of forest in Rwanda nestled in the southwest of the country managed in partnership by #Africanparks and #RDBrwanda.

Name: Forest Park Conservancy
Description:
Portland’s Forest Park is one of the largest urban forests in the United States, with more than 100 species of native trees, 112 species of birds and more than 60 species of mammals beneath its 5,200-acre canopy. Thousands of human visitors walk, hike, bike and run along Forest Park’s 80 miles of trails, all entirely within the Portland city limits. 

And yet it’s so much more than just a city park: Forest Park is where the Oregon Coast Range forest reaches east to welcome the people of Portland and tourists into its uniquely beautiful  world: a vast forest ecosystem that stretches from California to Alaska. 

The Forest Park Conservancy is a community supported non-profit that protects and restores Forest Park. We engage more than 1,000 volunteers who give tens of thousands of hours and significant financial resources to Forest Park because, quite simply, Forest Park gives so much to us. 

Currently, we are leading a 20-year strategy to restore and protect the Greater Forest Park Ecosystem which is a total of 15,000 acres, including Forest Park. This strategy, called the Greater Forest Park Conservation Initiative, will play a significant role ensuring the Portland region is a healthy place to live, work, and play.

We invite you to join us in our continuing work in the park, by becoming a volunteer and making a gift. You can become a part of our efforts to ensure its health for future generations.

Visit us at www.forestparkconservancy.org

Name: Fondation Breizh Biodiv
Description:
Créée en 2021 à l’initiative de la Région Bretagne, en partenariat avec plusieurs acteurs majeurs du territoire (EDF, ENGIE, Crédit Mutuel Arkéa et le groupe La Poste), la Fondation Breizh Biodiv apporte un soutien aux projets vertueux et d'intérêt général dans toute la Bretagne. 

La fondation finance des projets concrets de préservation et de restauration de la biodiversité. Son action, au service du territoire breton, repose sur la coopération, la sensibilisation et l’implication des acteurs publics et privés que sont les collectivités territoriales, les agences, les entreprises, les associations, les scientifiques et les citoyens.

Elle garantit ainsi, une expertise scientifique de haut niveau et une évaluation rigoureuse des projets financés grâce à l’appui technique et scientifique de ses membres fondateurs que sont la Ligue pour la Protection des Oiseaux (LPO), le Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique (CNRS) et France Nature Environnement Bretagne (FNE) ou encore l’Agence Bretonne de la Biodiversité (ABB).

Domaines d'actions : 

 Préserver et restaurer la biodiversité
 Planter pour un avenir plus durable 
 Préserver la ressource en eau
 Atténuer les effets du changement climatique et préparer les territoires à s’y adapter
 Mobiliser la société

Name: Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari
Description:
Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari is a mainland ecological island located in the mighty Waikato, on the North Island of New Zealand. Surrounded by the world’s longest pest-proof fence, Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari's ancient forest offers a sanctuary for populations of many of our most endangered species – from birds to bats, frogs to reptiles, tuatara to giant weta.

Sanctuary Mountain Maungatautari is easily accessible to the public. Come and experience for yourself the New Zealand environment the way it used to be – an ancient, vibrant pest-free forest alive with native wildlife including many of New Zealand’s rarest and most endangered plants, insects, birds and fish. Step through the pest-proof gate into another world where ancient New Zealand forest towers majestically above well maintained walking tracks and the air is filled with birdsong. Enjoy a variety of hikes and bush walks around our eco sanctuary, take a guided tour or nature walk and learn about why this conservation project is so special.

Name: EU Ocean Governance 4 MPAs
Description: Ocean governance is about taking a coordinated approach to tackle major challenges that face the world’s oceans, seas and coastal areas: over-fishing, climate change, acidification, pollution, biodiversity decline and ecosystem degradation. The project is carrying out site restoration works in Indonesia, Malaysia and the Philippines. In and between the Atlantic and South-East Asia, it will build ‘twinning partnerships’, groups of marine protected area managers and other experts coming together to work on topics of shared interest (such as whale conservation or coastal resilience) to build skills and understanding and deliver more effective management of the marine environment. This project is building meaningful links between marine protected areas in the Atlantic basin and South-East Asia, for the benefit of wildlife conservation and local economies. The project brings together partners from North and South America, Europe, Africa and Asia. It is funded by the European Union and will last until December 2023. Funded by the European Union, it is implemented by GOPA Worldwide Consultants in consortium with B&S Europe and WWF Indonesia.

Name: Free the Bears Ltd
Description: Free the Bears works to end the exploitation of threatened bear species and supports the protection of bears in the wild. We work with governments and nongovernmental partners to strengthen wildlife law enforcement, improve environmental awareness and promote sustainable livelihoods for communities that previously relied on the exploitation of bears.

Name: CLUB ODD USenghor
Description:
Créé par des étudiants de la promotion 18 de l' Université Senghor à Alexandrie,  le Club #ODD s'érige en tant qu'espace dynamique dédié à la réflexion et au partage de savoir-faire en matière d'Objectif de Développement Durable (ODD).
Initié avec passion, ce club constitue un véritable creuset intellectuel, offrant une plateforme inclusive où convergent diverses compétences et idées novatrices.
Le club se veut un moteur de l'innovation, favorisant la synergie entre les membres, tous animés par une volonté commune de contribuer de manière significative à la réalisation de l'Agenda #2030 des Nations Unies.
En tant que véritable catalyseur d'idées, le club accueille avec enthousiasme toutes les initiatives, les transformant en leviers concrets pour atteindre les objectifs ambitieux fixés par la communauté internationale.
Au-delà d'un simple rassemblement d'étudiants, le Club ODD incarne un esprit de collaboration et d'engagement envers la durabilité.
#sdginnovationchallenge #SDG #ODD

Name: Grasslands Conservation Council of BC
Description:
WHO WE ARE: 
Our mission is to ensure the conservation, restoration and stewardship of British Columbia’s grasslands through integrated partnerships and collaboration across people, place and purpose.

British Columbia’s native grasslands are unique and important ecosystems. 
Did you know?
•	Along with the other diminishing temperate grasslands of the world, they have been internationally recognized as an ecosystem at risk. 
•	Covering less than 1% of the province, BC’s grasslands have local, regional, provincial, and federal significance as they are home to more than 30% of its species at risk. 
•	Okanagan and other interior grasslands are a continuation of the Great Steppe Grasslands that extend through the United States to Mexico and provide a critical corridor for species movement between Mexico and Canada. 
By working together to protect special areas, practicing active management, and engaging in wise stewardship, we can strengthen and renew our grassland ecosystems.

Name: Origin Océan
Description:
Origin est une équipe de scaphandriers engagée qui souhaite promouvoir une gestion écologique innovante des espaces portuaires et fluviaux.  
Nous constatons un déclin de la vie portuaire impactée par l’urbanisation et la pollution tellurique. 
Pour cela nous souhaitons unir la communauté locale et mettre en place des solutions concrètes, afin de dépolluer et restaurer les écosystèmes aquatiques.

Name: Direction des Parcs nationaux du Sénégal
Description: Mise en oeuvre la politique de l’Etat en matière de conservation de la biodiversité et de gestion des Aires protégées à travers les Parcs nationaux et les réserves naturelles du Sénégal.

Name: Fondation Albédo pour la cryosphère
Description:
Créée sous l’égide de la Fondation CNRS, la fondation Albédo pour la Cryosphère a pour ambition de financer la recherche d’intérêt français et francophone sur la question des pôles et des glaciers dans une volonté de trouver des solutions pour leur préservation. Elle a également vocation a conduire des actions à caractère d’information et de pédagogie à destination du grand public.

Elle est présidée par Frederik Paulsen.

Name: Canadian Conservation Photographers Collective
Description:
The Canadian Conservation Photography Collective (CCPC) was formed in 2021 with the objective of promoting conservation and science education through the production of strong, thought-provoking visual content.

The collective is composed of freelance photographers from across the country and from a wide variety of occupational training backgrounds including biology, education, journalism, social work and business. We firmly believe that networking as a team and combining our strengths and experience dramatically furthers our ability to shed light on pressing conservation issues and to reach, inform and engage a much wider audience. 

In addition to regularly publishing content on its social media platforms and through established media channels, the CCPC plans to expand its outreach activities to include conferences, presentations and in-house print publications. We recognize the importance and effectiveness of a collaborative hands-on approach and therefore also plan to offer workshops, mentorship sessions and guided expeditions aimed at a variety of skill levels and age groups. 

In order to effectively and accurately document and relate often complex and time-sensitive conservation issues throughout the entirety of the country, we work in concert with other collectives, NGO’s, non-profits, research organizations, governments and communities.

The CCPC strongly believes in photography as a tool to educate, inspire and engage and our goal is to produce content that will open dialogues, stimulate conversations and ultimately bring about change.

Name: Mariners for Action (MFA)
Description: A marine conservation organization focused on the protection and preservation of marine ecosystems and biodiversity. We are committed to advancing marine research, promoting sustainable practices, and fostering community development. Our goal is to address environmental challenges and empower coastal communities through education, capacity building, and conservation efforts that ensure the long-term health of marine environments and the well-being of those who depend on them.

Name: Parc naturel régional des Ardennes
Description:
Situé au nord du département, le Parc naturel régional des Ardennes vous étonnera par la richesse de ses patrimoines naturel, humain et bâti. Des méandres des vallées de la Meuse et de la Semoy aux landes tourbeuses du Plateau de Rocroi, en passant par les doux vallons bocagers de la Thiérache ardennaise, il vous surprendra par la diversité de ses paysages.

Financements FEDER

Name: Humans Music
Description: Love your Music. Love your Planet. Regenerate the planet through the music industry and the Music Planet Pack.

Name: Green Horizon
Description: Green Horizon is a foundation focused on integrated reforestation, afforestation and conservation projects in Sub-Saharan Africa. Green Horizon is characterised by an integrated sustainable model for reforestation in which the focus lies on conservation, biodiversity, and where all stakeholders’ interests are taken into account, in a way that there is a mutual interest for the projects to succeed in the long term and so being truly sustainable.

Name: ÉcoJeunesSénégal
Description: nous sommes une organisation environmental à but non lucrative axée sur l'education à l'eco-citoyeneté.nous ciblons la tranche d'age 11 à17 ans pour faire d'eux des adultes eco-responsable capable de developper le senegal dans la durabilité.

Name: Native Prairies Association of Texas
Description:
The mission of the Native Prairies Association of Texas (NPAT) is to conserve and restore native prairies and educate Texans about their remarkable benefits. Grasslands are the most endangered habitat on Earth. Of the 26.5 million acres of tallgrass and coastal prairies in Texas that existed prior to European settlement, less than 1% remains. 

We collaborate with landowners to encourage prairie conservation and restoration. We enlighten students, teachers, and community leaders about the beauty and benefits of native prairies. This comes to life through outreach, field trips, workshops, webcasts, videos, volunteer projects, and youth education programs.  

Texas prairies provide a variety of social, environmental, and economic benefits: surprising plant and animal diversity, natural beauty, rich soil, water filtration, flood mitigation, hunting and birding recreation, healthy livestock forage, and more reliable carbon storage than forests. They are part of our state's heritage, and we need to save them.

NPAT is a nonprofit membership organization and accredited land trust, founded in 1986 by a group of Texans concerned about our disappearing prairie heritage, especially endangered tallgrass prairies. We protect over 6,500 acres of this rare ecosystem through acquisition, partnerships, and conservation easements. Our holdings include 11 owned properties and 19 permanently protected prairies through voluntary agreements with private landowners.

Name: One Forest Youth Initiative (OFYI)
Description: An NGO is an international non-governmental organization for youth, founded on voluntary and committed participation of young people, women, and elders. It is a non-political and non-profit organization that is open to everyone without distinction of gender, origin, race or belief, which promotes issues related to sustainable development, forest ecosystems, and biodiversity, in accordance with the objectives, missions, and programs as conceived by the Founders and formulated below.

Name: Projeto Queamar
Description: Fundado pela Prof.ª Dr.ª Larissa Barreto, o Projeto Queamar (Quelônios Aquáticos do Maranhão) é vinculado ao Departamento de Oceanografia e Limnologia da Universidade Federal do Maranhão. Desde então, o laboratório une esforços com a comunidade científica para contribuir na elucidação de aspectos biológicos, ecológicos e genéticos de tartarugas e mamíferos marinhos encontrados em território maranhense. O projeto também conta com o Centro de Reabilitação de Fauna Marinha, localizado no Instituto Federal do Maranhão, Campus Barreirinhas.

Name: LPO Anjou
Description: La LPO Anjou est une association loi 1901 dédiée à la protection de la biodiversité dans le Maine-et-Loire (49).

Name: Long Live the Kings
Description:
Our mission is to restore wild salmon and steelhead and support sustainable fishing in the Pacific Northwest. Since our founding in 1986, we have combined innovative field work, pioneering science, broad partnerships, and sophisticated new management tools to help decision-makers advance salmon recovery while balancing the needs of fish and people.

Our vision is a Northwest with a growing human population, a thriving economy, and strong and vibrant salmon runs. With your support, we’re making that vision a reality.

Name: Fisheries Management Scotland
Description: Fisheries Management Scotland is the representative body for Scotland's District Salmon Fishery Boards, the River Tweed Commission and charitable Rivers and Fisheries Trusts. Our members work to conserve Scotland’s valuable and iconic wild salmon and freshwater fish and fisheries and the aquatic environment on which they depend.

Name: Windfall Ecology Centre
Description:
Windfall Ecology Centre is a non-profit social enterprise dedicated to building sustainable communities. The creation of Windfall was inspired by a vision of healthy communities, where economic, social, and environmental needs achieve balance through social innovation, community partnership, and community action. The Centre's activities focus on empowering communities to create lasting wealth in a carbon constrained 21st century

Working at the nexus of energy, water, food, and people we research, design, and deliver innovative climate change solutions. Our record of accomplishment includes achievements in public policy and education, residential energy conservation, renewable energy, electric mobility, water protection and leadership development.

We build opportunities for youth in all that we do because mentoring young leaders is fundamental to the sustainable future we envision.

Name: Nature4Health
Description:
Nature for Health (N4H) is a new global initiative working with partner organisations and countries to reduce future pandemics and related health risks. Specifically, N4H aims to prevent zoonotic spillover events by strengthening the environmental aspects of One Health, an inter-sectoral, interdisciplinary approach acting on the interdependencies of human, animal and ecosystem health.

N4H brings together leading UN agencies, Intergovernmental organisations and civil society groups in the field of environment and health in a consortium of initially eight Partners who leverage extensive multisectoral and diverse One Health practical experience. These are: The United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP), the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD), the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the World Health Organization (WHO), the World Organisation for Animal Health ((WOAH), the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) and EcoHealth Alliance.

Through an initial contribution of EUR 50 million from the German Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation, Nuclear Safety and Consumer Protection (BMUV) via Germany’s International Climate Initiative (IKI), N4H will work with six initial country partners: Ecuador, Ghana, Mongolia, Rwanda, Vietnam and Zambia, to implement on-the-ground actions and policies to conserve ecosystems and biodiversity and thus reduce pandemic risk.

N4H is structured as a Multi-Partner Trust Fund (MPTF), facilitating pooled funding and needs-based resource allocation. N4H is administered by the UN Multi-Partner Trust Fund Office (MPTFO) in New York.

Name: Reteti Elephant Sanctuary
Description:
Reteti Elephant Sanctuary is located in Namunyak Community Conservancy in Northern Kenya – home to the nomadic Samburu people. 

Established by the indigenous community in 2016 to care for orphaned elephant calves, the our mission is to release them back into the wild. Reteti is the result of a widely recognised and expanding grassroots conservation movement driven by communities across this key region – a movement that is growing new economies, transforming lives, and conserving natural resources.

Many elephant calves are orphaned every year in Northern Kenya due to drought, man-made wells, human-wildlife conflict, and natural mortality. The sanctuary was established in response to urgent requests from the local community to rescue these orphans and bring them to safety. 

In the past, it was difficult and expensive to rescue orphaned elephant calves in such a remote region, and local communities saw little value in raising the alarm. Today, with Reteti, there’s a strong desire to assist orphaned calves and help them remain in the landscape of their birth. 

The Kenya Wildlife Service and Samburu County Government have promoted the establishment of the sanctuary, with local communities leading the rescue, rehabilitation, and release of the calves.

Reteti’s Keepers are all recruited from within Namunyak Community Conservancy. They are all part of the Samburu community, which has lived alongside wildlife for thousands of years and deeply respect the elephants with whom they share the land and its resources.

Name: Young Protectors of Environment ( YPE)
Description: Young Protectors of Environment  est une association à but non lucratif, formée et dirigée par des jeunes, diversifiée et inclusive, qui s'est donné pour objectifs de protéger l'environnement et de promouvoir l'agriculture durable et défendre le climat . par nos gestes et actions, partout où nous irons, afin d'impacter notre communauté avec des pratiques respectueuses et durables.

Name: Conservation Spotlight Zambia
Description: Putting Conservation on Spotlight

Name: My Zalu
Description:
Welcome to the My Zalu, where we believe that every child deserves the opportunity to learn about and preserve the environment in a way that is meaningful and relevant to them. Our mission is to raise environmental care and awareness among children around the world through story, cultural exchange and hands on activities.

Our goal is to provide children with an easy and fun way to learn about the world around them in their own language, with Zalu as a guide to inspire them to take action and make a positive impact on their environment. The project's slogan is "My Zalu, Your Zalu, Our Zalu" emphasizing that Zalu represents the tangible and the intangible things we all share, our planet, plants,  animals, and all of us.

Name: Dinokeng Game Reserve
Description:
Welcome to Dinokeng Game Reserve's LinkedIn page, where we're committed to conservation, sustainability, and research.

Located in the heart of South Africa, we're home to an incredible diversity of wildlife, including the Big Five. But we're not just a game reserve - we're a community-driven destination that's passionate about creating a sustainable future for both people and wildlife.

Our research focuses on innovative water management systems, community outreach programs, and other projects promoting conservation and sustainability. By working together, we can make a meaningful difference in the world.
