Whether you're a researcher, conservationist, or just passionate about nature, we invite you to join us on this journey of discovery. Let's work together to preserve and protect our environment for future generations.

Name: Sunu Plastic Odyssey
Description: Sunu plastic odyssey est une initiative dédiée à la lutte contre la pollution plastique et à la valorisation des déchets plastiques au Sénégal.

Name: The Water of Leith Conservation Trust
Description:
The Water of Leith Conservation Trust’s works to conserve and enhance the Water of Leith as a haven for biodiversity and be an educational and recreational resource for all. 

We are out 2 - 3 times a week with volunteers groups on the river doing a variety of tasks from maintaining the walkway, wading the river for invasive species, litter picking , tending  wildflower meadows and  community gardens. So feel free to contact us if you'd like to get involved.

As Scotland’s first river charity and guardians of the river, we strive to raise the profile of this key environmental asset and promote community action to help the river.

Name: Green Horizon Foundation (GHF)
Description:
-To protect and preserve the natural habitats of threatened species and promote food security with maintained biodiversity.
-To create a harmonious coexistence between wildlife conservation and the upliftment of local communities.

Name: Collectif Mauricien pour Roches Noires
Description: Bienvenue sur la page officielle du Collectif mauricien pour Roches Noires. Notre objectif : créer un parc naturel, pérenne et ouvert à tous, avec les ONGs et les habitants, et faire émerger des projets verts qui aideront le développement de la région.

Name: Bioverse
Description:
Bioverse identifies and enables new revenue streams for customers that manage or restore biodiverse forest systems, especially in the Amazon.

Bioverse helps to strengthen Non-Timber Forest Product supply chains, by connecting buyers and harvesters with digital tools and AI-driven analysis of forest canopy. 

Whether you are a bulk buyer of Non-Timber Forest Products or whether you are creating forest conservation projects for carbon credits, Bioverse can help add financial resilience and upside to your work. 

Bioverse co-designed digital tools with indigenous and local communities to ensure that the tools are easy to use and high impact.

Name: The Wildlands Conservancy
Description:
Founded in 1995, The Wildlands Conservancy (TWC) is dedicated to preserving the beauty and biodiversity of the earth and providing programs so that children may know the wonder and joy of nature.

In working to achieve this mission, TWC has established the largest nonprofit nature preserve system in California, comprised of 22 preserves encompassing 178,000 acres of diverse mountain, valley, desert, river, and coastal landscapes. These preserves are open to the public free of charge for passive recreation, including camping, hiking, picnicking, birding, and more.

In late 2021, The Wildlands Conservancy took the bold step to expand its model of stewardship and free public access into additional western states with the acquisition of the 30,000-acre Cherry Creek Ranch, now Enchanted Rocks Preserve.

Ultimately, saving our treasured landscapes from development means educating and instilling a love for nature in future generations. For this reason, TWC is also the state’s nonprofit leader in providing free outdoor education opportunities for California youth. Through these programs and our reverent stewardship of preserves—visited by more than 1.5 million people per year—we foster a love and respect for life in all of its magnificent forms.

Name: Fauna del Noroeste
Description:
Fauna del Noroeste (FAUNO) is a non-profit organization in northwest Mexico whose mission is to promote the proper management of natural resources through scientific research, restoration, education and collaboration. 

We believe that by developing scientific information and building community commitment it is possible to preserve the flora and fauna!

Name: SOIL CONSERVATION COUNCIL OF CANADA
Description:
The Soil Conservation Council of Canada (SCCC) has a rich history as the face and voice of soil conservation in Canada. The SCCC was founded in 1987, by a group of individuals under the leadership of Senator Herb Sparrow to advocate for the importance of soil conservation on a national scale. The SCCC delivered several national and regional programs with government support, and in 2014, successfully co-hosted the 6th World Congress on Conservation Agriculture. Since then, SCCC has hosted a series of Summits on Canadian Soil Health to examine key and emerging issues affecting soil health.

The SCCC is the only national organization to concentrate on the issues of soil health and soil conservation within a broadly based landscape context. It works to build a greater understanding of the importance of soil as an essential resource to society by facilitating the exchange of information with all stakeholders. Healthy soils are the foundation of sustainable food production, enhanced biodiversity and cleaner air and water for present and future generations.

Name: CITES Global Youth Network
Description: CGYN aims to build capacity in a global network of youths to take action against illegal wildlife trade via engagement with the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES). We are dedicated to increasing understanding, empathy, and youth involvement in wildlife trade-related issues. Under CGYN, we hope to equip youths with these essential knowledge, values, and skills to allow them to contribute practical and constructive solutions for sustainable and fair wildlife trade, and create a next-gen network of young leaders to develop partnerships and collaborations that transcend geographical, generational, and sectoral boundaries.

Name: Entre Parques BR
Description:
Uma expedição por todos os parques nacionais brasileiros, realizada ao longo de 3,5 anos por um casal: Letícia Alves, psicóloga, e Dennis Hyde,  economista.
Sem patrocínio ou fins lucrativos, foram visitados e documentados todos os 75 parques nacionais brasileiros, gerando inúmeras imagens, relatos e mapas, disponibilizados de forma gratuita a todos.
Com isto, foi criado o Instituto Entreparques, cuja missão é promover a conexão transformadora entre as pessoas e o patrimônio natural brasileiro, por meio da visitação consciente e da interpretação ambiental, inspirando a sociedade a conhecer, valorizar e engajar-se ativamente na conservação dos Parques Nacionais.

Name: Tree Kangaroo Conservation Program (TKCP)
Description:
The Tree Kangaroo Conservation Program works with local communities in rural Papua New Guinea to protect the endangered Matschie’s tree kangaroo and the habitat in which it lives. The program helped to establish the country’s first and only nationally-recognized Conservation Area, and works with communities and government to address local needs including livelihoods, health, and education. We believe that sustainable conservation needs to address not only the needs of wildlife and the environment, but the needs of the local people as well. And that in order to make a lasting difference, the change must come from within those communities themselves.

The Tree Kangaroo Conservation Program and the YUS Conservation Area serve as a national model for community-based conservation in Papua New Guinea, assisting the government in their efforts to protect the incredible diversity of wildlife and culture throughout the country.

The Tree Kangaroo Conservation Program (TKCP) is the umbrella name for the partnership between the Woodland Park Zoo’s TKCP and TKCP-PNG, an independent non-governmental organization registered in Papua New Guinea.

Name: European Ranger Federation
Description: All over Europe, natural areas aim at conserving biodiversity and reconnecting people with nature. A few men and women work in the field to care for these areas: the Rangers.

Name: The Kelp Rescue Initiative
Description:
Kelp forests are vanishing all around the world. Fast.

We're developing science-backed solutions to restore and strengthen them. 

Many of the world's kelp forests are declining. Over the last seven years, up to 90% of kelp has disappeared in some areas off North America's west coast. 

Increasing water temperatures are killing kelp forests at the surface. And the mass die of sea stars means urchins are killing them from below. The result is a stressed ecosystem that is severely out of balance. 

Just like jungles, healthy kelp forests are rich in life. They provide food and shelter for dozens of species, creating diverse and vibrant marine ecosystems. 

Kelp forests regulate water quality and reduce acidity in the ocean. They can also protect our coastlines from storms and erosion. 

Kelp forests also absorb CO2. Lots of it. Scientists believe it could become a powerful tool in the fight against climate change. 

Successful kelp restoration starts with asking the right questions, building strong partnerships, and creating scalable solutions.

Name: Alianza Ambiental de América
Description:
Alianza Ambiental de América creates the first regional ecolabelling program supported by regulators, standardization, and accreditation organisms. It promotes coordination of environmental standards to foster sustainable trade and better informed consumption options. 

The program establishes a voluntary label that facilitates the identification of products, processes, and services that have demonstrated compliance with characteristics and environmental criteria defined in national harmonized regulations between the participant States or in specific Product Category Regulations, contributing therefore to sustainable development in America and fostering a low-carbon, resource-efficient economy.

Name: Apalis Nature Ltd
Description:
At Apalis Nature Limited, we are driven by a vision of a thriving and resilient natural environment across Kenya. Our mission is to champion the conservation and restoration of ecosystems through innovative nature-based solutions. By collaborating closely with local communities, government agencies, and a diverse array of stakeholders, we are committed to safeguarding and revitalizing critical habitats, protecting endangered species, and promoting sustainable livelihoods.

Our approach emphasizes on partnership and collective action. We work hand-in-hand with the local communities to ensure that conservation efforts benefit both people and biodiversity. By engaging with government agencies, we advocate for policies that support environmental conservation and sustainable development. Additionally, we collaborate with academic and research institutions , NGOs, and other partners to leverage expertise and resources in our conservation initiatives.

Apalis’s commitment extends beyond environmental/biodiversity conservation to encompass sustainable development of communities. We recognize that thriving ecosystems are fundamental to human well-being, providing essential services such as clean water, climate regulation, and preservation of cultural values. Therefore, we work to improve livelihoods by promoting practices that support both economic prosperity and environmental stewardship through nature-based solutions.Through our collaborative efforts, we strive to inspire individuals and organizations to join us in ensuring a sustainable future for all generations by creating a harmonious balance between human needs and the health of our planet.

Name: AltijdWerkplaats
Description:
De AltijdWerkplaats is een gemeenschapsbedrijf (Community Interest Company). Wij willen natuurinclusief denken en handelen bevorderen en de biodiversiteit vergroten waarmee we tegelijkertijd lokaal toekomstbestendig werk en meer economische zelfstandigheid creëren (en daarmee zingeving, voldoening en waardering voor de mens oogsten). Als pioniers streven wij naar een nieuwe (complementaire en betekenisvollere) economie, waar meer oog is voor de natuur en de aarde waarbij wij tijd en energie van mensen inzetten die nu nog onbenut is. Ons concept is een unieke kans om meer van deze energie op te halen voor het herstel van de natuur daar waar de huidige economie te kort schiet. Hierin maken wij juist (!) geen onderscheid in doelgroepen, achtergronden en domeinen.

Wij willen dat ons werk inspireert, zodat mensen meer natuurbewust worden en vandaaruit gaan handelen. Natuur werkt en inspireert!

Name: Congaree Land Trust
Description:
Congaree Land Trust (CLT) is a 501(c)(3) organization founded in 1992 with a mission to promote  conservation of scenic lands, open spaces, farms, forests, natural areas and significant habitats in central South Carolina. CLT currently holds over 98,000 acres under conservation & agricultural easements within its 16 county area of operation and has acquired and transferred over 350 acres to SCPRT for public recreation.

In 2012, Congaree Land Trust received National Accreditation from the Land Trust Accreditation Commission. Accredited land trusts meet national quality standards, uphold the public trust and ensure that conservation efforts are permanent.

CLT currently serves the following counties: Aiken, Bamberg, Barnwell, Calhoun, Chester, Clarendon, Fairfield, Kershaw, Lee, Lexington, Newberry, Orangeburg, Richland, Saluda, Sumter, Williamsburg

Name: UAE Dolphin Project
Description:
The UAE Dolphin Project is a non-profit initiative dedicated to investigating the dolphin population along the coastline of the United Arab Emirates.  We aim to promote the conservation of these dolphin species and the local marine environment.  

This is achieved through work in 3 main areas: scientific research, public awareness campaigns, and educational programs involving both public and private sectors. 

As the first project monitoring coastal dolphins in the whole Arabian Gulf, it is a pioneering undertaking. It entails the collaboration of national and international, private and public institutions and organizations.  Learn more about the project and view our dolphin sighting map at www.uaedolphinproject.org.

Name: forest.ly
Description:
forest.ly grows sustainable forests. We are a pragmatic and progressive hub of talented activists and conscious organizations, aiming to create an interdisciplinary community around reforestation and nature preservation.

forest.ly buys or rents barren, eroded, or any other uneconomical land all over the world, and regenerates its former forests. We visualize the value forests bring to communities around the world. You can be part of these forests by donating to them and joining the community to create more forests, where they are needed most.

Name: Findhorn Watershed Initiative
Description:
The Findhorn Watershed Initiative is a multi-generational vision to restore a mosaic of nature rich habitats, grow a culture of nature connection and enable a nature-positive economy for the people and places of the Findhorn watershed, from the Monadhliath Mountains to the Moray Firth.

Catalysed by the global drive towards net zero and ecosystem restoration, the Initiative brings together land managers, communities, businesses, and other river users in an accelerated, collaborative programme of habitat restoration, carbon sequestration and river stewardship. 

Instigated and led by local environmental conservation intermediary – the Findhorn, Nairn and Lossie Rivers Trust – the Findhorn Watershed Initiative was created in response to national and global frameworks, such as the Scottish Government’s Just Transition Outcomes and the UN Decade of Ecosystem Restoration, as well as the Trust’s 2021-26 Management Plan, which prioritises conservation and restoration of its river catchments.

Name: WildlifeNL
Description:
The Dutch Wildlife Comeback: Dynamic co-management of human-wildlife interactions in the Netherlands (WildlifeNL).

With a consortium of academic and non-academic partners, WildlifeNL seeks to develop a critical transition in wildlife management, based on a comprehensive interdisciplinary analysis society’s relationship with wildlife that combines insights from social science, political science, philosophy and ecology. We propose to test innovative ways of managing human-wildlife interactions by exploring models that focus on the pro-active co-management of the behaviors of both humans and non-human animals. 

This shift towards managing behaviors is facilitated by the expanding use of novel smart technologies in wildlife management. WildlifeNL will help co-develop technologies that can improve human-wildlife interactions, such as serious games and improved monitoring through sensor networks, critically examine the effectiveness of these methods and other policy instruments, as well as study the broader social, ethical and philosophical consequences of an increasing role of technology in human-nature relationships. 
Together with stakeholders, we will explore these issues in two model landscapes; the National Park Zuid-Kennemerland and the Grenspark Kempen-Broek.

Name: Rivages de France
Description:
"Gestionnaires fédérés, littoral préservé !"

Depuis 1990, l’association Rivages de France fédère, représente, anime et valorise le réseau national des gestionnaires impliqués dans la protection et le devenir des espaces naturels littoraux et lacustres préservés.

Name: Vertas Ambiental
Description:
A Vertas trabalha para que o lixo tecnológico, o chamado e-lixo, produzido nas empresas, instituições e por cada um de nós, retorne ao ciclo produtivo e não seja descartado inadequadamente.
Atendemos clientes de todos os segmentos e tamanhos que necessitam soluções para o lixo eletrônico: gerenciando, transformando e dando a destinação correta aos resíduos.

Name: L.I.G.H.T. Foundation
Description:
The L.I.G.H.T. Foundation (LF and Foundation interchangeably) is an independent, Indigenous-led, charitable entity organized exclusively for educational, conservation, literary, and research purposes established in 2022.  It supports the restoration and cultivation of native Plant and Pollinator Relatives and the culturally appropriate and respectful conservation of habitats and ecosystems which are climate resilient and adaptive.

Specifically, LF was formed with a three-pronged mission to:
(1) Cultivate positive relationships between Indigenous Peoples and landowners in order to have shared outcomes in ecological stewardship, habitat conservation, and climate adaptation.
(2) Enrich communities by providing hands-on tools and support for teaching gathering traditions and honoring native plants and pollinators in culturally appropriate ways.
(3) Perpetuate the ecological heritage of Indigenous Peoples through intergenerational knowledge transmission and supporting traditional landscape practices.

The Foundation accomplishes this mission through the operation of five initiatives developed to support community-based and intergenerational climate resilience, food and water security, rights of nature, and the advancement of Indigenous Peoples rights and Tribal sovereignty.  These initiatives are: Roots for the Future, Secure Harvest, Certified Indigenous, First Food Pollinators, and Indigenous Climate Futures.  

Additionally, the L.I.G.H.T. Foundation also operates the We Swim Together Scholarship Program, developed to provide financial assistance to aspiring Indigenous academics.  Through the support of our all-volunteer Advisory Team and Council of Guides (i.e., Board of Directors), LF also engages in public comment opportunities at the local, regional, and national levels.  Learn more about our initiatives, team, and work at our website.

Name: Sustained Wild
Description:
Vision

A world where people understand the importance of wetlands and actively participate in their protection and restoration.

Mission

To bridge the gap between humans and nature by providing knowledge, raising awareness, and inspiring action to conserve wetland ecosystems.

Name: Malaysian Conservation Alliance For Tigers (MYCAT)
Description:
Founded in 2003, MYCAT emerged as a pivotal platform facilitating communication and collaboration between the Malaysian government and non-governmental organizations (NGOs) with a shared objective of the conservation of Malayan tigers. The founding members include the Department of Wildlife and National Parks Peninsular Malaysia (DWNP), Malaysian Nature Society (MNS), TRAFFIC, and WWF-Malaysia. In subsequent years, the alliance expanded with the inclusion of Wildlife Conservation Society-Malaysia in 2005 and Wildlife Society of Selangor (WILD) in 2017. 

In the subsequent decade, MYCAT has evolved to engage members of the public for a greater stride towards the recovery of wild Malayan tigers through its Citizen Action for Tiger (CAT) and Community Ranger (CR) programmes.

WILD is a non-profit charitable organization, administers and implements all MYCAT programs in compliance with Malaysian laws.

Name: Vermont Center for Ecostudies
Description:
The Vermont Center for Ecostudies advances biodiversity conservation through research, monitoring, and community engagement. We envision a society that sustains healthy ecosystems through science-based decision making.

How We Work
At VCE, we keep a watchful eye on species and ecosystems to assess their status and investigate environmental threats. Working closely with our partners and with community scientists, we develop knowledge gained from field studies into tools and strategies to protect biodiversity.

We excel in recruiting, training, and coordinating volunteers to gather and share ecological information in a rigorous, cost-effective manner. In fact, our approach has received international recognition as a model for the collection, organization, and dissemination of biodiversity data. We also specialize in convening conservation professionals and land stewards to implement evidence-based practices that benefit vulnerable species and ecosystems throughout the Northeast, the Caribbean, and far beyond.

Communication for Conservation Impact
We regularly share our findings with other scientists, policymakers, and land managers. We publish results in peer-reviewed journals, guide public policy with topical reports and legislative testimony, and freely share data and knowledge through dozens of online platforms.

Even as we lead regional and international collaborations among scientists and natural resource professionals, we continuously engage general audiences as well. We post, blog, write, lecture, live, and breathe nature. We produce educational newsletters and videos, and we give interviews to local and national media outlets. We’ve even hosted a popular radio show to share our enthusiasm for understanding and conserving nature. After all, as our founding director, Chris Rimmer likes to point out, conservation is as much about people as it is about ecology. It’s one reason our motto is Uniting People and Science for Conservation.

Name: Northern Rivers Wildlife Hospital
Description:
The Northern Rivers Wildlife Hospital (NRWH) is now open and providing vital veterinary care for sick, injured, and orphaned native wildlife across the Northern Rivers region of NSW.

Our purpose-built, bricks-and-mortar facility near Wollongbar is a community-led initiative responding to the urgent need for local, specialised wildlife care. Every year, thousands of animals are rescued across our region and services are constantly under pressure to meet the demand.
We provide high-quality, compassionate care for a all native species – from birds, possums, and turtles to wallabies, koalas, snakes and more. Our veterinary team works closely with wildlife carers, rescuers, and environmental agencies to ensure the best outcomes for every animal.

NRWH is 100% community-owned and governed by a volunteer board. Our operations are powered by a small, passionate staff team, an incredible group of volunteers, and the generosity of donors, sponsors, and supporters who believe in protecting and preserving our native wildlife.

Key programs and activities include:

Wildlife rescue, triage, treatment, and rehabilitation
Community education and engagement
Emergency response capacity building 
Partnerships with local, state, and national conservation groups
A monthly donor program – the Hospital Heroes – supporting ongoing care

We believe that together, we can create a future where all wildlife has access to the care it needs to survive and thrive.

 Learn more at www.nrwh.com.au
 Follow us for patient updates, good news stories, and ways to get involved.

NRWH services the seven Local Government Areas of the Northern Rivers, covering an area of approximately 21,000 square kilometres and encompassing the population centres of Grafton, Ballina, Lismore, Casino, Kyogle, Byron Bay, Murwillumbah and Tweed Heads.

Name: Wildlife Madagascar
Description: Our expertise is in collaborating with communities to develop and support local initiatives based on their expressed needs. We bring together individuals, key groups, local organizations, nonprofit partners, and government agencies to create programs that lead to lasting change. We seek to work together to alleviate poverty and support environmental conservation in one of the world’s most threatened and irreplaceable environments.

Name: RASAP-CI
Description: Le programme RASAP-CI est mené par le Centre Suisse de Recherche Scientifiques en Côte d’Ivoire depuis 2004. Il est issu du constat que les programmes de conservation étaient uniquement développés à l'Ouest du pays. Ainsi, il cherche à préserver des sites à haute valeur de conservation à l'Est de la Côte d'Ivoire, où l’on retrouve encore notamment des espèces endémiques et menacées. Pour pérenniser les actions de protection qui sont mises en place, l’implication directe des populations locales dans la gestion de ces espaces naturels, est une volonté forte. Les scientifiques constituant l'équipe de RASAP-CI développent une approche multidisciplinaire. En effet, une recherche dense est réalisée dans des secteurs aussi diverses que la socio-anthropologie, la biologie végétale et animale et l'économie.

Name: Khmer Ocean Life
Description:
Khmer Ocean Life (KOL) is a non-profit organisation, registered in the UK, based in Cambodia and partnered with local NGO Samaky Organization, Kampot.

KOL's mission is to promote the conservation of Cambodia’s marine ecosystems and marine megafauna through encouraging and highlighting research, capacity building, outreach, and community collaboration.

Name: Pôle-relais lagunes méditerranéennes
Description:
Initiative nationale pour favoriser une meilleure reconnaissance des lagunes méditerranéennes (d'Occitanie, Provence-Alpes-Côte d’Azur et Corse) et encourager de meilleures pratiques de gestion.
Le Pôle-relais lagunes méditerranéennes est animé par la Tour du Valat, en relation étroite avec le Conservatoire d’espaces naturels Occitanie (CEN Occitanie) et l’Office de l’Environnement de la Corse (OEC).

Name: Projeto Costão Rochoso
Description: O Projeto Costão Rochoso tem como objetivos gerar dados da dinâmica, ecologia e biologia da biodiversidade dos ecossistemas marinhos de costões rochosos e suas inter-relações com a atividade humana. O projeto atua no monitoramento de peixes, tartarugas, corais e demais invertebrados fornecendo informações fundamentais para à gestão marinha. Outro objetivo norteador do projeto são as ações de educomunicação ambiental onde aproximamos a comunidade dos valores históricos e funcionais, no contexto das pesquisas científicas, das técnicas de manejo e da sensibilização sobre a conservação dos recursos marinhos.
Location: Arraial do Cabo, Rio de Janeiro

Name: The SEA People
Description: The SEA People (locally named ‘Orang Laut Papua’), is a field based non-profit foundation working to contribute to the protection of one of the last locations on Earth where coral reef systems still thrive. Taking a holistic approach to marine conservation, we work with local communities, who depend on healthy coral reefs for both food and livelihoods, and aim to provide mutually beneficial solutions to ecosystems based management. By combining science and technology with traditional skills and management techniques, we focus largely on capacity building; strengthening the ability of local community members to obtain the skills, knowledge and understanding required for improved management, protection and enhancement of local marine resources.

Name: BiodivEarth
Description:
The BiodivEarth association is a not-for-profit organization created and based in Switzerland. 
Its vision is a world where human societies and nature thrive, nurturing one another in a respectful and sustainable manner.
Its mission consists in accelerating the delivery of existing conservation efforts and initiatives towards   halting then reversing biodiversity loss, with a medium-term objective of contributing to the 2030 CBD targets.

Building Resilience in High Biodiversity Areas for Nature and People
Without resilience, all conservation efforts will be in vain. 
The first project of BiodivEarth aims at fostering the resilience of effective conservation models implemented in high biodiversity areas.  
The project has a global focus and applies to both marine and terrestrial areas. It will be delivered through the creation and facilitation of a Community of Practice gathering stewards of efficiently managed high biodiversity areas.
The project articulates around three axes: 
1.	Promoting understanding of the multidimensional nature of resilience and providing a practical index for measuring resilience locally and monitoring its variation globally.
2.	Developing and facilitating a Community of Practice made up of stewards of well-managed high biodiversity areas to boost their resilience through increased visibility, exchange of know-how, and scaling up of successful management models. 
3.	Retrofitting resilience into existing successful models and initiatives by diagnosing gaps of resilience and promoting the development of solutions.
This project is funded for the next three years by the Fondation Hoffmann.

Name: Ghabati Hayati
Description:
Le Maroc est l’un des pays méditerranéens les plus riches en termes de biodiversité. Ses bioclimats et sa géomorphologie sont à l’origine de paysages et de milieux naturels de grande qualité. La forêt marocaine, avec neuf millions d’hectares, constitue l’élément fondateur de la richesse écologique du pays. Elle abrite 70 % des espèces animales et végétales du pays.

De par cette diversité biologique, la forêt marocaine assure des fonctions sociales, économiques et environnementales importantes. Cependant, en dépit de ses rôles déterminants dans la protection des eaux et des sols et la lutte contre la désertification, la forêt marocaine reste soumise à de fortes pressions humaine et pastorale et à des agressions climatiques récurrentes.

Lancée en 2020, par Sa Majesté le Roi Mohammed VI, la stratégie « Forêts du Maroc 2020-2030 », constitue un tournant important dans la gestion forestière. Elle a comme double objectif de préserver la biodiversité et de développer l’économie durable du secteur, en privilégiant une approche inclusive et tournée vers la création d’emplois.

Le programme Ghabati Hayati, « Ma forêt, ma vie », financé par l’Agence française de développement (AFD), est le premier programme de coopération à appuyer la stratégie « Forêts du Maroc 2020-2030 ». D’une durée de quatre ans, il soutient la mise en œuvre de cette stratégie, notamment en matière de conservation de la biodiversité et d’atténuation des effets du climat.

Name: Écolieu Notre Dame de Pène
Description:
Un site unique en France dédié au développement durable et à la Solidarité. 
A la fois tiers lieu environnemental pédagogique et plateforme de l'économie circulaire l'ecolieu ND de Pène contribue à la formation des eco citoyens d'aujourd'hui et de demain.

Name: Tanzania Research and Conservation Organization
Description: Tanzania Research and Conservation Organization (TRCO) is a non-profit organization established in 2021 by Tanzanian experts from different disciplines, and collaborate with international experts for biodiversity research and conservation.

Name: Conservation Amplified
Description:
Every day, people are doing awesome things to help nature thrive. There are plenty of effective solutions to our biodiversity challenges that often don’t get noticed, and the biggest barriers to helping nature are awareness and funding.

To create lasting positive change for the environment, we need conservation to become mainstream. We need to bring the people helping nature into the spotlight. We need to make it easy for people from all walks of life to become more curious and excited about conservation.

It’s only through building these foundations that we can expect society to demand more for nature… And how empowering would it be to live in a world where positivity and optimism around the environment are the norm?

That's where we come in...

We bring a megaphone to the world of conservation by creating content that amplifies the stories of people helping nature. We aim to make it easy for everyone to learn, understand, and feel like they’re a part of the solution.

Life in today’s world is busy enough as it is, so we do our best to keep it simple. One day, we’d love to build projects that raise more funding for conservation initiatives - but for now, we’re focused entirely on raising awareness.

If this interests you, feel free to join our community!

Name: WABiLED
Description:
The West Africa Biodiversity and Low Emissions Development (WABiLED) Program is a four-year program funded by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) with 3 core objectives: combat wildlife trafficking and enhance great ape conservation; reduce deforestation, forest degradation, and biodiversity loss in key transboundary forest landscapes; and reduce greenhouse gas emissions and increasing carbon sequestration from land use.

To achieve these ambitious objectives, WABiLED is working with partners to strengthen the capacity of national and regional networks and institutions to enforce and prosecute wildlife trafficking laws across the region; implement regional and transboundary cooperation and biodiversity conservation strategies in the key forested countries of Guinea, Liberia, Cote d’Ivoire, and Sierra Leone; and improve capacity for economic planning and development of low emissions development strategies to reduce West Africa’s greenhouse gas emissions thus contributing to national and global climate commitments.

Name: NAMSAL Climate Concern International
Description:
Namsal Climate Concern International (NCCI) is a Community Based Organization that assists nature through tree planting, waste management, and carbon emission reduction. Our agency provides services and support through provision of tree seedlings to
 institutions and individuals, training the households on energy clean cook stoves to reduce fumes that go in the sky and to reduce firewood consumption, trainings on waste management, recycling of plastic wastes, training programs on tree planting in the community.  NCCI was formed in March 2022.

Name: CampWild UK
Description:
Inspiring & enabling access to nature & wilder camping adventures
Unlocking ‘Wild Spaces’
The Ultimate Guide to Wilder Campsites
Adventure Camping Community

Name: Caraga Carbon Development, Inc.
Description:
CCDI is a stock corporation organized under the laws of the Republic of the Philippines.  It is the company that will conduct  programmatic activities of Project Caraga in the partner CADTs such as reforestation, afforestation, forest protection, biodiversity conservation, sustainable agriculture, social preparation, capacity development, and benefit sharing implementation. 

CCDI will  implement value-adding projects through agri-business development to support agricultural production in the CADTs.  Nature-based Tourism will also be developed and promoted to support the eco-tourism initiatives in the CADTs.

Name: REDD Early Movers Mato Grosso Program
Description: It is a REDD Program (Reduction of Emissions from Deforestation and Degradation) created by the German Government, which in the case of Mato Grosso also has the support of the United Kingdom Government.

Name: Bats & Birds
Description:
Wij zijn Bats & Birds: dienstverlener en producent van slimme ecologische oplossingen.

Met bouwkundige kennis onder de arm en oog voor beschermde diersoorten zetten we ons in als ‘verbinder’ van de bouw en ecologie. Dat doen we door ecologisch advies om te zetten naar de praktijk.

Wij garanderen met hoogwaardige producten en diensten ecologische compensatie en mitigatie voor al uw (ver)bouwplannen. Zo voorkomen of beperken we negatieve gevolgen voor beschermde diersoorten, waaronder de vleermuis, gierzwaluw en huismus. Innovatieve oplossingen zoals nestkasten, paalkasten en amfibieënschermen worden door ons geproduceerd en geplaatst. Van standaard product tot maatwerk.

Heeft u een ecologische uitdaging of bent u op zoek naar een oplossing voor naleving van de Omgevingswet? Laat ons meedenken en ontdek wat we voor u kunnen doen. Neem contact op via 078 616 25 24 of stuur een e-mail naar info@batsbirds.nl.

Name: Terra-NatureWild Conservation
Description: Redefining the narrative of conservation through sciencetelling and storytelling

Name: Conservatoire Botanique de Nouvelle Calédonie
Description:
Le Conservatoire botanique de Nouvelle-Calédonie a pour but d'étudier et de préserver la flore et les habitats naturels de notre Hot-Spot de biodiversité.

Une mission de préfiguration a été mise en place en Nouvelle-Calédonie pour préciser le périmètre du Conservatoire botanique de Nouvelle Calédonie et pour animer des plans de conservation des espèces rares et menacées et de leurs habitats naturels.

En 2023 cette mission a été rattachée à l'Institut Agronomique Calédonien, en étroite collaboration avec les collectivités et les acteurs locaux, l'objectif est d'ajuster le dimensionnement de cette structure, de définir une feuille de route concertée et de sécuriser des financements pérennes pour son fonctionnement et ses actions.

Le futur Conservatoire Botanique de Nouvelle Calédonie animera notamment les plans de conservation des espèces rares et menacées, il pourra porter des projets pour restaurer les milieux naturels et agira de concert avec les partenaires pour définir des stratégies et tout projets contribuant à la connaissance et à la sauvegarde de la flore, de la fonge et des habitats naturels.

La mission de préfiguration est pilotée par un comité de suivi composé de représentants de l'Etat, l'Office Français de la Biodiversité, la province des Îles, la province Nord, la province Sud, l'Institut Agronomique Calédonien et de l'association Endémia.

Name: Duke Lemur Center
Description: Duke Lemur Center is a research company based out of 3705 Erwin Rd, Durham, North Carolina, United States.

Name: Canadian Prairies Prescribed Fire Exchange
Description: The Canadian Prairies Prescribed Fire Exchange is an inter-agency collective established to increase capacity for knowledge sharing and training surrounding the use of prescribed fire as a management tool in Canadian prairie and parkland ecosystems.

Name: Veterinary Initiative for Endangered Wildlife
Description:
VIEW (Veterinary Initiative for Endangered Wildlife) is a nonprofit conservation organization focused solely on addressing health threats as part of a comprehensive strategy for protecting endangered wildlife populations around the world. 

The approach of VIEW's expert team of veterinarians is straightforward: We support locally sustainable wildlife disease investigation, prevention, and treatment. We accomplish this strategy by providing training to the stakeholders, tools and technology to conduct research, as well as promoting policies that ensure healthy environments for wildlife and the people and domestic animals that share their habitat. 

VIEW seeks to make a global impact on wildlife health by strategically choosing the regions in which we work – considering the biodiversity and the number of critically endangered species in the region, as well as the potential to partner with government and local agencies to create a sustainable wildlife conservation program. We are currently active in three regions: North America, Africa, and Asia.

Name: Environmental Defenders
Description:
Environmental Defenders (ED) is an environmental conservation and human rights organization dedicated to protecting biodiversity and Indigenous Peoples’ rights. Our work extends across Uganda and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, focusing on critical landscapes that connect ecosystems and communities, in particular Murchison-Semliki, Lendu Plateau, Bili-Uélé, and Ituri-Epulu-Aru within the broader Congo Basin. 

We foster resilience for both environmental and human security. We assist marginalized Indigenous Peoples and traditional communities in the Albertine Rift and the Congo Basin regions to live sustainably and safeguard their cultural practices, water sources, lands, and the surrounding environment.

Name: COOPERATIVE D'ACTIONS CITOYENNES
Description: La Coopérative d'Actions Citoyennes (CAC) s'engage pour l'économie circulaire et la lutte contre la pollution plastique. Rejoignez un mouvement écoresponsable qui sensibilise, forme et agit pour un avenir durable et inclusif en lien avec les ODD 1, 6, 11 et 13 . Ensemble, construisons un monde sans déchets !

Name: Tounes CleanUp
Description:
TounesCleanUp est une ONG environnementale fondée légalement en juillet 2019 par un groupe de jeunes
citoyens avec comme objectif principal la protection de l'environnement en Tunisie. Ces principales activités
ont pour finalités de lutter contre les catastrophes environnementales en organisant des actions de
reboisement, de dépollution, tri sélectif, de former la jeunesse à travers ses actions dans différentes écoles du
territoire et d’accompagner les acteurs locaux comme les agriculteurs, les forestiers ou les communes afin
d'instaurer un mouvement solidaire conscient et dans le respect des principes de l’ESS.
Nos actions consistent en des opérations de sensibilisation de la population principalement et en des réunions
avec les autorités locales en vue d’une meilleure prise de conscience des effets du changement climatique et
de la pollution dans sa globalité.
Nos actions de tri et de dépollution sont en collaboration avec les différentes localités et avec le soutien de
partenaires locaux et étrangers.
Depuis maintenant plus de cinq ans nous œuvrons dans la lutte contre le réchauffement climatique à travers
nos actions de reboisement dans plusieurs régions en Tunisie.
Nos actions de reboisement à visent à restaurer ce qui a été détruit par l'incendie et protéger la biodiversité
locale dans cadre de l'initiative nationale « Pacte pour une Tunisie verte » qui soutient la campagne nationale
de reboisement forestier et pastoral, à laquelle nous participons activement depuis 2019.
Afin de financer les différentes actions de l’association, un travail important est réalisé auprès de bailleurs
internationaux et partenaires nationaux privés.

Name: GIP Réserve nationale marine de La Réunion
Description: Gestionnaire de l'aire marine protégée "réserve naturelle nationale marine de La Réunion" s'étendant du Cap la Houssaye au Rocher aux Oiseaux, dans l'Ouest de La Réunion

Name: Natiora Defenders
Description: Founded in 2024, Natiora Defenders is the first organization championing and showcasing conservation leaders and heroes in Madagascar. We aims to recognize and bring to light individuals and organizations dedicated to safeguarding Madagascar's natural heritage. Our organization strives to amplify the voices and actions of these conservation champions, ensuring their efforts gain recognition and support beyond Madagascar's borders and, to foster a collaborative environment by facilitating gatherings where conservation leaders can connect, share experiences, and learn from one another.

Name: Irish Seed Savers Association
Description:
Irish Seed Savers is a nonprofit charity (CHY 13989) supporting the conservation of Ireland’s unique environmental heritage, preserving and distributing heirloom vegetable seeds, potatoes, grains and Irish heritage apple trees. We provide information and educate the public on agricultural biodiversity locally and globally. We want to create an awareness of Ireland’s very special heritage varieties of apple trees and heirloom vegetable seeds.

Due to modern farming practices it is estimated that over 80% of heritage fruit & vegetable varieties have been lost. People are becoming more aware of this, and the importance of conserving our unique heritage; be it cultural or environmental.  Here at Irish Seed Savers, our aim is to make the varieties that have been saved from extinction available to the public. These varieties are grown organically on site, are open pollinated, free from chemicals, suited to our Irish climate and as natural as nature intended. As Ireland’s only public Seed Bank we have a duty to protect and preserve them for future generations.

Our 20 acres of gardens, orchards and woodlands are open to the public, where we provide a space for community groups to come together and learn about Ireland’s diverse ecological heritage. We also provide a number of education and outreach programs to schools, giving talks, demonstrations and workshops helping them to attain a Green Flag award for their environmental education program.

Name: A2D - Action Pour le Développement Durable
Description:
A2D est une association crée dans le but de préserver et de protéger l'environnement, en luttant activement et de manière durable contre l'insalubrité en milieu urbain afin de donner un visage nouveau aux villes Camerounaises et Africaines.
l'insalubrité allant grandissante dans nos principales villes, nos actions auront pour but de sensibiliser les populations d'abord, ensuite contribuer avec le concours des collectivités locales aux travaux et projets visant à protéger l'environnement

Name: The Flow Country Partnership
Description:
The Flow Country Partnership (Scottish Charitable Incorporated Organisation, No. SC053100) unites crofters, farmers, landowners, local businesses, ecologists, and government bodies to strengthen the resilience of the Flow Country and its people. 
Located in the far north of Scotland across Caithness and Sutherland, this vast blanket bog system not only boasts incredible biodiversity, including rare flora and fauna, but also plays a crucial role in climate change mitigation.

In July 2024, The Flow Country achieved the prestigious status of the world’s first peatland World Heritage Site. From its expansive landscapes, characterised by a rich tapestry of peat and pools, to its role as a natural carbon sink, this unique environment serves as both an ecological treasure and a source of global inspiration.

Name: ciar-damar
Description: Rejoignez-nous à la CIAR 2024 pour restaurer nos forêts et bâtir un avenir durable ensemble.

Name: PANORAMA - Solutions for a Healthy Planet
Description:
PANORAMA Solutions is multi-organisation partnership initiative that shines spotlight on Solutions that work— transformative, proven and replicable approaches to conservation and sustainable development. We connect the dots between local innovation and global policy, helping practitioners share experiences, learn from others and adapt and scale success.  

PANORAMA documents inspiring real-world stories from around the world— highlighting practical ways to protect biodiversity, restore ecosystems and strengthen climate resilience. The PANORAMA web platform offers a valuable resource in the conservation sector, supporting practitioners in avoiding reinvention, replicating effective practices and implementing the Global Biodiversity Framework (GBF).  

Recognised by the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD), PANORAMA bridges the gap between grassroots action and international policy. With a flexible methodology and a strong commitment to inclusivity, PANORAMA engages diverse stakeholders across geographies and sectors.  

We celebrate innovation and impact and offer global visibility to those driving change on the ground.  

Our goal? To be the ‘go-to resource’ for Solution driven action in biodiversity conservation and sustainable development and to empower a growing network of Solution Providers making a difference for people and the planet.  

Explore. Learn. Replicate. Be part of the Solution.

Name: Erdheilungsplätze
Description:
Als anerkannte gemeinnützige Stiftung nehmen wir Land aus der Ökonomie und geben es der Natur zurück. Der ökonomische Wert des Bodens wird gesteigert, wenn die Natur zerstört wird. Der ökologische Wert ist dann minimal. Diesen Prozess kehren wir um, indem wir Erdheilungsplätze schaffen und diese ökologisch aufwerten. 

Du kannst uns bei unserer Mission unterstützen und Erdhelfer werden. Teile unser Engagement in deinem Umkreis und unterstütze ausgewählte Projekte mit steuerlich anerkannten Spenden. Wenn du möchtest, kannst du auch eine Patenschaft für einen Erdheilungsplatz übernehmen und dich gemeinsam mit uns um die weitere Entwicklung kümmern.

Die Natur wartet nicht, setze dich jetzt für sie ein! 

YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCKymWoGHPpWFDpucvykb5CQ
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/erdheilungsplaetze 
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/erdheilungsplaetze/

Name: Local Ocean Conservation
Description:
Who are we?

We are a private, not for profit organisation committed to the protection of Kenya’s marine environment. Our dream is to encourage people to look after their very own ‘Local Ocean’ in order to create a chain of properly managed and conserved areas of ocean all over the world. Imagine if everyone looked after his or her own little patch of ocean!

What do we do?

We work hard to ensure the future of sea turtles, the protection of a fragile marine environment and the promotion of sustainable livelihoods in the Watamu area. We campaign to both our local community and those further afield to love their “Local Ocean”.

How do we do it?

We run several programmes to help us achieve our goals:

>Nest Monitoring and Protection – We monitor and protect nesting female sea turtles, their nests and their hatchlings.
>Bycatch Release Programme – We work closely with local fishermen who accidently catch turtles in their nets. They call us so we can tag and release these turtles saving them from drowning or slaughter.
>Turtle Rehabilitation Centre – Sometimes turtles that come to us via our Bycatch Release Programme are not fit enough to be released straight away. We bring them back to our specialist turtle rehabilitation centre, for medication and care until such time as they are strong enough to survive in the wild.
>Community Outreach – We are always looking for ways to help our local community, especially when it comes to alternative income generating projects that take the pressure off already over exploited marine resources and dependency on tourism.
>Conservation Education & Awareness – We work with community groups and local schools to raise awareness regarding conservation issues.
>Data Collection and Research – We have several research programmes designed to monitor the sea turtle population and the environment they rely upon.

NB: Our organisation was founded as "Watamu Turtle Watch" and previously known as "Local Ocean Trust".

Name: Association Pays de l'Ours - Adet
Description:
Pays de l’ours Adet est l’Association pyrénéenne créée en 1991 pour initier et promouvoir le retour de l’ours dans les Pyrénées centrales. 
Ses missions sont la préservation et la valorisation du patrimoine naturel et culturel du territoire Pays de l’Ours, ainsi que l’éducation de tous à l’environnement. 
Pays de l'Ours - Adet milite pour la restauration d'une population d'ours viable dans les Pyrénées, informe et sensibilise le public sur l'ours et la biodiversité pyrénéenne, assure des animations, formations, conférences et des sorties sur le terrain pour tous publics.
Depuis 2020, l'association dispose également d'un Centre documentaire centralisant et rendant accessible gratuitement toute l'information et la documentation sur l'ours et son retour dans les Pyrénées.
www.paysdelours.com

Name: Timbo Afrika Foundation
Description: Timbo Afrika is the proud owner of the Koro River Camp & Koro Island Camp in Botswana in the Central Tuli. ALL revenues generated through these luxury safari camps is used for nature conservation project and community tourism development.

Name: Africa Mobility Group (AMG)
Description:
Africa Mobility Group (AMG) is a leading force in electric mobility and renewable energy solutions across Africa. We are transforming transportation by building a network of electric vehicle charging infrastructure while integrating innovative technologies to support a sustainable and environmentally friendly future.

With strong expertise in deploying AC/DC charging stations, AMG provides solutions that comply with international standards. Our interconnected network relies on intelligent platforms that optimize infrastructure management, ensuring a seamless and efficient user experience. By leveraging artificial intelligence and data analytics, we anticipate drivers’ needs while minimizing environmental impact.

Our approach also emphasizes interoperability. AMG connects key sectors such as hotel management systems, parking facilities, and car rental services while establishing bridges with European operators to ensure continuous and harmonized charging across borders. We integrate photovoltaic systems into our solutions, enabling real-time traceability of energy sources and reinforcing our commitment to green mobility.

At AMG, we believe in a future where mobility and energy converge to create sustainable and accessible solutions. Our vision is to connect individuals, businesses, and infrastructure in a clean, smart, and inclusive ecosystem. With strategic partnerships and ongoing innovation, AMG positions itself as a key player in Africa's energy transition.

Join us in this mission and discover how we are shaping the future of mobility with sustainable energy. Every charge is an opportunity to move the world toward a greener tomorrow.

#eMobility #GreenEnergy #Sustainability #ElectricVehicleCharging #Innovation #CleanFuture #SmartMobility #RenewableEnergy

Name: LIGHT FOR NATURE [LINAT]
Description: Light for Nature (LINAT) is a community-led organization made up of youths and other team of experts with sustainable development perspective , who focused on blue carbon conservation through the restoration of degraded mangroves ecosystem and behavior change in indigenous communities .

Name: Blue Ridge Bartram Trail Conservancy
Description:
The Blue Ridge Bartram Trail Conservancy, formerly known as the North Carolina Bartram Trail Society was organized on November 1, 1977. The Conservancy is supported solely by memberships and occasional grants. Donations are tax-deductible and most welcome.

Our Mission:
- To recognize and honor the Philadelphia-born naturalist, William Bartram, who traveled through the southeast between the years 1773 and 1777, writing exact and vivid descriptions of the plants and animals he saw and the native tribes he encountered, and who published these writings in 1791 as Travels of William Bartram.
- To establish and maintain a 110 mile memorial trail, following a route as close as possible to Bartram’s original route through northeast Georgia and western North Carolina.
- To promote further inquiry and knowledge about the plants and animals of the southern Appalachians, as well as the traditions and culture of the native Cherokee people, which Bartram encountered on his travels here in 1775.

Our History
The Blue Ridge Bartram Trail Conservancy, formerly known as the North Carolina Bartram Trail Society was organized on November 1, 1977, by eleven North Carolina residents. It was the culmination of several years of dreaming, research, and planning. Among the leaders of this group were the late Walter G. McKelvey, Mr. Charles Moore, and Mr. Rowell Bosse, all of Brevard. Dr. J. Dan Pittillo, of Cullowhee, professor emeritus of botany from Western Carolina University, was also instrumental in the formative years of the Conservancy and continues to serve on the Board of Directors today.

Today, the Conservancy has grown to more than 200 members, who come primarily from the Southeastern states but also from across the country and around the world, from British Columbia to New Zealand. It is governed by a 501c3 non-profit Board of Directors that meets quarterly to direct the growth of the Conservancy.

Name: GREENTUR Sustainable tourism
Description:
ES: Transformando San Andrés. 
Somos una iniciativa apoyada por el Ministerio de Asuntos Exteriores de Dinamarca (Danida) y su programa Danida Asociación Empresarial Verde. En Greentur trabajamos contra la contaminación por plásticos en este hermoso paraíso colombiano. Nuestra iniciativa de vasos retornables no solo protege nuestros arrecifes de coral y nuestras playas de ensueño, sino que también inspira a la comunidad a adoptar hábitos más sostenibles. Únete a nosotros y sé parte del cambio que el planeta necesita!

EN: Transforming San Andrés.
We are an initiative supported by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark (Danida) and its Danida Green Business Partnership programme. At Greentur, we work to combat plastic pollution in this beautiful Colombian paradise. Our returnable cup initiative not only protects our breathtaking beaches and coral reefs but also inspires the community to adopt more sustainable habits. Join us and be part of the change our planet needs!

Name: Scenic Rivers Land Trust
Description: A nationally accredited land trust, Scenic Rivers is a private nonprofit dedicated to protecting the special forests, wetlands, farmlands, and other natural areas that sustain Anne Arundel County, Maryland. To date, Scenic Rivers holds 80 conservation easements across the county, totaling over 3,800 acres.To learn about Scenic Rivers’ conservation and stewardship programs, visitwww.SRLT.org.

Name: Groupe Utalii Kwetu
Description:
Le Groupe Utalii Kwetu ‘GUK’ est une initiative congolaise du secteur privé (ONG, individus et entreprises) évoluant dans le domaine du tourisme et de la conservation de la nature en République Démocratique du Congo. Né du proverbe africain : « Si tu veux aller vite, vas-y seul. Mais si tu veux aller loin, allez avec va avec les autres », ces personnes se sont mise ensemble pour combiner leurs ressources, leur expérience, leur savoir-faire et leurs efforts dans le but ultime d’amener la RDC d’abord, et les pays du Bassin du Congo ensuite, à contribuer efficacement au maintien de l’équilibre mondiale des écosystèmes et de faire de l’écotourisme leur pilier de développement durable et inclusif.

Nous le faisons à travers nos trois domaines d’intervention qui : l’éducation, l’environnement et la promotion de l’éco-tourisme.

Name: Ogallala Land & Water Conservancy
Description:
The Ogallala Land & Water Conservancy (OLWC) was created specifically as a nonprofit organization in the State of New Mexico for the primary purpose of conserving the groundwater resources of the Ogallala Aquifer, which experiences only limited recharge, through the use of Water Right Lease Agreements and groundwater conservation easements, while transitioning irrigated farmland and ranchland to dryland cropping or grazing land. This is a looming issue for every farmer, rancher, and community dependent on the Ogallala Aquifer from Texas to North Dakota. 

The Ogallala Land & Water Conservancy’s primary goal is to ensure the long-term installation resiliency of Cannon Air Force Base (CAFB). Groundwater from the Ogallala Aquifer is essential to Cannon’s continual operation. The Department of Defense (DoD) has determined that the Cannon AFB REPI project is a priority.  The preservation of the Ogallala Aquifer for City and County residents is also a priority of the Central Curry Soil & Water Conservation District (CCSWCD), the Clovis City Commission, and the Curry County Board of County Commissioners.

The OLWC functions as the Eligible Entity (EE) for implementation of the DOD's Readiness and Environmental Protection Integration (REPI) Program project at Cannon Air Force Base, the Natural Resources Conservation Service (NRCS) Regional Conservation Partnership Program (RCPP) award, and the New Mexico Office of the State Engineer grant award. The combined REPI ($7,545,200), RCPP ($6,911,311), and OSE ($2,400,000) funding awards provide $16,856,511 to pay landowners, in a contiguous wellfield residing in a paleochannel that runs northwest and west of Cannon AFB, to discontinue irrigation and develop sustainable land and water management practices in Curry County, thereby preserving vital agricultural land and groundwater resources. Federal, state, and local funding make it possible to execute the Cannon AFB REPI Project.

Name: CPRW: The Welsh Countryside Charity
Description: Since 1928, CPRW has been standing up for the people and places of rural Wales and protecting our unique landscapes. From defending wild spaces from destructive development to creating sustainable rural communities, we are passionate about creating a countryside which works for everyone.

Name: Nature Mates
Description: At Nature Mates, we are dedicated towards the welfare and conservation of Wildlife. Our mission is to develop an ecosystem wherein wildlife and human coexist. Irrespective of the vulnerabilities we are working on landscape as well as taxa based conservation approach, joining hands with local people. Our efforts are focused on education, species and habitat conservation, human-animal conflict mitigation, gathering biodiversity knowledge and reaching out to citizen (through our citizen science programs), nature education and community outreach, healthcare, alternative livelihood and disaster relief to forest fringe villagers and animal rescue.

Name: Informa Petróleo
Description: O Informa Petróleo integra o Programa Macrorregional de Comunicação Social (PMCS), medida exigida pelo Licenciamento Ambiental Federal, conduzido pelo IBAMA.

Name: EtnoSur Lab
Description: Contamos con más de 10 años de expertise profesional en “know that” y “know how” en la ejecución de planes integrales de desarrollo territorial sostenible. Creamos impacto social mediante estrategias flexibles y adaptativas.
Location: Ciudad Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Autonomous City of Buenos Aires

Name: Young Conservationist Club (YCC)
Description: Since our founding in 2017, we have been determined to make an impact on the students of Universiti Malaysia Terengganu. The core of our efforts is to bring our team’s fresh ideas and passion to the range of activities we’re involved in. We are aiming to nurture leadership skills in all our members through serious conservation movements. Through all of our endeavors, we hope to display the conviction behind our beliefs.

Name: Flora Fauna y Cultura de Mexico
Description:
Asociación civil sin fines de lucro que trabaja por la conservación de especies y ecosistemas clave, así como en el desarrollo y bienestar comunitario de Quintana Roo.

Generamos alianzas con instituciones públicas, empresas y sociedad civil para sumar esfuerzos y lograr resultados que contribuyan a los Objetivos de Desarrollo Sostenible de la Agenda 2030 de Naciones Unidas.

Name: JEFFERSON CONSERVATION DISTRICT
Description:
Established in 1942, Jefferson Conservation District, a special district
and political subdivision of the State of Colorado, provides natural resources technical assistance to private landowners in Jefferson County and surrounding areas. Our mission is to provide leadership in a partnership effort to help people conserve, maintain, and improve our natural resources and environment. Current programs focus on forestry and noxious weed management. JCD is overseen by a volunteer Board of Supervisors.

Name: Seafar
Description:
Almost all maritime crimes are committed by vessels that have purposefully disconnected from standard vessel tracking systems. These "dark" vessels conduct the bulk of illegal fishing, a serious threat to food security, the livelihoods of coastal communities, marine life, and biodiversity.

Seafar has developed an innovative and cost-effective solution to the problem. Using machine learning and coastal radars, we track dark vessels in near real-time within nation's Exclusive Economic Zones. This enables data-driven, insight-based preventative measures to be implemented, such as targeted maritime patrols.

Name: Bee Green Forest Foundation
Description:
Bienvenidos al perfil oficial de nuestra organización, donde la naturaleza y las comunidades son el corazón de nuestra misión. 

¿Quiénes Somos?

Somos una fundación sin ánimo de lucro comprometida con liderar y ejecutar proyectos innovadores en el ámbito de la conservación y restauración de ecosistemas estratégicos. Nuestro enfoque se centra en abordar los desafíos ambientales más urgentes, especialmente aquellos relacionados con los impactos del cambio climático.

Nuestra Misión

Nuestra labor se orienta hacia la implementación de soluciones basadas en la naturaleza para mitigar los efectos adversos del cambio climático. Creemos firmemente en el poder de la investigación científica, la educación ambiental y el desarrollo de planes de manejo comunitario como herramientas clave para alcanzar nuestros objetivos.

Enfoque Comunitario y Territorial

En el núcleo de nuestra estrategia está el reconocimiento de las comunidades locales como actores cruciales. Trabajamos mano a mano con ellas para asegurar que nuestras intervenciones sean sostenibles y eficaces a largo plazo. Nuestro enfoque territorial nos permite comprender y atender las necesidades específicas de cada región, fomentando así un impacto positivo y duradero en el medio ambiente y en la calidad de vida de las personas.

Conéctate y Participa
Te invitamos a seguirnos y a unirte a nuestra causa. A través de este espacio, compartiremos nuestras últimas noticias, proyectos, y oportunidades para que te involucres en la protección de nuestro planeta. Juntos, podemos hacer una diferencia significativa para el futuro de nuestros ecosistemas y las generaciones venideras.

#Conservacionambiental #RestauraciónEcológica #Colombia #CambioClimático #SolucionesBasadasEnLaNaturaleza #ComunidadYMedioAmbiente #Boyacá #Cambioclimatico #Biodiversidad #Páramos

Name: Tree Line
Description:
Comprometidos con el planeta, comprometidos contigo.

En Tree Line, creemos que cada persona tiene el poder de hacer una diferencia. Al adoptar un árbol, no solo contribuyes a la sostenibilidad del planeta, sino que también reduces tu huella de carbono y generas un impacto positivo para las generaciones futuras. Adoptar un árbol es un gesto simple pero poderoso, y con Tree Line, te damos la oportunidad de ser parte de la solución al cambio climático. Elige tu árbol, apoya al planeta y nosotros nos encargamos del resto.

Name: GECONABI_RD Congo
Description:
GECONABI (Geomatics for the Conservation of Nature
and Biodiversity) is a Congolese nonprofit organization
based in Bukavu, Democratic Republic of Congo. Our
mission is to protect the environment, preserve
biodiversity, and promote the sustainable management of
natural resources.
A group of young graduates founded the organization to
apply the skills they acquired during their studies and
contribute to the development of their community.
Kwakanaba Mweze Adolphe, an environmental and land
resource management engineer who specializes in
geographic information systems, initiated the project. 

Our ambition is to build a Congolese society that
combines nature and technology to promote sustainable
development while respecting the environment and
biodiversity. To achieve this, we must transform current
growth models by integrating technological innovations
that promote the sustainable management of natural
resources.
Specifically, we aim to promote green technologies that
align with local realities, such as climate-smart agriculture,
renewable energies (solar and hydraulic), remote sensing
systems for monitoring ecosystems, and digital tools for
participatory and inclusive environmental governance.
Our vision also aims to reduce poverty, improve the well-
being of local populations — particularly in Bukavu — and
provide job opportunities for ambitious young people. This
will enable them to contribute to sustainable development
through meaningful and innovative projects.
Realizing this vision requires close collaboration among
local communities, researchers, public authorities, and
the private sector to design eco-responsible solutions that
limit pressure on forests, soils, and biodiversity while
stimulating the local economy.
Although is still in an informal framework, it is based on a
clear vision: to use geomatics tools to support nature
conservation, biodiversity preservation, and the promotion
of local knowledge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

Name: Sendenyu Community Biodiversity and Wildlife Conservation Reserve
Description: Sendenyu Community Biodiversity And Wildlife Reserve is a community conserved area in Sendenyu village under Tseminyu District, Nagaland. The conservation efforts started in 2001 and have been a pioneer of community conservation in Nagaland. The conservation area is 2200 hectares and has rich flora and fauna biodiversity life. It is identified in the IUCN Red List species list. The conservation area is owned and managed by the community through a committee. It has membership in Nagaland Community Conserved Area Forum and in the global network of Indigenous Community Conserved Area Consortium.

Name: Blue Restorers
Description: ONG unida para la conservación marina, trabajamos con todos.

Name: TEAMEnvironment KENYA
Description:
We are Your Partner in Environmental Sustainability!

We provide tailored solutions to drive environmental conservation, climate action, and sustainable land-use management. 

Our services include:
 Tree Growing Services: Comprehensive support for successful tree planting projects, from site and species matching to ongoing care.

 Climate Change & Environmental Consultancy: Expert guidance to address climate change challenges and promote sustainable practices.

 Quality Seedlings Supply: High-quality, assorted tree seedlings from our Ten Million Tree Seedlings Propagation Center, ensuring reliable results for forestry and conservation efforts.

 Landscaping Services: From garden design to irrigation systems, we create thriving outdoor spaces.

 Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA): Responsible evaluations of potential development impacts for sustainable outcomes.

 Green Facility Initiative: Partnering in Public-Private Partnerships to enhance physical infrastructure, water catchment management, and sustainable resource use.

At TEAMEnvironment Kenya, we transform visions into actionable solutions for a greener future. Let's grow together!

Name: Centre d'Excellence du Droit de l'Environnement - CEDE
CONNECT – Outdoor Sports & Protected Areas
Description:
CONNECT bridges the gap between outdoor sports and protected areas across Europe. Through research, pilot actions and guidelines, we build understanding, share best practices, and create sustainable partnerships.

Join our Connect Community: https://www.linkedin.com/groups/16008110/

The CONNECT Project is co-funded by the European Union and develops guidance and examples of good practice for effective collaboration between outdoor sports and protected areas.
Our objective: foster mutual understanding, promote partnerships, and create practical solutions that balance nature protection with outdoor activities.
Backed by a European consortium (ENOS, Surfrider Foundation Europe, EUROPARC Federation, MISTRA, Leave No Trace Ireland, IMBA Europe, European Ramblers Association,…), CONNECT combines research, pilot workshops, and practical tools to strengthen cooperation between sport and environmental stakeholders.

#CONNECTproject #OutdoorSports #SustainableOutdoors #NatureandSport #ProtectedAreas

Name: Picchio Wildlife Research Center
Description:
Pairing passionately crafted nature tours with the conservation of local species such as the Asiatic black bear, our organisation aims to put nature in the spotlight and promote peaceful coexistence among people and wildlife in Japan.

Through our guided nature tours in both Karuizawa and Shiretoko (Hokkaido), two areas of abundant wildlife and natural beauty, we hope to immerse people in the nature of Japan and show them the value of preserving it. 

Our tours also help to fund our conservation activities in Karuizawa. Since 1998, we have been working closely with the local town office to maintain a level of coexistence between people and bears. Our activities have involved innovative management techniques, such as collaring and monitoring individual bears and the use of bear dogs to track and deter bears, as well as community projects such as habitat restoration and resident education. 

We are involved in ongoing research relating to Asiatic black bear ecology and population dynamics on a national scale. Additionally, we are collecting our own behavioural observations and movement data.

Name: Friends of Rookery Bay
Description:
Friends of Rookery Bay’s ongoing mission is to help connect people with southwest Florida’s dynamic coastal environment in support of Rookery Bay National Estuarine Research Reserve.

The Friends provide vital monetary and in-kind support for Reserve programs and operations. Our members, volunteers and donors enable us to be a reliable partner to the Reserve and an advocate for preservation and coastal protection.

Name: GO-4-NATURE
Description: "MIT UNS WACHSEN NICHT NUR DIE BÄUME...SONDERN AUCH DIE MENSCHEN ZUSAMMEN."

Name: Emicap
Description:
Reducing your emission sounds like a distant dream? Not anymore.
Capturing your CO₂ starts here, today. Emicap’s technology captures carbon dioxide directly at the source and converts it into a liquid, ready for transport, storage or further processing. Our solution is specifically designed for industrial facilities with flue gas containing  8 to 15 Vol% CO₂.

Name: Ol Gaboli
Description:
Ol Gaboli is a community-owned safari lodge in Laikipia, Kenya — the only one of its kind run by a women’s group. Nestled by the Ewaso Nyiro River, we offer meaningful travel experiences that connect guests to wildlife, culture, and conservation. Every stay directly supports local livelihoods and helps protect one of Kenya’s most unique wild landscapes.

The name Ol Gaboli means “the place of the fig tree” in Maasai—evoking a place of shelter, rootedness, and quiet significance. Fig trees have long marked important gathering places in Maasai tradition, and we carry that spirit into everything we offer: warm hospitality, thoughtful design, and a deep respect for the land.

Our bandas—simple yet elegantly furnished—offer barefoot luxury and total immersion in nature. Whether you’re walking in the bush, relaxing beside the river, or learning about local conservation efforts, Ol Gaboli invites you to slow down, reconnect, and experience Kenya in its truest form.

Name: Science Conservation Centre for Art (SCICCA), Chulalongkorn University, Thailand
Description:
SCICCA is committed to advancing knowledge in art conservation. More than just a facility equipped with cutting-edge scientific tools and offering analytical services for artworks and cultural artifacts, SCICCA acts as a hub for interdisciplinary collaboration, training, and knowledge sharing. In addition to our conservation and analytical services, our mission encompasses offering educational opportunities, including workshops, seminars, and specialized training, for professionals in the art and conservation fields. By establishing robust networks and encouraging cross-sector cooperation, we strive to elevate conservation practices in Thailand to align with international standards.

Central to our efforts is a profound respect and affection for cultural heritage. We consistently recognize that conservation extends beyond technical aspects—it is a duty to safeguard the legacy of previous generations and ensure its preservation for future ones. We believe this knowledge should not be confined to scientists, conservators, or academics alone. It should be disseminated to the broader society to cultivate awareness, understanding, and, most importantly, appreciation for the cultural treasures that shape our nation's identity.

Name: Casa Gaia Perú
Description:
Circular Economy &  Sustaintable Marketing
| #Quechua essence
| Natural warehouse
| Eco certificate
| Zero waste 
|+51 993 354 302

∞ Andean bee conservation company and its natural derivatives: raw honey produced between 2,900 and 4,000 meters above sea level, pollen, propolis, royal jelly, among others. We also design sustainable marketing and strategic communication strategies. ∞

∞ We offer products and services sourced from nature through the circular economy — preserving natural and cultural heritage for a healthy, regenerative life, while caring for Mother Earth and ourselves. ∞


---

∞ *Empresa de conservación de abejas andinas y derivados:* miel cruda producida entre los 2,900 y 4,000 m s.n.m., polen, propóleo, jalea real, entre otros. Además, desarrollamos estrategias de marketing sostenible y comunicación estratégica. ∞  
   
∞ *Ofrecemos productos y servicios provenientes de la naturaleza, a través de la economía circular, conservando el patrimonio natural y cultural, para una vida saludable y regenerativa, cuidando de la Madre Tierra y de nosotros mismos.* ∞

Name: PEP RECYCLING
Description: PEP RECYCLING, est une start-up qui œuvre dans le recyclage de déchets du types électriques et électroniques. Ces déchets regroupent l’ensemble des appareils électriques endommagés qui sont la plupart du temps stockés dans les locaux. Notre objectif étant de réduire de manière considérable l’impact des déchets électriques sur l’environnement et aussi, de promouvoir l’employabilité au Burkina Faso. Dans le cadre de notre activité, nous nous occupons de récolter, de trier et de recycler ces déchets précités.A cet effet, nous sollicitons votre accompagnement en nous permettant de rentrer en possession si possible, de vos déchets électriques ou appareils électriques endommagés que vous comptez vous débarrasser.

Name: Green Digital Guardians
Description:
Our purpose is to create the community of GREEN DIGITAL GUARDIANS OF THE GREEN ARKS OF PLANET EARTH, where new technologies such as Blockchain and NTFs will allow laying the foundations for a decentralized model of environmental protection, supportive, visionary, and democratic.

Our greatest strength and delight will be the millions of DGGUARDIANS who will be able to monitor and protect the GREEN ARKS.

Blockchain and NFT technology will allow in the future that all members of the community DGGUARDIANS may participate in the decisions and deliberations that involve the GREEN ARK.

Name: La Terre du Futur
Description: La Terre du Futur développe un projet associatif dont l’objet est de promouvoir un aménagement du territoire adapté au changement climatique par un développement de la végétation permanente pour préserver le cycle de l’eau entre l’atmosphère et les sols, et notamment par les espaces forestiers.
