Le projet est articulé autour de plusieurs thématiques, comme l’analyse et la vulgarisation des études scientifiques, la réflexion sur l’aménagement du territoire et la gestion de la forêt, la communication sur les travaux effectués et des projets de reforestation.

Name: DéDu - 德笃
Description: Promotion de la connaissance et de la coopération France-Chine autour de l'aquaculture durable et biodiversité marine

Name: Spartanburg Area Conservancy (SPACE)
Description: The Spartanburg Area Conservancy (SPACE) is a private, not-for-profit land conservation organization whose purpose is to protect and preserve natural areas of ecological, historical, and aesthetic value.   Several of our properties are available for public use and enjoyment, including the Cottonwood Trail, Glendale Shoals and the Chinquapin Trail.

Name: Association RESPECTS Occitanie
Description:
Le dérèglement climatique ne nous empêchera pas de jardiner !

RESPECTS Occitanie est une « jeune » association. Née en 2020, elle reprend les « intentions » de sa grande sœur, active dans les Hautes Alpes depuis 2015, et déclare qu' « Il y a le feu à la maison Terre ! » mais que « Nous pouvons agir ! »

Ces intentions nous engagent car nous sommes convaincus que ce sont nos actions individuelles qui parviendront à modifier notre environnement, social et écologique, pour le bien de tous.

L’acronyme qui identifie l’association est explicite : nous souhaitons porter des actions constituant un Réseau d’Economie Sociale afin de Partager et d'Echanger des Connaissances en Solidarité avec la Terre.

Après trois ans, ce sont pas moins de 140 familles adhérentes qui bénéficient d'actions concrètes s’inscrivant dans les perspectives qu'offrent ces intentions :
- des groupements d’achats en circuits courts et équitables ;
- des campagnes de solidarité ;
- des ateliers « Biodiversité » mensuels, la création d’un jardin participatif à vocation pédagogique, l’organisation annuelle d’une vente solidaire de plants, des rencontres.

Notre activisme local nous a permis d’intégrer le Relais Solidaire de la ville, géré par le CCAS, où nous partageons nos actions avec les publics de trois autres associations.

Un projet qui se veut durable...

Nous souhaitons pérenniser et développer le Jardin participatif des Violettes, pour en faire un lieu de sensibilisation, de formation, d’apprentissage et d’expérimentation.

Sur la base de nos réalisations, nous voulons mettre en place une « université » de la biodiversité, ouverte à tous, pour faire le lien entre les actes de la vie quotidienne et les transformations globales des modes de production et de consommation. Elle se veut éducative, sociale et écologique !

Nos piliers : le jardin, une équipe de bénévoles impliqués et notre grande motivation.

Rejoignez-nous !!!

Name: Damn Green
Description:
Wij zijn Dennis & Keera en delen graag onze weg naar een duurzaam leven met jullie. Deze weg bewandelen we met kleine stappen, onze ‘babysteps’ om bij te dragen aan het redden van deze aardkloot. Want wees eerlijk, dat is ze waard!

Graag nemen we jullie mee op onze weg vol met fouten, valkuilen, en vooral ook successen via blogs en vlogs! Want serieus, dit hele 100% duurzame leven doe je echt niet ‘zomaar even’. Elke dag is een leerproces. Er zijn meerdere momenten geweest dat we er van overtuigd waren dat we het goed deden, maar er later achter kwamen dat we het alleen maar erger maakten. Oei, een boel eyeopeners! Het gaat echter om de wil om het goed te doen en de zoektocht naar wat dan ‘goed’ is!  Dit alles brengen we zonder oordeel, zonder propaganda. Puur bewustwording, leren van elkaar. Omdat elke stap, hoe klein ook, een bijdrage kan leveren aan een betere wereld.

Enne, er zijn zoveel kleine dingen die je kan doen die níet moeilijk zijn. Dus geef vooral op voorhand de moed niet op, lees onze verhalen en gebruik wat voor jou toepasbaar is!

Remember, there’s no Planet B!!

Name: Ocean World of Sound
Description:
Ocean World of Sound (WOS) is the first Passive Acoustic Monitoring (PAM) program in the Mesoamerican Reef (MAR). We analyze ocean soundscapes to gain a deeper understanding of the marine ecosystem and its relationship with us. Based in Cancun, we operate throughout the Mexican Caribbean, an area heavily reliant on tourism. This region has experienced exponential growth over the past 40 years, placing significant pressure on its natural resources. 

Our initiative serves as a valuable tool for policymakers and Marine Protected Area (MPA) management, complementing their efforts while also acting as a bridge between science and the community. We offer tools for better listening and innovative ways for individuals to connect with their marine ecosystem. Sound monitoring is a relatively new, resource-efficient approach for gathering extensive data, making it an ideal technology for low-income coastal communities along the Mesoamerican Reef. WOS is developing a cost-effective and easy-to-implement model that can be replicated throughout the MAR.

WOS is a project endorsed by the IOC Executive Secretary as part of the UN Ocean Decade.

Name: rewild - ecosystem restoration
Description: rewild empowers companies to regenerate damaged ecosystems in Switzerland through the purchase of Nature Shares. In partnership with Pro Natura Aargau, we acquire land in Switzerland and restore it following the principles of rewilding. Investors receive an annual report with valuable insights into their impact, strengthening their biodiversity strategy.

Name: Programa Arboretum
Description: O Arboretum é um Programa interinstitucional que reúne atores relacionados à pesquisa, à normatização e à extensão, em um ciclo que vai desde a coleta de sementes, produção de mudas e plantios para restauração e para uso sustentável de espécies florestais, numa estrutura de suporte técnico e logístico permanentemente vinculada às ações de campo.

Name: OpenSpace
Description: Specialist Ecological Consultancy and Conservation Contracting. Providing ecology and habitat solutions.

Name: Robin du Bois
Description:
Association qui lutte contre les coupes rases et promeut une gestion durable des forêts. 
Installée dans le Parc Naturel Régional du Pilat, nous sensibilisons aux bienfaits de la forêt et nous achetons des parcelles pour mettre en place des principes de gestion durable.

Name: Africa Conservation Impact
Description:
Working to conserve and secure Africa's most endangered Wildlife and Landscapes in partnership with local communities 
No Elephant left behind

Name: Sustainable Ocean and Reefs Inc.
Description: We developed and operate the largest in water invertebrate reef grazer nursery in the Tropical Western Atlantic. We specialize in the cultivation and restoration of keystone reef grazers—herbivores that are critical to coral reef health and recovery. From nursery innovation to ecosystem impact, SOAR is advancing the future of coral reef restoration.

Name: Northwest Connecticut Land Conservancy
Description: Founded in 1965, Northwest Connecticut Land Conservancy is the state’s largest land trust. As a guardian of natural and working lands, public recreation areas, and drinking water resources, NCLC permanently protects 14,200 acres (and growing) of vast, connected natural areas in Litchfield and northern Fairfield Counties.

Name: Youth Eco-Panel (YEP)
Description: Platform exploring opportunities for youth in climate change founded by dedicated youth future leaders with the interest in Nature-Based Solutions, Biodiversity Conservation, Climate Adaptation and mitigation, and Agroforestry.

Name: Marine Mammal Advisory Group
Description:
The Marine Mammal Advisory Group is a a cohort of stakeholders established to collaborate on the protection of biodiversity, and explore solutions to strikes and collisions in the boating sector.

Key actions to mitigate :

1.TECH SOLUTIONS & WATCHKEEPING
2.SOURCE & SHARE INFORMATION
3.RACE COURSE RISK ASSESSMENT
4.LIVE REPORTING & CITIZEN SCIENCE
5.EDUCATION & OUTREACH

Name: Skagit Environmental Endowment Commission
Description:
The Skagit Environmental Endowment Commission (SEEC) was established by a 1984 Treaty between Canada and the U.S. It is based on an Agreement between the City of Seattle and the Province of British Columbia to settle the controversy over the High Ross Dam proposal.

More Info Here: skagiteec.org/about/about-seec

Name: BioQuest
Description: A global NGO focused on biodiversity conservation using genomics, starting with Caribbean signature species

Name: Minka Preservation Society
Description: The Minka Preservation Society (aka Minka) is a community-focused organization that promotes the preservation of Japanese built-heritage structures such as minka or machiya, as well as their attendant traditional carpentry and artisanal techniques, by connecting enthusiasts and advocates through curated events, providing effective resources and fostering rural revitalization.

Name: UQROP
Description:
Notre mission est d'œuvrer à la conservation des oiseaux de proie et de leurs habitats naturels. Pour mener à bien cette mission, l'UQROP poursuit deux mandats.

Un premier mandat consiste à structurer un réseau québécois de soins pour les oiseaux de proie sauvages trouvés blessés, malades ou orphelins. Ce réseau est possible grâce à la participation de nombreux intervenants. Plus de 400 oiseaux de proie sont ainsi recueillis et soignés en moyenne chaque année.

Name: Tahoe Lead Removal Project
Description: We're a community-driven initiative to remove miles of old lead cable that have been left at the bottom of Lake Tahoe. The length of these cables spans nearly 10% of the shoreline. From Baldwin Beach to Rubicon Bay, they cross the mouth of world famous Emerald Bay. They are no longer in use, and haven't been used for decades. Regardless of the past, we're here to take them out to protect Lake Tahoe for future generations.

Name: Conservation Value Solutions
Description:
Conservation Value Solutions translates cutting-edge science into well-designed conservation and natural climate solutions strategies that generate valuable environmental and people benefits. 

We offer clients innovative approaches to nature based solutions strategy development, program architecture, and science-based due diligence. Together, we move science to action!

Name: AID Forests
Description: The Ankoatsifaka Initiative for Dry Forests aims to raise awareness about the plight of dry forests around the globe. We actively work to support the research and conservation of tropical dry forests while simultaneously striving to improve the well-being of communities living in and around these ecosystems.

Name: Kingsburg Coastal Conservancy
Description: Kingsburg Coastal Conservancy is a non-profit, charitable organization committed to: preserving headlands, beaches, and wetlands of the Kingsburg Peninsula and neighbouring areas on the south shore of Nova Scotia, and to safeguarding public access to our shorelines. In 2023, we expanded our mandate to include wetlands research, monitoring and restoration to prepare for and help mitigate climate change.

Name: In Conservation With...
Description: In Conservation With - a series of interviews with some of the leading figures in the natural history world. http://theurbanbirderworld.com/live-webinars/

Name: Dunkelgrün - Regenwaldprojekte e.V.
Description: Dunkelgrün - Regenwaldprojekte e.V. is a registered non-profit association with the aim of preserving the rainforest in Latin America in order to protect the climate and biodiversity.

Name: Estación/Estação Biológica Internacional DueroDouro (EBI)
Description:
La Estación Biológica Internacional Duero-Douro (EBI) es una organización hispano-lusa, para la conservación de los espacios naturales transfronterizos, a través de la investigación científica, la educación ambiental, el ecoturismo sostenible y la innovación tecnológica.

La EBI nació en 1994, se constituyó formalmente en 2002 con el apoyo del Instituto para la Conservación de la Naturaleza - ICNF junto a tres universidades europeas y en 2006 fue ratificada oficialmente por los Ministros de Exteriores de España y Portugal. Su equipo técnico y laboral lo forman españoles y portugueses, principalmente científicos, biólogos y submarinistas especializados en conservación de ecosistemas acuáticos.

Para salvaguardar su independencia política y principios éticos, todos sus trabajos de investigación y conservación de hábitats y especies, se financian a través de sus propios proyectos ecoturísticos; fundamentalmente los Cruceros Ambientales en la Reserva Transfronteriza de la UNESCO “MESETA IBÉRICA”, entre los que se encuentra el primer buque eólico-solar del mundo certificado con cero emisiones de CO2, cero residuos, cero efluentes y cero dB sobre la tasa de ruido ambiente.

Su repercusión económica para el territorio, supera cada año los 3 millones de euros a través del incremento en los restaurantes (+49%), en las compras (+21%) y en las pernoctaciones (+27%). De esta forma, sus habitantes se convierten en defensores del espacio natural y en los principales aliados de la EBI para la preservación de los ecosistemas acuáticos como fuente de riqueza. Este modelo de sostenibilidad ha recibido, entre otros, el 1º Premio Nacional de Turismo y la Medalla al Mérito del Congreso en Portugal y el Premio Ecoinnova en España.
Location: Frontera de Zamora (ES) con Miranda do Douro (PT)

Name: Pebblebed Heaths Conservation Trust
Description:
The Pebblebed Heaths Conservation Trust was established by Clinton Devon Estates in 2006. Its creation was in response to increasing national and European recognition that lowland heathlands are a habitat rich in wildlife, are highly threatened, and require professional management to maximise their wildlife value.

The Conservation Trust has management responsibility for the East Devon Pebblebed Heaths and the Otter Estuary.

Our charitable objectives:
To promote, for the benefit of the public, the conservation, protection and improvement of the physical and natural environment of the East Devon Pebblebed Heaths and the Otter Estuary.

Name: HERITOPIA CONSULTANCY
Description:
We are a premier consultancy firm specializing in cultural assets and natural heritage management, driven by a global team of distinguished experts
Our work empowers governments, institutions, and private entities to conserve and promote their heritage through innovative and sustainable solutions tailored to enhance local economies and protect natural resources.

Name: Atlantic Healthy Oceans Initiative
Description:
AHOI was established in 2019 to conduct marine and social science research that would inform our education and outreach programs to better look after the ocean and coastal communities of western, NL Canada together for generations to come.

To date, we run a Circular Economy and Plastic Waste Reduction Program as well as a Marine Conservation Program. Our Circular Economy Program focuses on helping our regional business operators transition towards more circular practices and away from the linear model to become truly sustainable. Our Marine Conservation Programs focus on using leading technology to better understand the health of eelgrass beds, impacts from aquatic invasive species, anthropogenic impacts to aquatic species at risk and overall ocean health that can support coastal communities and a thriving marine environment for decades to come.

Name: Rooiberg Breede River Conservancy
Description:
The Rooiberg Breede River Conservancy is a Non-Profit Organisation working to conserve the natural world of the majestic Langeberg and Rooiberg Mountains, between the towns of Worcester and Robertson in the Western Cape of South Africa. Here landowners work together for nature, to protect natural and agricultural land over more than 51,000 hectares. 

We have grown and evolved since we were established in 2006 and our Non-Profit Organisation was set-up in 2013, and today we are proud to have 32 landowner members. 

Why is this region so important for nature? It’s home to threatened fynbos, Succulent Karoo and renosterveld. In fact, four of our 14 natural vegetation types are facing extinction. Many of our plant species occur only here and nowhere else on Earth. 

These natural landscapes also provide corridors for wildlife between agricultural lands - from the iconic Cape leopard to the mysterious brown hyena. Our freshwater species are just as important, with the conservancy home to restricted populations of the Endangered Breede Redfin. 

It’s no surprise therefore that we form part of a Critical Biodiversity Area, as well as a climate corridor. 

The conservancy employs a team that works to find and implement solutions that support both nature and people. As such, we run projects that include rehabilitating landscapes, removing invasive alien plants, monitoring biodiversity, erosion control and fire protection. We also focus on environmental education and awareness and support our landowners to become biodiversity stewardship sites. 

Given our pristine setting, many of our members now showcase this vast open and ancient landscape through tourist offerings - from accommodation to activities. 

Learn more about us at: www.rooibergbreederiverconservancy.org

Name: Crocworld Conservation Centre
Description:
Crocworld Conservation Centre – Where Nature Meets Adventure!

Crocworld Conservation Centre, located on the picturesque Mid-South Coast of KwaZulu-Natal, has been a cherished destination for families, nature lovers, and adventurers since 1985. Originally established as a Nile crocodile farm, we’ve transformed into a leading hub for conservation, education, and tourism, welcoming over 1.5 million visitors through our gates.

Our beautifully landscaped grounds are home to an incredible array of wildlife, from our iconic Nile crocodiles to fascinating reptiles and a diverse collection of bird species, many of which have been rescued, rehabilitated and then sent to Crocworld to live out their days. Set against lush indigenous coastal forest, Crocworld offers an immersive experience that brings you closer to the wonders of the natural world.

We proudly offer:
 Interactive wildlife encounters and educational tours.
 A safe haven for injured and rescued birds and reptiles.
 A family-friendly restaurant, Fish Eagle Café, with stunning ocean views.
 Event hosting for birthdays, parties, and special celebrations at the Fish Eagle Café.
 Thrilling crocodile feedings and snake encounters – a must-see for all ages!

Whether you're planning a fun family outing, a school trip, or a special celebration, Crocworld combines adventure, learning, and conservation to create unforgettable memories for all.

Join us and be inspired by the beauty of nature, all while contributing to the preservation of our planet's biodiversity.

Discover Crocworld – where every visit is an adventure, and every moment counts!

Name: BEE Comores
Description:
BEE Comores est un bureau d’études et de conseils spécialisé dans les domaines de l’environnement et du développement durable, de la faune flore et de l’urbanisme durable. Il réunit les pôles majeurs suivants:

● Impacts environnementaux
● Gestion des ressources naturelles
● Conservation des écosystèmes et de la biodiversité
● Gestion de catastrophes naturelles
● Développement durable
● Cartographie & SIG
● Analyse & gestion de bases données
● Agro écologie
● Ecotourisme
● Valorisation des produits de pêche et sécurité maritime

Name: LeAP: Leadership for Africa's Parks
Description:
LeAP teaches essential conservation leadership skills through African universities & colleges to protected & conserved area staff to effectively conserve iconic parks and wildlife into the future. 

Unfortunately, graduates of African wildlife colleges and universities often are not trained in the conservation leadership & professional skills they need to effectively protect parks and wildlife, such as managing protected area staff and engaging with local communities & stakeholders. Experts agree these skills are essential for improving park performance and protecting wildlife. LeAP works to fill in these key gaps in training, partnering with faculty at African wildlife colleges and universities to empower all graduates with the leadership skills they need to improve park performance and wildlife protection.

Working closely with the Directors of RP Kitabi College in Rwanda and Garoua Wildlife College/ / L’Ecole de Faune de Garoua in Cameroon and key faculty from Njala University in Sierra Leone and University of Kisangani in Democratic Republic of Congo, LeAP mentors chosen faculty to develop new courses on leadership, teamwork, problem-solving, and conflict management. These courses will become embedded into university curricula, training hundreds of rangers and managers annually at the diploma and undergraduate level for protected & conserved areas. 

LeAP will expand the network by facilitating exchanges and joint training with faculty from other African universities, creating a Pan-African network of leadership training programs across the continent. 

Our goal is to develop capacity in Africa, for African institutions to better protect their parks and wildlife for all. 

LeAP’s founding partners include: 

RP Kitabi College, Rwanda www.iprckitabi.rp.ac.rw 

Garoua Wildlife College/ / L’Ecole de Faune de Garoua, Cameroon www.ecoledesfaunes.org 

Njala University Department of Wildlife Management and Conservation 
www.njala.edu.sl

University of Kisangani, DRC

Name: ALEECA
Description: ALEECA est une organisation de la société civile dont les domaines d'intervention sont l'environnement, le développement durable, les ressources naturelles, les changements climatiques et les droits humains

Name: Hutan
Description:
Hutan is a grassroots research and conservation organisation established in 1998 in the Kinabatangan floodplain of Eastern Sabah. Over the years, we have built a team of over a hundred staff from the Orang Sungai community, working with remarkable dedication and expertise to safeguard their natural heritage.

From long-term biodiversity research and monitoring to habitat protection and restoration, environmental education and conservation planning, Hutan works to preserve wildlife and ecosystems across the Kinabatangan landscape and beyond. Our holistic approach integrates science, stakeholder engagement and locally rooted conservation solutions.

Name: Tafraout technologies environnement
Description: Tafrouate Technologies Environnement assure le diagnostique , la maintenance préventive et curative de vos réseaux d’évacuation avec des solutions naturelles en accord avec la politique de développement durable.

Name: UNITE for the Environment
Description:
UNITE for the Environment works to conserve important wildlife habitats by empowering the next generation of conservationists. We make a difference in 
communities by improving education and access to sustainable activities. 

Currently, we work with schools and local communities around Kibale and Queen Elizabeth National Parks

Name: Kaniksu Land Trust
Description:
We care for the lands and people of the Kaniksu Region (Northern Idaho and Western Montana), today, tomorrow, and forever. 

In addition to our core work of helping private land owners conserve their land, our community and education work seeks to address local challenges with conservation-based solutions.

Name: Fishing Cat Ecological Enterprise
Description:
We are the Fishing Cat Ecological Enterprise Co., Ltd. (“FCEE”) a private limited company managed as a social enterprise.

We focus on reviving Cambodia’s wetlands. We take our lead from ecosystems to implement actions for a sustainable future.

Name: Snared
Description: Snared is a non-profit dedicated to tackling poaching, with a focus on the commercial use of snares that devastate wildlife populations around the world. The organization directs its resources toward training and equipping rangers on the frontlines, while also advancing advocacy efforts to raise awareness about the dangers of snares and poaching more broadly.

Name: for every star, a tree.
Description: Cultivating abandoned desert acres into a sustainable oasis.

Name: Bosque Guardian
Description: Bosque Guardián is a Non- Profit that is protecting an area of 140 hectares in the jungle of San Martin in Peru next to the Regional Conservation Area Cordillera Escalera. We reforest, do fauna and flora monitoring through camera traps, do research for conservation, and promote environmental friendly incomes for the local population where we are located.

Name: Aktion Fischotterschutz e.V. / OTTER-ZENTRUM
Description:
Sie wollen mit Ihren Lieben einen unvergesslichen Tag in der Natur erleben? Dann wird Sie das OTTER-ZENTRUM Hankensbüttel in Niedersachsen begeistern. Denn in diesem einzigartigen Naturerlebniszentrum am Rande der Südheide Gifhorn finden kleine wie große Besucher jede Menge Abenteuer und Unterhaltung.

Grund dafür ist ein abwechslungsreiches Programm rund um die possierlichen Tiere: Ob Schau-Fütterungen, Experimente, Natur-Spielplätze oder Restaurant mit Seeterrasse – hier gibt es jede Menge zu entdecken. Viel Spaß!

Name: Healthy Forest Regions
Description:
The Interreg funded Project: HealthyForestRegions project develops concepts to strengthen the commitment of key policy stakeholders to better safeguard forest ecosystems. The partnership tests new tools and solutions for the transition to ecosystem-based forest management in the project regions. 

3 years project time 

9 partner organisations - 6 project regions

Name: Bleu Massawippi
Description:
Nous sommes les principaux gardiens du lac Massawippi. Notre mission?
Informer, éduquer, influencer et agir sur les enjeux environnementaux menaçant la qualité de l’eau, la santé du lac Massawippi et la qualité de vie de ses résidents et utilisateurs.

Name: Mjejane Game Reserve
Description:
Mjejane Game Reserve is only 4 hour's drive from Gauteng and less than 60 minutes from Nelspruit’s Kruger Mpumalanga International Airport. The Reserve is 15-30 minutes from three major golf courses in the area. Malelane town has a host of convenience shops, medical facilities, and garages less than 10 minutes away.

Access to Mjejane is gained via the N4, between Malelane and Komatipoort, en route to Maputo and Swaziland. Shortly after passing Hectorspruit on the N4, you will reach the Mjejane Game Reserve entrance on the left-hand side of the road.

innovative thinking has created one of the most exciting private game reserves in Southern Africa. It embraces both a breath-taking 10km river frontage and private bush stands epitomizing the intimate bush experience and showcasing Africa’s Big 5.

Name: LC Nature Park
Description: LC Nature Park is a premier destination for nature enthusiasts and families seeking authentic outdoor experiences. Spanning across pristine wilderness, our park combines conservation efforts with educational programs and recreational activities to create meaningful connections between people and nature.

Name: Talal International Conservancy
Description:
Talal International is an organization that builds capacities and strengthen measures of national parks and game reserves conservation.
We reduce and manage negative impacts on national parks and wildlife reserves.
Location: Konza Ranch Opposite Konza Techno City

Name: ARTHEN - Association pour le Retour des grands Herbivores dans les Espaces Naturels
Description:
L’association ARTHEN a pour objet principal la promotion, l’étude et la réintroduction des mammifères ongulés et  en particulier des grands herbivores disparus du territoire métropolitain ou de leurs substituts.

Contact: arthen@arthen-tarpan-hope.fr

Name: National Heritage Ironwork Group
Description:
The National Heritage Ironwork Group (NHIG) is dedicated to the safeguarding and preservation of historic ironwork through promoting high standards of workmanship, conservation and repair.

The NHIG was established in 2009 by a group of experienced professionals from a broad range of related fields. Together they recognised that due to the sheer quantity of architectural wrought ironwork within the heritage sector there was a need for:

 - A representative body.
 - A focal point for the discussion and dissemination of information.
 - A reference point for expertise or consultation.
 - Training routes for professionals.

The NHIG was set up with the objective of providing these and is currently leading the way worldwide in creating a representative body for the conservation of heritage ironwork through discussion, formalisation, lobbying and distribution of information.

In achieving NHIG’s purpose of ensuring the conservation of ironwork for the benefit of the nation NHIG’s work can be categorised into the following areas of activity:

 - Education: developing and sustaining individual and collective knowledge.
 - Research: instigating, conducting and publishing research about heritage ironwork.
 - Professional Standards: defining and promoting high standards in the care of heritage ironwork.
 - Resources: a focal point for information
 - Advocacy: championing heritage ironwork within the conservation community and outside it, in public and private sectors,  nationally and internationally.

With the field of heritage ironwork being wide open, there is no shortage of work within the remit of the NHIG, resulting in many strands to our ambitions.

Name: Green Gold Social
Description:
Environmental Protection
Forest Regeneration 
Water Supply 
Forest Restoration

Name: TicoRico ProjectNature
Description:
Unter der klaren Definition von  „save nature – create ressources” bauen wir ein Naturschutzprojekt auf, welches an einem der wichtigsten Punkt überhaupt ansetzt.  Dem Bestandsschutz der Natur – „save nature“ 

Definiertes Ziel:  Wiederherstellung einer Primärwaldstruktur

Aktuell steht uns eine Fläche von knapp 1 million Quadratmeter (ca. 100 Hektar) im Rahmen des Projektes in Costa Rica unter unserer Schirmherrschaft. Mithilfe von Landpatenschaften, Aufforstung und Renaturierung soll der Baumbestand geschützt, ausgeweitet und dauerhaft neue Lebensräume für Tiere und Pflanzen geschaffen werden. 

Werde Teil des Projectes und schütze den wichtigsten und sensibelsten Lebensraum unserer Erde.

Name: green for humanitarian and development
Description: Environnement , humanitaire development

Name: School Meets The Reef
Description:
The School meets the reef and accompanies nature-based solutions as a mode of conservation of marine ecosystems and educating the young generations about the importance of coastal ecosystems.The project was created on behalf of the University of Wageningen Student Challenges.
Future collaborations are aligned with Earthlanka Youth Network of Sri Lanka.

Description:
El ECA MAENI representa a 15 CCNN y un asentamiento rural de colonos de la ZA de la RCM, así como a 3 organizaciones indígenas representativas de las comunidades: el Consejo Machiguenga del Río Urubamba (Comaru), la Central de Comunidades Nativas Machiguenga (Ceconama) y la Federación de Comunidades Nativas Yine Yami (Feconayy).

Name: Donau-Auen National Park
Description:
The Donau-Auen National Park preserves the last great floodplain landscape in Central Europe on an area of more than 9,600 hectares. With its foundation in 1996, this nature jewel was placed under international protection sustainably. Since then, nature has been able to develop here free from economic constraints.

By means of innovative river revitalization projects, side waters are reconnected to the river and natural Danube banks are fostered. The riparian forests are developing into species-rich forest wilderness. According to the motto "Free River. Wild Forest", the original natural character is being restored to the floodplain landscape. Rare animal and plant species have returned or improved their populations.

With its diverse range of visitor facilities, excursions and events, the Donau-Auen National Park can be experienced by guests, is a regional centre for environmental education and a popular destination for excursions.
Location: Orth an der Donau, Lower Austria

Name: Motuihe Trust
Description:
An island paradise situated in Auckland's Hauraki Gulf.

Motuihe Restoration is a conservation project run in partnership between Motuihe Trust and the Department of Conservation (DOC) to transform Motuihe Island into an authentic natural environment of beaches, native forests, wetlands and open spaces together with rare and endangered native birds and insects.

Name: Mondo Eco-Expeditions
Description:
Welcome to Mondo Eco-Expeditions, where education meets adventure!

Join us on a journey to some of the most beautiful and biodiverse locations in the world. Learn from leading experts in ecology and gain hands-on field experience. Immerse yourself in local culture and make a positive impact on the planet.

We offer a variety of expeditions and tours in Costa Rica. Visit out website to learn about all our unique offerings. We have something for everyone! 

Please email us at info@mondoecoexpeditions.com to book an information session or to request more details. We look forward to hearing from you!

Name: Aviary Park Indonesia
Description:
Aviary Park Indonesia is an immersive aviary sanctuary where incredible fauna thrives in diverse habitats of diverse ecosystems. With the recreation environment of forest, savannah, desert landscapes, and lakes converge to create the ultimate aviary adventure. 
Join us on a journey of discovery and experience the beauty of Aviary Park Indonesia – where the sky meets the earth in an unforgettable experience!

Name: Small Cat Advocacy and Research (SCAR)
Description:
Established in 2017, Small Cat Advocacy and Research (SCAR) is a non-profit organisation dedicated towards the protection of Sri Lanka’s lesser understood small wild cats, and small mammals.

Our focus is to gain a better understanding of the three species through conservation and research, and use the knowledge gained to conserve the species throughout the island by working closely with communities, stakeholders and government authorities. Our awareness campaigns on the three small wild cats will ensure wider reach and educate the general public that these lesser-known species are just as important as their larger cousin (i.e. the Sri Lankan leopard).

Name: West African Primate Conservation Action
Description:
Safeguard the future of endangered primates and their habitat in West Africa through a sustainable One Plan Approach to species conservation: the development of management strategies and conservation actions by all responsible parties for all populations of a species, whether inside or outside their natural range.

         DISCOVER, PROTECT, REINFORCE, CONNECT

Name: Lake Albert Foundation
Description:
The Lake Albert Foundation is committed to sustainable development of the highlands in the cross-border region north of Lake Albert and Uganda, in which food production and landscape regeneration support each other and thereby increase prosperity in the region. 

Examples of how we do this:
 Successional agroforestry design and implementation
 Nature conservation and restoration, e.g. wetlands
 Designing and implementing regenerative agriculture practices (composting, bio pesticides, bio inoculants, erosion control, etc.)
 Business model development for regenerative farming
 Accelerating women empowerment and entrepreneurship in regenerative farming

Check out our projects on: www.lakealbertfoundation.com

Name: Indo Coral Conservation
Description: Indo Coral Conservation, located on Gili Air, is an NGO dedicated to marine conservation. They’re focused on restoring and preserving coral reefs around the Gili Islands through coral farming since 2019, community engagement and education, and sustainable management practices to combat local environmental degradation.Their mission is to safeguard the long-term health and resilience of marine ecosystems while educating local communities on their importance.

Name: ONG Susténtate
Description:
ONG Susténtate es una entidad sin ánimo de lucro cuyos principales objetivos son la promoción, desarrollo, protección e investigación de los factores sociales, culturales y ambientales que supone la conservación de la Naturaleza.

Promocionamos el empleo de diversas herramientas para la conservación y el desarrollo rural sostenible. Paralelamente realizamos seguimiento y monitorio ambiental de las áreas de conservación que se encuentran en nuestra custodia.

Name: South Mountain Partnership
Description: The South Mountain Partnership is a collaborative network of people and organizations protecting and promoting the agricultural, natural, cultural, and recreational resources of the South Mountain region in Adams, Cumberland, Franklin, and York counties of southcentral Pennsylvania.

Name: NEMO IMS
Description:
NEMO IMS (NEguentropic MOney Internatioanl Monetary System)  is a global economic proposal aiming to reform monetary, banking, and financial institutions to achieve ecological transition, post-growth, and to ensure the conditions for life on Earth. The idea is to transform the extractive global economy into a sustainable and life-respecting model, moving away from extractive economic competition towards a cooperative model without sacrificing social progress and the Human Development Index.

NEMO SMI suggests that central banks coordinate around a new global exchange standard, the "NEMO Exchange Standard," which would free us from the constraints inherent in the current extractive model based on the dollar or key currencies (competition, productivism, external constraint).

It proposes the creation of new global economic institutions to accelerate the ecological transition process. The GAIA Economic Symposium would aim to determine global common interest missions and democratically define revenues labeled as "NEMO Green DTS." These would be convertible into national currencies by central banks for service provider companies.

In this way, the central currency is backed by globally consensus-labeled common good projects. This central currency then serves as a monetary regulation for the issuance of credit money by the banks.

Thus, nations obtain their liquidity by engaging in regenerative activities and protecting commons and are encouraged to use these funds in exchanges for goods and services with minimal harm.

Name: Kuno Earth
Description: Kuno is the nature platform for life on Earth. Kuno is building a platform to make it easy for local communities, nature lovers, experts and conservation groups to share their knowledge and love of the natural world and efforts to protect it.

Name: Lamu Marine Conservation Trust (LaMCoT)
Description: LaMCoT is an environment protection CBO based in Lamu, Kenya.

Name: Ecostudies Institute
Description: Ecostudies Institute is dedicated to the conservation of native species and their ecosystems. Our natural environment faces complex and dynamic threats which we address with effective partnerships, conservation research, and strategic, science-based management to create exceptional and lasting improvements for the species and habitats we support. Because ecological conservation is vitally linked to community, we work to promote environmental justice by removing barriers to engagement and improving access to environmental education and career opportunities for underserved populations. In particular, we recognize the inextricable connection between Indigenous peoples and their ancestral lands and seek to actively support that relationship.

Name: Cape RADD
Description:
The Cape RADD Marine Field Station is situated on the shores of False Bay, Cape Town and is a platform to host field projects in a logistical, advisory or training capacity, and provide training programs for early career scientists.
We aim to facilitate regional and international collaboration with multidisciplinary expertise to assist with marine conservation research and education for the False Bay ecoregion.

Name: Whale and Dolphin Conservation North America
Description: Whale and Dolphin Conservation (WDC) is a Candid Platinum-rated and Charity Navigator four-star nonprofit dedicated to protecting whales and dolphins. Our core mission is to educate people on the significant ecological role that whales and dolphins play in the marine ecosystem and to inspire global action to protect them. We accomplish our mission through education campaigns, advising governments, conservation projects, direct marine animal rescue and response, and field research – as we commit to sound science, education, and outreach to change the fate of marine mammals and our shared earth. Since opening our office in 2005, our North American programs have been dedicated to the protection of endangered whale species on the East and West Coasts of the U.S.

Name: Nature & Développement ( Nat&Dev)
Description: NATURE & DEVELOPPEMENT œuvre pour la valorisation des solutions fondées sur la nature afin de concilier conservation de la biodiversité et développement socioéconomique des communautés locales

Name: DarkSky Kenya
Description: DarkSky International works to protect the night skies for present and future generations. Our DarkSky Kenya Chapter seeks to facilitate the designation of conservancies, wildlife refuges, and national parks as International Dark Sky Places (IDSP) to encourage communities around protected areas to preserve and protect our dark sites through responsible lighting policies and public education. In doing so, we work in close partnership with the Travelling Telescope for educational programming and the Noctia Institute for public education and outreach.

Name: Le Cels'green
Description:
Association étudiante 
CELSA Sorbonne Université
 Objectif : promouvoir une transition sociale et écologique au sein et en dehors de l'école. 
https://linktr.ee/celsgreen

Name: iTaukei Women in Conservation
Description: iTWC is a women-led initiative that focuses on working with women and youths within our local community on blue and green carbon programs.

Name: LIFE Maronesa
Description:
O LIFE MARONESA é um projeto de Governança, Informação e Ação Climática, que visa a implementação de estratégias de adaptação às alterações climáticas através de um modelo sustentável de produção pecuária em modo extensivo.

Aborda a problemática do abandono das pastagens de montanha, em particular nas áreas baldias comunitárias, o aumento da intensidade dos fogos rurais de verão, a redução do stock de carbono sequestrado na matéria orgânica dos solos nas áreas de montanha e a redução do efetivo da raça bovina Maronesa. 

Tem como principal objetivo melhorar a resiliência climática das explorações pecuárias e promover o aumento da retenção de carbono na matéria orgânica do solo.

O projeto baseia-se num modelo de demonstração de práticas agrícolas e pecuárias sustentáveis, que tem vindo a ser implementadas por um produtor de vanguarda, e que serão implementadas em quatro áreas de replicação, através de produtores seguidores desse modelo de exploração.

Name: BAU-Regain
Description:
Bau-Regain is a non-profit organization dedicated to the design and production of circular urban furniture.
Based in Belgium, it places reuse, sustainability, and people at the heart of its creations.

Our mission is to give materials a second life while reimagining public spaces to make them more sustainable, inclusive, and aesthetic.
We work with reclaimed materials from local sources and collaborate with social economy partners to create furniture that connects citizens to their environment.

Each project is a meeting point between design, craftsmanship, and social impact.
Our creations contribute to a more responsible form of urbanism — one that values existing resources, promotes local employment, and raises awareness about ecological transition.

Bau-Regain is a team of designers, artisans, and citizens who believe in a more resilient city — where every bench, table, and structure tells a story of cooperation and regeneration.

Name: Conservate
Description: Utilizing innovative digital technology to  build local capacity for monitoring reporting and credible verification (MRV) of conservation impact for both funders and implementers to reverse the effects of climate change

Name: Wild & Free Foundation
Description:
Wild and Free Foundation pairs conservation with community uplift. Rhinos are keystone species; our model is community first: youth football and local livelihoods that replace the pull of poaching with pride and progress.

We back the people who live beside rhino habitats, because conservation lasts when communities benefit from wildlife alive.

Our flagship, the Rhino Cup Champions League, engages thousands of youth in structured football, creates local jobs, and builds conservation buy-in.

Launched in 2016 with 4 teams in Sàbié, Mozambique, the RCCL now fields 104 men’s and women’s teams across Mozambique, Namibia, Zimbabwe and Tanzania, including youth divisions. With an average of 20 members per team, we reach more than 2,000 players and coaches and support 100+ paid local roles. The result is stronger communities, safer parks, and a future that rewards protection over exploitation.

Back communities. Build futures. Protect rhinos.

Name: Wildlands Trust
Description: Wildlands Trust works throughout Southeastern Massachusetts to permanently protect native habitats, farmland, and lands of high ecologic and scenic value that serve to keep our communities healthy and our residents connected to the natural world.

Name: Pest Free Token Charitable Trust
Description:
The PFT project was created to help New Zealand reach its goal of being predator free by 2050.

We make and deploy traps nationwide, with 100% of our profits going to making more traps and/or funding other pest eradication organisations.

Order a trap and make your backyard count today!

Name: Women Beyond Biodiversity Conservation
Description:
Promote an ethic and gender-based conservation approach through women empowerment and leadership
Capacity Building
Mentoring
Networking
Fieldbased

Name: Ocean Alliance, Inc.
Description: Ocean Alliance’s mission is to protect whales and their ocean environment through research, scientific collaboration, public education, and the arts. Ocean Alliance, Inc., is a 501(c)(3) not-for-profit Organization, and was founded in 1971.

Name: Alameda County Resource Conservation District
Description:
Founded in 1972, the Alameda County RCD formed though the merger of two Soil Conservation Districts (Western and Eastern Alameda County Soil Conservation Districts). The mission of the ACRCD is to provide leadership in the County and region about natural resources conservation, wildlife, and agricultural enhancement through partnerships, education, outreach, resource services, technical assistance, and funding. 

Resource Conservation Districts (RCDs) are independent, non-regulatory, special districts of California that provide resource conservation services and are local hubs for connecting people with technical, financial and educational assistance they need to conserve and manage those resources. 

Together with the USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service (NRCS) Livermore office, we work as the Conservation Partnership, continuing to enhance the preservation & conservation of natural resources and facilitating agricultural improvements throughout Alameda County. Our work includes species management and habitat restoration, rangeland ecology and resilience strategies, climate change mitigation research, urban agriculture technical assistance and financial support, workshops, field tours, and conservation and stewardship educational opportunities.

We collaborate with many partners, including local, state, and federal agencies, organizations, corporations, non-profits, and local individuals.

Name: Peatlands Europe
Description:
Facilitating Collaboration, Connecting Resources, and Building Partnerships for a Greener Future

Dedicated professionals and passionate individuals accelerating peatland conservation and restoration through expertise, innovation, and collaboration at the intersection of policy, science, practice and business.

Name: Zoologist's Association of University of Sri Jayewardenepura (ZSJP)
Description: ZSJP stands to protect and conserve the animals and environment by making a conscious effort in educating the student community...

Name: Asociația Parcul Natural București
Description: We promote nature and nature based solutions in the urban area.

Name: Rhino and Forest Fund e.V.
Description:
Destroyed rainforests do not have to be lost forever! We restore rainforests and protect endangered animals
We are a team of scientists, experts and activitists who specialize in reforestation of tropical rainforests and habitat protection
With your help we can turn degraded land back into forests that ensure survival of critically endangered species.

Name: Deepwater Conservation Initiative
Description:
Deepwater Conservation Initiative’s primary goal is to provide opportunities for SCUBA divers in our community to utilize their diving skills to protect the aquatic environments we love. Opportunities include citizen science programs that focus on invasive and endangered species, habitat restoration, and coral restoration.

The end result will be healthy aquatic ecosystems for future generations to enjoy.

Deepwater Conservation Initiative is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

Name: EarthSenseAI Center
Description:
Climate change is driving ever more rapid shifts in our ecosystems. Understanding how our changing climate affects critical habitats and key species requires more and more data. Increasingly, conservation scientists utilize devices such as unmanned vehicles, camera traps, and hydrophone arrays. Unfortunately, they are now starting to drown in the enormous amount of data being generated. 

The ability to analyze the impacts of climate change and plan for the future is not keeping pace. Artificial Intelligence (AI) can help!

Our mission at EarthSenseAI Center, a new 501(c)(3) non-profit organization, is to make artificial intelligence accessible to scientists and government agencies who have only minimal cross-training by reducing barriers to AI and Machine Learning adoption.  

We are composed of computer scientists with diverse research and development backgrounds and all with a passion for connecting data collected in real-world environments to impactful conservation insights.

The EarthSenseAI Center is dedicated to the open sharing of neural network models that we train and software that we develop.  We will also be sharing progress on our projects and collaborations through blog posts by our machine learning specialists and conservation partners.

Name: Skerry Conservation Dogs
Description:
Skerry Conservation Dogs is based in Scotland and provides a professional dog team to carry out surveys for species of ecological concern.  SKD are flexible to travel so can carry out surveys both in Scotland and further afield.
Currently the team is ready to carry out bat and bird carcass searches on wind turbine sites and the dogs are available to imprint on other scents as clients wish.  
Lindsey, owner and primary handler, has extensive experience in the ecological sector and has been working as a primary dog handler on a large mustelid eradication in the north of Scotland over the past 3 years and during this time has worked alongside colleagues to refine the search methodology to provide the data needed by the whole team.

Name: Elephants for Africa
Description: Elephants for Africa is a company based out of Germany.

Name: Northumberland Land Trust
Description:
Our mission: to preserve the natural heritage of Northumberland County
Our mandate: to establish, maintain, and manage protected areas in perpetuity

Name: Alka Wildlife
Description:
Alka Wildlife
Scientists working for #wildliferesearch and #conservation in the Czech Republic 
Check out our website for information about our projects:
bit.ly/alkawildlife

Name: Blackland Prairie Raptor Center
Description:
Blackland Prairie Raptor Center is dedicated to environmental preservation through public education and the conservation of birds of prey and wildlife in their natural habitat.

Named after the tallgrass prairie that once covered more than 23,500 square miles of Texas from the Red River to San Antonio, Blackland Prairie Raptor Center is a rehabilitation and conservation education organization, specializing in fostering better public understanding of the relationship between birds of prey and healthy ecosystems.

BPRC is state and federally licensed:
Texas Parks and Wildlife permit #: REH-0715-124
U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service permit #: MB138982

In August 2004, Blackland Prairie Raptor Center was incorporated as a 501 (c) (3) nonprofit organization with a small but determined group of individuals and a common goal – to create a place where the people of North Texas could learn, experience and appreciate birds of prey and understand their importance in the environment as a whole.  We began by building appropriate caging for 8 non-releasable raptors and the use of an existing shed on a Board members property approximately 70 miles northeast of Dallas. We offered educational presentations to schools, festivals, camps and environmental groups as well as anyone else interested in learning about raptors, reaching more than 5,000 people in the first year.  BPRC signed a lease in 2007 with the US Army Corps of Engineers to use Brockdale Park on Lavon Lake to build our future. The vision was to build a facility for our education raptors, a clinic for raptor rehabilitation and a permanent education building open to the public on this 66-acre park.  Through donation of time and money, we've grown our center into one of the top  raptor centers in the country, taking in over 700 admissions a year.

Name: Fundación Plantae
Description: Fundación Plantae es una fundación chilena que nace el 2016 en Valdivia, Chile. Trabajamos con personas y comunidades para la conservación, acceso y uso conscientes de los espacios naturales.

Name: ReduNOx B.V.
Description: Supplier in Sustainable NOx Reduction  Our mission is clear: deliver advanced SCR systems for maximum nitrogen oxides (NOx) reduction.  Sustainability, technological excellence, and customer-centricity drive us. Together, we're on the path to a cleaner future!

Name: Northeast RISCC Management
Description: The Northeast Regional Invasive Species & Climate Change (NE RISCC) Management Network aims to reduce the compounding effects of invasive species and climate change by synthesizing relevant science, sharing needs and knowledge of practitioners, building strong communities, and conducting priority research. We are a network of scientists, natural resource managers and practitioners, policymakers, and stakeholders from the broader public. Founded in 2016, our mission as a boundary spanning organization is to connect and integrate science and practice to support informed management of invasive species in a changing climate.

Name: Life Circle
Description:
Life Circle is a holistic impact enterprise that combines ecological, social, and economic solutions in a single model. Through regenerative agriculture, aquaponic fish farming, rewilding, and cooperative organizational structures, Life Circle creates sustainable livelihoods for landless families in Namibia – and potentially beyond.

 Core Areas
Aquaponics: Fish farming & plant cultivation in closed water cycles
Organic Agriculture: Regenerative cultivation methods for vegetables, grains, and fruits
Rewilding: 10 hectares of reforestation & biodiversity per farm
Cooperative Model: Social participation & economic independence
CO₂ Certificates: Climate-positive model with certified carbon sequestration

 Goals
Establish up to 1,000 sustainable farms
Ensure food security and land access for families
Sequester CO₂ through rewilding (up to 1,000 ha)
Create a self-sustaining circular economy model
Export sustainable products and CO₂ certificates

 Target Groups
Impact investors & funding organizations
Food wholesalers & the hospitality industry
Companies seeking CO₂ compensation
Governments & development institutions

 Locations
Namibia: On-site implementation and expansion
Austria: Strategic management, financing & international partner network

 Mission
"Land becomes habitat. Work becomes value creation. Nature is regenerated."

Name: Naturpark Neckartal-Odenwald
Description: Der Naturpark Neckartal-Odenwald ist ein baden-württembergisches Großschutzgebiet und damit eine nationale Naturlandschaft. Seine Handlungsfelder sind 1. Bildung für nachhaltige Entwicklung, 2. Erholung und nachhaltiger Tourismus, 3. nachhaltige Regionalentwicklung und 4. Naturschutz und Landschaftspflege.

Name: Rappahannock River Roundtable
Description:
The Rappahannock River Roundtable is a strategic partnership of stakeholders dedicated to land and water conservation, community outreach and education, and capacity building throughout the entire Rappahannock River Watershed.

The Roundtable is a collaborative of organizations working to accelerate the pace of water quality improvement and pollution reduction guided by the Virginia Phase III Watershed Implementation Plan (WIP), the Chesapeake Bay Total Maximum Daily Load (TMDL), and many local TMDL implementation plans.

Name: CCISRI- TCHAD
Description:
Collectif des Chercheurs en Ingénieries des Sols et de Résilience contre les Inondations au Tchad (CCISRI-T). 
 Créée le 18 février 2022 à Mongo – Autorisation N° 291/RT/PGR/SG/2024

Nous sommes une organisation scientifique et sociale engagée pour le développement durable, la gestion durable des sols, la lutte contre les inondations et le renforcement de la résilience des communautés face aux défis environnementaux.

𝐍𝐨𝐭𝐫𝐞 𝐕𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧

Un Tchad résilient face aux inondations, où les ressources naturelles sont gérées durablement pour garantir un avenir prospère et équitable pour tous.

 𝐍𝐨𝐬 𝐌𝐢𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬:

•Promouvoir la recherche, l’innovation et les actions concrètes dans la gestion des sols et la résilience climatique.
•Renforcer les capacités des communautés et institutions face aux risques environnementaux.
•Contribuer à l’atteinte des Objectifs de Développement Durable (ODD) 2015-2030.

𝐍𝐨𝐬 𝐨𝐛𝐣𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐟𝐬 𝐠𝐥𝐨𝐛𝐚𝐮𝐱:

•Réduire les risques d’inondations et renforcer la résilience communautaire.
•Lutter contre la faim, améliorer la nutrition et promouvoir une agriculture durable.
•Assurer l’accès à l’eau, à l’hygiène et à l’assainissement.
•Préserver et restaurer les écosystèmes et biodiversités.
•Lutter contre les changements climatiques et la désertification.
•Promouvoir l’égalité des sexes et la protection des droits humains.
•Favoriser des partenariats nationaux et internationaux pour un développement durable.


𝐍𝐨𝐬 𝐨𝐛𝐣𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐟𝐬 𝐬𝐩é𝐜𝐢𝐟𝐢𝐪𝐮𝐞𝐬:

•Améliorer la productivité agricole et les revenus des producteurs.
•Répondre aux besoins nutritionnels des groupes vulnérables.
•Réduire la vulnérabilité face aux phénomènes climatiques extrêmes.
•Combattre les épidémies et maladies transmissibles.
•Éliminer les violences et discriminations envers les femmes et les filles.
•Promouvoir l’éducation au développement durable et la citoyenneté.

Name: UCAM Conservation and Development Lab
Description:
A conservation research group based at the University of Cambridge under the direction of Principle Investigator Prof Rachael Garrett. 

Research combines four major innovations to overcome the methodological and geographical limitations of the existing literature on company supply chain policies:
i) a coordinated pan-tropical data analysis of multiple forest risk commodities, ii) the simultaneous examination of conservation and livelihood outcomes, including newly accessible remotely sensed estimates of forest degradation, 
iii) assessing mechanisms of impact, not just measures of impact, and iv) a comparative study with triangulation across multiple scales and methods.

We conduct coordinated fieldwork across the world’s largest tropical hotspots (focus sites in Brazil, West Africa and Indonesia) of commodity-driven deforestation and urgent areas of reforestation. We aim to generate comparative and generalisable insights on the conditions under which forest-focused supply chains might lead to both improved conservation and livelihood outcomes (e.g., effectiveness and equity). The aim of our group is to provide empirical evidence and theoretical knowledge on how to design effective and equitable private policies.

We partner with a wide range of institutions and colleagues, including at EMBRAPA, IPB University, University of Queensland Australia, the University of Zürich, CGIAR/CIAT, UC Louvain, SEI, New York University, Basel University and UC Santa Barbara.

Name: Niagara River Greenway
Description: The Niagara River Greenway Staff and Commissioners work to fulfill the Niagara River Greenway Plan through review of proposed projects, stewardship programs, community events, collaboration with municipalities and organizational partners.  Our goal is to create a world class trail and greenspace network within the Niagara River corridor that communities can be proud of and cherish for generations to come.

Name: Anna Zemp-Stiftung
Description: Als Visionärin hat Anna Zemp (1896-1985) bereits 1978 eine Stiftung für den Um- und Mitweltschutz gegründet und ihr Haus mit dem 6300m3 grossen Naturgarten an paradiesischer Lage in die Stiftung überführt. Ihr war es schon damals bewusst , dass der Mensch nur über die Begeisterung und Liebe zur Natur für ein verantwortungsvolles Verhalten gewonnen werden kann. Diesem Credo folgen wir heute noch. In verschiedenen Formaten in- und ausserhalb unseres Gartens vermitteln wir zwischen Natur und Mensch und setzten uns für eine lebenswerte Zukunft ein.

Name: Saving Our Snake-Necked Turtle
Description: Southwestern snake-necked turtle populations found in wetlands throughout the Perth metropolitan are facing dwindling population numbers. This project aims to upskill the community to track and protect turtles and their nests, while providing critical information to inform management and prevent extinction of the snake-necked turtle, across Perth and regional southwest WA.

Name: Mumuko agroforestry enterprises
Description:
Mumuko Agroforestry Enterprises Limited is a business entity limited by shares dedicated to promoting sustainable agriculture. Our vision encompasses integrating crop production with native tree species and trading, thereby fostering ecological resilience and community prosperity. We promote conservation of native varieties of plants and traditional knowledge for inclusion in the modern strategies.
We are an agro-based company focusing on the production and trading of agroforestry organic produce, Maize grains, cereals, aromatic plants, and medicinal plants. Mumuko focuses on climate-smart agriculture ‘agroecology, post-harvest management, agro-processing, Native tree seedlings growing & supplies, consultancies, and embracing digital technology for agriculture and conservation.
At Mumuko, we uphold core values cantered on sustainability, community and farmers' empowerment, and global responsibility for interconnected issues. We firmly believe that empowering communities necessitates a comprehensive understanding of their needs.

Name: Alaska Marine Conservation Council
Description: We work to protect and restore the marine environment through sustainable fishing practices, habitat protection, and local stewardship. We support an ecosystem-based approach to research and marine resource management that incorporates the best science available, experiential knowledge, and the wisdom of tradition.

Name: Maui Nui Marine Resource Council
Description:
Founded in 2007, Maui Nui Marine Resource Council is a respected nonprofit organization recognized for our ability to work in partnership with the community toward our vision of clean ocean water, healthy coral reefs and thriving native fish for the islands of Maui Nui. 

We bring together a broad base of the community to create lasting solutions to the most challenging problems impacting our near shore ocean environments. Many problems facing local reefs originate on land, which is why our projects and programs work from mountains (mauka) to sea (makai).We work with current scientific data to ensure that the solutions we create are science based and effective.

Name: Terra Conscious
Description: Terra Conscious is a social impact & conservation start-up, founded in 2017, by Puja Mitra. We offer curated educational experiences that showcase marine & coastal ecosystems responsibly and also run impact programs in partnership with local communities focused on livelihood development and wildlife conservation.

Name: BlueLeaf Conservation
Description:
BlueLeaf Conservation est une ONG suisse engagée dans la protection de l’océan et de la biodiversité marine.

 Notre mission : reconnecter l’économie locale à la préservation de l’océan, en transformant des gestes du quotidien en impact concret et mesurable.

 Notre outil phare : Le Franc Bleu
Un dispositif de micro-financement innovant, développé avec des restaurants partenaires, qui permet de soutenir des projets de conservation marine sans solliciter directement le consommateur.

 Nos actions :
• Déploiement du Franc Bleu en Suisse romande
• Soutien à des projets de conservation marine concrets, menés par des ONG partenaires sur le terrain
• Partenariats avec des entreprises, restaurants et institutions engagés

 BlueLeaf Conservation est aujourd’hui dans une phase de professionnalisation et de déploiement, avec l’ambition de faire émerger un modèle de financement durable et reproductible au service de l’océan.

Name: BAREL COMPANY
Description: Nous recyclons les déchets pour fabriquer de la braise écologique, des pavés écologiques et des pots de fleur.

Name: Keep the Wild Alive
Description:
Keep the Wild Alive is a conservation-driven initiative dedicated to reconnecting people with nature through storytelling, education, and action. We believe that emotional connection is the key to lasting environmental change and protecting our living planet.

At our core, we champion collaboration over competition – bringing together individuals, communities, and organisations who share a passion for protecting the wild. By working in harmony, we can amplify our impact and create a stronger, more united movement for nature.

From immersive content and community engagement to accessible resources and advocacy, we empower people to understand, value, and protect our planet’s wild places and species.

Through every article, workshop, and project, we’re building a movement of nature guardians – people who not only learn about the environment but also feel it, fight for it, and help it thrive.

Together we shall rewild hearts, restore habitats, and rise as one to protect the living world we all share.

Name: SECOTO ECO SPORTS EVENTS
Description: Secoto mean Sustainable Environmental Conservation and Tourism with aims to raise new talented generations in Sports with the spirit of being Earth guardians and change maker in their communities

Name: Maasai Wilderness Conservation Trust / Fund
Description:
The Maasai Wilderness Conservation Trust (MWCT) works to protect the legendary ecosystems and astounding biodiversity of East Africa through conservation that directly benefits local Maasai communities.

The world increasingly relies on many traditional communities like the Maasai to protect the ecological treasures that exist within the land that they own. But the incredible wilderness and wildlife of Africa’s grasslands and the famous culture of the Maasai people both face daunting threats to their long-term survival. The fate of both rests with the Maasai themselves as they work to figure out how to benefit from their incredible natural resources while preserving them.

That’s what MWCT is all about—a pioneering partnership between professional conservationists, like US Board President Edward Norton, and dynamic Maasai leaders to show that the Maasai community can thrive, not just survive, by managing their ecosystem wisely.

Name: Washtenaw County Conservation District
Description: We assist Washtenaw County residents through countywide resource assessments,  conservation resource distributions, conservation education, and  one-on-one technical assistance.

Name: i3 Energy
Description:
i3 Energy is an energy efficiency company focused on assisting utility clients with achieving their energy savings and sustainability plans. Our team is focused on Inclusion, Integration, and Innovation (i3) to help each of our clients expand their energy efficiency program reach via trade ally networks, workforce development and technical trainings including electrical, refrigeration and HVAC. 
 
We seek partnership opportunities where we can bring our passion and expertise to the table. That expertise includes trade ally identification, recruitment, training, and inspections for small, medium, and large commercial programs as well as software solutions for trade ally delivered programs. Additional expertise extends to income-qualified and mobile homes programs where our employees conduct blower door tests and perform duct sealing, air sealing, underbelly repairs, and HVAC upgrades.
 
i3 Energy is driven by our passion for developing diverse talent and engaging people from all backgrounds. We aim to further community outreach while also providing opportunities to those with a challenging past.

i3 Energy is a woman-owned business and is currently applying for certification.

Name: Dark Sky Friends
Description:
Our Mission Statement

Dark Sky Friends was created to promote the education of responsible outdoor lighting at night, to raise awareness of the devastation artificial lights are having on our lives, to advocate for strict local, state, regional, national and global protection from light trespass and light pollution, including regional master lighting plans.

Name: Secluded Africa Wildlife & Community Trust
Description: Protecting wildlife, preserving culture, building a better future - Secluded Africa Wildlife & Community Trust.

Name: IG Lebendige Thur
Description: Die Interessengemeinschaft «Lebendige Thur» vereint fünf führende Umweltschutzorganisationen für eine nachhaltige Aufwertung der Thur und ihrer Zuflüsse. Wir setzen uns für den Erhalt und die Wiederherstellung flusstypischer Prozesse im gesamten Thur-Einzugsgebiet ein.

Name: OLY-agri
Description:
OLY-agri : Agir pour l'Environnement tout en promouvant la Paix et la Santé
OLY-agri s'engage activement dans la conservation de l'environnement en accord avec sa vision : Protéger la Nature, Préserver la Santé, et Innover pour Demain.

Notre Engagement
Dans notre quête de durabilité et de paix, nous lançons une initiative innovante visant à harmoniser la santé humaine, environnementale et le bien-être collectif. Nos efforts se concentrent sur trois axes principaux :

1. Protection de la nature: Nous mettrons en place des actions concrètes pour la conservation des écosystèmes locaux.
2. Préservation de la  santé: Parallèlement, nous promouvons des modes de vie sains et respectueux de l'environnement.
3. Innover pour demain: Nous encourageons l'innovation durable en soutenant des projets locaux utilisant des technologies propres et des pratiques agricoles durables et adaptées au contexte.

Name: OR ELSE
Description: Interdisciplinair onderzoek naar duurzame zandwinning

Name: African Penguins #NOOW
Description: #NOOW (Not On Our Watch) is an African penguin survival & awareness campaign by the Two Oceans Aquarium Foundation. It is going to take a collective effort to ensure their survival!

Name: Lomas de Paraíso
Description: La Asociación Circuito Ecoturístico Lomas de Paraíso VMT, es una organización de base comunitaria sin fines de lucro, fundada en 2013, cuya misión es conservar el ecosistema de lomas presente en el distrito de Villa María del Triunfo a través del ecoturismo, acciones concretas de conservación y educación ambiental, e inspirando a las personas a valorar la naturaleza. Área de Conservación Regional - Sistema de Lomas de Lima

Name: Collec'tif
Description: Collec'tif a été créée en 2022 pour structurer la collecte et la valorisation locale des cheveux humains, laines et poils animaux en Nouvelle-Calédonie

Name: Litter CleanUp
Description: Clean Planet Project is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization with a mission to enable outdoor conservation and volunteerism in support of a cleaner future for our planet. We believe that making a difference for the environment should be accessible and fun for everyone!

Name: PENSA
Description:
A low-impact on ecotourism, conservation and community development project in southern Mozambique.

The Vilanculos Coastal Widlife Sanctuary is a nature conservation area and low-density tourism development site located on the São Sebastião Peninsula in the Vilanculos District, Inhambane Province, Mozambique.

Name: Fishing For Litter
Description:
In 2004 KIMO International started a simple yet imaginative project to tackle the problem of marine litter. 

Fishing for Litter aims to reduce marine litter by involving one of the key stakeholders, the fishing industry.

Result? Cleaner seas, cleaner beaches and a healthier marine environment.

Name: APIFUSION
Description:
APIFUSION est une entreprise Ivoirienne innovante, engagée dans la promotion de l’Apiculture Durable et le Développement économique des communautés rurales en Côte d’Ivoire. 
Fondée par des passionnés d’Apiculture et de Développement Durable, nous œuvrons pour un avenir où l’apiculture contribue à la fois à la protection de l’environnement et à l’amélioration des conditions de vie des communautés locales.

Name: Saba Bank Management Unit
Description:
In 2012 the Saba Bank Management Unit was established by the Dutch Ministry of Economic Affairs, in close co-operation with the Saba Conservation Foundation and the Saba Island Government. Since its establishment in 2012, the SBMU has been responsible for day-to-day management of the Saba Bank. The SBMU is a separate unit within the Saba Conservation Foundation, with daily management by the Saba Conservation Foundation.

The Saba Bank lies about 4.3 km southwest of the island of Saba. It rises from about 1000 m above the general depths of the surrounding sea floor. The Saba Bank is the largest submarine atoll in the Atlantic Ocean and the fifth largest of its kind in the world. It covers an area of 2600 km2 and has some of the richest diversity of marine life in the Caribbean Sea. In 2010 it was designated as “Saba Bank National Park”, and it was officially recognized as a national park in 2012 by the Kingdom of the Netherlands, soon followed with the recognition as an Ecologically Significant Marine Area by the Convention on Biological Diversity in 2013. The National Park was established to provide protection to the biodiversity of the bank.

The marine life there consists of more than 20 species of whales and dolphins, sharks, sea turtles, over 200 species of fish, and many kinds of seabirds. The bank is highly valued to other nearby reefs, acting as a source of coral and fish larvae that spread to coral reefs in the region. The Saba Bank is considered as an important breeding and calving area for the migration of humpback whales, but also as a breeding ground for shark species.

Name: Comité ZIP du Sud-de-l'Estuaire
Description: La mission du Comité ZIP du Sud-de-l’Estuaire est de promouvoir et soutenir, par la concertation régionale, les actions visant la protection, la conservation, la réhabilitation des milieux perturbés et l’accessibilité au fleuve Saint-Laurent dans une perspective de développement durable.

Name: Vilanculos Coastal Wildlife Sanctuary
Description:
A low-impact on ecotourism, conservation and community development project in southern Mozambique.

The Vilanculos Coastal Widlife Sanctuary is a nature conservation area and low-density tourism development site located on the São Sebastião Peninsula in the Vilanculos District, Inhambane Province, Mozambique.

Name: Skutsi
Description: Skutsi (stadin slangia, metsä) on osuuskunta ja yhteisö, jonka tavoitteena on hankkia 20–30 hehtaaria metsää Etelä-Suomesta. Nämä metsät palautetaan luonnolliseen tilaansa, jotta niiden monimuotoisuudesta pääsisivät nauttimaan tulevatkin sukupolvet.

Name: Southern Interior Land Trust
Description: The Southern Interior Land Trust Society (SILT) was established in 1988 as the Okanagan Region Wildlife Heritage Fund Society. SILT seeks to create a legacy of protected, important habitats for all living things by acquiring and maintaining ecologically significant gems and jewels of wildlife habitat in British Columbia's southern interior region. Our mission is to create “stepping-stones” for ecosystem connectivity and animal movement wherever possible.

Name: Connecticut River Watershed Partnership
Description:
Formed in 2005, the Connecticut River Watershed Partnership (CRWP), formerly known as the Friends of Conte, is a diverse coalition of over 75 public and private organizations, agencies, and individuals dedicated to forging partnerships that benefit wildlife, people, and environmental quality in the Connecticut River Watershed. CRWP provides a forum and foundation for organizations of all sizes and types with varied missions related to the watershed, including land trusts, watershed associations, federal
and state agencies, regional planning commissions, tribal organizations, municipalities, and other interests. Our partnership believes in the benefits
of working together to accomplish goals in service of the Watershed and together we aspire to foster a collective, inclusive, and shared vision to achieve positive impact across the region.

Name: La Tribu Maritime
Description: La Tribu Maritime a pour but de protéger l'environnement et le littoral en réduisant l'empreinte écologique des activités maritimes et nautiques. Nous créons et développons une filière 3R (Réduire / Réutiliser / Recycler) des ressources et déchets maritimes et nautiques. #remploi #transition

Name: Project Janszoon - Restoring the Abel Tasman
Description: Since 2012, Project Janszoon has been on a mission to restore and preserve the Abel Tasman’s rich wildlife for all to enjoy. Project Janszoon partners with the Department of Conservation, iwi, local volunteers and the Abel Tasman Birdsong Trust, as well as scientists and tourism operators, to make a real impact on the future of the Abel Tasman National Park. Together we’re making a difference.

Name: Protected Areas Trust (Guyana)
Description:
Guyana is home to unique and globally important ecosystems, including everything from pristine rainforests, to tropical savannahs, ancient tower-like mountain ranges, and large stretches of coastal mangroves. Not surprisingly, Guyana’s protected areas are full of plants and animals found nowhere else on earth. These areas cover about 8.4% of the country’s landmass, providing important refuges for biodiversity and livelihoods for indigenous communities.

Guyana’s protected areas system is now poised to double in size, with the recent pledge by the country’s government to protect an additional two million hectares. The Protected Areas Trust has a vital role to play in conserving these globally important ecosystems and will continue to support the growth and long-term sustainability of Guyana’s protected areas.

Mission:
