To mobilize and allocate finances for the management of the National Protected Areas System in Guyana

Name: Second Nature
Description:
Second Nature is a portfolio of award-winning brands across conservation and development. Our mission is to build an ecosystem for people seeking purpose-driven careers.

Each brand within the Second Nature portfolio is dedicated to a specific audience, yet all are united by our commitment to driving positive change:

 GVI: A provider of international internship programs and expeditions across conservation and community development, operating in 25 countries around the world. 

 Bushwise: A provider of professional safari guide and conservation career training, equipping the next generation of wildlife guides and conservation professionals in Africa.

 World of Difference: A specialist in delivering purpose-driven international expeditions for School and University groups. 

 GVI Foundation: Our not-for-profit arm, responsible for fundraising to support the long-term, critical work of GVI’s local community and conservation activities on the ground.

Our philosophy is built upon three equally important pillars: 
- Empowering people to live fulfilling lives
- Creating tangible global impact
- Delivering strong, sustainable financial performance to support that global impact. 

We believe that doing good and doing well should go hand-in-hand, creating a cycle of positive impact. Our mission makes our programs more meaningful, and our success provides the resources to amplify that impact.

Brand websites
gviworld.com
Bushwise.guide
worldofdifference.travel
gvifoundation.org

Name: High Peaks Alliance
Description: High Peaks Alliance is the local voice for land conservation, trail stewardship, and outdoor access in Maine's High Peaks region. We collaborate closely with communities and partners to protect critical natural areas, maintain sustainable trails, and ensure public access to the outdoors for recreation, wildlife habitat, and future generations.

Name: Programme d'Appui - Gestion de l'Eau et de l'Environnement par les SIG - PALMEES
Description:
Le Projet PALMEES est un appui stratégique dans le domaine de l’eau et de l’environnement qui sera piloté par le Bureau d’Etudes Ingénieurs Conseils et Géomatique (BEICG) basé à Ouagadougou. Cet appui prendra forme à travers une vaste campagne de séminaires de formations dans différents pays de la sous-région et des cellules de recherches sur le développement des méthodes, techniques et outils de suivi permanents de l’évolution des complexes « eau-environnement » dans les pays sahéliens. 
Il sera ouvert à toute contribution, tout organisme ou entreprise évoluant dans le domaine et aura une durée prévisionnelle de 10 ans à compter de la date de démarrage officielle des activités.

Name: FOREST BRIDGES: THE O&C FOREST HABITAT PROJECT, INC.
Description:
Founded on the basis of trust in 2015, FOREST BRIDGES: THE O&C FOREST HABITAT PROJECT, INC. is a charitable non-profit collaborative that brings together people of different perspectives on forest management to foster sustainable forests and habitats through active conservation management and restoration solutions exclusively on the 2.9 million acres of O&C Lands in western Oregon.

The organization, which became a 501(c)(3) in 2019, sees itself as ‘attempting to fill a void’ as the only all-inclusive, consensus-based grassroots collaborative working to shift the management paradigm on the whole of the western Oregon O&C Lands and move beyond decades of polarization, an entrenched culture of litigation, and land allocations that have impinged the scale of active management and restoration efforts needed to address more than 100 years of fire suppression. These and other issues have contributed to exacerbating detrimental conditions on the O&C Lands, especially on overstocked dry forests, which face increasing high-severity wildfire; increased climate change-driven drought and invasive species; protracted seasonal wildfire smoke impacting public health; declining rural economies; and reduced public access for recreation.

Over the years, the Forest Bridges collaborative has risen to the challenge of developing management principles and approaches that are constructive and viewed as reasonable from the perspective of all our partners and interest areas: Tribal Nations, ecological and climate resilience, legacy forests, plant, wildlife and other biological habitats, timber and wood products production, county revenue expectations, recreation and other material and non-material values important to the community at large. We have prepared several "living" policy papers based on our principles and strategies and submitted proposed management alternatives to Bureau of Land Management & USDA Forest Service planning processes in the last year, which have been well-received.

Name: Resilient Ecosystems Institute A.C
Description:
Mission Statement: To advance the conservation and restoration of the world's ecosystems through education, research, and action. 

Thank you for your generous support! With your contribution, we are poised to lead by example in the realm of ecological restoration and regenerative living. Your confidence in our work inspires us to work even harder to fulfill our promises.

With your help, we will continue our mission to heal and restore our planet's precious ecosystems. Together, we'll create high-quality ecological restoration, research and ecology projects that educate and inspire others to join us in our efforts.

Your generosity lights the way forward, and we are deeply grateful for your partnership on this journey toward a more sustainable and resilient future.

Name: Amor Nativo
Description: En Amor Nativo impulsamos la conservación de la biodiversidad urbana y el cuidado del ambiente. Trabajamos en alianza con empresas, gobiernos y personas, generando soluciones concretas que fortalecen el vínculo con la naturaleza nativa y generan un impacto ambiental y social real.

Name: Atlantic Forest Association
Description: We are developing tailored scientific-supported conservation programs to protect all forms of life, from the microorganisms in the soil, plants and trees to the top predators of the food chain - including the endemic species that are critically endangered in the Atlantic forest's biodiversity hotspot. Our strategy also includes environmental education initiatives through the 'School of Nature' and the 'Playground in Nature' projects (please see our website for more information). Our goal is to implement community-based conservation programs through education and social engagement.

Name: Nambi Sanctuary Foundation
Description: The Nambi Sanctuary Foundation is an environmental NGO and steward of the biodiversity-rich Nambi Sanctuary — a 288-hectare mangrove forest and biological corridor located at the mouth of the Grande de Tárcoles River in Garabito, Puntarenas Province, Costa Rica.

Name: easy-france
Description: Valorisation de biodéchets par un système biomimétique, neutre en carbone, qui associe micro-méthanisation et phycoépuration du digestat par des microalgues pour produire du biométhane, du terreau de remptage et du biostimulant algal.

Name: Te Tapu o Tāne
Description:
Te Tapu o Tāne 

Established by ki Papatipu Rūnanga o Murhiku - Awarua, Hokonui, Ōraka-Aparima and Waihōpai. 

Translates to the sacredness of Tāne - Tānemahuta God of the Forest and the protection of his tamariki - the trees.

Our vision is guided by te ao māori, Murihiku whānui enhance mahinga kai and whenua connections, tikanga, rangatiratanga, and the mātauranga of our tīpuna, we will lead our people toward climate resilience.

Name: Qirab – قِرَاب
Description:
يهدفُ مشروع «قِرَاب» إلى دعم عمليَّة حفظ المخطوطات في المجال الثَّقافى الإسلامى؛ من خلال تبادل المعرفة والتكنولوجيا المفتوحة المصدر. ونحن من خلال هذا المشروع نعملُ على نشرِ ترجمات عربيَّة للنصوص والمعايير الفنية، وإعداد تصميمات مبتكرة من الأدوات والبرامج الحاسوبيَّة.

Qirab is dedicated to supporting the preservation of manuscripts within the Islamic cultural sphere through knowledge sharing and open source technology. We publish Arabic translations of technical texts and standards as well as produce innovative hardware and software designs.

Name: We For Biodiversity Conservation, Katari
Description: We for Biodiversity Conservation Katari is an Eco-Conservation organization, establish by students of the College of Natural Resource Management, Katari, committed to generating awareness about biodiversity conservation. The group actively engages students and the local community in initiatives such as afforestation, wildlife protection, clean-up drives, and sustainable resource management. Through research, workshops, and outreach programs, it promotes environmental responsibility and encourages proactive participation in safeguarding the region’s rich biodiversity for future generations.

Name: Utah Water Ways
Description: Established through HB 307 (2023 GS), Utah Water Ways is a 501(c)(3) public-private partnership dedicated to facilitating coordination of efforts within Utah to optimize the use of the state’s water resources. Our board is composed of various state agency officials, regional water conservancy district representatives, and business leaders from across the state — each dedicated to promoting the proper use of water and educating citizens and businesses alike regarding efficient water use in the state, in an effort to meet the State’s water conservation goals.

Name: Grupo de Ecología y Conservación de Islas, A.C.
Description: Grupo de Ecología y Conservación de Islas, A.C. es una asociación civil cuyo objetivo es la restauración integral de las islas de México y la conservación de sus especies.  Ensenada, México.

Name: Wolves and Humans Foundation
Description:
The Wolves and Humans Foundation is a UK based charity dedicated to the conservation of Europe’s large carnivores - the wolf, brown bear and lynx.

Wolves and Humans works with people and local communities to find practical solutions to co-existence with wolves and other large carnivores, helping their long-term conservation. 

Working in partnership with local biologists and conservationists in their home countries, Wolves and Humans provides resources, education and training, with the aim of restoring traditional methods of protecting livestock and preventing attacks by predators.

We also carry out monitoring of large carnivores to establish accurate population sizes, as official figures are often overestimated, and carries out research to assess their impact on wild prey species and domestic livestock, and how large carnivores live in areas affected by human activity, helping to dispel misconceptions and provide a sound basis for future conservation and management decisions.

Wolves and Humans works with educators to provide educational materials and give lectures and workshops for adults and children about predators, the natural environment in which they live, and the benefits to people of the continued existence of healthy ecosystems.

Name: Tree Uganda Academy (TUA)
Description: Established in 2015, Tree Uganda Academy (TUA) is a regeneration eco-inclusive social action research and advocacy youth led conservation and Agriculture Organisation to aid and philanthropy Community led conservation and poverty alleviation. The Organisation also catalogs and helps young people and communities examine what factors limit their potential for achievement, independence and meaningful contribution to nature conservation and national development. Rather than protecting people from nature, we look for practical solutions that allow humans and other species to thrive together.

Name: Fédération de la Haute-Vienne pour la Pêche et la Protection du Milieu Aquatique
Description: La Fédération de la Haute-Vienne pour la Pêche et la Protection du Milieu Aquatique est un acteur clé de la préservation des écosystèmes aquatiques et de la promotion de la pêche responsable dans le département. Située à Limoges, la Fédération regroupe des associations locales et œuvre pour la protection des cours d'eau, des plans d'eau, et de la faune aquatique. À travers des actions de sensibilisation, d'éducation à l'environnement, et de gestion durable des ressources, elle veille à concilier respect de la biodiversité et pratique de la pêche de loisir. La Fédération s'engage également dans des projets de restauration des milieux naturels et participe activement à la surveillance de la qualité des eaux et à la préservation des espèces emblématiques locales.

Name: EPAB - Etablissement public de gestion et d'aménagement de la baie de Douarnenez
Description:
L'Epab est un syndicat mixte visant à faciliter la gestion équilibrée de la ressource en eau, la prévention des inondations, ainsi que la préservation et la gestion des zones humides, conformément à l’article L213-12 du code de l’environnement, incluant la problématique liée aux algues vertes et celle liée aux milieux aquatiques.

Créé en 2012, il est reconnu établissement public territorial de bassin, EPTB, en 2014.

C’est la structure porteuse du SAGE (Schéma d’Aménagement et de Gestion de l’Eau) de la baie de Douarnenez.

Name: WildComm Research Stations
Description:
We envision a world in which conservation volunteering is not for profit but fills a real need in providing knowledge, connecting habitats, and giving landowners outside protected areas a reason to value their natural environment and co-exist with wildlife. 

Our mission is to provide affordable opportunities to dedicated conservation volunteers that will, by working collaboratively and alongside landowners outside protected areas, bridge knowledge gaps, foster habitat connectivity and cultivate a shared appreciation for the natural environment. 

We are doing this by establishing research satellite sites and placing volunteers on privately owned land, where wildlife research and conservation can have a big impact.

Name: Eco-Conscious Life
Description: Eco-Conscious Life is a conservation family from Rwanda, committed to change the world a better place than it was through discovering new places. As days go by, it needs everyone’s concern to consider future life of the planet. We save biodiversity _not for me nor for you_ for the future generations through traveling and creating a better home in all corners of the world.

Name: Glossy Black Conservancy
Description:
Working together to conserve the Glossy Black-Cockatoo and its habitat.

The Glossy Black Conservancy is an independent conservation and research association that provides resources, training and support to the broader community to help identify and learn about the charismatic Glossy Black-Cockatoo.

Name: Projet Obligations Bleues - Blue Bonds Gabon
Diploma en Conservación, Salud y Bienestar de Primates UCM
Description:
Diploma de Especialización en Conservación, Salud y Bienestar de Primates en la Universidad Complutense de Madrid.


El objetivo del programa es capacitar a profesionales de diversas disciplinas en los principios fundamentales de la salud, el bienestar y el manejo de primates, aplicables tanto en proyectos de conservación in situ como ex situ.

Name: Hutan Harimau
Description:
Founded in Padang, Sumatra in 2024, Hutan Harimau was legitimated based on the Indonesian Ministry of Justice and Human Rights Act number: AHU-0013482.AH.01.04.Tahun 2024.
Hutan is the Indonesian word for Forest and Harimau is the Indonesian word for Tiger. Hutan Harimau is the only organization focused exclusively on the conservation of wild Sumatran tigers and their rainforest habitats.
Hutan Harimau’s mission is to function as the leading Sumatran tiger-focused organization. Hutan Harimau works in partnership with government agencies, local communities, universities, and non-governmental organizations to better research and conserve Sumatran tigers.
Hutan Harimau’s vision is to maintain tiger populations, with a focus on the recovery of the species outside National Parks.

Name: Green Industry GmbH
Description:
Protecting rainforest. Preserving the future.
Green Industry connects industrial responsibility with real, measurable environmental impact.
In the Llano Bonito Nature Reserve in Costa Rica, we protect and reforest more than 65 hectares of rainforest – transparently, sustainably, and with proven results.

We believe:
Industry leaves traces.
Responsibility starts where we acknowledge them.
That’s why we give back to nature – tree by tree, hectare by hectare.

What we do
• Rainforest protection & reforestation
• Restoration of degraded land
• Sustainable jobs for local communities
• Monitoring, planting plans & impact reporting
• Symbolic rainforest shares for individuals and companies (incl. certificate & updates)

For companies
CSR with substance:
• Corporate rainforest shares from 500 m²+
• Impact reports & transparent documentation
• Custom branding landing page & communication package
• Employer branding through real responsibility

Our principles
Hope over guilt.
Impact over slogans.
Transparency over greenwashing.

We don’t just protect trees.
We protect life.

Name: Celebración Áreas Protegidas y Conservadas LAC
Description: Integrad@s por las Áreas Protegidas, Conservadas y su gente - 17 de octubre

Name: Bird Safe Campus Summit
Description: Bird Safe Campus Summit is the first of its kind virtual meeting to share breakthroughs, best practices, and create a lasting network for collaboration to make all of our campuses safer for birds!

Name: Toledo Institute for Development and Environment (TIDE)
Description: The Toledo Institute for Development and Environment (TIDE) was established in 1997 by concerned community members to combat illegal fishing and illegal harvesting of the West Indian Manatee. TIDE has grown to manage 3 Protected Areas and employs over 30 full time staff and many community stakeholders. TIDE hopes to continue to advance community participation in conservation for the benefit of all Belizeans and the region as a whole.

Name: Pacific Whale Fund
Description: The Pacific Whale Fund is launching soon…

Name: The Forest Collective
Description: At The Forest Collective we envision a world where forests and wildlife are valued and protected to ensure the future of Earth’s biological diversity and human wellbeing. We initiate and seek partnerships with experts, civil society organizations, government agencies, artists, and local communities to facilitate conservation of threatened forest ecosystems and species. We prioritize scientific research, education and outreach, capacity building, and collaborative and community-centered conservation.

Name: Heol Expedition
Description: Heol Expedition est une association dédiée à la réalisation d’expéditions scientifiques à la voile, engagée pour l'étude et la préservation de l'océan. Elle mène des recherches sur la biodiversité marine et sensibilise le public aux enjeux de la protection de l’environnement, notamment à travers l’observation des cétacés.

Name: Sahtú Renewable Resources Board
Description:
The SRRB considers its mandate as defined in the Sahtu Dene Metis Comprehensive Land Claim Agreement to encompass two key areas of work:
•	Supporting healthy ecological and social relationships among people, wildlife and habitat
•	Recognizing and encouraging Dene ts’ı̨lı̨ (ways of life) and the role of Sahtú Dene and Métis as stewards of wildlife and habitat for the present and future.

Name: RECAP : Capacités de recyclage des plastiques en Tunisie et au Sénégal
Description: RECAP est un projet de coopération internationale financé par la Chancellerie Bavaroise mis en oeuvre par bbw International en Tunisie et au Sénégal.

Name: Amazon Carbon
Description: Somos una empresa peruana apasionada por la naturaleza, especializada en la conservación de la Amazonía peruana a través de un innovador sistema de negocios centrado en la silvicultura y la generación de bonos de carbono.

Name: Florida Water Star
Description:
Florida Water Star is a certification program for homes and commercial buildings that use less water in landscapes, irrigation systems and indoors.

A Florida Water Star-certified home saves money, water and benefits Florida’s environment.

Name: National Centre for Genetic Resources and Biotechnology ( NACGRAB)
Description:
NACGRAB is the focal point on conservation and use of genetic resources in Nigeria. NACGRAB is under the National Biotechnology Development Agency in the Federal Ministry of Innovation Science and Technology.

Our research mandate includes
•  Exploration, collection, identification, evaluation, characterization, storage and conservation of rich stock of both animal and plant germplasm materials.

•  Acquisition, maintenance, utilization, exchange and dissemination of information on genetic materials of plants, animals and microbes.

•  National coordination of genetic resources programme and sustainable utilization.

•  Fostering relationship with other national satellite genetic research centers located in Research Institutes, Universities and Polytechnics as well as other International Organizations and Centres on programmes concerning genetic resources and biotechnology application.

•  Coordinating the activities of the National Committee on Naming, Registration and Release of Crop Varieties, Livestock Breeds and Fisheries.

•  Arresting rapid erosion and loss of the country's crop and animal genetic resources caused by cultivation, urbanization rural development, grazing, desertification, pest outbreak, national catastrophes etc.

•  Documenting appropriately the germplasm stocks held by the Centre, research institutes and relevant organizations.

The Programmes of the Centre include:
•  Conservation, preservation and maintenance of valuable plant and animal genetic resources, for immediate utilization and posterity.

•  Development of animal genetic resources (poultry, snailry, fisheries etc).

•  Application of tissue culture for plant conservation and overall agricultural development.

•  Servicing of the activities of the National Committee on Naming, Registration and Release of Crops Varieties, Livestock Breeds and Fisheries.

•  Networking and coordinating activities in the development of capacities in plant and animal genetic resources.

Name: The WAVE Coalition
Description: The WAVE Coalition is a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing animal welfare, environmental conservation, and STEM education through hands-on research, advocacy, community outreach, and innovative educational programs that empower youth and engage the public.

Name: Recicla RN
Description: A Recicla proporciona a redução dos impactos ambientais causados por resíduos e rejeitos despejados na natureza, principalmente alumínio, que é o material reciclado mais consumido no mundo, cujas aplicações crescem continuamente. O Brasil é atualmente um dos maiores recicladores de latas de alumínio do mundo, com 98% delas recicladas, e a Recicla acompanha esse crescimento investindo massivamente em seus colaboradores e em tecnologia para que a empresa acompanhe as tendências de mercado.
Location: São Gonçalo do Amarante, Rio Grande do Norte

Name: Waterberg Rhino UK
Description: Waterberg Rhino is a UK-based charity that funds conservation efforts to protect rhino in South Africa and support the local Waterberg community.

Name: Jardín Botánico de Quito
Description:
El Jardín Botánico de Quito (JBQ) es un centro vivo de conservación, investigación y educación ambiental ubicado en el corazón de la ciudad, dentro del Parque La Carolina. Somos custodios de la biodiversidad andina y ecuatoriana, y trabajamos para proteger, estudiar y difundir el patrimonio vegetal del país, acercando la naturaleza a la ciudadanía.

Nuestro espacio reúne una gran variedad de ecosistemas representativos del Ecuador: humedales altoandinos, bosque nublado, páramo, zonas áridas, jardines de plantas nativas, jardines etnobotánicos, colecciones medicinales, áreas ornamentales, senderos, lagunas, cascadas y zonas de descanso. Entre nuestros principales atractivos se encuentran dos orquidearios con especies de altura y tropicales, bromelias, helechos y colecciones botánicas únicas. El JBQ es reconocido como un laboratorio vivo donde la ciencia, la conservación y la educación se encuentran con la ciudad.

Nuestra misión es promover la protección de la flora nativa, la generación de conocimiento científico y la educación ambiental. Recibimos estudiantes, investigadores, instituciones aliadas y público general interesado en biodiversidad y sostenibilidad. A través de programas educativos, visitas guiadas, voluntariado y actividades culturales, fortalecemos la conciencia ambiental y fomentamos la conexión con la naturaleza.

Además de nuestro rol científico y educativo, ofrecemos espacios para eventos, proyectos corporativos y alianzas institucionales. Somos un entorno natural accesible, seguro y céntrico, ideal para actividades culturales, empresariales y de responsabilidad social. Cada alianza nos permite avanzar en restauración ecológica, investigación, educación y manejo de colecciones vivas.

El Jardín Botánico de Quito es un puente entre naturaleza y ciudad. Aquí florecen proyectos, ideas y vínculos que construyen futuro. Te invitamos a conocernos, colaborar y crecer juntos en un espacio verde comprometido con la conservación y la sostenibilidad.

Name: Participatory Conservation of Great bustard in Bukan-Iran (UNDP/SGP/GEF Project)
Description:
The main goal of the project is to prevent the reduction of the population of Great bstards in Bukan habitats so that the population can live and survive by relying on urgent management measures with the participation of the local community, especially farmers and ranchers. 
In this page, the activities and efforts made for Participatory Conservation of Great bustard in during the implementation of this project are reviewed.

Name: Branch Bounty Farm
Description:
Branch Bounty: Where Nature Thrives and Taste Buds Delight
 
Nestled in the heart of far north Queensland, Australia, Branch Bounty is more than just a farm – it's a haven for sustainable tropical agriculture and a vibrant community hub.  Free from the harmful practices of chemical sprays and monocultures, we cultivate our products in harmony with nature, nurturing the land with respect and care.  
Branch Bounty is where passion for the earth meets a passion for flavor.  We offer unique, high-quality produce to fine food outlets, showcasing the exceptional taste and quality that comes from truly sustainable practices.  
Beyond our delicious bounty, we're dedicated to shaping a brighter future.  Our farm is a living classroom, empowering rural youth to become the next generation of environmentally conscious farmers.  We provide hands-on training in ecology appreciation, equipping them with the skills and knowledge to cultivate a sustainable future for themselves and their communities. 

Run in partnership by two scientists Dr. Benjamin Robotham and Dr. Tina Koutouleas. The farm is our R&D playground. We are testing out new rainforest and reef specialty products that can be grown together with nature instead of against it.
Discover the goodness of the rainforest and contact us for your free B2B Australian Native samples.

Name: Asociación de Conservación de Aguajales y Renacales del Alto Mayo - ADECARAM Tingana
Description: La Asociación de Conservación de Aguajales y Renacales del Alto Mayo (ADECARAM), es una organización sin fines de lucro, creada en 2004, especializada en la gestión de los humedales del Alto Mayo; a través de estrategias de gobernanza, fortalecimiento de capacidades, adaptación al cambio climático y promoción de alternativas económicas sostenibles, contribuyendo a mejorar la calidad de vida de la población local y al equilibrio ecosistémico.

Name: Purple Martin Conservation Association
Description:
The Purple Martin Conservation Association is the largest conservation organization devoted to Purple Martins, with over thirty years leading research projects that study and protect the species. PMCA members are part of a dedicated community of individuals who realize the future of the Purple Martin is in our hands.

The PMCA has been banding Purple Martins since 1987, providing an extensive database for further study. In addition, we have been involved with state-of-the-art research using geolocator and GPS data-loggers to collect invaluable information on migratory pathways. Our biologists also design and test products that allow people to become successful landlords.

We invite you to join our community! Visit www.purplemartin.org to become a member today! Together we can work to "ensure the future of Purple Martins."

The Purple Martin Conservation Association is a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt charitable organization headquartered in the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (USA).

Name: Rebel Restoration
Description: Rebel Restoration partners with purpose-driven businesses to deliver tangible climate and biodiversity impact across the UK. Our mission is to make it simple, measurable, and meaningful for companies to amplify nature recovery by connecting their support with our curated portfolio of high-quality, grassroots-led restoration projects. Through these projects, we help businesses remove carbon, restore ecosystems, and empower local communities—creating visible impact near their operations and real value for their brand, people, and the planet. Together, we’re redefining conservation as a strategic, and inspiring part of doing business.

Name: Harbour Oysters
Description: We are, Lottie and Poppy, two sisters using native oysters to improve water quality and biodiversity. Through installing baskets of oysters in harbours and marinas we allow nature to use its powerful forces for good; oysters naturally remove contaminates from the waters and support the re establishment of clean and biodiverse ecosystems.

Name: Naturpark Schwalm-Nette
Description:
Der 1965 gegründete Naturpark Schwalm-Nette liegt im äußersten Westen Nordrhein-Westfalens an der niederländischen Grenze und erstreckt sich mit 435 qkm vom Kreis Kleve über die Kreise Viersen, Heinsberg und einem Teil der Stadt Mönchengladbach. Seit 1976 ist er Teil des deutsch-niederländischen Naturparks Maas-Schwalm-Nette.
Die typisch niederrheinische Landschaft des Naturparks ist vom Wasser geprägt. Seen, Flüsse, Bäche und Bruchflächen laden zum Erleben und vielfältigen Aktivitäten ein. Urtypische Wälder, Weiden, Ackerflure und Heidelandschaften bieten eindrucksvolle Erlebnisse und Erholung in der Natur.

Name: WildCare Oklahoma
Description:
WildCare Oklahoma is a nationally recognized nonprofit organization that provides wildlife and conservation education, technical assistance in resolving human/animal conflicts, medical and rehabilitative care to ill, injured, and orphaned wildlife, and members of the public a place to bring native wildlife struggling to survive with the goal of releasing healthy individuals back to nature.

WildCare admits and treats over 7,500 wild animals of over 240 different species annually.  Most of our wild patients have been injured or orphaned as a result of human activity as they try to cope with increasing urbanization of their natural habitats.  Common reasons for admission include being hit by cars, attacked by cats and dogs, shot, window collisions, poisoning, yard work accidents, and unnecessary trapping.  WildCare has 250-850 patients in care at any given time and is open to admit new patients 365 days a year.

WildCare is supported entirely by donations from caring individuals like you. Making a contribution will help ensure that there will always be a safe place for Oklahomans to bring wildlife in need.

Name: Butte County Resource Conservation District
Description: Butte County Resource Conservation District is a special district that help private landowners protect soil, water, wildlife habitat, and other natural resources.  We are here to "fill in the gaps" of conservation.  We help the rancher who needs to recover from wildfire, the farmer who wants to install money-saving irrigation, and even the large public agency seeking short-term help to do a specialized GIS project or a botanical survey.  Bottom line: If it's resource-related and you could use help, call us.

Name: One Ocean Diving
Description:
Marine Research. Shark Conservation. Ocean Education.
Empowering coexistence through science, safety, and respect for marine life.
Snorkel with sharks & learn from marine biologists.
Book online 24-7 OneOceanDiving.com

Name: Skol Endro
Description:
Association étudiante de l'Icam de  Vannes
Nos principales actions:
- Vente de panier légumes hebdomadaires
- Organisation de Clean-walk
- Sensibilisation auprès des étudiants

Name: Association DURAMEN
Description:
Duramen est une association d'intérêt général basée en région Centre-Val de Loire.
Elle permet aux entreprises ou particuliers d’agir concrètement pour l’environnement en finançant des plantations en forêt.
Vous êtes un particulier, un professionnel ou un forestier ?
Vous souhaitez vous investir activement dans le reboisement et le développement durable des forêts ?
Duramen et son mécénat environnemental sont là pour vous !
Venez découvrir l'association !

https://www.facebook.com/duramen.planterdesarbres/  
https://twitter.com/AssoDuramen
www.duramen.org
https://www.bulbintown.com/projects/duramen-tous-concernes-par-lavenir-de-nos-forets

Name: Fundación Centro de Rescate James Brown
Description: Trabajamos incansablemente en la protección, conservación y rehabilitación de la fauna silvestre. Atendemos animales rescatados por entes reguladores como el Ministerio de Ambiente, la Policía Ambiental, los Bomberos, y ciudadanos que los reportan. Brindamos atención médica especializada, los preparamos para regresar a su hábitat y realizamos liberaciones responsables. Además, desarrollamos investigaciones científicas, utilizamos tecnología avanzada para monitoreo, y llevamos a cabo programas educativos que sensibilizan sobre la importancia de preservar la biodiversidad. Cada esfuerzo busca construir un futuro sostenible para las especies y su entorno.

Name: Mulala Marine Sanctuary
Description:
Welcome to Mulala Marine Sanctuary! 

 Website: www.mulalamarinesanctuary.com

Located in the breathtaking coastal town of Nacala, Mozambique, Mulala Marine Sanctuary (MMS) stands as a beacon of conservation and sustainability in the realm of marine protection. Our mission is crystal clear: to preserve, protect, and restore the rich biodiversity thriving beneath the waves.

Covering expansive stretches of unspoiled coastline, Mulala Marine Sanctuary is a sanctuary for a diverse array of marine life. From vibrant coral reefs to majestic sea turtles, from magnificent whales to a kaleidoscope of colorful fish species, our sanctuary is home to a wealth of underwater wonders.

At MMS, we are committed to leveraging scientific research, implementing sustainable management practices, and fostering community engagement to safeguard our marine treasures. We understand the delicate balance between human activities and the marine ecosystem, and strive to maintain this equilibrium through responsible stewardship.

Our approach emphasizes the importance of sustainable fishing practices, eco-conscious tourism initiatives, and comprehensive environmental education programs. By empowering local communities, educating visitors, and collaborating with stakeholders, we aim to ensure the long-term vitality of Mulala Marine Sanctuary for generations to come.

Join us in our mission to protect and preserve our oceans. Together, we can make a difference.

Connect with us today to learn more about Mulala Marine Sanctuary and how you can support our conservation efforts!

Name: Echoes of the Okavango
Description:
Echoes of the Okavango is a unique kayak expedition through one of Africa’s most breathtaking and ecologically significant landscapes—the Okavango Delta in Botswana. In Summer 2025, our team will embark on a 300-kilometer journey, paddling through pristine waterways, encountering iconic wildlife, and engaging with local communities.

 

But this is more than an adventure. It’s a mission to explore, document, and protect the Delta while raising awareness about the urgent impacts of climate change on this fragile ecosystem.

Our Story
In 2023, Jay Frain attended the premier of a new film 'Into the Okavango' by Ollie Pemberton. Having never heard of, nor seen the delta before, Jay was mesmerised by the beauty of the Okavango and walked away with a dream to one day visit.

A year of wondering later, Jay decided to make his dream a reality. Thus planning for the Echoes of the Okavango expedition began in November 2024.

Our Mission
Our mission is to explore the Okavango Delta by kayak, capturing its natural beauty and unique ecosystem to raise awareness of the urgent impacts of climate change on this critical region.

Through creative storytelling, we will share the Delta's story with the world to deepen understanding of its environmental and cultural significance and inspire action to protect it for future generations. As a dedicated team, we will contribute to citizen science and creative projects to highlight the need to preserve the delta.

The Expedition
The team will take to the waters of the Okavango, starting at the small settlement of Sepapa before undertaking a 300km journey through some of Africa's most remote and untamed landscapes.

The expedition will take 14days of paddling to complete, with regular stops to complete our filming, photography and citizen science projects as well as speaking to the local people about their experiences in the delta.

The Delta is home to Africa’s most iconic wildlife—elephants that roam the floodplains, hippos lurking

Name: baselineSHIFT
Description:
Biological monitoring | Ecological restoration | Habitat creation |
Invasive plant management | Ecological research

Name: Mivule Forest Conservancy
Description:
Mivule Forest Conservancy puts forth efforts in advancing nature-based climate protection interventions for biodiversity conservation and sustainable development.
OUR PHILOSOPHY: 
For a multi-functional forest-society nexus, forests of the future ought to be socially embedded and communities of the future ought to be forest-conservation sensitive and responsive. Forests of the future need to bear social and ecological consciousness, that promote interspecies, multispecies, and multi-functional management

Name: Global Wildlife Fund
Description: Australian charity supporting effective conservation projects in Africa, South America and Asia-Pacific

Name: African Organization For Sustainable Development (AOSD)
Description: Cette association présente dans plus de 20 pays du monde s'engage de manière passionnée et concrète dans la promotion d'un avenir durable. En se concentrant sur des initiatives à la fois sociales et environnementales, elle œuvre à la création et à la mise en œuvre de projets qui bénéficient à la fois aux communautés locales et à la préservation de notre environnement. Son action combine intelligemment des projets sociaux visant à améliorer les conditions de vie des populations avec des initiatives de protection de l'environnement, assurant ainsi un impact positif à long terme pour les générations actuelles et futures.

Name: Maison du Salève
Description: Entre Genève et Annecy, la Maison du Salève vous accueille dans un cadre champêtre sur la commune de Présilly, au pied du Salève. Lieu culturel vivant, la Maison vous propose de visiter ses expositions et de découvrir le Salève par le biais d'animations nature, de visites guidées, de balades et de conférences. Venez nous retrouver en famille ou entre amis et ressourcez-vous en pleine nature ! À très bientôt.

Name: Cabalgatas Puebla
Description:
Preservamos la identidad de nuestra tierra!
La mejor manera de comprender nuestra cultura es a la silla de un caballo. Nosotros luchamos por un México que se extingue, por una identidad heredada de nuestros padres y abuelos. Somos una de las últimas generaciones que viven del campo y que mantienen la conexión con la naturaleza.
Todas las aportaciones de los visitantes son destinadas a la manutención de los más de 10 caballos rehabilitados de nuestra cuadra.

Name: Fondation Pierre Vérots
Description: Fondation Pierre Vérots is a company based out of 261 Praillebard, Saint-Jean-de-Thurigneux, France.

Name: Ducks Unlimited, Inc. - Colorado Field Office
Description: Ducks Unlimited conserves, restores, and manages wetlands and associated habitats for North America's waterfowl. These habitats also benefit other wildlife and people.

Name: Heart of Texas Conservancy
Description: CIBOLO CONSERVANCY is an environmental services company based out of 140 CITY PARK RD, BOERNE, Texas, United States.

Name: REWATER-D
Description: Rewater-D (Reuse Water Dehumidifier) es una proyecto de sostenibilidad que busca aportar una solución a la problemática de agua que vivimos actualmente y así contribuir con las ODS 6 y 15
