Description: 𝑷𝒓𝒐𝒎𝒐𝒖𝒗𝒐𝒊𝒓 𝒍𝒆 𝒅𝒆́𝒗𝒆𝒍𝒐𝒑𝒑𝒆𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝒅𝒖𝒓𝒂𝒃𝒍𝒆 𝒑𝒂𝒓 𝒍𝒂 𝒑𝒓𝒊𝒔𝒆 𝒆𝒏 𝒄𝒐𝒎𝒑𝒕𝒆 𝒅𝒆𝒔 𝒑𝒓𝒆́𝒐𝒄𝒄𝒖𝒑𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏𝒔 𝒆𝒏𝒗𝒊𝒓𝒐𝒏𝒏𝒆𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕𝒂𝒍𝒆𝒔 𝒆𝒕 𝒔𝒐𝒄𝒊𝒂𝒍𝒆𝒔 𝒅𝒆𝒔 𝒑𝒐𝒑𝒖𝒍𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏𝒔 𝒅𝒂𝒏𝒔 𝒍𝒆𝒔 𝒂𝒄𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏𝒔 𝒅𝒆 𝒅𝒆́𝒗𝒆𝒍𝒐𝒑𝒑𝒆𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕.

Name: Ocelot Working Group
Description:
The OWG aims to promote the conservation of ocelots, margays and jaguarundis throughout their distribution range. We are a global network of conservationists, researchers, and designers committed to preserving these small wild cats.

https://linktr.ee/ocelotworkinggroup

Name: Bass Lakes Area Environmental Partnership
Description:
The $60,000 goal of our fundraising campaign is to buy a DASH boat that will be used to reduce/eliminate the amount of chemicals put in our lakes each year to control Eurasian Water Milfoil (milfoil). Milfoil is an aggressive aquatic invasive species that grows in dense mats impeding light from reaching native aquatic plants. It can also choke out native plants by creating dense monocultures. These monocultures can hinder fish populations by limiting their habitat, food sources and hiding areas.  Milfoil also obstructs water recreation like swimming, boating, kayaking, fishing and water skiing.

Please consider making a donation today!

The Bass Lakes Area Environmental Partnership was created in 2021 by riparians from Big Bass and Little Bass Lakes in Irons, Michigan committed to education and the control and prevention of aquatic invasive species through innovation and cutting-edge science. Our mission is to promote public awareness, forge partnerships among citizens, scientists, and professionals, and foster the protection of the lakes' ecosystems for today and for  generations to come.

The founding principles of the Bass Lakes Area Environmental Partnership are:
*Provide education about the lake and shoreline ecosystems
*Prevent and combat the spread of invasive species in the lake and shoreline ecosystems
*Solicit grants and donations in order to take actions to prevent the spread of invasive species and to pay for educational programs
*Other charitable efforts related to lake and shoreline ecosystem health in the area

Name: Sustainable Hope In Real Action Foundation (SHIRA)
Description: Sustainable Hope In Real Action (SHIRA) is a youth led foundation focused on mitigating human-environmental conflicts. Connecting people from around the world, SHIRA emphasizes sustainable community development, coexistence, and the principles of One Health. In the face of climate change, SHIRA believes that by re-establishing relationships with the environment and wildlife, that we will create and establish climate resilient communities.

Name: Johnson County Conservation
Description:
Johnson County Conservation Board Vision:
The Vision of the Johnson County Conservation Board and Staff is a county where all citizens have access to natural areas, conservation/environmental education, and recreation opportunities; where the natural resources of the county are enhanced and protected; where conservation areas are restored and maintained at the highest standard, are safe, and sustain our mission of land conservation; and where this vision is accomplished through partnerships with various entities, interest groups, and individuals.

Johnson County Conservation Board Mission:
The Mission of the Johnson County Conservation Board is to improve the quality of natural resources and native species for Johnson County residents through programs emphasizing conservation of natural resources, conservation education, and natural resource based recreation.

Name: Wild Islands
Description:
Explore wildlife on islands.

Our mission is to create a nature positive world. By leveraging the power of people and the business community we tell stories of wildlife on islands.

We have three core aims;

1 - Highlight islands as critical centres of biodiversity.
2 - Share knowledge of nature; teach care and understanding.
3-  Inspire Island action; empower people to restore nature.

Our mission is to create a nature positive world. By leveraging the power of individuals and business community we tell stories of wildlife on islands.

Conservation Optimism is the central tenet that informs every action Wild Islands takes.

Name: Tū Mai Taonga
Description:
The Tū Mai Taonga Project is proceeding under the leadership, guiding vision, and tikanga of Ngāti Rehua Ngātiwai ki Aotea.

It has been developed through the mahi and engagement of many parties: first encouraged by the Aotea Conservation Park Advisory Committee and developed and promoted by the Aotea Great Barrier Environmental Trust with support from island sanctuaries and community groups. It is funded by Auckland Council, Foundation North, the Department of Conservation Jobs for Nature - Mahi mō te Taiao programme, and through Predator Free 2050 Limited.

The project aims to protect and restore native species and ecosystems through feral cat removal and intensified rat control, initially in the Aotea Conservation Park and Northern Aotea area. 

It plans to prove landscape-scale operational methodologies and build community support that open pathways to a predator free Aotea.
Location: Aotea/Great Barrier Local Board Area, Auckland

Name: Fédération de Meurthe-et-Moselle pour la Pêche et la Protection du Milieu Aquatique
Description:
Association agréée au titre de la protection de l'environnement, chargée de par la loi, de missions d'intérêt général et à caractère d'établissement d'utilité publique.
Fédération regroupant les 25 associations agréées du département, leur apportant soutien technique, administratif et financier pour mener à bien l'ensemble des missions du réseau.

Name: National Wild Pig Task Force
Description: The National Wild Pig Task Force (NWPTF) is a technical, scientific, and leadership alliance of state, federal, provincial, and private conservation partners working to reduce and eventually eradicate free-ranging populations of wild pigs in North America. Management of invasive wild pigs has arguably become one of the greatest wildlife management challenges facing natural resource professionals and landowners.  The damage these animals cause to forestry, agriculture, and natural resources throughout North America has been tremendous and is often measured in billions of dollars of damage each year.  Membership of the NWPTF consists of natural resource professionals employed by a state, provincial or federal agency, conservation group, or private company or is self-employed that has an interest in the reduction of wild pig populations.  The goals of the NWPTF are to provide national leadership and a collective voice for science-based control, damage reduction, and/or eradication of wild pigs, provide a forum for the exchange of information among the natural resource management field and among all relevant stakeholder groups.

Name: Fondation estrienne en environnement
Description:
Fondée en 1991, la Fondation estrienne en environnement (FEE) a pour objectif de favoriser la sensibilisation de la population sur les gestes concrets à poser pour assurer le développement durable et de récolter des fonds pour des projets environnementaux dans la grande région des Cantons-de-l’Est.

En plus de contribuer au développement durable de la région, la FEE soutient les activités du Conseil régional de l’environnement de l’Estrie ainsi que différents organismes qui développent des activités d’éducation et de sensibilisation dans les Cantons-de-l’Est, principalement auprès des jeunes.

Name: The Conservation Conversation
Description: The Conservation Conversation (TCC) is a communications and educational organization dedicated to environmental advocacy, sustainable development, and conservation leadership. Through powerful storytelling, innovative campaigns, and strategic partnerships, we empower individuals, businesses, and governments to take meaningful action for our planet.

Name: Association BLUE lingua
The Axolotarium at ASTATE
Description: We are a multidisciplinary student lead-initiative of 50+ plus students at Arkansas State University Campus Querétaro dedicated to the conservation of the Axolotl.

Name: Wetlandplan
Description: Met het Wetlandplan zal Natuurpunt tegen 2027 bijna 1.500 hectare wetlands herstellen in Vlaanderen. Wetlands - of waterrijke natuurgebieden - zijn uitstekende natuurlijke klimaatbuffers die beschermen tegen overstromingen en droogte. Natuurpunt koos 18 van haar eigen natuurgebieden uit om dit te realiseren, van de Kust tot de Kempen. Het plan zal worden gerealiseerd met Europese, Vlaamse en private middelen.

Name: Bleen.earth
Description:
Bleen is a Nature Intelligence company developing decision-support solutions for environmental risk management.

We design digital twins of ecosystems to understand living systems dynamics, anticipate ecological impacts, and guide effective conservation and restoration actions.

Grounded in science, predictive modeling, and field expertise, our technology supports public decision-makers, businesses, and local stakeholders in reducing risks, optimizing costs, and securing investments, while strengthening ecosystem resilience.

Protecting nature should no longer be a gamble, but an informed decision.Bleen was founded with a clear ambition: to use technology to durably protect living systems and turn environmental conservation into measurable, effective, and actionable decisions.

Name: Pit-Track K9 Conservation and Anti-Poaching Unit
Description:
Preservation & protection of water, flora & fauna with prioritisation of endangered species! 

The Pit-Track K9 Conservation & Anti-Poaching Unit is a registered Non-Profit Company made up of highly skilled, passionate and dedicated operators, members and corporate affiliates, who have devoted our lives to the protection of wildlife.

Pit-Track's current projects focus on the in-field protection of endangered species such as lion, leopard, pangolin, elephant, and rhino, as well as the detection of contraband and illegal wildlife products. It is our belief that the biggest threat our wildlife faces today is the thought that someone else will save them. We are not waiting for others. Our team is working tirelessly, both in training and on deployments, and is proud to be undertaking critically important initiatives.

Our field rangers are DH qualified in the art of human scent tracking and our DH 5 accredited substance detection handlers are all well equipped with knowledge and in field experience to be able to fulfill any function within a dangerous game area. Our unit is deployed in various high-risk key areas on the protection of endangered species and has a belief that there is strength in unity. 

Our team includes over 30 K9s, including contraband detection K9s, human scent tracking K9s who work as a pack, and patrol and protection K9s, making our unit one of the most diverse in industry. Our K9s are a vitally important part of our anti-poaching unit. They are our eyes and ears in the bush and perform a role that cannot be performed by humans. They are also a big part of our family; our best friends who outrank all other operators.

Name: Cultreevate
Description: Cultreevate advises commercial forest owners on developing voluntary carbon projects with internationally recognised registries such as VERRA. By generating premium carbon offsets, we provide forest owners with a realistic and viable alternative to traditional revenue from tree harvesting or forest clearance for other commercial uses.

Name: Coral Academy
Description:
Coral Academy is an outreach programme lead by Dr. Apple Chui, a research assistant professor from the School of Life Sciences, The Chinese University of Hong Kong, with the following objectives: 

To enhance public awareness and knowledge of the rich marine biodiversity of Hong Kong, with a special focus on coral communities and conservation through seminars, coral workshops and lab visits.

To establish a connection between participants and local corals through a first-hand experience of nurturing coral fragments to be used in local coral restoration works.

To promote individual behavioural and lifestyle changes to protect the marine environment.

Name: CIUDAD SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION DISTRICT
Description: The Ciudad Soil and Water Conservation District, a political subdivision of the state of New Mexico, promotes the conservation, improvement and responsible use of the natural resources on the rural and urban lands within its boundaries.

Name: Rio Paraná
Description:
The NGO works on three pillars: education, inspection and recovery

Education
To generate knowledge about the importance of the river as a bioindicator, whether by inspiring research at universities, working together with the press, municipal schools with early childhood education activities, or educational activities with riverside communities. 

Inspection
Acting in partnership with agencies at all levels, environmental police, Public Ministry and environmental secretaries, in addition to the federal environmental agencies IBAMA and ICMBIO. We engage with politicians who support environmental conservation to develop specific laws for recovery and protection. 

Recovery
Participation in initiatives aimed at curbing river pollution and promoting the regeneration of biomes, such as reforestation, sustainable sanitation initiatives, and agroforestry.
Location: Três Lagoas, Mato Grosso do Sul

Name: Somboon Legacy Foundation
Description:
An ethical Hands-Off elephant sanctuary in Kanchanaburi, Thailand

Somboon Legacy Foundation is a Non-Government Organization (NGO) that takes care of old elephants.

Our organization was founded by Khun Kulsak Chotiyaputta, Agnes Tammenga, Diana Harinck and Manouk Maas from the Netherlands on the 13th of March 2019, Thailand's National Elephant Day.

Our purpose is to rescue and rehabilitate elephants from a life of stress and exploitation.

Currently, we are taking care of two beautiful females, Kammoon and Malee. When the covid-19 pandemic improves we hope to give more elephants a well deserved retirement.

We aim to work together with the local community as much as possible.

With our visitors we focus on observing the elephants in their natural environment and giving education in our interactive elephant museum for young & old.

Our goal is to spread the message of truly animal friendly tourism around the world.

To find out more, check out our website!

Name: GeaSphere - Earth Matters
Description:
GeaSphere is a South African ‘Not for profit’ organisation registered in 2003. We campaign for the protection of Southern African water resources and the threatened grassland ecosystem, and oppose the expansion and entrenchment of the industrial timber plantation model (fake forestry).

We are actively engaged in land restoration in the Sudwala Rainforest Valley of Mpumalanga, South Africa.

Name: Whatcom Conservation District
Description:
Our Vision 
We envision a thriving community that protects and benefits from clean and plentiful water, productive working lands, and resilient natural habitats.

Our Mission
We form partnerships with Whatcom County residents and entities to advance resiliency and ecological processes on working lands, residential landscapes, waterways, and open spaces for current and future generations.

Name: Urban Paradise Guild
Description: Our mission is to re-create native habitats in the Urban areas of Miami-Dade County, and to promote sustainable models for healthy living that safeguard, enhance and integrate the human and natural worlds for the benefit of both.

Name: Zweckverband Schaalsee-Landschaft
Description:
Der Zweckverband Schaalsee-Landschaft 
– Wir gestalten Naturschutz entlang der ehemaligen innerdeutschen Grenze.

Die Schaalsee-Landschaft: ein einzigartiger Naturraum geprägt durch seine Vergangenheit. Wo einst die innerdeutsche Grenze zwischen Mecklenburg-Vorpommern und Schleswig-Holstein keinen Platz für Leben zuließ, blüht und zwitschert es nun.

Unmittelbar nach der Wiedervereinigung gegründet, ist der Zweckverband Schaalsee-Landschaft der einzige grenzübergreifend tätige, öffentliche Eigentümer von Naturschutzflächen an der Landesgrenze zwischen Schleswig-Holstein und Mecklenburg-Vorpommern. Seine Aufgabe ist es, die besondere Landschaft mit ihrem Biotopverbundsystem naturschutzfachlich zu bewahren, zu stärken und zu entwickeln. Damit leistet der Zweckverband einen bedeutenden Beitrag zum grenzübergreifenden Arten- und Klimaschutz auf kommunaler Ebene. 

Der Zweckverband Schaalsee-Landschaft setzt sich zusammen aus den drei Landkreisen Herzogtum-Lauenburg, Nordwest-Mecklenburg und Ludwigslust-Parchim sowie der Naturschutzstiftung „World Wide Fund For Nature“ (WWF).

Weitere Informationen zum Zweckverband Schaalsee-Landschaft und seinen Projekten finden Sie unter www.zv-schaalsee.de. 

Webseite:  https://zv-schaalsee.de
Telefon: 04541 / 879 11 60
E-Mail: zv-schaalsee@kreis-rz.de

Name: Tech4wildlife
Description: conservation

Name: Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre Romelia
Description:
Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre (Mixto) Romelia ( its acronym in R.N.V.S.M.R.) is a wildlife refuge in Montezuma on the Nicoya Peninsula. It was created on November 24, 1998, to contribute to the conservation of the Tempisque Conservation Area (A.C.T., from its acronym in Spanish Area de Conservacion Tempisque).

The Refuge stemmed from an initiative of Albert Ingalls and Gitza Gatti, who followed a dream of conservation for land that once was their home and the birthright of their children. The land, now the Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre Romelia (category mix), contributes to the A.C.T. as part of the peninsular biological corridor (Cabo Blanco Absolute Reserve, Curú Mixed Wildlife Reserve, Karen Morgensen Reserve, Nicholas Wessberg Reserve, and the Romelia Mixed Wildlife Refuge). It is adjacent to the Nicholas Wessberg Absolute Reserve, one of the country's first and most valuable protected wild areas.
The land of the R.M.S.V. exudes beauty. It is a beachfront, bathed by rolling Pacific Ocean waves, guarded by iconic sea stacks, and inland nurtured by peaceful rivers and tranquil waterfalls. It is one of the peninsula's last bastions of pristine wilderness. The R.N.V.S.M.R. is visited by tens of thousands of eco-tourists annually.

This Refuge is approximately 224 Ha, 8349 m2 (S.I.N.A.C., 2011). Within this territory are some distinct ecosystems: primary forest, secondary forest, riparian forest, and gallery forest. The avifauna includes more than 57 species of birds and 26 species of herpetofauna, including 13 reptiles and seven amphibians. There are also 26 species of mammals, including 11 types of bats.

The weather is characterized as humid, hot, and scorching. Annual precipitation is about 3,000 mm, distributed from May to December. However, rainfall presents a deficit between July and August during the period known as "Veranillo de San Juan," which lasts up to 70 days. The average annual temperature is between 24 and 27 C (75,2 to 80,6 F)

Name: Bios Cabo Verde
Description:
Conservação ambiental e desenvolvimento sustentável
Environmental conservation and sustainable development
Umweltschutz und nachhaltige Entwicklung

Name: Middlebury Area Land Trust
Description:
Middlebury Area Land Trust (MALT) is a 501(c)3 non-profit guided by a core set of values that shape and inform our decisions, policies, and strategies. 

Our mission: Working with our community to conserve natural and productive landscapes, and to enhance scenic, recreational, and educational opportunities.

Since 1987, MALT has worked in Addison County with communities and partners to ensure the well-being and long-term viability of the area’s natural and human communities. While what we do and who is involved may change over time, these principles continue to guide us: 

MALT dedicates our work to the ecological integrity of this place: the interconnected people, lands, and waters west of the Green Mountains and east of Lake Champlain. We are one community in the heart of Vermont, Addison County. 

MALT approaches the opportunities and challenges of our work in a spirit of pragmatic optimism. 

We collaborate with individuals, organizations, and communities, believing in our capacity to teach and learn from one another. 

We are grounded in our roots – connecting physically with our lands and trails, hands dirty and boots muddy.

Name: Blue Zero Project
Description: Our mission: to become the first modern vessel to circumnavigate the world with ZERO emissions.

Name: BUND Sachsen e.V.
Description:
Der BUND hat sich allen Themen des Umwelt- und Naturschutzes verschrieben - von der kritischen Auseinandersetzung mit der Atomkraft über den Erhalt der Artenvielfalt, die Energiewende hin zu Fragen der Landwirtschafts- und Verkehrswende.

Der Bund für Umwelt und Naturschutz Deutschland ist einer der größten und wichtigsten gemeinnützigen Natur- und Umweltschutzverbände Deutschlands. In Sachsen hat er mehr als 10.000 Mitglieder.

Name: Blue Metrics
Description:
Blue Metrics Indonesia was created by conceiving Indonesia seas as a mega biodiversity hotspot and a home to rare, threatened, and charismatic organisms.

Our project is focused on discovering and investigating Indonesia's marine biodiversity to support conservation through research. Whenever possible, we use an interdisciplinary approach in data collection to contribute to marine biodiversity databases nationally and globally.

By providing reliable data collection and research, we hope a major effect in consideration of policy making for better marine biodiversity and conservation. Because we believe, we can only conserve what we know.

Name: IGEMA (Institut de Gestion des Écosystémes Méditerranéens Aquatiques)
Description: Les écosystèmes aquatiques (eau douce, milieu marin) méditerranéens se dégradent actuellement à une vitesse jamais égalée. Changement climatique, pollution, surpêche, urbanisation, ces écosystèmes subissent une pression anthropique grandissante, dégradant ainsi la biodiversité, et fragilisant le lien qui existe entre nos sociétés et le milieu aquatique. C’est dans ce contexte que l’IGEMA (Institut de Gestion des Ecosystèmes Méditerranéens Aquatiques) à vue le jour. L’IGEMA est une structure associative (loi 1901) finalisée en novembre 2024. Elle est issue d’une ancienne association environnementale nommée MARE NOSTRUM qui existait depuis 2017.  L’association est structurée autour d'un conseil scientifique possédant des profils diversifiés dans les sciences aquatiques.

Name: Instituto o Canal
Description: O objetivo da atividade é criar empatia, respeito e pertencimento a todos da comunidade, visando a importância da preservação deste ambiente.

Name: ARPE-Action for the Respect and the Protection of the Environment
Description: The Action for the Respect and Protection of the Environment (ARPE), founded in 2016 in Ngaoundéré, Cameroon, by Marcelin Adamou Abou, addresses environmental protection, climate change, and sustainable resource management. Known for its strategic approach blending scientific rigor and community engagement, ARPE holds UN accreditations in biodiversity, climate, and desertification, enabling participation in global events like COP26. Using tools like QHSE, ESIA, and GIS, it ensures measurable results. ARPE partners with WWF, the World Bank, and PNDP, while mobilizing youth through YOUNGO and GYBN. It empowers vulnerable communities, advancing resilience and sustainability worldwide.

Name: Hobe Sound Nature Center
Description:
The Hobe Sound Nature Center is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that works in cooperation with the Nathaniel P. Reed Hobe Sound National Wildlife Refuge to educate, inspire, and empower people of all ages to protect and conserve South Florida’s unique, critical habitats and wildlife.

We host transformative educational experiences for people of all ages, which have contributed to the protection of countless acres of critical habitat, the restoration of fragile habitats, and ensured the survival of many threatened species.

Name: Yayasan IWF | Indonesian Wildlife Conservation Foundation
Description: Indonesian Wildlife Conservation Foundation (Yayasan IWF)Yayasan IWF | Indonesian Wildlife Conservation Foundation Berdiri sejak 1968 yang fokus pada upaya konservasi keanekaragaman hayati & habitatnya #IWF

Name: Al Tatweer Foundation
Description: A private non-profit organization prominent by decision /number/ 1699 in 2017. It's goal is to spread environmental and developmental awareness and culture by preparing scientific research and studies, supervising and implementing environmental and developmental and developmental projects in all field.

Name: Land Conservancy for Kingston, Frontenac, Lennox and Addington
Description: Welcome to the Land Conservancy for KFLA (Kingston, Frontenac, Lennox and Addington). Established in 2004, our purpose is to protect forever nature in our region. Now protecting 13 properties totalling 1100 acres. Follow us for news and updates.

Name: GADAEM - Groupement des Acteurs des Déchets et Alternatives Écologiques de Madagascar
Savia Foundation
Description:
The Savia Foundation, Inc. is a U.S.-based non-profit organization established in 2024. We are a group of storytellers, conservationists, scientists and lawyers who use our expertise to document, protect and defend biodiversity through land protection and restoration, photographic and videographic storytelling, and education and species monitoring.  We work with local stakeholders to foster sustainable, harmonic relationships between humans and the environment.

WE BELIEVE IN THE RIGHTS OF NATURE.
Wild ecosystems and the people that depend on them have the inalienable right to exist and to thrive. And we use our strengths and expertise to help defend these Rights.


WE BELIEVE IN THE POWER OF STORYTELLING.
Photography and videography can gather reliable data on wildlife and generate images with an impact, convincing others to join our cause.  And the tourism they generate can be a powerful economic means for the people who live with and care for natural ecosystems. 

WE BELIEVE IN EMPOWERING PEOPLE.
Those people living most closely with an ecosystem should the drive the push for conservation through their first-hand knowledge and their ability to project conservation into the future. We listen, dialogue, support and help amplify their efforts.

Name: AquaProtecta
Description: Onze focus ligt op bescherming tegen wateroverlast en waterschade.

Name: LIFE PINNARCA
Description:
European project devoted to the protection and restoration of the fan mussel Pinna nobilis populations in the Mediterranean Sea

With the contribution of the LIFE programme, the European Union’s financial instrument supporting environmental, nature conservation and climate action projects.

The main objective is preventing the extinction of Pinna nobilis at the short-medium term, through public awareness and collaboration, gathering existing information on the remaining populations and developing active recovery actions

Name: Domaine de Berinzenne
Description:
Ce domaine de 17 hectares est propriété de la Région wallonne. 
L’asbl Domaine de Bérinzenne gère les infrastructures qu’il abrite. Ses fonctions de porte ouverte sur la nature, de sensibilisation à l’environnement et de protection des milieux naturels s’expriment au travers de différents outils et projets. Outre le CRIE et le Musée, l’asbl chapeaute le projet LIFE Vallées ardennaises, co-financé par la Commission européenne et la Wallonie. 

Le domaine accueille également une association partenaire : le Parc naturel des Sources

Name: SOS VILLAGES D'ENFANTS en République Centrafricaine
Description:
Ce que nous sommes

Le premier Village d’Enfants SOS a été fondé en 1949 à Imst, en Autriche, par Hermann Gmeiner. Celui-ci s’était engagé à aider les enfants démunis - des enfants qui avaient perdu leur foyer, leur famille au lendemain de la Deuxième Guerre Mondiale. Avec le soutien de nombreux donateurs et collaborateurs, notre Organisation est aujourd’hui en mesure de venir en aide aux enfants dans le monde entier.

Nous agissons en tant qu’organisation non gouvernementale à vocation sociale. Indépendants, respectueux des différentes religions et cultures, nous intervenons dans des pays et communautés où notre mission peut contribuer au développement. Nous oeuvrons dans l’esprit de la Convention des Nations Unies pour les Droits de l’Enfant et nous soutenons ces droits à travers le monde.

Avec le concept de Village d’Enfants SOS, notre Organisation a ouvert la voie à la prise en charge familiale et à long terme des enfants orphelins et abandonnés. Ce concept repose sur quatre principes :

La Mère : Chaque enfant à une mère

La mère SOS crée une relation étroite avec chacun des enfants qui lui sont confiés et lui apporte la sécurité, l’amour et la stabilité dont il a besoin. Formée à la prise en charge professionnelle des enfants, elle vit avec eux, oriente leur développement et gère son foyer de manière autonome. Elle connaît et respecte l’histoire familiale de chaque enfant, ses origines culturelles et sa religion.

Les Frères et Sœurs : Les liens familiaux se créent naturellement

Filles et garçons d’âges différents grandissent ensemble comme frères et sœurs, les fratries naturelles ne sont pas séparées et chacune vit dans une même famille. Ces enfants et leur mère SOS nouent des liens affectifs qui durent toute la vie.

La Maison : chaque famille crée son propre foyer

La maison est le foyer de la famille avec un vécu, un rythme et des habitudes qui lui sont propres. Sous son toit, les enfants éprouvent un véritable sentiment de sécurité et d’appartenance.

Name: Site Natura 2000 du bassin de la Grosne et du Clunisois
Description:
Outils fondamentaux de la politique européenne de préservation de la biodiversité, les sites Natura 2000 visent une meilleure prise en compte des enjeux de biodiversité dans les activités humaines. Ces sites sont désignés pour protéger un certain nombre d’habitats et d’espèces représentatifs de la biodiversité européenne. La liste précise de ces habitats et espèces est annexée à la directive européenne oiseaux et à la directive européenne habitats-faune-flore.
Vous êtes ici sur la page du site Natura 2000 du bassin de la Grosne et du Clunisois. Ce site couvre plus de 45 000 ha et 51 communes sur plusieurs communautés de communes du sud-est de la Saône-et-Loire.
Par le biais d’une démarche volontaire et contractuelle, il est possible de mettre en œuvre des actions favorables à la préservation d’espèces emblématiques telles que le Sonneur à ventre jaune, l’Écrevisse à pattes blanches, la Loutre d’Europe et plusieurs espèces de chauves-souris ou de milieux rares comme les pelouses sèches ou les zones humides.

Name: Conservation Capital SA
Description: Conservation Capital leverages Nature-Based Solutions to restore ecosystems, empower communities, and develop impactful carbon projects across Sub-Saharan Africa. Through strategic partnerships and expert verification, we create lasting environmental and social benefits.

Name: WiNN: Nepal Chapter
Description:
Women in Nature Network (WiNN) aims to connect and empower women to achieve sustainable management and conservation of our Earth’s natural resources. As part of WiNN’s efforts to develop a global learning community of women in order to create and support a future generation of environmental leaders, a conference is held every year with different countries taking turns to host the conference. This year, the conference is being held in Nepal, the first time a conference of its kind is being hosted by young Nepali women, who are working relentlessly to protect the natural environment through conservation efforts and activism. WiNN 2022 aims to strengthen and help in the growth of this global community by considering the multiplicity of participants that are involved with their diverse values, backgrounds and stories.

WiNN 2022 participants will be invited to share their knowledge, expertise and experiences with the goal of highlighting local knowledge, conservation efforts and grassroots activism. WiNN 2022 aims to strengthen and help in the growth of this global community by considering the multiplicity of participants that are involved with their diverse values, backgrounds and stories.

https://linktr.ee/winnnepal

Name: Rivers and Forests Alliance (RAFA)
Description:
The Rivers and Forests Alliance (RAFA) is a nonprofit organization in Costa Rica dedicated to preserving and protecting the Pacuare River Basin, its forests, and biodiversity, alongside our communities for a sustainable future.

The Pacuare River flows 136.39 km (85 miles) from Costa Rica’s Talamanca Mountains, past Cabécar Indigenous communities and through dense jungle inhabited by endangered species, before reaching the Caribbean Sea. It is one of Costa Rica’s last large, free-flowing rivers, celebrated for its breathtaking scenery and world-class whitewater paddling. The entire river basin spans 914.11 km² (353 sq mi), forming a vital link in the Barbilla-Destierro Biological Subcorridor that connects national parks and private protected areas. It provides habitat for jaguars, pumas, ocelots, endangered great green macaws, the endangered bobo mullet fish, and many other wildlife species.

Currently, the Pacuare is protected from hydroelectric development by a presidential decree until 2040. However, deforestation, illegal hunting, extraction of river materials, and agricultural pollution threaten the river and local communities. While ecotourism is instrumental in protecting the Pacuare, it also puts environmental pressure on the river. Over the last 20 years, there has been a significant increase in the number of ecotourism businesses operating along a short stretch of the Pacuare River. Within a distance of 26.25 km (16.3 miles), there are 11 river lodges and 17 rafting companies operating, bringing thousands of tourists annually to this fragile ecosystem.

Our Guardians of the Pacuare program unites key stakeholders—including ecotourism businesses, conservation organizations, local communities, and government agencies—to protect the Pacuare River Basin’s forests, wildlife, and water resources. Our initiatives include reforestation, forest conservation, environmental education, biological corridor connectivity, and water quality monitoring.

Name: International Wildlife Refuge Alliance
Description: International Wildlife Refuge Alliance is the 501c3 friends organization that supports Detroit River International Wildlife Refuge

Name: Fundación Bosque Nuevo
Description:
Fundación Bosque Nuevo es una organización dedicada a la reforestación sostenible y la conservación ambiental en Venezuela. Nuestro proyecto insignia busca plantar 6 millones de árboles en la región, contribuyendo a la captura de aproximadamente 30,000 toneladas de carbono, ayudando a mitigar el cambio climático y regenerar ecosistemas vitales.

Más allá de la reforestación, también promovemos programas de educación ambiental y desarrollo social para las comunidades locales, empoderándolas con las herramientas necesarias para cuidar su entorno y mejorar su calidad de vida. Nuestros esfuerzos están respaldados por un equipo comprometido, enfocado en ofrecer soluciones ambientales a largo plazo, con un enfoque integral que abarca tanto el mantenimiento de los árboles durante los primeros 4 años como la futura producción de frutos como el merey.

Con sede en El Tigre, Anzoátegui, trabajamos en sinergia con instituciones públicas y privadas para garantizar que nuestros proyectos sean sostenibles y generen un impacto positivo en las generaciones futuras. Únete a nosotros en nuestra misión de preservar y restaurar el equilibrio natural en Venezuela.

Name: Organisation Pour l'Environnement et le Développement durable ( ONG OPED)
Description: Éveiller les consciences sur les enjeux et défis environnementaux afin de changer les modes actuels de production et de consommation non durables et permettre aux communautés vulnérables d’améliorer leurs conditions de vie.

Name: Friends of Malheur National Wildlife Refuge
Description: Friends of Malheur National Wildlife Refuge (Friends of MNWR) was formed in 1999 and is an independent, non-profit 501(c)(3) corporation committed to: Promoting conservation and appreciation of natural and cultural resources at Malheur National Wildlife Refuge through education, outreach, advocacy, and on-the-ground stewardship.

Name: Sauk Prairie Conservation Alliance
Description: The Sauk Prairie Conservation Alliance promotes cooperative conservation on the Badger lands and in the surrounding Sauk Prairie landscape.

Name: Association Un rêve d'abeilles
Description: Association de protection des abeilles en Haute-Savoie. Parrainage de ruches, ruchers en entreprise, rucher communal ou activité de sensibilisation sont autant d'actions que nous menons pour permettre la sauvegarde de ces précieux insectes que sont les abeilles. Participez dès à présent avec nous.

Name: Green Smiles
Description:
En 2023, en El Salvador, surge Green Smiles, una iniciativa que despierta conciencia y enciende la llama del activismo ambiental. 

Nos enfrentamos a una crisis ambiental sin precedentes y es nuestro deber actuar ahora. Con cada pequeño acto, estamos construyendo un futuro más sostenible y saludable para todos.

Cada gesto cuenta: desde reducir el consumo de plástico hasta promover la energía renovable. Juntos, podemos proteger nuestro hogar y nuestra naturaleza. 

Únete a nosotros en esta misión. Demostremos al mundo que estamos comprometidos con la preservación del medio ambiente.

Juntos, somos imparables. Únete a la revolución verde!  

#GreenSmiles #SalvaElPlaneta

Name: Huay Pakkoot Elephant Community Foundation
Description:
The Huay Pakkoot Community Foundation was founded in 2015 as a way to support the elephants and their families in the Huay Pakkoot Village. After the COVID 19 pandemic began, the Foundation was a necessity. COVID destroyed the tourism industry, and elephant camps returned the elephants to their families in the mountains with little or no notice. There was no financial support given during the pandemic, despite having worked with the families and the elephants for decades in some cases. 

The foundation’s inception allowed for the elephants to receive an equal amount of any funds that were raised and this alleviated the financial burden that the tourist camps had placed on the mahouts and their families. 

Today, the foundation has moved away from purely fundraising and has begun offering ecotourism packages. These allow tourists to have an authentic experience in Karen home-stays, as well as observing the elephants with their mahouts, in their natural habitats, where they display non-coerced behaviour.

Name: Siyafunda Wildlife & Conservation
Description:
"To Learn and To Teach" (“Siyafunda” in Zulu)

At Siyafunda Wildlife and Conservation, these guiding principles form the foundation of our mission and drive every project we undertake. Our goal is to deepen our understanding of wildlife and the ecosystems in which they thrive — and to share this knowledge with everyone who joins us on our Siyafunda projects.

Siyafunda was founded in 2004 to support the Greater Makalali Nature Reserve in monitoring its elephant population as part of the world’s longest-running elephant contraception program. Over time, our work has expanded to include the monitoring of all Big Five species, as well as other key predators.

Our projects are designed to provide dedicated support to our conservation partners. By collecting and analyzing specific data, we help reserves make informed decisions that promote the health and welfare of their wildlife. Siyafunda offers a unique and professional opportunity for individuals to engage with and contribute to the protection of these remarkable natural environments.

Name: EARS Hearing Ltd
Description: We are a comprehensive hearing conservation company. Our goal is to help companies reduce harmful noise exposure. We offer onsite hearing testing and can test multiple people at a time so you're more productive. We also by measure workplace noise levels and offer solutions to reduce any harmful levels. We offer noise assessments, acoustic recommendations, hearing testing, education and training, ongoing monitoring and testing, hearing compliance, we also offer innovative eartips with filters that are personalized, so they are comfortable enough to be worn all day while allowing workers to hear without damaging their hearing. The cost of these eartips are comparable with current ear protection. All of this helps companies be more productive and profitable while keeping their people safe.

Name: Leeuwetand
Description:
Sinds 2009 ijver ik voor effectiever agrarisch natuur- en landschapsbeheer. Ik ben  nu freelancer en werk vooral voor BoerenNatuur en agrarische collectieven.

Na het afronden van mijn opleiding Europese Studies, bestuur en beleid, ben ik op de werkvloer getraind als implementatie- en verandermanager. 

Toen ik in 2009 in de agrarische wereld terecht kwam wist ik dat daar de grootste uitdaging voor mij lag. En we staan nu temidden van een grote cultuurverandering in de landbouw. Net als iedereen draag ik hieraan een unieke bijdrage.

Als grondlegger van Natuurrijk Limburg, het grootste agrarische collectief van Nederland, heb ik me de laatste jaren gespecialiseerd in ledenbetrokkenheid en communicatie. Ook in deze periode heb ik verschillende trainingen in communicatie en groepsdynamiek gedaan, en heb ik de practitioner NLP (Neurolinguisitische Programmering) afgerond.

In 2022 besloot ik om landelijk te gaan werken, samen met BoerenNatuur en andere collectieven. Ik wilde hen graag ondersteunen bij de ontwikkeling en groei die nodig is om aan de maatschappelijke vraag tegemoet te komen. En om hun ambities handen en voeten te geven.

Het mooiste aan mijn werk vind ik dat ik mensen (bestuurders en medewerkers van agrarische collectieven) echt vooruit kan helpen. Zodat ze effectiever, efficiënter en met meer plezier hun taken kunnen uitvoeren. Precies zoals zij dat ook doen met hun deelnemers. 

Op mijn eigen website vind je informatie over wat ik doe, referenties van klanten, maar ook het 'Leeuwetand Log' waarop ik regelmatig mijn gedachten, ervaringen en inzichten deel. 

Neem gerust contact met mij op. 
Paulien Sijbers

Name: تراثية - Turathiyah
Description:
ما هو التراث؟ ماذا يعني لنا؟
تراثية هي مجموعة فنية معاصرة تسعى لاستكشاف التراث المادي، الحسي والشفهي. من خلال المشاركة مع المجتمع، والتعاون مع الفنانين، وطرح الأسئلة.
شاركونا وكونوا جزءًا من رحلتنا!

What is heritage? What does it mean to us?
Turathiyah is a contemporary art collective that seeks to explore tangible and intangible heritage. Through engaging with the community, collaborating with artists, and posing questions. Join us and be a part of our journey

Name: San Juan Islands Conservation District
Description:
The Conservation District is a non-regulatory, government entity established to provide landowners and residents with technical and financial assistance to meet regional natural resource conservation goals. In Washington State, the 45 Conservation Districts are authorized by RCW 89.08.

The San Juan Islands Conservation District was founded in 1947 and helps landowners conserve natural resources by providing free consultation services, project “cost share” funding and detailed technical assistance.
 
As one of Washington State's 45 non-regulatory Conservation Districts, we help residents throughout the San Juan Islands achieve their goals for land stewardship and productivity.  We are committed to conserving and enhancing natural resources in the San Juan Islands for farming, wildlife, public health, forestry, recreation and residential uses. Your Conservation District staff is available to listen and work with you.

Name: McNairy County Soil & Water Conservation District
Description: MCNAIRY COUNTY SOIL CONSERVATION DISTRICT is a renewables & environment company based out of 512 Mulberry Ave, Selmer, Tennessee, United States.

Name: Common Ground Capital
Description:
Our mission is simple: Deliver best-in-class mitigation solutions to provide the utmost certainty to all relevant stakeholders.

We provide certainty to conservation sites for the targeted species and their landscapes by protecting them forever under the robust, rigorous and proven USFWS conservation banking model.

We provide certainty to our industry partners by enabling the full transfer of liability away from their projects under the Endangered Species Act or under voluntary mitigation options with the assurance that their investments are delivering actual, quantified conservation benefits to the species. 

We provide certainty to our private landowner partners under a free market mitigation business model that recognizes that they have a scarce and valuable resource on their property. Our compensation model allows landowners to receive a meaningful up-front payment that allows them to make independent financial decisions for their operations and their families versus a government-type, least-cost annual payment structure.

Name: Dirty Weekenders
Description: We are a society at the University of Edinburgh that volunteers weekly at different nature reserves, community gardens, council land, and parks to help them with their conservation and gardening efforts.

Name: Save the Scenic Santa Ritas
Description:
Save the Scenic Santa Ritas (SSSR), is a nonprofit charitable organization in Pima County, Arizona. We work to protect the Santa Rita Mountains, a globally recognized biodiversity hotspot, renowned for iconic and endangered species like jaguars, ocelots, and Mexican spotted owls. The Santa Rita Mountains are culturally and spiritually critical to the Tohono O’odham Nation and other Tribes. Rising high above the desert, this Sky Island mountain range feeds vital watersheds that sustain the Santa Cruz River and Cienega Creek — essential lifelines for the region’s communities, outstanding wildlife, and riparian ecosystems. With unwavering commitment, SSSR stands as stewards of this irreplaceable treasure, ensuring its beauty and vitality endure for generations to come.

Since 1996, we have been fighting to protect the Santa Rita Mountains from the devastating effects of industrial, open-pit mining. We are 3,000 citizens united to defend the wild mountains we love. 

Current advocacy efforts center on fighting against the Copper World mining complex in the Santa Rita Mountains in Southern Arizona.

Name: Cincinnati Bassmasters
Description: One of southwest Ohio’s longest running clubs! Cincinnati Bassmasters is an Ohio B.A.S.S. Nation affiliated club based around Cincinnati and the tri-state area. We welcome all experienced anglers to join as well as any experienced high school anglers to fish as co-anglers. We fish a total 9 total tournaments per year, Eight local (125-mile radius) and one “destination” lake. Interested in joining?  Visit https://cincinnatibassmasters.com/about/ for more details!

Name: Groupe de travail "mines"
Description:
Le groupe de travail «mines» réunit des personnes intéressées
par les mines. Notre curiosité ou notre métier, guide notre intérêt
pour le milieu souterrain anthropique.
Artistes, historiens, techniciens et scientifiques partagent et
font partager leurs documentation, explorations, observations et
travaux.

Name: Echo Bonaire
Description: Echo is dedicated to protecting the Yellow-shouldered Amazon Parrot (Amazona barbadensis) on Bonaire in the southern Caribbean. With just over 1,000 individuals on the island, these parrots are at risk of extinction. Poaching, habitat loss, and introduced predators continue to threaten their numbers. Founded in 2010 by Dr Sam Williams, Echo conducts valuable research to understand the ecological and human factors that threaten parrots and establishes conservation initiatives that provide long-term solutions for parrots and people. Echo also has a rehabilitation and release program for injured and illegally captured parrots. The dedicated Echo team operates from a field camp inside a Birdlife International Important Bird Area and outside a Ramsar area.

Name: Ágora Verde
Description:
Desarrollamos proyectos de captura de carbono a través de soluciones basadas en la naturaleza. Identificamos oportunidades con alto potencial de negocio, evaluamos su viabilidad, establecemos acuerdos con propietarios forestales y gestionamos su ejecución para garantizar su éxito. 

Nuestro enfoque combina sostenibilidad y rentabilidad, asegurando beneficios tanto ambientales como económicos para todas las partes involucradas.

Name: Todos pelo Araguaia
Description: O Programa Todos pelo Araguaia é uma iniciativa do Estado de Mato Grosso, dedicada à recuperação ambiental e reflorestamento ao longo das margens do rio Araguaia. Nosso objetivo é promover a revitalização da bacia hidrográfica do rio, combatendo o assoreamento e promovendo o reflorestamento no bioma Cerrado. Trabalhamos em parceria com a iniciativa privada para expandir nossas ações e alcançar novos investimentos.

Name: Turtle Love & Conservation
Description: TLC is a JCU affiliated club, established to connect students with an interest in marine turtles. Through monthly turtle talks, and recurring social events, we aim to engage members with up to date research and conservation topics from around the world.

Name: Monarch Butterfly Fund
Description: Monarch Butterfly Fund is a nonprofit dedicated to conserving the magic of the monarch butterfly migration.

Name: Oakwood Clydesdales
Description:
Oakwood Clydesdales offers a modern approach to conservation by adapting and diversifying traditional horse-powered agricultural methods to deliver practical and effective land management solutions.

Our horses and equipment are highly efficient while leaving minimal impact on the environment. We use ground-driven machinery that operates without an engine—powered solely by the pull of the horses.

This approach provides a range of ecological and logistical benefits across various landscapes, particularly in sensitive or hard-to-reach areas. Our methods have an exceptionally low carbon footprint, support biodiversity, and cause minimal disturbance to wildlife and the surrounding environment.

Get in touch to learn how we can support your land management strategies with low-impact, horse-powered solutions.

Name: Ecologie & Collaboration - Eco
Description:
Aujourd'hui, je voudrais vous parler de notre association qui se concentre sur l'écologie et la collaboration. Nous sommes convaincus que ces deux éléments sont essentiels pour créer un avenir durable et prospère pour tous.

Notre association travaille sur plusieurs fronts pour promouvoir l'écologie et la collaboration. Nous organisons des événements pour sensibiliser les gens à l'importance de la protection de l'environnement et pour encourager la collaboration entre les différents acteurs impliqués dans cette cause. Nous travaillons également avec des entreprises et des organisations pour les aider à adopter des pratiques plus durables et à réduire leur impact environnemental.

Nous croyons que la collaboration est la clé pour résoudre les problèmes environnementaux auxquels nous sommes confrontés. En travaillant ensemble, nous pouvons trouver des solutions innovantes et efficaces pour protéger notre planète et assurer un avenir durable pour les générations futures.

Nous sommes également convaincus que chacun d'entre nous peut faire une différence. En adoptant des pratiques plus durables dans notre vie quotidienne, nous pouvons tous contribuer à la protection de l'environnement et à la création d'un avenir plus durable.

Nous espérons que vous vous joindrez à nous dans notre mission pour promouvoir l'écologie et la collaboration. Ensemble, nous pouvons créer un avenir meilleur pour tous.

Name: OTUS CONSERVATION
Description: FUNDACION RED DE CONSERVACIÓN CIENCIA Y COLABORACIÓN SIN FRONTERAS.

Name: SCERA
Description:
OUR MISSION
To promote biodiversity conservation in Nigeria using science-based solutions, through sustainable partnerships and public empowerment, working with people closest to nature.

Name: Waste in Med Islands
Description:
Nous sommes une équipe formée de 9 élèves-ingénieurs de Télécom SudParis réunis autour d'un projet citoyen de dépollution et de protection des eaux de la Méditerranée.
Fort de notre enthousiasme et de notre implication pour ce projet, nous serons cette année en étroite collaboration avec l'association Echo d’Océan pour les épauler dans leur lutte contre la pollution plastique de nos précieux littoraux méditerranéens.

Ce projet est pour nous une occasion en or de découvrir le monde de la gestion de projet et la collaboration dans le monde du travail et s'inscrit parfaitement dans la dynamique à laquelle nous aspirons en tant que futurs ingénieurs voulant œuvrer pour le bien commun.

Name: Bird Safe Campuses
Description: A central hub with resources, strategies, and data to support campus bird-window collision initiatives!

Name: Project wild
Description: Raising awareness for wildlife and wild places through imagery

Name: Jeunes Voix Vertes
Description:
"Young Green Voices" (JVV) is a dynamic national team of young Beninese accredited by Open Dialogue International https://www.open-dialogues.org/ as the Benin Branch responsible for organizing Open Dialogues on Climate Change (ODCC). These young people are determined and committed to the fight against climate change. 

They represent the aspirations of vulnerable species, indigenous peoples, as well as women and children exposed to the harmful effects of the climate crisis. 

The main objective of JVV is to demonstrate the potential of young people in combating climate change by promoting their active participation in decision-making processes for concrete actions.

Name: CarbonU
Description:
CarbonU (Carbon University) es una iniciativa estudiantil de la Universidad de los Andes dedicada a la captura y remoción de dióxido de carbono (CCS y CDR). Nuestro objetivo es formar talento interdisciplinar, desarrollar prototipos funcionales y traducir conocimiento académico en soluciones palpables para la crisis climática.

Nuestros pilares:
Formación: programas intensivos que acercan a los estudiantes a química de captura, procesos de separación, almacenamiento, y gobernanza
Creación: equipos multidisciplinares que prototipan soluciones modulares
Conexión: mentorías, alianzas con empresas, y vinculación con semilleros y laboratorios
Investigación: registro estricto de datos y producción científica que valide nuestros enfoques
Divulgación: comunicar hallazgos, fracasos y victorias para multiplicar el aprendizaje. 

CarbonU ofrece a los estudiantes la oportunidad de formarse en tecnologías de vanguardia y de participar en proyectos que pueden derivar en tesis, publicaciones y trayectorias profesionales en climate-tech. Al sector CCS, CarbonU ofrece evidencia técnica y divulgación basada en datos que faciliten la adopción responsable de CCS y CDR. Si eres estudiante, investigador, empresa o financiador y quieres colaborar, participar o apoyar: contáctanos!

Name: ResNat - Réseau des Associations Naturalistes de France
Description:
ResNat est une association visant à mettre en lumière les associations naturalistes et les activités qu'elles mènent en faveur de la préservation de la biodiversité
Notre carte collaborative :
https://resnat.gogocarto.fr/

Name: ReFi Cartagena
Description: Join the movement towards a more sustainable and equitable future in #Cartagena

Name: Coexistence Collective
Description: Founded by Ruth Ganesh, The Coexistence Collective is a collaboration between indigenous artisans, contemporary artists, creatives, and cultural institutions to harness the power of creativity and storytelling to address habitat loss, climate change and biodiversity loss, raising millions to power human-wildlife coexistence projects around the world. Ruth Ganesh specializes in imagining and delivering major public art events, having raised more than £15 million for conservation thus far.

Name: Aresilix
Description:
Dans un monde de plus en plus incertain, nous avons besoin de partage, de savoir-faire et de savoir-être. C'est en se réappropriant des compétences, en réalisant ensemble de manière juste et en harmonie avec notre environnement que l'on deviendra résilient. 
Nous avons tous besoin de sens et d'orientation favorable et durable.
L'important est de commencer chacun à son niveau une inflexion, un début de changement pour le mieux.
Nous vous accompagnons dans votre démarche d'adaptation au dere climatique :
Conseil, stratégie, gestion et réalisation de projets pérennes et positifs au sein d'un réseau sain, durable et convivial.

Name: Conservation Lac Brome
Description: NPO dedicated to the conservation of Brome Lake and its watershed

Name: James River Soil Conservation District
Description: Working together to regenerate and protect the natural resources of Dickey County by demonstrating, educating, and leading engaged communities and landowners.

Name: Blue Marlin Conservation
Description:
Blue Marlin Conservation (BMC) is a marine conservation and training center based in Gili Air, Indonesia. We welcome anyone who wants to learn about marine ecosystems while actively participating in their preservation.
As part of the international Conservation Diver Training Center network, we offer scientific diving courses, land-based workshops, and coral reef monitoring activities. Our goal is simple: to provide meaningful learning experiences while creating a real impact on the ocean.
Location: Kabupaten Lombok Utara, Nusa Tenggara Barat

Name: Associação Tubarões da Costa do RN
Description:
Somos uma associação civil sem fins econômicos com o objetivo principal de promover pesquisa científica, educação ambiental, conservação e uso sustentável de peixes marinhos e estuarinos do Rio Grande do Norte, com ênfase em tubarões e as raias.
Atuamos no intuito de contribuir na manutenção de ecossistemas marinhos e costeiros sadios e equilibrados, com ética e o respeito à toda as formas de vida.

Name: International Primate Rescue
Description: Discover IPR, a haven for primates nestled on the outskirts of Pretoria, South Africa. Our sanctuary provides a loving home for primates rescued from the pet trade, laboratories, and zoos. Supported by the generosity of volunteers and donors, we strive to ensure the well-being of all animals under our care. Join us in our mission to make a difference.

Name: REDO Rwanda
Description:
REDO was founded in 1999 with the main objective of championing environmental education and public awareness of sustainability in Rwanda and in the Great Lakes region

The Rural Environment Development Organisation (REDO) has been working for the development of rural communities and environmental conservation in Rwanda since 1999. Our objective is to champion environmental sustainability through the education and training of communities in nature conservation, while also addressing also the root causes of poverty through livelihood projects, education-based human rights interventions and advocacy initiatives. Our projects mainly target historically marginalized men, women and youth, making them models to the rest of Rwanda in natural resource protection, raising their awareness on the role of conservation and of the ecosystem in people’s lives. Our integrated conservation interventions also create economic opportunities for them, empowering communities by increasing their access to food and income, and encouraging equitable access to resources and decision making between all members of the community.

As priority sectors, REDO uses agriculture, beekeeping, non-timber forest production (bamboo), pottery and handicrafts to empower beneficiaries in areas of basic business/entrepreneurship skills and tap into existing economic opportunities strengthened by our linkages to markets. These market development interventions are supported by policy and advocacy actions to seek more engagement of policy makers in improving the livelihoods of poor and marginalized communities surrounding the reserved areas.

OUR VISION

A society where rural Rwandan communities live in a secure environment, and are empowered to sustainably utilise available resources and opportunities to end poverty and food insecurity.

OUR MISSION
To empower women and men, particularly those that have been historically marginalised, to actively participate improving their livelihood conditions, as well as ad

Name: Your Watershed Moment for the White River
Description:
Your Watershed Moment, Protecting Indiana’s White River through community, conservation and collaboration

The White River is one of Indiana’s most vital natural resources, supporting a third of the state’s population and its largest economic centers. A strong future for the White River requires connecting people across Greater Indianapolis to a healthy and restored river, communities investing in water quality, park and recreation, and economic development projects that strengthen the quality of life, place, and opportunity in the region. Though the river and broader watershed face mounting pressures from development, population growth and competing resource demands, we believe that informed residents, business owners, farmers, developers and policy makers will make smart choices for a stronger White River.

Recognizing the need for coordinated action, Partners for the White River formed a coalition of environmentally-focused nonprofit organizations dedicated to protecting, restoring, and advocating for the White River and its watershed. Our goal is to bring awareness and action to the choices--big and small--that help protect this important Central Indiana resource. 

Your Watershed Moment is a campaign designed to inspire Central Indiana residents to make everyday choices that protect and improve the health of the White River. By connecting personal actions to their collective impact on the watershed, the campaign aims to shine a light on the part we all play in keeping the river and broader watershed healthy.

Name: Amazonia Collective
Description:
Amazonia Collective believes that the forest and its peoples breathe as one.
Guided by the wisdom of Indigenous cosmovisions, our work bridges worlds to protect both the rainforest and the cultures that sustain it.

Rooted in deep listening and respect for ancestral knowledge, our communications and outreach efforts amplify Indigenous voices and their stories of coexistence with nature. Together, we nurture sustainable ways of living, grounded in reciprocity, care, and balance.

By walking alongside communities across the Amazon, we co-create initiatives that protect the forest’s ecological richness and the spiritual and cultural heritage of its original guardians — ensuring that both continue to thrive for generations to come.

Name: Detroit River - Western Lake Erie Cooperative Weed Management Area
Description:
The Detroit River-Western Lake Erie Cooperative Weed Management Area was established in 2011 by 13 partners working in federal, state, and local government, non-governmental organizations, and universities dedicated to the prevention and eradication of invasive species in Wayne and Monroe Counties.

All DR-WLE CWMA members work collaboratively to:

1. DETECTION, INVENTORY AND MONITORING. Cooperatively develop detection, inventory, and monitoring protocols across jurisdictional boundaries, and exchange data.

2. PREVENTION. Prevent the establishment of new invasive plant species.

3. INVASIVE SPECIES MANAGEMENT. Promote effective, integrated reduction methods, and eradication when possible, of invasive plant species that have already become established.

4. COOPERATION. Facilitate development of cooperative agreements for local invasive plant management, which include opportunities for shared funding sources, personnel, including volunteers, equipment, contract crews, expertise, etc.

5. INFORMATION EXCHANGE. Share technical information regarding new and established invasive species distribution, treatment locations, and reduction methods.

6. EDUCATION. Increase public and staff awareness of invasive plant and animal species.

Name: Akyem Tafo Water Initiative lbg
Description:
Naděje a ochrana vody
•
Jsem odhodlána změnit budoucnost. Zahajuji projekt, který má obyvatelům v africké Ghaně umožnit přístup k čisté pitné vodě. V projektu vidím příležitost, jak vyvolat změnu v chování místních obyvatel se zvýšením povědomí o důležitosti ochrany vodních zdrojů. 
•
Z místní studny jsem odebrala vzorky a vodu jsem otestovala jak v ČR, tak v Ghaně. S náčelníkem mám domluvenou koupi pozemku. Plánuji zde vyvrtat studnu a instalovat systém čištění vody s následným balením zdravé pitné vody k další distribuci.
•
Pro optimální fungování projektu je zde nezbytné implementovat fotovoltaický systém. Ghanská infrastruktura zápasí s velmi nestálou elektrickou sítí a pro zajištění energetických potřeb je nezbytné zařadit do projektu solární panely. Současně chci celý systém využít jako ukázkovou funkční jednotku poháněnou udržitelnou energií, uvědomělým nakládáním s odpady a zodpovědným přístupem ke krajině.
•
Tento projekt zajistí obyvatelstvu spolehlivý přístup k pitné vodě v místě bydliště.

Name: LIFE SakerRoads
Description:
LIFE SakerRoads, co-financed by the EU LIFE Programme, aims to increase the population size and breeding success of saker falcons in Hungary's Northern Great Plain.

Saker falcons face a number of threats, in particular high human-induced mortality of adults and juveniles. Between 2009-2019, this accounted for 95% of saker falcon deaths, where the cause was known, in Hungary. Electrocution on medium-voltage power line poles was the biggest culprit (51%), with other deaths caused by illegal poisoning, collisions with vehicles or overhead cables and shooting. There is also a high prevalence of mortality and breeding failure with unknown causes.  

Decreased breeding success due to low availability of preferred prey species also presents a threat. Recent surveys found that four taxa represent the bulk of saker falcons’ prey (86%) in Hungary: pigeon species, common starling, brown hare and common vole.

Decreased availability of safe nesting sites is another problem for saker falcons. As they do not build nests themselves, the birds are highly dependent on nesting sites built by other species or provided by humans.

Lastly, saker falcons are affected by low awareness among stakeholders and the public about the species and the threats it faces. Most land users tend to be unaware of the birds’ presence and whether their activities could accidently threaten their survival, breeding success or food supply. 

Specifically, the project seeks to:
- Decrease human-induced mortality
- Reveal the unknown causes of mortality and breeding failure
- Improve breeding success through increased availability of prey species and nesting sites
- Increase the awareness of key stakeholders and the public about the species and its threats

Name: APEPE-ONG
Walderlebnisraum Gais
Description:
Die Natur erleben, den Wald erkunden, Tiere beobachten, Sträucher bestimmen – der Walderlebnisraum Gais bietet die passende Infrastruktur, um für die Schönheiten und die Bedeutung des Waldes zu sensibilisieren.

Das zentrale Element bildet das Waldhaus im Grossmoos mit seinem offenen Holzbau mit Tischen und Bänken. Die Feuerstelle und der Brunnen vervollständigen die Anlage inmitten des Waldes. Für die Aktivitäten im Walderlebnisraum steht den Benutzerinnen und Benutzern im separaten Magazin zudem eine umfangreiche Sammlung an walddidaktischen Materialien zur Verfügung.

Das Angebot richtet sich in erster Linie an Gruppen wie Vereine, Firmen und sonstige Organisationen. Dabei ist der Walderlebnisraum Gais ideal, um im Rahmen eines Seminars, Workshops oder einer Exkursion naturrelevante Themen zu erarbeiten.

Ab der Haltestelle Appenzeller Bahnen Rietli führt ein Erlebnispfad zum Waldhaus (Fussmarsch ca. 20 Minuten). Der Weg ist gut ausgebaut und mit rund 50 Infotafeln beschildert, welche dazu einladen, Pflanzen, Tiere und den Boden mit allen Sinnen zu erleben und viel Wissenswertes zur Entstehung, nachhaltigen Bewirtschaftung und Zukunft von Wald und Riet zu erfahren.

Name: Coastal Underwater Resource Management Actions (CURMA)
Description:

CURMA has developed a unique conservation model that takes a holistic approach to conservation. This model encompasses several key pillars, including sea turtle conservation, waste management, reforestation of coastal forests and mangroves, and fostering sustainable communities. By uniting volunteers, citizen scientists, and leaders, we strive towards a future that is both sustainable and regenerative, with education and adherence to CURMA values at its core.

Name: Friends of the Tampa Bay National Wildlife Refuges
Description:
Our mission is to promote conservation, awareness, and appreciation of the Tampa Bay National Wildlife Refuges, and to assist the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service with their mission for those Refuges. 

We accomplish this through outreach, education, advocacy, and volunteering to protect the nesting and resting wading birds, seabirds, and other wildlife on the refuges as well as protecting the habitat they need to thrive. 

As Friends, we actively:

Manage volunteer opportunities that support the efforts of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service at the Tampa Bay Refuges. 

Raise funds for conservation, preservation, restoration, and education in support of the Tampa Bay Refuges and the National Refuge System.

Sponsor periodic Refuge clean ups of exotic plants, debris, and mono-filament fishing line which can damage the wildlife and habitats.

Offer public educational programs and field trips that highlight conservation, awareness, and appreciation of the natural world.

Provide volunteer docent staffing to the Egmont Key Guardhouse.
Organize volunteer bird stewards for Egmont Key's nesting bird colonies.

Survey nesting and resting birds at the Pinellas Refuges each month to measure changes in population and evaluate effectiveness of conservation programs.

Have a presence at local festivals and markets to promote awareness of the National Wildlife Refuge System and our local refuges.

Encourage membership to show support of the Tampa Bay National Wildlife Refuges.

Subscribe to our newsletter and become a member, here: https://tampabayrefuges.org/membership

Name: JEAI GLOGORENA
Description:
GLOGORENA is the result of a successful initial collaboration between an IRD researcher (Symphorian Ongolo) and a researcher at the Institute of International Relations of Cameroon (IRIC). GLOGORENA is also the result of a rigorous selection process and a highly selective competition aimed at strengthening links between North-South scientific communities.

JEAI GLOGORENA, a research facility in the social sciences, funded by IRD and housed at IRIC, aims at training and capacity building for young researchers through involvement in collective research processes and teaching through the mobilisation of empirical data. This system consists of a group of senior and junior researchers whose work focuses on public policy and natural resource governance in the Congo Basin, and in particular in Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). This IRD device, known as JEAI GLOGORENA, has a duration of three years (2024-2026), non-renewable.

GLOGORENA's approach is interdisciplinary! It combines the practices of sociologists, anthropologists, economists, and political scientists. All this to identify, grasp, and analyse the relations, practices, and power relations between the actors of natural resource governance in the Congo basin, particularly in Cameroon and the DRC, in an international atmosphere where discourses on the ecological transition and the enhanced presence of China predominate.

Our objectives are as follows:
To study the governance of natural resources in the Congo Basin.
To understand practices and relationships between actors.
To understand and integrate the socio-political and economic issues of public policies assimilated to natural resource governance, sustainability, and ecological transition.
To strengthen the capacities of local actors involved in these public policy processes for fair, sustainable, and equitable biodiversity governance.
To value empirical data on forest and mining governance in the region.
