Name: Paint Rock Forest Research Center
Description:
Research at the Paint Rock Forest Research Center is creating a new vision for forests in North America. In partnership with universities in Alabama and nationally, we are building a research program and campus that attracts researchers and attention from around the globe.

Why here?

Alabama is the center of forest and water diversity nationally. Northeast Alabama is the national center of deciduous forest diversity and a hotspot for forest endemism. By most measures, a few hundred acres of Paint Rock forest have more tree diversity than hundreds of thousands of acres of Great Smokey Mountains forest. 

Who's involved?

The nation’s top scientists— including our founders, the late E.O. Wilson from Harvard; Stephen Hubbell and Patty Gowaty, chairs of Evolutionary Biology at UCLA; and scientists from University of Georgia, Clemson University and University of Vermont.

The research program

 On a stunning 4,000-acre Nature Conservancy preserve in Paint Rock Valley, they’re measuring the depth and dimensions of Alabama’s riches in a way that’s never been done before.  Over 150 acres, every woody stem larger than a pencil is identified, carefully measured and mapped, and will be tracked for decades.  Companion research will probe the tens of thousands of unknown species in the soil, along with the remarkable diversity of wildflowers, insects, cave life, birds, amphibians, mammals, many of which are all but unknown outside of Northeast Alabama.

The fact that these forests are so much richer than forests elsewhere is telling us something very important about the nature of Alabama: It may well hold the keys to the future of forests and life throughout North America. 

A new generation of researchers

We recognize that the only way to appreciate the diversity of Alabama is to see it with the eyes of diversity. Alabama’s underserved teens, regardless of race and cultural background, will have a chance to work with the nation’s top scientists.

Name: York Land Trust
Description: York Land Trust is a nonprofit organization that protects and cares for significant natural lands and waterways in the greater York Maine area for the benefit of the community and the environment.

Name: A Rocha Perú
Description:
A Rocha Perú es parte de la familia global de A Rocha, una organización cristiana dedicada a la conservación del medio ambiente. Trabajamos en investigación, conservación, educación ambiental y promovemos el compromiso de la Iglesia con el cuidado de la creación.

Colaboramos con comunidades locales, realizamos estudios científicos para proteger la biodiversidad y desarrollamos programas educativos vivenciales que fortalecen el compromiso ambiental. Creemos que conservar la naturaleza es nuestra responsabilidad y también una oportunidad para mejorar la calidad de vida de las personas.

Name: Lewis & Clark Chapter of Safari Club International
Description:
By joining a Safari Club International Chapter…
You connect to a local network of like-minded individuals who share the common goal of protecting the freedom to hunt and promoting wildlife conservation.

You work to make a difference by getting involved in education, conservation, and humanitarian projects.

You give back to your community, because funds raised by your chapter stay at the local level.

Name: Keen Eye Wildlife Management
Description:
We care about your teams, the wildlife, and the environmnet. Let us assist in keeping your workers safe from human/wildlife conflicts, preventing loss or injury to humans and wildlife

We provide non-lethal wildlife management services including wildlife specialists, consultants, and monitors to keep your operational objectives moving forward by implementing management processes to affect the impacts of humans, wildlife, and habitats. 

Serving British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Nunavut, Yukon, and the North West Territories

Name: Friends of JACK Primate Sanctuary
Description:
FRIENDS OF JACK PRIMATE SANCTUARY is a US 501(c)(3) non-profit with the specific purpose to support the preservation of wildlife in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) through promotion of educational programs, funding for conservation efforts, and other activities aimed at preventing wildlife extinction. Our main conservation areas support the rescue and rehabilitation of primates in the DRC, mainly chimpanzees and many species of Afro-EuroAsian monkeys who are endemic to the DRC.  

To that end, the Organization will work with and support the DRC conservation organizations already working to advance these charitable goals.

Name: Pareto Eco Project
Description: We invented the Pareto Eco Score (PES) to promote sustainable banks and neobanks. Make a donation to deploy the PES in all digital user experience.

Name: #SEATHEBIGGERPICTURE
Description:
Formed in 2018, #STBP is an NPO that acts as an Agent for The Sea and is proactively involved in the restoration of South African beaches and oceans to their former pristine state.

#STBP runs marine education and awareness events, projects and ocean initiatives.
The organization drives positive change through actual science and constructive
engagement.

PROGRAMMES & EVENTS
Defenders of the Blue Ocean Conservation
Beach Clean- Up Events
Environmental Awareness Talks
Corporate and Civil Awareness 


SPECIALISTS IN

Marine Science
Ocean Conservation Education 
Environmental Awareness and Conservation
Research and Tracking

Name: Mannankulam
Description:
Mannankulam: Rejuvenating the East Coast Legacy. We're dedicated to preserving the rich heritage of our region – its culture, history, food, nature, and art – for generations to come. Our organic certified ancestral estate is home to biodiversity restoration efforts and four tropical villas offering curated experiences in art, nature, food, and wildlife for curious travelers exploring the East Coast of Sri Lanka.

From our heritage lands, we also produce: Delightfully fresh coconut oil from old stock trees, flavourful heirloom rice varieties (pachchaiperumal, suwandel, cheenatti, madathuwaal), and utterly delectable seasonal fruits and vegetables, especially our mangoes.

Name: Fishing Cat Conservation Alliance
Description:
The Fishing Cat Conservation Alliance (FCCA) is a team of conservationists, researchers and fishing cat enthusiasts working to achieve a single dream – a world with functioning floodplains and coastal ecosystems that would ensure the survival of the fishing cat and all species with which it shares a home.

Our goal is to conduct sound and meaningful research to determine the ecological needs of this unique species and to engage the community in conservation efforts. We aim to mainstream fishing cat conservation, to inspire fascination instead of fear, and to have a positive impact on the livelihoods of those who live alongside the fishing cat, from conflict to curiosity for the species. We work as a team, complementing each other and helping raise the bar for small wild cat conservation on a global scale. 

Our research is rapidly expanding our understanding of fishing cat populations. We are learning more about the ecology and behavior of fishing cats than has ever been known before. Join our community!

Name: EPAGE Aveyron aval
Description: Collectivité territoriale qui exerce la compétence GEstion des Milieux Aquatiques et Prévention des Inondations (GEMAPI) et ses complémentaires pour le compte des Communautés de communes et Communauté d'Agglomération présentes sur le bassin versant Aveyron aval. D'une superficie de plus de 1300km² et plus de 1250 km de cours d'eau, le bassin Aveyron aval dispose d'une multitude d'usages en lien avec l'eau et les milieux aquatiques. Le rôle de l'EPAGE Aveyron aval est de mettre en œuvre des programmes d'actions et de communications visant à améliorer la qualité et la quantité des eaux et des milieux tout en travaillant avec les acteurs du territoire pour s'assurer que les usages s'effectuent en cohérence avec les capacités du territoire.

Name: 90°SUD
Description: 90°SUD est une association reconnue d'intérêt général qui vise à sensibiliser le public aux risques liés au réchauffement des pôles et au dérèglement climatique, ainsi qu’à la préservation des milieux naturels et de la biodiversité. Au travers d'expéditions, de conférences et par la production de documentaires et de supports pédagogiques, 90°SUD tend à développer une conscience écologique auprès du grand public en favorisant la connaissance, la réflexion et la volonté de préservation de la nature et des êtres vivants.

Name: Cybelle Planète
Description:
Depuis 2005, Cybelle Planète agit pour la préservation de la biodiversité marine grâce à l’engagement citoyen. Nous permettons à chacun d’agir concrètement à travers des expéditions cétacés en mer Méditerranée et des sciences participatives.

Nos actions principales :
 Expéditions en mer Méditerranée : Nous organisons des missions d’observation des cétacés et de la faune marine. Nos écovolontaires collectent des données scientifiques essentielles pour la recherche et participent à la sensibilisation à la protection du milieu marin.

 Programme Cybelle Méditerranée (2009-2020) : Pendant plus de 10 ans, ce programme de sciences participatives a permis d’améliorer les connaissances sur les espèces marines du large. Il s'est arrêté en 2020, mais nos actions se poursuivent à travers nos expéditions en mer.

 OBSenMER, l’application d’observation en mer : Co-créée par Cybelle Planète et le GECC, cette application mobile permet aux plaisanciers et professionnels de signaler leurs observations de la faune marine et d’alimenter les bases de données scientifiques.

 Charte Éthique de l’Écovolontariat : Depuis 2014, nous défendons un écovolontariat responsable, éthique et bénéfique pour la conservation et les communautés locales.

Nos missions aujourd’hui :
Jusqu’en 2020, nous proposions des missions d’écovolontariat à l’international. Aujourd’hui, nous nous concentrons uniquement sur nos expéditions cétacés en France.

Pourquoi nous rejoindre ?
 Vivez une expérience immersive en pleine nature
 Agissez pour la biodiversité en contribuant aux recherches scientifiques
 Faites partie d’une communauté engagée

 Découvrez nos missions sur www.cybelle-planete.org

Name: MAHATSANGY MADAGASCAR
Description:
Apolitique et à but non lucratif, notre association agit au carrefour du Temps, de l’Eau et du Climat, en plaçant l’Environnement, le Social et la Gouvernance au cœur de chacune de nos actions. Nous croyons que l’impact réel se mesure autant à la vision qu’à la manière de la réaliser : gestion responsable des ressources, bien-être des membres et partenariats solides avec les acteurs éducatifs, scientifiques, communautaires et institutionnels.

Grâce à nos initiatives comme EduClimat, MAHATSANGY Live, Volihazo, l’accompagnement à l’éco-entrepreneuriat et la R&D appliquée, nous :

- FORMONS et SENSIBILISONS enseignants, jeunes et citoyens, 
- PLANTONS et RESTAURONS des écosystèmes, et 
- DIFFUSONS des connaissances scientifiques FIABLES pour éclairer le débat public et guider les décisions locales. 

Ainsi, nous voyons émerger des communautés malgaches éduquées, RESILIENTES, autonomes et solidaires, profondément ENGAGEES dans la préservation de leur biodiversité unique au monde, de leur environnement, soutenues par une gouvernance INCLUSIVE et des PARTENARIATS DURABLES. 

MAHATSANGY ne se contente pas d’agir pour Madagascar : NOUS CONSTRUISONS UN FUTUR DURABLE, concret et inspirant, main dans la main avec nos partenaires.

Name: CPRE Sussex
Description:
CPRE Sussex is dedicated to protecting, promoting and enhancing the Sussex countryside and shaping a greener future for the whole of our historic county.
Our priorities are:
Beautiful landscapes, rich in nature and greener towns and cities 
Countryside and green spaces which are accessible to all, enriching lives and health 
Sustainable development for thriving communities
Effective responses to the climate emergency

Name: Montana Trout Unlimited
Description: Montana Trout Unlimited's mission is to conserve, protect, and restore Montana's worldclass coldwater fisheries and their watersheds.

Name: SARIKA
Description:
SARIKA is a three-year WCS Cambodia initiative funded by the UK Global Centre on Biodiversity for Climate. Partnering with Indigenous Bunong communities, the Cambodian Indigenous Peoples Organisation, the Royal University of Agriculture, the University of Oxford’s Interdisciplinary Centre for Conservation Science, and Monash University, the project asks how to measure and fairly reward Indigenous forest stewardship. 

SARIKA centers Bunong community researchers, supporting them in documenting their knowledge, creating clear evidence of their contributions, and establishing practical pathways for fair recognition and reward. The project also explores how Indigenous traditions and values can shape innovative conservation and benefit sharing solutions that can be tested and scaled within Cambodia and beyond.

Name: Stichting Vergroening Singel030
Description:
De Stichting Vergroening Singel030 zet zich in voor meer beleving langs het water, meer biodiversiteit boven en onder water en daardoor een verbetering van de waterkwaliteit van al het Utrechtse oppervlaktewater.

We kunnen een beroep doe op meer dan 100 vrijwilligers voor de uitvoering, monitoring en onderhoud van de projecten, maar een veel kleinere groep voert op regelmatige basis taken uit.

◆ Wij willen met onze projecten op zoveel mogelijk plaatsen binnen de gemeente Utrecht deze situaties te verbeteren. Met de kennis die we in de afgelopen jaren hebben opgebouwd willen we de omvang en kwaliteit van projecten dermate opvoeren dat deze een wezenlijke bijdrage leveren aan de verbetering van de biodiversiteit in, op en naast de watergangen.

◆ Wij willen dit doen met behulp van vrijwilligers omdat dit door het kleinschalige karakter de beste resultaten kan leveren en goed is voor de bewustwording van het belang van deze projecten.

◆ Wij volgen het beleid vanuit de gemeente Utrecht rond de wateren kritisch en zullen onze visie daarop gevraagd én ongevraagd leveren.

Name: Potomac Valley Audubon Society
Description: Potomac Valley Audubon Society is a nonprofit organization that serves Jefferson, Berkeley, and Morgan counties in West Virginia and Washington County, Maryland with exceptional environmental education programs for youth and adults.

Name: Ramat Wildlife Society
Description: Ramat Wildlife Society is a grassroots association of community and private wildlife conservancies in Kajiado Central and West that fosters a model of community-based conservation. This is a large area in Southern Kenya that has had the least wildlife conservation investment even though it lies in between the famous Amboseli, Nairobi and Maasai Mara National Parks and conservation areas.

Name: BioObs
Description:
Cette association a pour but d'étudier et de promouvoir la connaissance de la biodiversité des milieux subaquatiques, notamment par le développement et la promotion en toute indépendance de l’outil BioObs (www.bioobs.fr) permettant de :
• Constituer des relevés d’observations des milieux subaquatiques,
• Constituer une banque de données photo des espèces et des habitats,
• Contribuer à une démarche scientifique d’inventaire des espèces et à différents programmes de sciences participatives en milieu subaquatique,
• Participer à des plateformes de Sciences Participatives,
• Contribuer à l’Inventaire National du Patrimoine Naturel (INPN),
• Contribuer à la directive cadre de surveillance du milieu marin (DCSMM),
• Connaître l’aire de répartition des espèces et de s’informer sur les espèces observables selon les différents sites,
• Contribuer à une information naturaliste du grand public et en particulier auprès des plongeurs amateurs et autres usagers des milieux aquatiques et subaquatiques.

Name: Integral Ecology Project for both Congo
Description:
The term ecology comes from the ancient Greek oikos, which means house, and logos means study or science. Ecology is defined as the study of the relationships between living beings and their environment, taking into account the interactions within ecosystems. 
The word "integral" means "complete," "whole," or "total and in the context of integral ecology, this refers to a holistic approach that integrates different dimensions: social, economic, cultural, and environmental.

Brazzaville and Kinshasa are the two closest capitals in the world and belong to two countries that share the same name, which is that of the second longest river in Africa after the Nile and the second largest in the world in terms of flow: the Congo River. 
Both Congos are very rich in biodiversity thanks to the Congo Basin forest and essential mines for new technologies found in the DRC like Cobalt, Coltan and so on but also oil and the Mayombe forest in Congo Brazzaville and many others.
So all Congolese therefore have a good reason to work together to protect the ecosystem of both Congo.

The problem of climate change and global warming concerns all countries in the world and unfortunately there is the problem of climate justice because of inequalities between the poorest and richest countries, people from poor countries suffer the most. 

Each country must take its responsibilities because unfortunately the consequences linked to this climate crisis or ecological crisis do not exclude any country, rich or poor. For that reason, let us join our forces and let's work together hand in hand to save our common home which is burning. 

In Africa, we have the philosophy of Ubuntu which is not just for african people because it concerns all human beings and the translation is "I am because we are": humanity toward others. That is what Congolese have understood and work together through integral ecology for the sustainable development of the two Congo.

Name: National Trust for Nature Conservation (NTNC), Nepal
INSTITUTO DA BIODIVERSIDADE
Description: Soluções ambientais sustentáveis.

Name: Dunia conservation
Description:
Dunia Center for Wildlife Conservation's key purpose is to create a network of researchers and learners by ensuring the exchange of experience and expertise. We envision a future where there is a synchrony between environmental education and field work to create holistic learning. Through our programs, Interns gain hands-on experience in the field. 

We are based in Amboseli, Kenya, but we shall be operating different field study sessions across Kenya. Through our programs, we ensure that students are involved in research and conservation activities. We also ensure that students gain entrepreneurial skills to create jobs as well.
Our research aims to ensure we save our wildlife through a people-based approach.
We aim to instill in the students a broad global view. Living in another part of the world opens your mind by engaging different cultures.  Students learn skills for collaboration, sharing, and giving, each of which is a valuable life skill that transcends career choices and contributes to greater confidence and maturity. Our Vision is aligned with World 17 sustainable Global goals by the United Nations. Here are the goals we’re addressing.

SDG 4 - Quality education
SDG 14 - Life below water
SDG 15 - Life on land
SDG 17 - Partnerships for the goals

Our inspiration is Wildlife Conservation through Research and Wildlife Education for the youth.

Name: Red para la conservación de Tortugas de Bolivia
Description: Sitio para la difusión de información sobre la diversidad y conservación de las tortugas en Bolivia

Name: SaveThePlanet
Description:
Save The Planet est un projet de jeu de société imaginé et développé par les étudiants du Master Marketing Direct et Digital (MDD) de l’IAE de Lille.

Notre objectif :
 sensibiliser à l’écologie numérique et aux enjeux environnementaux du digital
 de manière ludique, accessible et intergénérationnelle

À travers le jeu, nous voulons :
- encourager la réflexion collective
- provoquer le débat
- montrer que chaque décision a un impact

Save The Planet, c’est l’ambition de transformer des sujets complexes en une expérience de jeu engageante, pensée pour apprendre, échanger et décider ensemble.

Name: LECO_LIBERIA
Description: Liberia Environmental and Conservation Organization (LECO) is an innovative, community-driven organization dedicated to preserving Liberia’s environment, advocating for sustainable environmental governance, and providing alternative livelihoods for communities that depend on the forest. Through environmental awareness campaigns, sustainable livelihood programs, and research, LECO aims to address pressing environmental issues such as deforestation, land degradation, and biodiversity loss. LECO’s focus is on empowering communities with knowledge and tools for sustainable living while protecting Liberia’s rich natural resources for future generations.

Name: vzw Groothertogdom Flandrensis
Description:
Flandrensis (official: vzw Groothertogdom Flandrensis) is a Belgian environmental non-profit organisation that focuses on climate change and unites people worldwide who are concerned about global warming. Flandrensis is committed to informing, raising awareness and mobilising citizens about climate problems and encourages them to implement climate-friendly and sustainable measures in everyday life.
An important field of attention is Antarctica. Flandrensis strives to mobilise government leaders worldwide to take strong climate action and to have the entire Antarctic – within the spirit of the Antarctic Treaty – recognised as an internationally protected nature reserve in order to protect and restore wildlife and flora.

To achieve this objective, Flandrensis uses the concept of a “micronation”. It means that the non-profit organisation has the characteristics of a “state”, but which as such is not recognised under international law. The concept of micronationalism is applied to draw attention to climate in a creative way and has no purpose in gaining territorial recognition from the international community. As part of the micronational concept, Flandrensis has developed cultural symbols and characteristics that help promote the cohesion of the organisation and the achievement of its objectives.
In addition, the micronation also aims to let young people and adults develop their talents in a creative way in areas of governance, media, artistic design, communication, etc. and to organize local (cultural and- or ecological) activities. The practical implementation of the micronation is described in an internal regulation, officialy known as the Flandrensian Constitution.

Name: Algoma Highlands Conservancy
Description: The Algoma Highlands Conservancy is a not-for-profit organization created to protect the beauty and ecological integrity of King Mountain and Robertson Cliffs. Our lands offer an extensive network of trails that are open year-round for non-motorized recreational use.  Goulais, Ontario, Canada.

Name: OASIS SAUVAGE
Description:
Oasis sauvage est une association pour la biodiversité, reconnue d’intérêt général !
Elle permet de financer des actions liées à la protection de la biodiversité, comme la création ou la restauration de mares. 
Oasis sauvage propose également des actions pédagogiques, 
essentielles afin de partager l’émerveillement et de sensibiliser à la Nature.

Name: Asian Turtle Program
Description: To conserve Asia’s tortoises and freshwater turtles by conducting research, protecting habitats, raising awareness and building expertise.

Name: WildStep
Description:
Global wildlife populations have plummeted almost 70% in just 50 years. We need to take steps to protect our wildlife and natural landscapes. WildStep is a part of this movement.

We support small scale projects to protect and rewild land across the UK. We aim to provide all people with a route to support a more natural Britain, and provide opportunities to educate and work with schools and communities to protect land and ensure a healthy local environment.

TAKE A WILDSTEP...

How you can help us improve local environments:

- You can tell us of Land that is need of protecting or rewilding and ask for our help.

Or, you can:

- Sponsor a section of land
- Plant a tree
- Buy products
- Donate / buy products for us
- Make a donation
- Help finance any aspect of securing land

Name: Un Alto en el Desierto
Description:
Somos la primera red de cosechadores y recicladores de AGUA para adaptarnos al Cambio Climático y mitigar las crónicas sequías al sur del Desierto de Atacama en Chile.
Trabajamos para desarrollar soluciones que fomenten la conciencia ambiental y la participación comunitaria y además inspiren un cambio positivo en la forma en que interactuamos con nuestro entorno. Juntos, estamos construyendo un futuro más resiliente y sostenible para las generaciones venideras.

Name: Moyamba Junction Animal Sanctuary and Nature Reserve
Description:
Moyamba Junction Animal Sanctuary & Nature Reserve is a newly established conservation initiative dedicated to protecting Sierra Leone’s vulnerable wildlife and plant species, including over 1,500 critically endangered animals and plants.

We focus on rescuing and rehabilitating wildlife, preserving natural habitats, and maintaining vital ecosystems, while promoting environmental education, sustainable practices, and community engagement.

Our vision is to build a sanctuary that safeguards biodiversity, inspires awareness, and supports both nature and local communities across Sierra Leone .

Name: COLUSA COUNTY RESOURCE CONSERVATION DISTRICT
Description: COLUSA COUNTY RESOURCE CONSERVATION DISTRICT is a company based out of 100 SUNRISE BLVD., STE A-3, COLUSA, California, United States.

Name: INTEGRAL ECOLOGY RESEARCH CENTER
Description:
At its heart, IERC’s mission is to perform ecological research, and compile, organize, and disseminate the results of this research in order to increase scientific knowledge for informed conservation.

Vision: A future in which wildlife, wild lands, and watersheds thrive because decision-making is informed by rigorous science, accessible data, and collaborative stewardship.

Scientific integrity & rigor: Ensuring that all research is peer-reviewed, transparent, and reliable.

Open data & accessibility: Making datasets, methods, and results available so that others can build on them.

Conservation relevance: Focusing on research questions with real-world importance, from habitat fragmentation to toxicants to ecosystem restoration.

Collaboration & partnership: Working across disciplines, agencies, academic institutions, indigenous communities, and citizen scientists to achieve broader impact.

Education & training: Building capacity among emerging ecologists, students, and community partners.

Name: Wild Connections
Description:
Wild Connections protects and restores wild nature and public lands across the watersheds of the Arkansas and South Platte Rivers in central Colorado. We recognize these as the ancestral lands of the Ute, Cheyenne, Arapaho and other indigenous peoples.
Connecting Wildernesses and roadless areas in a landscape-wide wildlands network across the central Rockies benefits migratory animals, rare plant communities, clean water resources for urban and mountain communities , local economies, quiet recreation, historic and cultural locations, scenic vistas and spiritual values.

Name: Zakouma National Park
Description:
Le Parc National de Zakouma est le plus ancien parc du Tchad, créé en 1963 par le gouvernement de la République du Tchad. Il est géré par l’ONG African Parks en partenariat avec le gouvernement depuis 2010.

Zakouma abrite une biodiversité unique propre à la zone sahélo-soudanienne, comprenant notamment des éléphants de savane, des girafes de Kordofan, des lions ainsi que des centaines d’espèces d’oiseaux. Le parc est également le principal employeur des régions du Salamat et du Guéra, et contribue au développement communautaire à travers la promotion des économies locales, l’éducation, la santé et le tourisme.

 Visitez le parc en reservant votre séjour:
tingacamp@africanparks.org

Name: MyGreenCity
Description:
Welkom bij MyGreenCity – een actief platform voor duurzame ondernemers en initiatieven in Nederland. 

Onze missie: het verbinden en versterken van duurzame ondernemers en organisaties die samen bouwen aan een groenere toekomst.

Wat we bieden:
• Een actief netwerk van duurzame ondernemers
• Zichtbaarheid voor jouw duurzame initiatief
• Kennisdeling en expertisenetwerk
• Duurzame evenementen en meetups
• Verificatie van duurzame producten en diensten

Voor wie?
• Duurzame ondernemers en start-ups
• Bedrijven die willen verduurzamen
• Organisaties met focus op MVO
• Innovators in de circulaire economie

Word lid van MyGreenCity en:
✓ Vergroot je netwerk
✓ Versterk je zichtbaarheid
✓ Versnel je duurzame impact

Samen maken we de samenleving leefbaarder, duurzamer en toekomstbestendig. Start vandaag nog en sluit je aan - bezoek www.mygreencity.nl

#duurzaamondernemen #sustainability #mygreencity #duurzameimpact #mvo #circulaireeconomie #sdgs #duurzaamnetwerk

Name: Initiative pour une Biodiversité Durable aux Comores - IBDC
Wild Connection
Description:
Wild Connection, located in South Africa's KwaZulu-Natal, specialises in immersive, hands-on experiences with the Big Five and other wildlife. Our mission is to promote conservation awareness, educate on wildlife preservation, and inspire a love for nature through impactful programs.

Our base camp near Nambiti Private Game Reserve offers a range of activities for students.

Engage in Conservation
- Participate in vital conservation activities: predator monitoring, rhino education, and anti-poaching efforts.

- Contribute to wildlife research, collecting data that aids in protecting against poaching and habitat loss.

- Immerse yourself in reserve maintenance to support the thriving ecosystem.

Name: La Pieve Oserian Wildlife Sanctuary
Description: Oserengoni Wildlife Sanctuary

Name: Aire Marine Protégée Communautaire du Gandoule
Description:
Description de l'Aire Marine Communautaire Protégée du Gandoul
L'Aire Marine Communautaire Protégée du Gandoul, située dans les Îles du Saloum, abrite une biodiversité exceptionnelle incluant des mangroves, herbiers marins, et récifs coralliens. Les communautés locales jouent un rôle clé dans sa gestion durable, combinant conservation et développement économique. Ce sanctuaire écologique est un modèle de protection marine, attirant écotouristes grâce à ses paysages pittoresques et ses initiatives de durabilité.

Name: Reforest The World n.p.w.t. i.L.
Description:
REFOREST-THE-WORLD tritt mit Wiederbewaldung und Renaturierung dem fortschreitenden Klimawandel entgegen. Im Focus steht der Abbau des klimaschädlichen CO2. Renaturierung durch Wiederbewaldung ist nachweislich die erfolgreichste Möglichkeit, auf natürliche Weise den für Klimawandel verantwortlichen CO2-Überschuß in der Atmosphäre abzubauen. 

Die Wiederbewaldung erfolgt NICHT zu forstwirtschaftlichen Zwecken. REFOREST-THE-WORLD erbringt die Initialpflanzung. Die gepflanzten Waldflächen werden anfangs gepflegt und dann der Sukzession, der Wald sich also selbst überlassen.

Name: Réseau de Developpement des Réserves naturelles Communautaires (REDERC)
Description:
Le REDERC, qui commença ses activités par la création du Jardin Botanique de Papatia en 2001, fut créé par la communauté villageoise locale de Papatia pour résoudre les problèmes d'exploitation des ressources naturelles, en réunissant plusieurs groupes ethniques. Fort de l'expérience acquise dans le cadre de la création du Jardin Botanique de Papatia, le REDERC a partagé ses connaissances avec les autres communes avoisinantes. Plusieurs d'entre elles ont approché le comité de direction du Jardin, dans l'espoir de créer leurs propres zones protégées. Guérisseurs traditionnels, apiculteurs, fermiers, planteurs, groupes de femmes, étudiants, et bien d'autres groupes encore, s'organisèrent pour atteindre un objectif commun : protéger la biodiversité régionale dont ils dépendent tous. Toutes ces activités ont pour objet de protéger ou de rétablir la biodiversité en créant des zones communautaires protégées (comme le Jardin Botanique) qui sont au cœur d'une immense variété d'activités de préservation de l'environnement visant à générer des revenus. 

Domaines d'intervention: 
- Valorisation de la médecine traditionnelle
- Éducation environnementale
- Promotion de l’apiculture
- Promotion du maraichage
- Valorisation des espèces faunique en voie de disparition (Parc animalier)
- Création d’aires protégées à différents niveaux (aires protégées communautaires, sacrées et scolaires)
- Développement local et régional
- Protection et utilisation durable des ressources naturelles locales générant des revenus

Name: Takamanda Wildlife Conservation
Description: To ensure that the planet will still exist tomorrow, we combine species conservation, economic efficiency and social justice into a coherent overall package. We see ourselves as pioneers of an economy in which people experience the preservation of their livelihood as a benefit and actively participate in it.

Name: Wild Friends
Description:
Wild Friends es una plataforma para que los santuarios de vida silvestre de todo el mundo  puedan recibir donaciones a través de la tecnología. 

Apoyar a los animales del mundo real a través de un videojuego para interactuar con las versiones digitales de los animales.

Name: RePollinate
Description: RePollinate’s purpose is to conserve and enhance the UK’s threatened pollinators. This objective will be met through improving education on the importance of pollinators and their conservation, supporting scientific research on pollinators, working with policymakers at local and national levels, and working with communities and businesses to create a network of high quality pollinator habitats.

Name: Marataba Contractual National Park
Description: As a contractual National Park, Marataba is one of South Africa’s most innovative conservation models. From the vision of an iconic president to the unique public-private partnership that exists today, we protect the area’s diverse flora and fauna through modern and hands-on conservation practices.

Name: Association des Jeunes pour l'Environnement et l'Education Civique (AJEEC -NIGER)
Description:
L’Association des Jeunes pour l’Environnement et l’Education Civique au Niger (AJEEC-Niger) est 
une organisation de développement crée en 2016.Elle est à but non lucratif et non confessionnelle, qui œuvre 
pour la protection et à la préservation de l’environnement et la promotion de la citoyenneté responsable. 
Depuis sa création AJEEC s’est positionnée sur quatre thématiques (i) la lutte contre la désertification et la 
dégradation des ressources naturelles ; (ii) l’autonomisation et promotions des droits des femmes et des 
jeunes ; (iii) la participation citoyenne des jeunes et (iv) l’écocitoyenneté.

Name: Eau Vie Environnement
Description:
Développement durable & changement climatique
Une population informée, formée et motivée est capable de relever les défis de son développement avec ses propres ressources et celles de son environnement.

Name: Forests and Livelihood Organization
Description:
Forests and Livelihood Organization (FLO) is a local NGO, funded in 2010 and based in Kratie Town, Kratie Province, in North-Eastern Cambodia. 

FLO's vision is for local communities in Northeastern Cambodia to enjoy ownership rights over their natural resources and exercise their rights for dignified sustainable livelihoods. 

FLO helps the local communities to practice sustainable natural resource management and improve their livelihoods through wise socio-economic development. 

FLO supports the communities to strengthen their advocacy capacities for the recognition of their tenure over their natural resources.

Name: Hara Rang lagaya kya
Description: Our aim is to create positive thinking and a sense of responsibility towards nature in the minds of people through right actions and  proper examples. We all have to work together to make this miserable state of nature happy. We have to make this lovely earth of ours a green planet. So, have you planted green plants to make this earth green?

Name: Mashujaa wa Bahari
Description: Mashujaa wa Bahari is a marine conservation initiative dedicated to promoting ocean literacy along the Kenyan coast through art, education, and community engagement.

Name: Rumah Coral Bali
Description: Rumah Coral Bali is a Non-Profit Organization focuses on marine conservation based in Bali, Indonesia. We've been working on several coral restoration project and collaboration with few different methods. Our coral restoration sites located in Sanur and Amed.

Name: Fundación Ecosistemas en Acción
Description: La Fundación Ecosistemas en Acción (Eco-Acción) es una organización de la sociedad civil conformada por jóvenes, nuestra misión es mejorar la relación humano-naturaleza de comunidades rurales e indígenas al combinar el conocimiento científico con la sabiduría tradicional comunitaria.

Name: 50 Ducks
Description:
At 50 Ducks, we are pioneers in conservation and wildlife tracking technology, offering the only platform that provides live stream access to users for real-time observation of waterfowl. Our mission is to gather, analyze, and disseminate comprehensive data on the migratory patterns, behaviors, and habitats of various waterfowl species through advanced GPS tracking systems.

We are committed to utilizing cutting-edge technology to monitor and record the intricate journeys of these fascinating birds, providing invaluable insights for research, conservation efforts, and educational initiatives. Our passion for the outdoors and dedication to preserving our natural world drive our platform's endeavors.

50 Ducks stands out by not only aiming to raise awareness about the importance of conservation and the vital roles these creatures play in our ecosystems but also by engaging the community directly through our unique live streaming feature. This direct engagement allows users to witness the beauty and complexity of waterfowl migration firsthand.

We engage in ongoing partnerships with educational institutions, researchers, and conservationists, expanding our impact and fostering a greater understanding of waterfowl among the public. Join us on this extraordinary journey as we uncover the secrets of the skies, promote sustainable practices, and provide a window into the natural world like never before.

Name: MAMIDJA ENVIRONNEMENT
Description: Organisme environnemental à but non lucratif

Name: Heart Lake Turtle Troopers
Description: Our mission is to support the protection and monitoring of the local turtle population through a citizen science volunteer program, by working in partnership with community stakeholders to raise community awareness, recruit and engage citizen volunteers and organize and deliver public engagement activities.

Name: Crocodile Research Coalition
Description: A nonprofit organization devoted to promoting conservation of crocodiles and their habitats throughout Central America and the Caribbean through community involvement, research, and education.

Name: Wyoming Conservation Corps
Description:
"The Wyoming Conservation Corps’s mission is to promote individual development, stewardship, and education through localized national service."

The WCC is a non-profit dedicated to empowering youth in conservation around Wyoming. What sets the Wyoming Conservation Corps (WCC) apart from nearly all the other conservation corps is that we provide formal university level courses as part of the crew leader’s and member’s training. In the spring, crew leaders participate in upper level undergraduate course 4950-Leadership in Natural Resource Management while our members in the summer partake in 3700-Conservation Leadership offered by the University of Wyoming  as well as wilderness medical classes and a robust month of field training. The WCC takes our education component seriously and it is, in part, why our members and leaders go on to manage Wyoming and as well as other states’ natural resources. We believe that having a fully educated team working in the field makes for better work, morale, and a further interest in the projects.

Name: Projeto Conservação Recifal (PCR)
Description: O Projeto Conservação Recifal (PCR) busca a conservação dos ecossistemas costeiros marinhos com foco na preservação e manutenção da biodiversidade dos recifes de corais. O PCR promove a conscientização e a mobilização na sociedade sobre as ameaças ao ecossistema marinho, e a importância da conservação dos ecossistemas. Nossas atividades também estão focadas na pesquisa científica com a realização de mergulhos para censo visual analisando peixes e corais para acompanhar possíveis modificações na estrutura da comunidade relacionadas a impactos antrópicos, incluindo gestão de recursos pesqueiros e recuperação das Áreas Marinhas Protegidas. Atuando principalmente na APA Costa dos Corais, maior unidade de conservação costeiro-marinha do Brasil. Além de trabalhos desenvolvidos em Fernando de Noronha, Recife, Ilha de Itamaracá e APA Recifes-Serrambi. O PCR aprovou nos últimos anos mais de 10 projetos com apoio de diversas instituições nacionais e internacionais como Rufford Small Grant (RSG), Mohamed Bin Zayed Species Conservation Fund, Conservation Leadership Programme (CLP), WWF e Fundação Grupo Boticário.

Name: St. Andrew and St. Joseph Bay Estuary Program
Description:
St. Andrew and St. Joseph Bay Estuary Program at FSU Panama City

The Vision: Healthy, resilient bays and estuarine habitats that support native species, natural systems, recreation, fisheries and the economy, together with vibrant, resilient, and sustainable communities in the watershed that live in harmony with the needs of the estuary.

The Mission: To collaborate—as representatives of private, public, and non-governmental stakeholders—to improve our common sense, science-based understanding of the needs of the estuary, and to develop, promote and implement projects that protect and restore the health of the bays.

Name: OceanLove Foundation
Description:
OceanLove Foundation is dedicated to installing the global network of Cetacea Migration Pathways/Corridors to and from marine sanctuaries, and the bays where the cetacea regenerate and calf as a place of refuge for native dolphins and migrating whales in view of cetacea stranding prevention.

Tax deductions available in the UK and the USA
UK Foundation https://validaid.org/fundraiser/155
US Foundation https://validaid.org/fundraiser/143

Your Donations support:
Installing Cetacea Migration Pathways/Corridors to and from Marine Sanctuaries and Marine Monuments. 
Addressing The Cetacea Treaty to the International Whaling Commission and to the United Nations.
Developing The Cetacea Livestream platform for researchers, the ocean people, the International yachting and maritime community who wish to participate in bringing together the global network of Cetacea Migration Pathways/Corridors.
The Cetacea Treaty Vessel and Crew world tour mission.

NMorley is developing The Cetacea Livestream website for marine conservation to gain further understanding on how the whales communicate to each other worldwide, cetacea conservation and stranding prevention for Governments member of IWC and to the United Nations to install the global network of Cetacea Migration Pathways.

NMorley welcomes the partners for The Cetacea Treaty Vessel and Crew world tour mission.

OceanLove Foundation is a hosted partner of HOST: Ally for Action registered as Climate 2025 Ltd in the UK, who are partners with Chapel and York UK Foundation, and Chapel & York US Foundation.

The Chapel & York UK Foundation charity No 1172653                                 
The Chapel & York US Foundation is a registered 501(c)(3) with EIN 812161937  

For more information please contact-us via OceanLove.org website. Thank you

Copyright1999/2025 Natasha Morley/OceanLove Foundation. All Rights Reserved.

Name: UMUSHINGE
Description:
Welcome to the UMUSHINGE LinkedIn page!
UMUSHINGE is a pioneering company in the production of eco-friendly bricks in Burundi, founded by environmental enthusiasts dedicated to combating deforestation and promoting sustainable construction. Our mission is to revolutionize the construction sector by offering environmentally friendly alternatives while supporting the local economy and creating green jobs.

Our Values

We are committed to promoting sustainability and social entrepreneurship through:

Equity: Encouraging active participation of women in our initiatives.
Integrity: Upholding the quality and specificity of our products.
Respect: Fostering a collaborative and rewarding work environment.

Our Products

Our eco-friendly bricks are designed to reduce construction costs while minimizing the impact on forest resources. Made from a blend of earth, cement, and sand, they require less cement than traditional bricks, contributing to environmental preservation.

Name: Rhinomics
Description:
Rhinomics is a multinational initiative, working to develop a sustainable, legal, rhino horn trading model that is ethical, regulated, and traceable. A model that ensures the legal trade of rhino horn that promotes sustainable use, biodiversity protection, and equitable economic growth and transformation.

We work across nine rhino-range countries and with key decision makers in Africa, Asia, and Europe. We are guided by Rhino Elders and Rhino Vets a group of conservationists, scientists, economists, and wildlife management specialists who together contribute decades of seminal knowledge and on-the-ground rhino management expertise. Our partners inform our strategy to save and recover these species and broaden support for the Rhinomics conservation model.

We employ a multi-faceted approach to address the rhino poaching crisis that includes traditional strategies with new and improved ideas and technologies. These include improved security and management tools, technology for forensics, traceability and transparency of horns, and a sustainable finance mechanism for rhino management and protection.

We focus our efforts on Policy, Science, Research, Technology, and Finance interventions that we believe are the most important areas for Rhino Conservation today.

Name: Syndicat National des Professionnels de la Spéléologie et du Canyonisme
Description:
Au delà d'un regroupement professionnel qui défend ses intérêts et participe au développement de l’activité, le SNPSC est avant tout une grande famille dans un petit milieu, militant pour une pratique digne et responsable de ses activités.

Nos mots d'ordre sont : ÉTHIQUE, DÉONTOLOGIE, PARTAGE , ÉCHANGE, DÉCOUVERTE, OBSERVATION, COMPRÉHENSION, ÉMERVEILLEMENT ET PRÉSERVATION.

Name: Ocean Thinkers
Description:
By connecting with local projects and developing ocean literacy skills, we believe children have the power to tackle ocean conservation head-on. 

We are Ocean Thinkers, a social enterprise on a mission to inspire the next generation to learn more about our oceans, make a positive impact, and contribute to a hopeful future. 

We are designing inclusive social solutions with human needs front and centre. We need to turn to First Nations peoples who have successfully cared for country for millennia, and marine biologists who see firsthand the impact we can have.

Together with Australian children, we will raise much-needed funds and awareness to support the projects that care for our ocean.

Name: Yana Dive Tek
Description:
Passionnés par l’aventure, la plongée technique, et les découvertes scientifiques, les fondateurs de Yana Dive Tek ont créé l’association pour pouvoir développer la plongée technique à visée scientifique en Guyane.
Les plongées en Guyane, très techniques, demandent une expertise avancée en plongée. L'équipe de plongeurs professionnels (CAH 2B et 3B) organisent régulièrement des formations de plongée en circuit fermé (IANTD), écologie marine (IANTD) et théorie de la décompression (IANTD). L'équipe de plongeurs, entraînés à plonger dans les conditions difficiles de Guyane, organisent également des explorations dans les autres mers et océans du monde (Antilles, Mer Méditerranée, Océan Indien...)

Name: Gudlai Camp
Description: Climate adaptation and biodiversity lab in the Western Ghats, Wayanad using Design, Art, Education, Architecture, Terraforming and Agroforestry as tools and points of intervention

Name: Protect Sierra Leone
Description:
The Protect Sierra Leone Program, launched by the Ministry of the Environment and Climate Change, represents a bold and ambitious initiative aimed at safeguarding the nation’s rich biodiversity and natural resources. Among its many goals, this program is dedicated to achieving the Global Biodiversity Framework (GBF) 30x30 targets, which aim to protect 30% of land and marine areas by 2030.

By focusing on sustainable conservation practices, habitat restoration, and community engagement, Protect Sierra Leone aspires to create a resilient and thriving environment where both nature and communities can flourish. This initiative underscores the nation's commitment to preserving its unique ecosystems for future generations while contributing to global biodiversity goals.

We invite you to explore our website to learn more about the visionary plans the Ministry has developed for the future of Sierra Leone.

Name: Parques Nacionales Naturales de Colombia
Description: Administramos los Parques Nacionales Naturales y coordinamos el Sistema Nacional de Áreas Protegidas (SINAP) de Colombia, promoviendo la participación de diversos actores, con el propósito de conservar la diversidad biológica y cultural del país, contribuyendo al desarrollo sostenible y a un medio ambiente sano.

Name: InNature - Insel der Vielfalt
Description:
Über InNature auf LinkedIn

Willkommen auf dem offiziellen LinkedIn-Kanal von InNature!

Unsere Mission: Wildbienen schützen und die nächste Generation für Nachhaltigkeit begeistern. 

Was wir tun:
 Wir veranstalten Workshops an Schulen, um Kindern und Jugendlichen die Bedeutung von Wildbienen und Biodiversität näherzubringen.
 Gemeinsam mit den Schüler*innen gestalten wir anschließend Lebensräume für Wildbienen.

Unser Ziel ist es, nicht nur Wissen zu vermitteln, sondern auch direkt aktiv zu werden und die Natur vor Ort zu stärken.

Hier auf LinkedIn teilen wir:
 Spannende Einblicke in unsere Schulprojekte
 Tipps, wie jeder einen Beitrag zur Artenvielfalt leisten kann
 Möglichkeiten, wie Unternehmen und Organisationen uns unterstützen können

Lasst uns gemeinsam ein Bewusstsein für Nachhaltigkeit schaffen und Lebensräume gestalten – für Wildbienen und für uns alle. 

Folgt uns, um Teil dieser Mission zu werden und unsere Arbeit zu unterstützen!

Instagram: @innature.school

Name: Iniciativa para Desenvolvimento e Resiliência Climática
Description: Somos uma organização sem fins lucrativos dedicada ao desenvolvimento sustentável e à resiliência comunitária. Guiados pelos valores de imparcialidade, humanismo e honestidade, buscamos promover iniciativas que equilibram o crescimento econômico com a preservação ambiental. Junte-se a nós para construir um futuro onde a natureza e as comunidades prosperam em harmonia.  #Sustentabilidade #ResiliênciaComunitária #DesenvolvimentoImpactante

Name: Espoo Convention and its Protocol on Strategic Environmental Assessment
Description:
Adopted in Espoo, Finland, in 1991, the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in a Transboundary Context (Espoo Convention), serviced by UNECE, is a key international legal instrument and intergovernmental platform designed to prevent, reduce, and manage significant transboundary environmental impacts. It requires Parties to assess environmental impacts of certain activities early in the planning process and to notify and consult potentially affected countries on projects with cross-border effects.

Originally negotiated as a regional instrument, the Convention builds on principles from the 1972 Stockholm Conference on the Human Environment, emphasizing the responsibility of Parties to ensure activities within their jurisdiction do not harm the environment of other countries. Over time, it has evolved into a practical framework for cooperation, transparency, and good neighborly relations through transboundary EIA.

Through its programme of work, the Convention supports countries by strengthening capacities, promoting exchange of experience and good practices, and facilitating implementation at national and transboundary levels. It covers sectors such as energy, transport, extractive industries, and infrastructure development, contributing to sustainable development and environmental protection.

The Protocol on Strategic Environmental Assessment (SEA) complements the Convention by integrating environmental considerations into plans and programmes from the earliest decision-making stages. In force since 2010, it also promotes public participation and transparency, helping countries make informed decisions and advance sustainable development.

Name: St.Vincent and the Grenadines Environment Fund
Description:
The St. Vincent and the Grenadines Environment Fund is a non-profit company registered in St. Vincent and the Grenadines to support sustainable initiatives to promote the preservation of the beauty and natural value of the islands.
We are a group of Vincentian and international friends of St. Vincent and the Grenadines Islands driven by confidence that these islands have an opportunity to evolve positively whilst preserving their unique history, culture, and environmental heritage

Name: KlimaNetzwerk Frechen e.V.
Description:
Frechen 
 Unternehmen 
 Netzwerk 
 Klima

Name: Ilbirs Foundation
Description: Ilbirs Foundation is a non-profit environmental organization dedicated to conserving Kyrgyzstan’s unique biodiversity, with a focus on snow leopards and mountain ecosystems. Founded in 2016, we combine scientific research, community engagement, and policy support to protect wildlife while promoting sustainable livelihoods in high mountain regions. Our work spans wildlife conservation, environmental education, and applied research on climate change and rare species, strengthening both local stewardship and international collaboration for nature conservation in Kyrgyzstan.

Name: RangerLife Africa
Description:
RangerLife Africa is dedicated to ensuring the well being of Africa's wildlife protectors—the rangers who risk their lives daily to safeguard endangered species and vulnerable ecosystems. Every member pays for a life cover policy for a Ranger. 

We offer a unique membership program where you can directly fund a life insurance policy for these brave individuals.

£50 Membership funds life insurance for 1 ranger for a year.
£200 Membership supports life insurance for 5 rangers for a year.
£2,000 Membership provides life cover for 50 rangers for a year.

By joining this mission, you’re not just helping rangers,you’re ensuring the safety and well-being of their families and communities.

Name: Eik Partners
Description:
Bij Eik Partners zijn we toegewijd aan het versterken van MKB-bedrijven met een duurzame aanpak die verder gaat dan alleen zaken doen. Doe mee met onze missie om bossen te behouden en te beschermen door samen te werken in onze unieke bospercelen.

 Hoe het werkt:

1. Kies uw Beschermingsperceel: Kies uit onze diverse selectie bospercelen die beschikbaar zijn voor beheer, elk bijdragend aan het behoud en de bescherming van vitale ecosystemen.
2. Bosbeheer: Zie hoe uw bedrijf een essentiële rol speelt in het behoud van biodiversiteit, het beschermen van leefgebieden van wilde dieren en het waarborgen van de langetermijngezondheid van de longen van onze planeet.
3. Koolstofopslag: Ervaar de kracht van de natuur terwijl onze bossen actief koolstofemissies absorberen en opslaan, wat aanzienlijk bijdraagt aan de koolstofneutrale doelen van uw bedrijf.
4. Gepersonaliseerde marketingmaterialen: Ontvang gepersonaliseerde marketingmateriaal waarin uw toewijding aan bosbehoud en milieuvriendelijke praktijken wordt getoond.
5. Engage en groei: Benut uw milieu-inspanningen voor merkgroei, klantenloyaliteit en een positieve impact op de gemeenschap, allemaal geworteld in het behoud van onze kostbare bossen.

 Sluit je bij ons aan op de weg naar een duurzame toekomst. Laten we samen een groene groei realiseren! #MKB #Duurzaamheid #Bosbehoud #Natuurbehoud

Name: Vape Waste Project
Description: Our mission is to facilitate safe lithium battery recycling and reduce disposable vape waste to conserve and protect the environment. We intend to educate the public and promote recycling in order to ensure a safer and healthier community.

Name: North Branch Land Trust
Description:
North Branch Land Trust (NBLT) partners with landowners, communities, and stakeholders to conserve and care for Northeastern Pennsylvania’s lands and waters.

NBLT is a registered 501(c)(3) conservation nonprofit operating in Northeastern Pennsylvania.  NBLT conserves in an eight-county region of Pennsylvania (Bradford, Susquehanna, Sullivan, Wayne, Wyoming, Lackawanna, Luzerne, and Columbia) in the Susquehanna and Upper Lehigh Watersheds.

Name: United Tribes of Bristol Bay
Description: United Tribes of Bristol Bay is a Tribal consortium working to protect the traditional Yup’ik, Dena’ina, and Alutiiq ways of life in Southwest Alaska that depend on the pristine Bristol Bay Watershed and all it sustains, most notably Bristol Bay’s wild salmon.

Name: Réserve marine de Palavas-les-Flots
Description:
Au large de Palavas-les-Flots, la Réserve Marine de la Côte Palavasienne, également appelée RMCP, protège 100 hectares de vie marine exceptionnelle. 

Entre rochers comme Porquières et Couçansa et fonds sableux, de nombreuses espèces marines trouvent refuge : sars, dorades, girelles et gorgones. 

Cette réserve, créée en 2016, en collaboration avec les pêcheurs et la ville, a pour objectif de préserver les ressources marines, soutenir la pêche artisanale et sensibiliser à la protection de la mer.
Cette zone est gérée par l’Association de Gestion de la Réserve Marine de la Côte Palavasienne (AGRMCP) qui est chargée de protéger et valoriser la biodiversité marine au large de Palavas-les-Flots en collaboration avec la Prud’homie des patrons pêcheurs de Palavas-les-Flots.

Name: Water UCI
Description: Water UCI is an interdisciplinary center in the UC Irvine School of Social Ecology that facilitates seamless collaboration across schools, departments, and existing research centers around questions of fundamental and applied water science, technology, management, and policy.

Name: Reserva Tesoro Escondido
Description:
El trabajo de la reserva Tesoro Escondido se centra en:
- Investigación científica junto con universidades nacionales e internacionales, científicos, estudiantes y nuestro equipo de parabiólogos.
- Programa de educación ambiental permanente en 10 comunidades.
- Proyectos comunitarios como jardines agroecológicos y reforestación.
- Apoyo a proyectos como ecoturismo y pequeños negocios de chocolate en comunidades locales.

Name: Fundación Habitando Conservación
Description:
Somos una entidad sin ánimo de lucro que trabaja en pro del conocimiento, investigación y conservación de la biodiversidad en Colombia, apostándole a la apropiación del territorio por parte de las comunidades. 
Nos constituimos a partir de un grupo de trabajo interdisciplinar, con el objetivo de promover y facilitar proyectos y programas de investigación, que se articulen con iniciativas de apropiación del territorio y el conocimiento por parte de las comunidades. Lo anterior a través de tres ejes: Promoción de la investigación en ciencias básicas, articulación de esfuerzos con comunidades y producción sostenible.

Name: Programa Jucumari
Description:
The Jucumari Program seeks to promote the coexistence between the Andean Bear and the local communities that live with it, combining "local knowledge" with scientific knowledge, through research, training, education, outreach and development of sustainable initiatives.

We yearn for the "common good", where people can live with dignity and species can develop freely in their habitat.

Name: AquaProtecta FR
Description: AquaProtecta se concentre sur la protection contre les inondations et les dégâts des eaux. Nos solutions sont sur-mesure. Elles comprennent des cloisons et barrières anti-inondation qui – nous l’espérons – n’auront pas à faire preuve de leur efficacité, mais qui sont 100 % opérationnelles lorsque le besoin s’en fait sentir.

Name: Rettet den Regenwald - Schweiz
Description:
Rettet den Regenwald hat das klare Ziel, die Regenwälder dieser Erde als einzigartige Ökosysteme und Lebensräume für Menschen, Tiere und Pflanzen zu erhalten.
Wir unterstützen Partnerorganisationen weltweit dabei, Regenwaldzerstörung aufzudecken und zu verhindern. 
Als Schweizer Schwesterorganisation von Rettet den Regenwald e.V. arbeiten wir  zusammen und teilen die selben Ziele. Kampagnen und Öffentlichkeitsarbeit von RdR tragen wir in die Schweiz und setzen sie hier um. 

Auch in der Bildungsarbeit sind wir aktiv:
Unser selbstproduziertes Gesellschaftsspiel "Minanga" dient uns als Tool, um Kindern und Jugendlichen die Zusammenhänge zwischen Bergbau, Naturschutz und Menschenrechte nahe zu bringen. 
Minanga kann im privaten Rahmen gespielt, aber auch in der Schule mit Begleitmaterialien im Unterricht eingesetzt werden. 
Gerne kommen wir in den Unterricht oder stellen das Spiel auf Lehrerseminaren vor. Alle Informationen finden Sie auf unserer Webseite, oder schreiben Sie uns eine Nachricht.

Rettet den Regenwald - Schweiz (Juni 2023) ist aus der Luzerner NGO bergbau menschen rechte entstanden und fokussiert sich weiterhin auf die Zerstörung von Regenwaldgebieten und auf Menschen, die durch den Abbau von Rohstoffen bedroht sind.

https://linkup.top/regenwald

Name: Reseau Climat Jeunesse Burundi RCJ-Burundi en sigle
Description:
Le Réseau Climat Jeunesse Burundi (RCJ-Burundi) est une organisation engagée dans la promotion des initiatives climatiques et environnementales au Burundi. Il est constitué principalement de jeunes acteurs, d'organisations de la société civile, et de partenaires locaux et internationaux qui se mobilisent pour aborder les défis environnementaux auxquels le pays est confronté.

Voici quelques points clés pour décrire le RCJ-Burundi :

Mission et Objectifs : Le RCJ-Burundi a pour mission de sensibiliser les jeunes aux enjeux climatiques, de promouvoir des pratiques durables et d'encourager l'engagement actif dans les initiatives de protection de l'environnement. Il vise également à influencer les politiques publiques en matière de climat et d'environnement pour qu'elles prennent mieux en compte les besoins et les perspectives des jeunes.

Activités et Projets : Les activités du RCJ-Burundi incluent des campagnes de sensibilisation sur les questions climatiques, des formations pour les jeunes et les communautés locales, ainsi que des projets de terrain axés sur la conservation des ressources naturelles et la gestion des déchets. Ils peuvent aussi organiser des événements tels que des conférences, des ateliers et des forums de discussion.

Partenariats : Le réseau collabore avec divers partenaires, y compris des organisations non gouvernementales (ONG), des agences internationales, des institutions académiques, et des autorités locales pour renforcer l'impact de ses initiatives. Ces partenariats permettent d’accéder à des ressources, des expertises, et un soutien technique et financier.

Engagement Communautaire : Le RCJ-Burundi s'efforce d'inclure les communautés locales dans ses projets, en mettant l'accent sur l’implication des jeunes. Cela peut se manifester par des initiatives communautaires telles que des programmes de reforestation, des projets d’agriculture durable, et des actions pour promouvoir la gestion intégrée des ressources en eau.etc......

Name: Elephant Sanctuary Society
Description:
The Elephant Sanctuary Society (ESS) is a passionate student-led society dedicated to raising awareness and supporting conservation efforts that protect one of the world’s most iconic animals, elephants. Founded by students at UNSW, ESS is committed to education, advocacy and global impact, making it one of the few university societies in Australia focused on wildlife conservation and environmental sustainability.

With a growing membership of students from diverse faculties, ESS empowers members to make a difference by:
- Educating students and the community about the threats elephants face, from poaching to habitat destruction
- Advocating for the protection and expansion of sanctuaries as safe havens for elephants
- Partnering with global conservation organisations to support real-world projects
- Providing leadership and volunteering opportunities that enable students to contribute directly to meaningful causes

ESS focuses on uniting students with a shared passion for conservation, offering unique opportunities to connect with like-minded peers and industry leaders in environmental sustainability. Through events and campaigns, members develop valuable skills while contributing to a mission that safeguards elephants for future generations.

Name: Maungu Wildlife Conservancy
Description:
Maungu Wildlife Conservancy is a 53,000-acre wildlife conservancy and ranch located in south-eastern Kenya in Voi, Taita Taveta County.

It is part of the expansive contiguous Tsavo/Mkomazi ecosystem and the greater Kilimanjaro, Amboseli, Tsavo-Mkomazi landscape.

Name: Goshen Global Vision (GGV)
Description: We are a women-led organisation that improves the lives of women and youth in the Sekondi-Takoradi area. We engage in climate smart agriculture to assist farmers to get more yeilds and maintain soil nutrients. We plant native and economic tree species in forest reserves and cocoa farms to combat climate change and deforestation. Sustainable livelihoods is provided for these farmers to encourage financial independence and improve their economic standards. We plant decorative tree specoes along the road median of the Sekondi- Takoradi Metropolis. Our heart is improving the lives of people thus we have set up rabbitaries, small scale farms and have provided seedlings to schools, churches and people's home for urban forestry and improving the microclimate.

Name: Selamatkan Yaki
Description:
Become a Yakisaver! We're working to save the critically endangered Sulawesi Crested Black Macaques (Macaca nigra) and their native habitat. 

Biodiversity monitoring
Community conservation
Ecotourism
Environmental Education
Green Gospel
Species Action Planning
Sustainable Livelihoods
Wildlife Trade Mitigation

Name: Breathe Green
Description:
Breathe Green es un proyecto promovido por la Fundación Pondera que nace con la ambición de recuperar la salud de los territorios y de las comunidades indígenas dónde opera, a través de la reforestación y proyectos de formación y asistencia sanitaria a la población local.

Concretamente en Breathe Green Project, nos dedicamos a restaurar tierras devastadas por las industrias extractivas en la Amazonía. Combinamos la sabiduría ancestral de las comunidades indígenas amazónicas con conocimientos ecológicos modernos para plantar especies endémicas seleccionadas por los locales y respaldadas por ecologistas. De este modo, garantizamos el éxito de nuestros proyectos tanto ecológica como culturalmente.

Somos unos convencidos de que el bienestar del planeta comienza en los pulmones de la Tierra y su salud es el beneficio de todos. Por este motivo, nuestro ámbito de actuación comienza en los territorios dañados por la minería extractiva, la explotación petrolífera y la deforestación por las prácticas de agricultura intensiva dónde la Red Eclesial Panamazónica (REPAM) tiene capacidad de actuación directa y desarrolla desde hace más de 75 años una labor de cuidado social y ambiental.

Tu contribución tiene asignada una línea de acción concreta que persigue la regeneración de la Amazonía ecuatoriana dónde trabajamos para conseguir:
1) La recuperación de 1.000 Ha a través de la reforestación de 1 millón de árboles autóctonos en 3 años y 2) Mantener la viabilidad de los servicios que el Vicariato de Aguarico pone a disposición de la comunidad local: 6 centros de formación, 1 Hospital, 4 Centros de acogida y 2 Laboratorios con fines ambientales.

Únete al movimiento Breathe Green para preservar el medio ambiente. Tu contribución genera conciencia e impulsa acciones concretas que contribuyen al cuidado de la casa común: La Amazonía.

Name: Cloudbridge Nature Reserve
Description:
Cloudbridge is a private nature reserve that stretches from 1550m to 2600m (5085-8530 ft) in the Talamanca mountains of Costa Rica. Since 2002, 255 hectares (630 acres) of cattle pasture or cultivated land with a further 28 hectares (70 acres) of primary forest has been purchased by the reserve. Since it’s beginning, Cloudbridge has been dedicated to the conservation and reforestation of the cloudforest. The Cloudbridge nature reserve is on a mission to reforest areas of degraded agricultural land and nurture those areas back to a climax forest composition.
As we have grown, education and research have played a large part in what we do; teaching the importance of and expanding our knowledge of this unique habitat for wildlife, sustainable tourism and climate change. 
Cloudbridge provides research and volunteer opportunities in one of the world’s most beautiful and biodiverse regions. Participants can pursue independent research, contribute to our ongoing projects, or apply their existing expertise while gaining new skills through hands-on volunteering experiences.
Open to visitors 365 days a year for hiking and wildlife tours, we enable people of all generations and all walks of life to be able to enjoy and learn about this incredible part of Costa Rica.
Location: San Gerardo de Rivas, Perez Zeledon

Name: Kirtland's Warbler Alliance
Description: The Kirtland's Warbler Alliance was created in 2013 to help support conservation of the Kirtland's Warbler, the rarest songbird in North America. The Kirtland's Warbler was on the brink of extinction four decades ago. Today the bird's population is considered safe and secure. That does not mean our conservation efforts can stop. That's because the Kirtland's Warbler is what is known as a "conservation reliant" species. It will depend on humans to create new nesting habitat for it as far as we can see into the future. The warbler depended on fire to create new nesting habitat for it - young jack pine trees between five and 20 years old. Since we no longer allow fire to run across the landscape, we must continually harvest mature trees and plant young jack pines to create nesting habitat. This work also benefits several other species and helps keep the nearby AuSable River clean, cold and one of the best trout rivers in North America.

Name: Al-Sultan Abdullah Royal Tiger Reserve
Description:
Under the patronage of HRH The Regent of Pahang Crown Prince Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, Perbadanan Taman Negeri Pahang (PTNP) has enlisted Enggang Management Services to oversee reserve operations. Expert partners from BORA, Nature-Based Solutions, Panthera, and The Habitat Foundation bring specialized skills in prey augmentation, protected area management, enforcement, and community engagement to ensure effective implementation. This project marks the first time that a protected area in Peninsular Malaysia is managed through a private-public partnership . 

Situated adjacent to Taman Negara National Park in the Greater Taman Negara Forest Complex, the 92,649.33-hectare reserve, which will be further expanded to 134,183 hectares in the near future, significantly expands the core tiger habitat in one of Malaysia's largest and most important tiger landscapes. 
The funding will support targeted interventions to stabilize and ultimately increase the wild Malayan tiger population in the landscape, primarily through reducing death rates through stopping poaching, and increasing birth rates through boosting prey availability.

Name: Nature's Herald
Description: Somos una asociación civil dedicada a la gestión integral de residuos y la protección de la biodiversidad en México. Promovemos la participación ciudadana mediante el voluntariado y la educación ambiental, con el objetivo de fomentar una cultura de responsabilidad ecológica y construir un futuro sustentable.

Name: Maderas Rainforest Conservancy
Description:
Helping the Rainforest, through Education, Conservation, Research, and community services through: Veterinary, Medical, Dental missions and more.
Established in 2008 we have helped in Costa Rica, Nicaragua and in 2024 we will begin work in Guatemala. To join our medical teams or to organize an educational  travel student group in the rainforest contact info@maderasrfc.org

Name: Guardbook Conservation Foundation
Description: Welcome to Guardbook Conservation Foundation, where our mission is to safeguard nature while empowering communities. As pioneers in the sustainability domain, we are committed to protecting wildlife, conserving forests, and fostering sustainable development through innovative and impactful initiatives.

Name: Sibyllina
Description: Sibyllina connects organisations seeking biodiversity assets with landowners who steward landscapes with functional ecosystems and facilitates a transfer of natural capital with a lease.

Name: IDAHO ASSOCIATION OF SOIL CONSERVATION DISTRICTS (IASCD)
Description: The Idaho Association of Soil Conservation Districts is a non-profit trade advocacy organization working with Idaho's soil and water conservation districts on projects and programs to conserve Idaho's natural resources.

Name: Pusat Riset Konservasi Gajah dan Biodiversitas Hutan
Description:
Pusat Riset Konservasi Gajah dan Biodiversitas Hutan, abbreviated as PKGB, also known as the Research Center for Elephant Conservation and Biodiversity Forest, was established on May 22, 2018. PKGB is an organization engaged in elephant conservation and saving biodiversity in Aceh.

The establishment of this institution was based on the high threat to elephants and wildlife as well as a decrease in quality in terms of biodiversity, which directly impacts animals and leads to extinction. This institution has a mission to save endangered species through increased research and management of biodiversity resources as one of the efforts to increase the contribution of Syiah Kuala University to Aceh Province. In addition, this institution also has a mission to improve education through various disciplines, including biodiversity, conservation, animal ecology, plant ecology, veterinary medicine, agriculture, social, and education. On the other hand, we highly uphold forest sustainability, reducing conflicts between humans and animals through empowering the economy of communities in forest border areas that directly impact forest sustainability.

Name: Birds of Poole Harbour
Description: Birds of Poole Harbour is a charity which aims to raise the profile of bird conservation, preservation and observation in and around the Poole Harbour area through harbour-wide partnership working, high standards of people engagement work and continuous monitoring of the harbour and its bird populations.

Name: Ecodeed
Description: A Ecodeed.org arrecada fundos para compra áreas para garantir que eles se transformem em Reserva Particular do Patrimônio Natural (RPPN).

Name: White Tank Mountains Conservancy
Description:
The White Tank Mountains, located in the West Valley of Phoenix, are among the best conserved natural areas in the metropolitan region. The mountains are abundant with wildlife including mule deer, mountain lions, bobcats, desert tortoises, javelinas and other indigenous animals typically found in the Sonoran Desert. The mountains are also the site of two regional parks, the White Tank Mountain Regional Park and Skyline Regional Park, where in 2019 upwards of 700,000 residents and visitors came to enjoy the trails and breathtaking views.

Situated along the western edge of the urban environment, the White Tank Mountains stand in the path of continued growth in one of the nation’s fastest growing communities. Many people are attracted to this part of Arizona because of its natural beauty and accessibility to the recreational activities that the mountains offer. However, as development continues to grow at an unprecedented rate, the White Tank Mountains could be in jeopardy unless steps are taken to ensure the natural corridors that animals rely on for forage, migration and genetic diversity are not cut off.

The White Tank Mountains Conservancy was formed to bring together conservationists, developers and cities to balance environmental concerns with impending development. Established with the help of a diverse coalition of key stakeholders and partners, and supported by a team of volunteers, the White Tank Mountains Conservancy strives to protect the mutually beneficial co-existence of humans and a robust wildlife population which thrives in the open spaces and corridors of the vibrant White Tank Mountains.

Name: Oamaru Blue Penguin Colony
Description:
The Oamaru Blue Penguin Colony provides visitors with an opportunity to see little penguins in their natural environment. A tour during the day allows visitors to peek into the penguins’ nests, stroll through their habitat and learn about their fascinating lives. In the evening, visitors are captivated by watching the nightly arrival of penguins from their days’ fishing at sea.

The Colony has a strong conservation and research focus, with the aim of protecting the penguin population at the focus of the tourism operation.

Name: Australian Seabird and Turtle Rescue (ASTR)
Description:
A community-based not for profit organisation that rescues and rehabilitates vulnerable seabirds and marine reptiles since 1992. 

Our dedicated volunteers rescue stranded animals across vast expanses of the NSW coastline, inc. Lord Howe Island. We house rescued animals from days to months to rehabilitate them for release back into the wild. 

 We also engage with the community to highlight the pressures these animals face and the need for community led groups to rescue and rehabilitate them. 

We proudly put our shoulder to the wheel to rescue and rehabilitate marine fauna but rely on  the generosity of volunteers, as well as donors and corporate  sponsors  to keep us going. All forms of  help  and donations are welcomed.

Name: Amazon Conservation Group LLC
Description:
Join the Fight for the Amazon: Invest in Amazon Conservation Group’s Verified Carbon Projects. Empower Indigenous Communities, Protect Biodiversity, and Secure Sustainable Returns in the Voluntary Carbon Market.  Amazon Conservation Group's collective of dedicated staff, scientists, and partners identify, finance, develop and sustain transparent and measurable carbon finance projects. We unite experts and communities to develop better Carbon Finance Project models to protect the Amazon's biodiversity and enhance indigenous quality of life.  

Our current focus is working with the NGO DAS in the Peruvian Amazon with tribal communities spanning over 5 million hectares of at-risk communities and tropical forrest.
 
Our mission is purpose-driven to enhance the quality of life for indigenous tribal communities, while reducing climate and biodiversity risk.

Name: Maui Invasive Species Committee
