Description: The Maui Invasive Species Committee protects Maui’s watersheds, ecological resources, agriculture, and community by eliminating and containing priority invasive plant and animal species. MISC is a project of the University of Hawaiʻi- Pacific Cooperative Studies Unit supported with grant funding.

Name: HAMACA
Description:
Hamaca es la plataforma de audiovisual experimental de España, recoge las grandes obras de cine experimental, documental de autor, videoarte y generación de nuevos lenguajes y medios más destacadas desde finales de los 60 hasta el contexto presente para preservarlas y distribuirlas internacionalmente. Uniendo experimentación y diversidad, su catálogo de películas y vídeos puede entenderse como una incubadora de las tendencias y bases que marcan la producción audiovisual del presente. En él se recoge todo un imaginario que interactúa, descifra y construye los códigos sociales y la estética del mundo contemporáneo.

Desarrolla tres líneas de acción:
1) Distribución y difusión de este tipo de trabajos y autores
2) Apoyo a la producción de nuevas películas y apertura de nuevos mercados
3) Profesionalización del sector: capacitación de realizadores y educación a todo tipo de públicos

Operativa desde 2007, su actividad ha sido financiada desde entonces por la administración pública así como por fondos privados por la colaboración y contratación de servicios especializados.

Todo el fondo está digitalizado y puesto a disposición del público para su consulta a través de la plataforma web.

Name: Centre Erminea
Description: Centre de soins animaliers pour la faune sauvage

Name: Passaic River Coalition
Description:
Passaic River Coalition is a non-profit organization based out of 330 Speedwell Ave, Morristown, New Jersey, United States. The Passaic River Coalition (PRC) was launched in 1969 to address flooding issues in the Passaic River Basin of northern New Jersey. In fifty-five years, that focus has never changed, but we now address many additional environmental resource issues in the Passaic River Basin. We assist municipalities, non-profit organizations, neighborhood associations, individuals, and diverse communities in urban, suburban and rural areas. 

These initiatives underscore the importance of public education, outreach, advocacy, and volunteer involvement to address critical environmental issues affecting natural resources, health and quality of life in the Passaic River Basin. The Passaic River Coalition believes that a healthy, just and economically sound society requires protection of water resources and proper attention to how people are affected by these resources.

Name: Mara Siana Foundation
Description:
Mission: 
To continue the efforts of the conservancy by supporting and representing their vision in the areas of administration, growth, and strategy. We are committed to contributing to local members of the conservancy with organized efforts to uplift the community through education, access to health care, access to markets, and providing a platform for community planning.

Vision: 
Improve the quality of life for those who contribute to the conservancy by providing opportunities for employment, business development, educational opportunities, and access to healthcare. We aim to partner with organizations and benefactors who share in our mission of protecting wildlife, promoting conservation, and uplifting the local communities.

Name: TECOCARRE (TECO²)
Lipilichi Wilderness
Description:
Lipilichi Wilderness is a private company registered in Mozambique, dedicated to the long-term management of protected areas in Niassa in collaboration with governments and local communities.

Our work centres on empowering the communities of Chipanje Chetu and advancing conservation efforts through sustainable tourism as well as resilient food security mechanisms, participatory governance, anti-poaching, human-wildlife conflict mitigation, skills development and education initiatives.

Since 2007, Lipilichi Wilderness has been in partnership with the communities of Chipanje Chetu and the Provincial Government of Niassa, focusing on the management, conservation, and sustainable development of the region. Our efforts are directed towards enhancing living conditions, promoting ecotourism, and ensuring the preservation of vital wildlife species.

For over a decade, we have successfully developed and managed Chipanje Chetu as a community conservation area, achieving significant progress in both wildlife conservation and community resilience.

Name: Plymouth Environmental Action
Description:
Plymouth Environmental Action (PEA) is a practical nature conservation volunteering project which aims to involve and inspire people to conserve the natural environment within their local community - whilst having fun, gaining new skills, getting outside in the fresh air, and making new friends.

The group is led by dedicated volunteer leaders who have a great amount of ecological and conservation knowledge and countryside management experience. We carry out a variety of conservation and heritage-based projects including tree planting, wildflower meadow creation and management, wildlife pond creation and restoration, hedge-laying, tree care, coppicing, nature reserve infrastructure, biological surveying, beach cleans, and invasive plant species management to name but a few!

Name: Observatoire des Galliformes de Montagne
Description: L'Observatoire des Galliformes de Montagne est une Association Loi 1901 qui regroupe une cinquantaine de membres Alpins, Pyrénéens et Vosgiens, impliqués dans le suivi des galliformes de montagne et de leurs habitats : Tétras-lyre, Grand Tétras, Lagopède alpin, Gelinotte des bois, Perdrix Bartavelle et Perdrix grise de montagne.

Name: Nobilis.fauna
Description:
Organização social sem fins lucrativos atuante na conservação da fauna silvestre e ambientes naturais. 
Projetos de reintrodução de mamíferos ameaçados de extinção com o TamanduAsas, Onças do Ibitipoca.
Auxilio na gestão e/ou execução de projetos de UC´s, como RPPN Jacob, RPPN Retiro Águas Vivas.  
Parceria com ICAS, IEF, ENGIE, Ministério Público entre outros.

Name: Tupavu Kenya
Description: We sensitize Learners to plant and nurture fruit trees to promote nutrition and environmental conservation with particular emphasis on schools and communities around them through tree planting initiatives.

Name: KARIBU
Description:
Nous ramenons les enfants des rues dans leurs familles à l'aide de microcredits, de formations et d'un suivi rapproché a Kinshasa au congo RDC
Nous accompagnons des familles sans logements vers le logement social à Meudon dans les Hauts-de-Seine

Name: The World Owl Trust
Description:
The World Owl Trust works on owl conservation on a National and International scale, and we have members in many countries around the world. Our conservation programmes protect populations of endangered owls until their habitat has been restored. This has already been achieved for the European Eagle Owl and remains ongoing for the British Barn Owl.

The Trust is a registered charity and relies heavily on the generous donations it receives from our members, supporters and visitors. We also run a popular owl adoption scheme. We can't wait to welcome you to the new World Owl Centre, opening later in 2025!  Interested in supporting our work?  Contact us to discuss individual and corporate sponsorship opportunities and other ways to collaborate on wildlife conservation and environmental impact.  We'll also be sharing volunteer opportunities in the coming months.
Message us here, or email richard@owls.org

Name: OCEANIUM Ong
Description:
OCEANIUM est une ONG environnementale sénégalaise d’utilité publique, engagée depuis plus de 40 ans dans la protection des écosystèmes marins et terrestres au Sénégal et en Afrique de l’Ouest.

Fondée initialement en 1984 et transformée en ONG en 2021, OCEANIUM œuvre à la restauration des mangroves, la création d’aires marines protégées, l’éducation environnementale et le développement de solutions écologiques innovantes, telles que les foyers améliorés.
Avec plus de 150 millions de palétuviers replantés, OCEANIUM est à l’origine du plus grand projet de reforestation de mangroves au monde, soutenu par des partenaires comme Danone, WeForest ou Livelihoods Fund.

Name: EnJeux Durables
Description:
EnJeux Durables est une association à but non lucratif qui accompagne les collectivités locales et les acteurs territoriaux dans leurs initiatives pour sensibiliser les citoyens à l’économie circulaire et à la gestion durable des textiles.

Nos actions s’articulent autour de projets créatifs, ludiques et concrets, qui mobilisent activement les différents publics.

La Textile Race : Un défi éducatif et participatif pour les écoles, favorisant la collecte textile et l’apprentissage des principes des 5R (Réduire, Réutiliser, Réparer, Recycler, Refuser).
Campagnes événementielles : Collectes textiles, ateliers de réparation et événements de mobilisation citoyenne.
Programmes éducatifs : Sensibilisation dans les écoles et collèges pour former la nouvelle génération aux enjeux environnementaux.
Projets sur mesure : Conception d’initiatives adaptées aux besoins spécifiques des territoires.

Depuis 2022, nos actions ont permis de collecter plus de 63 tonnes de textiles en collaboration avec les opérateurs de collecte locaux. Nous avons également initié 814 réparations et customisations, contribuant activement à prolonger la durée de vie des textiles. Chaque année, des centaines de citoyens et d’écoles sont mobilisés autour de nos projets, reflétant notre engagement pour un impact environnemental et social durable. Jeune organisation ambitieuse, nous sommes portés par de grands projets pour transformer les défis en opportunités.

Grâce à notre partenariat avec Refashion, jusqu’à 75 % des coûts de nos projets peuvent être co-financés, facilitant ainsi leur mise en œuvre.

Rejoignez-nous pour transformer vos défis environnementaux en opportunités concrètes !

Name: Fungimap Inc
Description:
We record and map fungi in Australia. We spread the word about the essential role of fungi in biodiversity. We advocate for fungal conservation and investment in mycology. We would love you to join us and help with this work.

***

Fungimap is a not for profit, citizen-science organisation dedicated to furthering the conservation and knowledge of Australian fungi. We started our work in 1995 as a mapping project and incorporated as an association in 2005.

Our aims are to:
- Stimulate and support the study of Australian macrofungi through the accumulation, storage, analysis and dissemination of information about fungi.
- Link and bring together those with an interest in Australian macrofungi, providing opportunities for sharing and learning; and fostering relationships between groups and individuals that share the objectives of Fungimap.
- Promote the appreciation of fungi with a focus on Australian macrofungi in the natural environment.
- Foster the conservation of Australian macrofungi.

We are based at the Royal Botanic Gardens in Melbourne but our remit and membership are Australia-wide.

Our work is funded mainly by membership fees, donations and project-based grants. We are a registered charity and donations to us of more than $2 are tax-deductible.

We are governed by a committee of management that is elected by Fungimap members.

Name: Marine Protection Alliance
Description:
Local communities, natural heritage, and economy are at risk due to illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing.
Illegal industrial fishing is a crime that profoundly impacts the daily lives of people as it threatens marine resources, endangers the ecosystem’s life, and jeopardizes the livelihoods of fishermen. It also reduces the availability of fish as food for those who need it the most and devalues it along the production chain.

It encompasses a range of illicit activities, primarily carried out by foreign vessels that fish in territorial waters in violation of international conservation laws or monitoring requirements, without duly informing the competent authorities.

The fight against illegal industrial fishing is crucial to protect marine resources, the ecosystem, and the livelihoods of local fishermen. This necessitates strong cooperation with the competent authorities to enhance surveillance, enforce existing regulations, and promote sustainable fishing. Additionally, raising public awareness about its impact and encouraging responsible practices are essential.

Name: Guadalupe-Blanco River Trust
Description: The mission of the Guadalupe-Blanco River Trust is to preserve the unique natural heritage of the Guadalupe watershed for future generations, by protecting open landscapes, working farms and ranches, and wildlife habitat through conservation easements, education, and outreach that connects people to the water and land.

Name: Kris Reis Consultoria Ambiental
Description:
Kris Reis Consultoria Ambiental | Soluções Sustentáveis, Impacto Positivo 

Na Kris Reis Consultoria Ambiental, nosso compromisso vai além de cumprir normas – buscamos integrar a sustentabilidade ao coração de suas operações . Oferecemos uma abordagem personalizada e estratégica para que sua empresa não atenda apenas às questões ambientais, mas também se destaque como referência em práticas sustentáveis.

Atuamos em diversos setores, conectando inovação e responsabilidade ambiental . Desde a gestão de licenças ambientais e suas renovações, auditoria, perícia, planos de conservação da biodiversidade, nossa equipe multidisciplinar está pronta para guiar sua organização rumo a um futuro mais verde.

Nossos principais serviços incluem: 

 Consultoria em Gestão Ambiental : Assessoria para implementação de Sistemas de Gestão Ambiental (SGA) com base na ISO 14001. 

 Treinamentos Ambientais : Capacitação para profissionais em áreas críticas como resíduos sólidos , emissões atmosféricas e educação ambiental corporativa .

 Monitoramento e Reabilitação de Fauna e Flora : Soluções em biodiversidade , monitoramento de fauna silvestre e ações de reabilitação de espécies. 
 Regularização Ambiental : Elaboração e acompanhamento de licenças ambientais, relatórios de impacto e conformidade às exigências
Educação Ambiental: implementação de projetos de capacitação para colaboradores da empresa

Somos movidos pela missão de proteger o meio ambiente , promovendo práticas sustentabilidade dos negócios das empresas com visão no presente e no futuro.

 Juntos podemos transformar desafios ambientais em oportunidades de inovação e crescimento sustentável.

Name: SOS HUMEDALES
Description:
En SOS HUMEDALES nos dedicamos a la conservación y restauración de humedales de manera sostenible. Identificamos humedales vulnerables y trabajamos con propietarios para conservarlos, ofreciendo recompensas y acompañamiento técnico.

Con el apoyo de OSCs, científicos y asociaciones, creamos y ajustamos los planes de manejo adaptativos. Cada proyecto se sustenta con donaciones de Empresas, ONGs y Entidades Estatales, lo que permite recompensar a los propietarios y regenerar los humedales.

Al colaborar con SOS HUMEDALES, las empresas no solo contribuyen a la conservación del agua, la biodiversidad y la captura de CO2, sino que también cumplen con sus objetivos de sustentabilidad y metas ODS 2030. Nuestro enfoque garantiza una gestión transparente y eficiente de los aportes, ofreciendo una herramienta disruptiva y sostenible.

Sumate a nosotros y marcá la diferencia. El agua es vida.

Name: Reliefs Mauriciens
Description:
Nous visons un avenir où la valorisation des milieux naturels et l'effervescence des communautés culturelles se réunissent pour façonner des solutions innovantes en faveur de la protection de la biodiversité. Cette vision englobe la création d'aires protégées variées ainsi que le développement d'infrastructures et services favorisant la vitalité territoriale. 



Parallèlement, nous développons une offre d'activités écotouristiques, culturelles et éducatives qui permettent de sensibiliser et d'impliquer les actrices et les acteurs locaux dans cette démarche collective de conservation.

Name: AfrikImpact Eco-Sen
Description:
L'association Afrik Impact Eco-SEn est une association constituée de jeunes de divers horizons. L'association est une initiative des jeunes étudiants de santé publique. En février 2023, lors d'un atelier de la fresque du climat, nous avons été frappés par l'impact du changement climatique sur la survie de l'espèce humaine à l'échelle planétaire. Face à ce constat et au regard de l'intérêt que suscite cette problématique à l'échelle mondiale, nous sommes interrogés sur le sort réservé à l'Afrique devant de telles catastrophes dans un contexte actuel. C'est ainsi que nous nous sommes proposés de vulgariser cet atelier scientifique, ludique et utile dans nos pays d'origine. (05-09 février 2024) et par la suite dans toute l'Afrique. Ne voulant pas circonscrire les champs d'action eu égard au contexte actuel de l'Afrique en termes de gestion environnementale, nous nous proposons d'étaler nos actions sur l’environnement en général dans le but de promouvoir un comportement respectueux de l’environnement ainsi que l'entrepreneuriat social en Afrique.  
Le mois de février 2024 a accueilli le tout premier déploiement de l'association Afrik Impact Eco-SEn sur le terrain par un séminaire sur les enjeux climatiques couplé à l’atelier de la fresque du climat au Cameroun. 
L'association a également développé des partenariats avec des organisations locales, des entreprises et des institutions gouvernementales pour renforcer ses actions et avoir un impact plus important sur la société.
Au fil des ans, elle continuera à travailler avec détermination pour contribuer à un développement durable et inclusif en Afrique.

Name: Rewind Rhino Extinction
Description: Rewind Rhino Extinction (RRE) is a Colorado-based 501(c)(3), and our mission is to reverse the critically threatened extinction of the Northern White Rhinoceros, produce genetically pure Northern White Rhino calves and re-populate their former distribution. As a result of extensive poaching in Africa, the last male Northern Whie Rhino known to the world, Sudan, died in March 2018. There are only two known Northern White Rhinos left in the world, Najin and Fatu, both females. One would think this species extinct. But, for the first time in history, new reproductive technology could save this species. We have the opportunity to halt the extinction of these magnificent creatures but are in need of expanding our funding efforts to ensure we can provide hope for this iconic species.

Name: Otsego County Conservation Association
Description: Otsego County Conservation is an environmental services company based out of 7207 State Highway 80, Cooperstown, NY, United States.

Name: Fundación Naturaleza y Hombre
Description: Fundación Naturaleza y Hombre es una Organización No Gubernamental sin ánimo de lucro creada en 1994 en España, con sede en Astillero (Cantabria). Desde entonces la Fundación ha actuado tanto a nivel nacional (destacando la conservación de la Marisma de Alday, en Cantabria) como internacional, con proyectos en otros países como Tanzania o Argentina.

Name: Stiftung für Mensch und Umwelt
Description:
Seit 2010 lenken wir die Aufmerksamkeit auf Wild- und Honigbienen. Da sind wir nämlich mitten in der Biologischen Vielfalt (604 Wildbienenarten in Deutschland). Wir sind ein Pionier im Bienenschutz und konnten durch vielzählige (Kommunikations)Maßnahmen erreichen, dass sich die WildBienen in die Herzen der Menschen flogen. 

Wir motivieren erfolgreich die unterschiedlichsten Menschen, aktiv zu werden. Auch Personen, die bisher kaum etwas mit Natur- oder Bienenschutz zu tun hatten, sprechen wir mit unserer Initiative an. Und wir wenden uns an die Führungsebene von Institutionen. Denn jede und jeder kann etwas tun:

    - bienenfreundliche Stauden und Gehölze pflanzen
    - Nistmöglichkeiten schützen und neue bereitstellen
    - die Begeisterung für die Bienenvielfalt weitergeben

Zu unserer Initiative "Deutschland summt!": https://www.deutschland-summt.de/

Name: World of Water Action Forum
Description:
WOW-AF represents frontier discourse on the future of water and solutions that redefine and reshape existing approaches to sanitation, drinking and used water. 
With Mission Directors in multiple cities. WOW-AF has brought together a very rare collective of thinkers and doers in the water domain. 
The process of solutioning bring many game-changing approaches to original thinking on the science and application of solutions to water.

Name: FRACTAL ECOSYSTEM
Description:
FRACTAL Ecosystem is a pioneering organization transforming the socio-economic landscape through its collaborative ecosystem. 
We're on a mission to present a holistic model that creates, preserves and recycles wealth within a cooperative structure, harmonizing the interests of all participants, including nature. Guided by principles such as voluntary participation, circular democracy and cooperative economics, we encourage virtuous economic growth while recognizing the profound interconnection between humanity and its environment. 
By embracing unity and cooperation, and drawing on nature's wisdom through biomimicry, we are shaping a new paradigm for a sustainable and prosperous future. 
Join us in creating unprecedented transformative results and become part of the #FRACTALEcosystem.

Name: Iowa Learning Farms
Description: Iowa Learning Farms is a company based out of 609 Bissell Rd, Ames, Iowa, United States.

Name: Beluga Whale Sanctuary
Description:
The Beluga Whale Sanctuary, an initiative of the SEA LIFE TRUST, is dedicated to providing beluga whales with a safe and natural home. Established with the support of Merlin Entertainments and Whale and Dolphin Conservation (WDC), our sanctuary is the first of its kind, aiming to transform the care of belugas worldwide.

The aims of our Sanctuary are:
- To provide a more natural life for the belugas in our care
- To advance knowledge through research and education
- To support protection of belugas in the wild

We lead by example, demonstrating that sanctuaries are a viable alternative to traditional facilities. Our goal is to inspire change and improve the lives of over 3,000 whales and dolphins currently in captivity worldwide.

Join us in our mission to create a better future for belugas and all marine life. Connect with us to learn more about our work and how you can support our efforts.

Name: Cradle of Humankind World Heritage Site (COHWHS)
Description:
The Cradle of Humankind World Heritage Site (COHWHS), established in 1999, is one of South Africa’s leading World Heritage Sites spanning 53 000 hectare site with over 400 accompanying diverse offerings for visitors. 

Its mission is to protect, conserve and interpret the Outstanding Universal Value (OUV) of the Cradle of Humankind World Heritage Site. It was established and inscribed in 1999 on UNESCO’s World Heritage List because of the wealth of fossils found in the area and, in particular, fossils of ancient humans. It is an area that has yielded and has continued to produce an enormous amount of information about our human origins. 

Apart from its scientific value, the site has been developed in order to create employment and regional economic development through the use of science, conservation and tourism as economic drivers aimed at alleviating poverty and stimulating the economy of the region.

Name: Instituto Caminho da Mata Atlântica - ICMA
Description: O Caminho da Mata Atlântica é uma Iniciativa que tem como missão engajar a sociedade na conservação e recuperação da Mata Atlântica por meio de atividades ao ar livre e da conexão de áreas naturais ao longo de uma trilha de longo curso, promovendo o desenvolvimento socioeconômico inclusivo, a conservação da biodiversidade e a valorização do patrimônio natural e cultural.

Name: Freshwater Turtles and Tortoises Foundation
Description: We are dedicated to the comprehensive conservation, research, and education surrounding India's freshwater turtles and tortoises, which face severe threats including exploitation in illegal trades and habitat degradation. Through innovative conservation strategies, inclusive training programs, and capacity-building efforts, we engage stakeholders ranging from local communities to researchers to combat the imminent extinction risks these species confront. Our research endeavors aim to fill critical knowledge gaps, informing evidence-based conservation actions and securing funding for vital projects. Education lies at the core of our mission as we conduct outreach activities across diverse groups, fostering awareness, and cultivating a network of advocates for the protection of freshwater chelonians and their ecosystems. Together, we strive to empower individuals and communities to become guardians of India's freshwater biodiversity, ensuring a sustainable future for these invaluable species and their habitats.

Name: Fandroakando NGO
Description: The Fandroakando NGO team is dedicated to the protection of Madagascar Rainforest.  Our headquarters are in Maroantsetra, in NE Madagascar and located on Antongil Bay.  The Antongil Bay area is recognized for its biodiversity and as one of the world’s highest concentrations of endemic species.  It is estimated that the Antongil Bay area contains 50% of Madagascar’s biodiversity while covering only 2% of its land mass.

Name: Cumbre Glocal de Economía Circular
Description:
Es una iniciativa que une conocimiento,
innovación y sostenibilidad en el marco
de la economía circular. Este evento reúne
a expertos, empresarios, instituciones y
ciudadanos que están comprometidos
con un modelo de desarrollo regenerativo

Name: Tooro Botanical Gardens
Description:
Based in Fort Portal, Uganda, TBG leads ecological restoration and biodiversity conservation. Since 2001, it has restored 70% of the 100-acre Buhoiga Forest Reserve with indigenous trees and operates one of Uganda’s largest native tree nurseries. Restoration efforts have increased tree species from 17 to 116 and bird species from 34 to 113.
TBG focuses on reforestation, endangered species conservation, and propagation research to support sustainable ecosystem recovery.

Name: Réserve de Biosphère Basse Vallée de l'Ouémé (RB-BVO)
Shawnee Resource Conservation & Development Area Inc.
Description: We help to connect federal, state and private funding to local initiatives that build our community while preserving our environment. Our programs provide education to landowners on conservation, ecosystem restoration, and resources available to help achieve their goals. We also provide valuable volunteer assistance to ensure healthy private lands.

Name: Craig Boddington Endorsed Outfitters
Description:
CRAIG BODDINGTON
HUNTER - JOURNALIST - ADVENTURER
Craig Boddington was born in Kansas in 1952. As a teenager, when not involved with hunting and shooting, he spent a lot of time pursuing Boy Scout activities. At 17 years of age he became the youngest person to become Activities Director at his regional Boy Scout camp. Boddington attended the University of Kansas on an NROTC scholarship, graduating with an English degree and a regular commission in the United States Marine Corps. He retired from the USMC Reserves in 2005 with the rank of colonel after service in the Gulf War in 1991 and the Persian Gulf in 2002-2003.

Craigs Current Roles: Executive Field Editor of GUNS & AMMO, Petersen’s HUNTING, and RIFLESHOOTER; and co-host of Guns & Ammo TV.

Name: Turks and Caicos National Trust
Description: To safeguard the historical, cultural and natural heritage of the Turks and Caicos Islands for present and future generations and for the enjoyment of all.

Name: Up with Africa (UW/A)
Description: UW/A is based out of Denver, Colorado and was formed in 2018 by Steve Dobo. We currently partner with multiple local organizations in Uganda and Democratic Republic of the Congo. We focus on programs that address problems in these key areas: wildlife conservation, community development, pathways for refugees, and access to education.

Name: Rare Breed Goats
Description: We are a traditional farm that is dedicated to the conservation and protection of British Rare & Native Goat Breeds. We have four rare and native breeds of goats including Bagot goats, Angora goats, Golden Guernsey goats and British Primitive goats.

Name: Živá krajina Plzeňský kraj
Description:
Zaměřujeme se na praktická opatření v terénu – mokřady, tůně, meze, zatravnění a vegetační pásy. Spolupracujeme s obcemi, vlastníky půdy, zemědělci i dobrovolníky. Věříme, že zdravá krajina není luxus, ale základní podmínka pro udržitelnou budoucnost.

Pomáháme:
- identifikovat problémová místa v krajině
- navrhovat účinná a citlivá řešení
- zajišťovat financování a realizaci projektů
- zapojit lidi, kteří chtějí něco změnit

 Voda v krajině není samozřejmost. Ale společně ji tam můžeme vrátit.

 Více na: www.zivakrajinaplzen.cz

Name: Panbil Nature Reserve
Description:
One of the remnants of primary forests in the heart of Batam City, nestled within the exclusive residential area of Villa Panbil.
Panbil Nature Reserve (PNR) brings the vision of an Eco Edu Park to become a forest hub that provides educational insights about ecotourism and nature conservation.

Name: Syndicat Mixte du Littoral de la Baie du Mont-Saint-Michel
Description: Le Syndicat Mixte du Littoral (SML) de la Baie du Mont-Saint-Michel a été créé en 2020 pour assurer une gestion cohérente entre ces membres des ouvrages de protection contre les phénomènes de submersion marine en baie du Mont-Saint-Michel.

Name: Association NARIES
Description:
NARIES est une association à but non lucratif et reconnue d’utilité publique. Elle a pour but de promouvoir la protection de la biodiversité, principalement en Suisse, mais aussi en France et en Afrique du Sud. A l’heure actuelle, nous pensons qu’il est de notre devoir, en tant qu’humains, de prendre conscience des dégâts que notre société a causés à l’environnement et d’y remédier autant que possible. Découvrir, Partager, Informer, Sensibiliser et Agir pour protéger, telles sont les lignes de conduite de NARIES. Le sigle NARIES est la contraction anglaise de « Nature and Animal Rescue for Injured and Endangered Species », autrement dit : « Préservation de la Nature et des Animaux pour les Espèces Lésées et En danger ».

NARIES accueille également des stagiaires de tous niveaux et de tous horizons pour les former au travail de terrain, ainsi qu’au travail associatif. Si vous souhaitez venir dans notre structure pendant ou après vos études, pendant quelques jours/semaines/mois, n’hésitez pas à nous écrire en nous envoyant un CV et une lettre de motivation! Nous donnons également des formations, contactez-nous pour de plus amples informations!

Depuis 2018, NARIES a aussi une antenne « photo » à part entière qui organise les concours photo et les rencontres Clic’Nature et qui peut participer à des concours en tant que Groupe photo. De plus, depuis 2023, nous avons regroupé nos activités pédagogiques dans une antenne « jeunesse » afin de pouvoir s’inscrire comme membre au GLAJ-GE qui est le Groupement de Liaison des Activités de Jeunesse de Genève. Finalement, depuis 2024, nous participons aux Passeports Vacances de la Ville de Genève en proposant diverses activités durant le mois de juillet.

Name: LIFE Coteaux Gascons
Description:
Le LIFE Coteaux Gascons est le premier LIFE obtenu dans le département du Gers. 
Coordonné par L'ADASEA du Gers et mis en œuvre par l'ADASEA et ses 3 partenaires, le CPIE Pays Gersois, le Conservatoire Botanique des Pyrénées et de Midi Pyrénées et la Safer Occitanie, il a débuté en octobre 2020 et se terminera en décembre 2025.
Il est financé par la Commission européenne via son programme LIFE (L'Instrument Financier pour l'Environnement) et co-financé par la Région Occitanie, le Ministère de la Transition Ecologique et le Département du Gers.

Sur un territoire stratégique de 163 communes entre Armagnac et Astarac dans le Gers, maillon essentiel de la biodiversité européenne par la richesse de ses milieux ouverts agro-pastoraux, à la fois façonnés par et dépendants de l'élevage, le LIFE Coteaux Gascons met en place des actions concrètes de conservation des milieux pour répondre aux enjeux d'interdépendance entre élevage et biodiversité, de préservation du patrimoine naturel et du paysage rural, de l’adaptation au changement climatique et au contexte socio-économique..

Pour les éleveurs qui souhaitent améliorer leurs conditions de travail et la gestion de leurs herbages.
Et les propriétaires qui veulent assurer une gestion pérenne de leurs terres.
Pour les élus qui veulent valoriser leur territoire et les acteurs qui le façonnent.
Pour les acteurs professionnels liés à ces enjeux.
Et pour l’ensemble du public, habitants et visiteurs, qui vit, agit et profite de ce territoire.

Name: élossuob équipe d'élaboration (triplééé)
Description: élossuob équipe d'élaboration (triplééé) will provide innovative services to: 1. design aligned strategic and operational systems to build capacity of actors in DRC, 2. establish an extra-institutional platform to facilitate increased collaboration and assess collective impact, design community engagement best practices and test impact assumptions in the results chains of diverse approaches to conservation, and 3. pilot territorial-wide neoliberal conservation projects empowering women to disrupt dominant narratives and develop ‘alternative to bushmeat’ food systems, value chains and livelihoods based on circular economy principles to innovate system-level change and improve the conservation status of endangered species.

Name: Doorcas Africa Foundation
Description: Doorcas Africa Foundation is a conservation focused initiative leveraging AI and digital tools to protect Africa’s rich biodiversity, starting with Nigeria. Our work champions the preservation of endangered animal species and ecosystems, educating rural communities, and deploying innovative surveillance and reporting systems. We aim to transform conservation through accessible technology, research, and community action.

Name: Mazingirasafi
Description:
MAZINGIRA SAFI est une organisation hybride œuvrant avec Deux programme 
1. Programma sociale avec domaine d'intervention : Environnement, Protection, Education, Santé, Agriculture, VBG/PSEA, 
2. GREEN BUSINESS : Production et promotions des énergies renouvelable et agro écologie

Name: Guyana Marine Conservation Society
Description:
Who Are We?
We're a country-wide community of ocean lovers all working together towards our vision of a sea full of life, where nature flourishes and people thrive.

What We Do?
We fight for the future of our ocean through people-powered action, with science on our side.

Where We Work?
We work around Guyana protecting our seas and also contribute to international data and legislation.

Name: Society for Heritage & Ecological Researches
Description:
The Earth had been a beautiful place to live in, until the power of intelligence was realized to have more scope than Nature or life could afford. But we felt that it is never too late to start redressing the errors that have been committed solely through irresponsible human activities. And therefore we have made a beginning with ‘SHER’, our very own initiative to contribute to the cause to which many others have vouched their dedication long time ago.

So, ‘Society for Heritage & Ecological Researches (SHER)’ is not driven by an idea that is original but rather by an instinctive fear that however optimistic we may be, the coming generations will be deprived of the basic right to lead a healthy and safe life. We represent what we choose to call the ‘SOS-age NGO’; it has to be now, or sadly, never.
 
A few directions in which we intend to concentrate our endeavour to start with:
•	People have known from the beginning that the only means to avoid an environmental catastrophe and maintain ecological harmony is conservation of wildlife. It is a fact that a total of 17,291 species on this planet are facing the threat of extinction today. And most of us do not even recognize how closely related our own destiny is to this apparently distant piece of information. The aim of ‘SHER’ is to make people aware and make conservation a common goal - conservation of ecology, conservation of environment, conservation of wildlife. It is the only way to reclaim and restore what once belonged to us – clean air, pure water and prosperous earth.
•	It is also our objective to widen the span of our effort through extensive research and study based on science, sustainability innovations and process advancements that have proved to work. It is also important to make people aware of legislative procedures that help the cause of environment and the need to resort to legal activism if required. We would arrange seminars and symposia on relevant aspects of environment and ecology protection

Name: Stichting ErfGoedKlimaat
Description:
Stichting ErfGoedKlimaat heeft als doel om kennis uit te wisselen door experts uit de praktijk en academici bijeen te brengen. Door met elkaar in discussie te treden worden in gezamenlijkheid de noden van het erfgoedveld in kaart gebracht en oplossingen gedefinieerd die daar optimaal bij aansluiten. Op dit moment bestaat het netwerk uit de volgende partners: 

Rijksdienst voor het Cultureel Erfgoed (Bart Ankersmit & Marc Stappers) 
Inspectie Overheidsinformatie en Erfgoed  (Nina Duggen)
Rijksvastgoedbedrijf (Jaap de Jonge) 
Technische Universiteit Eindhoven (Rick Kramer)
DYSECO (Marco Martens) 
Kröller Müller Museum (Jean Hilgersom) 
Raadsaam Erfgoedprojecten (Agnes Vugts) 
Nationaal Archief (Gabrielle Beentjes)
KB Nationale Bibliotheek (Foekje Boersma)

Name: Djidomi
Description:
DJIDOMI accompagne les territoires africains grâce à l’éco-ingénierie.
Nous utilisons les données satellites et l’analyse géospatiale pour appuyer : la gestion durable des ressources naturelles, la prévention des risques environnementaux, la planification territoriale; la résilience climatique des communautés locales
Notre mission : transformer la donnée environnementale en solutions concrètes, mesurables et utiles aux décideurs.
DJIDOMI
Plateforme d’éco-ingénierie au Bénin
 Solutions environnementales
 Tourisme éco-responsable
 Collaboration avec communautés & entreprises
 Construisons des villes durables

Name: Marine Life Sanctuaries Society of British Columbia
Description: Non - profit

Name: Edmonton River Valley Conservation Coalition (ERVCC)
Description:
VISION

kisiskâciwani sipiy will be restored and protected as a life-giving corridor from the headwaters in the Columbia Icefields to the mouth at Hudson Bay through relationships of respect, reciprocity, and care.

MISSION

ERVCC works collaboratively to engage in knowledge sharing, advocacy, and stewardship using systems thinking that acknowledges the inextricable links between the ecology, culture, history, and spirit of the North Saskatchewan River watershed.

Name: Green Bay Conservation Corps
Description:
Conservation Corps crews are responsible for improving trail systems throughout Green Bay, invigorating pollinator habitats with new native plantings and improving native habitat with restoration practices.

Apply for our fall positions online: https://www.governmentjobs.com/careers/greenbaywi

Name: ITS ENVIRONNEMENT
Description:
ITS Environnement est une entreprise spécialisée dans la gestion écologique des déchets, engagée à fournir des services de haute qualité dans la collecte, le recyclage, et la valorisation de matériaux pour les professionnels du secteur. Avec un engagement profond envers la durabilité et l'environnement, nous disposons d'installations de pointe pour traiter et recycler une large gamme de déchets, assurant ainsi une gestion responsable et efficace.

Notre équipe, dynamique et experte, est toujours disponible pour offrir des conseils personnalisés et accompagner nos clients dans leurs démarches de gestion des déchets. Nous nous distinguons par notre flexibilité, capable de fournir des solutions sur mesure pour répondre à vos besoins spécifiques en matière de recyclage et de valorisation des déchets.

Que ce soit pour des services de collecte adaptés, des solutions de recyclage innovantes ou des conseils en gestion environnementale, ITS Environnement est votre partenaire de confiance. Nous collaborons avec des partenaires de premier plan pour garantir la fiabilité et la durabilité de nos services.

Pour toute demande d'information supplémentaire ou pour bénéficier de nos services, contactez-nous au 01 86 22 03 08 ou par e-mail à contact@itsenvironnement.com. Faites le choix d'ITS Environnement pour une gestion des déchets professionnelle et respectueuse de l'environnement, contribuant ainsi à un avenir plus durable pour tous.

Name: The Digital Collage Italia
Description:
Un laboratorio per comprendere in squadra e in modo ludico le sfide ambientali del digitale!

The Digital Collage (La Fresque du Numérique) è un laboratorio collaborativo e divertente della durata di 3 ore, con una pedagogia simile a quella del Affresco del Clima. L’obiettivo è sensibilizzare e formare i partecipanti sulle problematiche ambientali legate al digitale.

Il laboratorio mira anche a spiegare i principi fondamentali per un digitale sostenibile e ad avviare discussioni tra i partecipanti su questo tema.

Vero e proprio strumento di team building, questo laboratorio permette ai partecipanti di riunirsi per apprendere insieme come rendere il digitale più sostenibile.

Name: Boone County Conservation District
Description: The mission of the Boone County Conservation District is to promote the protection and wise use of natural resources through education and service to the citizens of Boone County.

Name: FEO Sri Lanka
Description: FEO is a registered Trust that is governed by a Board comprising of passionate conservationists who are committed to working with all relevant agencies / organisations to provide them with support in addressing crucial environmental issues, affecting Sri Lanka’s precious natural heritage.

Name: Entheome Foundation
Description: Building a genetic database of entheogenic plants and fungi.

Name: Sophia Botanika
Description:
Herbalist | Dipl. Kräuterpädagogin 
•Offering herbal walks & workshops for individuals, groups and companies (employee incentive programs)
•Blog
•Herbal Manufacture ++NEW! Online Shop!++

Name: The Wild League
Description:
The Wild League est un projet international et innovant qui crée un pont entre le sport professionnel et la conservation de la biodiversité. Plus d’un tiers des espèces animales utilisées comme emblèmes sportifs sont menacées d’extinction

Nos objectifs:
-Mobiliser clubs, supporters et acteurs de la conservation pour protéger les espèces emblématiques.
-Sensibiliser via des campagnes, du storytelling et l'engagement des fans et supporters.
- Agir grâce à des initiatives de financement et de recherche-action durables.

Notre plan de jeu sur 5 ans:
 Comprendre : Étudier les liens entre sport et biodiversité.
 Construire : Définir des actions et mobiliser des fonds.
 Agir : Mettre en œuvre des projets concrets et en mesurer l’impact.

Rejoignez-nous pour un sport engagé et porteur de sens !

Name: ADEV (Association de Défense de l'Environnement en Vendée)
Description:
Créée en 1968 à la Roche sur Yon. L’Association de Défense de l’Environnement en Vendée regroupe des salariés, bénévoles et adhérents muées par l’envie d’agir pour un meilleur équilibre entre activités humaines et respect de l’environnement, spécialement en Vendée. 

Agir pour l’environnement en Vendée est notre principale mission ! 
Pour cela, l’ADEV s’est dotée de deux pôles principaux qui remplissent cet objectif :

L’animation pour la sensibilisation et l’éducation à l’environnement à travers des sorties guidées « nature » à des groupes, à des scolaires ou même à des particuliers, et ce pour tous les publics et toute l’année !

Les études qui permettent d’apporter une expertise naturaliste (diagnostics, inventaires, études d’impacts, préconisations…) sur divers domaines aux publics ou aux privés qui la demande.

Dans ces instances, l’ADEV est amenée à donner son avis dans plusieurs commissions départementales ( SCoT, PLU et PLUI, Trames Vertes et Bleues = Corridors écologiques,  Zones NATURA 2000, COPIL, SAGE, Aménagements fonciers, enquêtes publiques, etc... ainsi que les risques sanitaires et technologiques, risques naturels majeurs, aménagements fonciers, déchets, eau, air, réserves naturelles, chasse et faune sauvage, SAFER, etc…)

En plus, l’ADEV est gestionnaire de l’Observatoire d’Oiseaux de l’Ile d’Olonne et sensibilise ainsi aux oiseaux des marais et de la côte Vendéenne.

Name: Les COmains
Description: Nous sommes un collectif qui a pour objectif d'agir dans le domaine de l'économie circulaire et de la réduction des déchets. Nous avons pour vocation de faire naître un tiers lieu du Libournais. Si vous partagez nos valeurs et souhaitez vous impliquer en tant que partenaire ou si vous voulez simplement en savoir plus, n’hésitez pas à nous contacter.

Name: Namib'ISEQ
Description:
Namib'ISEQ est un projet de l'association Human'ISEQ détenue par huit étudiants de l'école ISEQ à Metz.

Notre objectif est d'apporter notre aide à l'association Wildlife Sanctuary Foundation dans leur sanctuaire en Namibie.

Notre but est d'aider des espèces blessées, menacées et victimes de braconnage.

Name: Small Habitat Company
Description: Small Habitat Company is a habitat bank that works with forward thinking developers and their teams. We help you to strengthen planning applications with local, off-site biodiversity units.

Name: Morgan Duke Conservation Society
Description:
The Morgan Duke Conservation Society is a 501(c)(3) public charity nonprofit organization committed to addressing environmental challenges and conflicts across New York State and beyond. We focus on wildlife habitat management and forest conservation through partnerships, innovative solutions, and educational outreach.

Our Vision:
At the Morgan Duke Conservation Society, our vision is to create thriving habitats where wildlife can flourish without human interference. We aim to protect areas where environmental challenges are minimized, allowing people to enjoy the beauty of forests, beaches, and wildlife free from the impact of pollution.

Name: Save the North Pacific Right Whale
Description:
There are only 30 North Pacific right whales left off the North American West Coast - down from tens of thousands in the early 1800s before they were commercially whaled.

Every person talking about these whales is one more person who can make a difference. Endangered species recovery is all about public opinion. Not only do we have to be willing to change our actions to help these whales, but we also need to get that change rolling fast and strong enough to get others on board as well. Through public action and education we want to increase awareness and support for this endangered whale. We provide a space to engage in conservation efforts, whether that’s school presentations, reaching out to stakeholders or donating to the cause. We’re also here to get everyone as excited about this species as we are.

Name: 7000seeds
Description:
Wir helfen Euch, ein Begrünungsprojekt demokratisch zu planen und umzusetzen. Wir begleiten Euren Prozess und stehen Euch zur Seite.

Eure Bepflanzung wird durch den Stuttgarter Klimafonds teilsubventioniert und ihr bekommt wissenschaftliches Fachwissen vom Feinsten, damit Eure grüne Oase keine Wüste wird.

Außerdem bekommt Euer Projekt eine „Probinie“, einen Robinienstab, der insbesondere die Ansiedelung von Wildbienen fördert und der für Euch kostenlos ist.

#Stuttgart #jetztklimachen

Name: LeAP: Leadership for Africa's Parks
Description:
LeAP teaches new skills to African protected & conserved area staff to more effectively protect their countries' iconic parks and wildlife into the future. 

Unfortunately, graduates of African wildlife colleges and universities often are not trained in the conservation leadership & professional skills they need to effectively protect parks and wildlife, such as managing protected area staff and engaging with local communities & stakeholders. Experts agree these skills are essential for improving park performance and protecting wildlife. LeAP works to fill in these key gaps in training, partnering with faculty at African wildlife colleges and universities to empower all graduates with the leadership skills they need to improve park performance and wildlife protection.

Working closely with the Directors of RP Kitabi College in Rwanda and Garoua Wildlife College/ / L’Ecole de Faune de Garoua in Cameroon and key faculty from Njala University in Sierra Leone and University of Kisangani in Democratic Republic of Congo, LeAP mentors chosen faculty to develop new courses on leadership, teamwork, problem-solving, and conflict management. These courses will become embedded into university curricula, training hundreds of rangers and managers annually at the diploma and undergraduate level for protected and conserved areas. 

LeAP will expand the network by facilitating exchanges and joint training with faculty from other African universities, ultimately creating a Pan-African network of leadership training programs across the continent. 

Our goal is to develop capacity in Africa, for African institutions to better protect their parks and wildlife for all. 

LeAP’s founding partners include: 

RP Kitabi College, Rwanda www.iprckitabi.rp.ac.rw 

Garoua Wildlife College/ / L’Ecole de Faune de Garoua, Cameroon www.ecoledesfaunes.org 

Njala University Department of Wildlife Management and Conservation 
www.njala.edu.sl

University of Kisangani, DRC

Name: Pesca.Blue
Description: Blue Turtle Sustainable LLC is a hybrid business offering wellness yoga retreats with the mission to support research and education for the conservation of sea turtles and other marine life by collaborating with coastal communities, academic institutions and non-profit organizations.

Name: Dronde Están Las Tortugas
Description: DELT is a non-profit organization dedicated to environmental conservation and the protection of endangered species. Through research projects and collaboration with other organizations and governments, we work to ensure a sustainable future. DELT provides resources and technological solutions with drones equipped with artificial intelligence to help organizations in their conservation tasks, such as detection, tracking, or analysis of specimens. DELT is focused on the detection of sea turtles to track nesting, thus saving many hours of work for volunteers and collaborators. DELT's mission is to facilitate the task, increasing speed, efficiency or ground covered, so that the organizations with which it collaborates can do more with less.

Name: Sahelian Initiative for the Environment and the Climate (ISEC)
Description:
ISEC or SIEC is a non for profit organisation that works to promote environmental conservation and combat climate change in the Sahel region of Cameroon- with its headquarters in Mokolo, Far North region of Cameroon.

We work on;
- Raising awareness among local communities about the importance of environmental protection and well-being.  
- Encouraging active participation of local communities in environmental protection.  
- Promote sustainable development within local communities.  
- Collaborate with other environmental organizations to strengthen ongoing initiatives.  
- Advocate for stricter environmental protection laws to local or national authorities. etc

Name: Orokonui Ecosanctuary - Te Korowai o Mihiwaka
Description: Orokonui is a 307 hectare wildlife sanctuary near Dunedin, NZ

Name: Maono Conservation
Description: We are building advocacy and awareness of innovative, highly successful community-led conservation programs.

Name: Stichting Hergebruik Nu
Description:
Wij leiden een bruisende beweging die hergebruik moeiteloos, effectief en inspirerend maakt, door samen te werken, kennis te delen en met één stem een circulaire toekomst te creëren.


Onze huidige prioriteiten:
- Één krachtige sectorstem ontwikkelen
- Wet- en regelgeving verduidelijken
- Bewustwording en adoptie van hergebruik vergroten

Name: Blue Turtle Sustainable
Description: Blue Turtle Sustainable LLC is a hybrid business offering wellness yoga retreats with the mission to support research and education for the conservation of sea turtles and other marine life by collaborating with coastal communities, academic institutions and non-profit organizations.

Name: PEPS : Partage et Emerveillement au Potager des Surprises
Description:
PEPS : Partage et Émerveillement au Potager des Surprises !
Une association qui souhaite regrouper autour d’un potager d’expérimentation en syntropie toutes les personnes curieuses des incroyables possibilités de la nature. 

L’idée : Réhabiliter le potager et le verger du Château de Thouaré sur Loire dans une démarche d’agroécologie.

Name: Stichting Bosboeren
Description: Stichting Bosboeren heeft als doel om landbouwgrond op te kopen en beschikbaar te stellen voor voedselbosbouw. Daarnaast bevorderen we voedselbosbouw in de breedste zin van het woord, door middel van educatie en bewustwording van onze gemeenschap.
Location: Koog aan de Zaan, Noord Holland

Name: Heritage Cell, Aligarh Muslim University
Description: The Heritage Cell at Aligarh Muslim University, established in 2013, aims to raise awareness and restore heritage buildings and sites. Led by Convener Prof Mohammad Farhan Fazli, the Cell is dedicated to preserving the university's legacy through meticulous efforts. They organize workshops, training programs, and exhibitions to promote cultural heritage restoration. Notable programs include workshops on traditional techniques, facade adornment, conservation of manuscripts, and construction in heritage buildings. The Cell has successfully completed several restoration projects, including Jama Masjid,  Sir Syed Hall (South), Union Hall, Sultan Jahan Manzil, Church of Accession, and installation of historic plaques. These endeavors demonstrate their commitment to restoring the incredible heritage of our nation.

Name: Conservation Journey
Description: Tu veux que ton métier ait un impact positif sur la préservation des espèces sauvages ? Découvre la variété de métiers et les parcours pour y arriver !

Name: Projeto Baleias & Golfinhos do Rio
Description: O projeto Baleias e Golfinhos do Rio de Janeiro é um programa de monitoramento sistemático das baleias e golfinhos que ocorrem nas águas costeiras do Rio que visa promover a conservação das espécies em ecossistemas naturais da região.

Name: Organisme de bassin versant du Saguenay
Description: L’Organisme de bassin versant du Saguenay a pour mission de coordonner et mobiliser les acteurs du milieu pour la planification et la réalisation d’actions visant la protection, la restauration et la mise en valeur de l’eau et de ses usages.

Name: Newport Tree Conservancy
Description: Protecting, Promoting, and Replanting Newport's Legendary Urban Forest.

Name: Berrien County Conservation District
Description:
Berrien County Conservation District is a local unit of government and a political subdivision of the State of Michigan. The District's authorities, powers, and structure are contained in Michigan Conservation District Law. Berrien Conservation District comprises all of Berrien County and was formed in 2006 with the merging of the Galien River and St. Joseph River Conservation Districts that were established in the 1940's.
 
Our goal is to conserve and protect land, air, water, and wildlife today for future generations.We are board-driven and monthly board meetings occur on the second Monday of each month at 12pm; exceptions occur when holidays land on a Monday. The public is welcome to attend the board meetings. Please check the Events calendar for dates and location of meetings.

We partner with the USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service and are housed with them in the USDA Service Center's Berrien Springs office at 3334 Edgewood Rd, Berrien Springs, Michigan, United States.

Name: Foundation for Sustainable Forests
Description: In his essay, "A Forest Conversation" about this organization and its founder, Troy Firth, author Wendell Berry wrote "A forest, kindly used, will outlive unimaginably any of its trees." The Foundation for Sustainable Forests is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization dedicated to conserving and thoughtfully managing the working forests of Pennsylvania, New York and beyond.   Through direct conservation of land and ecological forestry, FSF protects healthy, resilient ecosystems and a sustainable timber economy.

Name: BioPlatgesMet
Description:
BioPlatgesMet és un projecte impulsat pel Servei de Gestió de Platges de l’AMB, l’Institut de Ciències del Mar – CSIC i l’Institut Metròpoli, en col·laboració amb altres 15 socis de 8 països, com a part del projecte europeu GUARDEN, dins del Programa Horizon Europe.

Aquest programa se centra en la protecció de la biodiversitat a les platges metropolitanes i en conscienciar a la ciutadania sobre la seva importància.

Name: Boombijen
Description:
Boombijen wil de natuurlijke wildheid, weerbaarheid en vitaliteit van de inheemse honingbijen laten zien. Hoe honingbijen in het wild leven en wat hun natuurlijke habitat is. Dit als uitnodiging om na te denken over de manier waarop we vandaag met bijen omgaan, wat de verstrekkende gevolgen daarvan zijn en hoe dit anders zou kunnen. 
Dit doet zij door in gesprek te gaan, door exposities en lezingen te houden en door organisaties, instanties, bedrijven en burgers bij elkaar te brengen en te enthousiasmeren.

Wilde honingbijen
De honingbij is het meest bestudeerde insect op aarde, en we weten dan ook veel over deze bijzondere dieren. Het gros van het onderzoek is echter in ‘gevangenschap’ gedaan. Een van de weinige onderzoekers die de honingbijen in het wild heeft bestudeerd is Thomas D. Seeley. Zijn bevindingen geven mooie aanknopingspunten om de habitat en overlevingskansen van bijenpopulaties te verbeteren, zowel van de honingbijen als de andere wilde (solitaire) bijen en hommels.

Aanpassingsvermogen
In Nederland kennen we de honingbijen als ‘bijen waar de imker voor zorgt’. Weinig mensen weten dat honingbijen eigenlijk wilde dieren zijn. Die uitstekend voor zichzelf kunnen zorgen. Sterker nog: dit is cruciaal voor hun voortbestaan. De manier waarop honingbijen in het wild leven zorgt er namelijk voor dat zij zich kunnen aanpassen aan veranderende omstandigheden zoals nieuwe parasieten, predatoren en ziekteverwekkers, maar ook aan de gevolgen van globalisering en klimaatverandering. De huidige imkerpraktijk, die gebaseerd is op productie, verkleint op termijn juist de overlevingskansen van bijen en daarmee van veel planten, dieren en mensen.

Habitat herstel
Boombijen zet zich in om de habitat van honingbijen te herstellen door natuurlijke nestruimten te creëren. Zij gaat boomstamholten realiseren en op geschikte locaties plaatsen. Nestruimten die de wilde honingbijen zelf gaan bevolken! Zonder ingrepen van de mens kunnen de bijen hier naar eigen natuur leven.

Name: FOSTER GREEN COMMUNITY BASED ORGANISATION
Description: Our organization is dedicated to conservation efforts that focus on environmental conservation through tree growing and local advocacy programmes . We believe we can contribute to the overall health and balance of our planet. Through our conservation programs, we work tirelessly to protect and restore habitats, conduct research to better understand the needs of endangered species, and collaborate with local communities and governments to implement sustainable practices.

Name: Vivero Pumahuida
Description:
Nos dedicamos a la domesticación y desarrollo de especies originarias de la Zona Norte y Centro de Chile, poniendo énfasis en aquellas de valor ornamental, funcionales, endémicas y/o con problemas de conservación.

Estamos permanentemente realizando investigación aplicada para generar conocimiento sobre su comportamiento y requerimientos de cultivos, constituyéndonos en un referente a nivel nacional.

Actualmente, contamos con más de 200 especies en producción y todos los años vamos incorporando nuevas especies, las que ponemos a disposición de nuestros clientes.

Name: CENTER FOR TECHNOLOGY AND NATURE CONSERVATION
Description:
The Center for Technology and Nature Conservation (CTNC) is a leading non-governmental organization in Vietnam, committed to conserving biodiversity and natural ecosystems through the application of advanced technology, sustainable cooperation, and active community participation.With a multifaceted approach, CTNC conducts scientific research and knowledge transfer in natural resource management, enhancing climate change impact mitigation. We are committed to working for a green and sustainable Vietnam.

By using advanced tools such as Geographic Information Systems (GIS), Remote Sensing (RS), and specialized conservation software like SMART, CTNC helps promote data-driven decisions and effective monitoring of protection efforts. This strong technological foundation allows CTNC to leverage innovative initiatives aimed at nature conservation, ensuring the maintenance of crucial habitats and the protection of animal species.CTNC prides itself on a team of experienced professionals in scientific research, applying GIS, remote sensing, and SMART systems in natural resource management and conservation. We consistently update our methods with the latest advancements to ensure work effectiveness. We build and maintain extensive collaborative networks with government agencies, international organizations, domestic non-governmental organizations, and local communities. These strong partnerships are the foundation for the success of our conservation projects.

Name: Upper Tana-Nairobi Water Fund Trust
Description: The Upper Tana-Nairobi Water Fund Trust (UTNWFT) is a groundbreaking initiative that addresses one of the world’s most critical challenges: water security. As Africa’s first water fund, it offers a straightforward solution by focusing on the principle that it’s more cost-effective to prevent water problems at the source than to deal with them downstream.

Name: Association Nationale pour la Protection des Eaux et Rivières
Description: Créée en 1958 et reconnue d'utilité publique en 1985, ANPER lutte depuis tout ce temps pour la protection des milieux aquatiques de leur biodiversité.

Name: La Plata Open Space Conservancy
Description: Based in Durango, Colorado, LPOSC protects over 20,000 acres of land through conservation easements across six counties. We partner with landowners and organizations to preserve open spaces, wildlife habitat, working farms and ranches, and culturally significant areas. Additionally, we’ve supported the protection of 10,000 more acres through collaborations with other land trusts.

Name: Fundación Los Canelos
Description:
Conservamos 70 hectáreas de bosque nativo en Pedregoso, Villarrica, donde trabajamos por la regeneración ecológica, la educación ambiental y la investigación científica.

Un espacio vivo para aprender, proteger y reconectar con la naturaleza.

Name: Big Cats France
Description:
BIG CATS est une association qui œuvre pour la protection et la défense des Grands Félins. Nous avons deux objectifs principaux : 

Le premier est de créer le premier sanctuaire Français fermé au public pour Lions, Tigres et Léopards: FCWS, French Captive Wildlife Sanctuary for BIG CATS, situé dans les Landes sur la commune de Mézos.

Le deuxième objectif est de lutter contre la disparition des espèces menacées. Nous travaillons sur un programme d’élevage des espèces citées sur la liste rouge de l’UICN, comme le tigre de Sumatra, le léopard de l’Amour ou la Panthère des Neiges pour une réintroduction en milieu naturel. La population des Léopards de l’Amour n’est par exemple plus que de 50 : 7 à 12 individus en Chine et entre 20 et 25 en Russie…Il est essentiel aujourd’hui de lutter pour préserver ces espèces en voie d’extinction.

Name: Saint Kateri Conservation Center
Description: The Saint Kateri Conservation Center is a Catholic conservancy and land trust that promotes faith, integral ecology, biodiversity, and climate resilience.

Name: Hnutí DUHA Šelmy
Description:
Podporujeme spontánní návrat velkých šelem − vlků, rysů a medvědů − do české krajiny. Zaměřujeme se na osvětu o jejich životě, chování a roli v přírodě. Vyvracíme pověry a dezinformace. Koordinujeme „Vlčí a Rysí hlídky“ − skupiny dobrovolníků, které se podílí na přímé ochraně a monitoringu šelem v místě výskytu. Jedná se o jedinečnou aktivitu − jeden z největších projektů Citizen Science v Česku.

Jsme pobočným spolkem Hnutí DUHA, který je organizačně samostatný a soustředíme se výhradně na téma ochrany šelem a záležitostí s ní spjatých.

Name: Monterey Audubon Society
Description: Founded in 1940, the Monterey Audubon Society is a nonprofit organization dedicated to conserving and celebrating the birds and wildlife of the greater Monterey Bay region. Our chapter includes 1,200 members throughout Monterey and parts of Santa Cruz and San Benito Counties. We engage in an array of educational, citizen-science, and advocacy initiatives geared toward protecting the region's birds and its biological diversity.

Name: Paisajes Efímeros
Description: Our photo documentary project aims to shed light on the devastating effects of global warming on Ecuador's glaciers. Through stunning imagery and storytelling, we will capture the retreat of these glaciers and the impact it has on the country's water supply. We want to raise awareness that glacier retreat is only a symptom of a bigger issue - climate change - and that Ecuador's vital water source, the páramos which store, regulate and release the water are under threat. Join us on a journey to discover the beauty of Ecuador's glaciers and the urgent need to address the climate crisis.

Name: Mianus River Gorge
Description:
Founded in 1953, Mianus River Gorge, Inc. is an independent, not-for-profit organization, whose mission is to preserve, protect and promote appreciation of the natural heritage of the Mianus River watershed through land acquisition and conservation, scientific research and public education throughout the region.  A 501(c)(3) non-profit conservation and education organization, Mianus River Gorge:

    -- Protects over 1,000 acres in Bedford, North Castle and Pound Ridge, NY and Stamford CT, including one of the last stands of old-growth forest in the northeast US,

    -- Manages a 794-acre nature preserve which is open seasonally with 5 miles of hiking trails and outdoor exhibits,

    -- Conducts applied research and offers innovative research-based education programs for high school, undergraduate and graduate students, and

    -- Works on initiatives to protect the Mianus River Watershed from its headwaters to the Long Island Sound.

The Mianus River Gorge was the first land project of the Nature Conservancy as well as the first National Natural History Landmark in the United States.

Name: Xploregaia
Description:
Inspired by the mother earth herself, Gaia was established to answer the urgent call to conserve the endangered Malaysian hornbills. 

For the past 20 years, we combined the power of science and arts in all our efforts. Gaia focuses on research and innovation to protect the hornbill population, then amplified by impactful artworks, visuals and initiatives to inspire the public to do the same.

Mission: To conserve Malaysia’s iconic species through continuous scientific research, creative innovation, impactful solutions, beneficial collaboration and people empowerment.

Vision: Building a thriving and sustainable future for the Malaysian forest, biodiversity and the people

Name: Heiloo Energie
Description: Heiloo Energie zet zich in voor energiebesparing en duurzaamheid in Heiloo. Ons doel is dat iedereen in Heiloo toegang heeft tot gratis advies.

Name: Willdlife Management Institute
Description: The Wildlife Management Institute (WMI) is a private, not for profit (501(c)(3)), scientific, and educational organization dedicated to the conservation, enhancement, and professional management of North America's wildlife and other natural resources.

Name: Youth for Our Planet Zambia
Description: YfOP is the global youth movement calling for urgent action on the nature crisis and the climate emergency

Name: TANZANIA ECO-TECH AND CONSERVATION HUB (TEACH)
Description:
We work on Research & publication, and carries out projects based on conservation, ecotourism, sustainable engineering, On-job Technical training, community empowerment, renewable energies and conservation agriculture 
https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VbAuIR590x2zuezZNb3u

Name: EcoMatch
Description: So, remember, our goal is to inspire individuals like you to participate in projects that can propel them into entrepreneurial ventures. We aim to MATCH young entrepreneurs with projects that will create a positive impact on the environment.

Name: Tulukani - Conservation | Education | Tourism
Description: Based in Zambia, Tulukani is a consultancy offering Protected Area Management Effectiveness (PAME) assessments, conservation project management services, eco-tourism product development and environmental education programme design.

Name: Know your Wildlife and the Environment Zambia - KWEZ
Ren Discover
Description:
Welcome to Ren Discover! 

Our mission is to celebrate and preserve the remarkable diversity of wildlife and the natural world, focusing on the urgent need for global conservation efforts. From majestic creatures roaming the wild to the intricate ecosystems they inhabit, we aim to highlight the vital importance of protecting nature in its purest form.

As we face unprecedented environmental challenges, the urgency for responsible stewardship of our planet’s biodiversity has never been more pressing. Through informative content, advocacy, and collaboration with experts and organizations, we seek to raise awareness about wildlife threats, such as habitat loss, climate change, and poaching, while promoting sustainable practices that help preserve the natural world for future generations.

Our page serves as a platform to engage individuals, organizations, and communities with a passion for wildlife conservation and environmental education. Whether you are a professional in the field, a conservationist, or someone who deeply cares about nature, we encourage you to join our mission. Together, we can make a difference.

We aim to inspire action, foster education, and encourage collaboration on a global scale. 

Join us in advocating for a sustainable future where humans and wildlife coexist harmoniously. Let’s work together to ensure that the beauty of nature is preserved and cherished for generations to come. 

#WildlifeConservation #EnvironmentalAwareness #Sustainability #Biodiversity #Conservation #NatureProtection #SustainableLiving #EcoFriendly #WildlifeProtection #ClimateAction

Name: OEJV.CH Ökologischer Jagdverein Schweiz
Description:
Der Ökologische Jagdverein Schweiz (oejv.ch) setzt sich für eine ökologisch ausgerichtete Jagd ein, die den Prinzipien der Nachhaltigkeit, der wissenschaftlichen Erkenntnissen und des Tierwohles verpflichtet ist.

Wir

bieten regelmässig diverse Anlässe an, wie Exkursionen, Diskussionsrunden und Weiterbildungen.
fördern den respektvollen Dialog mit Partnerorganisationen aus Forst, Landwirtschaft, Naturschutz und Bildung.
setzten uns positiv und aufklärend für eine nachhaltige, ökologische und wissenschaftsbasierte 
Jagd ein.

Name: Aranyani Life
Description: A storytelling platform that brings together nature, culture, art, and mythology.

Name: Wild Waikawa
Description:
Wild Waikawa is a restoration project for a 200 hectare Significant Natural Area at the head of Boons Valley, Waikawa, New Zealand.

Led by Michelle Clark & Nigel Muir and encouraged by many supporters, sponsors & volunteers. Wild Waikawa has recently received the 2025 Landscape & Habitat Award - Port Marlborough Marlborough Environment Awards. We would like to acknowledge our key partners with Te Åtiawa Iwi, Waikawa Marae, Marlborough District Council. Department of Conservation, NMIT Nelson-Marlborough, Trees that Count, Waitohi Kindergarten, Untouched World Foundation, The Student Volunteer Army, Marlborough Boys College and numerous partners supporting funding, volunteers & direction.

Our 400-year mission is to recreate an ancient, natural, wonderland that our ancestors used to know.

In restoring this precious whenua/land that includes Te Maunga o Pirpiri, the sacred mountain of mana whenua in this area, Te Ātiawa o te Waka-a-Maui. Wild Waikawa is seeking to achieve three major legacy benefits:

1. Establish a rich wildlife corridor from the two highest points in Picton (Te Maunga o Piripiri and Mt. McCormick/Te Maunga o PiriPiri Te Hautapu) past the Waikawa Marae and to the sea (The Snout and Kaipupu Sanctuary).

2. To bring People and Nature together so both can rejuvenate.

3. Amplify the conservation actions in our region and globally

Please reach out to us if you are interested in;

- Volunteerism - individual, group, community, business, schools
- Conservation training - we host interns supporting practical experience
- Native Plant supply 
- Eco-Tourism
- Nature event facilitation
- Nature connection experiences - half day and full day
- Partnerships - to protect our environment, and support our young people

Please feel free to connect Michelle Clark or Nigel Muir. See website for more information www.wildwaikawa.co.nz

Name: Society for the Conservation of Nature of Libera - SCNL
Description:
The Society for the Conservation of Nature of 
Liberia (SCNL) was established in 1986. Since its 
establishment, SCNL has remained the lead national 
conservation organization in Liberia. We are a non
for-profit organization helping to protect Liberia’s 
biodiversity. 
Our mandate is to educate, encourage and assist 
the society understand and appreciate nature, build 
consensus on effective management of biodiversity 
by ensuring that natural resources are sustainably 
managed.

Name: Sustainable Ceder
Description:
Sustainable Ceder's main purpose is the development and implementation of a strategic and collaborative approach to the re-establishment of the Clanwilliam cedar tree. We will act as the central link between various organisations, partners, role-players, tourists, and communities within the Cederberg area.

Through this integrated conservation model, we strive to increase the effectiveness of our conservation efforts while also uplifting the communities within the area.

Name: Forever Reef Project
Description: The Forever Reef Project is dedicated to preserving the genetic biodiversity of coral species worldwide, beginning with the Great Barrier Reef. By collecting, cataloguing, and storing living coral fragments and genetic samples in a state-of-the-art facility, we act as a ‘coral ark,’ protecting these vital ecosystems from climate change. Our project also supports global reef research and restoration by providing a land-based repository to accelerate knowledge and conservation efforts. We have a critical window to secure the future of coral species—join us before it's too late.

Name: Kenya Rhino Range Expansion
Description:
The Kenya Rhino Range Expansion (KRRE) Initiative represents a once-in-a-generation opportunity to transform Kenya’s conservation landscape and drive socioeconomic growth. By restoring black rhino populations across their historic range, KRRE will expand Kenya’s rhino habitat to become one of the largest in the world. Yet this is more than a conservation program — it is a catalyst for ecological integrity, economic prosperity, and national pride.

KRRE is built on decades of conservation success. Form a historic low of -380 individuals in the 1980s, Kenya’s black rhino population has now surpassed 1,000. But overcrowding in existing sanctuaries threatens further growth. Working in partnership with KWS, conservancies and other stakeholders, KRRE will provide the space and support needed for Kenya’s black rhino population to thrive.

KRRE current focus areas include Central Kenya and the Tsavo ecosystem. Together, these geographies hold the country’s largest black rhino populations, face critical overcrowding challenges, and offer the greatest potential to expand, reconnect, and secure habitats while delivering wide-reaching benefits for people and biodiversity.

Name: Horseshoe Crab Recovery Coalition
Description:
A coalition of conservation leaders aiming to reverse years of decline and fully restore one of the world's oldest living species, the American Horseshoe Crab.  Our goals include:
1.  End the harvest of female horseshoe crabs to ensure that populations are large enough to support the needs of birds and fish that need their eggs to survive.
2.  Reform the horseshoe crab bleeding industry to require transparency and reduce mortality to zero, and work with states to ensure compliance.
3.  Speed the adoption of synthetic lysate alternatives for use in biomedical toxicity testing by partnering with the biomedical industry to encourage the use of the synthetic alternative, rFC.
4.  Engage organizations and volunteers in outreach and community science programs aimed at rebuilding crab population, by building a movement and by restoring and protecting horseshoe crab habitat.

Name: Selati Wilderness Foundation NPC
Description:
The Selati Wilderness Foundation NPC (SWF) was established in 2018 to assist in supporting the unique conservation responsibility we all have at the Selati Game Reserve and surrounds. The specific objectives of the SWF are: 

- Support the local surrounding communities.
- Encourage and support the ethical conservation management of the Selati Game Reserve and surrounding areas.
- Focussed support on the endangered wildlife in the area.
- Support of the anti-poaching focussed primarily on the protection of rhino populations and the protection of the only known wild colony of the Encephalartos dyerieanus cycad.

The SWF is a registered Non-Profit Company (NPC), we prioritise transparency in handling donations, channelling funds towards achieving our stated objectives. The SWF is also  a registered Public Benefit Company which allows for tax efficient donations to be made.

Join us at the Selati Wilderness Foundation NPC as we continue our journey towards conservation excellence, community empowerment, and sustainable wildlife protection. Together, we can create a brighter future for our natural heritage and the communities that depend on it.

Name: eMission-X gGmbH
Description:
Wir verbinden Unternehmen mit hochwertigen Wald- und Naturflächen, um messbare, transparente und langfristige Klimawirkung zu schaffen.
Emission-X ermöglicht nachhaltige Investitionen in Biodiversität, CO₂-Bindung und den Schutz unserer natürlichen Lebensräume – wissenschaftlich fundiert, digital unterstützt und wirtschaftlich sinnvoll.
 Jetzt Kontakt aufnehmen und mehr erfahren.

Name: European Geoparks Network, EGN
Description: The goal of the EGN is to be a sustainable, professional and inclusive network which, through collaboration and innovation, generates action for the conservation, holistic understanding and enjoyment of Europe’s geological and related heritage and supports local communities.
