Description:
Plasticrea - Créateur de Solutions Innovantes en Plasturgie Recyclée

Passionné par l'innovation et la durabilité, Plasticrea se spécialise dans la conception et la fabrication de produits plastiques de haute qualité à partir de matériaux recyclés. Avec une expertise approfondie dans la plasturgie, nous offrons des solutions sur mesure pour répondre aux besoins spécifiques de nos clients.

Nos compétences clés :
- Conception et développement de produits : De l'idée initiale à la production finale, nous transformons vos concepts en réalité.
- Innovation durable : Nous intégrons des pratiques respectueuses de l'environnement dans nos processus de fabrication, en utilisant exclusivement du plastique recyclé.
- Qualité et précision : Nos produits sont fabriqués avec une attention méticuleuse aux détails et aux normes de qualité les plus élevées.

Notre mission :

Contactez-nous pour découvrir comment nous pouvons collaborer pour réaliser vos projets les plus ambitieux.

Name: Ecocidades Unilivre
Description:
A Ecocidades é um hub de inovação em projetos urbanos e ambientais. Nosso trabalho une tecnologia, impacto social e sustentabilidade para transformar cidades em espaços mais resilientes e humanos.

Coordenamos iniciativas de grande escala, como recuperação socioambiental, reurbanização, contenção de cheias, saneamento ecológico e planejamento de espaços públicos.

Nosso compromisso é criar cidades mais inteligentes, equilibrando progresso e qualidade de vida para as comunidades.

Name: Wildlife Coexistence Trust
Description:
At Wildlife Coexistence Trust (WCT), we drive practical, community-led solutions that ensure wildlife and people thrive together in the Mara region. We champion peaceful coexistence by reducing conflict, restoring balance, and strengthening local stewardship of natural resources.

We partner with communities to protect biodiversity, preserve cultural heritage, and secure sustainable livelihoods. By empowering local voices, we build trust and ignite collective action that safeguards both the wildlife and the people who call this region home.

WCT works at the frontlines of conservation—resolving human-wildlife conflict, educating communities, and mobilizing long-term support to keep the Mara thriving. Together, we’re not just imagining a better future—we’re creating it.

For Wildlife. For People. Now and Forever

Name: Native Plant Initiative of Greater New Orleans
Description: Increasing the use of native plants in our area by expanding public awareness of their ecological benefits, boosting availability, and by preserving and creating native plant communities.

Name: C.A.R.E. Centre for Animal Rehabilitation and Education
Description: C.A.R.E. is a highly regarded wildlife conservation & welfare charity that has been specialising in baboons since 1989.  A fully equipped, ethical, reputable, government and NSPCA endorsed rehabilitation centre, situated on the banks of the beautiful  Olifants River, within the ecologically diverse and important wildlife habitat of the Greater Kruger National Park.  Volunteer, Help and Support our sanctuary to help us protect & conserve baboons and their habitat.

Description: The world we are living  is one, that is  for us and for our future generation, land pours water to one pool "our Ocean", climatic change and global increase of temperature caused stress to our healthy pool. We as Blue Vision we are dedicated to help community mitigating the further effect due climatic changes. We believe in community because community have power to change the world.

Description:
Popasus œuvre pour la réhabilitation des zones humides et instruit à la survie en milieu naturel.

Name: Ducks Unlimited Canada Campus Club - University of Guelph
Description: The Ducks Unlimited Canada Campus Club aims to engage and empower students to take meaningful action for conservation. Our purpose is to build awareness and inspire conservation efforts that enhance and protect wetlands, ecosystems, and biodiversity, while fostering leadership and collaboration on campus and beyond. The Club will offer opportunities in three key areas: Conservation – Members will participate in hands-on activities such as habitat restoration, citizen science, and local conservation projects, gaining real-world experience in environmental stewardship. Career Development – Students will build professional skills by leading initiatives, organizing events, and connecting with conservation experts through workshops and networking opportunities. These experiences will enhance resumes and prepare members for careers in environmental fields. Community – We will create an inclusive space for students from all disciplines to connect with nature and each other. Outreach events, guided nature walks, and partnerships with local organizations will strengthen ties between campus and the wider community. By combining education, action, and collaboration, this Club will give students the skills and confidence to become agents of change. It will not only benefit the environment but also foster leadership, professional growth, and a stronger sense of community among students.

Name: Community Nature Reserve
Description: Community Nature Reserve (CNR) is a UK-wide network transforming private gardens into connected wildlife habitats. CNR empowers homeowners, renters, and communities to combat biodiversity loss from their own gardens. Every registered space- from balconies to farmhouse acres- becomes part of a distributed nature reserve creating vital corridors for pollinators, birds, hedgehogs, and other native species.

Name: Organización Ecológica Sol y Verde
Description:
We are a rural Guatemalan community organisation dedicated to restoration of the Mayan jungle, permaculture principles, local engagement and education, regenerative agroforestry, and sustainable living in harmony with nature. 

Our main project site is 2.5 hectares of degraded land near the village of Paxcaman (Peten, Guatemala). We are restoring the area by planting a diverse food forest with agroforestry trees (cacao, ramon, papaya, etc), medicinal herbs, spices, legumes and nutritious vegetables. A biodiverse mix of forest trees, palms and flowers are also being planted to encourage insects and birdlife. We use ancestral practices, organic methods, culturally significant species and permaculture design. We are building an ecological school on the site using traditional bioconstruction techniques with locally sourced, eco-friendly materials. The school will be a space for permaculture focussed learning, community groups, and school visits. We are also creating a tree nursery grown from locally collected seeds and an aquaculture system with tilapia, to provide supplies of native agroforestry seedlings and fish for local families.

Beyond the project site, we run outreach activities, such as nature restoration community events and providing technical support for farmers to transition to permaculture practices. We also host international volunteers and partner with organisations locally and overseas.

Name: Northampton County Conservation District
Description:
Over 65 years ago Pennsylvania state legislators recognized the need to support grassroots local conservation efforts.  As a result, the PA Conservation District Law was passed authorizing the creation of county conservation districts in every county, except Philadelphia. The Northampton County Conservation District was founded in 1961 when a group of farmers joined together to promote the use of conservation practices to protect soil and water resources in Northampton County.
 
Today, the Northampton County Conservation District's main goals are to promote soil conservation, maintain or improve water quality and promote environmental education. To achieve these goals, the Conservation District performs a variety of programs ranging from reviewing erosion and sediment pollution control plans for earth disturbance activities to providing advice on starting a local watershed association.

The Conservation District is led by a seven member Board of Directors. District Directors are volunteers that are nominated by an approved local organization and then appointed by Northampton County government. Their responsibilities range from policy creation to supporting efforts of district staff and their partner agencies to provide quality administrative, technical, and educational assistance to all county residents. Four members of the board are farmer directors, two are public directors and one is a representative of Northampton County Council.

Name: Fundación Krucial
Description:
At Krucial Foundation, we believe in a world where the interests of nature, businesses, and people not only coexist but align to create a more resilient and prosperous future for all. We know that balancing economic progress, social well-being, and the protection of natural resources is not only possible but crucial for the survival of our species and all forms of life on Earth.

En la Fundación Krucial, creemos en un mundo donde los intereses de la naturaleza, los negocios y las personas no solo coexisten, sino que se alinean para crear un futuro más resiliente y próspero para todos. Sabemos que el equilibrio entre el progreso económico, el bienestar social y la protección de los recursos naturales no solo es posible, sino esencial para la supervivencia de nuestra especie y de todas las formas de vida en el planeta.

Name: Wildbienenglück GmbH
Description: Wildbienenglück produces high-quality, mostly handmade nesting aids for wild bees consisting of beech wood, spruce glue wood and stainless steel.

Name: Hike And Draw
Description: Hike And Draw is a nature-based art program that teaches people of all ages and backgrounds about the great outdoors through drawing and nature journaling.

Name: HumedaliaAC
Description:
Somos una organización que surge por la inquietud de preservar los humedales del país, reconociendo la importancia de los servicios ecosistémicos que brindan y la estrecha y compleja relación entre el bienestar humano y su conservación.
Trabajamos prioritariamente en el humedal de Xochimilco, Ciudad de México en donde trabajamos por recuperar la biodiversidad y rescatar la cultura lacustre cuyo eje principal abarca la chinampería, pero también comprende actividades tradicionales como la pesca y artesanía que están desapareciendo. Para esto, ofrecemos capacitaciones en bioconstrucción, restauración ecológica, reproducción de especies nativas, y tenemos un programa de educación ambiental permanente a través del cual conectamos a los visitantes con el patrimonio biocultural del sitio, invitando a la reflexión y acciones para su conservación.

Name: SeizeTheSea
Description: SeizeTheSea aims to educate on ocean conservation efforts. Resources & activities are posted to help engage the public. Education is prevention, preventing further damage to our oceans will help our climate crisis and help educate individuals on the pros & cons behind their actions. Science-based knowledge is acquired and used as the primary resource for education efforts/ promotion projects. Meaningful partnerships have been established since 2015 with cities, organizations, and celebrities.

Name: UniVersElles
Description: Universelles est une association qui a pour but d'apporter une aide en matière de protections hygiéniques aux femmes en situation de précarité dans la métropole lilloise.

Name: Belize Wild Cats
Description: We do small wild cat research and conservation for margay and jaguarundi in the Central American country of Belize.

Description:
Avec WK, protégeons la nature et préservons l’avenir.   
Engagé(e) dans la sensibilisation, la protection de l’environnement et le développement durable, je travaille pour créer un impact positif à travers des actions concrètes, des partenariats responsables et l’éducation écologique.  

Nous croyons que chaque geste compte. Ensemble, construisons un avenir plus vert, plus sûr et plus durable pour les générations futures.  

 Sensibilisation  
 Protection des ressources naturelles  
 Actions locales et globales  

#Environnement #DéveloppementDurable #WK #Écologie #AvenirVert

Name: Fundació Comunitària La Nou
Description:
Un grupo de jóvenes construyó una masía abandonada en la década de los 80 y creó actividades cooperativas educativas. Mas de Noguera celebra su 40º aniversario promoviendo La Nou, una fundación que busca ser un motor de cambio para el medio rural valenciano. 

La Fundació La Nou tiene varios objetivos sostenibles, incluyendo la educación, la cultura rural, la difusión de iniciativas sostenibles y la colaboración con empresas y Antenas comarcales. 

La Nou cuenta con el apoyo de miembros del Patronato, la Asociación Española de Fundaciones, la Universidad Jaume I de Castelló y la Caixa Popular.

Name: Village and Wilderness
Description: We are a network for community-based innovators supporting efforts to discover, prove and share replicable climate adaptation strategies.

Name: Tierra Resource Management
Description:
Tierra Resource Management is a full-service consulting firm that specializes in land conservation.  We are experts in conservation property assessments, conservation easements and the management of western rangelands, forests and wildlife habitats.  

We are devoted exclusively to the American Farmer and Rancher and other rural owners of private agricultural or forested lands.  We strongly support the concept of private land ownership and management that fosters sustainable and profitable family ranches and farms. 

Our Strengths and Capabilities:

>   FOCUS ON PRIVATE LANDS: Conservation makes good business-sense for agricultural producers.  We work entirely on private lands and we support agriculture in its many forms. 

>   A SOLUTION-ORIENTED APPROACH:   We seek win-win solutions where ecological benefits and economic incentives go hand in hand.   We're making sure your concerns are turned into practical conservation solutions.  

>   REGULATORY AGENCY ACCEPTANCE:  We collaborate cordially with federal, state, and local agencies and work hard to maintain professional relationships with government officials, many of whom we know personally as colleagues and peers.  

>   MINIMIZING PROJECT COSTS:  We save you money, but we don’t cut corners. Knowing relevant regulations, applying the most practical technologies and our knack for developing innovative ideas allow us to offer you the most competitive services.  

>   MAXIMIZING BENEFITS:  We work for you, not radical environmental groups.  We represent landowners in dealing with agencies, finding funding, project planning and implementation.    Our goal is to offer you economical and profitable solutions that support your family, your lifestyle and your land.

Name: Evergreen Wildlife Management
Description:
At Evergreen Wildlife Management we offer everything from Commercial Wildlife Management & Exclusion to Domestic Pest Control.

Our tried and proven systems allow us to achieve a success rate better than anyone in our field of work. 

Our services stretch nationwide, so no job is too big or too far......

More more information on the services provided at EWM, head over to our website @http://www.evergreenwildlifemanagement.co.uk

Name: Burlington Wildways
Description:
Burlington Wildways is a partnership that connects and protects the wild places and paths of Burlington, Vermont.

We are a partnership between Burlington Parks, Recreation and Waterfront, Winooski Valley Park District, Rock Point, Burlington Conservation Board, Burlington City Council, and the Intervale Center.

Name: Noctura
Description:
Exploring how darkness connects ecology, astronomy & culture.

Tours & guide training across East Africa in sustainable astrotourism & noctourism.

Name: GreenBlue Impact
Description:
GreenBlue Impact is a mission-driven company founded in early 2025 by Vincent Balandras and Vanessa Plessy, with a clear purpose: to take action to protect the environment, water, and biodiversity. We believe the key challenge lies in our cities; That’s where the battle begins.
We must stop waste before it reaches water: rivers, streams, and ultimately our seas and oceans. Only then can we truly protect marine life and preserve biodiversity for future generations.
----------
GreenBlue Impact est une entreprise à mission créée début 2025 par Vincent Balandras et Vanessa Plessy dont la raison d’être est "d'agir pour la préservation de l’environnement, l’eau et la biodiversité". Notre conviction est que l’enjeu est en ville et qu’il faut stopper le déchet avant qu’il n'atteigne l’eau, les rivières et les fleuves pour protéger nos mers et nos océans, et pour préserver la biodiversité.

Name: Fondation Ruisseau Bouchard (FRB)
Description:
À propos de la FRB:
La Fondation Ruisseau Bouchard (FRB), fondée en 2021 à Dorval, sur l’île de Montréal, est un organisme de bienfaisance enregistré dont la mission est simple mais ambitieuse : restaurer la santé du ruisseau Bouchard, un des rares cours d’eau urbains subsistants sur l’île, et démontrer que des solutions concrètes, réalistes et durables peuvent faire une différence.
La FRB travaille en collaboration avec des partenaires locaux, notamment des entreprises, petites et grandes, qui possèdent des terrains dans le bassin versant, ainsi que des citoyens, des écoles, des étudiants, et des municipalités. Ensemble, nous visons à redonner vie à ce cours d’eau tout en posant les bases d’un modèle de gestion des bassins versants urbains applicable ailleurs.
-----------------------
ABOUT THE FRB: 
The Bouchard Creek Foundation (FRB), established in 2021 in Dorval on the Island of Montreal, is a registered charity with a simple yet ambitious mission: to restore the health of Bouchard Creek, one of the few remaining urban watercourses on the island, and to demonstrate that practical, realistic, and sustainable solutions can make a meaningful difference.
The FRB collaborates with local partners, including businesses—both large and small—owning land within the watershed, as well as citizens, schools, students, and municipalities. Together, we aim to revive this vital watercourse while laying the groundwork for an urban watershed management model that can be applied elsewhere.

Name: Society For Enviromental Conservation
Description:
The organization was established in 2006 and registered on
December 8, 2008 in Monrovia. SEC’s mission is to promote and protect ecologically-sustainable, environmental justice in a participatory development that result in equality and equal opportunities with strong sense of ownerships for all. SEC currently works across four sectors including 1) Forestry and Natural Resources Management 2) Sustainable Livelihood/Agriculture/Energy/ Climate Change Adaptation and Mitigation 3) Environmental Justice 4) Biodiversity Conservation.
The aim of its Forestry and Biodiversity Conservation program is to  of Liberia’s remaining tropical rainforest while empowering local communities in biodiversity

organization trained financial officers who guide the uses of the institution’s fund

Name: UBC Animal Behavior and Cognition Lab
Wildlife Health Watch
Description:
Wildlife Health Watch is a national initiative strengthening wildlife health surveillance across Kenya. Launched in 2023, the project trains rangers to report wildlife health events in real time using the EarthRanger platform, enabling faster veterinary response and early detection of emerging threats.

Led by the Smithsonian’s Global Health Program in collaboration with the Kenya Wildlife Service, the Wildlife Research and Training Institute, the Directorate of Veterinary Services, the San Diego Zoo Wildlife Alliance, and the International Livestock Research Institute, the project has trained more than 1000 rangers across 21 conservancies and generated over 250 wildlife health reports to date.

Wildlife Health Watch is now integrating artificial intelligence to improve outbreak detection and is preparing for a structured handover to government agencies, ensuring long-term sustainability and national ownership. Partners such as Mpala Research Centre, Darwin Initiative, the Zoological Society of London, 51 Degrees, and Vetinwild Consultancy Limited continue to support the growth and expansion of this surveillance system.

Name: Envol sur le Bassin du Congo
Description:
Faire découvrir le bassin du Congo à travers ses extraordinaires écosystèmes biotiques et abiotiques (i.e., les systèmes forestiers, fluviaux, lagunaires, les éléments de relief, etc.) et partager des informations environnementales afin de sensibiliser davantage sur la protection de ce Bassin qui a une superficie de 4 millions de km2, niché au cœur de l'Afrique.
Les forêts du Bassin du Congo constituent la deuxième plus vaste forêt tropicale et poumon vert de la planète après l'Amazonie. 
Elles possèdent une exceptionnelle valeur écologique.

Name: UGPO - Lac de Bizerte
Description:
EcoPact Bizerte : Un engagement pour un avenir durable 
EcoPact Bizerte est un programme intégré de dépollution visant à restaurer la qualité des eaux du lac de Bizerte et de ses écosystèmes. Labellisé dans le cadre de l’initiative Euro-Méditerranéenne Horizon 2020, Il renferme plus que 30 projets d’investissement et d’accompagnement pour un développement socioéconomique durable.
Grâce à un budget de 81,115 millions d’euros, EcoPact Bizerte agit sur plusieurs fronts :
 Dépollution industrielle 
 Assainissement 
 Gestion des déchets solides pour un environnement plus propre
 Appui institutionnel et renforcement des capacités locales
 Aménagement des infrastructures côtières pour une meilleure qualité de vie
Bailleurs de fonds :
Le programme est financé grâce à l’appui de l’Union Européenne, via un don à travers l'instrument pour le Voisinage, ainsi qu’à des crédits de la Banque Européenne d’Investissement (BEI) et de la Banque Européenne pour la Reconstruction et le Développement (BERD), en complément de la contribution de l’État tunisien.
Avec plus de 530 000 habitants bénéficiaires, ce programme est une initiative participative qui fédère acteurs économiques, autorités locales et société civile pour une Bizerte plus verte et durable. 
 En savoir plus : EcoPact Bizerte

Cette version reflète fidèlement les sources de financement et le budget actualisé.  Besoin d’autres ajustements ? Je suis là pour affiner !

Name: Washington County Soil & Water Conservation District
Description:
Founded in 1950, the Washington County Soil & Water Conservation District is a state agency dedicated to the conservation and proper management of natural resources.

 

As our name suggests, we focus on soil and water management, which typically leads us to participate in a number of agricultural projects, such as water use management plans for irrigation and soil testing.

 

We also contribute to a number of wilderness conservation programs throughout eastern Maine, such as river restoration and shoreline erosion assistance. These projects can extend past county lines, so you might find us working outside of Washington County. 



As an agency, we are dedicated to service. We are happy to answer any questions and offer any assistance to the people of Downeast Maine. From composting to pond building, and everything in between, we are eager to help!

Name: GRID
Description:
Around the world, transportation infrastructure connects people and drives economies. But when poorly planned, it fragments habitats, increases emissions, and weakens resilience. The Greening Transportation Infrastructure Development (GRID) program aims to balance transportation infrastructure needs with protecting and restoring nature. 

GRID is supported by the Global Environment Facility, led by World Wildlife Fund, and implemented in partnership with the Asian Development Bank and the United Nations Environment Programme. Running from 2025 to 2031, the program brings early, integrated planning and decision-making to the sustainable development of ports and roads. 

Alongside its global knowledge platform, projects in Malaysia, Nepal, the Philippines, Suriname, and Ukraine will demonstrate nature-positive approaches to protect ecosystems while strengthening communities. Together, these efforts advance a global transition toward transportation infrastructure that safeguards, conserves, and restores key coastal, marine, and terrestrial ecosystems.

Name: Epro Ltd
Description:
Based in Taupō, Epro is an industry leading, privately owned, family business that delivers a range of predator control services to private and public sector clients throughout New Zealand. For more than 20 years the company has been committed to delivering specialised services working on the front lines of the battle to protect New Zealand’s biodiversity through aerial, ground and survey control programmes. 
We, as a team, are passionate about protecting New Zealand’s most vulnerable native wildlife, contributing to the regeneration of our native flora and fauna and protecting our valuable export market by eradicating TB. There are exciting opportunities in the industry and we continue to expand our operations so we can deliver great results for our clients and enhance New Zealand’s biodiversity. 
Epro has sought after expertise for other related activities, such as providing specialist technical advice, helping develop industry innovations and participating in formal research.  We recognise that quality and innovation are key drivers of business success along with the continuous development of our highly skilled staff, which has made Epro a market leader.

Name: ReduNOx Industrial Services

Graag stel ik ReduNOx Industrial Services B.V. aan u voor.

Wij zijn gespecialiseerd in het professioneel reinigen van industriële katalysatoren en DPF-filters, en hebben hierin al meerdere jaren ervaring opgebouwd. Daarnaast beschikken wij over specialistische kennis van bepaalde doseersystemen, zoals DeNOx-systemen, die binnen diverse industrieën veel worden toegepast.

Dankzij ons deskundige team, moderne apparatuur en veilige werkwijze zijn wij in staat deze reinigingen en onderhoudswerkzaamheden snel, efficiënt en met minimale overlast uit te voeren – zowel op locatie als in onze eigen werkplaats.

We merken dat in diverse sectoren, van maritiem tot industrie, regelmatig behoefte is aan betrouwbare ondersteuning op dit vlak. Daarom willen wij graag met u in contact komen om te bespreken of wij ook uw organisatie kunnen ondersteunen bij het reinigen van katalysatoren, filters of het onderhouden van doseersystemen.

Name: Food For Rhinos NPC
Description:
We have been caring for Rhinos since 2005, and in 2021 established the NPC “FOOD FOR RHINOS” to  raise funds for their continued care. 
We provide daily feed for over 60 rhinos, as well as 24-hour anti-poaching security. We also provide 24-hour veterinary care, and daily observation of the rhinos to ensure their wellbeing. In addition, we maintain and are responsible for the upkeep of the property that houses the sanctuary. These activities make a significant contribution to the conservation and safe keeping of our world rhino population.

Name: NACOFA
Description:
The National Alliance of Community Forest Associations (NACOFA) is a community network that advocates for and supports the rights of communities to take centre stage in forest and general environment management in Kenya.
NACOFA was formed in 2002 and finally registered in 2005 for the purposes of creating a community driven focal point (federation) through which communities and relevant stake holders in the NRM sector can channel their ideas and decisions that can influence policy makers and implementing agencies in the sector.
The main target of (NACOFA) was and has been to reach out to forest adjacent communities to form Community Forest (CFAs) around the country, to have a strong voice and be recognition as primary stake holders in the forest sector. Have focused community empowerment programs and advocacy for better policies that ensure sustainable management of natural resources in Kenya.

Name: National Protected Area Authority and Conservation Trust
Description:
National Protected Area Authority (NPAA) and Conservation Trust Sierra Leone is a leading organization dedicated to the preservation, protection, and sustainable management of Sierra Leone's natural resources. With a strong commitment to biodiversity conservation and environmental stewardship, NPAA plays a vital role in safeguarding the country's unique ecosystems and promoting sustainable development.

As the national authority responsible for managing protected areas, NPAA works tirelessly to ensure the effective governance and enforcement of conservation policies. Through collaboration with local communities, government agencies, and international partners, they strive to create a harmonious balance between human activities and nature.

NPAA takes pride in its comprehensive approach towards conservation. By conducting research, monitoring wildlife populations, implementing habitat restoration initiatives, and raising awareness about environmental issues among stakeholders, they actively contribute to the long-term sustainability of Sierra Leone's natural heritage.

With their dedicated team of experts in ecology, biodiversity management, community engagement, and policy advocacy; NPAA is at the forefront of innovative conservation strategies. They empower local communities by involving them in decision-making processes while providing training opportunities that promote sustainable livelihoods linked to nature-based tourism.

Through their tireless efforts in protecting Sierra Leone's rich biodiversity hotspots such as rainforests, coastal areas, wetlands, and marine ecosystems; NPAA ensures that future generations can continue to enjoy these invaluable natural treasures.

National Protected Area Authority and Conservation Trust Sierra Leone: Committed guardians of nature's legacy for a sustainable future.

Name: Sanctuary Of Sentient Beings
Description:
Sanctuary of Sentient Beings is a charity dedicated to providing refuge for sick, rescued and orphaned animals by caring for country.

Located just an hour west of Melbourne in the Brisbane Ranges National Park, we believe in the power of kindness, compassion, and creativity to connect people with animals and nature.

We are committed to wilderness regeneration, restoring natural habitats, protecting wildlife and regenerating farm land. Through these efforts, we aim to foster a harmonious relationship between humans, animals, climate and country.

Name: Frame the Wild
Description:
Our journey began with an idea, a tribute to the animals lost in the ongoing struggle between human encroachment and conservation efforts. Each booking for our photographic safaris fuels a noble cause – part of your fees goes directly to conservation.
Embrace the adage, "If you love what you do, you will never work a day in your life." Our journey began with a profound idea born from the heart—a tribute to the animals lost daily in the ongoing struggle between human encroachment and conservation efforts. It ignited a fervent desire to immerse ourselves in nature's embrace and safeguard it for generations to come. Because it is the right thing to do.

Hosana, Tavangumi, Hukumuri, Shangwa, Nyeleti, Scotia—their names echo the tragic toll of human-wildlife conflict. Recent incidents involving the timbavati and Maribye leopards, ensnared and wounded, serve as stark reminders of our collective responsibility to do better for conservation.

From this imperative, "Frame the Wild" and "Frame it Forward" emerge. Each booking for our photographic safaris fuels a noble cause—part of your fees goes directly to a conservation non-profit organization of your choosing. Not only do you immerse yourself in nature's wonders and capture timeless memories, but your participation in our safaris directly supports initiatives in the Greater Kruger, particularly in the Kruger and Greater Kruger region. Sales of photos on our website also goes towards your chosen conservation effort. Together, we fortify fences and deploy dedicated teams to combat poaching, ensuring a safer, more sustainable future for our wildlife.

Name: Urbanden
Description:
Urbanden es un proyecto que explora cómo crear oasis frente al caos urbano, a través de huertas que generan beneficios cruciales cada vez más escasos en la ciudad.
El beneficio ambiental es un cimiento de sociedad, no un lujo.

Huir al campo no resuelve el problema. El crecimiento urbano desordenado es un reto que se expande y complejiza. Afrontarlo implica actuar desde dentro, donde el conflicto es más visible y manejable.

Trabajamos en ese borde: la tensión fértil entre ciudad y naturaleza, poniendo a prueba soluciones accesibles que potencian la adaptación y funciones de las plantas en entornos urbanos.

Nuestro enfoque es atrevidamente práctico: exploramos, aplicamos y ajustamos sin esperar condiciones perfectas, combinando ingenio, saberes tradicionales y acción directa para demostrar y replicar mejores formas de habitar la ciudad hoy.

Name: Olympia Ecosystems
Description: Olympia Ecosystems is established to protect, preserve and restore the diverse ecosystems in and around Olympia, Washington that include the freshwater, shoreline, tidal waters, grasslands and upland forests that are home to the Pacific Great Blue Heron, cutthroat trout, salmon and companion species. In coordination with community members, local agencies, and other non- profit groups, we will work to ensure that these ecosystems remain vital in perpetuity.

Name: Almada Mata Atlântica Project
Description: AMAP is a non-profit organization dedicated to the conservation of the Mata Atlântica and its biodiversity in the state of Bahia. AMAP began its work in the region in 2017 with the aim of protecting the habitat of the endangered golden-headed lion tamarin, a small species of marmoset endemic to the Cocoa Coast of Bahia and a flagship species of the region. In the following years, AMAP expanded its activities in the region to include the conservation of the Mata Atlântica and its flora and fauna for the well-being of humans and nature. Golden-headed lion tamarins are the symbol of our mission.

Name: Red de Jóvenes Líderes en Áreas Protegidas y Conservadas de América Latina y el Caribe
Community & Conservation Investment Alliance (CCIA)
Description: The CCIA selects and introduces conservation projects to financial institutions that would like to trade and develop the biological assets on formally conserved natural habitats. We facilitate the baseline studies, management plans, trading of assets and remuneration to these conservation areas. Projects in our portfolio receive monetary remuneration for conservation success such as the reintroduction and nett growth of priority species populations, increase in biodiversity and promotion of United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. The CCIA leverages its relationships with strong Alliance partners to fulfil its operational mandate to global financial institutions.

Name: Fundación Soy Conservación
Description: Contribuimos activamente a la conservación de la biodiversidad, centrándonos en la transformación socioambiental de comunidades rurales a través de la ciencia ciudadana, la educación, el arte, la comunicación y los negocios verdes.

Name: Wildlife Free to Roam
Description:
Welcome to Wildlife Free to Roam (WFR), a conservation company dedicated to improving ecological connectivity between protected areas for the long-term resilience of wildlife and ecosystem services under global change.

Our mission is to provide scientific support to guide conservation management by conducting ecological corridor research and development. We collaborate with organizations such as the Peace Parks Foundation to investigate connectivity corridors at landscape scale. These regions support vast amounts of biodiversity, including threatened mammal species and numerous endemic plant, bird, reptile, and amphibian species.

We believe that improving ecological connectivity and allowing animals to move freely within Transfrontier Conservation Areas is of utmost importance to ensure the long-term resilience of these conservation areas. Our team of experts is committed to providing guidance and support to enable sustainable conservation management practices that will benefit wildlife and related ecosystem services.

Join us in our mission to conserve wildlife and ecosystems by promoting ecological connectivity. Connect with us to learn more about our research and initiatives or to collaborate on conservation projects.

Name: Turtle Rescue of the Hamptons
Description:
Turtle Rescue of the Hamptons is a not-for-profit 501(c)(3) charitable corporation located on the North Fork of Long Island. Our mission is to rescue, rehabilitate, and release wild chelonian species native to Long Island, to provide a sanctuary with a high quality of life for those who have sustained a permanent disability, and to allow injured animals to live out their lives with dignity and respect. We work to promote a safe and healthy natural environment by providing public education services regarding the threats and challenges facing our native populations.

Our state-of-the-art rehabilitation facility, “Turtle Manor,” is a charming c. 1920 farmhouse, fully converted and equipped to rehabilitate over 200 turtles annually. The sanctuary is located on one acre of natural woodlands and manmade ponds and is home to our permanently disabled residents. 

We are also proud members and supporters of 22 national charities.

Name: Four Mile Run Conservatory Foundation
Description: The Four Mile Run Conservatory Foundation was established in 2016 to formalize what had been a decade or more of community efforts to improve Four Mile Run Park. Our mission is to promote nature, culture, and community at lower Four Mile Run through restoration, advocacy, recreation, and education. We serve a diverse community of residents who live, work, study, or play within two miles of the tidal portion of Four Mile Run and its associated parklands in Arlington and Alexandria, Virginia. We are working to ensure the natural amenities of this urban ecosystem are protected and nurtured, and to amplify the mutually-reinforcing cycle of community stewardship of natural resources and human health, well-being, and enjoyment in the city. We envision lower Four Mile Run as a cherished community resource and a model for natural lands stewardship in an urban setting.

Name: Capital RC&D
Description: The Capital Resource Conservation and Development (RC&D) Area Council is a locally led nonprofit that creates partnerships of citizens, businesses, agencies, and organizations in its seven county area to enhance the quality of life for those who live and work here. Serving South Central Pennsylvania in Adams, Cumberland, Dauphin, Franklin, Lancaster, Lebanon, and York counties, Capital RC&D promotes the wise use and conservation of our natural and community resources.

Name: Pasa-Napa
Description: Pasa-Napa (meaning: साथीसगँ ) is a Nepali Heritage Games creates educational products based on Nepal's cultural heritage and architecture.

Name: La Sorgue et Nous
Description: La Sorgue et Nous a été créé pour aider à la préservation de la flore et de la faune de la Sorgue. Name: Nsombou Abalghe-Dzal Association (NADA)
Description:
Notre Mission #
La gouvernance et la gestion durable et équitable des ressources naturelles établies et maintenues par les Peuples Autochtones et les communautés locales gabonaises elles-mêmes de leurs terres ancestrales et leur diversité bioculturelle.

Notre Vision #

Name: SouSA Consortium
Description:
With fewer than 500 individuals left in the country, the humpback dolphin is South Africa's most endangered dolphin species. We want to do something about it!

The SouSA consortium is an intergroup collaboration on scientific research into Indian Ocean humpback dolphins in South Africa.

Through data collection and sharing, we aim to assess in detail the conservation status of humpback dolphins along our coastlines, and generate vital information for accurate conservation management.

Name: Conservation Tax Credit Transfer
Description:
CONSERVATION TAX CREDIT TRANSFER, LLC
Conservation Tax Credit Transfer, LLC (CTCT) was founded in 2000 after Carl and Karen Spina placed a conservation easement on their ranch and qualified for a conservation easement tax credit that year. They became very familiar with the conservation easement donation process and the credit transfer process through their donation and subsequent sale of their tax credit. They sold most of their credit in 2001 and realized that many other landowners could benefit from selling their credits as well.

We are very involved in state and national policy and conservation efforts. We are members of the Land Trust Alliance, our national conservation organization. We maintain strong relationships with state officials at the Division of Conservation and the Department of Revenue. 

CTCT makes annual financial and in-kind contributions to the Colorado Coalition of Land Trusts, land trusts and to landowners in the form of grants to help with the costs associated with making a donation.

Name: PermaWaldGarten gUG
Description:
We are a dedicated team committed to sustainable development and environmental protection. By combining specialist knowledge and practical experience, we strive to develop effective and innovative solutions to the challenges of our time.
Our projects range from restoring natural habitats to promoting biodiversity and sustainable agriculture.
Our goal is to leave a positive impact on the environment while raising awareness and responsibility within the community.
We believe in the power of collaboration and that each individual can make a difference.

Name: Quahog Bay Conservancy
Description: The mission of Quahog Bay Conservancy is to revitalize the ecosystem of Quahog Bay to a robust and resilient state for all communities that depend on it. Through sustainable aquaculture, ecosystem monitoring, and community education, we aim to conserve natural habitat, protect native wildlife, foster environmental stewards, and support Maine’s working waterfront.

Name: ITGA
Description: O Instituto Técnico de Gestão Ambiental (ITGA) oferece assessoria técnica e administrativa em sustentabilidade para a conservação e recuperação do meio ambiente por meio de projetos e serviços sustentáveis para poder público e setor privado. Dentre os serviços, temos: plano de gerenciamento de resíduos, restauração e conservação de florestas, aquisição de licenças ambientais, plano de educação ambiental escolar, implementação de energias renováveis, entre outros.

Name: All Florida
Description: An organization designed to connect people to a place and a purpose.

Name: Pachaland
Description: We foster entrepreneurship around land regeneration.

Name: Gorilla Conservation Coffee Australia
Description:
Gorilla Conservation Coffee is a social enterprise that was launched after Dr. Gladys Kalema-Zikusoka visited farmers living adjacent to Bwindi Impenetrable Forest. Here she learned that the farmers were not being given a fair price for their coffee and were struggling hard to survive, forcing them to use the national park to meet their basic family needs for food and fuel wood

Gorilla Conservation Coffee was created through a partnership between Conservation Through Public Health and World Wide Fund for Nature Switzerland. Gorilla Conservation Coffee pays a premium of $0.50 per kilo above the market price to coffee farmers living next door to the gorillas around Bwindi Impenetrable National Park. Gorilla Conservation Coffee further supports the farmers through training in sustainable coffee farming and processing. This helps to improve the coffee quality and increase production yield. Supporting local farmers helps to protect the critically endangered gorillas and their fragile habitat.

The coffee is 100% premium Arabica that is selectively harvested for only red ripe cherries, hand picked, wet processed and dried under shade. Our coffee is tested for quality parameters at every level. The coffee is roasted medium and packed to the highest quality standards. Each cup has a unique aroma with hints of caramel, butter notes and almond, with a citrus taste and a sweet finish. Our first blend is named after Kanyonyi, the lead silverback of the Mubare gorilla group located in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, Uganda.

Gorilla Conservation Coffee is the only Ugandan coffee expressly created to help conserve the mountain gorillas by directly supporting farmers living around the gorillas’ habitat.

Name: fanya association
Description:
Mouvement des jeunes congolais engagés dans la protection de l’environnement en menant des actions concrètes. 
contact@fanya.org

Name: Georgia Trout Unlimited
Description: Welcome to the Georgia Council of Trout Unlimited, part of a national network of grassroots volunteers dedicated to trout and salmon conservation. Georgia TU is devoted to conserving, protecting and restoring Georgia’s trout fisheries. All anglers and those interested in protecting our waters are welcome.

Name: ALIEM Vigil
Description: ALIEM Vigil is a European programme co-financed by the Interreg Italy - France Maritime programme, which aims to create and lead a cross-border network for the management of invasive alien species (IAS) of plants, insects and marine species in the Mediterranean.

Name: Coral Sense Bali
Description:
At Coral Sense, we’re on a mission to restore and protect coral ecosystems in Bali. In partnership with Livingseas Asia, we manage two key sites: the Coral Sense Coral Garden near Amed, and the Livingseas Coral Restoration Site in Padangbai, the largest of its kind in Bali.

We’re creating a lasting impact by combining hands-on coral restoration with scientific research, focusing on the vital relationship between corals and seagrass meadows. Our work supports marine biodiversity and offers unique opportunities for volunteer engagement, making it possible for individuals to contribute to real-world conservation efforts.

By fostering collaboration and sharing knowledge, we aim to inspire a global community of marine conservationists, divers, and researchers to help protect our oceans for future generations.

Name: BlueForest NFT
Description:
We at BlueForest are excited to introduce our impact-driven BlueForest Utility NFTs, which allow individuals to actively support the conservation of endangered coastal rainforests while earning rewards and passive income.

As a BlueForest Warrior, you will own a unique parcel of endangered rainforest with specific geo coordinates, and will be rewarded for your conservation efforts. This can include receiving passive income from the sales of Blue Carbon Credits or renting out your lodge ‘timeshare’. You can also earn rewards for actively participating in the BlueForest DAO, promoting our NFTs on social media, and playing ‘earn to play’ and ‘earn to learn’ games/apps within the BlueForest Metaverse.

In addition to the financial benefits, you will also be making a positive impact on the world by protecting these vital ecosystems. The focus in the NFT market is shifting away from digital art, memes, and paintings, to real-world assets that offer real-world utility and impact. These assets, such as rainforest conservation land, give you the opportunity to make a positive impact on the world and generate potential returns.

We are launching the Genesis Drop NFTs, which will help protect the first 297 hectares of a 1,270 hectare Conservation Reserve located on the Caribbean Coast of Panama. A total of 9,162 NFTs will be available for minting on a specific date and time, and priority access will be given to the most active members of our community through "ticketing" and "whitelisting".

At BlueForest, we believe that traditional conservation strategies have failed to protect these fragile coastal ecosystems due to a range of issues, including bureaucracy, mismanagement of funds, and a lack of transparency. Our goal is to create an effective, transparent and scalable model that makes a real positive impact in protecting endangered coastal rainforests and wetlands.

Join us in protecting the Cradle of Life and become a BlueForest Warrior today.

Name: Projeto Botos da Barra
Description: Atuamos para salvaguardar a pesca cooperativa entre botos-de-Lahille e pescadores artesanais de tarrafa no Litoral Norte do Rio Grande do Sul

Name: CAMHera
Description: Foundation of Conservation of Material and Art Heritage is a not for profit organization registered under Section 8 of Companies Act 2013 in India. The company is formed by Dr. Maninder Singh Gill and Sreekumar Menon.

Name: Festival del Jaguar
Description:
El Festival del Jaguar es un encuentro comunitario y autogestionado que une arte, ciencia y saberes ancestrales para celebrar al jaguar y resaltar su papel como guardián de la Amazonía. Durante varios días, el festival ofrece actividades culturales, diálogos con expertos y experiencias inmersivas que inspiran a cuidar esta especie emblemática y su hábitat.

La iniciativa también apoya a los pueblos indígenas que conviven con el jaguar y lo protegen desde tiempos ancestrales, fortaleciendo la defensa de sus territorios y de la riqueza natural que resguarda la selva.

Name: Bailies of Bennachie
Description:
The Bailies of Bennachie is a voluntary conservation society whose objectives are to encourage and stimulate the public’s interest of Bennachie.
The Bailies of Bennachie were founded in 1973 by Dr Danny G Gordon (with help also from James R MacKay, Algy Watson, Dr James Gill, Helen Fraser, Rev James Wood and more) from a mere 40 enthusiasts initially, now nearly 3000 members.
Membership is now for life - once a Bailie - always a Bailie.

Name: Local Earth Club
Description:
We are an eco-research and education group that blends academic-level fieldwork with grassroots conservation. By combining citizen science with rigorous academic standards, we aim to shift the locus of knowledge production toward coastal communities and frontline youth—those who live the science every day. 

We fund this vision through sustainable eco-tourism: research-based trips to our sites for individuals eager to learn directly from our partner communities. Every visit supports local conservation work, youth education, and the long-term sustainability of our programs.

Name: Magnificent Missouri
Description:
Magnificent Missouri began in 2012 as two friends — both farmers who love the countryside — discussed new ways to draw attention to Missouri conservation issues. Dan Burkhardt (Bethlehem Valley Farm & Vineyards) and John McPheeters (Bowood Farms & Garden Center) realized that the best way to entice Missourians to focus on the rural landscape around them was through a conservation celebration featuring amazing local food prepared by award-winning chefs.

We build on the work of the Katy Land Trust, which was created in 2010, after Dan and Connie put their land in a conservation easement to protect and conserve their land in Warren County. The conservation work that followed developed organically, as the Burkhardts worked to protect the land, prevent invasive species, and help others in the region access the same opportunities. We continue to work with Katy Land Trust as a sister organization to conserve properties. Today, the work of Katy Land Trust is incorporated into the mission of Magnificent Missouri.

Name: fhink.ai
Description:
Seek evolution in the questions the we ask about farming and youth.

Improve resilience of farms and advance consciousness during youth.

Make farmers relish farming. Augment the joys and possibilities of youth.

Name: Friends of Otter Rock Marine Reserve
Description:
Friends of Otter Rock Marine Reserve is a non-profit organization based on the central Oregon coast. Our mission is the protection and preservation of rocky shore intertidal life, nesting and migrating sea birds, and marine mammals that inhabit or benefit from Otter Rock Marine Reserve. 

Otter Rock Marine Reserve is one of 5 areas in the ocean along the Oregon Coast that are dedicated to conservation and scientific research.  In the reserves, all ocean development and removal of marine life is prohibited.

Management and scientific monitoring of the sites is overseen by the Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife (ODFW). Friends of Otter Rock Marine Reserve collaborates with ODFW and a large number of other conservation and stakeholder organizations to deliver public educational, community science, and outreach programs.

Name: ASSOCIATION POUR LA PROMOTION ET L'EVEIL DE LA FEMME/APEF-RDC
Description:
L'Association pour la promotion et l'éveil de la Femme APEF est une organisation non gouvernemental et non discriminatoire qui a vu le jour en République Démocratique du Congo dans la ville de Kalemie chef-lieu de la province de la province du TANGANYIKA, territoire de Kalemie.

L'ONG-APEF-RDC a son siege à kalemie, chef-lieu de la province de TANGANYIKA au No 22, Avenu Hopital II, au Quartier kataki II.

L'APEF a un personnel qualifié, et capable d'intervenir dans différents projets selon que l'arbre à problème nous impose la voie à suivre pour rapprocher la situation vécue par les vulnérables à la situation idéale envisagée. APEF-RDC a exécuté et exécute divers projets dans les secteurs: 
            - Protection et santé
            - Développement et pacifications
            - Education 
            - Sécurité alimentaire
            - Environnement

Name: Lobster Aquatic
Description: At Lobster Aquatics, we specialise in building affordable and sustainable lobster hatcheries for harbours, community groups, and research institutions across Europe. With years of hands-on experience managing successful hatcheries, we offer expert consultancy on all aspects of hatchery setup, from system design and grant writing to ongoing operational support. Whether you're starting from scratch or improving an existing facility, our tailored solutions ensure the success of your hatchery. Our mission is to support sustainable fishing practices, provide educational resources, and create impactful marine conservation initiatives that benefit local communities and marine ecosystems.

Name: Association Roannaise de Protection de la Nature
Description:
L’ARPN œuvre depuis 35 ans pour faire connaître la richesse des milieux naturels et pour protéger les
écosystèmes locaux. Pour cela, l’ARPN s’est donné plusieurs missions :
- Connaître son environnement en réalisant des études, suivis et actions naturalises sur la
faune et la flore et en alimentant des bases de données.
- Sensibiliser petits et grands de façon ludique autour d’ateliers, formations ou sorties nature.
Elle intervient à tous les niveaux scolaires et pour tout public sur des thèmes en lien
l’environnement : biodiversité, zone humide, écosystème forestier, eau, climat, déchet,
agriculture…
-Participer à des instances publiques pour plaider la cause de la nature et l’environnement
auprès des décideurs

Name: MAZINGIRA BORA "EQUUS MAPPARUM Team"
Description: "MAZINGIRA BORA s'engage à promouvoir un environnement sain en travaillant pour réduire la pollution de l'air, améliorer la qualité de l'eau et préserver la biodiversité, pour le bien-être actuel et futur de notre société."

Name: Conservation Confluence
Description:
Confluence helps people help nature in Latin America by bringing together conservationists, ideas, and resources to save essential ecosystems and keep watersheds working.

Confluence ayuda a las ONGs a proteger la naturaleza en América Latina reuniendo conservacionistas, ideas y recursos para salvar ecosistemas esenciales y mantener cuencas saludables.

Name: STA Logger
Description:
Automatic and Efficient Data Logging of Weed Management Activities.
The STA logger is an automated GPS and data logging attachment designed to integrate with your existing weed control tools.

Name: MEGUNTICOOK WATERSHED ASSOCIATION
Description: The mission of the Megunticook Watershed Association is to protect, preserve, and promote the resiliency of the natural resources of the watershed to ensure a healthy ecosystem for the benefit and safe enjoyment of all

Name: Arbres Connectés
Description:
Arbres Connectés est un système unique, opérationnel et non-intrusif. Il permet de connaitre, en permanence et à distance, la vitalité et la statique des arbres 

 Surveiller en permanence la santé de vos arbres permet de :
1) Faire des diagnostiques et expertises
1) Suivre la vitalité et la statique
3) Prédire la santé
4) Anticiper les soins 
5) Augmenter l’espérance de vie
6) Accroître la surface de canopée
7) Lutter contre les îlots de chaleur
8) Optimiser et rationaliser les tâches
9) Economiser des ressources
10) Faciliter la prise de décision 
11) Préserver l’environnement 

Notre système non-intrusive répond aux standards éthiques de respect des arbres et représente un outil intelligent permettant de développer concrètement sa stratégie environnementale et climatique 

Il offre plusieurs opportunités :

 Réduire : garder en permanence un œil sur la vitalité et la statique de ses arbres est un moyen de s’assurer de leur bon développement. En grandissant, les arbres captent du CO2, ils participent ainsi à l’effort de réduction d’émission de gaz à effet de serre (GES).

 S’adapter : face à l’augmentation des facteurs de stress (manque d’eau, sècheresse, tempête, etc.) due au changement climatique, le système des Arbres connectés est un bon moyen de mieux comprendre pour mieux s’adapter. Il permet non seulement de protéger la population en minimisant les risques de chute, mais il permet également de renforcer la protection de l’arbre en lui-même.

 Documenter : grâce à la multitude de données récoltées par le capteur, vous avez accès à des données inédites. Il est possible de mieux déchiffrer et documenter les changements climatiques. C’est un moyen facile et efficace pour analyser l’impact de l’évolution de notre climat sur le territoire. Ces analyses peuvent ensuite servir à la sensibilité et informer la population.

Name: Community Coop CIC
Description:
A not-for-profit organisation dedicated to working in partnership with schools, care homes and community organisations. We provide on-site access to bespoke Poultry projects to support the conservation of rare breed chickens, ducks and geese. Whether you’re a nursery who’d like to run an egg hatching project for your little learners or a residential home looking for a hen house that your residents can spend time in, we can help plan and deliver the perfect project. 

With access to funding to support stretched budgets, we can work with you to provide the best impact for your community. 

We’ll help you to choose breeds that will suit your aims, understand how much involvement you’d like to have in ongoing care and establish a robust plan for the management of your project so that you have nothing to worry about. 

With projects starting at £150 with ‘ready to hatch’ eggs for those short of time or looking for shorter projects to long term projects with permanent on-site chicken runs or duck ponds, we can help whatever your aims. 

Preserving rare breed poultry is important in future food security and to help to make sure that birds are diverse and resilient to disease. 

Poultry projects offer excellent opportunities to embed STEM and contextualise academic ideas, bringing science to life. 

It’s more important than ever to introduce young people to farming careers, as progression planning in agriculture becomes more and more of an issue.

Sustainable and ethical livestock care is achievable, affordable and rewarding for everyone involved, as well as offering opportunities for learning around environment issues and food technology.

Chickens can be excellent company and provide opportunities for enrichment and companionship. 

Free eggs for your stakeholders and local community. 

Send us a message to start a conversation about conservation or to book your hatching experience - now taking bookings for spring 2023.

Name: SOuL Sanctuary Strategies
Description: formerly The Angels

Name: CHOSE GROUP INTERNATIONAL
Description: Join us in our mission to conserve land, promote outreach, and engage in restoration efforts. Climate change poses a significant threat to some of the Earth's most magnificent landscapes, and it is our objective to safeguard these areas, ensuring they remain pristine for future generations. However, we recognise that achieving these goals requires collective action and support.

Name: Coalition Marocaine pour la Justice Climatique
Fundación Uraku
Description:
URAKU significa para los Arhuacos, uno de los cuatro pueblos que habitan el sistema montañoso litoral más alto del planeta tierra; la Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta, el lugar donde la familia convive, en armonía y bienestar – la casa, el hogar.

 Somos una organización no gubernamental (ONG) cuyo objetivo principal es promover la protección y conservación del patrimonio ambiental y la búsqueda de alternativas para el mejoramiento de la convivencia con el entorno y  así comprender que somos un componente más de un todo y no el centro de este.

Name: Captain Life
Description:
L’association Captain Life est acteur de la protection de l’environnement en agissant concrètement, sur le terrain, pour réparer ce qui peut l’être, protéger ce qui doit l’être, et sensibiliser ceux qui construiront le monde de demain.

 Responsables par nature
Nous sommes une bande de copains, amoureux de la nature qui a décidé de passer à l’action pour protéger l’environnement ! 

 Notre truc ? 
Ramasser les déchets, entretenir les forêts oubliées et planter des arbres dans une ambiance conviviale qui nous caractérise. 

 Notre objectif ? Rendre la planète un peu plus verte, un sac-poubelle à la main. 

Parce que nous ne voulons pas oublier que l’humain fait partie de la nature!

Name: Loudoun Valley Raptor Center, Inc.
Description: Our mission is advancing raptor conservation and protection through rehabilitation, education, and research, with the goal of releasing native birds of prey back to their natural habitat, healthy and thriving.

Name: East Pacific Corridor Alliance Foundation
Description: EPCA is a newly formed Foundation to promote oceanic conservation in the Eastern Pacific Corridor between Galapagos, Cocos Island and the Mainland.  EPCA scientists work closely together with a broad network of researchers, photographers, museums and authors to educate the public about the importance of managing this nutrient rich region wisely. Key issues include wasteful, aggressive shark fishing for shark fin soup and other illegal activities that harm the natural balance. “Fishes of the Galapagos Island” (originally published by Stanford University Press, Grove and Lavenburg) will also be upgraded and republished with the assistance of EPCA. Museum installations for the Museum of Biodiversity in Panama City are also currently being planned. Go to EPCAFoundation.org to learn more and to dialogue with our scientists.  We welcome your thoughts and ideas, and would be pleased to showcase your photography of the region.

Name: Flora, Fauna, & Fungi Ecological Resource Network
Description:
Supporting Conservation by Promoting Synergy between Individuals, Communities, and Organizations

Our work addresses the need for greater connection and communication in conservation.

We do that by promoting information equity which connects individuals with the social and physical resources to facilitate communication and knowledge.

We also connect ecological knowledge-bearers across different specialties so they can collaborate on mutual goals.

These often come together to engage people with the natural world as we bring culture and nature together into events and eco-experiences across New England.

Name: Medway Valley Countryside Partnership
Description:
OUR AIMS
Create, Sustain and Enhance Kent’s Landscape & its wildlife
Develop, Manage and Support community-based environmental initiatives
Develop, and Promote life long outdoor learning
Encourage and Enhance greater access in the outdoors and the corresponding health benefits.
Since 1988 we deliver these aims through many practical conservation projects throughout the Medway catchment and in the boroughs of Maidstone and Tonbridge & Malling.

OUR PROJECTS
We currently have dozens of active conservation projects that are being delivered.

We work with many partners in the public, private and third sectors. We offer opportunities to volunteer, train and learn. 

WORKING WITH US
We carry out a huge range of projects, activities, events and community engagement. We work with communities and local authorities, directly with landowners, schools, colleges and myriad specialist conservation groups in Kent. We are able develop and deliver project opportunities in a number of ways.

PROJECT IDEAS
We help define and develop project ideas, aiding group development, site management planning, sourcing grants and lending practical support.

EXTERNAL FUNDING
We are active in seeking external funding for most of the work that we undertake, and help communities and volunteers to find grant money as and when they need it. We advise and work in partnership with like minded organisations.

PROVIDING OPPORTUNITIES FOR WILDLIFE 
Enabling and carrying out conservation management, restoration and replacement of key habitats, including chalk grassland, ancient woodland, hedgerows, ponds and verges.

CONSERVING CHARACTER
Enabling work to conserve traditional landscape features that comprise the North Downs and low weald landscapes, such as hedgerows, traditional orchards, ponds, copses and shaws.

INTERPRETATION AND EDUCATION
We run dozens of events for schools, the wider public and special interest groups.

Name: Clean Earth Initiative
Description: Our mission is to educate and engage our communities and elected officials on the importance of preserving our local habitats for future generations.

Name: Proelo de Serviços e Comércio
ARK Expeditions
Description:
ARK Expeditions is a Wildlife tour company focused on providing ethical and authentic experiences to our clients. 
We work with an amazing network of different NGOs and partner organisations around the world. 
There are so many incredible species out there and all we want to do is give you the opportunity to observe them in their natural habitats and learn more about these fascinating creatures from our experts.
We work closely with various ongoing projects using our funds to help support amazing conservation projects around the world, helping our partners where we can.

We hope to see you on one of our trips very soon!

Name: Worcester Civic Society
Description:
Worcester Civic Society recognises that our city’s rich historical legacy is a defining asset—one that must be protected, enhanced, and celebrated. As Worcester grows, it must do so with care, vision, and strong leadership. Without this, we risk becoming a ‘nearly’ city or simply a suburban extension of a wider West Midlands sprawl. We believe that progress should never come at the cost of character.

Our aim is to champion Worcester’s unique identity by ensuring that change is thoughtfully managed and sustainable. We are committed to promoting civic pride, supporting heritage-led regeneration, and encouraging debate about the future of the city’s built and natural environment. These conversations must shape a shared vision—one embraced by leaders and residents alike.

As a civic society, we strive to:
	•	Be a strong voice for the environmental and architectural quality of Worcester.
	•	Promote pride in the city’s heritage and identity.
	•	Contribute to Worcester’s continued growth as a vibrant Cathedral and University City.
	•	Ensure that all new developments enhance, not erode, the city’s distinctiveness.

We envision a city where everyone can live life to the full, with high-quality, affordable housing and inclusive spaces that serve all ages. We support the development of safe, accessible public places, cultural venues, and green infrastructure, including walking and cycling routes. Worcester must offer more than just infrastructure—it must foster belonging, engagement, and opportunity.

We believe in strengthening local democracy and involving the public in shaping the future. By building partnerships and encouraging active participation, we can ensure Worcester grows with purpose and pride—protecting what makes it special while embracing its potential.

Name: Friends of the River Medway (FoRM)
Description:
The Friends of the River Medway aim to restore the health and vitality of the River Medway, focusing first on our local catchment area: the Upper Medway. The group’s activities are part of a wider movement to transform humanity’s relationship to the world’s precious rivers and the wildlife that lives within them.

Our Vision

We believe that all rivers should be clean, healthy, nature-rich and flowing naturally. They should be thriving ecosystems that provide a natural habitat for a diversity of life, whilst offering an accessible space for people to connect with nature. We want them to be drinkable, as they were for our ancestors and for most of the planet’s history.

Through uniting practical and legal action with the sacred, we strive to actively regenerate the Upper Medway, creating projects that serve the River, her inhabitants and our local community. Our dream is also to serve as a successful template that inspires and reaches other communities who want to improve the health of rivers.

Name: Soluções Higienização e Limpeza
Description:
A Soluções Higienização e Limpeza foi fundada em 2009 com a missão de fornecer soluções de limpeza industrial e institucional de alta qualidade para empresas. Nossa abordagem diferenciada envolve um atendimento especializado, conduzido por profissionais com profundo conhecimento no setor.

Ao longo dos anos, estabelecemos parcerias sólidas com nossos clientes, resultando em uma troca de informações que impulsiona novas alternativas para o mercado. Essa parceria nos permite atender empresas com os mais altos padrões de controle de qualidade, garantindo uma higienização rigorosa e essencial para os resultados finais dos produtos.

Nossa equipe de profissionais altamente qualificados é treinada para lidar com qualquer tipo de serviço, garantindo a aplicação de métodos e procedimentos que asseguram um padrão consistente de qualidade. Estamos comprometidos em oferecer soluções técnicas de excelência para atender às necessidades exclusivas de cada cliente.

Name: Sea of Clouds
Description: We are a nonprofit preservation practice interested in the stories of place.

Name: Esmeralda Hope
Description:
Esmeralda Hope es un proyecto socioambiental que nace con un propósito claro: reconectar al ser humano con la naturaleza. Creemos que solo a través de la comprensión profunda de nuestro entorno podemos aprender a valorarlo, protegerlo y convivir en equilibrio con él.

Trabajamos en iniciativas que integran educación ambiental, regeneración ecológica y conciencia social, promoviendo una nueva forma de habitar el mundo: más empática, responsable y sostenible.

Name: Southwestern Resource Conservation & Development
Description:
Resource Conservation and Development Councils are authorized by the United States Congress and then organized as 501c(3) organizations so that local citizens can develop and carry out a proactive plan for social, economic, and environmental betterment of their own communities. Southwestern NC RC&D Council is 1 of 10 councils in North Carolina and 1 of 375 in the United States. Our RC&D Council works closely with local governments, but is a stand alone non-profit organization.

Currently we have a staff of eleven including an Executive Director, Administrator and program support specialists helping USDA deliver Farm Bill Programs. Through grants and service agreements, we have a consultant team that works on many agricultural, forestry and natural resource projects. In addition, our capacity is expanded greatly by partnering with County and State agencies and other non-profit organizations. We are active members of the North Carolina Association of RC&D Councils, the Southeastern Association of RC&D Councils, and hold a Director’s position on the National Association of RC&D Councils.

Name: The Green Alliance RV University
Description:
The Green Alliance is fully student-led and student-driven.
We’re a collective of changemakers who believe that waiting on the world to change is no longer an option. As students, we recognize our unique power — and responsibility — to act. From awareness drives to hands-on sustainability initiatives, everything we do is imagined and executed by students who care deeply about our planet. At R.V. University, we’re building more than a greener campus — we’re building a culture of action, accountability, and hope.

Name: Chippewa Luce Mackinac Conservation District
Description: The Chippewa Luce Mackinac Conservation District (CLMCD) is local unit of State government with a mandate to assist private landowners with their conservation needs. CLMCD covers all of Chippewa, Luce, and Mackinac Counties in Michigan's Eastern Upper Peninsula. CLMCD is the largest Conservation District in the State by landmass and has the shorelines of 3 of the Great Lakes (Superior, Huron, Michigan) as well as the St. Mary's River which also acts as the international Boarder with Ontario, Canada. CLMCD provides natural resources conservation technical assistance, programs, education, and services to all landowners but focuses their efforts in several key areas including: Agriculture, Forestry, Water Stewardship, Invasive Species, Habitat, Recreation, and Education/Outreach.

Name: Project Reuse
Description: We partner with food outlets in Maui to implement practical reuse solutions for replacing disposable foodware.

Name: Cartão Verde Floresta em Pé
Description:
Cartão Verde – Floresta em Pé
Preservação Ambiental | Crowdfunding Verde

O Cartão Verde – Floresta em Pé é uma iniciativa de preservação ambiental que conecta pessoas, empresas e proprietários rurais na missão de manter nossas florestas em pé.

Através de um modelo inovador de adoção de árvores, reunimos recursos para proteger áreas de mata nativa, implementar planos de conservação, monitoramento via satélite, inventário florestal e ações de prevenção contra degradação.

Produtos e serviços:

- Adoção de árvores em áreas preservadas
- Projetos corporativos de compensação e responsabilidade socioambiental
- Monitoramento e inventário florestal com tecnologias como LIDAR e imagens de satélite
- Programas de educação e conscientização ambiental
- Apoio a proprietários rurais na preservação de florestas

Nossa missão é incentivar indivíduos e empresas a se engajarem na proteção do meio ambiente, oferecendo uma maneira acessível e transparente de contribuir para a preservação de florestas através da adoção de árvores. Buscamos criar um impacto positivo duradouro, promovendo a conscientização e ações concretas para enfrentar os desafios das mudanças climáticas e da qualidade do ar que respiramos.

Name: Angel Force of Africa
Description:
Angel Force of Africa is a web3 based NFT brand consisting of 6000 digitally generated NFTs. 

All base characters and traits are hand-drawn-sketched by the founder using Procreate. No two of the 6000 are the same.

Name: Southern Wisconsin Land Conservancy
Description: Protecting wild and working lands in southwest Wisconsin

Name: Daan Jacobs natuur | management | advies
Description:
Met Daan Jacobs natuur | management | advies werk ik als zelfstandig (project-, omgevings- en interim)manager en adviseur aan het verbeteren en beschermen van natuur en biodiversiteit in het landelijk gebied én aan het duurzaam samenleven met in het wild levende dieren.

Met mijn ervaring, kennis van natuur én breed netwerk binnen Nederland help ik (semi-)overheden en natuurorganisaties bij het behalen van hun natuuropgaven.

Name: HERS Lab
Description:
What we do:
Applied Ecological Research and Monitoring
Native Plant Materials Supply and Restoration
Student and Early Career Professional Mentoring
Synthesis and Translation of Conservation Science

We study how populations of plants and animals respond to changing environmental conditions. We contribute to the native seed and plant supply for sagebrush steppe restoration. The lab established long-term monitoring programs focused on American pika and bat species in the Pacific Northwest and collaborates with National Park Service, US Forest Service, and other partners on studies of rare plants and at-risk communities.

Through our long-term monitoring programs, we collect baseline data necessary to understand impacts of changing environmental conditions on plants, wildlife and ecosystems. By working with research and management partners across the Pacific Northwest, we can ensure that comprehensive, high-quality data are used to identify changes in our landscape. Our research models provide understanding of local impacts and landscape-level ecological trends.

Habitat connectivity across fragmented landscapes is critical for effectiveness of conservation strategies employed in protected areas, such as national parks. We use emerging technologies like acoustic monitoring techniques and laser altimetry to study species distributions at landscape scales. The Lab also collaborates with vineyard certification groups to identify on-farm biodiversity standards that can support habitat connectivity across working landscapes.

Research and monitoring programs co-produced by researchers and resource managers can result in more effective and efficient conservation outcomes. Our National Park Service Park Studies Unit works with management partners to address questions of rare plant persistence, invasive plant management, ecological resilience, resistance to invasion, responses to climate change and bat species responses to land use and disease threats.

Name: Honey Bee Watch
Description: Honey Bee Watch is a global coalition of bee scientists, bee experts, beekeepers, and concerned citizen scientists, tasked with better understanding the biological, behavioral, and environmental factors that bolster survivorship of free-living honey bee colonies. The aim is to discover and apply nature-based solutions to lower colony mortality rates, increase beekeeping's sustainability, and ultimately protect threatened populations.
