Welcome to a place where we envision a greener, more sustainable world. As a pioneer in sustainable landscape solutions, our mission stretches far beyond delivering top-tier artificial grass. We're here to transform the way we interact with our environment and bring about a future that is conscious of water scarcity.

At the heart of our mission is the belief that everyone, regardless of their project size or scope, should have access to green living. From residential homes aiming to minimize water use, to commercial and public spaces seeking to balance aesthetics with sustainability, our job is to make it possible. We're motivated by the notion that every small step towards water conservation matters.

Our team isn't just a group of experts, but a community of passionate individuals dedicated to making a real-world difference. Every landscape we help create is a testament to this commitment, a blend of aesthetics, functionality, and care for the environment.

Join us. With each project, you're not just choosing a service, but becoming part of a collective endeavor to preserve our planet's resources. Together, let's shape the landscapes of the future.

Our commitment goes beyond environmental conservation, as we firmly believe that education is the key to empowering communities and positively transforming our surroundings. Description: The Gunnison Conservation District is among more than 3,000 districts in the United States. The GCD is the local unit of government through which the Natural Resource Conservation Service (NRCS) administers technical assistance and facilitates cost-share programs to local ranchers and landowners. The GCD exists for the sole purpose of promoting and facilitating soil, water, and wildlife conservation, management, and improvement practices on private land in the Gunnison Basin area.

Name: Craw Ctrl
Description:

 We combine engineering, ecology, and technology to design smart, eco-conscious traps and data-driven monitoring systems that support sustainable crayfish control and native species conservation.

 Our work spans AI-based detection, IoT-enabled field deployment, and biological insight—all aimed at developing scalable, ethical solutions for invasive species management.

Based at the University of Sheffield | Collaborating across science, engineering, and environmental fields

 Interested in conservation tech, sustainability, or student innovation? Connect with us!

Name: WA Better by Nature
Description:
WA Better by Nature is a growing alliance of Traditional Owner groups, conservation organisations, community not-for-profits and nature advocates from across the state, working together to make sure WA’s unique nature is looked after for future generations.

We believe WA is made better by nature, and that we could do better for nature. By working in partnership — we’re helping shape practical and proven measures that support the long-term protection of our nature like Indigenous-led conservation, more national and conservation parks, public-private partnerships and investing in private protected areas.

Together, with our supporters and partners, we’re making the case for a future where WA’s nature, culture and outdoor way of life are looked after for future generations.

Name: Instituto Altair Sales
Description: O Instituto Altair Sales surgiu da união e reflexão de um grupo de pessoas que acredita na pesquisa, no estudo, na educação e na sustentabilidade ambiental, social e econômica do Cerrado. E crê num mundo digno, baseado na justiça, na ética e na sustentabilidade. O objetivo maior do IAS é o resgate da dignidade da pessoa humana fundamentado nos princípios da ética, da solidariedade e do conhecimento. Conhecimento e cooperação capazes de trazer a força necessária para enfrentarmos as intensas mudanças deste século, principalmente nos chapadões centrais do Brasil. O nosso grupo de associados é formado por pessoas com habilidades e conhecimentos adquiridos dentro dos muros da academia e fora destes, na labuta diária de povos e populações pela busca do aprendizado da sobrevivência.

Name: We are Pollinator Pioneers CIC
Description:
Born from a common passion to connect communities with pollinators, across the land and sea, our vision is to support ecological systems, increase the pollinator footprint locally and champion ocean conservation to strengthen biodiversity. 

We benefit the community and wildlife through conservation and community programmes, teaching apiary’s, advocacy and collaborations. 

Pollinator Pioneers aim to inspire mind-set change with initiatives that value pollinators, our green and blue space, and the vital role of the humble bee in building environmental resilience.

We believe everyone can make a difference; anyone can become a pioneer: nature, empowerment, and collaboration should be accessible to all.

Name: Rocky Point Bird Observatory
Description: Rocky Point Bird Observatory seeks to influence and inform conservation and ecological management practises of migratory birds in western North America through monitoring, scientific research, and public education.

Name: North Sky Raptor Sanctuary
Description: North Sky Raptor Sanctuary is a rehabilitation center located in Grand Traverse County, Michigan, that is also dedicated to education, and increasing the overall public awareness of wildlife, ecology, and environmental issues as they relate to raptors.  The husband/wife duo operate the organization on private property, and have a passion for raptors, and ensuring their role in the wild, for the enjoyment and appreciation of future generations.

Name: Scottish Rainforest Education Centre
Description:
The aim of the Scottish Rainforest Centre (SRC) is to support all activities and projects which are working to protect and regenerate the Scottish temperate rainforest. The Centre is situated within the UNESCO Wester Ross Biosphere. Through courses, lecture’s and training programs at the Centre the aim will be to encourage learning, scientific discussion, research and debate on rainforest regeneration.

For more information check out our website: https://scottishrainforestcentre.org.uk/

Name: Tétras-lyre Alpes du sud
Description: Le projet Tétras-lyre Alpes du sud est un programme écocontribution porté par la fédération régionale des chasseurs de PACA et initié en 2022. Depuis, les objectifs sont d'améliorer la sensibilisation des professionnels de la montagne et du grand public à la présence du Tétras-lyre dans les Alpes du sud. En hiver des formations et des conférences sont organisées pour sensibiliser au dérangement hivernal, l'été la fédération intervient auprès des gestionnaires et éleveurs pour favoriser le pastoralisme extensif et les pratiques limitant le dérangement lors de la reproduction de l'oiseau.

Name: Pardos Conservation Charitable Trust
Description:
"Wilderness Meets Innovation, A Sustainable Wildlife Future"
Welcome to Pardos Conservation Charitable Trust, where our commitment to wildlife and the natural world is at the heart of everything we do.
Our mission is simple yet profound: to safeguard and protect our planet's precious biodiversity. We firmly believe that every species, from the smallest insects to the most majestic mammals, plays a vital role in the intricate web of life on Earth. Through dedicated conservation efforts, we strive to ensure that this rich tapestry endures for generations to come.
At Pardos Conservation, we envision a world where humans and wildlife coexist harmoniously, where nature's wonders are cherished, and where every ecosystem thrives.

Name: Centro Golfinho Rotador
Description:
Desde 1990, trabalhamos pela conservação dos golfinhos-rotadores e do arquipélago de Fernando de Noronha (PE), por meio de ações de pesquisa, sustentabilidade, educação ambiental e envolvimento comunitário.

O Projeto Golfinho Rotador é realizado pelo Centro Golfinho Rotador, com patrocínio da Petrobras, por meio do Programa Petrobras Socioambiental.

Name: National Champion Tree Program
Description: The National Champion Tree Program keeps the record of the largest trees in the United States through public engagement and education. It started in the 1940s at American Forests and, as of 2023, is housed at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Long Live Our Champion Trees!

Name: Ecology & Conservation ARU Society
Description: The Ecology and Conservation Society at ARU brings together students with a shared passion for animals, ecosystems, and the natural world. Rooted in the Animal and Environmental Biology community, we host talks, field trips, workshops, and outreach activities that explore everything from wildlife conservation to sustainability and ecological research. Whether you’re looking to connect, learn, enjoy a social night or take action for the planet, this is your space to grow—academically and environmentally.

Name: Parques Nacionales Chilenos
Description: Visita y explora cada uno de los bellos Parques Nacionales Chilenos. Encuentra guías para planificar y descubrir senderos, atractivos naturales y lugares imperdibles para que tu visita sea una experiencia inolvidable.

Name: The Tamarin Trust
Description:
The goal of Tamarin Trust is to support conservation efforts to protect threatened species of tamarins and marmosets, a wonderful group of primates found in South and Central America, from Panama down to southern Brazil. They include some of the most critically endangered primates on the planet.
As pressure has grown on the habitat of these beautiful monkeys, several have now become highly threatened and will not survive without conservation efforts to secure the remaining populations in the wild. This may include habitat protection and restoration, rescues, translocation and introductions. Constant survey work is needed to highlight populations in danger, and wherever these primates live, it is essential to work with local communities to protect them and their remaining habitat.
Through education and conservation, we aim to stabilise the population declines in the wild and promote the conservation of their ever decreasing habitats.

Name: Flowsurfer
Description:
Flowsurfer transformiert die dynamischen Muster des Surfens in ein revolutionäres Workbook, das deine kreativen Fähigkeiten entfesselt und dich auf den Weg zum stressfreien Erfolg führt.
Mit jedem Buch unterstützt du aktiv das Ocean Cleanup Project, während du auf eine Reise gehst, um Freiheit und Balance in deinem Alltag zu entdecken.

Wer betritt die FlowSurfer Welle?
FlowSurfer richtet sich an kreative Visionäre, dynamische Unternehmer und transformative Führungskräfte, die erkannt haben, dass ihr persönlicher Flow das Geheimnis ihres beruflichen und persönlichen Erfolgs ist. Es ist für jene, die stets die nächste Herausforderung suchen, diejenigen, die sich lebendig fühlen, wenn sie das scheinbar Unmögliche möglich machen.

Diese Plattform ist für diejenigen konzipiert, die bereit sind, die volle Verantwortung für ihr eigenes Wohlbefinden zu übernehmen, für die Harmonie von Körper, Geist und Seele. FlowSurfer spricht diejenigen an, die sich nicht mit dem Status Quo zufriedengeben, sondern ständig danach streben, ihre Grenzen zu erweitern und die beste Version von sich selbst zu werden.

FlowSurfer ist für diejenigen, die verstanden haben, dass der Weg zur Spitzenleistung nicht linear ist, sondern vielmehr eine Mischung aus Höhen und Tiefen, aus Herausforderungen und Erfolgen. Es ist für diejenigen, die bereit sind, sich auf die Wellen des Lebens zu stürzen, die Balance zu finden und mit voller Kraft durch das Wasser zu gleiten.

Bist du bereit, deinen persönlichen Flow zu entdecken und zu beherrschen?

Name: Promocarpathians
Description:
About Us

Promocarpathians is a non-governmental organization based in Ukraine, dedicated to the preservation and promotion of the Carpathian National Nature Park and the surrounding municipalities. Our mission is to safeguard the region’s rich biodiversity and foster sustainable economic development through partnerships with local and international allies. By supporting locally-led initiatives, we aim to position Yaremche and neighboring villages as premier destinations for ecotourism and sustainable economic growth, ensuring that our pristine natural resources will thrive for generations to come.

Our efforts unite local businesses, government, community members, and global stakeholders in a shared vision for sustainable development. Together, we are creating a future where economic prosperity and environmental stewardship go hand-in-hand.

 Our Principles

 Effective & Ethical Resource Management – We prioritize efficiency and integrity in our use of resources.
 Community Collaboration – We engage in responsible partnerships to create impact that resonates locally.
 Financial Sustainability – We pursue sound financial management and cultivate funding opportunities with partners who share our values.
 Transparency & Trust – We maintain accurate records and remain accountable to our partners and communities.

Join us as we work to protect the Carpathians and create a vibrant, sustainable future for this remarkable region!

Name: Eco Era Rainforest Conservation
Description:
We are the stewards of 7,500 acres of rainforest in one of the most biodiverse regions of Costa Rica. Our mission is to protect the rainforest's ecosystems by preventing poaching and deforestation and re-connecting people to nature. 
 
Eco Era wildlife refuge was founded in 1991 as a US based non-profit environmental and educational organization. This rainforest wildlife refuge is located in the Cerro Nara watershed of Costa Rica’s Central Pacific Coast.

Our lands have been studied extensively by international biologist because of our regions biodiversity, critical habitats, pure waters and endemic species. Our current global environmental crisis demands that our rainforests be protected to ensue a future for life on earth.
 
Eco Era was created to protect biodiversity and endangered species by ensuring that critical habitat is preserved in a pristine state and to educate people about the importance of protecting the earth to ensure a sustainable future for all.

We partner with organizations that promote education, wellness and mental health through connection to nature. Our connected programs are designed to bring participants into deeper connection to the earth and re-create a global culture that is aware of our individual connection to the natural world and the importance of conserving it.

Name: MARS Wildlife Rescue
Description: MARS Wildlife Rescue is a non-profit Wildlife Rescue Centre based out of Merville B.C.

Name: Desert Tortoise Preserve Committee
Description: The Desert Tortoise Preserve Committee is dedicated to the recovery and conservation of the Desert Tortoise (Gopherus agassizii) and other rare and endangered species inhabiting the Mojave and western Sonoran deserts.
