Description:
La société de mycologie de Bulle a été fondée en 1960 et a pour but l'étude et la connaissance des champignons.  Elle contribue à l'éducation du public en vue de sauvegarder la flore mycologique de nos forêts. 

Plongez dans le monde merveilleux des champignons !
Devenez membre de la société de mycologie de Bulle !
Participez à nos cours pour débutants ou avancés.
Organisez avec nous votre prochain évènement d'entreprise.

Name: Great North Bog
Description: The Great North Bog is a large restoration initiative covering over 90% of the upland peatland in the North of England. It is a £200m project and aims to restore nearly 7,000 square kilometres of upland over 20 years. It is a partnership between the North Pennines National Landscape team, Yorkshire Peat Partnership and the Moors for the Future Partnership. The area covers five national parks — the Peak District, Yorkshire Dales, North York Moors, Lake District and Northumberland.

Name: BLUE EDEN STEWARDSHIP ALLIANCE
Description:
BESA (Blue Eden Stewardship Alliance) pioneers a harmonious blend of development and conservation, championing innovative, scalable, and profitable marine solutions that support both economic growth and environmental restoration.

 For Developers, Planners, and Corporations:
BESA ensures sustainable coastal development through comprehensive marine species management. We survey, harvest, and relocate corals and mangroves pre-construction, nurturing them in land-based farms for resilience before replanting. This revitalizes ecosystems while delivering long-term ROI, proving that development and environmental stewardship can thrive together.

 For Recreational & Leisure Venues:
Elevate eco-tourism with BESA’s sustainable offerings. From PADI-certified reef restoration dives to boutique coral experiences and interactive nurseries, we help resorts attract eco-conscious, high-value guests. Our programs boost brand reputation, deepen guest engagement, and promote conservation—benefiting both business and the planet.

 For Schools & Institutions:
BESA’s K–12 marine education programs integrate coral farms and mangrove nurseries into classrooms. Aligned with STEAM curricula, students engage in hands-on restoration and real-world research with global experts, nurturing future leaders dedicated to sustainability and scientific discovery.

 For Governments & Policymakers:
BESA offers turnkey marine restoration solutions, simplifying policy implementation. Our advanced aquaculture technologies and private-sector partnerships support biodiversity, streamline regulatory compliance, and strengthen sustainable policy enforcement with science-based methodologies.

 For Everyone:
Coral reefs and mangroves underpin biodiversity, food security, climate regulation, and coastal defense. BESA’s mission is to protect these ecosystems, delivering measurable economic and ecological benefits.

Join us in building a future where progress restores, not destroys.

Name: Bongo Surveillance Project
Description: Our mission is to protect and conserve the critically endangered eastern or mountain bongo and its habitat, by working with local communities and stakeholders worldwide. We strive to raise awareness about the eastern or mountain bongo, a species on the brink of extinction, and to mobilize resources and support for their conservation. Through education and community engagement, we aim to empower local communities to become advocates for the protection of the bongo and its habitat.

Name: Quinn Wildlife Sanctuary
Description: Quinn Wildlife Sanctuary is dedicated to giving injured, orphaned, and vulnerable native animals a second chance at life. We will work to heal, protect, and safely return these species to their natural habitats, while also educating the public about the vital role each creature plays in maintaining healthy ecosystems.

Name: Art for our Sea
Description:
Art for our Sea turns creativity into living habitat.

Born inside PeaceCabin, we’re turning strategy and art into seagrass - and stories that grow back.

10% of every client fee flows into the fund. Our first collection of artworks, Sea Bathing (2023), proved the model; the new series will convert limited editions into planted habitat.

We now focus on sustaining donor seagrass beds across the Thames estuary with science-led partners - protecting coastlines, biodiversity and carbon-rich seabeds. We’re helping restore over one million m² of UK seagrass, with a five-year ambition to capture two million tonnes of CO₂.

AFOS is more than a fund - it’s a creative testbed. Ideas we trial here loop back into PeaceCabin’s strategy work, keeping it human, visual and regenerative.

Follow for releases, field notes and collaborations. Makers, scientists and sea people - let’s build together.

Art that plants hope. Proof you can see.

Watch our film here: https://www.peacecabin.earth/art

Name: LORAIN SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION DISTRICT
Description: LORAIN SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION DISTRICT is an environmental services company based out of 42110 RUSSIA RD, ELYRIA, Ohio, United States.

Name: Las Pumas - Centro de Rescate y Santuario | Rescue Center & Sanctuary
Description:
Las Pumas Rescue Center and Sanctuary in Costa Rica was founded by the visionary Swiss couple, Werner Hagnauer and Lilly Bodmer. Their journey began when they moved to Costa Rica in 1959, with Werner working at the renowned “Hacienda La Pacífica” in Cañas, Guanacaste. In 1971, they purchased the ranch and embraced sustainable farming. Witnessing the rampant deforestation and its devastating effects on local wildlife, the couple pivoted towards conservation. By 1989, they had established Las Pumas Rescue Center and Sanctuary on a portion of their land, dedicating their lives to the cause of wildlife protection.

The center started as a response to the urgent need for a safe haven for animals displaced by habitat loss or caught in the illegal pet trade. Lilly Bodmer’s deep-rooted conservationist ethos drove her to begin rescuing wildlife, collaborating with local authorities and even purchasing animals to save them from peril. Over the years, Las Pumas has grown into a sophisticated operation, involving the rescue, rehabilitation, and when possible, the release of wild animals back into their natural habitats. The facility is equipped with a veterinary clinic, quarantine, and pre-release areas where animals are carefully nurtured towards recovery.

While some animals are successfully reintroduced into the wild, others, due to injuries or prolonged human contact, find a permanent home at the sanctuary. Open to the public, the sanctuary educates visitors about each animal’s backstory, the challenges they faced, and the reasons they remain in captivity. 

Today, Las Pumas manages an average of 350 new animal cases annually, with species ranging from parrots and primates to felines and other mammals like coatis and raccoons. The center not only focuses on animal care but also plays a crucial role in environmental education, aiming to raise awareness about critical issues such as habitat destruction, hunting, wildfires, and illegal possession.

Name: AquaTerra Ingeniería Ambiental
Description:
Somos un equipo de expertos comprometidos con la protección del medio ambiente y la creación de un futuro más sostenible.

Trabajamos con una amplia gama de clientes en diversos sectores, brindando soluciones eficaces y personalizadas como remediación de suelos contaminados y cuerpos de agua, estudios de caracterización ambiental de sitios contaminados y atención a emergencias ambientales, por mencionar tan solo algunos de nuestros servicios.

Nuestro equipo multidisciplinario de profesionales altamente capacitados en campos como ingeniería civil y ambiental, geología y geofísica, gestión de recursos naturales y legislación ambiental, nos permite abordar de manera integral los desafíos ambientales más complejos.

Name: SAVANA (Satu Aksi Konservasi Alam) Indonesia
Description: SAVANA Indonesia adalah platform edukasi, aksi, dan kampanye konservasi sumber daya alam. Dengan komitmen menjaga kelestarian, SAVANA mendorong pengelolaan lingkungan berkelanjutan berbasis kearifan lokal dengan berfokus pada bidang ekonomi, ekologi, sosial budaya, serta digitalisasi. SAVANA membentuk agen perubahan yang mampu menjadi pondasi dalam gerakan yang mendorong aktivitas pngelolaan lingkungan berkelanjutan di tingkat akar rumput.

Name: HOTSPOT28
Description:
Der HOTSPOT28 vereint als Modellvorhaben im Aktionsprogramm Natürlicher Klimaschutz (ANK) Biodiversitäts- und Klimaschutz, um so negativen Auswirkungen der Klimakrise sowie dem fortschreitenden Verlust der biologischen Vielfalt entgegenzuwirken und gleichzeitig die positiven Leistungen von Ökosystemen als natürliche Klimaschützer zu stärken.
 
HOTSPOT28 ist ein Verbundprojekt des BUND MV, der Gemeinde Ostseebad Poel, der Hansestadt Lübeck, der Stiftung für Umwelt- und Naturschutz MV sowie des Landschaftspflegevereins Dummersdorfer Ufer. Das 700 Quadratkilometer große Projektgebiet erstreckt sich entlang der Ostseeküste von der Hansestadt Lübeck in Schleswig-Holstein bis hin zur Stadt Ostseebad Rerik in Mecklenburg-Vorpommern.

Das Modellprojekt im Aktionsprogramm Natürlicher Klimaschutz wird gefördert durch das Bundesamt für Naturschutz (BfN) mit Mitteln des Bundesministeriums für Umwelt, Naturschutz, nukleare Sicherheit und Verbraucherschutz (BMUV) und kofinanziert durch das Landesamt für Umwelt, Naturschutz und Geologie Mecklenburg-Vorpommern (LUNG) mit Mitteln des Ministeriums für Klimaschutz, Landwirtschaft, ländliche Räume und Umwelt Mecklenburg-Vorpommern (LM MV) sowie kofinanziert durch das Ministerium für Energiewende, Klimaschutz, Umwelt und Natur Schleswig-Holstein (MEKUN).

Name: Área de Protecção Ambiental das Ilhas Primeiras e Segundas
Fazenda Mais Segura | Fazenda Mais Verde
Description:
Saiba como fazer a gestão de todo o ambiente do seu negócio, prevenindo-se do fogo e dos prejuízos legais e financeiros decorrentes dele.
Através da Metodologia GAR nós elaboramos planos de prevenção e mitigação de acidentes tanto na área produtiva quanto na ambiental, provocados pelo fogo e outras fontes.
Acesse nosso site e saiba mais sobre como podemos te ajudar!

Name: Awesome Animal Academy/CS the Critter Saver
Description:
Have you noticed that the Next Generation of youth is frustrated and overwhelmed every time they hear someone talk about climate change, habitat loss, and the increased number of endangered species? Young people are looking for ways to step up and help make an impact, but they don’t know how!

Here is the solution!

In partnership with zoos, aquariums, wildlife conservation centers, and farms, Awesome Animal Academy and C S the Critter Saver are here to help grow the next generation of conservation leaders and expand the impact of your education programs with the Conservation Club “Critter Saver Champions,” Leadership Labs, Skill-Builder Courses, Coaching, and a ‘Grow Young Conservation Leaders Toolkit.’

Visit AwesomeAnimalAcademy.org to learn more.

Name: Congo Basin Institute Club @ UCLA
Description:
The Congo Basin Institute Club at UCLA seeks to raise awareness about the environmental problems facing the Congo Basin and the people who live there. We achieve this through supporting the Congo Basin Institute, a joint initiative between UCLA IoES and the International Institute of Tropical Agriculture, by organizing various events; each year, we organize fundraisers, movie nights, device drives, and research panels. 
By hosting various activities on campus and weekly meetings, we serve as a networking club for students interested in tropical ecology. We also offer support to the Cameroon Environmental Science Practicum Team.

Name: Preserve Rural Durham
Description:
Preserve Rural Durham is advocating for:
Conserving and protecting our natural resources and wildlife
Establishing county-wide sustainable development practices
Improving existing infrastructure before building new homes

Name: Wisconsin Conservation Hall of Fame Foundation, Inc.
Description: In coordination with our partners, we envision inspiring a strong conservation ethic among all Wisconsinites to help guide their actions today and tomorrow just as conservation ethics guided the actions of our Inductees into the Wisconsin Conservation Hall of Fame throughout their respective lifetimes.

Name: Okinawa Kame Centre
Description:
沖縄の野生のリュウキュウヤマガメとその生息地の保護・保存に取り組んでいます。
Our association protects and conserves the Ryukyu mountain turtle, and their habitats, in Okinawa Prefecture.
Location: 国頭郡恩納村, 沖縄県

Name: Agir pour demain
Description: Association a pour but d'unir les membres animés d'un même idéal et créer entre des liens d'entente et de solidarité.

Name: MAIA Conservación
Description: Somos un proyecto dedicado a la conservación de abejas nativas sin aguijón en Morelos, cuyo objetivo principal es dar a conocer estas especies para valorizar su existencia.

Name: Tolona Pona Lobi
Description: Tolona Pona Lobi est une organisation qui travaille dans le développement des zones rurales, à travers l'agriculture écologique et la plantation des arbres fruitiers.

Name: ATLAS Protection Incendie
Description: Nous agissons dans toute la Suisse pour la sûreté et le maintien aux normes de vos locaux en matière de protection incendie

Name: Safari Defenders Initiative
Description:
Africa's vast and vital conservation areas, home to unparalleled biodiversity and cultural heritage, are facing a silent crisis: 70% are critically underfunded. This impacts not just wildlife, but also the local communities that depend on these landscapes.
Safari Defenders Initiative (SDI) is a pioneering coalition dedicated to transforming this reality. We unite leading expertise in tourism, conservation, technology, and finance to drive financial sustainability for Africa’s protected areas through innovative, regenerative tourism models.

Our mission is ambitious yet achievable: to enable 100% of Africa's conservation areas to become financially self-sufficient, generating over $1000 per sq km per year in tourism revenue.

Name: Fundación Ñankulafkén
Description: Buscamos promover la conservación de las aves rapaces por medio de la investigación científica, educación ambiental y divulgación.

Name: Corporación Viboral Biodiverso
Description:
La corporación Viboral Biodiverso es un colectivo nacido en diciembre de 2019 con la firme misión de difundir el conocimiento científico y fomentar la apropiación social del conocimiento sobre la biodiversidad en El Carmen de Viboral y el oriente antioqueño. Fundado por cinco biólogas y biólogos carmelitanos graduados de la Universidad de Antioquia, su enfoque se centra en compartir el saber acumulado en diversas ramas de la biología, incluyendo la botánica, herpetología, ornitología y micología, con el propósito de resaltar, enaltecer y celebrar la riqueza natural de nuestro territorio.
Sus objetivos principales se centran en la investigación, la sostenibilidad a largo plazo, la colaboración y alianzas estratégicas con otras instituciones y organizaciones, así como la asesoría en el desarrollo científico y la conservación de la flora, fauna y funga autóctonos de nuestro territorio. El propósito de este colectivo es abordar las problemáticas locales y regionales a través de la generación de conocimiento científico sólido, la sensibilización y la apropiación social del conocimiento de los ecosistemas naturales de nuestra región.

Name: Leelanau Conservation District
Description:
The Leelanau Conservation District was organized in 1943 by a petition to the state of over 600 landowners. The District works closely with Federal, State and local agencies and organizations to promote the wise use of our natural resources through information and technical assistance to the landowners and users of Leelanau County.

Our Services:
• Natural Resource Site Visits
• County Soil Erosion Permits
• Forestry Assistance & Referrals
• Plant, Insect & Disease Identification
• No-Till Drill Rental
• Spring Tree Seedling & Shrub Sale
• Native Wildflower Sale
• Fall Conifer Seedling Sale
• 5th Grade Conservation Field Day
• Michigan Envirothon Support
• Workshops & Demonstrations

Name: 1 million d'arbres
Description:
Les objectifs de l’Association 1 Million d’Arbres sont :
Planter des arbres, restaurer les forêts, sensibiliser et éduquer les citoyens aux enjeux liés au changement climatique, à la préservation de l'environnement, de la biodiversité, à la valorisation du vivant, des arbres et des forêts.

2017 – création de l’Association 1 Million d’Arbres à Montpellier par Jean Paul Wabotaï
2018 – naissance du concept « Plantons 1 Million d’Arbres en France »
* partenariat avec la ville de Montpellier

2019 - obtention du Haut Patronage du Président de la République, Emmanuel Macron
* appel national de plantation aux communes, départements et régions : 30 arbres par communes pour obtenir 1 Million d’Arbres
* Pierre Monzani, Directeur Général des Assemblées des Départements de France appelle les 101 départements à participer à l’
* Jean-Luc Moudenc, Président de Toulouse Métropole et Maire de Toulouse, nous rejoint et propose de planter 10 000 arbres par an

2020 - obtention du Haut Patronage d’Elisabeth Borne, Ministre de la Transition Écologique et Solidaire
* l’association lance la 1ère Semaine Nationale de Plantation d’Arbres en France en collaboration avec la ville de Toulouse

2021 - obtention du Haut Patronage de Barbara Pompili, Ministre de la Transition Écologique
* 2e Semaine Nationale de Plantation d’Arbres en France à Toulouse et dans la Métropole

2022 - Le département de l’Isère lance le programme « 1 arbre - 1 habitant » et s’engage à planter plus d’un million d’arbres d’ici 2028
* l’Association « 1 Million d’Arbres et l’Organisation Congolaise pour l’Afforestation en RD Congo » voit le jour, avec l’objectif de créer :
- la pépinière et la forêt de l’Humanité et le centre de formation pour la plantation d’arbres
2023 - obtention du Haut Patronage du Ministère de la Transition Écologique et de la l’Agriculture
L’Association lance sa 3e Semaine Nationale de Plantation d’Arbres en France en partenariat avec Kawneer France et la Fondation Arconic

Name: Center for Conservation Excellence Foundation
Description:
The Center instructs on key concepts of conservation law to strengthen the professional conservation community, preparing them to contribute in their fields in line with the North American Model of Wildlife Conservation, public trust stewardship in state wildlife management, and beyond.

There have been few programs and projects undertaken to create awareness for wildlife law and policy with the mission of protecting Americans’ rights to hunt and fish. Though strong in intention, the scalability has been challenging when addressing this need for protecting modern conservation efforts, hunting heritage, and wildlife law. Additionally, the animal rights movement has been at large since the early 2000s and special interest groups are pushing their agenda of "legal personhood" for animals at an alarming and well-funded rate.

With support from the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service, Association of Fish & Wildlife Agencies, National Wild Turkey Federation, National Rifle Association’s Hunter Leadership Forum, and more, the Center is standing up to threats against modern conservation efforts and creating better access and quality of conservation and wildlife law education in America's law colleges.

While the Center currently offers courses in a handful of law schools and is continuously offering Continuing Legal Education seminars across the country, we won’t settle with good enough! The Center continues to place more courses and seminars, as well as connect more students and graduates with state agencies, non-profit organizations, and other conservation positions.

Name: LAZAC Foundation
Description: We work alongside local communities to ensure wildlife and habitats thrive in Latin America.

Name: Future Rhinos NFT
Description:
Future Rhinos will be a collection of 9 999 unique NFTs with its main goal to raise sustainable funding for circular rhino conservation impact. A circular approach includes people, planet and youth – one not more important than the other.

The name comes from our goal to conserve future rhinos (the species in general) by using NFT technology.

Name: FinAI
Description: FIN-AI intends to develop existing software, for the purposes of identifying individual marine mammals within populations, to simplify marine institute’s surveying and research.

Name: Idaho Coalition of Land Trusts
Description: The Idaho Coalition of Land Trusts (ICOLT) is a group of twenty nonprofit land trust organizations and affiliate members working on private land conservation and voluntary conservation agreements throughout Idaho. ICOLT also helps to coordinate association members to achieve legislative, administrative, communications and policy goals.

Name: Grosse Ile Nature and Land Conservancy
Description: We are an independent, 501C3, non-profit organization. Our Mission is to promote for public benefit the preservation, and understanding of the natural resources in the Township of Grosse Ile.

Name: One Ocean Network
Description:
Incredible work from conservation groups, marine researchers, and ocean enthusiasts has long been scattered across social media and forums. There wasn't a dedicated space bringing it all together.

One Ocean Network is that space. From conservation efforts by small non-profit groups around the world, to stunning photos from scuba divers and surfers, to everyday moments captured by fishermen and dog walkers on our coastlines - every ocean story has a home here.

Our one connected ocean deserves a connected community.

Name: ESG-Schweiz
Description: Aufforstung von Waldflächen in der Schweiz. Umsetzung und Erfahrungsaustausch.

Name: BUND Naturschutz in Bayern e.V. Kreisgruppe Pfaffenhofen
Description:
Die Kreisgruppe Pfaffenhofen ist eine lokale Organisation des Bund Naturschutz in Bayern e.V. (BN), die sich für den Schutz und die Erhaltung der Natur und Umwelt in der Region Pfaffenhofen an der Ilm einsetzt. Die Gruppe besteht aus engagierten Mitgliedern, die sich für eine nachhaltige Entwicklung und den Erhalt von natürlichen Lebensräumen und Artenvielfalt einsetzen.

Die Kreisgruppe Pfaffenhofen organisiert regelmäßig Veranstaltungen, Vorträge und Exkursionen zu Umweltthemen und bietet damit eine Plattform für den Austausch von Wissen und Erfahrungen. Sie setzt sich auch für den Erhalt von ökologisch wertvollen Flächen ein und engagiert sich in der Umweltbildung und Öffentlichkeitsarbeit, um das Bewusstsein für die Bedeutung von Natur- und Umweltschutz zu stärken.

Die Kreisgruppe Pfaffenhofen ist Teil des größten Natur- und Umweltschutzverbandes in Bayern, dem Bund Naturschutz, der sich seit über 100 Jahren für den Schutz der Natur und Umwelt einsetzt. Zusammen mit anderen Kreisgruppen und dem Landesverband arbeitet die Kreisgruppe Pfaffenhofen an langfristigen Strategien und konkreten Projekten, um die Natur und Umwelt in der Region nachhaltig zu schützen.

Name: Fundación Dominicana de Estudios Marinos (FUNDEMAR)
Description: En FUNDEMAR nos dedicamos a la conservación de los ecosistemas y su biodiversidad de manera holística incorporando la investigación, educación e integración de las comunidades y sus usuarios.

Name: INDO - MYANMAR CONSERVATION
Description: Indo - Myanmar Conservation

Name: Gilbert's Potoroo Action Group Inc.
Description: Gilbert's Potoroo Action Group Inc. is a not-for-profit community group seeking to help save the Critically Endangered Gilbert's Potoroo through fundraising, awareness raising and volunteering.

Name: Hualapai Department of Natural Resources
Description: The Hualapai Department of Natural Resources serves as the primary authority over all Natural Resources of the Hualapai Tribe, and monitors the health of the environment and the natural resources from an active management perspective on the Hualapai Reservation. Our mission is to conserve, protect and enhance the natural resources of the Hualapai Reservation while providing for multiple consumptive and non-consumptive uses, and ensuring the overall goal of long-term sustainable and balanced multiple uses of natural resources under the direction of the Hualapai Tribal Council.

Name: Rhode Island Land Trust Council
Description:
The RI Land Trust Council leads efforts to conserve land in both rural and urban areas of Rhode Island to secure thriving farms, resilient forests, healthy watersheds, welcoming shorelines and parks, well-maintained trails, biodiverse habitats, and clean drinking water for the benefit of all Rhode Islanders.

The Council leads these efforts by supporting land trusts and their collaborators through capacity building, convenings, and public engagement, and by advocating for sound land conservation policies.

Name: Friends with honey
Description:
Helen is a certified permaculture designer, a member of the Victorian Apiarist Association and a passionate beekeeper.  She has travelled the world working in the field of sustainable tourism and events and has been a lead judge for the Victorian tourism awards.

“Early on in my beekeeping journey, I became aware that my friends and family were fascinated by my exploits and adored the raw honey from my bees.  But, I wanted to tell them that bees are so much more than honey -  I wanted to convey the message of sustainability, biodiversity, and the power of pollinators. So I created Friends with Honey, and the rest is history.”

Name: Blueheart movement
Description:
BlueHeart Movement is a youth-led environmental initiative based in Kilifi, Kenya, dedicated to ocean conservation, plastic pollution awareness, and community-driven clean-up actions.

Founded out of a deep love for the ocean and a desire to protect our coastal ecosystems, we bring young people together to take meaningful climate action - through beach clean-ups, public education, partnerships, and creative environmental campaigns.

We believe in the power of community, consistency, and collaboration to protect our planet, one shoreline at a time.

Name: Savanhaya
Description:
En tant qu'étudiant en 2ème année à l'EGPN (École de Gestion et de Protection de la Nature) nous organisons un webinaire sur la savane. Il aura lieu le Samedi 13 avril 2024 à 18h.
Nous sommes aussi présent sur Instagram https://www.instagram.com/savanhaya?igsh=MTd1bWtnMGV6a3NydQ==

Name: Wildculture Africa
Description: Explore, Educate, and Empower African youth to preserve and promote Africa's natural and cultural treasures through generative tourism by 2035.

Name: Association AVENTURE PLURIEL
Description:
Depuis 1995, Aventure Pluriel rassemble de nombreux passionnés de bateaux traditionnels en bois.
Son objectif premier est la sauvegarde du patrimoine maritime et fluvial à travers un grand nombre de projets de restauration, de réflexion et de navigation.
Aventure Pluriel sait mobiliser de nombreux bénévoles dans les divers lieux :
En région SUD :
- Au "CAC"(Chantier Associatif de la Campanette) à Cagnes-sur-mer où 3500m2 sont destinés à l'entretien et à la restauration de nos bateaux et à ceux de nos membres,
- Sur le "Quai Patrimoine au port du Cros-de-Cagnes" où sont réunis 5 de nos bateaux "collectifs ou Partagés",
- Sur le "Ponton Patrimoine" du port Vauban d'Antibes,
- Aux ports Golfe-Juan et Sainte-Maxime où sont basés quelques-uns de nos "bateaux partagés",
- Au port de Saint-Tropez avec 2 bateaux.

En OCCITANIE :
- Sur le Canal du Midi, à Ventenac-en-Minervois ou nous avons entrepris la restauration d'un des plus anciens bateau d'Europe (1855) bénéficiant d'une Inscription régionale aux Monuments Historiques.

Régulièrement, les membres se retrouvent pour vivre des navigations hors du temps ou participer à des travaux. Nous nous réunissons aussi pour des moments de formation dans le cadre d'ERASMUS + ou de réflexions sur l'avenir du patrimoine maritime et fluvial.

Un programme d’activités annuel ambitieux est aussi proposé tout au long de l’année. Vous pouvez le retrouver sur notre agenda. 

Enfin, l'association participe à de nombreuses rencontres de vieux gréements en France et à l’étranger, développe des activités dans le cadre d’Handisport et met à disposition des bateaux, partagés ou collectifs, pour rendre la mer accessible à tous, partout et tout le temps, en rencontrant d'autres passionnés ici et ailleurs.

L'aventure vous tente ? Alors embarquez et bonne nav' !

Name: Ramat Wildlife Society
Description: RAMAT Wildlife Society is a representative body for conservancies in Central Kajiado. The bulk of these conservancies are found in the Matapato Rangelands, a 3200 sq km landscape in the northern periphery of the Amboseli National Park Ecosystem.

Name: Margay - Reserva Natural & Lodge de Selva
COMMUNITY TRAINING WORKS, INC.
Description: Our vision is a world where individuals utilize intelligence and positive energy to improve their lives, community, and environment. Our business exists to help land, water and road managing agencies who struggle to complete backlog maintenance and restoration work. CTW, Inc. is committed to making a positive impact on communities and natural environments. We aim to foster community engagement, empower youth, and contribute to the enhancement of public spaces and natural landscapes through our unique workforce development program.

Name: Vernetzungsstelle für die Ökologischen Stationen in Niedersachsen
Description: Die 28 Ökologischen Stationen in Niedersachsen tragen dazu bei den Herausforderungen des regionalen Natur- und Umweltschutzes besser gerecht zu werden. Sie gewährleisten eine kontinuierliche und langfristige Vor-Ort-Betreuung von Schutzgebieten. Im Fokus stehen dabei die Natura 2000-Gebiete, die sich besonders auf Flora, Fauna und Vogelschutz konzentrieren.

Name: Balai Besar Taman Nasional Lore Lindu
Description: Sebagai kawasan pelestarian alam yang mempunyai ekosistem asli, Taman Nasional Lore Lindu (TNLL) memiliki fungsi perlindungan sistem penyangga kehidupan, pengawetan keanekaragaman jenis tumbuhan dan satwa serta pemanfaatan secara lestari sumber daya alam hayati dan ekosistemnya.

Name: We Become
Description:
We Become helps individuals and communities improve their quality of life, realize their full potential and build shared prosperity. 

We Become's Mill Creek Conservation Corps works to monitor, protect and improve water quailty & tree canopy in the Mill Creek subwatershed and surrounding communties.

Name: Conservation West Virginia
Description:
Our families, our communities and our economy are all stronger when we take care of our natural heritage.

We advance this important value by informing West Virginians about key conservation issues, taking positions on legislation and policy affecting the environment, and communicating our views to elected officials.

To make real change, conservationists must be engaged in policy development and advocacy. In addition, we need to educate the public about elected officials and candidates for public office based on their conservation views and mobilize voters to elect conservation leaders.

Name: Sloth World
Description: Sloth World is a one-of-a-kind immersive attraction coming to Orlando’s International Drive, offering guests the rare opportunity to connect with sloths in a safe, educational, and unforgettable environment. Blending conservation, hands-on experiences, and family-friendly fun, Sloth World is designed to inspire curiosity, respect for wildlife, and lasting memories for visitors of all ages.

Name: Common Coast Research & Conservation
Description: Common Coast Research & Conservation is a non-profit organization dedicated to the study and protection of Great Lakes migratory birds and the lands and the waters upon which they depend.

Name: ACPE (Action Citoyenne pour la Protection de l'Environnement)
Description:
Mot de bienvenue

Chers membres,

C’est avec un immense plaisir que nous vous souhaitons la bienvenue sur la plateforme officielle de l’ONG Action Citoyenne pour la Protection de l’Environnement (ACPE).

Votre présence ici témoigne de votre engagement et de votre intérêt pour la noble cause de la protection de l’environnement et de la préservation de la biodiversité. Ensemble, nous formons une communauté de citoyens responsables et solidaires, déterminés à agir pour un avenir durable et respectueux de notre patrimoine naturel.

Depuis sa création, ACPE s’est investie avec conviction dans plusieurs programmes de conservation, et elle joue un rôle actif aux côtés des autorités compétentes dans la lutte contre la criminalité faunique. À travers son projet phare ALCRIF (Appui à la Lutte contre la Criminalité Faunique), l’organisation mène des actions concrètes en Guinée pour protéger les espèces menacées et faire respecter les lois de conservation.

Cette plateforme est votre espace. Un lieu d’échanges, de partages d’expériences, d’informations, d’initiatives et de collaboration. Ensemble, faisons de cet outil un levier puissant pour renforcer notre impact, mobiliser davantage de citoyens et construire des solutions durables face aux menaces qui pèsent sur notre environnement.

Encore une fois, bienvenue parmi nous. Votre engagement est précieux.

L’équipe de l’ACPE

Name: The Manda Projects Ltd
Description: On the island of Manda in the Lamu Archipelago, in amongst the mangroves and seagrass beds, and sandwiched between the blue waters of the Indian Ocean and the coastal acacia forests of the Kenyan mainland, The Manda Projects is just beginning.

Name: Instituto Ora - Conservação da Natureza
Description: PRODUÇÃO DE NATUREZA É GERAR VALOR COM PROPÓSITO.

Name: Les Robins des Choix
Description:
Tenez-vous prêts! Les Robins des Choix vont sévir dès cet été en Romandie pour aider les citoyens à prendre les bonnes décisions pour l’environnement. Leur méthode ? Faire pédaler leurs cibles pour produire les précieux “ShareWoods”.

Tu veux en savoir plus ? Rejoins la communauté des Robins des Choix.

Avec notre projet, nous proposons un système d’échelle comparative avec une unité fictive (le ShareWood) afin de faciliter la prise de conscience de son propre impact sur l’environnement. Nous mettrons notre échelle en scène dans le cadre d’une manifestation grand public.

L'animation consistera à faire pédaler les visiteurs sur un vélo qui indiquera le nombre de « ShareWoods » produits dans un laps de temps donné. La mise en perspective de la production de “ShareWoods” et des équivalences affichées (p.ex. train ou voiture, jus de fruit local ou importé) éclairera ainsi le visiteur dans ses prises de décision quotidienne. 

Pour matérialiser l’effort et motiver les visiteurs à venir participer, l'énergie produite par les coups de pédales sera revalorisée sous forme électrique et/ou mécanique selon les possibilités techniques de nos partenaires et les souhaits de l'événement. 

Photo arrière-plan: Airwolfhound https://www.flickr.com/photos/24874528@N04/7766667614/sizes/l/

Name: Conservation Lakefield
Description: Conservation Lakefield is a local organization dedicated to the conservation of land in the Gore, Mille-Îles and Wentworth regions. We are a non-profit, registered charity, working to preserve land of exceptional ecological value through the acquisition or gifting of property, establishment of agreements with land owners and other conservation options. We collaborate with other nature conservation organizations in the Laurentians to maximize our impact. Whether you are a land owner or a nature lover, you can help us protect and preserve our valuable natural environment.

Name: Blooming Boulevards
Description:
We connect neighbourhoods to nature by growing native plants and creating habitat boulevard gardens throughout Mississauga. 

These plants support our native pollinators and contribute to habitat corridors throughout the city.

Name: Löwen River Rewilding
Description: Our goal is to revitalize Nature's canvas by restoring vegetation and reintroducing native wildlife.

Name: Colorado River Sustainability Campaign
Description: The Colorado River Sustainability Campaign serves to provide support, capacity, and coordination to conservation groups’ efforts to ensure that the Colorado River, its tributaries, and its Delta sustain Tribes that have lived in the Basin for millennia, healthy and diverse populations of fish and wildlife, and outstanding recreational opportunities, while providing water to support resilient communities, business, and agriculture.

Name: ЕкоOбщина - устойчив град и общество
Description:
Националният конкурс за насърчаване и споделяне на иновативни екологични практики "ЕкоОбщина - устойчив град и общество” е инициатива на Посолството на Франция в България и Френския Културен Институт в София. Той има за цел да открои значението на прилагането на добри практики на местно ниво за едно устойчиво градско развитие. 

ЕкоОбщина си сътрудничи с всички български общини, да кандидатстват в конкурса за проектни предложения за осъществяването на проект в близост до обществото, за допълнително осигуряване на добавена стойност към съществуващ проект или за мобилизиране на други средства в рамките на устойчивото градско развитие. 

Заeдно със своите партньори,  ЕкоОбщина предоставя своята експертиза и подпомага общините в техния проект.

Name: Kennebecasis Watershed Restoration Committee
Description:
The actions of the KWRC are guided by a Committee of stakeholders composed of representatives from various provincial and federal departments, municipalities of the area, and local interest groups. These stakeholders have been planning, securing funding for, and implementing restoration, monitoring, and educational activities within the Kennebecasis Watershed since 1994.

The important restoration, monitoring and education outreach work we undertake in the Kennebecasis Watershed could not be possible without the financial and in-kind contributions of various groups and organizations.

Name: Tauranga Moana Biosecurity Capital
Description:
Tauranga Moana Biosecurity Capital (TMBC) is a collaborative organisation with a mission to shift New Zealanders perception of biosecurity, how it connects to our everyday lives, and how we can all get involved. 

Our focus is on working in partnership with mana whenua, connecting our communities to the importance of biosecurity, and building collaboration across iwi, hapū, community organisations, industry, business, science and mātauranga Māori experts, central government and local government to achieve biosecurity excellence.

Name: Solidago Conservancy
Description: Solidago Conservancy is a nonprofit land trust working with local families and partners to protect and enhance the Salt Valley Greenway, an “emerald necklace” and connecting green corridors in Lincoln and Lancaster County, Nebraska. We focus on conserving tallgrass prairie, Nebraska’s Eastern Saline Wetlands, riparian corridors, and other significant landscapes that provide habitat, open space, and ecosystem services. Solidago Conservancy partners with a wide range of organizations and agencies dedicated to protecting the region’s special places.

Name: YAGA YOGO Association
Description:
Ebauche YAGA YOGO Association

YAGA YOGO est une association culturelle Camerounaise régie conformément aux dispositions de la loi N°90/053 du 19 Décembre 1990 sur la liberté d’association. Aut' N°136 / 2014/RDA/C19/SAAJP.

OBJECTIFS :
- Servir de cadre de réflexion, de concertation, d’échange et d’action concrètes en vue d’une mobilisation continue pour la promotion de la culture
- Favoriser l’émergence d’une société africaine et mondiale plus harmonieuse  et plus apte aux défis de la mondialisation
- Œuvrer pour l’atteinte des objectifs du développement durable fixés par l’ONU, à travers le PNUE.

YAGA YOGO Association, œuvre dans la médiation culturelle internationale. Elle produit des évènements (Kamer Reggae Festival, RUA / Recyclage Utile et Agréable (Initiation au recyclage, à la préservation de l'environnement, tout en associant des jeux ludiques (Jeux d'antan issue de notre patrimoine culturel matériel et immatériel).
- EVMC / Éducation à la Vie Minimaliste et à la Citoyenneté. Etc.), afin de mettre en mouvement les peuples autour des idéaux de partage, d’amour et de découvertes. 

SECTEURS D’ACTIVITE :

•	Médiation Culturelle
•	Patrimoine
•	Environnement
•	Ecotourisme

Name: Seneca Park Zoo
Description: Seneca Park Zoo is a conservation organization where unforgettable memories, a stronger community, and a healthier planet get their start.  Located at 2222 Saint Paul St, in beautiful Monroe County, Rochester, New York, United States.

Name: BirdLife Western Australia
Description: Birdlife WA is the Western Australian branch of Birdlife Australia. For over 120 years, we’ve been at the forefront of bird conservation in Australia. Thanks to our supporters, and to the science which informs our actions, we’ve achieved some incredible outcomes for threatened birds – but now, we’re facing our greatest challenge yet: Australia’s extinction crisis. Saving birds is what we're all about. Over 200 species of birds across Australia are currently threatened with extinction, and many more are in decline. BirdLife Australia works to stop threatened birds from becoming extinct, with a program of conservation action. Many of these programs are carried out in Western Australia where habitat destruction is the most serious threat to endangered birds. Through partnerships, conservation, education and advocacy, our mission is to save birds. On this site, we'll share insights affecting WA bird species.

Name: Especies, Sociedad y Hábitat A.C.
Description:
Somos una organización de la Sociedad Civil, enfocada en el desarrollo de proyectos de conservación, restauración y educación para la sostenibilidad en el Noreste de México, enfocando nuestros esfuerzos en la protección y recuperación de espacios y especies, siempre considerando a las comunidades y usuarios de la biodiversidad como socios y colaboradores prioritarios de nuestras iniciativas. 
Buscamos integrar la conservación con el desarrollo local sostenible, para lo cual, creamos alianzas entre comunidades rurales, propietarios particulares, Organizaciones no Gubernamentales, iniciativa privada, agencias de gobierno e instituciones académicas. 

MISIÓN: “La Conservación de la biodiversidad, como un elemento estratégico para el desarrollo social sustentable, para lo cual, agrupamos diversos perfiles e intereses, buscando propiciar un carácter multidisciplinario, interinstitucional y basados en conocimiento científico” 

VISIÓN: “Construir una sociedad educada y comprometida, que valore, proteja y aproveche sustentablemente la biodiversidad, y reconozca su papel fundamental en la conservación de la misma”

Name: Teller Wildlife Refuge
Description:
Teller Wildlife Refuge is made up of 1,300 acres of diverse habitats in the Bitterroot River, riparian corridor, including the river, streams, spring creeks, irrigation ditches, emergent and open water wetlands, uplands and agricultural fields. These resources are managed using a variety of methods to improve, restore, and maintain healthy land, water and wildlife habitat.

Management techniques include habitat enhancement, integrated weed management, wildlife-friendly agriculture, education and research, youth programs, active volunteer involvement, and landowner outreach. Teller also carefully manages access (public and private) for fishing and hunting activities to maintain healthy wildlife populations and a quality recreational experience for visitors.

Name: Arizona Association of Conservation Districts
Description:
The Arizona Association of Conservation Districts (AACD) was established by Arizona's Conservation Districts in 1944 as a means of support to coordinate and fund conservation efforts across the state. In 1992, AACD was recognized as a nonprofit and registered 501(c)3.

Arizona’s Conservation Districts – made up of farmers, ranchers, and volunteers – conserve our water, soil, wildlife habitats, open spaces, and other limited natural resources. Conservation Districts are hubs of conservation at the local level, providing education, community outreach, and other essential functions in supporting conservation across the state.

In 1941, the Arizona Legislature charged Arizona's Conservation Districts with the statutory responsibility to provide for the conservation and restoration of lands, water, wildlife, and other natural resources, and to protect water rights, the tax base, public lands, and the general health and welfare of the people.

Conservation Districts are the only organization in Arizona – local, state, or federal – that is not restricted to working on a specific resource (e.g., water) or land ownership/usage type (e.g., federal or state land).

Name: Resolve Wildlife
Description:
Resolve Wildlife is an African-led conservation organisation committed to protecting wildlife and habitats at a landscape scale.
Through long-term partnerships, we build capacity in protected area management by delivering effective training, advice, and support. Our holistic and innovative approach ensures sustainable biodiversity impact while contributing to shared climate and conservation goals.

Name: Mille Écorces
Description: Société spécialisée dans le développement et le déploiement de projet de contribution à la neutralité planétaire et lutte contre l'érosion de la biodiversité

Name: ARMS Restore
Description: We are a coral reef restoration project located in southwest Madagascar. We use Autonomous Reef Monitoring Structures, or ARMS, devices to regenerate and restore reef health and combat biodiversity loss due to overfishing. Thus we aim to better the food access and the health of the local Malagasy population who rely on the reefs as a primary food supply. We partner with Harvard University, Reef Doctors, and Institut Halieutique et des Sciences Marine to make our research possible.

Name: Proyecto NATURPORTS
Description:
Este proyecto se desarrolla con la colaboración de la Fundación Biodiversidad del Ministerio para la Transición Ecológica y el Reto Demográfico, a través del Programa Pleamar, y se cofinancia por la Unión Europea por el FEMPA (Fondo Europeo Marítimo, de Pesca y de Acuicultura), está liderado por la Autoridad Portuaria de Vigo y tiene como socios a Universidade de Vigo, Universidad de Oviedo y Instituto de Investigacións Mariñas – CSIC.
El proyecto NATURPORTS se centrará en diseñar y validar estrategias de restauración ecológica innovadoras en entornos portuarios, aplicando y adaptando Soluciones Basadas en la Naturaleza (SbN) y combinando herramientas de restauración y rehabilitación, al tiempo que se promueve la funcionalidad ecológica de estos entornos y la provisión de servicios ecosistémicos. De este modo, la iniciativa se enfoca en crear sinergias entre la conservación ambiental y el desarrollo portuario sostenible, resaltando la importancia de los ecosistemas marinos saludables para la economía azul.

Name: thelma_app
Description:
thelma est une application citoyenne et environnementale développée par SPALLIAN.

Gratuite et collaborative, thelma crée un lien entre citoyens, institutions, collectivités et entreprises engagés pour améliorer durablement le cadre de vie.

Déjà adoptée par plusieurs villes ambassadrices et soutenue par la COP15, thelma s’impose comme un outil concret de mobilisation et d’action environnementale.

 Ensemble, cultivons l’engagement. Et faisons pousser le changement.

Name: St. Clair Region Conservation Foundation
Description: The St. Clair Region Conservation Foundation is a registered, charitable organization. Our purpose is to raise funds to support the conservation programs of the St. Clair Region Conservation Authority. Programs such as protecting communities from flooding and erosion dangers, educating children about nature and environmental concerns, tree planting, and the protection of wetlands, soils, and forests are all helped through the efforts of the Foundation.

Name: Ithaca 2030 District
Description:
The Ithaca 2030 District is an interdisciplinary public-private collaboration working to create a groundbreaking high-performance building district in Downtown Ithaca. A project of the Tompkins County Climate Protection Initiative (TCCPI), the Ithaca 2030 District is part of a larger effort in Tompkins County to reduce our greenhouse gas emissions 80% by 2050.

By making the business case for efficient operations, the District is driving innovation through collaboration, leveraged financing, and shared resources. The District builds on the TCCPI model to provide a non-competitive collaborative environment where building owners, community organizations, and professionals come together to share best practices and accelerate market transformation in Ithaca's built environment.

Name: Les AJOnc (Amis des Jardins Ouverts et néanmoins clôturés)
Description:
Les AJOn ont pour objectif est la (re)création de lien social à partir de la nature. Il se concrétise par l'accompagnement
de groupes d'habitants jardiniers dans la reconquête de leur environnement et de leur cadre de vie.
Notre projet a trait à l'éducation et à la protection de l'environnement, à l'émergence de pratiques citoyennes dans la durée. Aujourd'hui, l'activité de l'association s'étend auprès de plus de 70 jardins
naturels partagés installés ou en cours d'installation dans la Région Hauts-de-France. Ces dernières
années, l'activité de compostage sur les jardins partagés s'est prolongée hors des jardins, sous forme de
composteurs de rue.

Name: Bull Run Mountains Conservancy
Description:
Bull Run Mountains Conservancy is an independent, nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization founded in 1994 to protect the Bull Run Mountains through education, research, and stewardship. 

We promote the preservation of the Bull Run Mountains from their southern ridges in New Baltimore to their northern end in Aldie, Virginia.

BRMC offers educational programs to adults and children that focus on the ecological, historical, and cultural features of the Bull Run Mountains. Research underpins our education program. For 25 years, BRMC has sponsored, assisted, and facilitated numerous baseline studies that add important data to our understanding of the landscape.

At BRMC preservation does not mean a hands-off policy, but rather a focus on drawing people to appreciate the resources at hand and the completion of research to better understand and protect those resources.

Name: Association MAMPIVOATSY
Description: " Concilier bien-être des communautés et protection de l’environnement pour un développement responsable et pérenne".

Name: Society for Conservation Biology- Africa Region-Uganda Chapter
Reefers Oceanographic Association
Description:
¿Sabías que los arrecifes de coral son uno de los ecosistemas más diversos y valiosos del planeta? Lamentablemente, estos ecosistemas están en peligro debido al cambio climático, la contaminación y la sobrepesca. Es por eso que organizaciones como La Asociación Oceanográfica Reefers son tan importantes.

La Asociación Oceanográfica Reefers es una organización sin fines de lucro dedicada a la conservación de los arrecifes de coral. La organización trabaja en proyectos de investigación, restauración y educación para proteger estos valiosos ecosistemas.

Además de su trabajo en la conservación de los arrecifes de coral, La Asociación Oceanográfica Reefers también promueve la conciencia sobre la importancia de los arrecifes de coral y la necesidad de protegerlos. La organización trabaja en estrecha colaboración con comunidades locales, científicos y otras organizaciones para lograr sus objetivos. 

Apoya a La Asociación Oceanográfica Reefers y ayúdanos a proteger los arrecifes de coral para las generaciones futuras!

Name: Fundación Conserva tu Bosque
Description: Organización sin fines de lucro creada con el propósito de conservar el Corredor Biológico Nevados de Chillán - Laguna del Laja, generando una red colaborativa entre iniciativas públicas y privadas. Nuestro trabajo se basa en tres objetivos estratégicos: la educación de niños, niñas y jóvenes; la investigación y restauración del territorio; y la conservación en sí mediante herramientas legales como el Derecho real de conservación.

Name: Kent High Weald Partnership
Description:
Kent High Weald Partnership aims to connect people and nature through conservation, education, and wellbeing. Through our work we want to support the development of resilient communities and resilient ecosystems that mean people and wildlife can thrive together.

“No one will protect what they don’t care about; and no one will care about what they have never experienced.” (Sir David Attenborough)

Our small team are passionate about nature and work hard to conserve our local greenspaces and wildlife, promote community wellbeing using the great outdoors, and bring the magic of Forest School to as many children as we can.

We could not do what we do without our fantastic and dedicated team of volunteers and the valuable partnerships we hold with the local councils, community organisations and schools.

Name: Good Fish Guide
Description:
The Good Fish Guide began in 2002 as a book to help consumers and businesses navigate a complex seafood marketplace. Twenty years on, our powerful online tool evaluates the sustainability of over 120 different seafood species, with over 600 individual ratings covering the majority of the EU marketplace. Our guidance is used by a range of businesses, from supermarkets to fish & chip shops, covering 20% of the seafood sold in the UK.  

Modern consumers have high expectations around sustainable sourcing. We offer free guidance to help your business meet these demands.

Name: Český svaz ochránců přírody
Description: Český svaz ochránců přírody is a company based out of Michelská 48/5, Prag, Středočeský, Czech Republic.

Name: Belwin Conservancy
Description:
The wellbeing of each and every one of us is tied to the health of our natural world. That’s why we are happier and healthier when we spend time in nature. It’s also why we need everyone to feel passionate about protecting it. At Belwin, we spark that passion every day through conservation, education, and immersive experiences on 1,500 acres in the Saint Croix Valley.

Belwin Conservancy permanently protects nearly 1,600 acres in the Valley Creek Watershed. Through partnerships, public access, and innovative programming, we welcome people to celebrate and learn from our protected landscapes. In fostering connection and engagement with the land, Belwin has inspired thousands of people to protect our natural world—one hiker, one student at a time.

• Education, arts and culture, and research programs at Belwin inspire new avenues of perception, understanding, engagement, and environmental stewardship.

• We balance human use in a way that is compatible with habitat restoration and ongoing care for the land. We are a key contributor to healthy habitat, air, and water in the St. Croix Valley.

• Belwin strives to make our spaces, partnerships, and programs inclusive, accessible, and equitable. You have a place in nature.

Name: ohneGift
Description: Der Verein ohneGift setzt sich ein für den Schutz von Mensch und Natur vor Giften. Dazu gehören in erster Linie schädliche Pestizide. Wir informieren die Öffentlichkeit über diese Gefahr und zeigen auf, was jeder Mensch dagegen unternehmen kann.

Name: LCOY Ecuador
Description: LCOY Ecuador 2025 es la Conferencia Local de Jóvenes sobre Cambio Climático, un espacio oficial reconocido por YOUNGO (constituido ante la CMNUCC – Convención Marco de las Naciones Unidas sobre el Cambio Climático). Su propósito es empoderar a adolescentes y jóvenes para que participen activamente en la acción climática, fortalezcan sus capacidades y eleven sus voces en los procesos de decisión a nivel local, nacional e internacional.

Name: The Naturalist
Description:
نحن مبادرة تهدف إلى نشر المعرفة وزيادة الوعى بالتنوع الحيوي والحفظ من خلال محتوي بسيط باللغة العربية من أجل مستقبل مزدهر ومستدام.

The Naturalist is an initiative that aims to disseminate knowledge and raise awareness about biodiversity and conservation using simple, comprehended content in Arabic for a flourished, sustainable future.

Name: Nested Sustainability Consulting
Description: As scientists who have worked with international to local-sized companies, we have experience with all aspects of sustainability and a holistic understanding of reporting that will set the stage for high-quality assessment, goal setting, and reporting for the future.  From assessing the biodiversity on your land (E), to the environmental justice initiatives of your volunteer hours (S), to the gender and racial diversity on your board of directors (G), we can help you

Name: Munus Vergroent
Description:
Wij zijn Munus Vergroent. Specialist op het gebied van vegetatiedaken voor de zakelijke en private markt. 

Samen met ons team rollen wij de groene ideologie uit. Wij zijn overtuigd van de noodzaak om onze bebouwde omgeving aantrekkelijker te maken voor ons allen; planten, dieren en mensen. 

Dat doen we o.a. door het plaatsen van groene daken en muren. Hiermee reduceren we hittestress en wateroverlast en creëren we zgn. steppingstones voor flora&fauna en een prettiger leefomgeving voor mens en dier.

Neem contact met ons op, met plezier komen we langs! We denken met je mee en bekijken de mogelijkheden van jouw pand. Ook het aanbrengen van extra isolatie, zonnepanelen en het bergen van water kunnen onderdeel uitmaken van ons dienstenpakket. Zo leveren wij een duurzaam eindproduct.

Name: Association EDEN 49
Description:
Créée il y a 30 ans par les fédérations de pêche et de chasse de Maine-et-Loire, EDEN est agréée au titre de la protection de l’environnement et répond à des projets de territoire pour les collectivités et les entreprises sur le thème du Bocage, sous le triptyque « haie – mare – prairie ».
Notre principale force est de maîtriser l’ensemble de la chaîne du projet, de l’étude aux travaux.

Name: Ramille
Description: Inspirée par la philosophie de la permaculture, notre mission est de favoriser la biodiversité sous toutes ses formes, tout en œuvrant vers une autonomie alimentaire durable et résiliente

Name: SOuL Sanctuary Society
Description:
PlastikPay+
PlastikPay+ is a digital platform that brings together individuals, recyclers, and businesses to trade, value add, and transport recyclable plastics and other commonly discarded materials. Powered by big data science, spatial AI business analytics, and Solana blockchain technologies—jointly developed by SOuLSANCTUARY Strategies Consultancy Inc. (a corporation) and SOuL Sanctuary Society Inc. (a non-profit) and supported by Superteam Japan—the platform facilitates transparent and traceable recycling and upcycling transactions. By supporting community-led logistics and mapping activity, PlasticPay+ helps extend the lifespan of plastic materials, minimizes environmental impact, and promotes a competitive, scalable approach to the circular economy.

We address the interconnected global challenges of deforestation, organic waste mismanagement, and unsustainable food systems. Every year, the world loses over 10 million hectares of forest (FAO, 2020), largely due to agricultural expansion, which threatens biodiversity, disrupts water cycles, and accelerates climate change.

 ClimateChic
ClimateChic is a climate-smart, evidence-based, and nature-based circular economy model that regenerates degraded agroforestry systems while creating local income opportunities. It turns organic and food waste into black soldier fly larvae (BSFL), which serve as a sustainable, antibiotic-free protein source for free-range chickens.

 SOuL
SOuL is an innovative DePIN startup that employs a quantum network of IoT + lEo devices suited for remote forest areas to intelligently grow forest RWEcosystems only possible in Solana.

Name: CIBOLO CONSERVANCY
Description: CIBOLO CONSERVANCY is an environmental services company based out of 140 CITY PARK RD, BOERNE, Texas, United States.

Name: Milford Opportunities Project
Description: Milford Sound Piopiotahi maintains its status as a key New Zealand visitor icon and provides a world-class visitor experience that is accessible, upholds the World Heritage status, national park and conservation values and adds value to Southland and New Zealand Inc.

Name: 800 Trust
Description: Protecting Taranaki’s biodiversity through innovative conservation and education initiatives. Focused on creating safe habitats for vulnerable native species.

Name: Capi - Capturer l'invisible
Description:
Notre association propose des excursions guidées dans l’arc jurassien sur les traces de la faune sauvage, avec qui nous partageons forêts et pâturages. Nos sorties ont lieu en journée, afin de limiter les dérangements sur la faune, et nous nous aidons de pièges photographiques pour les observer en tout temps. 
C’est ainsi que l’on peut "capturer l’invisible" !

Name: Cervicide
Description: The team was established in 2011 by a group of college kids with a passion for hunting across the nation. Most of the group of hunters that comprise  this team grew up in the mountains of central Pennsylvania and are a tight-knit group. The remaining members are passionate hunters from other states, who met the team through college. While much of the hunts take place in the mountains of central PA, many of the guys travel the nation to hunt, primarily whitetail deer. The aim of Cervicide is to bring forth quality HD hunting angles without the extra frills while telling a story through their hunts. This group of guys have a love for the outdoors and a passion for filming. They are not about hunting huge managed farms or doing fenced hunts. For the team it’s about memories and friendships made in the field and capturing their story on video, the Cervicide story. Although the team doesn’t hunt big managed properties often seen on TV, they are by no means amateurs. Many deer are taken on public ground that is pressured, along with some small tracts of private ground that isn’t large enough for proper management. Each year the team harvests some incredible trophies given these conditions, the very conditions that millions of hunters in America can relate to! Team Cervicide is ready to bring forth some awesome hunting footage and relate to their fellow hunters. Team Cervicide would like to be your premier choice for online hunting videos.

Name: Children Conservationists Programme (CCP)
Description:
We are a non-profit organisation aiming at promoting sustainable conservation of the national parks and game reserves in Uganda and Rwanda through empowerment, leadership development, and educational support for the children and the youth within the borderline of these resources. 
We believe in conservation, not only because it protects native plants, birds, and animals, but also because it protects us!

Name: Rhododendron Species Botanical Garden
Description:
The Rhododendron Species Botanical Garden, located between Seattle and Tacoma, is a 22-acre woodland garden, and home the largest collection of Rhododendron species in the world. With a Victorian stumpery, Rutherford Conservatory, pond garden, extensive fern collection, rock garden, and more, visitors will experience a diverse botanical collection in a PNW forest setting. The garden is home to beautiful companion plants, including the famous Himalayan Blue Poppies, Camellias, Magnolias, Japanese maples, and many rare plants.

As a living museum, the RSBG conserves and shares its international collection with both the scientific community and the general public. Known worldwide for the garden’s contributions to scholarship and research, the RSBG’s educational and scientific offerings include a semi-annual Rhododendron species symposium, workshops, seed and pollen shipment, and a yearly catalog with up-to-the-minute findings.

The RSBG Visitor Center features interpretive information about the garden and a year-round nursery with plants that have been propagated and cultivated in our own production greenhouses. Annual events include the Spring Plant Sale, Mother’s Day Weekend, Blue Poppy Day, and Fall Foliage Festival.

Name: Nebraska Grazing Lands Coalition
Description:
Dedicated to the enhancement of grasslands, the Nebraska Grazing Lands Coalition is comprised of ranchers and private landowners all focused on creating public awareness and improvement of the grazing lands in Nebraska. The organization’s top focus is to provide voluntary technical assistance and educational opportunities on grazing land management. Healthy Nebraska grazing lands translate directly into forage for livestock, habitat for wildlife, economic benefits for landowners and rural communities, and clean water for much of the Great Plains.

The NGLC is a non-profit organization funded solely by grant money. Objectives of the volunteer board are to strengthen partnerships, promote volunteer assistance and participation, respect private property rights, and encourage diversification to achieve and promote education. The organization co-sponsors numerous statewide grazing conferences, workshops, tours and seminars to pursue common interests with other grazing groups.

NGLC is the state entity of the national Grazing Land Conservation Initiative — which is supported by the USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service to enhance privately owned grazing lands in the nation.

Name: Our Eco Planet
Description: Our marine conservation expeditions provide a unique opportunity for individuals to actively engage in protecting biodiversity and endangered species. By bridging the gap between scientists and citizens, we offer hands-on experiences where participants contribute meaningfully to conservation efforts. Each expedition is a chance to make a tangible impact, learn new skills, and connect with a network of passionate, like-minded individuals. Join us in protecting our ocean and making a difference.

Name: Yakona Nature Preserve & Learning Center
Description: We are focused on education, restoration, & preservation through the arts, education, & stewardship.

Name: WWF Environment & Disaster Management Program
Description: The World Wildlife Fund (WWF) Environment and Disaster Management program collaborates with humanitarian nongovernmental organizations, government agencies, the United Nations, and academic institutions to integrate environmental considerations into disaster recovery, reconstruction, and risk reduction programs and policies. The initiative was formed during the aftermath of the 2004 Indian Ocean Tsunami. In the years since, it has provided support and training for crafting new approaches to environmentally responsible disaster management worldwide.

Name: Upper Waitematā Ecology Network
Description:
The purpose of the Upper Waitematā Ecology Network is to provide a forum for collaboration, sharing, support and joint action by community restoration groups in the Upper Waitematā / Upper Harbour Local Board area.

We aspire to be an effective and sustainable network that is valued by its members and other stakeholders and acknowledged widely as a force for environmental restoration and enhancement of the Upper Waitematā area.

Name: Hi Mountain Partners
Description:
At Hi Mountain Partners (HMP), we help Owners and Asset Managers accomplish 2 things.

1.  We reduce costs by up to 50%
2.  We increase the valuation of your Portfolio of Assets by $1-6 Million

We also partner with new and existing multi-family property owners, retirement community developers, commercial, industrial, non-profit and REITs throughout North America. We work closely with our asset managers during their site acquisition and due diligence phases.

Name: Eco Life
Description: Reviving Biodiversity Made Simply via Pollinator Habitats

Name: Wild Rutland
Description:
Imagine a place where your senses come alive with the sights and sounds of the natural world, and everything that it has to offer.

Imagine an experience where all of this and more is open to delight and inspire every generation of your family.

Welcome to Wild Rutland - the destination for the curious adventurer.

Name: Jeunesse Camerounaise pour l'Action Climatique
Description: JLAC est une initiative regroupant les jeunes issus de la Lekie ou y résidant pour réfléchir sur des moyens décisifs et innovants permettant à notre génération d’apporter des réponses concrètes aux enjeux climatiques affectant le sol, l’hydrographie, le relief, la flore et la faune de ce grand département. Notamment les problèmes associés, tels que la déforestation, la pression foncière et son corollaire qui est l’appauvrissement des terres cultivables et la disparition des espèces fauniques, l’agriculture non durable, la baisse de la pluviométrie, etc.

Description: BACK TO THE WILD INC is a 501(c)3 non-profit wildlife rehabilitation and nature education center located at 4504 Bardshar Rd. in Castalia, OH.

Name: Reserva El Potrero
Description: Somos el área protegida con mayor extensión de Entre Ríos, Argentina. En El Potrero trabajamos todos los días para promover la convivencia entre producción y conservación, con un modelo de desarrollo sostenible en el tiempo, que protege los ambientes naturales y su biodiversidad.

Name: SETVaD Recyclage
Description:
Startup Ecologique de Transformation et de Valorisation des Déchets.

Pour les entreprises de BTP, les collectivités locales et les établissements scolaires qui cherchent à réduire leur empreinte écologique tout en gérant efficacement leurs déchets plastiques, SETVaD Recyclage est une solution complète de collecte, tri et recyclage des déchets plastiques qui transforme ces déchets en matériaux réutilisables écologiques pour la construction ou l'aménagement, contribuant ainsi à l’économie circulaire. Contrairement aux services traditionnels de gestion des déchets qui se limitent souvent à l'élimination ou à l'enfouissement, notre solution favorise la circularité des ressources, tout en respectant strictement les réglementations environnementales et en encourageant la participation citoyenne.

Name: Zijn R
Description:
Zijn R. specialiseert zich in soorteninventarisaties van vleermuizen, gierzwaluwen en huismussen. Onze onderzoeken worden uitgevoerd door getrainde professionals en volgen altijd de wettelijke protocollen.

We combineren expertise met maatwerk en zetten ons in voor de bescherming van deze bijzondere soorten.

Name: BUND Naturschutz Kreisgruppe München
Description: Die Kreisgruppe München ist mit über 30.000 Mitgliedern die größte Kreisgruppe des BUND Naturschutz in Bayern e.V.  Sie ist in ganz unterschiedlichen Bereichen tätig wie: allgemeine Umweltberatung, Flächenpflege, Arten- und Baumschutz, Wiedervernässung von Mooren, Wespen- und Hornissenberatung, Umweltbildung an Kitas und Schulen, Biodiversitätsberatung sowie Stellungnahmen zu Planverfahren und auch im Bereich ökologischer Ernährung und nachhaltiger Mobilität ist die Kreisgruppe tätigt. Geleitet wird die Kreisgruppe München und ihre 20 Ortsgruppen in Stadt- und Landkreis von ehrenamtlichen Vorständen, die Kreisgruppe wird darüber hinaus durch Hauptamtliche unterstützt. Dank parteipolitischer und finanzieller Unabhängigkeit kann sich die Kreisgruppe München eine starke unabhängige Position im öffentlichen und politischen Diskurs erlauben.

Name: Project Avigate
Description: Project Avigate is a student-led initiative from Nanyang Technological University (NTU), with the aim of reducing bird-building collisions on campus and across Singapore. We have been actively working towards a win-win situation through our monitoring, research, and stakeholder engagements. Our vision is to create a vibrant built environment where wildlife and people coexist in harmony.

Name: Wahoo Bay & Shipwreck Park
Description:
Wahoo Bay: A Living Laboratory for Marine Education and Conservation

Wahoo Bay is a dynamic hub for marine education, research, and digital engagement. As a living laboratory, it merges hands-on learning, scientific inquiry, and innovative outreach to promote ocean conservation and STEM literacy.

Located at Roy L. Rogers Family Park – Hillsboro Inlet, Wahoo Bay – A Marine Educational Park provides immersive, research-based experiences for students from pre-K through college. Through guided snorkeling excursions and educational activities, visitors explore marine ecosystems and gain awareness of ocean conservation.

Students can deepen their experience at the Wahoo Bay Ocean Sciences & Conservation Lab, housed at Florida Atlantic University’s A.D. Henderson Campus. The lab involves students in studying marine pollutants, such as microplastics, and developing conservation strategies.

Wahoo Bay’s Online Educational Portal extends its reach globally, offering live data from underwater sensors, real-time environmental monitoring, virtual curriculum tools, and livestream access to its 360-degree self-cleaning underwater camera.

In partnership with Florida Atlantic University, Wahoo Bay creates a unique marine research environment. One highlight is the use of SEAHIVES—eco-friendly, hexagonal concrete structures that reduce shoreline erosion, enhance marine habitats, and provide educational snorkel trails for visitors and researchers.

The park’s high-resolution underwater camera streams 24/7 video and audio, complemented by weather and water quality sensors. This real-time data supports research, education, and public engagement from anywhere in the world.

Partnerships with organizations like the Ocean First Institute and the Stiles-Nicholson STEM Teacher Academy extend Wahoo Bay’s impact into classrooms. These collaborations empower educators with hands-on and virtual STEM learning, enabling inquiry-based teaching that inspires the next generation of scientists and e

Name: PRO NATUR - BIODIVERSITY
Description:
Companies, banks, investors, non-profit organizations and private individuals should reinvest part of their profits in preserving our environment so that we can still find a future worth living in tomorrow.
Converting financial capital into natural capital is the solution to preserve living quality for us and to secure well-being for future generations. As a sustainable dividend, nature provides us with vital ecosystem services.
Invest in PRO-NATUR 'Natural Capital Shares'to protect homeland ECOSYSTEMS!

Name: Blue Religion
Description: Blue Religion is an ocean conservation organization that generates profit from organizing unique expeditions to encounter whales, sharks, and much more. With our volunteers, we organize beach, ocean, and land cleanups. We have kids education programs about ocean conservation and we are working on our shark conservation campaign in collaboration with fishermen in Nayarit.

Name: Oak Creek Watershed Council
Description: The Oak Creek Watershed Council is a nonprofit dedicated to preserving the health and integrity of Oak Creek in northern Arizona. We leverage stewardship, science, education, and the power of volunteers to protect its flowing waters and surrounding red rocks.

Description:

the world’s northernmost population of the jaguar,
its unique natural habitats,
and native wildlife under its protection
as a flagship, keystone, and umbrella species.

Bird Treatment and Learning Center
Description: Bird Treatment and Learning Center's mission is to care and advocate for Alaska's wild birds through rehabilitation, education, and outreach.

Name: PLANETHER
Description:
help businesses, governments, and non-profit organizations tackle sustainability issues the climate crisis, and wildlife protection.

Through advanced modelling, we help organizations understand their exposure to climate risk and provide advice on managing and mitigating that risk.

We provide analysis, advice, and support for people wishing to implement practical solutions in the workplace.

Name: JAMAICA CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT TRUST
Description:
Established in 1988, The Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust (JCDT) is a non-governmental organization and charity with a mandate to conserve Jamaica’s natural environment.

The activities of the JCDT centre on the 41,198 hectares (101, 313 acres) of rain and cloud forest that make up the Blue and John Crow Mountains National Park including it's core, Preservation Zone which covers 26,251 hectares (65,000 acres) and is inscribed as a World Heritage Site. In addition JCDT's work focuses on the approximately 28,494 hectares of land around the protected area called the Community Buffer Zone.

The Trust receives about 30% of the annual budget for the National Park from the Government of Jamaica through the National Resources Conservation Authority and the National Environment and Planning Agency. The JCDT relies on grants, donations and income from the National Park’s recreational areas to finance the full cost of the park management programme.

Since its inception, the JCDT has partnered with several bi- and multi-lateral agencies, foundations and businesses, including: American Bird Conservancy, Environmental Foundation of Jamaica, Inter-American Development Bank, Jamaican Energy Partners, Jamaica National Parks Trust Fund, MacArthur Foundation, Sandals Foundation and United States Agency for International Development on conservation initiatives for the National Park. The JCDT has over 25 years of experience developing and managing environmental projects.

Name: Sequens viam Naturae (SVN)
Description:
How can we pave the way for earth to breathe again? 

Sequens Viam Naturae (SVN) is on a mission to restore ecosystems, empower local communities, and offer transparent green investment opportunities. 

Current projects: Suriname Reforestation Project - Pilot Stage 

Find out more at www.svn.earth.

Name: Conserve Consultoria e Serviços Ambientais
Women Conserving Southern Africa
Description:
About Women Conserving Southern Africa

At Women Conserving Southern Africa (WCSA), we believe that empowering women is key to creating a sustainable future for both people and nature. Across 12 southern African countries, women are increasingly stepping into roles as community leaders, researchers, guides, rangers, and policy makers. Yet, opportunities to connect, exchange ideas, and collaborate across borders remain limited.

Name: Environmental Real Estate Solutions
Description:
Environmental Real Estate Solutions advances the pace of land conservation. As a conservation nonprofit and real estate expert, Jim Daus crafts and executes complex land protection programs, strategies, transactions. With over 25 years of experience, he provides additional capacity for nonprofit conservation organizations, communities, and public agencies to fulfil their land protection goals, combat climate change, secure environmental justice for all people, and protect natural habitat as it is meant to be.

Jim supports his clients with his unique combination of proficiencies in:

·  Innovative project development and partner collaborations

·  Complex, multi-party real estate transactions

·  Current conservation finance and fundraising tools
