Name: Missouri Hunting Heritage Federation, Inc.
Description:
The Missouri Hunting Heritage Federation, Inc. (MHHF) is a service-oriented organization of volunteers who are passionate about Missouri's hunting history.  MHHF introduces youth to an outdoor way of life featuring our hunting tradition, the shooting sports and the enjoyment of being in Missouri's natural landscape.

Through education and hands-on training, youth learn how to safely handle firearms, gain an understanding of wildlife conservation and are introduced to the many enjoyable aspects of Missouri's hunting heritage. 


MISSION
MHHF introduces youth to an outdoor way of life that features hunting and the shooting sports through free, weekend clinics. In this way we pass along Missouri’s hunting heritage in the best and safest way possible.


PURPOSE
MHHF exists to introduce youth and their families to the hunting experience.


VISION 
To create Missouri’s next generation of hunters.

VALUES 
Safety, Consistency, Integrity and Ethics

Name: Schwäbisches Streuobstparadies e.V.
Description:
Der Verein Schwäbisches Streuobstparadies e.V. wurde im Mai 2012 in Weilheim/Teck gegründet.
Die Landkreise Böblingen, Esslingen, Göppingen, Reutlingen, Tübingen und Zollernalbkreis, die Regierungspräsidien Stuttgart und Tübingen, zahlreiche Städte und Gemeinden, sowie Vereine, Initiativen, Bildungseinrichtungen und Betriebe aus den Handlungsfeldern Obst- und Gartenbau, Naturschutz, Tourismus, Dienstleistung und Produktion zählen zu unseren Mitgliedern und machen den Verein durch Ihre Kenntnisse und Kompetenzen ebenso vielfältig wie unsere Streuobstlandschaft.

Das übergeordnete Ziel des Vereins Schwäbisches Streuobstparadies ist der Erhalt und die Vermarktung der größten zusammenhängenden Streuobstlandschaft Mitteleuropas. Wir nutzen hierzu die vielen bestehenden Kompetenzen, Netzwerke und Aktivitäten unserer Vereinsmitglieder und führen sie  konsequent zusammen, um Synergien zu nutzen.

Name: Lizadeelrdc
Description: La vision de la LIZADEEL est celle d’une société où les personnes vulnérables, femmes et enfants, touchées par des violences sexuelles, des injustices sociales ou des sévices de la pauvreté ont la possibilité d’être entendues, prises en charge pour des soins médicaux, réhabilitées dans leur droit en tant que victimes et de bénéficier d’un accompagnement psycho-social pour une réinsertion socio-économique.

Description:
The Mufuti Sanctuary is a Zimbabwean sanctuary for sick, injured, and orphaned animals. Based 90 minutes outside of Harare in Mutorashanga, The Mufuti Sanctuary is aimed to successfully rescue and rehabilitate wildlife in Zimbabwe.

A 20 hectare piece of land has seen the start of this great venture and since a lot of hard work, dedication and determination goes into building this wildlife haven. The Mufuti Sanctuary will be home to many animals.

Name: The Water Warriors
Description:
At The Water Warriors, we are dedicated to protecting and preserving freshwater resources for future generations. Through advocacy, education, and community engagement, we inspire action and drive sustainable solutions to the global freshwater crisis.

Our mission is to empower communities, influence policy, and raise awareness about the critical importance of clean and accessible water. From presenting to councils and committees to pass regulations that safeguard freshwater, to speaking at schools and events to educate others about the crisis and share actionable solutions, we strive to create meaningful change. By collaborating with individuals, organizations, and governments, we are building a future where freshwater ecosystems thrive.

Join us in championing freshwater protection and sustainability—because every drop counts.

Learn more about our work at www.thewaterwarriors.ca or message us today to connect!

Name: Terravista
Description:
Terravista develops and supports applied biodiversity and carbon projects designed to deliver measurable outcomes and long term value for territories, communities and project partners.

Our work focuses on transforming ecological potential into structured, credible and actionable projects by integrating biodiversity conservation, carbon sequestration and other ecosystem functions. We support initiatives throughout their full lifecycle, from early planning and project design to development, implementation and strategic guidance.

While our project development activities are rooted in Canada, Terravista accompanies biodiversity and carbon initiatives internationally, adapting methodologies to local ecological, social and economic contexts.

We work closely with communities, municipalities, landowners, organizations and project developers facing complex environmental and territorial challenges. Our approach is grounded in ecological science, field experience and pragmatic decision making, ensuring projects are both environmentally sound and operationally viable.

Terravista positions biodiversity not as a constraint, but as a strategic asset, enabling conservation, territorial resilience and sustainable development through applied projects.

Name: Southern Idaho Reptile Rescue
Description: Southern Idaho Reptile Rescue was founded by reptile owners, for reptile owners (and their reptiles). We are a state licensed and federally recognized 501 (c)(3) nonprofit organization. Our primary mission is to educate and inspire owners on the wonders of reptile keeping. Additionally, we are dedicated to the rescue and preservation of all types of reptiles. When pet owners are unwilling or unable to tend to their animals for any reason, we step in and offer a safe place for rehabilitation and rehoming. Located in Buhl, Idaho, Southern Idaho Reptile Rescue specializes in offering top of the line care, education, and adoption services.

Name: LiFE Club Africa 254-Chapter

On the 6th of March 2025, Assistant Secretary-General - The Commonwealth, Prof Luis Franceschi officiated at the launch of the Kenyan Chapter of the LiFE Club. The development of the club is a project of Tsavo Heritage Foundation in Partnership with the Sarova Hotels and Resorts, Saracen Marketing and Marketing Africa with the purpose of Mainstreaming corporate titans in Africa into the active landscape restoration space.
The launch marked the official opening of the club’s membership registration that’s categorized into 4 distinct but mutually supporting Tiers and Platforms, these being 

Corporate TIER
-Corporate Tier 

Individual Tiers
- Patrons Tier 
- Champions Tier 
- Anchor Tier
- Tsoldiers Tier
For details on registration please get in touch with Info@tsavoheritage.com to facilitate your registration process.

The LiFE Club
LIFE Club is a specialized initiative focused on restoring and conserving Africa’s ecological treasures, starting in Kenya and Tanzania, particularly in the Southern Kenya region. Known for its unique wildlife, climate, and cultural heritage, this region faces increasing environmental challenges that put its future at risk. LIFE Club unites influential leaders from various sectors who are dedicated to fostering a sustainable legacy. By becoming part of this esteemed community, these individuals not only help protect one of Africa's most essential landscapes but also exemplify the spirit of environmental stewardship.
Vision 
To create a model for sustainable conservation starting with the Southern Kenya region, ultimately inspiring leadership-driven environmental stewardship across East Africa and beyond. 
Mission 
To unite and empower leaders to take collaborative action in protecting East Africa's natural landscapes, fostering innovative sustainable lifestyles, practices, and building a legacy of sustainability.

Core Values 
LEADERSHIP
Encouraging bold action to address critical lifestyle cha

Name: Ostréiculteur Traditionnel
Description:
OSTREICULTEUR TRADITIONNEL est une marque associative depuis 2007.

Des centaines d'ostréiculteurs répartis sur l'ensemble des bassins français se sont rassemblés pour défendre les huîtres naturelles, préserver la tradition de notre métier et l'environnement !

Nous luttons pour plus de transparence car vous avez le droit de choisir ce que vous mangez : des huitres naturelles VS des huitres issues de laboratoires.

Name: Syndicat de Lutte contre les Moustiques du Bas-Rhin (SLM67)
Description:
Le Syndicat de Lutte contre les Moustiques du Bas-Rhin (SLM 67) est l'opérateur public de démoustication dans le Bas-Rhin. Depuis plus de 40 ans, le SLM 67 organise les traitements au sein de ses communes membres avec pour objectifs :
    La réduction de la nuisance due aux moustiques
    Le respect des équilibres environnementaux
Depuis quelques années, le SLM 67 a été chargé par l'Agence Régionale de Santé Grand Est d'un nouvel objectif en lien avec les enjeux sanitaires actuels :
    La surveillance des espèces exotiques

Name: Bitterroot Water Partnership
Description: The Bitterroot Water Partnership leads reliable and intentional conservation initiatives that promote and provide clean, ample water for people and healthy habitats by working with key partners and our communities.

Name: Blue Hope Alliance
Description: The Blue Hope Alliance is a coalition of groups who have joined together to cooperate on citizen science surveys. Our citizen science surveys focus on mapping and recording the condition of maerl in the Wester Ross MPA.

Name: Syndicat mixte d'aménagement de la basse et moyenne vallée de l'Ognon
Description:
Le Syndicat Mixte d’Aménagements de la Moyenne et Basse Vallée de l’Ognon (SMAMBVO) ainsi que le syndicat intercommunautaire du bassin de la haute vallée de l’Ognon (SIBHVO) sont des établissements publics de coordination intercommunaux. 

Ils ont pour mission de coordonner et de réaliser des actions en faveur de la protection des milieux aquatiques et humides au sein de leur territoire. Depuis 2018, les Syndicats détiennent la compétence GEMAPI (Gestion des milieux aquatiques et prévention des inondations), qui leur a été transmise par les communautés de communes.  Dans ce cadre, les syndicats effectuent des restaurations de cours d’eau, permettant ainsi un retour de la biodiversité et une réduction des risques d’inondation, des travaux de restauration de la continuité écologique ou encore des analyses sur la qualité de l’eau des cours d’eau du territoire.

Name: Réseau des Jeunes Africains Apporteurs de Projets Innovants (RAJAPICOP-GUINÉE)
Regenerados
Description:
Empoderar a las personas a tomar acción y decisión en sus estilos de vida a favor de la sostenibilidad y la regeneración con información basada en la ciencia y en casos validados de prácticas sostenibles, costo-eficientes y con potencial de generar ahorro.

El propósito de Regenerados aborda una problemática clave: la falta de conocimiento accesible y práctico para adoptar estilos de vida sostenibles, especialmente en un contexto donde los desafíos globales como el cambio climático, la crisis económica y la incertidumbre exigen nuevas formas de actuar.

Problemáticas que Resuelve:

Falta de acceso a información confiable y práctica:
Muchas personas no saben cómo implementar prácticas sostenibles en su día a día o dudan de la efectividad de estas acciones. Existe una brecha entre el conocimiento científico sobre sostenibilidad y su aplicación práctica en la vida cotidiana.

Percepción de altos costos en la sostenibilidad:
La sostenibilidad a menudo se percibe como un lujo o algo costoso. Esto desalienta a quienes quieren adoptar estos hábitos pero temen el impacto en su economía.

Desconexión entre el impacto personal y global:
Las personas a menudo no ven cómo sus decisiones individuales pueden contribuir al cambio global. Esto genera pasividad y falta de acción en temas ambientales.

Falta de herramientas para tomar decisiones informadas:
Las personas carecen de guías claras que expliquen cómo ser sostenibles de forma costo-eficiente y, en algunos casos, generar retornos económicos.

Baja confianza en marcas y prácticas sostenibles:
La saturación de información y el greenwashing (marketing engañoso) generan escepticismo hacia las marcas que promueven la sostenibilidad.

Name: Waste-Free Wednesdays
Description:
Waste-Free Wednesdays inspires, educates and supports individuals and organizations on their journey to reducing their waste and then eliminating it.

Waste comes from many sources. It includes spoiled food that goes to landfill, clothing that is thrown away, debris from construction and renovation, disposal of electronic devices and much more. We often don’t think about how to reduce what we buy, purchase items that last longer or find products with less packaging. When items spoil or wear out, we often put them in the trash without thinking about alternatives for reuse, repair or recycling.

Waste-free Wednesday builds on the success of Meatless Mondays to tackle another critical issue in the climate change conundrum. Along with reducing greenhouse gas emissions, reducing or eliminating waste can help people lower their expenses and improve their health. We provide ideas, insights, examples, stories and support to help individuals and organizations reduce waste and move towards eliminating waste entirely.

Name: Canadian Organization for Tropical Education and Rainforest Conservation
Description:
Our mission is to advance the conservation of tropical ecosystems
through scientific research, education, and immersive learning that
empowers the next generation of environmental stewards.

Name: Observatoire Citoyen des Communs O2C
Description:
Collectif citoyen, espace d'échange, d'enrichissement mutuel, de réflexion et de recommandations. Les Communs, c’est aussi un mouvement, un idéal de réappropriation citoyenne de la gouvernance et de l’exploitation des ressources qui permettent la satisfaction des besoins essentiels afin de vivre en sécurité : se loger, se nourrir, se soigner, se déplacer, …

L’eau, l’air, le sol, l’alimentation, l’éducation, l’enfance, la démocratie, la mobilité, la monnaie…
- parce qu’ils sont à tous et à personne à la fois,
- parce qu’ils sont menacés et doivent être protégés,
- parce qu’on vivra mieux s’il on en prend soin.

Parce qu'il n'y a pas de Planète "B."

Name: Emergent Reefs Foundation
Description: Our mission is to advance tools and technologies that rehabilitate coral reefs as whole ecosystems, using location-specific, ecologically-sound principles that support the way they naturally function and adapt.

Name: Lights Out Connecticut
Description: Lights Out Connecticut is building a coalition of property owners, local businesses, nonprofits, and local officials to protect migratory birds in our state. We work together to ensure that all nonessential lights are turned off or dimmed at night during the weeks of spring and fall migration (Apr 1–May 31 and Aug 15–Nov 15). Participation is completely voluntary, self-monitored, and self-assessed.

Name: Actions pour l'Avenir des Forêts du Bassin du Congo (AFORCO)
Description:
Nous  œuvrons dans les domaines de recherche et développement en vue de la promotion de la gestion durable des ressources naturelles, notamment dont forestières en de renforcer la résilience des populations dépendantes de ces ressources à l’ère des enjeux climatiques.
La vision de l’AFORCO est de rendre les écosystèmes forestiers plus résilients pour la durabilité de leurs services vitaux. Notre devise est: Loyauté-Union-Prospérité pour tous. Dans dimension écosystémique et la dimension One health pour garantir le développement durable en faveur des peuples dépendants des forêts.

The DNCTF aims to catalyze growth capital for nature-based companies, State/ Local Governments and Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) in the form of grants to augment the preservation, conservation and management of Dominica’s biodiversity. The Fund seeks to realize the goal of building viable, sustainable systems that contribute to Dominica’s development impact for years to come.

ODLC works with landowners in northern Virginia to place conservation easements on their properties in order to protect agricultural land, native habitat, wetlands, and riverine assets.

Name: Muck & Magic
Description:
Permaculture: teaching, mentoring, consulting. 

We help nature-lovers grow well: at home and at work; in their communities and in their teams. 

Our services include:
- Permaculture Courses (e.g. teaching 'Intro to Permaculture' training)

Name: Resilient Indiana: Technical Assistance for SWCDs
Description:
We provide soil and water resource-related technical assistance to agricultural, woodland, and urban land users within a regional team consisting of members of local, state, and federal partners. Our staff primarily focus on SWCD projects. 

Their assistance will take many forms, ultimately facilitating the implementation of measures that protect and enhance the quality of soil, water, and other natural resources.

RITA is an initiative of the Indiana Association of Soil and Water Conservation Districts (IASWCD), an Indiana-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to enabling the conservation of Indiana’s natural resources through the combined support of its 92 member Soil and Water Conservation Districts (SWCDs).

RITA is made possible through the support of the USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service (NRCS) and the Clean Water Indiana (CWI) program.

Name: Anti kraak door bijen
Description:
Anti kraak door bijen is een initiatief dat zich richt op het tijdelijk transformeren van 250 miljoen m2 ongebruikte, braakliggende terreinen in bloeiende habitats voor bijen. 

Onze missie is het creëren van een duurzame toekomst, door projectontwikkelaars, grondbeheerders, gemeenten en individuen samen te brengen rond een gemeenschappelijke visie. 

Volg ons op LinkedIn voor updates over onze projecten!

Name: Road to Recovery
Description: Road to Recovery (R2R) is a bird population recovery initiative dedicated to restoring North America’s most rapidly declining bird species, called "Tipping Point Species," by bringing biological and social scientists, land managers, governments, conservation organizations, and communities together to pinpoint the causes of decline and co-produce practical, targeted conservation strategies. Through collaborative partnerships and innovative tools like our Recovery Progress Wheel, R2R supports more than 50 species recovery teams across the hemisphere to turn science into real conservation impact.

Name: Syndicat Mixte du Bassin de la Seudre
Description: Établissement public de coopération locale en charge de la gestion des milieux aquatiques du bassin de la Seudre

Name: DelAtlantic Conservation District
Description:
The DelAtlantic Conservation District is a regional government agency dedicated to the conservation and responsible management of natural resources in Atlantic, Cape May and Camden Counties, New Jersey. As one of 14 soil conservation districts in the state, our mission is to protect soil, water, and related environmental resources through education, technical assistance, and regulatory programs.

Established in 1946, the District implements the New Jersey Soil Erosion and Sediment Control Act (Chapter 251) and works in partnership with landowners, developers, farmers, municipalities, and state and federal agencies to promote sustainable land use and natural resource protection. Our work spans a variety of focus areas, including soil erosion control, stormwater management, farmland preservation, forestry, watershed protection, and conservation education.

Our team provides technical services, field inspections, GIS mapping support, plan certifications, and guidance on conservation best practices for both public and private projects. We also support schools and community groups through educational programming, classroom presentations, public workshops, and student engagement activities such as the annual Poster Contest and the New Jersey Envirothon.

With a growing Community Forestry Program and active involvement in statewide conservation initiatives, the Cape Atlantic Conservation District continues to serve as a vital resource for protecting the region’s environmental health while supporting responsible development and community resilience.

Learn more about our programs, partnerships, and career opportunities at www.capeatlantic.org

Name: Hai Reef Foundation
Description: We are run by a team of action-oriented, fully qualified, and optimistic individuals who care deeply about Indonesia’s Coral Reefs. Through our Coral Reef Restoration and Community Outreach Programs, we innovate, collaborate, and scientifically validate. We take the community with us on this journey.

Name: Belitong UNESCO Global Geopark
Description: Akun Resmi Badan Pengelola Belitong UNESCO Global Geopark

Name: Ricci Hall Archives Conservation & Acquisition Project (RHACAP)
Description: The Ricci Hall Archives Conservation and Acquisition Project (RHACAP), The University of Hong Kong was established in 2016 with the endorsements by Ricci Hall, Ricci Hall Students’ Association (RHSA), and Ricci Hall Old Boys’ Association, The University of Hong Kong, with the following aims: (1) To conserve and acquire physical and digital artefacts related to Ricci Hall; (2) To prepare for the publications commemorating the anniversary celebrations of Ricci Hall, including data collection and analysis, interviews, editing and publishing; (3) To organize professional training programs and co-organize learning activities and social events closely related to hall identity and values.

Name: Zoo Herpetology Association
Description: The Zoo Herpetology Association supports zoo herpetologists by providing collaborative partnerships, networking and career development opportunities, and small grants for conservation and research with the ultimate goal of conserving herpetofauna and habitats.

Name: Welsh Historic Gardens Trust
Description:
The Welsh Historic Gardens Trust (WHGT) is a national conservation and heritage charity set up to protect and conserve the historic parks and gardens in Wales.

The WHGT campaigns to save historic gardens and parks from neglect, indifference, insensitive planning and inappropriate planting for future generations.

Wales has many outstanding parks and gardens which are as much part of the Welsh heritage as the buildings and towns they were designed to complement.
WHGT aims to promote the cultural importance of these sites.

WHGT  aims to:

Raise the profile of parks and garden heritage in Wales which make such a rich and varied contribution to our landscape.
Promote interest and enjoyment in the beauty and diversity of Welsh parks and gardens including the famous and less well-known, large and small, formal and picturesque, to the widest audience to ensure their survival for future generations.
Promote the restoration and conservation of threatened parks and gardens, and those At Risk of becoming lost gardens, which are of special historic interest in Wales.
Research and document the garden history of Wales, an important element of the historic and cultural identity of the Nation.
The Trust promotes the enjoyment and understanding of all these landscapes through its program of visits, lectures and research activities.

Trust members enjoy a varied and interesting programme of events, including:

Garden visits
Lectures and Study Days
Researching the history of local gardens
Monitoring planning applications
Fund raising and publicity
Raising awareness of the garden heritage of Wales
Garden surveys
Practical conservation work
Please join if you are interested in the garden and parkland heritage of Wales.

Name: National Malleefowl Recovery Group Inc
Description:
The National Malleefowl Recovery Group (NMRG) has been operating since 1989 and has representatives from ACT, NSW, SA, Vic & WA.

The main purpose of the National Malleefowl Recovery Group is carrying out the National Recovery Plan to help save the endangered Malleefowl in all regions of Australia.

One of the main actions being done by the NMRG is monitoring the health of Malleefowl populations across Australia to see what impact actions such as predator control and fire management have on breeding populations and species numbers.

Name: Wild Vision Systems
Description: Our unique, custom technology enables the creation of streamlined, scalable solutions to control of a wide variety of animal species. We bring specialized skills, experience, and a commitment to ecological restoration and conservation.

Name: Deadwater Valley Trust
Description:
The organisation was set up in the mid 1980's.

The Trust has charitable status and manages the Deadwater Valley Local Nature Reserve and Bordon Inclosure on behalf of Whitehill Town Council, which includes areas of land in the Deadwater Valley, for quiet recreation and conservation of habitat.

The Trust also manage Hogmoor Inclosure in partnership with The Land Trust on behalf of the Whitehill And Bordon Regeneration Trust.

Name: Stichting Ecologisch Groen Utrecht-West
Description: Stichting Ecologisch Groen Utrecht-West (SEGUW) brengt de natuur in de stad. Dat doen we door groengebieden ecologisch verantwoord in te richten en te beheren. Inheemse planten en dieren krijgen zo weer een kans.  We doen dit samen met de gemeente Utrecht, omwonenden en andere geïnteresseerden. Beheersactiviteiten van SEGUW worden mede mogelijk gemaakt door een bijdrage uit het leefbaarheidsbudget van de gemeente Utrecht.

Name: Wildlife Inventory Services & Enrichment
Description: Wildlife Inventory Services provides quality consultation to landowners in Southern Montana and beyond. We provide quality analysis of land and provide recommendations for management programs that will enhance the value of your land. We specialize in avian and plant inventory and surveys (native & invasive) and can customize our services to fit the expectations of landowners.

Name: AANE - Bangalore
Description:
AANE was launched on World Elephant day, August 12th 2022 with the objective of championing the welfare of elephants, united in our passion to create awareness and find harmonious ways to coexist with elephants and nature.
We aim to research and bring forth empathetic indigenous models of working with these gentle giants, while creating general awareness around the evolved nature of Asian elephants.

Name: BeeWildProject
Description:
Wir sind das BeeWildProject! Wusstest du schon, dass durch Verstädterung, Überdüngung und Übernutzung von Agrarflächen viele Wildbienenarten massiv vor dem Aussterben gefährdet sind?

Deswegen haben wir uns einer besonderen Mission verschrieben: Dem Schutz der Wildbienen. Dafür wollen wir am Fuße von Strommasten wildbienenfreundliche Lebensräume schaffen. Mit der Anpflanzung von regionalen Wildblumen und Stauden bringen wir mehr Farbe ins Spiel und schaffen einen Ort, an dem Wildbienen ein neues Zuhause finden.

Name: Minnesota Steelheader
Description:
Minnesota Steelheader (MS) is a non-profit conservation organization bringing awareness to Minnesota's North Shore Steelhead, Salmon, and Trout fisheries by  informing, educating, and entertaining veterans and new-comers alike to this great  Lake Superior fishery.

The mission of Minnesota Steelheader is to inform, educate and entertain anglers about the Lake Superior Steelhead, Salmon, and Trout fisheries of Minnesota's North Shore Streams and Rivers, primarily through a content rich website and a committed volunteer staff working passionately within the community, on-line and on the water.

Name: The Clovelly Estate Company Ltd
Description: Clovelly Estate Company is a family run business that encompasses Clovelly Village, Clovelly Court Gardens, the Red Lion Hotel, the surrounding woodlands and coastline. CEC’s aim is to protect and develop the unique charm and beauty of Clovelly and preserve it for future generations to come. Whilst we welcome tourists from the UK and overseas, both as day visitors to the village, and overnight stays in the Red Lion. Clovelly is also a living, working community, with many families having lived in the village for over 100 years.

Name: CoNFine
Description:
[EN] Preserving cultural heritage is vital for many European citizens, organisations, and countries, as it is deeply tied to their identities, traditions, histories, and economies. However, heritage buildings constructed from stone are particularly vulnerable to damage from microorganisms, which can cause chemical, physical, and aesthetic deterioration. The use of toxic biocides to combat these microorganisms poses risks to both the environment and human health, while current sustainable alternatives often lack efficacy and stability. Supported by the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions programme, the CoNFine project aims to develop a range of natural-based solutions for mitigating bio-deterioration. These new treatments will offer high specificity, improved environmental and operational safety, and the effectiveness needed to protect heritage stone buildings for future generations

[SP] La conservación del patrimonio cultural es fundamental para muchos ciudadanos, organizaciones y países europeos, ya que está profundamente ligada a su identidad, tradiciones, historia y economía. Sin embargo, los edificios patrimoniales construidos en piedra son especialmente vulnerables al daño causado por los microorganismos, que pueden provocar un deterioro químico, físico y estético. El uso de biocidas tóxicos para combatir estos microorganismos supone un riesgo tanto para el medio ambiente como para la salud humana, mientras que las alternativas sostenibles actuales suelen carecer de eficacia y estabilidad. Con el apoyo del programa Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions, el proyecto CoNFine tiene como objetivo desarrollar una serie de soluciones naturales para mitigar el biodeterioro. Estos tratamientos ofrecerán una alta especificidad, mayor seguridad medioambiental y operativa, y la eficacia necesaria para proteger los edificios patrimoniales de piedra para futuras generaciones.

Grant agreement ID: 101209275

Name: Adept Conservation Network (ACN)
Description: Adept Conservation Network (ACN) is a Non-Governmental and not-for-profit organization by a team of Kenyans who are qualified with years of experience in the field of conservation. The Organization seeks to provide practical, evidence, and nature-based solutions to conservation problems for the sustainability of nature as well as of communities dependent on the environment they live in. Over the years, nature has been faced with numerous challenges leading to the loss and even extinction of some species due to the inability to cope with the changes.

Name: Foster Creek Conservation District
Description: To protect natural resources, enhance sustainable, profitable agriculture, and improve the quality of life in Douglas County; by encouraging good land stewardship through education, incentives, and quality technical assistance. Protecting individual and private property rights and building strong coalitions with other groups, including but not limited to, government assistance as a valid conservation partner to agriculture by providing quality, intelligent, technical assistance to cooperators and partners.

Name: Bye Bye Lionfish
Description: By the start of 2020, Wasteless World was getting ready to start building its first community-run recycling station in a small coastal town in Colombia. Unfortunately due to the worldwide Covid-19 pandemic, the project was put on hold. During the lockdown, our founder Tom was stranded on an island off the Caribbean coast of Panama known as Bocas Del Toro. This area not only needed a system like the one he had planned but also had a community calling for change. By the end of 2022, Wasteless World had helped build the community's first recycling center and set up a factory converting the community's post-consumer waste into synthetic lumber, sequestering tonnes of otherwise wasted plastics funding the whole operation, and more. We are committed to helping reconnect people with the community and our environment to drive a movement towards a cleaner, healthier future for all.

Name: Lateteatoto
Description:
Lateteatoto est une association de loli 1901, née d'une envie profonde de faire changer nos modes de consommation. Surconsommation, surremballages, made in ToFarAway... autant de points sur lesquels il est possible de changer. Fabriquer ses produits d'entretien, acheter local, acheter éthique, et sans plastique.
Laeteteatoto propose un répertoire de commerces inscrits dans une démarche éco-responsables, mais propose également un espace collaboratif sur lequel partager recettes et astuces devient possible!

Name: LureonX
Description:
A LureonX é uma fábrica de tokens ambientais regenerativos, lastreados em atributos reais como carbono, água, solo, biodiversidade e polinização.

Atuamos como ponte entre o campo e o capital, integrando ciência, blockchain e governança para transformar territórios regenerados em ativos de valor rastreável.

Somos mais do que tecnologia: somos uma convocação ética para a nova economia da integridade ecológica.

 Missão: Regenerar valor natural através da ciência, reputação e confiança.
 Modelo: Certificação científica + Score Regenerativo (SRL) + Risco (LRF) + Tokens LURE + Governança ESG e Burn.

 Porque regenerar é mais do que preservar — é investir no que importa.

Name: MOZ SNAKE & REPTILE RESCUE
Description: À MOZ SNAKE & REPTILE RESCUE (MSRR) é uma empresa sociedade unipessoal que presta serviços de resgate em todo Moçambique, tanto na comunidade como para empresas. Também realizamos avaliações de risco relacionadas a cobras, consultamos e desenvolvemos procedimentos de conflitos entre pessoas e cobras, e promovendo a preservação da herpetofauna moçambicana, bem como contribuir para a conservação da classe de criaturas designada por "reptilia". As actividades educativas e de conservação devem ser realizadas em conformidade com a Lei da Vida Selvagem de moçambique. e fornecemos equipamentos de resgate.

Name: Dente-de-leão
Description: Você sabia que quando a gente sopra um dente-de-leão cada fiapo daqueles que voa é uma semente? O vento transporta e espalha essas sementes, fazendo nascer outras flores onde elas caem. Nossa intenção é fazer como essa flor e dispersar valores como empatia, dignidade, transformação e solidariedade. Cada boa ação ajuda a espalhar o bem, e assim acreditamos que aos poucos construiremos um mundo melhor!

Name: West Africa Conservation Alliance
Description: West Africa Conservation Alliance (WACA) is a nature conservation NGO that seeks to explore and understand conservation problems through research in West Africa and devise sustainable solutions to those problems.

Name: Green and clean
Description: GREEN AND CLEAN est un club socio-environnemental qui s’intéresse à l’environnement de la ville Béni Mellal et ses environs, ce club a pour but l’instauration d’une éducation environnementale dans la ville en général et notre école en particulier, on compte mener plusieurs actions comme le boisement, la collecte des déchets, des campagnes de sensibilisations dans les écoles primaires, et encore plus d’activités.

Name: Association "Roots and Dreams"
Description:
This association aims to:
- Promote our relationship with nature within the agricultural world;
- Encourage active hospitality of wild life and biodiversity in all its forms (fauna, flora, habitat);
- Support reflections on new modes of agriculture based in particular on
collective organization and agroecology.

This approach aims to fight against the massive erosion of biodiversity
in a context of climate change

Name: ECF Caucasus
Description: The Eco-Corridor Fund for the Caucasus (ECF) is a financial instrument dedicated to supporting biodiversity conservation in the diverse landscapes of the Southern Caucasus through contractual nature conservation. By funding ecologically sustainable land use in selected eco-corridors in the Caucasus, the fund actively contributes to connecting protected areas and enhancing ecological sustainability while ensuring the socio-economic status of communities is not harmed and in many cases is improved.

Name: Chelonia, Investigación y Conservación de Tortugas Marinas
Taman Nasional Manupeu Tanah Daru dan Laiwangi Wanggameti
Description: Balai Taman Nasional Matalawa merupakan Unit Pelaksana Teknis (UPT) pada Direktorat Jenderal KSDAE, Kementerian LHK yang mengelola kawasan konservasi di Pulau Sumba

Name: Animal Activists Kenya
Description: We are a non-profit, child-run initiative founded to reduce issues caused by human-wildlife conflict – including habitat loss and bush-meat hunting. We raise money to support various conservation projects, charities and organizations in their efforts to preserve Kenya’s Wildlife and Natural Habitats.

Name: Treemer gemeinnützige GmbH
Description: Treemer verfolgt neue Ansätze in der Schaffung klimaresilienter, artenreicher Wälder  in Deutschland. Neben Aufforstungsprojekten umfasst dies auch gemeinsame Pflanztage, Firmenevents und Waldexkursionen in der landschaftlich reizvollen Umgebung der Fränkischen Schweiz.

Name: Pelindung Alam Nusantara
Description: Indonesian company advancing biodiversity and forest carbon investments through Nature-Based Solutions.

Name: G7 EcoCarbon
Description: A G7 EcoCarbon é uma empresa com mais de 30 anos com expertise em consultoria, monitoramento e desenvolvimento de projetos Créditos de Carbono, sendo a única com Tecnologia Satélite que faz mensuração a partir do banco de dados da NASA e ESA para Carbono Vegetal.

Name: Center For A Sustainable Coast
Description: Center for a Sustainable Coast is a non-profit environmental advocacy organization devoted to conserving, protecting, restoring, and sustainably using the natural resources of coastal Georgia.  The Center diligently supports science, policy reform, and the law to pursue its mission, frequently publishing commentary on relevant public issues in various media outlets.

Name: BSO KST Herbiforus
Description: Badan Semi Otonom Kelompok Studi Tumbuhan Herbiforus merupakan organisasi di bawah naungan Himabio FMIPA UNY yang berfokus untuk mempelajari keilmuan dan keterampilan pengelolaan tumbuhan. Kegiatannya seperti eksplorasi, konservasi, pemanfaatan, dan penelitian seputar tumbuhan.

Name: Southeastern Plant Conservation Alliance
Description:
The Southeastern Plant Conservation Alliance (SE PCA) is a cross-cutting partnership of public and private conservation professionals working in the Southeastern United States. The SE PCA seeks to bridge gaps between local and national efforts by fostering regional cooperation and promoting a diversity of partners. The Alliance is tailored to multiple interests to provide training opportunities, fill information gaps, identify conservation needs, prioritize efforts, and work collaboratively to conserve imperiled plants. This is achieved by adapting successful models, novel partnerships, and creative solutions to challenges while leveraging shared resources.

The SE PCA was informally launched during the 2nd Southeastern Partners in Plant Conservation event in March 2020 and formalized through virtual meetings and planning throughout that year. Conference breakout sessions created a forum to share and build skills for collaboration, communication, fundraising, and volunteers. Small-group skills assessment workshops formed the basis for larger group sessions led by a professional facilitator that sought to identify regional needs and how they can be met by a formal partnership. We were prompted to consider our biggest challenges and what success could look like for such a collaborative network, including solutions to our shared challenges.

Name: Ember Global
Description:
We help conservation and impactful companies around the world flourish so our world can flourish too.
Experience across >a dozen startups from forestry to giant ocean fans. 

We've worked with companies in North America, Africa, South America. 
Industries span climate tech and international development, and include tech, manufacturing, CCS and ocean carbon, mining, marine technologies, water and sanitation, biotech and synthetic biology, forestry, recycling, sustainability, MRV and AI. 

Deep experience with fundraising, market assessments, strategy, product-market-fit, and growth.

Name: Milieuwerkgroep Houten
Description: De Milieuwerkgroep Houten is een natuurvereniging voor Houten en omgeving. Wij houden ons bezig met uitvoerend natuurbehoud, maken jou in de praktijk enthousiast voor onze lokale natuur en laten onze stem horen richting beleidsmakers.

Name: Principauté LE BRUSC
Description:
Nous voulons connaître l'état de santé de la Lagune du Brusc (Six Fours les plages - VAR 83) et pousser toutes les initiatives pour la restauration de ce milieu naturel pour, à terme, voir le retour de hippocampes. La Lagune du Brusc est un site UNIQUE qui est le seul lagon fermé par un récif-barrière de posidonie en Méditerranée. Sa protection est un objectif majeur pour notre objectif final : la réintroduction d'hippocampes . Aidez-nous : Est-ce que vous avez vu des hippocampes au Brusc ? Pouvez-vous partager vos expériences avec nous ? Avez-vous observé des agressions contre la Lagune ? Avez-vous des solutions à proposez ? 
Au plaisir de vous rencontrer...

Name: Ecole du chat Libre du Parisis
Description:
L'Ecole du Chat Libre du Parisis est une association de protection animale à but non lucratif, située dans le Val d’Oise.
Notre action consiste à venir en aide et à limiter la prolifération des chats errants en les faisant stériliser, soigner, tatouer et identifier pour ensuite trouver des familles d'adoption pour les chats les plus sociables. Les autres chats sont gardés à l'Ecole du Chat ou relâchés dans leur milieu naturel, sous la protection de bénévoles qui les nourrissent.
L'association s’organise autour d’une chatterie et de familles d’accueil. Elle est financée par les adhésions et par des dons.
Nous recherchons continuellement des bénévoles et des familles d'accueil.

Name: NAJU NRW
Description: Die Naturschutzjugend (NAJU) NRW ist die eigenständige Jugendorganisation des NABU. Sie ist mit über 20.000 Mitgliedern in NRW der führende Jugendverband in der außerschulischen Umweltbildung, sowie im  praktischen Naturschutz. In 130 Gruppen vor Ort setzen sich Kinder und Jugendliche bis 27 Jahre für den Natur- und Umweltschutz ein. Wer sich aktiv für die Natur engagieren möchte, ist bei der NAJU genau richtig. Zusammen erkunden und schützen wir die Natur und vertreten unsere Positionen, für den Erhalt unserer Mitwelt.

Name: ASOC. PARA LA DEFENSA DE LA NATURALEZA Y LOS RECURSOS DE EXTREMADURA (ADENEX)
River Corps
Description:
River Corps is an AmeriCorps Program administered by 501c3 nonprofit - River Science

Mission: Provide trained technicians to support the capacity to partner organizations focused on watershed challenges.

Members are dedicated to help your watershed identify, construct, monitor, and maintain restoration and/or river health related projects.

Members receive comprehensive training in water quality sampling, hydrology data collection, restoration implementation, GIS, watershed coordination, stakeholder engagement, and water uses/supply/law.

Name: RiverWatch – Society for the Protection of Rivers
Description:
RiverWatch – Society for the protection of rivers
RiverWatch is a society for the protection of rivers. Our ambition is to preserve the last unimpaired rivers as well as restoring those already canalized or dammed.
RiverWatch engages globally against projects that destroy rivers, particularly dam projects.
RiverWatch is a campaigning organisation; that is, we act in opposition to certain projects and promote public awareness.
We consider ourselves a platform for all those who care about rivers.
 

Our ambitions

RiverWatch draws attention to the value of rivers; their beauty and their importance to flora and fauna, as well as to humans. We engage globally against destructive projects, particularly dams, and promote public awareness through campaigning.

 

Why rivers?
Rivers are our Earth’s lifeline. The majority of species can be found in or by streams. They are hot spots for biodiversity.

Unfortunately, due to over-utilisation, rivers have become the most endangered habitats globally. Over the past 40 years, rivers and their floodplains have been destroyed on a much higher scale than forests or oceans.

Rivers need a stronger lobby: while numerous NGOs advocate exclusively for the protection of rain forests, oceans, or mountains, hardly anyone concern themselves with rivers on a global scale. The only two exceptions are International Rivers based in Berkeley (USA) and their small equivalent in France (European Rivers).

 

Riverwatch is registered in the Transparency Register of the EU with public ID 352790841645-65.

Name: Nationalparkverwaltung Sächsische Schweiz
Description: Die Nationalparkverwaltung ist das Kompetenz- und Dienstleistungsunternehmen im Naturschutz für die Nationalparkregion Sächsische Schweiz. Im Auftrag des Freistaates Sachsen betreut und verwaltet sie den Nationalpark und das umgebende Landschaftsschutzgebiet. Als Teil der sächsischen Landesforstverwaltung nimmt die Nationalparkverwaltung gleichzeitig Aufgaben als untere Forstbehörde für den Nationalpark und angrenzende Bereiche wahr.

Name: MAREA MAnutenzione REstauro Arte srsl
Description: Società che si occupa di conservazione, manutenzione, restauro, promozione e valorizzazione dell'arte e dei beni culturali.

Name: International Conservation Development Partnership Inc.
Description:
International Conservation Development Partnership Inc. (ICDPI) is an autonomous limited liability company established to offer professional services, advice, guidance, and actionable solutions to the partner entities in research, policy and strategies formulation and development interventions in diverse thematic areas. ICDPI is registered in the Office of the Company Registrar, Ministry of Industry, Commerce and Supplies, Government of Nepal and in the Department of Internal Revenue. 
Our organization has been providing comprehensive services since 2019 in the fields of Forestry, Natural Resource Management (NRM), Watershed and landscape Management, Wildlife and Conservation/Protected Area Management, Enterprise development, Agriculture, Agroforestry, Socio-economic dynamics and Climate Change Adaptation and Mitigation with a primary focus on sustainability approaches of resources management for the benefits of dependent communities, bringing together multi-disciplinary personnel with expertise in diverse fields. 
ICDPI works in partnership with the government agencies, non-governmental organizations, networks and federations, civil society organizations, development partners and political parties to deliver quality services for the benefit of nature, natural resources and community. Our company has been facilitating its partners in developing and executing projects on resources mobilization at local levels, counselling people on social justice and human rights, designing and implementing livelihood programmes, ecosystem strengthening, database development and management, formulation of forest and NTFP management plans, developing local adaptation plans and environment auditing. 
We stand for women, Dalit, indigenous people and local communities, small and medium sized enterprises, small and marginalized farmers, workers, poor and landless families, children, youths and conflict affected people to safeguard their rights.

Name: Beegreen Ltd
Description:
Our center Beetopia is committed to the conservation of bees through research and education. We aim to be the conduit between research institutions and beekeepers, ensuring that knowledge is transferred both ways and we can all learn from each other. Our focus lies on stingless bees (Meliponini) but we also include honeybees and solitary bees in our operations.

We sell high-quality honey of Stingless Bees, sustainably harvested with beekeepers on the slopes of Mt. Kilimanjaro and Mt. Meru.

At BEEtopia we base our activities on four pillars:

Training: We provide training for sustainable stingless beekeeping in Northern Tanzania.

Education: Beetopia is an outdoor classroom that offers educational bee experiences.

Research: We collaborate with researchers, universities and institutions around the world and help gaining more knowledge through research facilitation.

Apitourism: We invite the global citizenry to visit Beetopia and learn about the smaller things that run our world.

Karibuni! Welcome!

Name: Amakali Conservation Trust
Description: Amakali Conservation Trust e.V. is a non-profit organisation dedicated to creating resilient landscapes where nature and people thrive. We catalyse and support conservation by strengthening ecosystems that sustain and connect humans and wildlife. We do this by collaborating with local partners and initiatives, providing resources and technical support, and amplifying global impact through effective communication.

Name: Channel Islands Cetacean Research Unit
Description:
Channel Islands Cetacean Research Unit is a non-profit 501(c)(3) organization based in Santa Barbara, California. While formally organized in July 2013, the director, Michelle Kowalewski ran a similar program through the Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History since 2002.

During her tenure at the museum, Michelle developed a bio-surveillance program conducting thorough investigations into the mortality of whales, dolphins, and porpoises. This program produced quantitative results on toxin, bio-toxin, heavy metal, and disease burdens in the local cetacean population. CICRU continues this bio-surveillance while expanding the education and research potentials.

To report a dead / stranded whale, dolphin or porpoise, please call or text our Stranding Hotline: 805-500-6220
Please note the exact location and provide a photo, if possible.

Name: Lebanon County Conservation District
Description: LCCD was formed in 1949 under the Conservation District Law and is one of 66 Conservation Districts throughout Pennsylvania. Conservation Districts address local natural resource concerns at a county level by providing technical assistance and education on matters such as reducing soil erosion, protecting water quality, and conserving natural resources.

Name: SOS SAHEL International Sénégal
Description:
SOS SAHEL International Sénégal a été fondée au Sénégal en 1976 par le Président Leopold Sédar Senghor à la suite d'une période de sécheresses extrêmes. SOS SAHEL agit aujourd'hui dans 11 pays de la bande sahélienne, du Sénégal à Djibouti.

SOS SAHEL a pour mission de renforcer la sécurité alimentaire et nutritionnelle des communautés rurales d’Afrique sub-saharienne. Son approche intègre tous les aspects du développement durable — social, économique et environnemental — pour soutenir les populations les plus vulnérables et renforcer leur résilience.»

Nos réalisations des 20 dernières années :

107 projets de développement, dont plus de 40 au Sénégal d’une valeur d’un milliard CFA minimum.

Santé : 40 centres de santé construits et équipés, dotation d’ambulances, programmes nutritionnels et dons alimentaires.

Agriculture et environnement : 27 000 hectares de terres régénérées, reboisement et bois villageois, protection des espèces médicinales.

Hydraulique villageoise : 800 points d’eau aménagés (forages, puits traditionnels et contre-puits), fourniture de pompes solaires et thermodynamiques, création de bassins et réseaux d’adduction.

Éducation et microfinance : construction de salles de classe et cases foyers, alphabétisation, formations en aviculture, élevage, couture, teinture et transformation agroalimentaire.

Énergie : initiatives en compensation carbone, solaire et éolien.

Impact : plus de 400 villages bénéficiaires et 700 000 personnes directement touchées par nos projets.

Notre approche : toutes nos actions reposent sur l’implication active des communautés locales, faisant des populations rurales les premiers acteurs du changement.

SOS SAHEL International Sénégal : mobiliser l’expertise locale pour un Sahel résilient et durable.


Mail : sossahelsenegal.partenariat@gmail.com

Name: NEPHROPS Global Prod
Description:
Au cours des dernières années, NEPHROPS a développé une série de trousses pédagogiques et dispositifs éducatifs consacrés à l’éducation au développement durable et à la biodiversité.
Ces outils ont été conçus pour aider les enseignants, les animateurs et les institutions à dynamiser leurs activités en classe ou en milieu parascolaire, à travers des contenus visuels, interactifs et accessibles.
Ces expériences nous ont permis d’affiner une approche unique, sur mesure et éprouvée sur le terrain, capable de transformer les grands enjeux environnementaux et sociaux en expériences d’apprentissage concrètes, inspirantes et inclusives.
Notre équipe conçoit aujourd’hui des programmes éducatifs adaptés aux contextes locaux, articulés autour des Objectifs de Développement Durable (ODD).
Nous intervenons ainsi sur un large éventail de thématiques : éducation environnementale, climat, eau, énergie, économie circulaire, gestion des ressources naturelles, métiers durables, inclusion sociale et bien d’autres encore.
Chaque projet est pensé comme un écosystème complet, intégrant à la fois contenus scientifiques, narration pédagogique, outils numériques et supports immersifs.
Cette approche sur mesure nous permet d’accompagner les fondations, institutions publiques, ONG et entreprises dans la création et le déploiement de dispositifs pédagogiques adaptés à leurs missions, à leurs publics et à leur territoire.

Name: Interreg HATCH
Description: The Interreg project Hadriaticum Dana Hub (HATCH) project is capitalizing on results of the recently finished Interreg Italy-Croatia projects with the aim to harmonize chemical, physical, biological and ecological parameters from various monitoring programs and contribute to effective conservation of the Adriatic Sea.

Name: Sylvan Heights Bird Park
Description: Sylvan Heights Bird Park is home to more than 2,000 birds from around the world. The park is dedicated to educating the public about the importance of conserving birds and their habitat, with a focus on waterfowl and wetlands. Sylvan Heights Bird Park seeks to advance conservation of waterfowl and wetlands, to act as a local educational resource for avian biology and wetlands ecology, and to serve as an international center for avicultural training and research.

Name: MERLIN TUTTLES BAT CONSERVATION
Description: Merlin Tuttle’s Bat Conservation was founded in 2014 to protect often misunderstood bats worldwide. Merlin’s more than 60-year legacy of experience in bat research, conservation, and photography is unsurpassed. MTBC relies on a powerful combination of science, field knowledge, and photography, to help save millions of bats; while protecting public health, economies, and ecosystems worldwide by teaching people to live harmoniously with bats through Merlin’s one-of-a-kind experience. Learn more at merlintuttle.org

Name: 3D-STELLAR
Description:
The 3D-STELLAR project aims to treat and valorize the toxic OMWW according to the principles of green and sustainable chemistry for a circular economy. Current regulations only authorize the direct spreading of these materials in a small proportion of the volumes generated, and industrialists are looking for other ways to manage this toxic liquid waste. The 3D-STELLAR consortium will explore the possibilities of treating these effluents and proposes to study the means of accelerating their evaporation/drying in order to better transform and valorize them. 3D-STELLAR project is based on two main technologies: 3D-Solar Distillers, which have the advantage of having a low environmental impact to clean water and, flash pyrolysis with thermal shock that does not generate by-products and is very fast. A mobile prototype (semi-pilot) capable of treating the equivalent of a 1 m3 tank will be built and installed in the field to validate the concept described in the 3D-STELLAR project.
Our proposal addresses section 2.2 “Farming systems”, A step toward carbon-neutral farms: coupling renewable energy sources with circular farming systems. This project proposes a circular, low-carbon approach to olive oil processing. With the implementation of 3D STELLAR, water will be recycled and harvested using solar energy and the concentrated sludge will be converted into nutritious/value-added products. Thus, Mediterranean farms will benefit from the water for irrigation/reuse, while the remaining sludge will be used directly as fertilizer and/or will be transformed into biochars to amend the soil.

Name: SAVE - Saving Asia's Vultures from Extinction
Queremos Tupungato
Description:
Buscamos conservar los valles aledaños al volcán Tupungato, que no quedaron resguardados por el Parque Nacional Glaciares de Santiago. Se trata de 70 mil hectáreas de territorio público en el Cajón del Maipo de la RM.
#QueremosTupungato o #QueremosParque, somos la misma campaña.

Name: Sleeping Bear Wildlife Fund
Description:
We are a new conservation organization in Michigan’s northwest lower Peninsula. We bring years of experience working with wildlife, communities, and habitats across the globe. 
     We will be a leader in Northern Michigan in supporting wildlife through rehabilitation services and sanctuary, through wildlife research and population restoration programs, and by working with the community through inspiring educational programs to nurture relationships with nature.

Name: POLE FRUITIER DE BRETAGNE
Description:
Inventaire, conservation, étude et valorisation de la biodiversité cultivée fruitière présente et à venir en Bretagne.
Accompagnement de projets visant la gestion autonome et durable des espaces de vergers conservatoires.

Name: Canadian Herpetological Society/Société d'Herpétologie du Canada
Description:
The Canadian Herpetological Society is a registered Canadian charity that advances reptile and amphibian research and conservation in Canada by:

promoting scientific research on reptiles and amphibians and disseminating the results;

facilitating collaboration among amateur and professional herpetologists;

advancing public understanding of our native reptile and amphibian species, the threats they face and the conservation solutions that exist; and

promoting, supporting and leading conservation and stewardship projects.

CHS is made up of researchers, conservation practitioners, naturalists, educators, and other individuals with an interest in Canada's reptiles and amphibians.

The Society holds an annual general meeting and conference where members can present their work, learn what others are doing, and identify opportunities for partnerships and collaborations.

The Canadian Herpetologist is an annual publication of the CHS that is available to all members. Back issues are posted on the web-site and are available to non-members.

Name: Save Beisa Oryx Community-SBOC
Description:
Save Beisa Oryx Community Trust (SBOC) is an Indigenous pastoralist-led conservation network focused on preserving the critically endangered Beisa Oryx, other threatened antelope species, and the dryland acacia forest catchments in Northern Kenya. Founded by Paul Edonga in 2021, SBOC is committed to protecting biodiversity while promoting social development, climate justice, and poverty eradication among pastoralist communities.
SBOC is the only African Indigenous Pastoralist-led Conservation Network, driven by a unique grassroots alliance of pastoralist youth, women, ambassadors, and volunteers. Our network operates on the principles of community engagement and collective action to achieve lasting environmental and social impact.
Our Vision
To lead a global movement where Indigenous communities are at the forefront of conservation efforts, balancing biodiversity preservation with human rights and social development.
Our Mission
To empower Indigenous pastoralist communities in conserving biodiversity and safeguarding natural ecosystems while advancing climate justice, social equity, and sustainable livelihoods through inclusive, participatory, and gender-responsive approaches. This is achieved through innovative community-based conservation programs, citizen science, ecosystem restoration, and fostering partnerships that ensure equitable benefit-sharing and resilience for both people and nature.

Name: AGROMAP GHANA
Description:
AGROMAP est une société sous régionale spécialisée dans le développement de projets de restauration des forêts, de conservation de la biodiversité et d’amélioration des revenus des producteurs. Nous sommes convaincus que les solutions fondées sur la nature, notamment l’agroforesterie, la reforestation, de restauration des mangroves ont une capacité de séquestration du carbone pour lutter contre les effets du changement climatique avec la forte implication des communautés locales et les petits producteurs.

Notre organisation est structurée en 4 départements : 

•AGROMAP NBS (Nature-Based Solutions) : Reforestation, Agroforesterie, Restauration de forêts & mangroves, Projets à génération de crédit carbone & Biodiversité,

•AGROMAP CONSULTING : Conseil en Stratégie ESG, Chaine d'approvisionnement responsable, plan d'aménagement de forêts, Analyse des risques de déforestation, rédaction des documents de projets de crédit carbone, Accompagnent à la due diligence de l'RDUE et ARS-1000.

•AGROMAP TECHNOLOGIE : Mapping & Géo monitoring de forêts & zones préservées, Analyses GIS, plan d'occupation des sols, Digitalisation de services agricoles, Solutions de traçabilité et Gestion des données des projets.

•AGROMAP ENERGIES RENOUVELABLES: Conception et fourniture des outils de production d’énergie (foyers améliorés, biogaz), Promotion de l’usage des énergies renouvelables, Valorisation des déchets d’origine agricole et forestières (compost, biochar),Gestion et Valorisation des Bioressources.

Name: Arte moderna e contemporanea
Description: L'azienda opera anche nel settore dell'arte moderna. Nel 2014 per l'apertura del  Museo del  Novecento a Firenze, è stata effettuata una campagna di interventi di restauro su 33 opere e nel 2015 sono stati presentati i risultati nell'evento "Novecento presenta"  – I segreti dell’artista: incontri con i restauratori a cura di Valentina Gensini

Name: Saskatchewan Stock Growers Foundation
Description: Working to conserve and protect native grasslands and the ranching landscape in Saskatchewan, in collaboration with landowners.

Name: Árbol & Vida
Description: Con el apoyo de Acción Andina y la Universidad Nacional de Jujuy,  apadrinado con orgullo por la Fundación de Actividades Biosféricas, Árbol y Vida se esfuerza por impulsar prácticas agronómicas innovadoras que contribuyen al bienestar ambiental.

Name: SOSA
Description: At SOSA (Save our Species Alliance), we’re on a mission to inspire passion for wildlife conservation and reconnect humanity with the natural world. As the Saving Our Species Alliance, we unite conservation enthusiasts with dedicated wildlife professionals, partners, and non-profit organizations to protect vulnerable species and ecosystems. Through awareness-driven merchandise, immersive wildlife experiences, and support for wildlife organizations, we foster a community of action. Our innovative approach combines education, storytelling, and hands-on conservation efforts to create lasting impact. Join us in safeguarding our planet's precious wildlife for future generations. #JoinTheAlliance

Name: Marine Genome Project
Description: Collecting genetic information from marine organisms with the intention of creating science-based educational resources to spread awareness about the fragility of marine species and the ecosystems they inhabit.

Name: Rebel West
Description: We're a 501(c)(3) non-profit that partners with people, wildlife, and the land to promote biodiversity and wildlife-friendly farming.

Name: INACO
Description:
INACO aims to support cultural and natural heritage sites located in river basin areas in the face of climate change challenges. Climate change, especially extreme weather events, poses an increasing threat to cultural and natural heritage, as well as to the safety of their users. Lack of appropriate action, inadequate restoration and lack of management plans increase the vulnerability of these areas.

The project will develop risk management tools, which will then be tested in eight pilot sites across Central Europe, including Croatia, Italy, Austria, Hungary, Germany, Slovenia, the Czech Republic and Slovakia.
The project includes three different pilot activities, which concern:
1. Nature reserves, historic villages and gardens located downstream and on the coast (including Po Delta - Italy, Dubrovnik River - Croatia).
2. Nature reserves, historic buildings and archaeological sites located near lakes (Lake Neusiedl - Austria, City of Fonyód - Hungary).
3. Historic complexes, parks and gardens in the upper reaches of rivers (Wiesent and Rednitz Valley - Germany, Jelenia Gora Valley - Poland, Kosice Region - Slovakia, Vltava River Valley - Czech Republic).

Name: The Good Carbon Farm
Description:
Instead of allowing carbon once stored in green waste to be re-released into the sky as carbon dioxide, we intervene and recapture carbon into solid form. Re-homing solid carbon in schools and community gardens creates a carbon sink. It’s the opposite of mining — returning natural resources, rather than extracting them.

Our work is in demand, and we’re upscaling to keep up. Watch this space for details about joining our mission to create hundreds of community carbon sinks storing carbon for good.

Name: Frogs & Friends e.V.
Description: Wissenschaftler, Biologen und Kreative haben sich zusammen getan, um die dramatischste Aussterbewelle seit Verschwinden der Dinosaurier ins Rampenlicht zu rücken: Online und Offline, auf Websites und in Zoos. Frogs & Friends arbeitet mit einem internationalen Netzwerk aus Zoos, Forschungseinrichtungen und unabhängigen Experten zusammen.

Name: Bamff Wildland
Description:
Bamff Wildland is a pioneering family-run rewilding project in North East Perthshire. Twenty years since beavers began unravelling and reimagining our waterways, we are turning over farmland to nature in perpetuity.

Ecological restoration at Bamff began in the early 1990s with the re-creation of wetlands through reverse drainage schemes and the planting of various native woodlands. This was followed in 2002 by the pioneering introduction of beavers.

At the start of the 2020s, we initiated a catalysed rewilding project to create a richly biodiverse area of wild land out of 180 hectares of marginal farmland and plantations. The long term aim is to allow all of this land to become self willed – a self regenerating mosaic of dynamically evolving habitats and their associated wildlife species. This happens by putting nature back in charge and ultimately withdrawing the influence of humanity: to rewild.

In 2022, we began working in partnership with 10 neighbouring landowners to restore waterways on a wider landscape scale, through a new project called Braes of Alyth: Wild Cores and Corridors. This pioneering work will create new 'corridors' of nature-rich habitat weaving through two sub-catchments of the River Isla, reconnecting biodiversity 'cores' such as Bamff and fragments of ancient woodland within an actively farmed landscape. By remeandering straightened channels and restoring woodland cover over denuded streams, we will support the participating landowners to revive biodiversity and deliver natural flood mitigation alongside existing land use.

Name: Sri Lanka Elephant Project
Description: An interdisciplinary research initiative to help Sri Lanka be a better place for elephants and the people who live among them.

Name: ONG Wiwe World
Description: We defend human rights through freedom, inclusion, diversity and peace. Achieving the SDGs requires preserving the environment, fighting climate change, education, health and innovation digital

Name: IUCN SSC Bear Specialist Group
Heal the Blu
Description: Protecting and restoring the health of our oceans through research, education and community engagement.

Name: Trapinator
Description:
Manufactures and suppliers of Trapinator DOC series traps from New Zealand, helping to conserve our wildlife. We work closely with Conservation Trusts, pest controllers and gamekeepers throughout the world protecting against rodents, mustelids and depending where in the world you are against other species as well.

Our DOC series traps have the approval from the Agreement on International Humane Trapping Standards (AIHTS) and also The Spring Traps Approval order 2018 in the UK.

Name: Territoire des possibles
Description: Festival des possibles

Name: Eure-et-Loir Nature
Description: Association de protection de l'environnement basée sur Morancez.

Name: Falmouth Land Trust
Description: The mission of the Falmouth Land Trust is to preserve and care for natural and agricultural lands in perpetuity, to enhance habitat for plants and wildlife, to advance the quality of life for all in our community, and to educate the public on the history and stewardship of protected lands.

Name: State of the Rockies Project
Description: Encouraging students to explore critical environmental and social challenges in the Rocky Mountain West.

Name: Ecophilic Scientific Research and Innovations (ESRI)
Description:
Ecophilic Scientific Research and Innovations (ESRI) is a mission-driven environmental research and conservation organisation dedicated to protecting Nigeria’s freshwater biodiversity, ecosystems, and riparian landscapes. Registered under the Companies and Allied Matters Act (RN 7341854), we conduct scientific research, ecological assessments, and conservation projects focused on freshwater and inland habitats.
Our work spans biodiversity surveys, threatened species protection, water quality studies, and ecosystem restoration. We collaborate with communities, government agencies, and academic institutions to design sustainable solutions that balance human needs with environmental resilience.
Through field research across diverse ecological zones, we have advanced understanding of Nigeria’s freshwater biodiversity, from the Ikogosi Warm Spring, and contributed to species discovery, habitat restoration, and pollution mitigation.
Driven by science, collaboration, and integrity, Ecophilic champions evidence-based conservation that supports the UN Sustainable Development Goals, especially SDG 15 (Life on Land). We are committed to building a future where Nigeria’s inland waters and biodiversity thrive for generations to come.

Name: North Wales Seal Research Organisation
Description:
NWSRO is a marine education organisation centred around seal research, conservation, and protection in North Wales.
Founded in 2017 by Bangor University alumni, NWSRO aimed to fill the knowledge gaps that existed in pinniped research on the North Wales coast in order to better understand their populations and threats through monitoring, public engagement, awareness campaigns, and research.
In 2019 we initiated our citizen scientist sightings project and over the years have become increasingly involved in marine education and science communication with multiple organisations and research projects with many stakeholders.
We have teams of dedicated volunteers covering all along the coast and regularly present at public events and scientific conferences. Inclusivity, kindness, and environmental action is at the forefront of the NWSRO ethos and we welcome individuals from all backgrounds and abilities to join us to learn more about protecting our important welsh marine species.

Name: John Hawes Foundation
Description: The VISION of the foundation is to support the understanding, conservation, interpretation, revitalisation and celebration of the heritage of John Hawes for the good of the community.

Name: ConservAção Brasil
Description: Somos um movimento vivo, inovador e integrador, dedicado a transformar a relação da sociedade com o meio ambiente e despertar a consciência popular para a conservação da sociobiodiversidade brasileira.

Name: Sri Lanka Wildlife Conservation Society
Description: We are committed to the protection of Sri Lanka’s endangered wildlife through conservation research and community engagement. For three decades, we have been working to protect Sri Lanka’s most endangered wildlife through community-based conservation projects and scientific research.

Name: Wildlife Habitat Trust
Description: The Wildlife Habitat Trust was created to address the urgent challenge of habitat loss. The land available for conservation in much of Kenya is rapidly shrinking due to increased human populations with settlements and urbanization expanding into wildlife dispersal areas. The Trust supports the wildlife conservancies on community lands in the Maasai Mara and Amboseli eco-systems, home to more than a quarter of Kenya’s wildlife, including elephants, the big cats, over a million migrating wildebeest, and hundreds of thousands of zebra and gazelles.

Name: Ol Jogi Wildlife Conservancy
Description:
Ol Jogi Wildlife Conservancy strives to be a leading model for private conservation in Africa, where meaningful hospitality and the care of wildlife exist in true partnership. Spanning 58,000 acres in Laikipia, Kenya, Ol Jogi safeguards life in all its forms while welcoming guests into a luxurious family home of elegant private cottages, accommodating guests in complete seclusion. The property demonstrates how thoughtful tourism can sustain land, wildlife and neighbouring communities. Every stay directly supports anti-poaching operations, advanced veterinary care, the Wildlife Rescue Centre and education programs, while also enabling the innovative scientific approaches, monitoring techniques and community initiatives that strengthen long-term conservation on private land.
At its heart, Ol Jogi is both a home and a philosophy, shaped by privacy, respect and intention. Our hospitality reflects these values: exclusive, personal and designed to deepen one’s connection to the wilderness. Here, luxury is purposeful, and every experience contributes to a larger legacy: safeguarding endangered species, protecting the ecosystems that sustain them, and supporting the local communities who share this landscape.

Name: African Conservation Tillage Network
Description: ACT believes that the stakes in improving productivity, sustainable land management and environmental conservation are extremely high and will, therefore, remain focused on quality service delivery, innovativeness and adherent to ethics and standards so as to meet and exceed client’s expectation.

Name: Hudson Taconic Lands
Description:
The Rensselaer Plateau Alliance (RPA) is an environmental nonprofit organization that works with the community to promote land conservation on the Rensselaer Plateau in eastern New York.

The Rensselaer Plateau is one of the largest and most ecologically intact native habitats in New York State. The preservation of this landscape for future generations is the vision of the Rensselaer Plateau Alliance.

The Plateau covers about 118,000 acres in the towns of Berlin, Brunswick, Grafton, Hoosick, Nassau, Petersburgh, Pittstown, Poestenkill, Sand Lake, Stephentown and the Village of East Nassau.

An escarpment steeply rising from the surrounding lower elevations marks the Plateau’s boundary. The Plateau’s relatively high elevations (1,000 – 1,800 feet) and cool climate, acidic soils, and its poor drainage contribute to plants, forests, and wetlands more similar to the Adirondacks than to the surrounding local area.

Name: Conservation Action Network (CAN)
Description: Conservation Action Network (CAN) is a community-driven initiative rooted in Indigenous knowledge and local leadership. Founded in Borneo, we work to protect forest landscapes, rescue wildlife, and support sustainable livelihoods. From empowering Community Forest Rangers to restoring degraded ecosystems, CAN unites traditional wisdom with innovative conservation strategies, for forests, for wildlife, and for future generations.

Name: Dr Beynon's Bug Farm
Description: We are a wildlife reserve, farm, research and visitor centre, set in 200 acres of the beautiful Pembrokeshire countryside. With a focus on nature recovery and sustainable farming/food, we have a Tropical Bug Zoo, Museum, Art Gallery and a series of Wildlife Walks and Farm Trails. We are proud to have scrumptious, locally-farmed food onsite from Grub Kitchen, with or without innovative insect ingredients. We are also the home of Bug Farm Foods, innovating food made from insects.

Name: Flo Gardens
Description:
We create space for connection by helping people regenerate the native rainforests and freshwater ecosystems on privately owned land across the Northern Rivers

We are industry leaders of riparian, wetland and land management. 

Transformative projects that restore your land back to the way it was before being cleared for farming and development.

Leaving a sustainability legacy for future generations.

Name: Máster Universitario en Técnicas de Conservación de la Biodiversidad y Ecología-URJC
Description: Programa oficial de la URJC que une ciencia, técnica y compromiso para conservar la biodiversidad del futuro.

Name: Society for Aquatic Resources Conservation
Description: A student society dedicated to the conservation of aquatic resources

Name: Eden Honduras
Description:
Mission: To restore Honduras's forest ecosystems through reforestation, conservation, and sustainable management practices that promote the comprehensive development of local communities. We promote job creation, environmental education, and community participation in the restoration of their own territories, strengthening ecological and social resilience in the face of the effects of deforestation and climate change.
Vision: To be a leading organization in ecological restoration and sustainable development in Honduras, recognized for regenerating native forests, protecting watersheds, and improving the quality of life of rural communities. We aspire to a future in which people live in balance with nature, where restored forests are a source of life, prosperity, and hope for present and future generations.                                                                                       Misión
Restaurar los ecosistemas forestales de Honduras mediante acciones de reforestación, conservación y manejo sostenible que impulsen el desarrollo integral de las comunidades locales. Promovemos la generación de empleo, la educación ambiental y la participación comunitaria en la restauración de sus propios territorios, fortaleciendo la resiliencia ecológica y social frente a los efectos de la deforestación y el cambio climático.
Visión
 Ser una organización líder en la restauración ecológica y el desarrollo sostenible en Honduras, reconocida por regenerar los bosques nativos, proteger las cuencas hidrográficas y mejorar la calidad de vida de las comunidades rurales. Aspiramos a un futuro en el que las personas vivan en equilibrio con la naturaleza, donde los bosques restaurados sean fuente de vida, prosperidad y esperanza para las generaciones presentes y futuras.

Name: Projet APA Nagoya II
Description:
