Sauvegarde Faune Sauvage
Heal the Wild
Description:
We'll help sick and injured animals get the care they need through an Action Network that supports wildlife rescue and rehabilitation partners in the field. Launching August 2023.

Information for potential partners: www.healthewild.com/partners

Name: Eco Peace Nation
Description: Eco Peace Nation focuses on education about climate change and wildlife conservation efforts. This includes animal rights, wildlife restoration and rehabilitation, climate change topics, and the correlation between them. We have a goal of spreading awareness about issues impacting our Earth and the residents within the environment.

Name: Friends of Hunting Island State Park, Inc.
reWild Wainui
Description:
Living in or visiting Wainui, you might have walked out your door, smelt the bush or the sea, heard the birdsong of the local bellbird, or felt the whoosh of the Kereru flying past.  Or you might have been for a walk on the beach, in the bush, or outdoors and felt a sense of peace and relaxation. The environment around us, nature, is calming and restorative, and research confirms that it benefits our mental health and wellbeing. Nature is good for humans.

But while we’re lucky to have rich natural environment in Wainui, it is constantly under threat. Only one percent of the original forest on Banks Peninsula remains, we have numerous weeds or environmental factors that threaten our native bush and landscape, and pests such as possums, rats and stoats are wreaking havoc on our native bird species.  

reWild Wainui is an idea that as a community we can lend a hand to mother nature, helping our fauna and flora thrive and flourish, while at the same time helping ourselves.

Name: Dynamique Entreprenariat Environnement Developpement Durable, DEED asbl
Description: En

Name: Salve o Rio Vermelho
Description: Organização socioambiental criada com o propósito de defesa do Rio Vermelho, localizado na Ilha de Florianópolis - SC, protegido por Área de Preservação Permanente, mas que vem sendo atacado institucional e ilegalmente.

Name: Multnomah Anglers & Hunters
Description: Multnomah Anglers & Hunters is a sportsmen's organization located in Portland, Oregon, the oldest in Oregon, third oldest in the United States, and longest continuously-running organization of its kind west of New York. Established January 5, 1883, as the Multnomah Rod & Gun Club, we have spent the last 140 years dedicated to wildlife conservation, habitat protection, and staunch support of ecological education for the benefit of hunters, anglers, outdoor enthusiasts, and all citizens of Oregon. We are a registered non-profit 501(c)(3) organization.

Name: GSEAC
Description:
Grupo sin fines de lucro, dedicado a la realización y difusión de estudios sobre la Sistemática y Ecología (aunque otros temas afines tienen cabida) de los artrópodos del Caribe. La membresía incluye tanto a profesionales como aficionados, pero se concibe como un espacio para el intercambio y actualización de información científica.
Se admite y promueve la divulgación de artículos publicados, fotografías originales y cualquier fuente similar y confiable que contribuya a los objetivos antes mencionados. Contamos con especialistas altamente calificados en este perfil que pueden también actuar como revisores de manuscritos inéditos, bajo solicitud de sus autores.
Sólo se permite lenguaje ético y respetuoso, aunque dentro de este marco es bienvenida la interacción y discusión entre sus miembros, siempre en español y/o inglés.

Name: Frogs Victoria
Description:
Frogs Victoria is an association for anyone with an interest in Victorian frogs.

We are a networking hub for researchers, conservationists, enthusiasts and the frog curious. Frogs Victoria aims to raise the profile of Victoria’s frogs, fostering an interest in them and offering support to scientific research, conservation, education and the wider community.

Name: CI Bird Sanctuary
Description:
The Centre Island Bird Sanctuary is dedicated to preserving critical habitats for migratory birds and fostering conservation through immersive experiences. By offering guided birdwatching tours aboard historic sail and motor vessels, we engage the public in hands‑on education and citizen science—logging sightings in eBird and supporting real-world research.

Through dynamic partnerships with schools, local nonprofits, and businesses, CIBS brings nature to the forefront—whether on the water or in the classroom. 

Our goal: connect people to wildlife, deepen environmental stewardship, and inspire the next generation of conservationists.

Name: NOETZIE CONSERVANCY OWNERS’ ASSOCIATION
Description:
About Us

The Noetzie Conservancy Owners’ Association (NCOA) is a non-profit public benefit organization dedicated to preserving the unique natural beauty and biodiversity of the Noetzie Conservancy in South Africa. 

Our mission is to promote conservation, responsible development, and sustainable practices within the Conservancy and its surrounding areas.

Our Vision: A thriving Noetzie Conservancy where the natural environment is protected, indigenous plants and animals flourish, and the community enjoys a harmonious relationship with nature.

Our Objectives:
* Conserve and rehabilitate the natural environment.
* Protect indigenous plants, animals, and landscapes.
* Represent and protect the interests of property owners and members.
* Collaborate with local authorities and organizations to achieve our conservation goals.
* Enhance the amenities and attractiveness of Noetzie while maintaining its ecological integrity.

Get Involved: We welcome all who share our passion for conservation and sustainable living. 

Join us in protecting this unique ecosystem!

Name: Wildfire Action Fund
Description: 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to educating, informing, and taking action to combat wildfires by mobilizing our common community and empowering donors

Name: Caracal Conservation And Research Project
Description: Caracal Conservation and Research Project (CCARP) is India’s first initiative dedicated to studying and conserving the Asiatic Caracal and its unique ecosystem. While our primary focus is caracal conservation, we are equally committed to protecting other lesser-known species that share their habitat. At CCARP, we aim to challenge conventional approaches in the conservation sector, fostering innovation and creating a workplace culture that prioritizes the well-being and growth of our team. Together, we strive to ensure ecological balance and a sustainable future for India’s wildlife.

Name: Southern Virginia Land Conservancy
Description:
Working with rural landowners in Halifax, Henry, Patrick and Pittsylvania counties and the cities of Danville and Martinsville, Virginia, to forever protect farms, forests and wildlife habitat. A 501(c)(3) nonprofit accredited by the Land Trust Accreditation Commission. 

Land Savers United dba Southern Virginia Land Conservancy

Name: BIRD ENDOWMENT
Description:
BIRD ENDOWMENT is on a mission to save the critically endangered Blue-throated Macaw from extinction in the wild in the Beni Savanna of Bolivia, the birds endemic range. 
Bird Endowment's primary campaign to achieve this goal is our Nido Adoptivo “adopted nest” project in partnership with the Bolivian NGO Asociación Armonía. This annual campaign raises funds through donations and nest box sponsorships to build, monitor and protect the nest boxes on the Laney Rickman Blue-throated Macaw Reserve in Bolivia.
As of 2022, an incredible 113 Blue-throated Macaws have now fledged from the nest boxes on the Laney Rickman Reserve. With estimates of only 400 - 450  of these critically endangered birds in the wild, this number is truly significant - about 25% of the wild population.
With continued support, the Blue-throated Macaw is flying toward a brighter future!

Name: Jax Oyster Conservation
Description: Our Mission: Jax Oyster Conservation is a sustainability and resiliency initiative, working with our community to reduce waste in our landfills and rebuild habitat through oyster shell recycling and restoration across our estuaries.

Name: Nile Crocodile Park
Description:
NCP is a Limited Liability Corporation registered in 2008 in Kampala, Uganda as a commercial farming, conservation, and tourism enterprise. NCP services include Agro-industry and Hotel& Tourism.
NCP is not just a crocodile farm; it is a complete wildlife experience offering a range of displays and activities to suit all age groups. It is a 24-hectare botanical garden of indigenous trees and plants, which attract a unique variety of over 200 free-flying bird species. The main
attractions are the few hundred Nile Crocodiles ranging from newborn hatchlings to 100-year-old mammoths and “Isis” the African fish the eagle that will greet you as you enter the park. These descendants of the dinosaurs live in pools and pens with beautiful, natural, and manicured surroundings, which allow public close-up viewing. But the park does not only house crocodiles it also houses several other animals such as Vervet Monkeys, Mongoose, Mountain rats, farmyard animals, and a lot more bird species which include the walk-through aviary where the public can see the national bird the Crested crane. The Reptile Park will allow you to face your wildest fear by facing some of Africa’s most venomous reptiles. Visitors can spend hours meandering the paths and byways enjoying the flora and indigenous bird life that abounds.

Name: Friends of Eastern Road Nature Reserve
Description: We’re a volunteer-led community conservation charity working to protect, preserve, and promote the Eastern Road Nature Reserve in Lindfield, West Sussex.

Name: FUNDACIÓN AGUA Y TIERRA
Description: Fundación sin fines de lucro, conformada por profesionales panameños, dedicada a la protección, conservación y manejo comunitario sostenible de las tortugas marinas y la biodiversidad en la Zona Especial de Manejo Marino Costera Zona Sur de Veraguas, en la costa Pacífica de Panamá.

Name: Gonçalves Soluções e Serviços Ambientais
Description: Empresa especializada em serviços ambientais

Name: Save Natura
Description: Die Save Natura Artenschutzinitiative ist ein Projekt des Vereins Fair and Green e. V., das sich für den Schutz bedrohter Tier- und Pflanzenarten einsetzt. Sie arbeitet mit Landwirten, Unternehmen und Privatpersonen zusammen, um gezielt Lebensräume für gefährdete Arten zu schaffen und zu erhalten – zum Beispiel durch spezielle Maßnahmen in Weinbergen und anderen landwirtschaftlichen Flächen. Die Initiative verbindet wissenschaftliche Erkenntnisse mit praktischen Projekten und finanziert sich unter anderem durch Spenden und den Verkauf zertifizierter Produkte. Ziel ist es, die Artenvielfalt nachhaltig zu erhalten.

Name: Sagittaria
Description:
Der ehrenamtlich geführte Verein Sagittaria setzt sich seit 1988 für den Schutz des Atlantik-Urwaldes, dessen Biodiversiät und die Existenzgrundlage der Indigenen Mbyá ein. 

In der Nähe der weltberühmten Iguazú-Wasserfälle befindet sich einer der letzten Atlantik-Urwalde. Er gehört zu den artenreichsten und ältesten Wäldern der Welt. Zum Schutz des Regenwaldes und der Mbyá kauft Sagittaria mit Hilfe von Spenden in der Provinz Misiones – im nord-östlichen Zipfel Argentiniens – Regenwaldflächen, um sie der Abholzung zu entziehen. Den wertvollen subtropischen Regenwald gibt Sagittaria anschliessend den einstigen Waldnomaden Mbyá zurück. Das über Jahrtausende alte Volk versteht sich als Teil der Urwaldorganismen und lebt sorgsam mit ihnen im Einklang: «Wir sind Teil des Waldes und der Wald ein Teil von uns.» Das indigene Volk erhält so nicht nur ihre Existenzgrundlage zurück, sondern lebt als Teil des Urwaldes und schützt das Gebiet vor Abholzung sowie die einmalige biologische Vielfalt. Mit der Rückgabe der Atlantik-Urwaldflächen an die Mbyá ist der subtropische Regenwald auf lange Zeit geschützt.

Name: POB Conservation
Description:
Canadian conservation initiative leveraging the carbon-offset market to drive sustainable practices for a greener tomorrow. 

Initial phase in the Laurentians, the largest privately owned park in the region, just 1h from Montreal. //   https://www.instagram.com/pobconservation/

Name: Friends of Upper Lesmurdie Falls Charity
Description:
Friends of Upper Lesmurdie Falls is a dedicated community volunteer group committed to protecting, restoring, and enhancing the natural beauty of the Upper Lesmurdie Falls area in the Perth Hills. Since forming in December 2012, our mission has been to rehabilitate this precious part of Mundy Regional Park, which had suffered significant degradation due to invasive weeds and loss of native flora. Through passion, perseverance, and collaboration, we have transformed the area into a thriving natural space that supports biodiversity and provides a welcoming environment for visitors.

Our volunteers actively participate in hands-on conservation efforts, including weed management, planting thousands of native species, and habitat restoration for local wildlife. We work closely with local councils, environmental organizations, and the community to create long-term sustainability projects. In addition, we focus on improving the visitor experience by developing resting areas, picnic spaces, and walking trails that encourage people to connect with nature in a low-impact, respectful way.

Education and community engagement are central to our work. We regularly host “busy bee” planting days, where volunteers of all ages can contribute to environmental care while learning about the unique ecosystems of the Darling Range. These events not only foster a strong sense of community but also inspire the next generation to take an active role in environmental stewardship.

Our vision is to see Upper Lesmurdie Falls flourish as both a vibrant natural habitat and a cherished recreational space for future generations. Whether you are a passionate conservationist, a casual bushwalker, or simply someone who values the beauty of Western Australia’s landscapes, we invite you to join us in making a difference.

Name: Dhole Conservation Fund
Description:
Dhole Conservation Fund is a new initiative to help with funding local conservation projects for the Asiatic wild dog, called a dhole. Dholes, like many carnivores, are losing valuable habitat to human use and development. They are often persecuted by humans as they have been known to take livestock when their native prey has been depleted.

The Dhole Conservation Fund is dedicated to helping solve these issues and help bring about a larger global awareness of these amazing social canines. We see the dhole having a bright future in its home range, but it won’t happen without some hard work and the dedicated people out there trying to ensure that future.

Name: Forest Rent
Description:
We help people protect real forests and take part in real climate action — no greenwashing, no nonsense.

At Forest Rent, you can rent forest land, support nature, and help absorb CO₂ — whether you're an individual, a family, or a company trying to do better.

Why? Because forests are one of the best ways we can fight climate change, and everyone should have the chance to be part of the solution.

 Track your forest’s impact
 Offset your footprint
 Support biodiversity
 Be proud of the change you’re making

 It’s easy. It’s meaningful. It works.

Name: Williams Environnement
Description:
Notre mission est d'éviter le gaspillage de ressources en offrant une seconde chance aux objets de valeur avant qu’ils ne soient envoyés au recyclage ou, pire encore, à l’enfouissement.

Chez Williams Environnement, nous sommes déterminés à récupérer les contenants consignés et réutilisables. Nous prenons en charge leur collecte, leur tri, et leur acheminement vers les endroits appropriés, dans une démarche respectueuse de l’environnement.

Name: Watonet Tanzania
Description:
Whale Shark Tour Operators Network of Tanzania (WATONET),  Represent a large part of whale sharks tour operators of Tanzania understanding general lack of  awareness and knowledge regarding whale sharks conservation and  urgent need to develop and practice social-cultural, scientific, technical and institutional capacities to provide the basic understanding upon for planning and implementing appropriate whalesharks` conservation measures.

The whale sharks tour operators of Tanzania are resolved to come together and collaborate as a national organization recognizing the need to organize, unify and consolidate the efforts, works and activities of the organization’s founding principles in a collective, collaborative and concerted manner to ensure sustainable development of whalesharks’ conservation and ecotourism in Tanzania

Name: Friends of Penhill Conservation Area
Description:
The Friends of Penhill Conservation Area (FPCA) is an organisation focused on the conservation, environment, and animal welfare of the Penhill Conservation Area. This area is located in Eersterivier, Cape Town, specifically between Penhill Estate, Stellenbosch Farms, the R102 road, and the Welmoed cemetery.

The primary objectives of the FPCA include:
*  Promoting the conservation and maintenance of the Penhill Conservation Area.
*   Working towards the enhancement of the environment within the area, recognising it as an irreplaceable asset or educational, scientific, recreational, and leisure activities.
*  Researching, rehabilitating, protecting, securing, and enhancing the systems and ecological qualities of the Penhill Conservation Area and its surroundings.
*  Preserving and protecting archaeological and historical sites, structures, and artefacts located on and near the conservation area.
*   Creating awareness and educating surrounding communities and the general public about the importance of the conservation area.

The FPCA also works in collaboration with departments of the City of Cape Town, such as City Parks, Roads and Stormwater, and Environmental Management who are stakeholders in the greenbelt area.

Name: Feathers and Fur Rehab and Education Inc
Alliance Verte
Description:
ALLIANCE VERTE

Femmes – Jeunes – Inclusion – Environnement

Un programme national pour un Sénégal plus vert et plus juste


Alliance Verte, c’est une initiative solidaire et inclusive qui met les femmes, les jeunes et les personnes en situation de handicap au cœur de la protection de l’environnement.

De la collecte des déchets à leur valorisation durable, ce programme agit concrètement pour un Sénégal propre, engagé et autonome.

 NOS OBJECTIFS

 Autonomiser les femmes par la création de coopératives environnementales
 Promouvoir l’emploi des jeunes et leur former au plaidoyer environnemental
 Inclure toutes les communautés, sans distinction, notamment les personnes en situation de handicap
 Soutenir les AGR (Activités Génératrices de Revenus) des femmes à travers des micro-fonds écologiques.
 Mettre en place des cases d’alphabétisation dans chaque zone d’action
 Sensibiliser et agir localement pour un impact national

 Un programme national – Présent dans toutes les régions du Sénégal
 Cases d’alphabétisation – Pour apprendre, comprendre et entreprendre

 Des actions concrètes – Pour l’environnement et pour la communauté

ENSEMBLE POUR UN AVENIR DURABLE

Alliance Verte, c’est un réseau de coopératives locales, une dynamique de transformation sociale et un engagement pour l’environnement, l’emploi et la dignité de toutes et tous.

Name: Above The Canopy
Description:
We help conservation professionals, especially those just starting out or shifting paths, navigate their careers with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Through Above the Canopy, I work at the intersection of conservation impact, personal growth, and wellbeing.
With over 15 years of experience leading conservation programs and capacity sharing initiatives across zoos, aquariums, and global landscapes, I founded this consultancy to grow the bold, inclusive, and skilled conservation practitioners our planet urgently needs. In my consultancy, I use the term conservation capacity sharing rather than development to emphasize mutual learning, equity, and the recognition that expertise exists across all contexts, shifting away from hierarchical models toward more collaborative, reciprocal approaches to strengthening conservation practice.
As a conservation capacity strategist, I support individuals and organizations in strengthening their people, programs, and leadership pipelines. My work is especially grounded in:
Supporting zoo and aquarium professionals in building resilient communities of practice
Designing skills-based capacity initiatives that drive tangible conservation outcomes
Leading with feminist values to champion equity, belonging, and authenticity in conservation leadership
Whether facilitating cross-cultural dialogue, mentoring the next generation, or designing transformative learning environments, my mission is to help conservation leaders rise with readiness, build with purpose, and maximize their impact. 
Areas of Expertise:
Strategic Conservation Capacity Sharing
Conservation Career Coaching for Emerging & Pivoting Professionals
Women in Conservation Leadership
Skills-Based Training & Facilitation
Cross-Cultural Mentorship & Learning Communities
Strategic Planning and adaptive management for conservation
Conservation capacity development for the Zoo & Aquarium Field

Name: Jaguar Foundation
Description:
[ En ]

Jaguar Foundation is a Digital Organization founded to help raise funds through the sale of NFTs (Non-fungible Tokens). We are committed to distributing 60% of all funds raised among institutions that collect and care for these animals in the country.

There will be 8,888 collectibles created for sale, which will help in the conservation and preservation of the Jaguar in Brazil, so we will be able to help expand places where these (and other) animals live, build new places, and also for other animal needs, including food and medicine.

[ Pt-br ]

Fundação Onça-pintada é uma Organização Digital fundada com o intuito de ajudar a arrecadar fundos através da venda de NFTs (Non-fungible Tokens). Nos comprometemos a distribuir 60% de todos os recursos arrecadados entre instituições que recolhem e cuidam destes animais no país.

Serão 8.888 colecionáveis criados para venda, e que irá ajudar na conservação e preservação da Onça-pintada no Brasil, assim, conseguiremos ajudar a ampliar locais onde habitam esses (e outros) animais, construir novos locais, e também para outras necessidades dos animais, entre elas, alimentos e medicamentos.

Name: Paysage Pour Plouzané
Description: Mouvement associatif ayant pour objectifs de recréer le bocage Plouzanéen et d'améliorer le cadre de vie de ses habitants.

Name: CCJ INVESTMENT CO. LTD.
Description: Gestión y conservación medioambiental

Name: Mr Turbino
Description:
Mr. Turbino est un dispositif sous-marin intelligent de dépollution écologique breveté.

Conçu par Alan D’Alfonso Peral et développé au sein de TUDO & Co SAS avec le soutien de Fons Conseil, Mr. Turbino associe innovation technologique, circularité et engagement citoyen.

 Technologie brevetée : système pneumatique à air multivoies, garantissant la sécurité biologique totale de la faune et de la flore.

 Innovation intelligente : version ROV autonome intégrant IA et traçabilité via la plateforme Mr Turbino Ocean Tracker.

 Écoconception : matériaux recyclés et recyclables à 95 %, production locale (Saint-Junien / Limoges).

 Impact social : programme participatif “Adoptez votre Mr. Turbino” et partenariat avec l’association Recyclamer AWARE.

Notre mission : rendre le nettoyage des océans accessible, mesurable et durable.

 Adoptez Mr. Turbino – le héros des océans.

Name: Payette River Project
Description: The Payette River Project is dedicated to the preservation of riparian and wetland ecosystems on the Payette River of Idaho. To date, we have preserved, restored, and actively protect nearly 30 acres of precious habitat. Our goal is to reclaim and restore nature’s most valuable ecosystems.

Name: Wild Rest
Description:
Wild Rest is a creative studio and regenerative platform designing experiences that reconnect people to nature, beauty, and rest. We work at the intersection of ecology, art, and spirit, crafting immersive environments, rituals, and gatherings that nurture the land, the heart, and the nervous system. From rewilding green deserts and land left for dead to rekindling the forgotten fire in the human spirit, Wild Rest restores what was once abandoned, turning neglect into bloom.

Rooted in slowness, ceremony, and the New and Ancient Story, Wild Rest is an invitation to rewild the heart and imagine new ways of belonging.

Name: Planeteers United
Description:
Planeteers United inspires environmental change through hands-on education, conservation, and exploration. Located along the Texas Gulf Coast, we utilize local natural habitats as our outdoor classrooms. Our education programs and public conservation efforts provide unique opportunities for participants to take part in creating cleaner oceans and a greener future. As we all share this planet, we welcome anyone who wants to unite to make a difference.
Planeteers United is set to officially launch mid-2024.

Name: Teens4Forest
Description:
About Teens4Forest
Teens4Forest is a youth-focused environmental organization dedicated to empowering teenagers to become active agents of forest conservation and environmental stewardship.
Our Mission
We believe that sustainable conservation begins with education and youth empowerment. Through interactive workshops, practical engagement, and community-based initiatives, we equip secondary school students with the knowledge, skills, and passion needed to protect our forests and combat climate change.
What We Do

Deliver comprehensive forest conservation education programs in secondary schools
Facilitate hands-on conservation activities including community clean-ups, tree planting, and school garden projects
Support the establishment of student-led Conservation Clubs
Conduct awareness campaigns on critical environmental issues including deforestation, biodiversity loss, and sustainable practices
Partner with academic institutions and conservation organizations to ensure scientifically-sound, locally-relevant programming

Our Impact
Since our inception, we have reached over 1,000 secondary school students across Nigeria, achieving measurable increases in environmental knowledge and fostering demonstrable attitude shifts toward conservation. Our student-led Conservation Clubs continue to amplify our impact, creating a sustainable ripple effect within schools and communities.
Our Approach
We combine scientific education with practical, community-centered action. By addressing local environmental challenges, such as bush burning and deforestation. We ensure our programs resonate deeply with students' lived experiences, transforming passive learners into passionate advocates.

Name: Le Pro Des Nuisibles
Description:
Votre espace protégé de tous nuisibles !
Avec plus de quatorze ans d’expérience dans le domaine de la gestion des nuisibles, nous avons développé une expertise significative qui nous permet de faire face à une variété de défis dans ce secteur. Dites adieu
aux nuisibles !
Faites confiance à notre expertise pour éradiquer efficacement et durablement toute infestation en respectant l'environnement.

Name: Plantes à gogo
Description: Plantes à gogo a pour but de reconnecter l'humain à la nature! Prendre soin de soi au naturel grâce à des ateliers d'herboristerie, s'émerveiller et changer de regard grâce à des balades/animations nature et sensorielles. Plantes à gogo a à cœur de mettre en avant les plantes mais surtout les plantes locales afin favoriser le local, la simplicité et l'authentique. Publics adultes, enfants, team building, EVJF, évènementiel...

Name: NATÚLENS
Description:
Natúlens es un emprendimiento socioambiental y educativo que crea experiencias transformadoras para reconectar a las personas con la naturaleza y las culturas locales.

A través de un modelo que combina educación, fotografía y turismo sostenible, diseñamos vivencias en Áreas Naturales Protegidas que inspiran el cuidado del patrimonio biocultural y fortalecen la relación humano-naturaleza.

Nuestro propósito es fomentar la conexión entre humanos y naturaleza e inspirar a las personas al cuidado y difusión del patrimonio biocultural, contribuyendo al desarrollo económico local y a una cultura de respeto por la vida.

Desde la co-creación con comunidades, escuelas y empresas, Natúlens impulsa un turismo educativo y regenerativo que promueve la reflexión, la acción y el aprendizaje significativo en el entorno natural.

Name: Scotland's Evolution Projects
Description: Scotland's Evolution Projects (SEP) pioneers ecological regeneration and sustainable education initiatives, fostering community resilience through hands-on learning and impactful projects. Focusing on permaculture and horticultural workshops, offering opportunities to gain skills in organic gardening, regenerative land management, and biodiversity conservation. Join us in learning and sharing practices that promote a harmonious relationship between humans and nature.

Name: Wharfedale Naturalists
Description:
Wharfedale Naturalists Society is a thriving charity with over 600 members, first  established in 1945. With the support of over 60 volunteers we  are involved in the management of 6 Local Nature Reserves and run a program of talks, walks and visits throughtout the year charitable objects are
To promote and foster the study of all branches of natural history (by observation, research and record keeping)
and thereby to add to the existing knowledge of these sciences in the world at large and in Wharfedale in
particular;
ii) To promote the conservation, protection and improvement of the physical and natural environment.
For the purposes of these Objects ‘Wharfedale’ is defined as ‘all the catchment of the River Wharfe above the 430 km.Easting grid line near Rougemont Carr in Weeton Civil Parish near Harrogate, North Yorkshire’

Description:

Description:
UNE OASIS ÉDUCATIVE ET GOURMANDE
 Un espace collectif autogéré et ouvert à tous·tes basé sur les valeurs de One Health, d’écologie et de coopération.
Un lieu ressource, en connexion avec le vivant, pour partager le bonheur de vivre pleinement des expériences natures.

Graines de Bonheur est, depuis sa création en 2019, une histoire collective qui vise à développer les activités nature à destination des enfants et des familles par le bien-être, la créativité et le partage.

Name: Tumbuhan Asli Nusantara Foundation
Description: Research and plant conservation in Indonesia.

Name: Rhino Cup Champions League (RCCL)
Description:
At the RCCL, our mission is to utilize the unifying power of amateur soccer to tackle pressing social issues such as poaching, teen pregnancy, school dropouts, and substance abuse in Africa. Through community engagement, empowerment, and sportsmanship, we strive to create sustainable solutions that uplift both people and wildlife, fostering a brighter future for all.
The RCCL, comprising 120 men's, women's, and youth teams across Mozambique, Zimbabwe, Namibia, and Tanzania, serves as more than just a soccer league. It's a beacon of hope for communities residing near national parks and wildlife reserves. By providing employment opportunities for over 100 individuals, including referees, team transporters, and social media managers, the league injects vitality into these areas.
The RCCL aims to break the cycle of poaching by empowering and uplifting these communities. By engaging with poaching communities directly, the RCCL fosters dialogue and cooperation, offering alternatives to a life of crime.
The league's impact transcends the soccer field, offering tangible solutions to a multifaceted problem. It not only provides a source of income but also instills a sense of purpose and pride within these communities. By addressing the underlying issues fueling poaching, the RCCL stands as a beacon of hope in the fight to protect Africa's rhinos.
The world's greatest game has the power to save the world's greatest animals. Through collaboration, compassion, and community empowerment, the Rhino Cup Champions League is paving the way towards a future where humans and wildlife can thrive in harmony.

Name: Save My Future Foundation (SAMFU)
Description:
Save My Future Foundation is a local non-governmental conservation and development organization founded in 1987.

SAMFU works to support and promote environmental protection and bio-diversity, sustainable natural resource management, and inclusive community participation.

We envisage a Liberia where natural resources are effectively/efficiently and sustainably governed and managed to ensure equity in benefits-sharing.

Name: Aloha ʻĀina Drones
Description:
The Aloha ʻĀina Drone Company believes cutting edge drone technology can, and more importantly should, empower, protect, and benefit Hawaiʻi’s natural resources. To honor this belief, and the principal and promise of Aloha ʻĀina, we chose this name to remind, guide, and inspire ourselves to prove that advanced technologies should exist to not only better humanity, but all natural systems.

Comprised of three drone experts with over 30 combined years of experience in using drones for conservation efforts, our team has dedicated their professional lives to making the natural world a better place through transforming cutting-edge technologies into practical, field-ready tools capable of significant conservation impact.

Our expertise and experience is world-class in designing, developing, and implementing successful drone based projects that create significant positive change. We have worked with all levels of national and international governments, regional to global NGOs, and most importantly local communities, to catalyze significant benefits for natural resources via drone operations.

With our advanced drone systems, expertise, and Part 107 certification we can map landscapes in high resolution, detect endemic and invasive species, measure crop and forest health, and generate significant impacts and cost-savings for agriculture and conservation.

And with our heavy-payload drone platforms and 137 certification we can carry, spray, and spread over 50 lbs or 5 gallons of seeds, fertilizer, or pesticide per flight, treating 100 acres a day more precisely, efficiently, and safely than traditional methods—protecting farmers’ livelihoods and Hawaiʻi’s natural resources.

Based in Hilo, Hawaiʻi, we have conducted ground-breaking conservation and agricultural focused drone operations on Hawaiʻi Island, Maui, Lāna'i, Kahoʻolawe, Oahu, and Kauai, as well as island systems in the Galapagos,  Caribbean, Tahiti, Fiji, The Marshall Islands, Palau, Wallis and Futuna, and the USA

Name: Mariposario Arlequín Corp.
Description: Somos especialistas en el manejo y cuidado de las mariposas y los mariposarios. Nuestro enfoque principal es la educación y la conservación de las mariposas mediante la propagación en cautiverio para su posterior liberación en áreas naturales adecuadas.

Name: Wellington Conservation Center
Description:
Wellington Conservation Center is home to all unwanted, and abandoned animals. We care for orphaned birds and mammals and specialize in the conservation of exotic animals under the threat of habitat loss and poaching, including two critically endangered species. 
Our mission is simple; provide loving help and care to as many animals as we can! Whether it is taking in orphaned wildlife for rehabilitation, or an unwanted or abandoned pet that needs a happy life, we are here FOR THE ANIMALS! Even if we ourselves cannot give the animal the help it needs we do are darndest to find someone who will. We have pointed countless wildlife inquiries in the proper direction and placed multiple big cats and bears to specialized organizations all over the country!
Our story starts a long, long time ago in a foreign land called … Illinois. A family run farm originally suited for horses, slowly morphed into something even more special. With a heart too big to deny animals a home when needed, animals of all shapes and sizes started to call a new sanctuary their own. Lions, tigers, cougars, monkeys, alpacas, ostriches, and our favorite animal, the black bear. Not just any black bear, but one named Pooh, who would send us on a mission that we are still fighting for to this day. After over 20 years of running our facility in Chicago, the decision was made to move to this location in Wellington, Florida!

Illinois and Florida are a bit different, so we made a shift in our focus to wildlife rehabilitation and, utilizing the tropical environment, conservation programs for species under the threat of extinction. WCC now boasts four breeding programs for Linnaeus’s Two-toed Sloths, Southern Tamanduas, Black and White Ruffed Lemurs and Red Ruffed Lemurs! However the calls for rescues keep on pouring in, so we also supply lifelong homes to as many displaced animals as we can! Now at over 50 animals strong, we are a little full, but are always open for expansion!

Name: Rehkitzrettung Birsigtal
Description: Wir sind ein gemeinnütziger Verein, der es sich zur Aufgabe gemacht hat, Rehkitze mittels Multikopter und Wärmebildkamera zu retten. Dies ist die sicherste Methode um Rehkitze vor Mähmaschinen zu retten. Sie ist schnell, effizient und zuverlässig.

Name: A Dialogue
Description: A Dialogue’ focuses on biodiversity & traditional ecological knowledge, using in-depth immersive research involving Stanford bio design principles and further help our partners implement it on the ground with a well-defined impact and outcome matrix. Organisations that focus on the environment- sustainability, biodiversity, traditional ecological knowledge, food research & similar initiatives with the commitment to the cause of a sustainable future.

Name: Congo River Foundation
Description: The Congo River Foundation is established as a continuation and expansion of the vision developed by the Virunga Foundation (albeit fully independently), demonstrating the possibility to harmonize nature conservation, economic development, and regional security in one of the world’s most fragile ecosystems. Our purpose is to replicate and adapt this proven model throughout the Congo Basin, supporting local initiatives that integrate conservation, peacebuilding, and inclusive economic development. We aim to protect the biodiversity and ecological integrity of the Congo Basin while enhancing the socio-economic conditions of local communities

Name: Red Boliviana de Senderos
Description: En el año 2020, a partir de experiencias internacionales, fue fundada la Red Boliviana de Senderos de Larga Distancia con el objetivo de conectar el territorio nacional a través de senderos, actuando como una herramienta de conservación, aparato de recreación y alternativa para generar oportunidades de trabajo, ingresos económicos y desarrollo social a las comunidades locales.

Name: Gateway Conservation Alliance
Description: Our mission is to protect and enhance what we know and love about Gallatin Gateway, Montana, by addressing critical issues that threaten the area's water quality, citizen health, traffic safety and wildlife. Through community engagement, conservation initiatives, and advocacy, we strive to preserve the ecological integrity, promote sustainable development, and ensure the well-being of our environment and local residents for generations to come.

Name: Ecological Conservation for Biodiversity (ECODIV)
Description:
Ecological Conservation for Biodiversity (ECODIV) adalah organisasi yang berfokus pada peningkatan kapasitas individu maupun kelompok dalam bidang konservasi keanekaragaman hayati. ECODIV hadir sebagai wadah kolaboratif untuk membangun kesadaran, pengetahuan, dan keterampilan dalam pelestarian biodiversitas melalui kegiatan edukasi, pelatihan, penelitian, serta pengabdian kepada masyarakat.

Dengan mengusung semangat keberlanjutan dan pendekatan ilmiah, ECODIV berupaya menjembatani dunia akademik dan praktik konservasi di lapangan. Organisasi ini terbuka bagi pelajar, peneliti, profesional, dan masyarakat umum yang memiliki minat dalam bidang ekologi, konservasi spesies dan habitat, serta pengelolaan sumber daya alam yang berkelanjutan.

Name: Rekawa Turtle Watch
Description: Conserving The Marine Environment Of Sri Lanka.

Name: Naturpark Wendland.Elbe e.V.
Description:
Naturpark Wendland.Elbe e.V. – Gemeinsam für Natur, Mensch und Region

Der Naturpark Wendland.Elbe ist einer von 106 Naturparken in Deutschland – und etwas ganz Besonderes: Wo die Elbe auf das Wendland trifft, entsteht eine einzigartige Kulturlandschaft mit weiten Auen, artenreichen Lebensräumen und lebendigen Dörfern.

Als Träger des Naturparks setzen wir uns mit Engagement und Kreativität für nachhaltige Regionalentwicklung, Umweltbildung, Natur- und Landschaftsschutz sowie zukunftsorientierten Tourismus ein. Unsere Projekte stärken die Region – ökologisch, sozial und wirtschaftlich.

 Unsere Themen: 
– Schutz & Entwicklung naturnaher Lebensräume
– Umweltbildung & Naturerlebnis für alle Altersgruppen
– Stärkung nachhaltiger Wirtschafts- und Tourismusstrukturen
– Netzwerkarbeit & Zusammenarbeit mit Kommunen, Vereinen und Akteur:innen der Region
– Kommunikation, Marketing & Veranstaltungsmanagement für die Destination Wendland.Elbe

Mit dem Projekt Wendland.Elbe – nachhaltige Destination verbinden wir Natur- und Kulturerlebnis mit innovativem Tourismusmarketing. Wir fördern das, was die Region stark macht – authentisch, verantwortungsvoll und zukunftsfähig.

 Sitz des Vereins: Lüchow (Wendland)
 Mehr erfahren: https://naturpark-elbhoehen-wendland.de

#NaturparkWendlandElbe #NachhaltigeRegion #Naturerleben #TourismusmitZukunft #Wendland #Elbe #Regionalentwicklung #BiosphäreElbe

Name: Zambezi Impact Foundation
Description:
The Zambezi Impact Foundation makes tangible impact on the ground in Africa using exponential technologies.

The Zambezi Impact Foundation is made possible with the alignment in purpose and partnership of the Wild is Life (WIL, a 501 (c)3) and Zimbabwe Elephant Nursery (ZEN) organizations in Zimbabwe. Wild Is Life and the Zimbabwe Elephant Nursery have long worked on the rescue and rehabilitation of elephants and other animals.

Wild Is Life has a 20-year legacy. Beginning with a few rescued wild orphans to now being a quality animal sanctuary for injured, orphaned and rescued wildlife, as well as other endangered and specially protected species. They have rehabilitated more than 170 animals from over 25 different species. Wild Is Life manages conservation initiatives for animal welfare, community development, anti-poaching, and habitat restoration at both their Harare and Panda Masuie locations.

Name: DuraVault
Description: DuraVault supports the CDR by ensuring DURABILITY for Direct Biomass Storage projects

Name: Salonga Conservation Initiative
Description:
Our mission is to preserve the biodiversity of the Salonga National Park ecosystem, stronghold of the endangered bonobo.  We carry out critical field research that assesses wildlife populations, habitats and threats to guide impactful conservation strategies. 

We envision a Salonga National Park that sustains thriving bonobo and wildlife populations alongside flourishing communities within Africa's largest protected rainforest ecosystem.

Name: Friends of Pleasant Bay
Description: The Friends of Pleasant Bay is a grassroots environmental group dedicated to the preservation of one of Cape Cod's most stunning recreational and ecological resources. Since its founding in 1985 the group has been successful in achieving State designation of the Bay as an Area of Critical Environmental Concern, and was instrumental in development and State approval of a Resource Management Plan for the Bay. The Plan identifies the Bay as a regional resource requiring coordinated management of the surrounding towns. To meet this task, the four towns bordering the Bay subsequently formed the Pleasant Bay Alliance. The Friends provide ongoing support for implementation of the Resource Management Plan and host a range of other programs.

Name: Schoolcraft Conservation District
Description: Conservation districts are government subdivisions organized by the local community to provide natural resource management services and implement conservation measures that address local concerns. Our mission is to promote and facilitate the wise use of natural resources on private lands in Schoolcraft County, meeting the needs of both the current and future generations.

Name: Pusat Konservasi Peradaban Melayu Penyengat
Description:
Pulau Penyengat represents an outstanding cultural landscape that embodies the intellectual, spiritual, and political heritage of the Malay world. Situated in Tanjungpinang, Riau Islands, this historic island served as the center of the Riau–Lingga Sultanate and played a pivotal role in the development of Malay civilization in Southeast Asia.

The island preserves exceptional tangible and intangible heritage assets, including the Sultan Riau Grand Mosque, renowned for its unique construction using egg whites, and the tomb of Raja Ali Haji, a distinguished scholar recognized as the Father of the Indonesian Language. Together with the burial sites of other royal and cultural figures, these landmarks reflect the island’s profound contribution to language, governance, religion, and cultural identity across the Malay Archipelago.

Accessible by traditional pompong boats from Tanjungpinang, Pulau Penyengat has evolved into a carefully managed heritage tourism village, where historical authenticity, living traditions, and community-based tourism coexist. Local transportation such as bentor (motorized rickshaws) reinforces the continuity of daily cultural practices while supporting sustainable visitor engagement.

With its integrity, authenticity, and enduring cultural significance, Pulau Penyengat holds strong potential to be positioned as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, representing the universal values of Malay civilization and the shared heritage of humanity.

Task-Force:

Name: CASuDev
Description:
CASuDev is a community based nonprofit, nonpolitical and nongovernmental association, dedicated to the conservation of ecosystems and its biodiversity and to the promotion of sustainable development in rural areas. 

CASuDev est une association communautaire à but non lucratif, apolitique et non gouvernementale, qui se consacre à la conservation des écosystèmes et de leur biodiversité et à la promotion du développement durable dans les zones rurales.

Name: Bolton NEWT
Description: Empowering local people, providing space to dig & grow, volunteer opportunities and large outdoor community events

Name: Econatural Society Foundation
Description: Yayasan ini bergerak pada berbagai aspek dan dimensi dalam bidang perbaikan mutu lingkungan dan pengelolaan lingkungan pada umumnya dan lebih khusus lagi pada sektor Kelautan dan Perikanan dalam bentuk kajian keilmuan yang berhubungan dengan wilayah pesisir, pengembangan Sosial dan Budaya Masyarakat Pesisir dalam mengelola sumberdaya alam

Name: Raptor Conservation Initiative
Description: The Raptor Conservation Initiative (RCI) is a non‑profit committed to protecting and restoring vulnerable raptor species across sub‑Saharan Africa. By establishing locally managed rehabilitation centres, leveraging GPS‑tracking and toxin‑analysis research, and delivering hands‑on education programmes in schools and universities, we create sustainable jobs, empower communities and safeguard vital ecosystems for generations ahead.

Name: Laikpia Nature Conservancy - Gallmann Memorial Foundation - Kenya
Description:
Due to its unique topography which spans from 2000ft ASL to 6500 ft ASL, Ol Ari Nyiro encompasses several different habitats and eco-systems and is refuge to a wide variety of plant and animal species including elephant, lion, melanistic leopard, colobus monkeys, over 4000 cape buffalo and over 478 bird species, over 40 of which are on the IUCN endangered red list. The conservancy has been classified as a Key Biodiversity Area and an Important Bird Area.

Home to the last remaining stand of the indigenous cedar forests that up until 40 yrs ago spanned large areas of the plateau, the conservancy is a refuge where several endemic, rare, and threatened species of plants and animals survive. Due to careful protection of the environment for more than a generation, it is a biodiversity oasis of outstanding environmental and educational value for Kenya and the planet.

Name: Syndicat Intercommunautaire de la Vallée de l'Yèvre
Sustainable Seas Conference

The Miami University Five Rivers Fishing Club is the university’s first premier fishing and conservation organization — connecting students who share a passion for angling, outdoor adventure, and environmental stewardship.

As an official chapter of Trout Unlimited, we promote responsible fishing, waterway conservation, and community through education, service, and on-the-water experiences. Name: Directorate of Archaeology and Museums Maharashtra
Maisha Mlima
Description:
Maisha Mlima, ‘Mountain Life’ in Swahili, is an organization set up to develop trails and eco-friendly hut systems across Kenya. The mission of the organization is to promote conservation in some of the most beautiful and underused mountain ranges. 

We plan to build our first network of huts on Mt Kenya, the second highest
peak in Africa, with a technical summit at 17,000 ft elevation.

We realize that a lack of basic infrastructure on Mount Kenya limits accessibility and use of the park and pushes hikers to cut their own trails and campsites which damage the environment.

We are working to create new huts in 5-6 locations that span two different sides of the mountain.

Name: BeTreed Adventures
Description:
We welcome you to BeTreed Adventures in Preah Vihear Province, Cambodia. Working closely with the local community, we have developed a unique ecotourism destination for the off-the-beaten-path travellers, nature seekers and adventurers in our ecostay in a small part of wild Cambodia.
BeTreed Adventures is working on the frontline to protect the Phnom Tnout Phnom Pok Wildlife Sanctuary in Cambodia: 42,000 hectares of Cambodian’s “savannah” land, or dry dipterocarp forest. Through visiting, you will support these conservation efforts, encouraging the will of the people to preserve and protect their forest as they realise that conservation can provide a tangible and immediate benefit to them.
Here you can experience raw nature and find yourself finally away from the madding crowds of Cambodia. It is home to endangered and threatened species of Cambodian wildlife: banteng (wild cow), pileated gibbons, silver langurs, barking deer, sambar deer and many species of birdlife. And you might be lucky enough to see these special creatures. You can also explore Angkorian era “cave-temples”, and the quarries where the ancients carved out their stone. And specially constructed for the adventurous, our pair of 300 metre ziplines traversing a saddle of the mountain provides panoramic views of the surrounding forest.
You can become a part of preserving Cambodia’s natural heritage. Visit our ecotourism project in wild Cambodia. Maybe you like to experience an ecostay in our treehouse, “The Monkey Pod,” or  one of the more grounded stilt houses? Or just follow our occasional blog, or our Facebook page to keep up to date on developments in the forest.
Location: Ta Bos Village, Songkom Thmey District, Preah Vihear Province

Description: Whether through one of our guided walks or on your own, there is plenty to discover at Twelve Hills Nature Center. Name: Margaree Salmon Association
Description:
The Margaree Salmon Association, established in 1982, is a volunteer, non-profit organization dedicated to the conservation, protection, enhancement and habitat of Atlantic salmon and trout.

Since its founding, the Margaree Salmon Association has established itself as the voice for the salmon resource and salmon conservation on the Margaree River, including being a lobbying force on behalf of the Margaree Fish Hatchery.

An affiliate of the Atlantic Salmon Federation, (ASF), and the Nova Scotia Salmon Association, (NSSA), the MSA has been an active participant in the Regional Fisheries Advisory Committee, (RFAC), the Zonal Management Advisory Committee, (ZEMAC) and the Collaborative Fishing Initiative, (CSI Cape Breton), which is chaired/managed by Unama’ki Institute of Natural Resources. The association was also involved in the nomination and eventual designation of the Margaree River-Lake Ainslie System as a Canadian Heritage River.

Through armour rock bank stabilization programs and the construction of flow diverters and tree revetments, much of the Association’s efforts have centered on habitat restoration and repair. More recently, the association has put more focus on the tributaries feeding the main river which are, in reality, the river’s “hatcheries”. This work has been carried out in partnership with a variety of government agencies as well as the NS Adopt-A-Stream Program, administered by NSSA.

Due to the support of our membership, the river and its salmon resource have taken on a spiritual life of their own. Both yearly and occasional visitors, having come under the spell of the river, have felt compelled to do what they can to preserve the river and its salmon by whatever means they are able, whether physically or financially.

Name: Plum Gorgeous Conservation Retreat

We work with environmental non-profits, the Sunshine Coast Council and local community organisations to educate, protect and advocate for local biodiversity.

We welcome environmental specialists, wildlife workers and volunteers who are interested in rainforest rejuvenation and learning about Australia’s unique bush heritage and wildlife. 

We acknowledge the traditional owners of this land, the Gubbi Gubbi people, and pay respect to their Elders past, present and emerging.

Name: FarWide Conservation Trust
Description:
FarWide Conservation Trust is dedicated to the conservation of fish and wildlife habitat to further support the expansion of outdoor recreational opportunities.

As a group of conservationists working to protect outdoor recreation - the FarWide Conservation Trust will accomplish its mission to promote outdoor recreational opportunities and conserve important habitats for fish and wildlife by focusing on three important initiatives: 

1) Habitat Conservation in the PPR (Prairie Pothole Region)
2) Advancement of "Regenerative Agriculture" to improve waterway and habitat ecosystems.
3) Supporting the conversion of “Landlocked” Public Lands
 
The vision of FarWide Conservation Trust is an abundance of outdoor recreational opportunities that are compatible with the long term conservation of habitats that support healthy and plentiful populations of fish and wildlife.

Name: The Fly Connection Foundation
Description:
Fly fishing is all about connections. Through it we connect us to the Earth around us. To a world of water, land and sky. We connect to ourselves. To our pasts, presents and futures. We connect to each other. To our friends, old and new. This is why we fish. This is why we created The Fly Connection.

Our core values are simple:

We are an open and engaged community who stand for equality, diversity and inclusivity.

We are committed to bringing change through advocacy, knowledge sharing and ethical sustainability.

We are a place created by and for our members, and we place independence, integrity and transparency at the heart of all we do.

Name: WILD ALLIANCE AFRICA
Description:
Wild Alliance Africa is a conservation organisation driven by a commitment to protect our planet's biodiversity and encourage a sustainable coexistence between delicate ecosystems and the communities that surround them. With a vision of leaving a legacy of thriving ecosystems, empowered communities, and unity through conservation, we hope to educate, restore, protect and preserve our wild spaces for the benefit of all.


Vision:

Wild Alliance Africa envisions a world where wild spaces are protected, restored, and understood for generations to come. We are committed to preserving the biodiversity of our river, estuarine, and marine environments through research, education, and conservation initiatives.


Mission:

Wild Alliance Africa is dedicated to fostering a harmonious relationship between humans and nature by advocating for the protection and restoration of our wild spaces. Through strategic partnerships, research, impactful education programs, and sustainable conservation projects, we strive to create a future where both wildlife and communities thrive in balance.

Name: Eastern Maine Conservation Initiative
Description:
The Eastern Maine Conservation Initiative (EMCI) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to preserving and enhancing the natural and cultural heritage of Maine's Downeast coast. Founded in 1996, EMCI fills a vital role by focusing on the distinctive coastal region east of Mount Desert Island up to the Canadian border.
Our mission encompasses three core priorities:
1.	Building environmentally sustainable coastal communities.
2.	Preserving and celebrating Downeast Maine's rich cultural geography.
3.	Advancing ecological conservation through environmental research and education.
EMCI achieves these goals through two primary channels:
First, we fund scientific research and conservation projects that help guide sustainable coastal management practices. Working with private landowners, government agencies, and nonprofit partners, we develop strategies and support projects that protect unique ecological communities while supporting the region's economic and cultural vitality.
Second, we partner with historical societies, museums, libraries, and cultural institutions to preserve and make accessible the region's historical artifacts and documents, ensuring these valuable resources remain available for future generations.

Whether they are farms or private nature reserves, everyone is pulling in the same direction. This deep understanding makes it possible to protect and promote this incredible diversity of plants. There are very few places in the world where so many endemic species have survived.

Name: Bloomy Earth
Description:
Bloomy Earth empowers individuals and companies to take meaningful environmental action through tree planting, carbon offsetting, and sustainability engagement. We partner with vetted reforestation projects and NGOs across the globe to make climate impact accessible, transparent, and measurable.

With our no-code tools, plug-and-play APIs, and automated certificates, Bloomy Earth makes it easy to plant trees, track carbon offset, and share progress. Users can visualize their impact through personal and business dashboards, while businesses benefit from integration-ready solutions like Tree-as-a-Service, Bloomy Website, and product-linked planting.

At the heart of Bloomy Earth is a vibrant community — a social layer where people and organizations connect, celebrate progress, and inspire each other to act for the planet. Whether you’re an individual planting your first tree or a company embedding sustainability into your value chain, Bloomy Earth helps you grow your environmental impact — one tree at a time.

 Act local. Impact global. Join the Bloomy Earth movement.

Name: Impact for Climate Fund
Description:
Impact for Climate Fund (ICF) is an innovative investment fund dedicated to financing local climate projects, mainly in the Global South. Our aim is to strengthen the resilience of the most vulnerable communities in the face of climate change by mobilizing financial and technical resources from companies, NGOs and committed individuals.

We support local initiatives with a strong social and environmental impact, enabling companies, institutions and citizens to participate actively in the fight against climate change through an equitable and inclusive financing model.

Name: Le Parc Naturel du Gazou, Charente, France
Vanasa Conservation Foundation
Description:
Vanasa Conservation Foundation is established to safeguard biodiversity, restore ecological harmony, and promote the importance of conserving lesser-known species.

At Vanasa, we foster species survival through innovative research, evidence-based initiatives, and collaborative partnerships with stakeholders.

Our emphasis on evidence-based conservation action ensures that every step taken is backed by scientific knowledge and optimized for success. By drawing upon data and research, we prioritize conservation efforts, allocate resources efficiently, and implement practical solutions that yield positive outcomes for species at risk.

Our team is committed to prioritise species survival at a grassroot level. At the heart of our approach lies our commitment to advanced research. We explore ecosystem intricacies, study behaviour, habitats and threats faced by different species. By understanding the unique ecological needs and challenges of wildlife, we develop targeted conservation strategies that are effective and sustainable.

We recognize that conservation is a collective effort and involves working with local communities, hospitality ventures, government bodies and NGOs. Our skilful partnerships are key to project success. Engaging stakeholders at all levels cultivates a shared sense of responsibility for preserving our natural heritage. This approach amplifies our impact and empowers locals to become conservation advocates too.

Name: Ecoterra Bio

A ECOTERRA-BIO atua no setor de bioenergia realizando projetos turnkey de biogás para geração de energia elétrica, biometano, CO2 e fertilizante orgânico, a partir de resíduos sólidos urbanos, resíduos agroindustriais, plantas energéticas, resíduos da produção animal. A consultoria e o suporte biológico para o bom funcionamento das plantas de biogás é feito com base na experiência de profissionais que atuam neste ramo, a mais de 10 anos, tanto no Brasil como na Alemanha.
NEGÓCIO
Projetos na área de energia renovável, assistência, consultoria e venda de produtos para o bom funcionamento do processo biológico de plantas de biogás.

Name: Jongowe Limited
Description:
A consultancy firm that specializes in conservation and sustainable development. With over 20 years of experience in the third sector, I have a Master's degree in Environmental Science and a Bachelor's degree in Business Studies, which enable me to combine scientific knowledge with strategic planning and management skills.

My core competencies include scaling, impact evaluation, grants management, and community conservation. I have a proven track record of building and leading effective teams, securing and managing grants, and developing and implementing innovative solutions for complex challenges. 

Most recently, I have worked with sector leaders such as the National Geographic Society, Blue Ventures Conservation and the Australian Climate and Biodiversity Foundation to apply a rights-based approach to conservation globally. I am passionate about elevating the rights and livelihoods of those most in need and restoring the ecosystems upon which they rely.

La Fundación Tchendukua tiene como misión propiciar la conservación del medio ambiente natural y el mejoramiento de la vida de las comunidades indígenas enmarcado dentro del respeto a su cultura. 
•	Conservación del medio ambiente: Desarrollar acciones y proyectos destinados a proteger y preservar los ecosistemas naturales, incluyendo la biodiversidad, los recursos hídricos y la mitigación del cambio climático.
•	Mejoramiento de la vida de las comunidades indígenas: Desarrollar programas y proyectos que promuevan el desarrollo sostenible en las comunidades indígenas, principalmente en temas de seguridad alimentaria y derechos territoriales.
•	Promover el dialogo interculturalidad: Fomentar el respeto y la valoración de la cultura, tradiciones y conocimientos ancestrales de las comunidades indígenas, promoviendo la participación activa y el diálogo intercultural en diferente espacios institucionales o académicos.

Name: ONG AMPEF
Description:
Association Malienne pour la Protection de l’Environnement de la Faune

L’ONG-AMPEF – Association Malienne pour la Protection de l’Environnement et de la Faune – a pour mission de participer au développement socio-économique et à la sauvegarde de l’environnement. Elle vise un monde dont la pauvreté et l’injustice seront bannies. En ce qui concerne le terrain : l’ONG-AMPEF intervient pour la restauration et la conservation des sols et des ressources biologiques en milieux fluvial et lacustre, la restauration et la valorisation d’espèces végétales, la gestion de l’eau et le maraîchage.

Name: Projeto Eco-Comunidade
Description:
O Projeto Socioambiental Eco-Comunidade, tem como o objetivo diminuir a vulnerabilidade social presente na comunidade a partir de iniciativas que fomentem uma mudança de cultura em relação aos resíduos sólidos, produzidos todos os dias, promovendo  impactos no âmbito social, ambiental, de saúde, e na geração de renda e acesso a produtos e serviços. A metodologia deste projeto, direciona a população para uma mudança de comportamento em relação aos resíduos sólidos, a partir de participação social, prova disso, são os resultados obtidos em 24 meses.
Com o recolhimento cerca de 55 toneladas de resíduos sólidos, em sua maioria, embalagens plásticas, que equivalem a 70% dos recicláveis consumidos nesta região, evitando o descarte inadequado nas vias públicas, em praias, mares e esgotos, distribuiu  mais de 120 toneladas de alimentos para as famílias participantes do projeto. Além disso, proporcionou acessibilidade a produtos de primeira necessidade na Loja Eco Troca Cavalo Marinho, em parceria com a cooperativa da cidade para se tornar um centro de triagem, gerando mais empregos e renda no território. A média anual de coleta em 2023 era de 1,3 toneladas, mas somente no primeiro trimestre de 2024  média de coleta subiu para 2,2 toneladas, quase 70% a mais que no mesmo período de 2023.

Name: Projeto SurfLimpeza
Description: Localizado em Santos-SP, o Projeto SurfLimpeza tem como objetivo diminuir o micro lixo das praias e garantir um fim para esses resíduos, além de proporcionar qualidade de vida a todos os participantes.

Name: Pour l'Amour Des Abeilles, Association Lois 1901 d'Intérêt Général
Description:
Pour l’Amour Des Abeilles

Association Loi 1901 • Intérêt général • Écologie & Éducation

Pour l’Amour Des Abeilles développe et déploie des solutions écologiques, pédagogiques et territoriales au service du vivant, des citoyens, des organisations et des collectivités.
Basée sur le territoire pilote de Saint-Fons, notre association conçoit des modèles reproductibles appliqués ensuite à l’échelle nationale, interrégionale et internationale, en coopération avec des acteurs engagés du développement durable.

 Nos solutions & services

 iBox Verte — Support pédagogique écologique

Un outil éducatif innovant pour sensibiliser les enfants, les familles, les écoles et les entreprises à la biodiversité, à l’agroécologie et au fonctionnement du vivant.

 FPFormation — Centre de formation certifié Qualiopi

Des formations professionnelles en permaculture, agroécologie et autonomie alimentaire :
	•	Approche systémique
	•	Transmission intergénérationnelle
	•	Compétences pratiques et transition écologique opérationnelle

 Modèles de fermes écoles & modèles pédagogiques

Création de fermes-écoles clés en main, reproductibles et adaptées aux territoires :
	•	Productions résilientes
	•	Éducation à l’alimentation durable
	•	Espaces vivants à forte valeur sociale et écologique

Nous développons également des modules pédagogiques, accessibles à tous les âges, pour comprendre et protéger les écosystèmes.

 Préservation & multiplication des espèces autochtones

Propagation, sauvegarde et valorisation des espèces végétales et mellifères locales, en ville comme en zone agricole.

 Projets coopératifs & innovation

La Route du Cacao

Un projet international innovant, développé en co-construction avec GaiaLyss et Act4GlobalImpact, reliant biodiversité, navigation, économie régénérative, savoirs autochtones et solutions durables.
Un voyage vers l’Eldorado des solutions pour un futur plus fertile et coopératif.

Name: National Trust Tasmania
Description: The National Trust is Australia’s leading charity that advocates for the built environment. The Tasmanian branch of the Trust was formed in 1960 and is represented by eleven historic properties across Tasmania’s three regions. The Trust runs conservation appeals for more than twenty properties in community ownership. The Trust has been a leading advocate for Tasmania’s heritage, encompassing individual buildings, historic towns, industrial heritage and natural heritage.

Name: Friends Of Lagoon Creek
Description:
Friends of Lagoon Creek Group is a community not for profit conservation organisation dedicated to the protection and preservation of Lagoon Creek catchment, located in Caboolture. 
Our group has a strong focus on creating community commitment to environmental stewardship while working to enhance the biodiversity and ecological health of the local area.
Our mission is to be actively engaging the local community in conservation efforts and actively seek community involvement. We strive to create a sustainable future by promoting the restoration of its ecosystems, advocating for responsible land management practices, and raising awareness about the importance of protecting the unique biodiversity found in this area Through our dedicated team Friends of Lagoon Creek Group undertakes a wide range of conservation initiatives.

Name: Apollo Wildlife Club
Description:
Passionate about wildlife conservation and environmental stewardship? Join us at Apollo Wildlife Club, where we're empowering the next generation of conservation leaders!

 As the premier organization dedicated to wildlife education and advocacy, Apollo Wildlife Club is committed to fostering a deep appreciation for nature and wildlife among young individuals. Our mission is to inspire action, promote awareness, and drive positive change for the planet.

 At Apollo Wildlife Club, we offer a dynamic platform for students, nature enthusiasts, and aspiring conservationists to connect, learn, and make a difference. Whether you're interested in educational workshops, hands-on conservation projects, or engaging field trips, there's something for everyone to get involved in.

 By joining Apollo Wildlife Club, you'll have the opportunity to:

- Participate in workshops and seminars on wildlife conservation and sustainability.
- Engage in hands-on conservation projects to protect habitats and wildlife.
- Connect with like-minded individuals passionate about environmental causes.
- Gain valuable skills and experience in conservation advocacy and leadership.

 Together, we can make a real impact on the future of our planet. Join us at Apollo Wildlife Club and be part of the movement towards a more sustainable and wildlife-friendly world. Let's protect, preserve, and prosper together!

#WildlifeConservation #EnvironmentalStewardship #YouthLeadership #ApolloWildlifeClub

Name: Naturgarten Ruggell
Description:
Der Naturschaugarten ist ein Projekt der Gemeinde Ruggell, das sich dem Erhalt der einheimischen Flora widmet. Der naturnahe Garten ist ein lebendiges Beispiel dafür, dass sich Biodiversität und Naherholung nicht ausschliessen. 

Auf unserer LinkedIn-Page nehmen wir euch deshalb mit auf eine inspirierende Reise durch die vielfältige Flora und Fauna in Liechtenstein. Unser Ziel: Bildung und Bewusstsein für die Bedeutung der Natur zu fördern. Jeder Beitrag wird mit Liebe zum Detail und Respekt für unsere Umwelt erstellt.

Folgt dem Naturgarten Ruggell, um keine unserer faszinierenden Naturabenteuer zu verpassen!

Name: The Green Vision
Description:
Founded by Philanthropist and Activist Amy Green, The Green Vision is a formidable force in uniting leading organizations to tackle critical global issues such as climate change, wildlife preservation, and biodiversity. 

The Green Vision and partners have helped pioneer an impressive range of successful conservation initiatives across more than 20 countries, increasing vital protection for over 50 million hectares of land and more than 40 different threatened species. 

The Green Vision is also the force behind the Footprint of Life Gala, a signature event bringing together top organizations to create a conservation movement, galvanizing support and raising substantial funds for initiatives like Tusk.org, an initiative of Duke of Cambridge, Prince William; and Planetary Guardians founded by Virgin Group CEO Sir Richard Branson.

Name: Ambiental Engenharia
Description: Há mais de 21 anos, contribuindo para a preservação e recuperação do meio ambiente, promovendo a sustentabilidade e o equilíbrio ecológico.

Name: Programa Ojos Verdes
Description: As a non-profit program, Ojos Verdes develops projects that remediate and restore degraded rivers with a plant-based approach.

Name: Rolling Ridge Conservancy
Description: The Rolling Ridge Conservancy stewards a gem of protected land nestled between the Appalachian Trail on the Blue Ridge to the east and the Shenandoah River to the west. Our mission is to protect, preserve, and nurture this entrusted wilderness and ensure that human interactions with the land are sustainable, respectful, inspiring, and inclusive now and for generations to come. A volunteer board of trustees ensures that these 1600 acres are preserved through conservation easements and nurtured by thoughtful, effective stewardship and respectful use.

Name: Monarch Legacy Project
Description:
I’m the founder of Monarch Legacy Project, a mission-driven initiative to prevent the extinction of monarch butterflies through education, conservation, and ecological stewardship.

Building on that foundation, I’m launching a new branch in Fall 2025 focused on native pollinator landscaping consultation—what I call ReNativization. This is more than landscaping; it’s a movement to restore native plant communities, rebuild critical habitats, and reweave the ecological fabric of our yards, parks, and shared spaces.

Through science-based design, community collaboration, and a deep commitment to biodiversity, I help landowners and organizations create purposeful landscapes that support pollinators, native wildlife, and long-term sustainability.

Whether you’re looking to replace a traditional lawn, support monarch migration, or transform unused space into a thriving habitat, my work turns your land into part of the solution.

Name: Idaho Bee Atlas
Description:
The Idaho Bee Atlas is a statewide volunteer initiative documenting and conserving the more than 720 species of native bees found in Idaho. The project emphasizes data sharing, specimen collection and curation, outreach, and bee conservation knowledge linked to the state’s native flora. 

The collection program draws on the tools and structure of the Oregon State University Master Melittologist Program while maintaining its own Idaho-focused framework. A growing group of volunteers contributes to both field collections and specimen curation. This work is hosted at the Orma J. Smith Museum of Natural History at the College of Idaho.

Data on Idaho bees is sparse and scattered.  The Idaho Bee Atlas has initiated the creation and dissemination of an Open Checklist of the Bees of Idaho, which will provide a roadmap for future collection work and other bee research.  We invite and encourage contributions and assistance in validating our work on this important project.

The Idaho Bee Atlas highlights the critical role of citizen science in expanding knowledge of pollinator diversity and ecology in the region.

Name: Campo Los Hornillos - Reserva de Bosque Serrano
Description: Campo Los Hornillos es una propiedad de 230 hectáreas que conserva de manera voluntaria bosque serrano (chaco forest) y pastizal de altura, así como también parte de las cuencas hidrográficas de Los Manantiales y Los Hornillos en las Sierras Chicas de Córdoba. Forma parte de la Red Argentina de Reservas Privadas. En el mismo se realizan actividades de investigación, llevadas adelante por diversas universidades y por investigadores del CONICET (Consejo Nacional de Investigaciones Científicas y Técnicas). En el marco de su sustentabilidad económica realiza diversas actividades y otras en transición: ganadería regenerativa, producción orgánica, recepción de personas o grupos que quieran estar en contacto profundo con la naturaleza. Promueve el concepto de "quiet parks", la "meditación y descanso", entre otras prácticas de nulo o bajo impacto como birdwatching, shinrin shoku (baño de bosques)... Todas herramientas de financiamiento que hacen posible la conservación privada de la naturaleza ante la gran presión por avance inmobiliario para desarrollo urbanístico. Promueve el respeto e interrelación permanente con prácticas culturales locales como la producción de ganadería bajo monte serrana, la recolección de plantas medicinales y hongos, la actividad de caminar por senderos serranos, la protección de paisajes e hitos de valor cultural y natural.

Name: Ecological Restoration Association at UCLA
Description:
Stone Canyon Creek (SCC) is an active restoration site in the heart of UCLA’s Westwood campus. This site is the focus of extensive habitat restoration currently managed by the student-run Ecological Restoration Association (ERA). Located adjacent to the Anderson School of Management, SCC protects one of the last open streams, and surrounding riparian ecosystem, in the area. The site is inhabited by many native shrub, tree, and grass species including, but not limited: white alder, mugwort, California sycamores, and California roses that aid in the health of the surrounding ecosystem and provide students with a secluded area perfect for relaxation and enjoyment of nature.

The Stone Canyon Creek Restoration Project was established in 2006 by The Bay Foundation with the aid of the UCLA Lab School. The goal of this restoration project was, and still currently remains: remove invasive plant species and plant/promote the growth of native species. We hope that continual, diligent efforts of our volunteers over the years will bolster the health of the ecosystem, restore SCC’s biodiversity, and ultimately bring back some of the area’s lost native animal species. Today, this work is carried out by the students of ERA, under the supervision of Dr. Alison Lipman of UCLA’s Institute of the Environment and Sustainability (IOES).

The primary goal of ERA is to preserve the natural beauty and biodiversity of Stone Canyon Creek by removing invasive plant species, planting + caring for native species, and spreading awareness of opportunities to become involved in restoration projects and events. With time and effort, it is the hope that our work and the contributions of our volunteers will one day see SCC restored to its former natural state and beauty!

If you have an interest in ecological fieldwork, preservation of natural habitats, or just enjoy spending time in nature, then please feel free to become involved in ERA!

Name: Holmes Soil & Water Conservation District
